Text: It’s Maester Luwin, my lord.
ls it true about Stannis and Renly?
Kevan Lannister:  First we must return to Casterly Rock to raise –
We speak in the presence of the Drowned God. In his name we father today to choose a new king as our leader. Who makes a claim?
May Euron, your servant, be born again from the sea as you were. Bless him with salt. Bless him with stone. Bless him with steel.
Let the fish eat the scales off his eyes.
What is dead may never die, but rises again harder and stronger.
What is dead may never die.
They stole our best ships.

Listen to the waves. Listen to the god. He is speaking to us and he says we shall have no king but Euron Greyjoy. Let the sea wash your follies and your vanities away. Let the old Euron drown. Let his lungs fill with seawater.
Lord, take your servant Balon back beneath the waves. Feed the creatures of your kingdom on his flesh. Pull his bones down to your depths to rest beside his ancestors and his children.
The Salt Throne is not yours to swear upon, not unless the kingsmoot chooses you.
Your father does not get to choose. The law is clear. Perhaps you'll win. Perhaps you’ll be the first woman in history to rule the Ironborn. And perhaps not.
(chanting)  The King in the North! The King in the North! The King in the North! The King in the North!
(chanting)  The King in the North! The King in the North! The King in the North! The King in the North!
The King in the North! The King in the North! The King in the North! The King in the North! The King in the North! The King in the North! The King in the North! The King in the North!
Long may she reign.
Sword! Sword! Sword! Sword! Sword! Sword! Sword! Sword! Sword!
Save our sons from war, we pray
Halfman! Halfman! Halfman!
We do!
We do!
Hail King Joffrey. Hail Queen Margaery.
All hail Bran the Broken!
The Queen in the North! The Queen in the North! The Queen in the North! The Queen in the North! The Queen in the North!
Long may he reign!
Aye!
Aye!
Aye!
Aye!
Aye!
Aye!
Aye!
What is dead may never die.
And now his watch has ended.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: To Gendry!
Rah! Rah! Rah! Rah!
What is dead may never die.
Stand together!
Aye!
And now their watch is ended.

And win no glory. I shall live and die at my post. I am the sword in the darkness. I am the watcher on the walls. I am the shield that guards the realms of men. I pledge my life and honor to the Night's Watch, for this night and all the nights to come!
Man says he saw your uncle at Hardhome the last full moon.
Could be. There are ways to find out.
Over there.
For the Watch.
Get ready!
A hundred thousand you say?
You can say it if you like. We should've sealed the tunnel while we had the chance like you suggested.

Do you know what leadership means, Lord Snow? It means that the person in charge gets second-guessed by every clever little twat with a mouth. But if he starts second-guessing himself, that's the end. For him. For the clever little twats. For everyone. This is not the end. Not for us. Not if you lot do your duty for however long it takes to beat them back. And then you get to go on hating me and I get to go on wishing your Wildling whore had finished the job.
Archers knock, everyone else hold.
I said knock and hold you cunts! Does knock mean draw?!
Does fucking hold mean fucking drop?!
You all plan to die here tonight?
That's very good to hear. Draw!
Now?
I'm going down then. Brother Slynt, you have The Wall.
What are you fucking waiting for, loose!

Brothers! A hundred generations have defended this castle. We've never fallen before. She will not fall tongiht. Those are Things at our walls. They eat the flesh of the men they kill. Do you want to fill the belly of a Thing tonight?
Tonight we fight. And when the sun rises I promise you, Castle Black will stand.
The Night's Watch will stand.
With me now! Now, with me!
Protect the gate!
Hold the fucking gate! Hold it!
Open the gates.
You have a good heart, Jon Snow. It will get us all killed.
Lord Snow.
This is no place for wild beasts. Lock him away or I'll let Hobb throw him in tonight's stew.
We've been preparing.
And how would we range north?
Coward. You would cut off our legs, pluck out our eyes, leave us cowering behind the Wall hoping for the storm to pass?
This castle has stood for thousands of years. The Night's Watch has defended her for thousands of years. And in all those centuries, we have never sealed the tunnel.
The bars on those gates are four inches thick. Cold-rolled steel.
Remind me which order you belong to, Lord Snow.
Are the stewards responsible for maintaining the tunnel?
Who would that be?
Ah, the builders. First Builder Yarwyck, Lord Snow here recommends sealing the tunnel, leaving us unable to carry out our duties as sworn brothers of the Night's Watch. Do you agree with him?
Given your deep knowledge of the wildling army, Lord Snow, you and Tarly will take night duties atop the Wall until the full moon.
Moving on. First Builder, we'll need 100 barrels of pitch brought atop the wall.
Lord Commander, it is my duty to tell you I believe this mission to be reckless, foolhardy, and an insult to all of the Brothers that have died fighting the wildlings.
You’re losing all your friends, Tarly.
We've been fighting them all for thousands of years. They've slaughtered villages. They've slaughtered our brothers.
Grenn's a ranger.You're a steward. Maybe you forgot that while you were off with your wildling bitch, but I didn't.
Tell them your name.
Well, you couldn't be any worse than you look. See what he can do.
On your feet. Pick up your sword. Hit him till he finds his feet. It seems they've run short of poachers and thieves down south. Now they send us squealing bloody pigs. Again, harder.

Looks like the bastard's in love. All right then, lord Snow, you wish to defend your lady love, let's make it an exercise. You two. Three of you ought to be sufficient to make lady piggy squeal. All you've got to do is get past the bastard.
We're done for today. Go clean the armory. That's all you're good for.
What are you waiting for? Attack him! You, get in there.
You think this is funny, do you? When you're out there beyond the Wall with the sun going down, do you want a man at your back? Or a sniveling boy?
Enjoying yourselves? You look cold, boys.

A bit nippy, yeah, by the fire, indoors. It's still summer. Do you boys even remember the last winter? How long has it been now? What, 10 years? I remember. Was it uncomfortable at Winterfell? Were there days when you just couldn't get warm, never mind how many fires your servants built?

That's admirable. I spent six months out there, beyond the Wall during the last winter. It was supposed to be a two-week mission. We heard a rumor Mance Rayder was planning to attack Eastwatch. So we went out to look for some of his men... Capture them, gather some knowledge. The Wildlings who fight for Mance Rayder are hard men. Harder than you'll ever be. They know their country better than we do. They knew there was a storm coming in. So they hid in their caves and waited for it to pass. And we got caught in the open. Wind so strong it yanked 100-foot trees straight from the ground, roots and all. If you took your gloves off to find your cock to have a piss, you lost a finger to the frost. And all in darkness. You don't know cold. Neither of you do. The horses died first. We didn't have enough to feed them, to keep them warm. Eating the horses was easy. But later when we started to fall... That wasn't easy. We should have had a couple of boys like you along, shouldn't we? Soft, fat boys like you. We'd have lasted a fortnight on you and still had bones leftover for soup. Soon we'll have new recruits and you lot will be passed along to the Lord Commander for assignment and they will call you men of the Night's Watch, but you'd be fools to believe it. You're boys still. And come the winter you will die... Like flies.
Lord Snow. What do you think you're doing?
And that someone isn't you. Go find a chamber pot to empty. Go on. Do it. You traitor's bastard. Give me an excuse. Mormont's not hereto protect you now.
Get back to work. Now! I said now!
You think I care if they like me? We're at war.
Come on, get on with it. What are you waiting for? Summer?
Your foray to Craster's Keep, I'll sanction it. But I won't order anyone to go with you. Volunteers only.
(nods)
What took you so long?
By what?
We just went over this, boy. -Justice can wait.
If that were a real sword, you'd be dead. Lord Snow here grew up in a castle spitting down on the likes of you. Pyp. Do you think Ned Stark's bastard bleeds like the rest of us?
Next!
Next!
Lord Snow, it appears you're the least useless person here. Go clean yourselves up. There's only so much I can stomach in a day.
There he is, Sam the Slayer.
If we go after them, we'll be giving them what they want. They want to draw us out, pick us off a few at a time.
You're a champion of the common people, Lord Snow. What do you say to Brother Pyp's proposition?
We need to shore up Castle Black and we need to defend the Wall. That is our job.

I can't argue with any of that. (stands up) But who does Jon Snow want to command? Night's Watch or the wildlings? Everyone knows he loved a wildling girl and spoke with Mance Rayder many times. What would have happened in that tent between those two old friends if Stannis's army hadn't come along? We all saw him put the King-beyond-the-Wall out of his misery. Do you want to choose a man who has fought the wildlings all his life, or a man who makes love to them?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Crowded. You'd think we were serving venison stew.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: It’s time, Ser Davos. Open the door and the men inside can rejoin their brothers in peace. We’ll even set the wolf free north of the Wall where it belongs. Nobody needs to die tonight.
Come on.
Attack!
Fight, you cowards!
For thousands of years the Night’s Watch has held Castle Black against the Wildlings.
You fucking traitor.
These men need a firm hand. Always have. They’re poachers and thieves.
Not soldiers.
So you admit you murdered Qhorin Halfhand?
No? You put your sword through a brother of the Night's Watch. What do you call that?
Don't talk about the Halfhand as if you knew him. He was my brother.
There's a difference between sneaking off to the Mole's Town brothel and sleeping with the enemy.
Impossible. You can't get 50 wildlings together before they start killing each other.
I am acting commander here, Maester Aemon.
And I don't trust the bastard.
And you always know when a man's telling a lie? How did you acquire this magical power?
You all know why you’re here. Jon Snow is dead.
I did.
And Bowen Marsh and Othell Yarwyck and the other officers in this castle.
You’re right!

We’ve committed treason, all of us. Jon Snow was my Lord Commander. I had no love for him. That was no secret. But I never once disobeyed an order. Loyalty is the foundation on which the Night’s Watch is built, and the Watch means everything to me. I have given my life, we have all given our lives to the Night’s Watch. Jon Snow was going to destroy the Night’s Watch. He let the wildlings through our gates, as no Lord Commander has ever done before. He gave them the very land on which they reaved and raped and murdered. Lord Commander Snow did what he thought was right, I’ve no doubt about that. And what he thought was right would have been the end of us. He thrust a terrible choice upon us, and we made it.
Ser Davos, we have no cause to fight. We are both anointed knights.
I will grant amnesty to all brothers who thrown down their arms before nightfall. And you, Ser Davos, I will allow you to travel south, a free man with a fresh horse.
What?
We’ll give you food. You can bring the Red Woman with you if you like. Or you can leave her here with us, whichever you choose. But surrender by nightfall or this ends with blood.

I had a choice, Lord Commander. Betray you or betray the Night’s Watch. You brought an army of wildlings into our lands. An army of muderers and raiders. If I had to do it all over knowing where I’d end up, I pray I’d make the right choice again.
I fought, I lost. Now I rest. But you, Lord Snow, you’ll be fighting their battles forever.
She admired your spirit, Your Grace.
She
She tore the paper in half, Your Grace.
Cinda Lannister.
Well, perhaps she's gotten a little larger than she
I squired for you once, you know.
The tournament, the day of Willem Frey's wedding.
You did. Your squire had gotten so drunk the night before that he threw up.
Bryan.
That's right. I ran up and volunteered. "Let me, my lord. Let me. " My father was furious. Afraid I'd embarrass our family in front of the Family.
You really remember?
When I think back to that day
I Never mind. It's embarrassing.

I remember everything about that day, your helmet, your horse, the rake lines in the dirt along the list, where the sun was in the sky when you knocked Balon from his horse, and the dent in your shield when you handed it back to me. I'll remember it all until I die. That was the best day of my life. And I remember being on the field after it was over. All the competitors were done. I was the last one out there. And I couldn't leave. I couldn't bring myself to go and sit with my family at a table so far on the edge of the feast you could barely even see the bride. And I couldn't bear to tell them what it had been like squiring for you, when I knew that they could never have the faintest idea what I was talking about.
How could you? Sorry, I I didn't mean to doubt you.
Which knight was it?
What was he like?
It's hard to put into words.
(nods) Leaving that battlefield was like being dragged off to prison.
Did you squire for him again?
(smiles)
And have you thought about
And?
(nods)
What is it?
Well, let me help you.
Tell me.
I will, Your Grace.
I did. They have not broken his spirit, Your Grace.
I will, Your Grace.
I am, Your Grace.
An honorable request, Your Grace.
These are... Your Grace, these are...
King Joffrey is a Baratheon, Your Grace.
My lord?

Text: You're good. You're not as good as you think you are.
Aye, but you took your sweet time of it. You won't be fighting straw men, little lady. Show me your position.
Keep your elbow high. You want your back doing the hard labor. You're holding. Never hold.
Your muscles tense up when you hold. Pull the string back to the center of your chin and release. Never hold.
Never aim.
Your eye knows where it wants the arrow to go. Trust your eye.
That's about far enough.
Begging your pardon, m'lady, but we'll be the judge of that.
Bodkin point. Penetrates plate at 200 yards. Can you make 'em?
I'll get you the steel.
That's 'cause you're a girl.
We're outlaws. Outlaws steal. You're lucky we didn't kill you.
Some of us were swineherds. And some of us tanners and masons. That was before.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: And a kinder fate than you deserve. Lions you call yourselves. At the Mummer's Ford, girls of seven years were raped and babes still on the breast were cut in two while their mothers watched.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Murder is a crime.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Better than anyone you've ever met.
Why? So you can kiss him?
Now, apologies, but you're one ugly fucker and I'd rather not see you no more.
(sarcastically) Watch your head. Off we go.
Just a dirty little cub, I think.
Generous.
The fat to your left and the lad beside him.
Here's the thing, fat
When I'm done talking, that arrow's falling down on your fat head. So I advise you move, because I'm done talking.
Half the country's starving and look at this one.
Hey, hey.
One waits for him to drink until he passes out.
My queen, our first contest. Who will triumph: the strong or the quick?
We ask again: who will triumph?
A Meereenese champion?
...or a Westerosi Knight?!
Free citizens of Meereen! By the blessings of the Graces and her majesty the Queen, welcome to the Great Games!
Lothor Brune, freerider in the service of Lord Baelish.
Ser Dontos the Red of House Hollard.
(Yells) Ser Dontos the Red of House Hollard!
Knock!  Draw!
You’ll find out very soon.
Prince Doran hopes this satisfies your concerns about the princess’s wellbeing.
Yes, my prince.
I remember how.
Drop you weapons!
Drop your weapons!
When you were whole, it would have been a good fight.
The prince does not wish to be disturbed.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Lord Stark.
We weren’t there.
Our prince wanted us here.
I wish you good fortune in the wars to come.
And now it begins.
Better.
No one’s seen him.
He called us his little birds. He gave us sweets.
What are you doing here?
How did you know we'd come this way?
How did you kill those guards? Was it hard?
Where?
My dancing master was from Braavos.
I want to. But I can't. I need to find my brother and mother. And my sister. I need to find her, too.
What is it?
Could it buy a horse?
Then what good is it?
Valar Morghulis.
Please don't go, Jaqen.
Valar Morghulis.
Mind if I keep warm?  Frey Soldier #2: Fuck off.
But I'm hungry.
I've got money.
It's worth a lot.
Sorry.
From you.
The first man.
(whispers) Valar morghulis.
People coming. You can shit later, there’s people coming!
Morning.
About ten more miles.
Are you a knight?
But you know how to use that sword.
Does it have a name?
Mine’s “Needle.”
Who taught you how to fight?
Mine never wanted to. Said fighting was for boys.
My mother’s dead.
You’re not a northerner.
Why didn’t you?
The Bloody Gate’s ten miles.
I don’t care what you swore!
I don’t need saving.
You’re gonna die?
I’ll last longer than you.
I want to see the captain.
I want to go north, to the wall.
I can pay.
I wouldn’t need a cabin. Please, I could work, scrubbing the floors, or…
Where’s home?
Wait, I have something else.
It’s not silver, it’s iron.
Valar morghulis.

You were the first person on my list, you know. For killing Syrio Forel, remember him? Probably not. I’ve gotten a few of the others. The Many Faced God stole a few more for me. I’m glad he left me you. Do you know who I am?
I can’t hear you.
You know who I am. I’m Arya Stark.
Do you know who you are?
You’re no one. You’re nothing.
No! No! No, you don’t die! Don’t die! (sobs)
He was my friend.
But if you’re… (looks to the Jaqen who drank the potion and is seemingly dead) Who’s this?
I can’t see!
What’s happening?! What’s happening?!
My name is Arya Stark. I want you to know that. The last thing you’re ever going to see is a Stark smiling down at you as you die.
l'm not a boy!
Arry.

(pointing Needle at Hot Pie) You want it? l'll give it to you. l already killed one fat boy. l bet you never killed anyone. l bet you're a liar. But l'm not. l'm good at killing fat boys. l like killing fat boys.
lt was a gift.
Which are you?
Don't! Don't kill him.
You're so dangerous, aren't you? Saying scary things to little girls. Killing little boys and old people.  A real hard man you are.
You're wrong.  I know a killer. A real killer.
You'd be like a kitten to him.  He'd kill you with his little finger.
No.
Don't kill him!  Please.  Please don't.
No one’s going to believe you’re a hog farmer if you eat them all.
I know they’re still there.
I’m not afraid.
I knew fear when I saw it in you.  You’re afraid of fire.
When Beric’s sword went up in flames, you looked like a scared little girl.
And I know why, too.  I heard what your brother did to you.  Pressed your face to the fire like you’re a nice juicy mutton chop.
Might do.
Someday I’m gonna put a sword through your eye and out the back of your skull.
Oysters, clams, and cockels! Oysters, clams, and cockels! Oysters, clams, and cockels! Oysters, clams, and cock--
Oysters, clams, and cockels! Oysters, clams, and cockels!
Three coppers.
The Thin Man wasn’t hungry today.
Tomorrow.
Could l have one? A lemon one or any of them.
How about a nice fat pigeon?
Do you have any stale ones from yesterday? Or any burnt ones?
Hey, where's everyone going? What's happening?
Who?
Let me go.
Where's Jaqen?
Where's Jaqen?
I need him now. Lord Tywin's marching tonight.
He's helping me.
Where?
Where?
Where?
I'm sorry. I'll
(annoyed) Where were you?
Tywin Lannister was right here, and now he's gone.
How long after I give you the name does it take you to kill someone?
He's taking his army to attack my brother. I need him dead right now.
You promised you'd help me.
And you'll kill them? Anybody?
All right. Jaqen H'ghar.
That's right.
I'm not joking. A man can go kill himself.
No.
I'll un-name you.
If you help me and my friends escape.
Fine, Jaqen H'ghar.
A girl will obey.
He didn't say. He just said, "Walk through the gates."
He didn't say anything about the guards.
We have to trust him.
Shut up. Stay here if you're afraid.
There's no one worse than you.
You're lying.
Is that the Blackwater?
Back to King's Landing to Joffrey and the queen.
But why?
Why would they be at the Twins?
Lots of things make me happy.
Killing Polliver, killing Rorge.
At least I could have been there to watch. I wanted to see the look in his eyes when he knew it was over.
You protected him for most of his life. You think you could have saved him?
That's your stupid pride talking. It's why you'll never be a great killer. I'd have killed Joffrey with a chicken bone if I had to.
You should have let me burn it.
That fleabite's got you walking a lot slower than you used to.
You really think my aunt will pay for me?
I've never even met her.
I'm not a lady.
My name is Lana. I’m an orphan. When I was 8 I begged enough money to buy my first bucket of oysters, and I sold that bucket and made enough money to buy 2 more.
Left onto Ragman Lane, where I do most of my trade.
How will she serve him?
What will she do there?
See what?
Oysters, clams and cockles!
Oysters clams and cockles! Oysters clams and cockles!
Best in the city.
So why would a captain make the wager in the first place?
If the captain dies, the thin man pays his family a lot of money.
The Many Faced God. You said you didn’t know what I would see at the docks.
And then what?
You’re good at that. Where did you learn?
And good at putting holes in them.
What happened to the actress? The one who wanted you dead.
Mmm.
I can’t.
I don’t think I could remember all of the lines.
You wouldn’t be safe. Not while she’s looking for me.
She doesn’t have a name.
Essos is east and Westeros is west. But what’s west of Westeros?
Nobody does. That’s where all the maps stop.
I’d like to see that.
What’s that?
I don’t want any.
Lady Crane?
Lady Crane?
You told her to kill me.
A girl is Arya Stark of Winterfell and I’m going home.

Iit took a while but I finally saved enough to buy myself an oyster cart. Now every morning I make my way down to the canals. I pass the fish mongers and the bakers. I usually see Lhara, my first customer of the day on my way home. Then I turn left onto Moonsinger Lane--
You said right, but you went left.
Only 'cause you lied.
I was so. I watched, but you...
My father wouldn't send you.
And I don't have to go with you if I don't want.
Come with me. Run.
Not today.
Not today. Not today.
Needle.
What do you want?
Leave me be. My father's a lord. He'll reward you.
Stay away!
Shut up. What did you bring?
Yes, my lord.
I'll bring something else.
No.
I'll eat in the kitchen later.
I eat a lot. I just don't grow.
Have you ever lost before?
(shakes her head)
Dragons?
Aegon and his sisters.
It wasn't just Aegon riding his dragon. It was Rhaenys and Visenya, too.
Rhaenys rode Meraxes. Visenya rode Vhagar.
Visenya Targaryen was a great warrior. She had a Valyrian steel sword she called Dark Sister.
(smiles)
Most girls are idiots.
From my father.
Have you met many stonemasons, my lord?
My mother served Lady Dustin for many years, my lord. She taught me how to speak proper Properly.
Yes.
I don't talk to traitors.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: But you did. You took the gold and you gave him up.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: He's not my one true god.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I don't care what you swear because you're a liar. You lied to Gendry, you lied to me. You'll lie to anyone. I hope the Lannisters kill you all.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Could be soldiers.
You shouldn't be sitting out here like this.
So why go on?
Nothing could be worse than this.
Nothing isn't better or worse than anything. Nothing is just nothing.
My name is Arya. Arya Stark.
You were Yoren's prisoners when he was taking me to the Wall. He told me he'd fuck me bloody with a stick.
He can't be. I don't know his name.
Thank you.
You're doing it wrong. You need to burn away that horrible bit there. Otherwise it's gonna get infected and fester. I know you don't like fire, but if you don't do it right--
It'll only take a second. It won't hurt that much.
Why?
Let me wash it out and help you sew it up at least.
You’re Westerosi.
I want to book passage home.
Why do you care?
I want a cabin. And we’ll leave at dawn.
See you at sunrise.
Are you sure you want to do this?
And what does honor demand?
All right, then. Get on with it.
My sister asked you a question.
You told our mother
Are you all right?
You did the right thing.
I'm just the executioner. You passed the sentence. You're the Lady.
I was never going to be as good a lady as you. So I had to be something else. I never could have survived what you survived.
I believe that's the nicest thing you've ever said to me.

I miss him.
Pardon, my lord.

Text: Joffrey. Cersei. Ilyn Payne.
What?
But I have to aim.
Never aim?
There's someone out there.
I don't like that woman.
What does that have to do with anything?
What are you doing? Let go of him.
Tell them to stop. Come on. He wants to be one of you. He wants to join the Brotherhood. Stop them!
Did the Lord of Light tell you that or did she?
You're not doing this for your god. You're doing it for gold.
You're a witch. You're going to hurt him.
What are they doing with the bodies?
No! I’ve been here for weeks. I’m not scrubbing one more corpse until you tell me why I’m doing it.
When?
When do I get to play the game?
The game. The game of faces.
I didn’t even know I was playing.
No one.
Well who are you? What are you doing here? How’d you get here?
What?
Arya.
Westeros. My family home is Winterfell. I’m the youngest daughter of a great lord. Eddard Stark. He died in battle.
After he was executed I fled the capital. Had to kill a stable boy. Drove my sword through his back.
I stabbed him in the gut. I tried to find my mother and brother. I never did. They were murdered by Walder Frey. An outlaw kidnapped me. A man called Polliver.

A man called the Hound. Sandor Clegane. He tried to sell me but was wounded in a fight. He begged me to kill him but I wouldn’t. I left him in the mountains to die. I wanted him to suffer, I hated him.
I hated him.
I hated him!
That’s not a lie!
Yes.
I’m not playing this stupid game anymore!
Valar dohaeris.

I know. Don’t be afraid. I used to be like you. I was sick. I was dying. But my father never gave up on me. He loved me. More than anything in the world, just like your father loves you. So he brought me here. My father prayed to the many faced god. And I drank the water from this fountain. It healed me.
I’ve devoted my life to him now. You don’t want to hurt anymore. Drink.
Nothing.
Three times.
No.
You’re very good.
So change it. It would all just be farting, belching, and slapping without you.
The queen loves her son more than anything. And he was taken from her before she could say good-bye. She wouldn’t just cry. She would be angry. She would want to kill the person who did this to her.
Mercy.
I have to go. My father’s waiting for me.
Careful of that one. She wants you dead.
Father used to watch us from up here.  He wouldn't say much.  You probably don't remember.  You were inside knitting all the time.

One time the boys were shooting arrows with Ser Rodrick.  I came out here after and Bran had left his bow behind just lying on the ground.  Ser Rodrick would have cuffed him if he saw.  There was one arrow in the target.  There was no one around, just like now.  No one to stop me.  So I started shooting.  And every shot I had to go out there and get my one arrow and walk back and shoot it again.  I wasn't very good.  Finally I hit the bullseye.  It could have been the 20th shot or the 50th.  I don't remember.  But I hit the bullseye and I heard this.

I looked up and he's standing right here smiling down at me.  I knew what I was doing was against the rules.  But he was smiling so I knew it wasn't wrong.  The rules were wrong.  I was doing what I was meant to be doing and he knew it.  Now he's dead.  Killed by the Lannisters with your help.

That's your pretty handwriting.  Septa Mordane used to crack my knuckles because I couldn't write as well as you.  "Robb, I write to you today with heavy heart.  Our good King Robert is dead.  Killed from wounds he took in a boar hunt --

"Father has been charged with treason.  He conspired with Robert's brothers against my beloved Joffrey and tried to steal his throne.  The Lannisters are treating me well and providing me with every comfort.  I beg you, come to King's Landing, swear fealty to King Joffrey and prevent any strife between the great houses of Lannister and Stark.  Your faithful sister, Sansa."
Did they.  With a knife at your throat?  Did they put you on a rack and stretch you until your bones started to pop?
So was I.  I would have let them kill me before I betrayed my family.

And you were stupid enough to believe them.  I remember you standing on that platform with Joffrey and Cersei when they dragged father too the block.  I remember the pretty dress you were wearing.  I remember the fancy way you did your hair.
I was there standing in the crowd near Baelor's statue.
I wanted to.
I didn't betray him.  I didn't betray Robb.  I didn't betray our entire family for my beloved Joffrey.
I was training.
Oh, I don't know about that.  I can imagine quite a lot.
I guess we'll never know.
I don't know yet.
You're scared, aren't you?  What are you scared of?  You didn't commit any crimes.  No one's going to hang you.
Are you scared I'll show it to Jon and he'll be angry?  No.  That's not Jon.  He'll understand.  You were just a scared little girl all alone with the wicked Lannisters.

The northern lords will read it.  They wouldn't think much of Lady Sansa if they knew how she did Cersei's bidding.  What would little Lyanna Mormont say?  She's younger than you were when you wrote this.  Are you going to say, "But I was just a child"?
Sometimes fear makes them do unfortunate things.  I'll go with anger.
I came to kill Cersei. Your queen got there first.
Try telling Sansa.
Jon. She knows who you are. Who you really are. You'll always be a threat to her. And I know a killer when I see one.
Say another word about killing my brother and I'll cut your throat.
I can't.
(chuckles)  I'm not going back north.
What's west of Westeros?
No one knows. It's where all the maps stop. That's where I'm going.
Right here.
I don’t want to practice today.
They killed Jory. My father is hurt. I don’t care about stupid wooden swords.
Yes -
I don’t like dolls and k -
Are you dying because of your leg? Is that why you’re sending us home?
You can’t. I’ve got my lessons with Syrio. I’m finally getting good.
Can’t we take Syrio back with us?
Seven hells.
The lion’s not his sigil, idiot. He’s a stag, like his father.
Come on! (dragging Sansa)
Maidenpool, my lord.
House Mooton, my lord.
(nods.)
Barrowton, my lord. House Dustin. Two crossed longaxes beneath a black crown.
They call him the Young Wolf.
They say he rides into battle on the back of a giant direwolf. They say he can turn into a wolf himself when he wants. They say he can't be killed.
No, my lord. Anyone can be killed.
(gasps)
I was always a girl.
You're one of them now. I should have let you burn.
I didn't have a choice.
Three what?
I can name anyone and you'll kill him?
The one who tortures everyone.
I don't know his name. They call him the Tickler.
You should stand sideface.
Sideways.
Smaller target.
You're practicing for a fight. You should practice right.
Kill him!
No! ARYA: Let go of me! Let me go!
Burn in hell!
You can't let him go. He's a murderer. He's guilty.
You can't!
What are you doing?
Why?
Have you lost your mind? When the Lannisters find this place, you think they'll spare the smiths? They'll cave your head in with your own helmet.
You don't have to do this.
Robb needs good men, too. We're leaving tomorrow. And then you--
You just said you were serving Lord Beric.
I can be your family.
Joffrey. Cersei. Ilyn Payne. Ser Meryn. The Hound. What are you going to do with me?
I'm a hostage and you're selling me.
But it is that way.
More is than isn't.
So why don't you?
No.
I thought he killed you.
But how?
Could you bring back a man without a head? Not six times. Just once.
I would. You're alive.
Joffrey. Cersei. Walder Frey. Meryn Trant. Tywin Lannister. The Red Woman. Beric Dondarrion. Thoros of Myr. Ilyn Payne. The Mountain.
I can't sleep until I say the names.
Only the ones I'm going to kill.
If he were here right now, what would you do?
I'm almost done. Only one name left.
The Hound.
Practicing.
No one's going to kill me.
It's not fighting. It's water dancing.
The greatest swordsman who ever lived. Syrio Forel, the First Sword to the Sealord of Braavos.
What do you know about anything?
It was not.
Yes.
He was killed.
Meryn Trant. That's why Ser Meryn...
He was outnumbered.
Syrio didn't have a sword. Or armor. Just a stick.
Who was the first?
Where did they go?
A girl has no desires.
Who?
A girl will poison the rum. Lady Crane is the only one who drinks it.
If a girl could use one of the faces from the hall…
She’s a good actress.
Seems like a decent woman.
No. Who wants her dead?
The younger actress. She’s jealous because Lady Crane is better.
A girl has decided.
He didn't.  He trusted you to hold it for him.
These are mother and father's chambers.
Nothing.
What?
You always liked nice things.  It made you feel better than everyone.
They were insulting Jon and you sat there and listened.
Their opinions are important to you?
Not if they lose their heads first.
And if Jon doesn't come back, you'll need their support, so you can work together to give you what you really want.
You're thinking it right now.  You don't want to be, but the thought just won't go away.
My lady.
Come on. I'm not gonna hurt you.
I'm not a beggar. I live here.
I want to see my father.

My father is Hand of the King! I'm not a boy. I'm Arya Stark of Winterfell and if you lay a hand on me, my father will have both your heads on spikes! Now are you going to let me by or do I need to smack you on the ear to help with your hearing?
They said they were going to kill you.
I didn't see them, but I think one was fat.
I'm not lying! They said you found the bastard and the wolves are fighting the lions and the savage... something about the savage.
In the dungeons, near the dragon skulls.
Chasing a cat.
I'm a girl!
How many guards does my father have?
You wouldn't let anyone kill him, would you?
On your own?
Dragon fire.
Dead people.
Does anyone live?
Joffrey. Cersei. llyn Payne. The Hound. Joffrey. Cersei. llyn Payne. The Hound. Joffrey. Cersei. llyn Payne. The Hound.
The Mountain. What are you doing?
Joffrey. Cersei. llyn Payne. The Hound.
Polliver. The Mountain.
Safer to travel, my lord.
Can I take this hood off yet?

Text: In there.  I live here.

I'm getting into this castle one way or another.  If I'm not who I say I am, I won't last long.  But if I am, and Sansa finds out that you turned me away...
Do I have to call you Lady Stark now?
I didn't run.  You need better guards.
It suits you, Lady Stark.  Jon left you in charge?
That doesn't look like him.  It should have been carved by someone who knew his face.
We're not.
They say you killed Joffrey.  Did you?
Me too.  I was angry when I heard someone else had done it.  However long my list got he was always first.
Of people I'm going to kill.
It's a long story.  I imagine yours is too.
Mine neither, but our stories aren't over yet.
You saw me?
So did I.
Most of them are dead already.
Where did you get this?
Littlefinger, he's here?
Why would a cutthroat have a Valyrian steel dagger?
Are you sure?  It's Valyrian steel.
Don't fight someone like her in the first place.
He didn't beat the hound.  You did.  I want to train with you.
You swore to serve both of my mother's daughters, didn't you?
I won't cut you.  Don't worry.
No one.
I am celebrating.
Congratulations.
You'll be a wonderful lord, and any lady would be lucky to have you. But I'm not a lady. I never have been. That's not me.
We need a word.
I respect that.
We needed her. We needed her army, her dragons. You did the right thing. And we're doing the right thing telling you we don't trust your queen.
I'll never know her. She's not one of us.
That's all right. I don't need many allies.
We're family. The four of us. The last of the Starks.
You're my brother. Not my half-brother or my bastard brother. My brother.
What is it?
I swear it.
Me neither.
Don't like heroes.
Felt better than dying. You're heading to King's Landing?
Me too.
Neither do I.
Probably.
Syrio says a water dancer can stand on one toe for hours.
Syrio says every hurt is a lesson and every lesson makes you better. Tomorrow I'm going to be chasing cats.
He says every swordsman should study cats. They're quiet as shadows and as light as feathers. You have to be quick to catch them.
Now that Bran's awake will he come live with us?
He wants to be a knight of the King’s Guard. He can't be one now, can he?
Can I be Lord of a holdfast?
No. That's not me.
Why do they call you Littlefinger?
It's been awhile since I've trained.
I can't sleep.

Text: Why are you helping him? He takes us prisoner and now he's our friend?

So I can walk away, then?
You remember the last time you were here?
What are you doing? We're about to leave.  Hot PIE: I'm staying.
Winterfell.
I'm sure.  Hot PIE: Well, I made you something.

That's the tail?
Good-bye, Hot Pie.
Hey, Hot Pie. It's really good.
Gonna rain soon. Where are we?
You think? You don't have a map?
Maybe we should get one.
How far is it to the Eyrie?
And you're sure we're going the right way?
On your way where?
I'd like to see Braavos one day.
I have friends there.
We were just watering the horses. We'll be on our way. Forgive my father. He was wounded fighting in the war. Our cottage burned down while he was gone and my mother with it.
He's never been the same.
The Tullys of Riverrun.
Father!
I'm so sorry.
Really good.
What did you do?!
You told me you weren't a thief.
He took us in. He fed us and you...
You don't know that.
You're the worst shit in the Seven Kingdoms.
I've been sweeping this floor for days.
I didn't come here to sweep floors.
You said that I could be your apprentice. You said you'd teach me how to a Faceless Man.
I want to serve. (Jaqen stops).
Which one is the Many-Faced God? I see the Stranger. I see the Drowned God. I see the Weirwood Face.
Where are they taking him?
What do you want?
What?
(After a moment, perks up). No one.
Ow! Cunt!
You're about to find out.
(Stands up). I am ready.
What do we do with them after we wash them?
No one.
Arya Stark.
Her father was Eddard Stark. Her mother was Catelyn Stark. She had one sister, Sansa, and four brothers.
Three brothers.
Arya Stark left him to die. He was on her list.
He was not on her list anymore. She’d taken him off it.
She did and she did not.
Yes, she was.
Which name would you like a girl to speak?
A girl has no name.
No one.
Rob, Bran, Rickon. And a half-brother, Jon.
They may be dead for all a girl knows.
Also dead.
Cersei Lannister, Gregor Clegane, Walder Frey.
I'm practicing.
The Prince.
He's a liar and a coward and he killed my friend.
The Hound does whatever the Prince tells him to do.
You're a liar. And if you told the truth, Mycah would be alive.
Go away!
Mine.
I wasn't playing. And I don't want to be a lady.
It's called Needle.
Stick 'em with the pointy end.
I was trying to learn. I asked Mycah to practice with me. I asked him. It was my fault.
I hate them! I hate all of them. The Hound, the Queen and the King and Joffrey and Sansa.
So was I! He is a liar.
But how you can let her marry someone like that?
Winter is coming.
I don't hate her. Not really.
I can keep it?
Who are you?
It's too heavy.
What if I drop it?
I'm a girl.
A needle.
(Stops walking). What do you want?
(Walks toward the cave). I have friends.
You should have asked nicely.
They don't scare me.
What are gold cloaks doing this far from King's Landing?
They're looking for me.
If you got within a mile of a battle, you'd fill your pants.
Liar.
So?
(Gendry walks over to get more water). Gendry's an armorer's apprentice. Hot Pie, tell Gendry what makes it a battle.
What do the gold cloaks want with you?
You're a liar.
Then I wouldn't get to insult anyone. (Follows him).
No good's ever come? Who asked questions before?
Who asked questions?
Lord Stark.
What did they ask about?
Who's your mom?
And who was your father?
I'm not a girl!
Stupider. Night's Watch doesn't take girls, everyone knows that.
I am not!
I don't need to take a piss… Lommy and Hot Pie can’t know. No one can know.
My name's not Arry. It's Arya. Of House Stark. Yoren is taking me home to Winterfell.
He was never a traitor! Joffrey is a liar.
No. I mean yes. My mother was a lady, and my sister -
Do not call me milady.
Would you please shut up about it?
I'm not listening.
Shut up.
Jaqen got us out of Harrenhal, so why are you complaining?
Where are we going?
If we were going north, we should have come to the Red Fork River by now.
It's 100 feet wide. How could we have passed it? If we hit the Red Fork, we can follow it west to Riverrun. My mother grew up there. My grandfather he's a lord. He'll protect us.
Shut up.
Shh.
Don't.
You go on down the road. Just keep on singing so we know where you are. Leave us be and I won't kill you.
What friends?
Who are you?
No, who do you fight for?
I don't drink ale.
We're not children.
Gendry's a smith. He was apprenticed in the armory.
Gendry stole us weapons.
He knows how to use a sword and so do I.
My brothers taught me.
You'll free us?
He's just a statue.

A girl has no name.
A girl has no name.
Hello, Hot Pie.  Sit down.  Who's that for?
Mmm.  This is good.
I didn't do that.
One or two.
Big lady?
She found me.
You got any ale?
King's Landing.
I heard Cersei's queen now.
Hmm.
Cersei would do that.
Why would I go there?  The Boltons have it.
What?
You're lying.
Thanks for the pie.
Thanks.
Take care of yourself, Hot Pie.  Try not to get killed.
Nymeria?
Nymeria, it's me, Arya.  I'm heading north, girl, back to Winterfell.  I'm finally going home.  Come with me.
Come with me.
That's not you.
You make my weapon yet?

Go!

No.

You're not very smart, are you?
You're fine with murdering little boys, but thieving is beneath you?
You think I'm gonna escape? Where would I go? I'd be dead by nightfall without you. My family's gone. I've got no one.
I'm hungry. You're hungry.
I know him. The small one. His name is Polliver. He captured us and took us to Harrenhal.
He killed Lommy.
He was my friend. Polliver stole my sword and put it right through his neck. He's still got it.
My sword Needle.
Lots of people name their swords.
My brother gave me that sword.
He killed my friend.
Something wrong with your leg, boy?
Can you walk? I've got to carry you?
Fine little blade.
Maybe I'll pick my teeth with it.
Thank you.
I can’t see.
When people ask you what happened here, tell them the North remembers. Tell them winter came for House Frey.
That's a pretty song. I've never heard it before.
I don't want to steal your food.
King's Landing.
Not so bad, is it?
What are you doing in the Riverlands?
Oh, no, I couldn't. You don't have enough.
You're a long way from home. I'm sure you've had some adventures, though.
Boy or girl?
Why?
It's really good.
I'm going to kill the queen.
How did you survive a knife through the heart?
Needle.
Once or twice.
Valyrian steel.
Too heavy for me.
She doesn't like your queen, does she?
She's the smartest person I've ever met.
I'm defending our family. So is she.
Don't forget that.
First I robbed you.
That was a nice ax you made for him. You've gotten better.
Thanks. So do you.
Stay close to that forge, then.
Don't call me that.
Here's my wish.
Can you make it?
Can you make it or not?
You don't know any other rich girls.
You used to be taller.
Leave him be.
Move!
You're not supposed to be here. No one is supposed to be here. It's a mercy. He's dead already.
Your Grace. The trial will be getting underway soon.
I’m very sorry, Your Grace. Very sorry.
Sire, I served you well.
Selyse, you're my sister!
Tell him! Tell him!
(fusses)
(fusses)
(fusses)
'Balon Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands and invader of the North. I give you until the full moon to order all ironborn scum out of the North and back to those shit-stained rocks you call a home.
'Leave the North now or more boxes will follow with more Theon. Signed Ramsay Snow, natural-born son of Roose Bolton, Lord of the Dreadfort and Warden of the North.' Get that out of my sight.
Theon disobeyed my orders.
The boy is a fool. He cannot further the Greyjoy line. I will not give up the lands I have seized, the strongholds I have taken. Get this away from me.
Son? He's not a man anymore.
Watch yourself. I've made my decision.
The plans are made. It's time you heard them.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: The wolf pup has gone south with the entirety of the Northern army at his back. While he's tangling with the lion in the Westerlands, the North is ripe for the taking. The ironborn will reave and pillage, as it was in the old days, all along the northern coast. We'll spread our dominion across the green lands, securing the Neck and everything above. Every stronghold will yield to us, one by one. Winterfell may defy us for a year, but what of it? The rest shall be ours, forest, field, and hall. Yara, my daughter, you'll take 30 longships to attack Deepwood Motte.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You'll take a ship to raid the fishing villages on the Stoney Shore.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Your brothers were warriors, both of them dead at the hands of those you seem so eager to protect.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: What are our words? Our words?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: We do not sow. We are ironborn. We're not subjects. We're not slaves. We do not plow the fields or toil in the mine. We take what is ours. Your time with the wolves has made you weak.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Nine years, is it? They took a frightened boy. What have they given back?
We shall see. Stark had you longer than I did.
And how do you feel about that?
Who gave you those clothes? (Stands up).  Was it Ned Stark's pleasure to make you his daughter?

It will. (Begins walking forward). That bauble around your neck. Did you pay the iron price for it or the gold? (Theon looks down). I asked a question. Did you pull it from the neck of a corpse you made or did you buy it to match your fine clothes? Iron or gold?
I'll not have my son dressed as a whore. (Balon walks towards the fireplace and looks into it). My fears have come true. The Starks have made you theirs.
Yet, (Turns around), the Stark boy sends you to me like a trained raven, clutching his message.
He heeds your counsel?
No, not here, (Walks forward), not in my hearing, you will not name him brother. This son of the man who put your true brothers to the sword. Or have you forgotten your own blood?
I see. I destroy Robb Stark's enemies for him and he will make me King of the Iron Islands once again.
Oh, you will?
(Puts his arm around her). My dear.
And why not?
This isn't Winterfell, boy. Your sister took over command of your eldest brother's ship after your new father killed him.
(Watching the letter burn in his hand). No man gives me a crown. I pay the iron price. I will take my crown. (Throws the letter in the fire). That is who I am. That is who we have always been.
Who said anything about the Lannisters?
And the Ironborn who held it?
What is dead may never die.
They we will take more.
Because I order it.
Not if our captains defy my orders, abandon their posts, and sacrifice our men on foolish mission.
Then where is he?

And I lost three sons. The War of the Five Kings, they call it. Well, the other four are dead. When you rule the Iron Islands, you can wage all the peace you wan. But for now, shut your mouth and obey or I will make another heir who will.
Let me pass. You fool, move aside for your king.
I thought you’d be rotting under some foreign sea by now.
You can mock our god without my help.
I heard you lost your mind during the storm on the Jade Sea. They tied you to the mast to keep you from jumping overboard.
And when the storm passed, you cut out their tongues.
What kind of an Ironborn loses his senses during a storm?
Lord Tyrell! On behalf of the Iron Bank, may I be the first to welcome you to the free city of Braavos. I hope your journey was trouble free.
And how goes the harvest in the Reach?
I’m afraid I don’t partake.
I'm glad we see eye-to-eye on this matter.
Most unfortunate for the glovers.
We are not gamblers here at the Iron Bank, Lord Tyrell.
I’m afraid I still have a good deal of work to do.
I don't have that gift.
They will come, Your Grace. When they're ready.
The masters will take advantage of this situation. The men serving them will be slaves in all but name.
We're not gonna sneak an army through a back gate.
Ser Jorah.  You can use an extra sword.
Men who fight for gold have neither honor nor loyalty. They cannot be trusted.
2,000, Your Grace. Armored and mounted.
(nods)
Under 2,000, Your Grace.
10,000 Unsullied.
Gladly, Your Grace.
Your Grace, I am yours to command.
Your Grace, the Kingsguard is a sworn brotherhood.
Your Grace...
The man who profaned his blade with the blood of the King he had sworn to defend!
A hall to die in and men to bury me.
Even now I could cut through the five of you like carving a cake!
The Yunkish train bed slaves, not soldiers. We can defeat them.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: The Yunkish are a proud people. They will not bend.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: He was reeling from the wine.
His squire, from the king's own skin.
King Robert's seal. Unbroken.
Those were the King's words.
I don’t believe so, your Grace.
It was, your Grace.
Mm, you were first through the breach at Pyke?
Thoros of Myr. Bloody madman. Robert knighted you after the battle?
(laughs)
Robert would have laughed. He was a good man, a great warrior. And a terrible king. I burned away my years fighting for terrible kings.

Yes. And a man of honor keeps his vows, even if he's serving a drunk or a lunatic. Just once in my life before it's over, I want to know what it's like to serve with pride, to fight for someone I believe in. Do you believe in her?
Of course he wanted her dead. She's a Targaryen. The last Targaryen.
I didn't sit on the small council.
Traditionally, yes, but I killed a dozen of Robert's friends during his rebellion.
He didn't want advice on how to govern from a man who had fought for the Mad King. Can't say I minded much. I always hated the politics.
Mm-hmm.
She'll have good men around her to advise her, men with experience.
Forgive me, Ser Jorah, for what I'm about to say, but your reputation in Westeros has suffered over the years.
I don't know if your presence by her side will help our cause when we go home.
We both want her to rule. Am I wrong?
If we're truly her loyal servants, we will do whatever needs to be done, no matter the cost, no matter our pride.
And we have taken the Meereenese Navy, Your Grace.
93, Your Grace.
9,300, not counting sailors.

They've been fighting Joffrey's wars for years. They're tired, dispersed. And now their king is dead. 8,000 Unsullied, 2,000 Second Sons sailing into Blackwater Bay and storming the gates without warning.
The old houses will flock to our queen when she crosses the Narrow Sea.
No. I stood vigil for him myself last night. He had no one else.
Bad luck for him... Going against the Mountain.
All the knights draw straws, Lord Stark.
Life is strange. Not so many years ago we fought as enemies at the Trident.
You're too modest. I've seen you cut down a dozen great knights.
He was a fine man, your father. What the Mad King did to him was a terrible crime.
Perhaps Lord Arryn left him some money? I hear the King wants to joust today.
Robert tends to do what he wants.
I was thinking of all the times your brother made me go with him down from the Red Keep, into the streets of King's Landing.
He liked to walk among the people, he liked to sing to them.
Yes. Rhaegar would take a spot on the Hook or the Street of Seeds and then he'd sing. Just like all the other minstrels.
I made sure no one killed him. And I collected the money. (Daenerys looks surprised). Well he liked to see how much he could make.
He was very good. Viserys never told you?
Rhaegar never liked killing. He loved singing.
Well, one time, he gave it to the next minstrel down the street. One time, he gave it to an orphanage in Flea Bottom. One time... we got horribly drunk. (They both laugh).
Your Grace. (Leaves).
Your Grace, may I have a word? The city is yours. All these people,they're your subjects now. Sometimes it is better to answer injustice with mercy.
The Walk of Punishment is a warning, Your Grace.
To any slave who contemplates doing whatever these slaves did.
Leave this place, Your Grace. Leave tonight, I beg you.
We can find sellswords in Pentos and Myr.
Three.
(Shakes his head.)

When your brother Rhaegar led his army into battle at the Trident, men died for him because they believed in him, because they loved him, not because they'd been bought at a slaver's auction. I fought beside the last dragon on that day, Your Grace. I bled beside him.
I did, Your Grace. Finest man I ever met.
You will win the throne with dragons, not slaves, Your Grace.
Aegon Targaryen proved that.
Of course, your Grace.
A Tyroshi. Never learned the name.
Lance through the heart.
I was there that day. You were only a squire, 16 years old.
A good fighter, Toyne, but he lacked stamina.
I believe he means to...
Ignore him, Your Grace. These are meaningless words.
Your Grace, I've won more single combats than any man alive.
I think you should exercise restraint, Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: For one thing, he may have valuable information.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: How do you know that?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: We do not know what this man did or didn't do. (to Daenerys) Give him a trial, at least. A fair trial. Show all of the citizens of Meereen that you are better than those who would depose. Teach them a better way.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Your Grace, a word, please, I beg you.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: About your father. About the Mad King.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Your Grace, I served in his Kingsguard. I was at his side from the first.  Your enemies did not lie.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: When the people rose in revolt against him, your father set their towns and castles aflame. He murdered sons in front of their fathers. He burned men alive with Wildfire, and laughed as they screamed. And his efforts to stamp out dissent led to rebellion that killed every Targaryen except two.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: No, Your Grace. Thank the gods. But the Mad King gave his enemies the justice he thought they deserved. And each time it made him feel powerful and right. Until the very end.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I'll stand guard outside your door tonight, Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Your Grace.
Gambling, Your Grace.
163, Your Grace. I'll tell our men to ride ahead and bury them. You don't need to see this.
Yes, your grace. They left it on the body.
It was only a matter of time, your grace. Conquerors are always met with resistance.
The Sons of the Harpy will hear that message.
The honor is mine, my queen.
King Robert is dead. I have been searching for you, Daenerys Stormborn, to ask your forgiveness. I was sworn to protect your family. I failed them.
Yes, him.
We want you to leave Slaver’s Bay. Take your dragons and your mercenaries and go.
This is where I leave you.
The Wall is not just ice and stone. Ancient spells were carved into its foundations. Strong magic to protect men from what lies beyond. And while it stands, the dead cannot pass. I cannot pass.
The great war is coming and I still fight for the living. I’ll do what I can… as long as I can.
I wish you both good fortune.

I led a ranging party deep into the North to find White Walkers. They found us. A white walker stabbed me in the gut with a sword of ice. Left me there to die. To turn. The Children found me. Stopped the walker’s magic from taking hold.
The same way they made the Walkers in the first place. You saw it yourself.
You are the Three-Eyed Raven now.
You must learn to control it before the Night King comes.
Drink.
One way or another, he will find his way to the world of men. When he does, you will be there waiting for him. And you will be ready.
I wanted to be here when you saw it for the first time. I'm leaving this morning.
I'm the First Ranger. My job is out there. There have been disturbing reports.
The kind I don't want to believe.
You're not going. You're no ranger, Jon.
Better than no one! Here... A man gets what he earns, when he earns it. We'll speak when I return.
The Night's Watch is a joke to you, is it? Is that what we are, Lannister? An army of jesters in black?

I hope we've provided you with some good stories to tell when you're back in King's Landing. But something to think about while you're drinking your wine down there, enjoying your brothels... Half the boys you've seen training will die north of the Wall. Might be a wilding's axe that gets them, might be sickness, might just be the cold. They die in pain. And they do it so plump little lords like you can enjoy their summer afternoons in peace and comfort.
Call me what you like.
You know, my brother once told me that nothing someone says before the word "but" really counts.

You're right. The wildlings are no different from us. A little rougher maybe. But they're made of meat and bone. I know how to track them and I know how to kill them. It's not the wildlings giving me sleepless nights. You've never been north of the Wall, so don't tell me what's out there.
Enjoy the capital, brother.
Sit. You'll be fed. Untie them.
Welcome.
Is he dead yet?
You got bigger. I rode all day. Didn’t want to leave you alone with the Lannisters. Why aren’t you at the feast?
Well, you’re always welcome on the wall. No bastard was ever refused a seat there.
Jon…
The Wall isn’t going anywhere.
You don’t understand what you’d be giving up. We have no families. None of us will ever father sons.
You might, if you knew what it meant. … I’d better get inside. Rescue your father from his guests. We’ll talk later.
Of course I did. Just a lad. But he was tough, Ned. A true Ranger.
The two he was with are still missing.
Maybe. Direwolves south of the wall. Talk of the Walkers. My brother might be the next Hand to the king. Winter is coming.
Robb boy. How are ye?
Clegane.
They’re our men. Or they were. They attacked a nearby sept and murdered the villagers. Why do you want them?
It’s the Brotherhood’s good name they’ve dragged through the dirt.
You can have one of them.
No.
We gave you two of the three out of respect for your loss. That’s generous.
You ought to join us. We could use you.
Cold winds are rising in the North.
We need good men to help us.
True enough. But the Lord of Light gave you the power to defeat me. Why?

Aye, you’re probably right. You’re a fighter. You were born a fighter. You walked away from the fight. How did that go? Good and bad, young and old, the things we’re fighting will destroy them all alike. You can still help a lot more than you’ve harmed Clegane, it’s not too late for you.
Come sit by the fire, child.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I didn't like giving up the boy.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: The Red God is the one true god. You've seen his power. When he commands, we obey.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: No? Who's yours?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: How many?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: How far?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: (nods)
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: To take you home, and I will. But we need to do this first.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I swear to you, this isn't--
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: One day you'll understand, but now--
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Anguy, bring her back.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: It's a long way down.
Forgive my manners. I don't see many ladies these days.
The other side? There is no other side. I have been to the darkness, my lady. He sent you to us for a reason.
Forgive me, lad.
We serve the Lord of Light and the Lord of Light needs this boy.
We're fighting a great war.  Wars cost money.
You don't much look like him.
Your father.  I suppose you favor your mother.

Of course I did.  When he was Hand he sent me off hunting for The Mountain.  Your Wildling friend told me the red woman brought you back.  Thoros has brought me back six times.  We both serve the same lord.
The north didn't raise you from the dead.
He wants you alive.
I don't know.

I think about that all the time.  I don't think it's our purpose to understand except one thing.  We're soldiers.  We have to know what we're fighting for.  I'm not fighting so some man or woman I barely know can sit on a throne made of swords.
Life.  Death is the enemy.  The first enemy and the last.

The enemy always wins.  And we still need to fight him.  That's all I know.  You and I wont find much joy while we're here.  But we can keep others alive.  We can defend those who can't defend themselves.
Maybe we don't need to understand any more than that.  Maybe that's enough.
As you command.
He will. But not today.
Not in the eyes of god.
Enough! The judgment isn't ours to make.
Go in peace, Sandor Clegane. The Lord of Light isn't done with you yet.
Do I frighten you, child?
You're angry with me. And I don't blame you. But letting him go was the right thing. I have more reason than most to want him hanged.
He did.
Thoros how many times have you brought me BACK?THOROS: It's the Lord of Light brings you back. I'm just the lucky drunk who says the words.
How many times?
Six.
Then I was stabbed in the belly.
Then the Lannisters caught me and executed me for treason. Was it a hanging or a dagger in the eye?
Second time I've been killed by a Clegane.
I know. Every time I come back, I'm a bit less. Pieces of you get chipped away.
He was a good man, Ned Stark. He's at rest now, somewhere. I would never wish my life upon him.
We don't want to go beyond the Wall, we have to.  Our lord told us the great war is coming.
Here we all are at the edge of the world at the same moment heading in the same direction for the same reason.
It doesn't matter what we think our reasons are.
There's a greater purpose at work and we serve it together whether we know it or not.  We may take the steps but the Lord of Light --
No. Fighting in a war makes you a soldier.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: And I won't see them again.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Last I heard, you were King Joffrey's guard dog. But here you are 1000 miles from home. Which of us is running?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Ned Stark ordered me to execute your brother in King Robert's name.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Aye. I've been reborn in the light of the one true god. As have we all. As would any man who's seen the things we've seen.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You don't deny killing this boy?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You stand accused of murder. But no one here knows the truth of the charge, so it is not for us to judge you. Only the Lord of Light may do that now. I sentence you to trial by combat.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Aye. She might be. But it's me you'll fight.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Fighting for the Night King now.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: My lady. It's good to see you again. I'm sorry we parted the way we did.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: That's all right.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: The Lord of Light has brought us together all the same. This is his moment. When light-
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: This seems like a good place to spend the night.
Seems deserted to me. No livestock. No smoke coming from the chimney.
How do you think it ended for them?
Girl died in her father's arms. Both of them covered in blood and a knife at their feet. I'd say they were starving. And rather than letting his little girl suffer, he ended it for both of them.
No, doesn't matter now.
Aye. I think the first time we met was at that tournament--
Thank you, Clegane. That warms the heart.
You're right about that.
You think I don't ask myself that? Every hour of every day? Why am I here? What am I supposed to do? What does the Lord see in me?
I don't know. I don't understand our Lord.
I don't know what He wants from me. I only know that He wants me alive.
Aye. Almost seems like divine justice.
Do you believe me now, Clegane? Do you believe we're here for a reason?
Did you find anyone?
It's a message. From the Night King.
Lady Crane, they loved you.
Save my father.
Save him, please.
Father! No! Oh, father.
I had two lines.
I rang very false?
You weren’t complaining about me last night.
Hello.
You've got no money.
You like her? They all like her. They all want to fuck a queen.
You're just saying that.
Why?
(Smiles). Alright, then. I'm warning you. We’re gonna have to wash you first.
Come on.
Of course you can. You're shy.
Have another drink.
Aye.
Thought he'd never shut up.
The giant must have took him.
Gone, along with the wildling woman. The one you were fucking.
Greyjoy!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: We caught this one on his way back from Torrhen's Square. Took out two of ours before I got his sword.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Where are we headed, Captain?
And who decides if we've done our jobs well?

I have been reaving and raping since before you left Balon's balls, Captain Don't reckon I've got much use for your ideas on how to do it. Don't reckon I've got much use for a captain at all. I'm thinking I can do the job of captain real well myself. All I need is a ship. You wouldn't know where I could find myself a ship now, would you?
(laughs)
The Night’s Watch! Heading North!
Gendry! His name is Gendry.
The Night's Watch! Please, stop!
They took him up the Kingsroad, heading north!
He's got a bull's head helmet. He made it himself.
I’m magic. (They laugh).
Hey, Great Khaleesi. Move your ass.
Maybe she saw a ghost. My friend’s mother saw a ghost and her hair turned white.
You think she’s got white pussy hair too? You ever been with a girl with white pussy hair?
She doesn’t have to be smart to get fucked in the ass.
I’ll ask Khal Moro for a night with you. What do you think?
Pink people are afraid of the sun. It burns their skin. This one stands too long in the sun and her hair goes white.
Only when I was fucking your grandma.
Pretty eyes, but she’s an idiot.
I like to talk when I’m finished. Otherwise, we might as well be dogs.
For you, my Khal. The white-haired girl we found in the hills.
Look at those lips, blood of my blood.
Killing another Khal?
Breaking a wild horse, forcing it to submit to your will.
Conquering a city and taking her people as slaves, and taking her idols back to Vaes Dothrak.
Drink long and deep, my noble king, from this cup of sweet wine.
The last drink he shall ever take. And vengeance shall be mine.
What is that I hear and smell? Someone I’ll soon send to hell.
It’s time to see if truth they told who said Tywin Lannister shits gold.
My greatest crime. Now I must flee.
Fear me!
Then sail across the Narrow Sea to do unto you more treachery.
Don’t fear the winter.
He brought you here, Ned Stark, ‘cause you’re the brightest fellow in the land. You should be king of us all and I should be your Hand.

Worry not! All is well. I have here a decree from my father Tywin Lannister, the richest man you’ll see. He proclaims me Hand of the King. The position’s mine for life. And he’s give me permission to take Sansa as my wife.
You’ll learn that what I lack in height I make up for in appetite.
So let’s forget about your plight, and go rehearse our wedding night.
What you need, love, is for someone to ring you true.
May I get you some wine, Lady Crane?
Of course we’re going to be intimate.
May they have your talent.
Our scouts tells us he’s got no more than 6000 men. More than half of them are mounted, however.
I don't know--
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: He's not in the dungeons.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Last cage on the right.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Where is Lady Bolton?
Liar.
I can’t wait to see what parts Ramsay cuts off you this time.
It’s a bloody woman.
A girl's mouth around-
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: You shouldn’t be alive. It’s not right.
Ser Barristan.
Crow!
Crow!
Crow!
Crow!
Oh! ( laughter )
In the old times, whenever Braavos stood in danger, the titan would step with fire in his eyes. He'd wade into the sea and smash the enemies.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Osha, what are you doing?
Tell us what medicine to get from your chambers.
They burned it down. They burned everything.
I don't want to leave you.
Don't, Hodor.
Maybe we shouldn't stay.
There are a lot of stories about this place. Horrible stories.
So did I. Once.
You ever heard about the Rat Cook?

Just a cook in the Night's Watch. He was angry at the king for something, I don't remember. When the king was visiting the Nightfort, the cook killed the king's son, cooked him into a big pie with onions, carrots, mushrooms and bacon. That night he served the pie to the king. He liked the taste of his son so much, he asked for a second slice. The gods turned the cook into a giant white rat who could only eat his own young. He's been roaming the Nightfort ever since, devouring his own babies. But no matter what he does, he's always hungry.
It wasn't for murder the gods cursed the Rat Cook or for serving the king's son in a pie. He killed a guest beneath his roof. That's something the gods can't forgive.
Hodor.
My brother, he's in the Night's--
No, I'm not.
Take us north of the Wall.
I don't want to. I have to.
Please, Sam. I have to go north. I have to.
What is it?
Why? What are they for?
Take it, Hodor.
I wish I could. I really do.
Jojen. Jojen! Look, Jojen, look.
Help them!
Who are you?
You’re the three-eyed raven.
I didn’t want anyone to die for me.
You’re going to help me walk again?
Thank you, Uncle Benjen.
I’m the Three-Eyed Raven now. I have to be ready for this.
Help them! Now!
lt had three eyes. lt told me to come with him, so l did. We went down into the crypts and my father was there.
You're afraid. Just like Hodor.
You've lived beyond the Wall. What are you scared of? l'm a crippled boy and l'm willing to go.

That's my grandfather, Lord Rickard. He was burned alive by the Mad King, Aerys. That's Lyanna, my father's sister. King Robert was supposed to marry her, but Rhaegar Targaryen kidnapped her. Robert started a war to win her back. He killed Rhaegar, but she died anyway. That's where l saw Father.
Rickon!
What are you doing down here? Come back up with us.
How many times have l told you? He's in King's Landing with Sansa and Arya.
Saw him when?
The Gift, I think.  Brandon The Builder gave all this land south of the Wall to the Night’s Watch for their sustenance and support.  Maester Luwin taught me that.
Wildlings.
Sorry.  But they come over the Wall and raid, steal, carry off women.
I don’t see any.
How are we going to get past the Wall?  My uncle said it’s 700 feet high.
How did you get past it?
It would take us two months to get to the Bay of Seals.
Even Hodor’s not strong enough to climb the Wall with me on his back.
My uncle said the gates were sealed with ice and stone when the castles were deserted.
Hunting.
[To Hodor] Hush, Hodor.  No, no more Hodoring.
Hodor, calm down.
Hodor, be quiet.
Hodor, calm down.  Hodor, be quiet!
Nothing.  I don’t know.
I can’t do it by choice.  I don’t know how.  It happens in my dreams.
I can’t.
[Looks at JOJEN] You were right.  I can get inside Summer’s mind whenever I want.
They can’t do that north of the Wall?
[Looks at RICKON]  When I was looking through Summer’s eyes, I saw Jon.
He was with the wildlings.  They tried to kill him but he got away.
For all we know, Castle Black’s already under attack.  If this many wildlings got through-
Everything Jojen told me is true.  You saw what I did to Hodor.  I have to find the three-eyed raven.
Don’t worry, I’m not asking you to come with me.  It won’t be safe for Rickon.
No.  You and Osha and Shaggydog head for the Last Hearth.  The Umbers are our bannermen.  They’ll protect you.
Right now, I have to protect you.
Robb’s at war and I’m going beyond the Wall.  If something happens to us, you’re the heir to Winterfell.
[Looks at OSHA]  Will you know how to find the last Hearth?
You don’t have to do any of this.
You’re leaving now?  It’s the middle of the night.
What is it? What's happened?
Where are you going?
But it's the middle of the night.
They have more men than we do.
Can't I come with you? I can ride now. You've seen me riding.
I will.
How long have you been hiding out there?
They'll be back soon.
Please watch over Robb.
What are you doing here?
You're not a slave.
Well, your friend did put a knife to my throat.
What did you mean about hearing the Gods?
It's only the wind.
Go back and find your clothes, Hodor.
Are there really giants beyond the Wall?
We couldn't risk going to the kitchens.
And if Theon found out, he'd hang them.
Wait, I know that farm. I sent those two Winterfell orphans there.
We can't risk it. If Theon tracks us here, he'll torture them until he finds out where we are.
They'll be after us with hounds.
It's all right, Osha.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: The raven's been coming to me ever since I fell from that tower. He wants me to find him. I don't have my legs anymore. This is what I have now.

You said, "I did warn you not the trust me."
Come in.
Samwell Tarly.
I remember everything.
You helped us get beyond the Wall. You're a good man.
I became the Three-Eyed Raven.
I can see things that happened in the past. I can see things happening now all over the world. Why did you come to Winterfell?
He's on his way back to Winterfell with Daenerys Targaryen. You you saw this  in a vision?
He needs to know the truth.
About himself. No one knows. No one but me. Jon isn't really my father's son He's the son of Rhaegar Targaryen and my aunt.
Dornish bastards are named Sand.
Are you certain?
Robert's Rebellion was built on a lie. Rhaegar didn't kidnap my aunt.
He loved her. And she loved him.
He's never been a bastard.
He needs to know. We need to tell him.
Theon?

Text: Why?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New
