Text: You're both very good at skinning rabbits.
Stop it!
You've been fighting since you met. Just stop it.
You've been nasty to her every day. Of course she's nasty back.
First time I met you, you held a knife at me. We can't fight each other. We'll never make it to the Wall. I want you both to make peace.
Osha!
He's having one now?
You saw him? At Castle Black?
Meera. They found us.
Who are you?
Uncle Benjen. The last letter Jon wrote me said you had been lost beyond the Wall.
How?
Dragonglass. A shard of dragonglass plunged into your heart.
I didn’t have time to learn. I can’t control anything.
Why do you think I came all this way?
Lord Tyrion you will be my Hand.
And I don't want to be king.
I choose you.
Yes, I can. I'm king.
He just got it. He's made many terrible mistakes. He's going to spend the rest of his life fixing them.
You were exactly where you were supposed to be.
We appear to be missing a Master of Whisperers. And a Master of Laws. And a Master of War.
And Drogon? Any word?
Perhaps I can find him. Do carry on with the rest.
(smiling) I'm sure it will.
Come on, Dancer. (to his horse)
Robb?
I’m not alone, my brother is with me.
I can’t. The saddle, the straps.
I’m Brandon Stark of Winterfell and if you don’t let me be, I’ll have you all killed!
Robb!
No, don’t.
Yes, it doesn’t hurt.
We can send two orphan boys from Winterstown home with you to help watch over your flock if you can give them room and board.
(to RICKON) Stop it. If that's everyone, I'm going to go for a ride before dark.
Hodor.
We have to help them.
If we can't protect our own bannermen, why should they protect us? Go, Ser Rodrik. Take the men you need.
So, what does it mean?
I did ask him. He'd never heard of a three-eyed raven.
You're lying.
You know what it means.
You didn't give me an honest answer.
Well, it's not far off.

I dreamt that the sea came to Winterfell. I saw waves crashing against the gates and the water came flowing over the walls It flooded the castle. Drowned men were floating here, in the yard. Ser Rodrik was one of them.
I know. It's just a stupid dream.
Osha. The three-eyed raven, what do they say about it north of the Wall?
They already have stopped me.
You've seen it, too.
We will.
Stop.
Stop. Stop!
Stop! Please.
Please, get off her!
Please! Please, leave her alone!
Please, stop. Stop.
What are you doing? Leave her alone!
No!
Is Jon with you?
Hodor. Hodor, get his knife. Cut me loose.
Hodor, get his knife. Hodor. Cut me loose, Hodor.
Jojen and Meera, free them. Go!
Jon.
Jon!
He's my brother.
Hodor.
We need to free Summer. And we need to go.
It was you. You made the White Walkers.
From whom?
What?
He saw me, the Night King! He saw me!
I don’t know. He was close, but --
But he can’t get in.
I’m sorry. I didn’t mean to.
The time for what?
But am I ready?
Ravens.  We need to send ravens.
The Greyjoys.
I don't know them.
"Unbowed, unbent, unbroken".
"Righteous in wrath".
"Family, duty, honor".
"Family, duty, honor"... Is that the right order?
Family comes first?
How can she protect the family, if she's not with her family?
And then she left!
Why did she leave?
Do you know where she is now? Today?
Then how can you promise me she'll be home soon?
I'll never shoot another arrow.
You need legs to work a bow.
Really?
How?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Mother.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Mother, I need to find it. It's here. It's calling me.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Mother! Mother!
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Do you hear that?
I'm going out there.
I'm going.
Summer, he's hurt. They've caught him in a trap.
I didn't see, but they have my brother's wolf. They have Ghost.
They're Night's Watch. Look. Jon might be here.
No.
I'm not leaving without Summer.
The east side of the keep.
I'm Brandon Stark! I'm Brandon Stark of Winterfell.
Do you know who this belonged to?
Chaos is a ladder.
I'm not Lord Stark.
Maester Wolkan built it for me so I can move around more easily.
You're leaving?
No, I don't.
Thank you.
For helping me.
I'm not really, not anymore.  I remember what it felt like to be Brandon Stark, but I remember so much else now.
You came home.
I saw you at the crossroads.
I see quite a lot now.
I thought you might go to King's Landing.
Cersei is on her list of names.
Littlefinger gave it to me.
He thought I'd want it.
Because it was meant to kill me.
Someone very wealthy wanted me dead.
It doesn't matter.
I don't want it.
It's wasted on a cripple.
It's the same as the one Daeron Targaryen built for his crippled nephew 120 years ago. I liked that one.
I'm not Lord of Winterfell.
I don't really want anymore.
You shouldn't envy me. Mostly I live in the past.
It's your choice.
I don't want to see anyone.
I don't want to go.
Kneel, Hodor.
Yes. Well, I mean I did like to.
I'm not a cripple.
Will I really be able to ride?

Every night it's the same. I'm walking and running, but I'm not I'm not me. I'm running through the godswood, sniffing the dirt, tasting blood in my mouth when I've made a fresh kill, howling. Old Nan used to tell me stories about magical people who could live inside stags, birds, wolves.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: So she was lying? They don't exist?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: No, my dreams are different. Mine are true. I dreamt of my father dying. And Rickon had the same dream.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: That’s my father.
Ser Arthur Dayne.
Father said he was the best swordsman he ever saw.
He’s better than my father.
But Father beat him.
I know he did. Heard the story a thousand times.
He stabbed him in the back.
What’s in the tower?
I want to see where he’s going.
Father!
Why did you do that? Take me back there. I want to go back.
He heard me.
He heard me.
What’s in that tower? I want to go back there.
Why do I want to return? So I can be a cripple again? So I can talk to an old man in a tree?
So why did you?
I don’t want to be you.
Learn what?
Hello, Sansa.
Yes.  I need to speak to him.
I can never be the Lord of Winterfell.  I can never be lord of anything.  I'm the three-eyed raven.
It's difficult to explain.

It means I can see everything.  Everything that's ever happened to everyone.  Everything that's happening right now.  It's all pieces, fragments.  I need to learn to see better.  When the long night comes again I need to be ready.
The three-eyed raven taught me.
I told you it's difficult to explain.

I'm sorry for all that's happened to you.  I'm sorry it had to happen here at our home.  It was so beautiful that night.  Snow falling just like now.  And you were so beautiful in your white wedding dress.
I'll stay a bit longer.
I hate your stories.
Those weren't my favorites. My favorites were the scary ones.
Maybe we do.
I did though. It's true, isn't it... what Maester Luwin says about my legs? I'd rather be dead.
I'd rather be dead.
Father?
Why not?
It's all right, Hodor.
No, it was the three-eyed raven.
I tried to kill it, but I couldn't. There was a boy.
But you asked.
I didn't ask for black magic dreams.
We don't know who might be after us. No one even knows we're alive.
No.
Where's Rickon and Hodor?
What's out there?
It's all right. The wolves will protect him.
Only when I'm asleep.
It's not just wolves. Sometimes in my dreams there's a-
You've seen it?
Does it have anything to do with warging?
Seeing things that haven't happened yet?
When my father died, I dreamt it.
You have the sight, too?
Your father is Howland Reed? Yeah.
He saved my father's life during the rebellion.
What else have you seen?
Why did you wake me?
I was hungry.
I was just eating.
Hodor, take me to the tree.
I know where we have to go.
They were all so happy.
My Aunt Lyanna. I’ve seen her statue in the crypt. My father never talked about her.
Hodor?
Hodor talks?
Please, a little longer.
You finally show me something I care about and then you drag me away.
I wasn’t drowning. I was home.
Wylis.
I saw you as a boy. You could talk. What happened?
Where’s Meera?
You shouldn’t be here. It’s not safe outside the cave.
I saw that Hodor wasn’t always Hodor.
What’s wrong?
The Three-Eyed Raven says there’s a war coming.
The things we do for love.

Text: You weren't sorry then. You were protecting your family.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: You still would be, if you hadn't pushed me out of that window. And I would still be Brandon Stark.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: No. I'm something else now.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: He'll come for me. He's tried before, many times, with many Three-Eyed Ravens.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: An endless night. He wants to erase this world, and I am its memory.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: His mark is on me.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: He always knows where I am.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: No. We need to lure him into the open before his army destroys us all. I'll wait for him in the Godswood.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I don't know. No one's ever tried.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: No.

Text: That’s my father. And my Uncle Benjen.
Jon said he was a deserter.
Our way is the old way?
Is it true he saw the White Walkers?
So he was lying?
Where will they go? Their mother’s dead.
NO!
Please, father!
I saw the king! He’s got hundreds of people!
But he’s coming right now! Down our road!
What?
Come on, you.
Ten
King Robb. And it's not his war. He didn't choose it.
Joffrey killed my father, your liege lord. Do you remember your vows, Ser?
I didn't like the way he was talking about Robb.
Don't have any pain.
That way.
I don't dream.
I don't. Heard some of the men talking about the comet. (Bran looks up at the comet). They say it's an omen. They say it means Robb will win a great victory in the South.
The dragons are all dead. They've been dead for centuries.
You were at the Fist of the First Men. You were at Hardhome. You've seen the Army of the Dead. You've seen the Night King. He's coming for us. For all of us.
Almost.
We don't have time for all this. The Night King has your dragon.
He's one of them now. The Wall has fallen, the dead march south.
Waiting, for an old friend. It's time to tell Jon the truth.
I'm not his brother. He trusts you more than anyone. Now's the time.
I promise.
What about you?
Where are you going, huh? Aren’t we better looking than the fat man?
Even in White Harbor, I’d want a kiss.
One little kiss.
A man gets lonely guarding the Wall.
There’s no need to be rude.
Sam the Slayer. Are you gonna slay me with that sword?
Alright. Hold still.
You’re gonna get yourself killed.
Walk.
One or two tried.
Not interested.
I don't serve the Starks. I serve Lady Catelyn.
Burying them.
I think these women would understand if I don't care what you think.
Traveling a prisoner.
If you've quite finished up
All right, we'll be going.
The Starks.
No, stealing is a crime.
To Riverrun.
Steal from the Tullys, it's their dungeons you rot in.
I don't give the orders. He must be important to someone.
I hope you gave them quick deaths.
If this is the Kingslayer, I think I'd know about it.
He's not the Kingslayer. Sorry to disappoint you. If he was, I'd only be traveling his head.
Two quick deaths.
I don't serve the Starks. I serve Lady Catelyn. I told you I'd take you to King's Landing, and that's what I'm going to do. Stay.
Podrick. Podrick! Where are the horses?
What sort of hobble?
If you did it like I taught you then the horses would be here.
It’s at least thirty miles to the Eyrie from here. You’re carrying the saddle bags.
Morning!
I like your sword. Are we getting close to the bloody gate?
Did you hear that Podrick? Only about ten more miles to the Bloody Gate.
No.
I do.
“Oathkeeper.”
Good name.
My father.
Mine said the same. But I kept fighting the boys anyway. Kept losing. Finally my father said, “If you’re going to do it, you might as well do it right.”
Seven blessings. I’m Brienne of Tarth, this is Podrick Payne.
You’re Arya Stark.
I swore to your mother I would bring you home to her.
I know. I wish I could’ve been there to protect her.
I know, but I swore a sacred vow to protect her.
She commanded me to bring Jaime Lannister back to King’s Landing.
I’m not paid by the Lannisters.
Jaime Lannister gave me this sword.
I swore to your mother by the Old Gods and the New -
Arya!
She is.
Come with me Arya. I’ll take you to safety.
And that’s what you’re doing? Watching over her?
I have no wish to kill you Ser.
Arya! Arya! ARYA! Where is she?
Why weren’t you watching her?
Which way Pod? Which way did she go?
Arya!
How do you know it’s Stannis?
I do not fight for the Boltons. I’m Brienne of Tarth. I was Kingsguard to Renly Baratheon. I was there when he was murdered by a shadow with your face. You murdered him? With blood magic?

In the name of Renly of House Baratheon, First of his Name, Rightful King of the Andals and the First Men, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms, and Protector of the Realm, I, Brienne of Tarth, sentence you to die. Do you have any last words?
Brienne of Tarth.
All my life men like you have sneered at me. And all my life I've been knocking men like you into the dust.
You have a keen military mind, Pod.
My name is Brienne of Tarth. Please inform Ser Jaime Lannister I’ve come to speak with him. Tell him I have his sword.
Why would you assume that?
I don’t think you know many girls like her.
I’ve come for the Blackfish.
Lady Sansa desires to take her ancestral seat back from the Boltons and assume her rightful position as Lady of Winterfell.
The Tully army.
The Tullys are rebels because they’re fighting for their home?
As a reward for betraying Robb Stark and slaughtering his family.
You’re a knight, Ser Jaime. I know there is honor in you. I’ve seen it myself.
I do no such thing. Take Riverrun without bloodshed. Ride south with your mission complete and your army intact.
Allow me to enter Riverrun under a flag of truce. Let me try to persuade the Blackfish to give up the castle.
Because you’ll allow him to lead the Tully forces safely north.
No.
I need your word. If I persuade him to abandon the castle, you’ll grant us safe passage north.
You gave it to me for a purpose. I’ve achieved that purpose.
one last thing, Ser Jaime.
Should I fail to persuade the Blackfish to surrender, and if you attack the castle, honor compels me to fight for Sansa’s kin.
To fight you.
I have a signed letter from your niece Sansa Stark.
He always spoke highly of you.
Riverrun cannot stand against the Lannisters and the Freys.
He’s not my friend.

Ser Jaime kept his word to your niece Catelyn Stark. He sent me to find Sansa, to help her as Catelyn wanted. He gave me this sword to protect her. That is what I have done and I will continue to do until the day I die.
You have more than she does.
Tell her I failed.
Come with us.
Your family is in the North. Come with us. Don’t die for pride when you can fight for your blood.
Find the maester. We need to get a raven north to Sansa.
Your crimes are past forgiveness, Kingslayer.
You've harmed others, those you were sworn to protect, the weak, the innocent.
You will not provoke me to anger.
I've never seen you fight.
Do you take me for an idiot? In!
Maybe one day we'll find out, Kingslayer.
No farther.
Don't enter without an invitation, man.
Threatening my lady is an act of treason.
Your son returns at dawn, my lady?
The Kingslayer won't last the night. The more they drink, the angrier they'll get. And when the Karstarks draw their swords Who wants to die defending a Lannister?
I thought you were gone.

Text: Have they told you what they plan to do with me?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Ah!
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Bit of comfort never hurt anyone. We've been sleeping in ditches the last few weeks. I think we can treat ourselves to a featherbed for the night and a hot meal not cooked by you.
Just don't start expecting silk underclothes. Not working for your former lord any longer.
Don't get drunk.
No, this will do nicely, thank you. Kidney pie is wonderful.
No.
We're looking for someone.
A girl, tall, red hair, very pretty. Her name is Sansa Stark, but she may be using a different name.
I pledged my life to their mother Catelyn Stark. I swore to her I'd bring her daughters home.
What?
You wouldn't be screwing your face up like that if it was nothing.
You're not interesting enough to be offensive.
What about? Not kidney pie.
No one's seen Arya Stark since her father was beheaded. She's presumed dead.
When was that?
So what happened to her?
The quick version.
Um, you were saying?
Her mother's dead, grandfather's dead, Walder Frey is the new lord of Riverrun. The Brotherhood isn't going to Riverrun.
Why the Eyrie?
Sansa could be there, too.
You sure about this?
I thought you were dead.
I was only trying to protect her.
She's alive. Arya.
Winterfell.
The only one that needs protecting is the one that gets in her way.
Ser Jaime.
We both saw what just happened. We both saw that thing.
Oh, fuck loyalty!
This goes beyond houses and honor and oaths. Talk to the queen.

Yes. Most kind of them. You're a Stark bannerman, Lord Bolton. I am acting on Lady Stark's orders to return Jaime Lannister to King's Landing.  Roose BOLTON: When King Robb left Harrenhal, his mother was his prisoner. If she wasn't his mother, he'd have hanged her for treason.
I am charged with bringing Ser Jaime to--
Aye.
Ser Podrick.
Long may he reign.
I think we can all agree that ships take precedence over brothels.
No! No.
I won't leave him.
Not that way.
It looked like Stannis.
In the shape of Stannis.
Will you stay there long, my lady?
I never knew my mother.
It's a bloody business.
Once you're safely back amongst your own people, will you give me leave to go, my lady?
I swore a vow.
I only held him that once as he was dying.

I do not know your son, my lady. But I could serve you, if you would have me. You have courage. Not battle courage, perhaps, but, I don't know, a woman's kind of courage. And I think that when the time comes, you will not hold me back. Promise me that you will not hold me back from Stannis.
Then I am yours, my lady. I will shield your back and give my life for yours, if it comes to that. I swear it by the Old Gods and the new.
Thank you, my lord.  Roose BOLTON: Find suitable rooms for our guests. We'll speak later.
What are you doing here?
There's another tub.
Why should I care how you die?
Don't you mock me.
You need trust to have a truce.
Of course.
If this is true why didn't you tell anyone? Why didn't you tell Lord Stark?
Help! Help! The Kingslayer!
Didn't they teach you how to ride a horse?
Perhaps you should have stayed with him. It's not going to be a pleasant journey for you. It could take weeks to get to the Wall, depending on the weather.
Well, Lady Sansa's brother's at Castle Black. If I were her, that's where I'd go. Feel free to stop at any point.
I've made it this far in the world without a squire. I don't see why I need one now.
I'm not a knight. And I'm not a slaver, either. I don't own you.
I am releasing you from that oath. That means you could leave.
What do you think will happen if you leave?
Did you remove the skin?
Have you ever cooked a rabbit before?
Did you ever cook anything for Lord Tyrion?
What are you doing?
I've been removing my own armor for quite some time, thank you very much.
What exactly did you do for Lord Tyrion?
Oh. Whilst in Lord Tyrion's service, did you ever do anything remotely related to combat?
Who?
How did you kill a Kingsguard?
Bloody ridiculous. Help me with these straps.
(Turns around). Better off with the Boltons who murdered her mother and brother? Sansa's in danger even if she doesn't realize it.
Thank you. You've lived here a long time?
Did you know Lord Eddard?
Not all of them. I know who's inside that castle.
I'm not talking about the Boltons. I need to get a message to her. To Sansa Stark.
Someone who swore to keep her safe.
Her mother.
That doesn't release me from an oath. I served Lady Catelyn. I serve her still. Who do you serve?
Lady Sansa asked you a question.
I’ve sworn to protect you, my lady.
We can send the Blackfish a raven.
I don’t like leaving you here alone.

Not him. He seems trustworthy. A bit brooding, perhaps. I suppose that’s understandable, considering. The others, though. Davos and the Red Woman helped a man murder his own brother with blood magic. And when Stannis paid for his crime, where were they? Already out looking for a leader with better prospects. And that wildling fellow with the beard --
Then why did you lie to him when he asked you how you learned about Riverrun?
He's going to fall. He's going to fall off his horse. Someone help him.

Text: "Ser Jaime Lannister. Knighted and named to the Kingsguard in his 16th year. At the sack of King's Landing, murdered his king Aerys II. Pardoned by Robert Baratheon. Thereafter known as the Kingslayer."
Valyrian steel.
I can't accept this.
I'll find her. For Lady Catelyn. And for you.
I don't need a squire.
He'll slow me down.
Oathkeeper.
I saw what happened. I saw Stannis’ forces defeated in the field.
We’ve met before. I was Kingsguard to Renly Baratheon. Before Renly was assassinated with bloodmagic.
Yes, it’s in the past. It doesn’t mean I forget. Or forgive. He admitted it, you know.
Stannis. Just before I executed him.
I did next to nothing.
I'm not a --
Thank you, Podrick.
Don't lunge.
Don't go where your enemy leads you.
Up.
And don't --
Nice sword.  Very nice dagger.
I can go and find the Master of Arms for you, My Lady.
Move aside, Podrick.
You can't use that, My Lady.  It's too small.
I'll try not to.
Who taught you to do that?
I told you I was.
I did.
Why does he get to go again?
Wrong! Wrong, wrong, wrong! Drink!
You're drinking wine, but you prefer ale!
I have to piss.
Please pardon me for a moment.
I didn't drink?
I drank.
This is not the game. This is only drinking.
It's the first thing I learnt when I came to the North. Keep your fire going. Every time you leave the room, put more wood on.
Piss off.
It grows on you.
(almost whispering) You sound quite jealous.
Oh, move aside.
What are you doing?
I've never slept with anyone before.
I told you--
Euron Greyjoy ambushed Queen Daenerys and her fleet. One of the dragons was killed, several ships destroyed, Missandei captured.
They're going to destroy that city. You know they will.
You're not like your sister. You're not. You're better than she is. You're a good man and you can't save her. You don't need to die with her. Stay here. Stay with me. (voice breaking) Please. Stay.
You were married. Before Sansa.
Your Grace, I ask the honor of a place in your Kingsguard.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I will be one of your seven, pledge my life to yours, and keep you safe from all harm.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: And you should kneel when you approach the King.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: At once, Your Grace. Shall I return after?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: If you'll follow me, my lady.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I fought for my king. Soon I'll fight for him on the battlefield. Die for him if I must. And, if it please you, Brienne's enough. I'm no lady.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You were armed when we were taken.
All my life I've been hearing, 'Jaime Lannister, what a brilliant swordsman.' You were slower than I expected. And more predictable.
And I'm a woman. I was still beating you.
Maybe you were as good as people said once. Or maybe people just love to overpraise a famous name.
Would I?
You think I care about my teeth?
What does it matter?
If you were a woman, you wouldn't resist? You'd let them do what they wanted?
My lord, I am Brienne of Tarth. Lady Catelyn Stark commanded me to deliver Ser Jaime to King's Landing.
(screams)
We don't. We go around. (She turns her horse and begins leaving).
Doesn't matter. I know where they're going.
Aren't you getting a bit old to be a squire? How did you end up squiring for the Imp?
Well, he's not here to complain about it, is he?
He borrowed it?
As punishment. For both of you.
(Walks away to get firewood). Yes, all of your lords were very kind to you. All except me. Sorry you have to squire such a nasty person.
I'm sorry I'm always snapping at you.
You want to be a knight, Pod?
Starting tomorrow, we'll train with the sword twice a day, before we ride in the morning and after we camp for the evening. (Sits down). And I'm going to show you how to ride properly.
I can't knight you, but I can teach you how to fight.
I was.
He was.

(After a few moments). When I was a girl, my father held a ball. I'm his only living child, so he wants to make a good match for me. He invited dozens of young lords to Tarth. I didn't want to go, but he dragged me to the ballroom. (Pauses). And it was wonderful. None of the boys noticed how mulish and tall I was. The shoved each other, and threatened to duel if they thought it was their turn to dance. And whispered in my ear how they wanted to marry me and take me back to their castles. (Smiles). My father smiled at me and I smiled at him. I'd never been so happy. (Her smile drops). Till I saw a few of the boys sniggering. And then they all started to laugh, they couldn't keep the game going any longer. They were toying with me. ‘Brienne the Beauty’, they called me. Great joke. And I realized I was the ugliest girl alive. A great lumbering beast. I tried to run away, but Renly Baratheon took me in his arms. “Don't let them see your tears,” he told me. “They’re nasty little shits. The nasty little shits aren't worth crying over.” He danced with me and none of the other boys could say a word. And he was the king's brother after all.

Yes, Pod, he liked men, I'm not an idiot. He didn't love me, he didn't want me, he danced with me because he was kind and he wouldn't see me hurt. He saved me from being a joke. From that day until his last day. And I couldn't save him in return. Nothing's more hateful than failing to protect the one you love. One day, I will avenge King Renly.
A shadow with the face of Stannis Baratheon. I know it was Stannis. I know it in my heart. Stannis is a man, not a shadow. And a man can be killed.
Yes.
By putting one foot in front of the other.
I'm here to take you to King's Landing and bring back Lady Stark's daughters in exchange. Dull is fine.
If Lady Stark is unhappy with any aspect of my service, I'm sure she'll let me know. She's an honest woman.
Not your concern, Kingslayer.
How would you know?
Move.
Gods, no.
Shut your mouth.
I knew him as well as anyone. As a member of his Kingsguard, he trusted me with everything. He would have been a wonderful king.
I did not fancy him.
I'm not interested in foul rumors.
Shut your mouth!
South. You?
And you.
He doesn't.
We're not doing it. He's an innocent man.
Silence, Kingslayer.
Good luck.
Be quick about it.
Get up.
Get up now.
I'm no lady, Your Grace.
Apologies, Your Grace. I never did master the curtsy.
I just wanted to congratulate you both and wish you good fortune. The country has been at war too long. I hope your reign is long and peaceful.
As you say, Your Grace.
In truth, he rescued me, Your Grace. More than once.
Not such a fascinating story, I'm afraid.
I don't serve your brother, Your Grace.
Your Grace.
What is it?

Text: My lady. My lady. Please pardon me for interrupting. My name is Brienne of Tarth.
My lady, I know this is a very busy time for you, but if I could just have a moment.
A shadow with the face of Stannis Baratheon. I swear to you by all the gods it was Stannis. He plunged his sword through Renly's heart and disappeared. One day, my lady, I will avenge our king.
I meant no offense.
There she is.
And? You made a promise.
To keep them safe.
A complication does not release you from a vow.
Look me in the eye and tell me that you think she'll be safe in King's Landing.

Us? The only reason you’re here is that Jaime Lannister told me you weren’t safe in the capital. You’re hundreds of miles from King’s Landing. No one knows what you look like. No one cares. You’re safe.
Do you even know what a squire is?
I’m not a knight. That means you’re not a squire.
I don’t care, I’m not your mother.
I found Arya. She didn’t want my protection.

Will you shut your mouth? I didn’t ask for advice. I don’t want anyone following me. I’m not a leader. All I ever wanted is to fight for a Lord I believed in. The good lords are dead and the rest are monsters.
Lady Sansa, I offer my services once again. I will shield your back and keep your council and give my life for yours if need be. I swear it by the old gods and the new.
Nope.
Nope.
Why is he still here?
He wants something.
Maybe I’ll come visit you sometime.
Don’t wait too long. I’ve a noble woman to marry back home.
You don’t even have to do anything, do you?
You just sit there, a rich slab of beef, and all the birds come pecking.
She doesn’t want me. She wants your golden fingers up her twat.

Not blonde enough?
Maybe I’m not in the mood.
Fuck it.

l took her.
From, uh, Ser What's-his-name? l don't know. Ginger cunt, three tents down.
He said something.
Oh, well. l, uh, think l'll go and find myself one.
Drink.
There's a Braavosi knife game l could teach you.
Not if you win.
This sounds like a boring game.
But my mother hit harder.
lt was a woman. (Tyrion drinks. Shae looks at Bronn, surprised.) She swung an axe at me.
Work.
The whole shit-stained world could drink off that one.
Our lord here used to be married.
You hear lots of things playing dice with Lannister soldiers.
l'm guessing the lady and l can tell more unpleasant stories than your lordship.
For three chickens, l should hope so.
Well, l imagine that was the end of all that.
l would have killed the man who did that to me.
You're sleeping through the war.
They stole a night's march on us. They're a mile north.
You don't have a squire.
Stay low.
lf you're lucky, no one will notice you.
You're a shit warrior.
You're alive.
We wouldn't be having this conversation if we didn't.
Yeah, good.
To the boys!
Drunk Lannisters.
Thank you very much.
Well, now let's see. First time, I was five. My mam smacked me with an iron poker.
It wasn't me she was aiming at. She was trying for my little brother. Now he was a real pest.
Second time, I was nine. Got in a scrap with a few older boys. They won. Third time
Eh, you don't want to know about the third time.
Don't feel sorry for him. He'll be halfway up your arse before the night's through.
Welcome, friends. This round's on me.
(whispering) I don't think he likes me.
I know it.
It's warm in here. We've got beautiful women and good brown ale. Plenty for everyone. And all you want is to put one of us in the cold ground with no women to keep us company.
And quicker. Eh?
Aye I expect he will someday.
One more drink before the war? Shall we?
The ships are in the bay.
I know what "in" means. Do you know how to use that?
I saw you kill a man with a shield. You'll be unstoppable with an ax.
Don't get killed.
Oh, are we friends now?
Enhances it, really.
Been spending time with fancy folks.
Am I going to be happy at the end of this walk?
The most beautiful woman in the world.
Only the one.
Prince. Sorry about the other day.
I’m glad to hear it. The pie looks good.
Will you shut up? There's hill tribes all around here.
I should just take your food and leave you here.
You don't think I'd do it, do you?
Fair enough.
Tyrion. Tyrion. Tyrion.
You wouldn't know him.
I like to keep me hands clean.
I don't want to wear a gold cloak.
A cloak slows you down in a fight. Makes it hard to move quietly. And the gold catches the light, so you're nice and easy to spot at night.
We had a deal and wearing a gold cloak wasn't part of it.
What?
(scoffs) You don't want me cleaning me nails. You don't want me looking your way. Why am I here?
And one of these explains how to beat him?
Chevalteesh. I'd swap all your books for a few good archers.
Me and the lads rounded up all the known thieves.
No. It's just the unknown thieves we need to worry about now.

Aye, we talked about it. Have you ever been in a city under siege? Maybe this part's not in your books. See, it's not the fighting that kills most people. It's the starving. Food's worth more than gold. Noble ladies sell their diamonds for a sack of potatoes. Things get bad enough, the poor start eating each other.
The thieves, they love a siege. Soon as the gates are sealed, they steal all the food. By the time it's all over, they're the richest men in town.
(nods)
We could throw books at his men.
We don't have that many men, either.
Getting a bit old to be a squire, aren’t we?
Podrick fucking Payne. I thought you’d be dead by now.
Are they in there?
You think they’re fucking?
Why not? I’d fuck her. You’d fuck her, wouldn’t you?

Oh. Well, he’d fuck her, that’s for sure. And she’s fuck him, don’t you think? The way she looks at him. The way all women look at him is frankly irritating. I preferred working with the little brother on that account. Come on, you’re the one with the magic cock.
You must have shown it to her by now.
Is she? Oh.
Then how come an old cunt like me can still sneak up and murder you?
Now, that’s the truth, isn’t it? You want to learn that sort of fighting?
All right, let’s start with your footwork.
Show me your stance.
Now, listen to me, Pod. See how your feet are about a yard apart?
Lesson number one, assume everyone wants to hit you. ‘Cause they do, Pod. Everyone wants to hit a fucking squire. Do it again. Come on, don’t sulk.
She's a foot taller than you.

Text: My lord.
Do you like 'em? Eh? Gloves are doeskin. Softer than a virgin's thighs.
I've been a bit busy.
My lonesome bachelor days are over. I'm to wed Lollys Stokeworth.
I wouldn't say I had a single sort of girl.
If I wanted wits, I'd marry you.
Falyse. Aye, I did know about the older sister.
Falyse is 40 and barren.
She does. Unless she happens to perish before her father. Then Lollys gets the castle.
What? Ladies fall from their horses and snap their pretty necks all the time.
You once said if anyone ever asked me to sell you out, you'd double their price.
One of each will do. But if you want me to kill the Mountain for you, it'd better be a damn big castle.
I have gold. What can I buy with gratitude?
Your sister's a Lannister, too.

If and may and could. It's bloody cold up north. Lollys is soft and warm. And close. If I gave you the choice between fucking Lollys and fighting the Mountain, you'd have your britches down and your cock out before I could blink.

I'd be a bloody fool if he didn't frighten me. He's freakish big and freakish strong. And quicker than you'd expect for a man of that size. Maybe I could take him, dance around until he's so tired of hacking at me, he dropped his sword, get him off his feet somehow. But one misstep... ...and I'm dead. Why should I risk it?
Aye, I'm your friend. And when have you ever risked your life for me?
I like you, pampered little shit that you are. I just like myself more.
I'm sorry it has to be this way.
We had some good days together.
What will you do?
I hope to hear them sing it one day.
It’s against my code to hurt a woman.
I wouldn’t say you beat me.
Wonderful. Wouldn’t feel right to leave Dorne without a new scar.
No great hurry. Dornish women are the most beautiful women in the world.
I said Dornish women. I didn’t say you.
I’ve seen quite a few women in all the Seven Kingdoms.
Well now, in King’s Landing there was an absolutely gorgeous...
Was I?
Was there? My memory’s not what it was earlier.
You seem concerned with it. You must really like me.
My head… You don’t even wanna know what’s going on in…
It’s nothing. It’s the dry air.
Now, that is a sorry attempt at a siege. Someone needs to teach those sad twats how to dig trenches.
Oh, no. Not me. I’m just an upjumped sellsword.
Aye, knights don’t get paid.
That’s like saying I have a bigger cock than anyone in the Unsullied army.
You promised me a lordship and a castle and a highborn beauty for a wife.
Don’t say it. Don’t fucking say it.
Good thing we’re friends or we’d be fucking you in the ass right now.
Have your lads start digging perimeter trenches. Set pickets every hundred yards. And work double-time on those siege towers and trebuchets.
A parley or a fight?
You’ve got one hand. My money’s on the old boy.
Oil?
How many barrels?
Get 500 more.
I still enjoy it when they call me "my lord."
If we live that long. Men without cocks. You wouldn't find me fighting in an army if I had no cock. What's left to fight for?
I spent my life around soldiers. What do you think they spend that gold on?
Not without a cock, you don't.
Yet, your brother--
I think we're about to be the downtrodden.
Welcome, my lords. Your friends arrived before you did.
I've been sent to escort you all to the meeting.
Come on! You can suck his magic cock later.
I'm sure your new queen--
Don't you worry about me. I'm doing all right. Looking after myself.

I put yourself at risk. Important difference. It's your head Queen Cersei's offered a bag of gold for, it's not mine. Now, thanks to me, she's got two traitors' heads coming right through her door. She can lop them both off as soon as she gets tired of the clever words that pour out their pieholes. All thanks to Ser Bronn of the fucking Blackwater. If that's not looking after myself, I don't know what is.
Yeah, you, too.
Come on, Pod. Let's you and me go have a drink while the fancy folks talk, eh?
You.

Brothers oh brothers, my days here are done, the Dornishman’s taken my life. Bur what does it matter, for all men must die, and I’ve tasted the Dornishman’s wife! Yes I’ve tasted the Dornishman’s wife!
I’ll stand for the dwarf.
No. He did.
I’m coming to the best part.
This song really is all about the ending.
Once we got the princess, then what?
That explains the golden hand.
Well, she’s made herself at home.
Why don’t you let them have some time alone, son?
Let’s not do something stupid.
That was something stupid.
Oh for fuck’s sake.
Your Grace.
The farther away, the better.
Long may he reign.
In full, my lord Hand.
Indeed we can.
The Master of Coin looks forward to helping the Master of Ships, but first he has to ensure we're not wasting coin, or soon there won't be no more coin.
You Master of Grammar now too?
The strong live and the weak don't.
Oh, speaking of builders, all the best brothels burned down. The Master of Coin is willing to fund reconstruction.
Well, I imagine he isn't using them properly.
I think that's a very presumptuous statement.

The Dornishman’s wife was as fair as the sun and her kisses were warmer than spring. The Dornishman’s blade it was made of black steel and its kiss was a terrible thing. The Dornishman’s wife would sing as she bathed in a voice that was sweet as a peach...
You fight pretty good for a little girl.
Jon! Jon!
Your first? You need a woman. Nothing like a woman after a fight.
Give me 10 good men and some climbing spikes... I'll impregnate the bitch.
It will be my pleasure.
There's that mind of yours you keep going on about.
Good thing. I'd be out of a job. What about your father?
Not after what he did to your birthday present.
He's talking about you.
People think you're pulling the king's strings. They blame you for the city's ills.
You don't need to convince me.
(scoffs)
If I could tell you how many crazy old men I've seen pushing carts around army camps making grand claims about jars full of pig shit. No offense meant.
To do what?
If you could get real soldiers to man the catapults, then maybe you'd hit your target one time in ten, but all the real soldiers are in the Riverlands with your father.

I don't know if you've ever seen a battle, old man, but things can get a bit messy. 'Cause when we're flinging things at Stannis, he's flinging them right back at us. Men die, men shit themselves, men run, which means pots falling, which means fire inside the walls, which means the poor cunts trying to defend the city end up burning it down.
Well, he's not living anymore. And all his pots of wildfire didn't help him, did they? Men win wars, not magic tricks.
This is a shit idea.
What the fuck were you doing back there?
You saw the dragon between you and her?
And?
Listen to me, cunt.  Till I get what I'm owed, a dragon doesn't get to kill you.  You don't get to kill you.  Only I get to kill you.
You're fucked.
No, I do not.  Dragons are where our partnership ends.  I'm not going to be around when those things start spitting fire on King's Landing.
Best well jump back in that river.
All the more reason for you to train.  Unless you plan on fighting Dothraki 12 year olds.
And here I am ready to train.
I don't think you want people to see how you look swinging that thing yet.
Oh, you won't be swinging it at me.
I'll leave you to it.
You can have my room.
Careful, now. We don't want to get blood all over your pretty white cloak.
The little king's backed up. Clogged from balls to brains.
There's no cure for being a cunt. But the boy's at that age. And he's got nothing to do all day but pick wings off flies. Couldn't hurt to get some of the poison out.
That was me knocking your ass to the dirt with your own hand.
You learned to fight like a good little boy. I'll bet that thrust through the MadKing's back was pretty as a picture. You want to fight pretty or you want to win?
All the time. He got used to it.
No. Oh, he hated the little twat, sure. But who didn't? And poison's not his style. Or murder, for that matter. You want to know for sure, why don't you ask him?
You haven't been to see him yet, eh?
Your brother ever tell you how I came into his service?

Aye. But only when Lady Arryn demanded the trial take place that day. You were his first choice. He named you for his champion because he knew you would ride day and night to come fight for him. You gonna fight for him now?
Compliments of Lord Tyrion. His axe from the Blackwater.
What are you waiting for, a kiss? Ready the lady's horse.
Why are we on a merchant ship? Why no Lannister sails?
You ever been to Dorne?
I have. The Dornish are crazy. All they want to do is fight and fuck. Fuck and fight.

(Gets up, walking to his knife). There's nothing like a good fight to get you in the mood for fucking. (Pulls out his knife from the sandbag). And there's nothing like a fuck-mad Dornish girl to clear your head for the next fight. It's how the two work together. And we're gonna do a lot of fighting, I am sure. Well, I am. But I don't imagine we'll get to stick around for the rest. Not after we've kidnapped their princess.
(Incredulously) Your niece?
I've been doing this sort of thing a long time. I'm good at it. (Sits down).
I know. Why are you here? Why not send 40 of me, or an army? Unlike most folks, you actually got one.
That still doesn't explain why you're doing it.
(Gets up starts walking). If I'm putting something of a lit together, a one-handed man, (Grabs some rope and walks back), who happens to be one of the most recognizable faces in Westeros, is not -
(Sheaths his sword). You set your brother free, didn't you? I bet your sister didn't like that.
Well, if you ever see the little fucker, (Lays down), give him my regards.
Breakfast.
That would have been a shit way to die.
Aye. But your lot like to give the singers a good ending.
No? Two knights, off to rescue a princess. Sounds like a good song to me.
In my own keep, drinking my own wine, watching my sons grovel for my fortune.
I've had an exciting life. I want my death to be boring. How do you want to go?
She want the same thing?
The captain to that ship… What was he, Braavosi?
And he's headed to Oldtown?
What's to stop him from docking down the coast and telling the locals that Jaime Lannister's in Dorne?
I'm not sure you understand how much people hate your family in this part of the world.
And I bet he swore all kinds of oaths to get it. But you won't be around if he breaks them.
How many you count?
How many you think you can take?
Fucked in the arse. (Both get up). Hiya! (The riders approach them). Morning, lads. Glad we found you.
Cooper. (Points to Jaime). This is Darnell.
Accent gave me away? Flea Bottom. Whelped and whipped.
Our ship capsized in the night. We managed to swim ashore. It was a near thing, really.
Dolphins maybe.
Boys, there's no need for this. Just, point us in the right direction. We'll find our way home.
That one should be slow enough.
Nice move! (Jaime turns to him).
You had a wonderful teacher.
Always wanted a Dornish stallion. Beasts can run a day and night without tiring. We'll ride to the Water Gardens with a nice breeze in our face.
Birds have to eat too.
Do you know how long it will take us to dig all those holes?
You just won the biggest prize in the world.  What could you possibly have to be upset about?  Come on.  You can tell me.  Queen of Thorns give you one last prick in the balls before saying goodbye?
There is no more High Septon.
There is still the question of my prize.
It's not a castle.
How about that one?  It's available.
I beg to differ.
Oh, is that why you're so fucking glum, eh?  All your new riches weighing you down?
Right.  Just not to me.
Yes, I'm sure Queen Cersei's reign will be quiet and peaceful.
Like what?
I'm not much for shoveling weight.
Come on.  Your father's not here.
Men shit themselves when they die.  Didn't they teach you that at fancy lad school?
Well, I learned it when I was 5.
Listen.
In line now!
Get back to King's Landing.
You're a commander, not a damned infantryman.  These fuckers are about to swamp us.
Go get it then.
Where are you?
Come on you fucker.
I knew you were fucking her. A pair of tall, blond toffs. Must be like looking in the mirror.
Oh, this? This is for you. For both of you.
You boys are a pair of gold-plated cunts. Do you know that?
Thanks to me. And that title's worth as much as a blond hair from your brother's ballsack.
Shut your mouth.
I've never hit a dwarf before, but say another word and I will belt you.
You couldn't do it on your best day, you one-handed fuck. And your best days are long gone.
I did not break your nose.

Because I've been breaking noses since I was your size, and I know what it sounds like. Now listen. Your sister offered me Riverrun. Nice big castle, good lands, plenty of peasants who do what they're told.

I knew your sister was dead the second I saw those dragons. Now, your army may be torn to shit, but I'd still bet on your Dragon Queen to win. And it just so happens I'm a betting man. If Cersei's dead, she can't pay up.
Mmm, that's good. Of course, the odds change if the Dragon Queen's Hand turns up dead. Maybe a few of her top generals get picked off one by one. All of a sudden -
Why not? Only death will shut you up.
If anyone offered me money to kill you, you'd pay me double. What's double Riverrun?
The way I see it, I only need one of the Lannister brothers alive.

No? Who were your ancestors, the ones who made your family rich? Fancy lads in silk? They were fucking cutthroats. That's how all the great houses started, isn't it? With a hard bastard who was good at killing people. Kill a few hundred people, they make you a lord. Kill a few thousand, they make you king. And then all your cocksucking grandsons can ruin the family with their cocksucking ways.
Highgarden. Give me your word.
Oh, aye. And he has company. Filthy old stoat. Almost hate to interrupt.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: No, I don't.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Come on, I'm here later. Come on.
Tonight, then. Looks like dull reading.
It's an opinion I share with some of the finest men I know.
Unless Lord Twatbeard made up a bunch of numbers to hide what he's really up to.
She's not bad with second-timers, either.
Pace yourself, lad.
Let me guess. He's not a magician.
He's stealing it?
What's wrong with that?
Seeing as it's his grandson's ass on the throne, I imagine he'll forgive that debt.
I've never borrowed money before. I'm not clear on the rules.
And what if I don't?
But what if I don't?
The lad's practically skipping.
He's a squire. You don't pay him.
Maybe they're trying to curry some favor with the new Master of Coin.
What did you tell them?
Of course they seemed to like it. They're paid to seem to like it.
What are you saying? These ladies enjoyed him so much, they gave him the time for free?
No. No, no, no. No, please. Don't! You can't do this to me!
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: The lads will escort you. The streets aren't safe at night, my lord.
Boys?
Without question? No. I'd ask how much.
He tells me you shit gold just like your father.
There's this knight, Leygood, got thunderbolts on his shield.
Right here is where I fuck his wife. She's a screamer, that one. If they don't hear her, they won't hear us.
I've never seen Valyrian steel before. She's a beauty. The problem is, if you fight with an edged blade, I'll have to.
And if I fight with an edged blade, I'll have no one left to pay me.
Best time to attack a man.
Mind yourself. JAIME;  If I still had my right hand...
Plan on growing it back?
All taken care of.
Aye, she's on it.
No one knows she's there but you, me, and Varys.
Because if someone follows me without an invitation, I'm the last person they ever follow.
She's gone. I know you don't want to believe it, but she is. Now, go drink till it feels like you did the right thing.
Don't who?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: They certainly do, my dear. They certainly do.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: It's a fine place, isn't it? A fine place. I never thought I'd end up settling down in a place like this.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Pardon?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Oh.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: You know what I think? I think you're a good person. And your sister is a mean person.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I've been all over the world. And if there's one thing I've learned, it's that meanness comes around. People like your sister, they always get what's coming to them eventually. One way or another.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Jaime fucking Lannister.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Ser Jaime.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I was very sorry to hear about your father.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Run along, love.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Just get on with it.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: What you're here for. There's no way this little visit could possibly mean anything good for me.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Lollys will be marrying Ser Wyllis Bracken.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: We made a deal, your sister and I.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: How is this good for me?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Return from where?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: This is my betrothed, Lollys.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Can't imagine why.
What if you took off that bit there?
Trouble is, I've never had much imagination.
I didn't hear that.
Back away. Keep backing away till you're outside this establishment. Then back away some more.
I will murder you, boy.
Oh, look at these two shining warriors. Ser Taryn Mant and... Ser Whosit of Whocares.
Ser Bronn of the Blackwater.
Pod.
That's exactly who I am. And you're a grub in fancy armor who's better at beating little girls than fighting men. Now, I have an appointment with Lord Tyrion.
As much as I appreciate a walk in the sunshine, your lordship, I am wondering why you sent for me.
I've been doing that for a while now.
I grow poor protecting you.
Briefly.
And now my pockets are empty. You've given me a taste for the finer things. And if you want me to carry on protecting you, you'll need to pay more.
We are. But I'm a sellsword. I sell my sword. I don't loan it out to friends as a favor.
Double.
I'm a knight now. Knights are worth double.
Which means you can afford it.
How many Dornishmen does it take to fuck a goat?
Seems to me the smart place to meet travelers is in a tavern. That way, one party is late, the other party can drink some ale inside.
If he's so damned important, how come they sent you to meet him?
And just in case the Martells of Dorne are looking to spill some Lannister blood, it may as well be yours, eh?
Oh.
Yellow balls?
I need a sigil.
Some accomplished diplomacy that was. Now where?
How do you plan on finding a single Dornishman in a city this big?
I'd probably go to sleep, but I'm getting old.
It started that way, aye. Now I'm a knight.
Killed the right people, I suppose.
I am the only man you ever met who shot a dragon.
Nearly killed it.
All right, can we stop talking about the fucking dragons now?
You're kidding me.
Sorry, ladies. Another time perhaps.
Which girl?
She once gave me a castle and a wife, then rectified me right out of them.
So, she wants to murder someone, but she can't send her soldiers. If it's the Dragon Queen she's after-
Yeah, well, good luck with that.
That fucking family.
Sell your fish somewhere else.
For the Watch.
For the Watch.
For the Watch.
For the Watch.
But you’ll never make it--
It appears to be a tie, Maester.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: And now his watch has ended.
Well done. Well done.
Jon Snow! Jon Snow! Jon Snow! Jon Snow!
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Look at the crowd there.
The God’s love to reward a fool.
What? He’s my nephew, I love him.  And he’s a damned fool.
That’s the point. [Still talking to ROOSE while the servant pours him more wine] Didn’t you marry one of these Frey girls?
Pardon, my lord, my lady.  I need to find a tree to piss on.
I’ve said no three times already.
I haven’t seen her since she was a child. I don’t know her signature. I don’t know you. And I will not surrender.
Double the guards tonight.
Yes, Brienne of Tarth. I know your father. A good man.
And if he were here now, I’d tell him the same I’m telling you.
If you think I’m abandoning my family’s seat on the Kingslayer’s word of honor, you’re a bloody fool.
We can stand longer than your one-handed friend thinks we can.
No?
Who gave you permission to cross the siege line and enter the castle? Who gave you that sword with the gold lion on the pommel?
She’s exactly like her mother.
I don’t have enough men to help her take Winterfell.
She wants her home back. I understand that. But this is my home. And if Jaime Lannister wants it, he can bloody well take it the way everyone else does.
Don’t let him in.

He’s been their captive ever since they murdered his king at the Red Wedding. Why do you think the people who murdered his king at the Red Wedding would decide to let him come home? Because it’s a trap, you idiot. Don’t let him in.
You’re not obeying Edmure’s commands. You’re obeying the fucking Kinglsayer’s commands.
WIth a knife to his throat. That is not a valid order.
I’ll have your head before I surrender Riverrun.
Go on.
I’ve run before from the Red Wedding. I’m not running again. This is my family home.
You’ll serve Sansa far better than I ever could.
Go on, now.
I haven’t had a proper sword fight in years. I expect I’ll make a damn fool of myself.
That rain will cost us another day.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Prickly?  That what you call it? Might be the least pleasant man I've ever met.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I've seen wet shits I liked better than Walder Frey. Apologies, Your Grace. Spent too many years around lancers and pikemen.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: (laughs)
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Go on, then. Cut his throat.
Kingslayer.
I assume you’re here to fulfill the vow you gave my niece. I don’t see Sansa and Arya.
Pity. Do you wish to resume your captivity?
Then why are you here?
Or you’ll kill Edmure?
My nephew’s marked for death no matter what. Hang him and be done with it.
Hundreds of mine. Thousands of yours. If you can breach the walls.
Your honor? Bargaining with oathbreakers is like building on quicksand.

As long as I’m standing, the war is not over. This is my home. I was born in this castle and I’m ready to die in it. So you can either attack or try to starve us out. We have enough provisions for two years. Do you have two years, Kingslayer?
Sieges are dull. And I wanted to see you in person, get the measure of you.
Aye, now I have. I’m disappointed.
Your father does realize we're in the middle of a war?
Listen to me and listen very carefully. You--
The laws of my fist are about to compel your teeth.
You recall that heroic engagement?
Why don't you shut your mouth about that damned mill? And don't call him "nephew." He is your king.
You're lucky I'm not your king. I wouldn't let you wave your blunders around like a victory flag.
Martyn is 15, I believe.
We seem to be running short of patience here.
It often comforts me to think that even in war's darkest days, in most places in the world absolutely nothing is happening.
Maybe? Your father was a stubborn old ox. I was surprised when he died. Didn't think death had the patience.

After 30 years of fighting, I don't think he remembered what started it. He asked me to stop calling myself Blackfish. He said it was an old joke and it was never funny to begin with. I told him people had been calling me Blackfish for so long, they don't remember my real name.

You mustn't think it. We don't know the truth. They could be in hiding. Robb believes they're alive. And he must go on believing. He's got to remain strong if he's to prevail. And you must remain strong for him.
Here’s your wages, funny man. It’ll last the rest of your life.
Why?
Alright. He’s funny.
Yes.
Queen Margaery! Queen Margaery! Queen Margaery!
Give this a good beating. Shake those fleas out. That’s a dear.
Yes, you are adored by people and animals alike.
Ooh! Ooh, what’s happened here, then?
We men of the North are right good hands at keeping people lawful.
I’d ask him for permission, but he smells too bloody awful.
The Iron Throne’s what I demand.
I can.
I will.
I dare yes.
What’s that mean?
What’s that mean?
What’s that mean?
So move along, give me my chair and there won’t be no more trouble.
But we had a deal all done and dusted.
I’m starting to think you can’t be trusted.
Well, don’t worry, love. They usually go away in five or six years.
Oh, wash this, darling. It stinks of me.
You’re seeing him.
No you don’t.
There is nothing in the north but ice and war and pirates.
I’m not going north, child, we’re going home.
The Free City of Braavos.
More silver won’t make a difference.
How did you… valar dohaeris. Of course, you shall have a cabin.
Forward, march!
Company, halt! Face front! Turn!
Lock spears!
Order up!
Tarth, Ser Jaime. (Shot of an island). The Sapphire Isle.
Ready! Nock! Draw!
Your sister's dead?
You saw him take her?  And you got away?
You wouldn't be here if you tried.
Walder Frey is a dangerous man to cross.
And you mean to do it anyway?
I know that seems important to you.

Your father didn't love me when we married. He hardly knew me or I him. Love didn't just happen to us. We built it slowly over the years, stone by stone, for you, for your brothers and sisters, for all of us. It's not as exciting as secret passion in the woods, but it is stronger. It lasts longer.
Why not? Because she's not beautiful? Because she's not exotic and exciting?
And you agreed to it. You gave him your word. Treat your oaths recklessly, and your people will do the same. If your father lived his life for one thing
Robb. Robb! You've ruined your sword.
Shh! Shh! Shh!
My boy. They have your sisters. We have to get the girls back. And then we will kill them all.
l want to see him. Now! Leave us.
Leave us. (the guards leave)
l will kill you tonight, Ser. Pack your head in a box and send it to your sister.
That is what you want the world to believe, isn't it? That you don't fear death.
Because you are going to the deepest of the seven hells, if the gods are just.
Because of men like you.
My son Bran. How did he come to fall from that tower?
Why?
Why?
Are you sure about this?
It's dangerous.
Why are you telling me?
We have enough men?
If Walder Frey cooperates.
If reinforcements arrive from King's Landing, before we take the castle, we'll be caught between Tywin's army and the sea.
Show them how it feels to lose what they love.
[Looking at EDMURE and talking to BLACKFISH] He complained about this marriage the entire ride from Riverrun and now look at him.
[Laughs] Uncle.
Don’t you drink, Lord Bolton?
I hope she makes you very happy.
Poor girl.
Oh, Ned forbade it.  He said it wouldn’t be right if he broke a man’s jaw on our wedding night.
Robb!

Lord Walder!  Lord Walder, enough!  Let it end!  Please!  He is my son! [ROBB is still cradling TALISA on the floor]  My first son!  Let him go and I swear that we will forget this!  I swear it by the Old Gods and New!  We will take no vengeance.
Take me for a hostage but let Robb go!  Robb, get up! Get up and walk out!  Please!  Please!
On my honor as a Tully, on my honor as a Stark, let him go or I will cut your wife’s throat!
Keep shooting them down. We can't risk Lord Walder sending word of your movements to the Lannisters.
The Freys have held the crossing for 600 years, and for 600 years they have never failed to exact their toll.
l agree. l'll go.
l have known Lord Walder since l was a girl. He would never harm me.
lt is a great pleasure to see you again after so many years, my lord.
ls there somewhere we can talk?
l'm sure she will give you many sons.
He is quite ill, my lord.
My lord, l –
l'm sure there were reasons –
To ask you to open your gates, my lord, so my son and his bannermen may cross the Trident and be on their way.
lf you could climb your own battlements, you would see that he has 20,000 men outside your walls.
You swore an oath to my father.
Why don't you?
Lord Walder has granted your crossing. His men are yours, as well.
Less the 400 he will keep here to hold the crossing against any who would pursue you.
You will be taking on his son Olyvar as your personal squire. He expects a knighthood in good time.
And Arya will marry his son Waldron when they both come of age.
And when the fighting is done you will marry one of his daughters. Whichever you prefer. He has a number he thinks will be suitable.
l did.
One was – Do you consent?
Not if you want to cross.
No!
lt is not your sword l want. Give me my daughters back. Give me my husband.
Take him away and put him in irons.
For the girls.
Robb
Bran and Rickon are captives in Winterfell. Sansa and Arya are captives in King's Landing. I have five children, and only one of them is free.
I grieve for your sons, my lord.
Killing Jaime Lannister would not buy life for your children, but returning him to King's Landing may buy life for mine.
Robb. How many men did we send in pursuit of the Kingslayer?
Robb.
You've had this since dawn?
A courtesy?! My husband has been taken prisoner.
Ned rots in a dungeon and you speak of patience? He is your brother by law!Does family mean nothing to you?
You will not support us then? Do I understand you correctly?
That is what I ask.
If you fear for the safety of your son...
And that is why we have to stop them.
Robb's brought the North with him.
You look well.
I had not thought to be here.
I remember the day you came into this world red-faced and squalling.
No one? Who were those men I saw here?
All of them are seasoned in battle.
Oh, would that I could.
From the Queen, you mean.
How many men do you have?
you would never be allowed to leave. No.
Do you know what happened to the Targaryen children, when the Mad King fell?
On the orders of Tywin Lannister. And the years have not made him kinder.
I suppose it does.
"The late Lord Frey" my father calls him.
Robb.
What is it?
Lord Karstark, this man is our prisoner.
And crippled mine. He will answer for his crimes, I promise you, but not here.
You will strike me down? Have you forgotten me, ser? I am the widow of your liege, Lord Eddard Stark. I am the mother of your king!
How dare you!
I understand your grief, my lord, better than most, I understand it. But in the name of my son, the King in the North, stand down.
Wise men do not make demands of kings.
Take him to the stockades. Bind him with every chain you can find!
And gag him!
So they say.
Come.
I need to be alone with him.
Your orders, which I just gave you, are to leave me alone with him.
Do you hear them out there? They want your head.
You strangled his son with your chains.
You are no knight. You have forsaken every vow you ever took.
She is a truer knight than you will ever be, Kingslayer.
You are a man without honor.
Brienne.
(to BRIENNE) Your sword.
Lord Walder is prickly by nature.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Yes, and that is why--
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Lord Frey will take this delay as a slight.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: He's getting a wedding. It was a king he wanted.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Lady Talisa.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Robb.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Lady Talisa
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Maegyr? Forgive me, I do not know this name.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Yes, you look positively forlorn.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I wish that you were free to follow your heart.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You have inherited your father's responsibilities. I'm afraid they come at a cost. You are promised to another.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: A debt that must be paid.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I told you, never trust a Greyjoy!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Let me go and talk to Theon.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You're willing to risk our freedom and our lives for a chance at a prettier wife?
Lord Tyrion, you are accused of hiring a man to slay my son Bran in his bed, and of conspiring to murder my sister’s husband; Lord Jon Arryn, the Hand of the King.
By the mother. My son has no interest in the Iron Throne.
And the wording of this oath?
And in return for my son's loyalty?
Our two houses have always been close, which is why I am begging you to reconsider this battle. Negotiate a peace with your brother.
No, wait, it wasn't her!
We've got to leave. They'll hang you for this.
You can't avenge him if you're dead.
To me it just looked like... A shadow in the shape of a man.
We should reach my son's camp tomorrow.
Only long enough to tell Robb what I have seen. After that, I will leave for Winterfell. My two youngest need me. I've been away from them for far too long.
I'm sorry.
My own mother died on the birthing bed when I was very young.
What comes after is even harder.
You mean to kill Stannis.
But Stannis has a great army around him. His own guards are sworn to keep him safe.
I'm as good as any of them. I should never have fled.
Renly's death was no fault of yours. You served him bravely.
He's gone, Brienne. You serve nothing and no one by following him into the earth. Renly's enemies are Robb's enemies as well.
When the time comes, I will not hold you back.
I vow that you shall always have a place in my home and at my table and that I shall ask no service of you that might bring you dishonor. I swear it by the Old Gods and the new.
The Karstarks are Northmen. They won't forgive the killing of their lord.
Spare his life. Keep him as a hostage.
Remove his hood.
I did... often and loudly.
And why would I do that?
Shadowcats and hill tribes are the least of your concerns.
Five years ago.
I am not a murderer, Lannister.
The dagger found
Rodrik?
To whom do I speak?
There was no time to send word.
That's why there was no time. He is my prisoner.
My sister will decide what he looks like.
Lysa, you wrote me about the Lannisters, warning me t-
This man is my prisoner. I will not have him harmed.
How dare you?
You betrayed Ned.
I trusted you. My husband trusted you. And you repaid our faith with treachery.
Get out!
Have you lost your mind? Get out!
You have Arya, too?
What do you want?
Of course they will. Jaime Lannister for two girls? Robb will never agree to those terms.
You think I keep secrets from my son?
I don't want your gifts. A token of Tyrion Lannister's goodwill. He wants you to understand that this exchange of prisoners is offered in good faith.
Good faith?
What's this?
Get out.
I had not thought to be here, Lord Stannis.
Listen to yourselves. If you were sons of mine, I would knock your heads together and lock you in a bedchamber until you remembered that you were brothers.
We share a common enemy.
Brandon.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: How many times have I told you? No climbing.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I want you to promise me. No more climbing.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Promise me. Promise me! Promise me!
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Promise me! Promise me, Bran! Promise me!
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: And to you.
I was still Catelyn Tully the last time I stayed here. You, Ser... Is that the black bat of Harrenhal I see embroidered on your coat?
And is Lady Whent a true and honest friend to my father... Lord Hoster Tully of Riverrun?
The red stallion was always a welcome sight at Riverrun. My father counts Jonas Bracken amongst his oldest and most loyal bannermen.
Knight of House Frey: Lord Walder is well, my Lady. He has asked your father for the honor of his presence on his 90th nameday. He plans to take another wife.

This man... Came into my house as a guest and there conspired to murder my son, a boy of 10. In the name of King Robert and the good Lords you serve, I call upon you to seize him and help me return him to Winterfell to await the king's justice.
Lord of Winterfell and King in the North.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You are most kind.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: It will be enough to know that justice was done, my lord. Your Grace.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I do not sit on my son's war councils. And if I did, I would not share his strategies with you.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: My son is fighting a war, not playing at one.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: And all of them young and bold like your Knight of Flowers? It's a game to you, isn't it?  I pity them.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Because it won't last. Because they are the knights of summer and winter is coming.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You fought bravely today, Lady Brienne.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: A person could almost be forgiven for forgetting we're at war.
I've missed you, Uncle. Father missed you, too, from the day you left. Maybe he never said it in so many words--
I'm glad you were with him. I wish to the gods I had been. Did you make peace in the end?

Every time he would leave for the capital or fight in a campaign, I'd see him off. 'Wait for me, little Cat,' he'd say. 'Wait for me and I'll come back to you.' And I would sit at this window every day when the sun came up, waiting. I wonder how many times did Bran or Rickon stare across the moors of Winterfell waiting for me to return. I will never see them again.
(Sighs.)
It's nine years since I've set foot in the capital. And no one knew who I was the last time I came either.
I would. We've done nothing wrong.
Instructed? I don't know who's providing your instructions, but...
You little worm! You take me for some back-alley Sally you can drag into a…
How dare you bring me here! Have you lost your mind?
How did you know I was coming to King's Landing?
Lord Varys.
How did you know I was coming?
Do you know whose dagger this is?
Yours?
To whom?
Ned! [poking her head out of a window]
We have proof. We have the blade.
Petyr has promised to help us find the truth. He's like a little brother to me. He would never betray my trust.
I won't forget this. You're a true friend.
I wish I could see the girls.
Just for a moment.
I know they did it, Ned. The Lannisters. In my bones, I know it.
My temper? Gods be good, you nearly killed poor Littlefinger yesterday.
Does he?
You don’t want Balon Greyjoy for an ally.
He is not trustworthy!
I hadn't seen him in years. I don't even know how many.
Will I be wearing manacles when I lay my father to rest?
Something else?
Have you received any demands?
Have you heard anything from Theon at all?
You're afraid of her. And she knows it.
No.
You can't help because a mother makes one for her children to protect them. Only a mother can make them.
Twice.
After a fashion. I prayed for my son  Bran to survive his fall.

Many years before that, one of the boys came down with the pox. Maester Luwin said if he made it through the night, he'd live. But it would be a very long night. So I sat with him all through the darkness. Listened to his ragged little breaths, his coughing, his whimpering.

Jon Snow. When my husband brought that baby home from the war, I couldn't bear to look at him. I didn't want to see those brown stranger's eyes staring up at me. So I prayed to the gods, take him away. Make him die. He got the pox. And I knew I was the worst woman who ever lived. A murderer. I'd condemned this poor, innocent child to a horrible death all because I was jealous of his mother. A woman he didn't even know. So I prayed to all seven gods, let the boy live. Let him live and I'll love him. I'll be a mother to him. I'll beg my husband to give him a true name, to call him Stark and be done with it, to make him one of us.
And he lived. And I couldn't keep my promise. And everything that's happened since then all this horror that's come to my family it's all because I couldn't love a motherless child.
I would have dressed, your Grace.
I never knew.
I am grateful..
You've said it.
I want you to leave.
17 years ago you rode off with Robert Baratheon. You came back a year later with another woman's son. And now you're leaving again.
That's what men always say when honor calls. That's what you tell your families, tell yourselves. You do have a choice. And you've made it. I can't do it, Ned. I really can't.
Talk to Poole about it.
I don't care about appointments!
I have to take care of him.
What if he's wrong? Bran needs me.
Close the windows! I can't stand it! Please make them stop! (Wolf’s howling repeatedly)
No!
What I am about to tell you must remain between us. I don't think Bran fell from that tower. I think he was thrown.
Someone tried to kill him twice. Why? Why murder an innocent child? Unless he saw something he wasn't meant to see.
I don't know. But I would stake my life the Lannisters are involved. We already have reason to suspect their loyalty to the crown.
I don't trust a raven to carry these words.
No. There must always be a Stark in Winterfell. I will go myself.
I must.
Too large a party attracts unwanted attention. I don't want the Lannisters to know I'm coming.
I have prayed to the Seven for more than a month. Bran's life is in their hands now.
Do you have to?
Ned. Ten is too young to see such things.
All these years and I still feel like an outsider when I come here.
I wonder if the old gods agree.
I am so sorry, my love.
There was a raven from Kings Landing. Jon Arryn is dead. A fever took him. I know he was like a father to you.
They both have their health. Gods be good. [pause] The raven brought more news. The king rides for Winterfell. With the queen and all the rest of them.
You can always say no, Ned.
We need plenty of candles for Lord Tyrion’s chamber. I’m told he reads all night.
How much could he possibly drink? A man of his … stature.
In any case, candles.
Gods, but they grow fast. [Seeing BRAN on the wall] Brandon!
How many times have I told you: No climbing!
I want you to promise me: No more climbing.
D’you know what?
You always look at your feet before you lie. Run and find your father. Tell him the king is close.
Where’s Arya? Sansa, where’s your sister?
Your Grace.
My queen.
Then he is the stupidest prince that ever lived.
Hush now. Your father hasn’t even said yes.
He’d have to leave home. He’d have to leave me. And so would you.
Sansa…
I’m sure it’s very grim, after Kings Landing. I remember how scared I was when Ned brought me up here for the first time.
I hear the same.
I won’t let him take you.
I’ll say, ‘Listen, fat man, you are not taking my husband anywhere. He belongs to me now.’
He only stops eating when it’s time for a drink.
This was sent from the Eyrie. What’s she doing at the Eyrie? She hasn’t been back there since her wedding.
She’s fled the capital. She says Jon Arryn was murdered. By the Lannisters. She says the king is in danger.
Lysa’s head would be on a spike right now if the wrong people had found that letter. Do you think she would risk her life, her son’s life, if she wasn’t certain her husband was murdered?
They murdered the last Hand. Now you want Ned to take the job.
He spent half his life fighting Robert’s wars. He owes him nothing. (To NED) Your father and brother rode south once on a king’s demand.
You don't want Balon Greyjoy for an ally.
They say a million rats live in the sewers of King's Landing. Shall we rally them to fight for us?
I don't trust Lord Greyjoy because he is not trustworthy. Your father had to go to war to end his rebellion.
I mothered more than just rebels, a fact you seem to have forgotten.
You want to leave Sansa in the Queen's hands? And Arya, I haven't heard a word about Arya. What are we fighting for if not for them?
It's time for me to go home. I haven't seen Bran or Rickon in months.
I beg your pardon?
Why in the name of all the Gods -
I haven't seen Renly Baratheon since he was a boy. You have a hundred other lords -
I will ride at first light.
You've done so well. Your father would be proud.
King Renly. There's a king in every corner now.
Ser Jeremy. My father's bannerman.
Be careful on the stairs, Grand Maester. There are so many.

Your Grace, in the judgment of your Small Council, it would be neither proper nor wise for you to wed the daughter of a man beheaded for treason, a girl whose brother is in open rebellion against the throne as we speak. For the good of the realm, your councilors beg you to set Sansa Stark aside.
A joke. Joffrey did not mean it.
Let him make his threats. Hmm? He's a bitter little man.
Come along.
We have so much to celebrate. A wedding to plan. You must rest.
Come.
Leave.
So, enjoying married life? An unhappy wife is a wine merchant's best friend.
Deserve? Be careful with that. Start trying to work out who deserves what and before long you'll spend the rest of your days weeping for each and every person in the world.
(chuckles)
I won't be marrying Ser Loras.
You're not me. You want to make things better for Sansa? Give her a child.
So she can have some happiness in her life.
Not very. But if it weren't for my children, I'd have thrown myself from the highest window in the Red Keep. They're the reason I'm alive.
Until we've dealt with all our enemies.
Then I suppose it will go on for quite a long time.
Bloody Martells.
You're dismissed, Grand Maester.
Not anymore.
You can save him?
Do everything you can. Come to me with anything you need.
Will it weaken him?
Very well then.
I'm opening it again.
I will not.
I don't care. I will stay in King's Landing where I belong with my son, the king.
