Is ‘no’ the only word that you know?
He said they ate meat.
My brother didn't know anything about dragons. He didn't know anything about anything.
She was Drogo's first gift to me.
I promised to protect them. Promised them their enemies would die screaming. How do I make starvation scream?
Does it ever end?
And you're sure there's no other way?
No one will take my dragons.
(Turns to Jorah) As you are mine.
(Speaking Dothraki) Rakharo, Aggo, Kovarro. Take our remaining horses. You will ride east, you southeast, and you northeast.
(Speaking Dothraki) Cities, living or dead. Caravans and people. Rivers or lakes or the great salt sea. Find how far the Red Waste extends before us, and what lies on the other side.
(Speaking Dothraki) Rokharo, you are my last hope, blood of my blood.
You never have.
Not fast enough. I can't wait that long. I need an army
The greatest slave-soldiers in the world. The distinction means a good deal to some people.
It's too beautiful a day to argue.
Don't mock them. They're the first Dothraki who have ever been on a ship. They followed me across the poison water. If they'll do it, others will. And with a true khalasar ...
Tell the good master there is no need.
You take a babe from its mother's arms, kill it as she watches, and pay for her pain with a silver coin?
How many do you have to sell?
8,000 dead babies.
Once I own them, these men ...
Once I own an army of slaves, what will I be?
(to Jorah) The warlocks.
I owe you my life, ser.
You know this man?
Ser Barristan.
Where's Daario Naharis? Where's Grey Worm?
Gambling?
How long have they been at it?
What's the prize to winning this stupid contest?
The last man holding his sword can find a new queen to fight for.
Have you ever been to Meereen?
And?
And now an army of former slaves is marching to her gates. You think the Great Masters are worried?
You were told to ride at the back of the train.
All right, what is this matter of strategy?
Would you like to walk at the back of the train instead of riding?
Would you like to walk without shoes?
You are a gambler, aren't you?
How many miles are there between here and Meereen?
You will do no such thing. I will see each and every one of their faces. Remove her collar before you bury her.
Sons of the Harpy?
They’ve never killed before.
I didn’t conquer them. Their own people did.

Then they will have to learn to see things differently, Mossador. He did not risk his life fighting for freedom so cowards in masks could take it away. And I did not take up residence in this pyramid so I could watch the city below decline into chaos. What was the name of the man you lost?
I want him buried with honor. Publicly. In the Temple of the Graces.
Angry snakes lash out. Makes chopping off their heads that much easier. Find the men who did this and bring them to me.
Good.
Concessions?
I’m not a politician. I’m a queen.
I don’t expect the Wise Masters to be happy. Slavery made them rich, I ended slavery.
The fighting pits? Where slaves fought slaves to the death?
No fighting pits.
I do not respect the tradition of human cockfighting.
How many times must I say no before you understand?
Whatever he wants from me he’s not going to get it. If he really believes I’m going to reopen the fighting pits…
What?
Why?
You were sold into slavery, forced to fight to the death for the amusement of the masters, and you’re defending the fighting pits?
That’s why I have the Unsullied patrolling the streets.
I don’t want another child’s bones dropped at my feet. No one’s seen Drogon in weeks. For all I know he’s flown halfway across the world. I can’t control them anymore.
Viserion? Rhaegal?
Easy. Easy. Easy.

I am Daenerys Stormborn of the House Targaryen, the First of Her Name, the Unburnt, Queen of Meereen, Queen of the Andals and the Rhoynar of the First Men, Khaleesi of the Great Grass Sea, Breaker of Chains and Mother of Dragons.
I will not lie with you. And I will bear no children, for you, or anyone else. Not until the sun rises in the west, and sets in the east.
I was wife of Khal Drogo, son of Khal Bharbo.
I know. I burnt his body.
If you will escort me back to Meereen, I will see that your khalasar is given a thousand horses, as a sign of my gratitude.
Shall we begin?
Thank you for inviting us into your home, Lady Stark. The North is as beautiful as your brother claimed, as are you.
Whatever they want.
Your sister doesn't like me.
She doesn't need to be my friend but I am her queen. If she can't respect me-
The dragons are barely eating.
They don't like the North.
Go on.
Nobody does. Until they ride a dragon.
Then I've enjoyed your company, Jon Snow.
Whatever you can.
We could stay a thousand years-
No one would find us.
So keep your queen warm.
Don't be afraid.
So you're the man?
The one who saved Ser Jorah when no one else could.
I'll have to make some changes in the Citadel when I take my throne. A great service merits a great reward.
Well, there must be something I could give you.
For what crime?
From the Citadel?
Not Randyll Tarly?
I offered to let him retain his lands and titles if he bent the knee. He refused.
Your brother stood with your father.
Of course.
Do not touch me.
How many today?
They may suit my needs. Tell me of their training.
Well, I'll come with you.
That honor goes to Ser Jorah and Ser Barristan as neither of them kept me waiting this morning. You two will ride in the rear guard and protect the livestock.
It was a good speech. Didn't want to interrupt.
Let's go home.
I'd leave 'em up a few more days. Sends a message.
What's this?
His troubles are done. He's feeding the crops now.
His wife, too. You want to keep a man silent, you silence him.
(laughs)
Halt!
My lord, over here. I think I've found what we're looking for.
Send the old man home.
(nods)
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: My prince. You cannot let that stand. He must pay.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: No, he has to pay the iron price. They'll never respect you while he lives.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Come.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Come on, I'll take you out.
Dagmer, your first mate.
They're not gonna respect you until you prove yourself.
You're not.
They're all Iron Islanders. Do they do as they're told or do they do as they like?
Your Grace.
Yes, Your Grace. He chose us himself.
Please, Your Grace.
Haystack Hall.
Littlefinger's a fancy man.
She's beautiful.
Mhysa!
I wanna go with you.
How are you keeping?
It was just a bit of blood.
I'm not a highborn.
A recent state of affairs. I was born in Flea Bottom just like you.
I lived below the Street of Flour.
The shit that poured from their privy pipes flowed down the side of Aegon's Hill along Tanner's Row and right in front of my front door on Gin Alley.
The Street of Steel, with your armor and your knights? Pfft. You lived in the fancy part of town. And here we are now. Two boys from Flea Bottom in the castle of a king.
If you mistrust fancy people so much, why were you in such a hurry to trust her?
She does know her way around a man's head, I'll give her that.
Oh, that's a long story.
Many years ago I helped King Stannis out of a difficult situation. He rewarded me with a lordship. And this.
I didn't want to be a lord. I nearly didn't accept.
I did it for my son. I didn't want him to step over a river of shit every time he stepped through his front door. I wanted him to have a better life.
He's dead.
Following me.
First night of the full moon.' Why is there a G in night?
Well, your father's not going to go to that.
I've been trying to stay out of those dungeons my whole life.

Aye, old Balerion's definitely better than anything your cousin Rylene's got on offer. But now that I'm your father's Hand again, I have to keep him informed. The king does not have time for this drudgery. 'To all the lords and knobble men of Westeros.'
Stay here. Bar the door.
And you take credit because you dropped a leech into the fire?

Your Grace, the world has got so far bent. I've seen things crawl out of nightmares, but my eyes were open. I don't know if Robb Stark died because of the Red Woman's sorcery or because at war men die all the time, but I do know that uniting the Seven Kingdoms with blood magic is wrong. It is evil. And you are not an evil man.
Aegon Targaryen.
On the back of his dragon Balerion the Dread.
You do not need to burn the boy. If what you say is true, a drop of his blood killed Robb Stark--
His name is Gendry. He's a good lad. A poor lad from Flea Bottom who happens to be your nephew.
Everything.
You're leaving.
Yes, but not on you.
Aim for that star. Don't stop. There's bread and water. Go slow with it. If you finish it off, no matter how thirsty you get, don't drink seawater.
Row for a full day and night and you'll reach Rook's Rest. You'll want to stop there. Don't. She'll find you.
You must keep the coast on your left side until you reach King's Landing.
They were looking for me for 20 years. Do they know your face?
I'd worry more about the Red Woman.
The other way.
You ever been in a boat before?
Don't fall out. Go on.
Because it's right. And because I'm a slow learner.
When you get to Flea Bottom, have a bowl of brown for me.
No. I let him go.
Aye, I do.
There has got to be another way.
I don't know, Your Grace. I can't see the future in the fire.
I understand. But since you haven't yet unnamed me Hand of the King, it is my duty to advise you against it. You're gonna need me.
Matthos taught me before he died so I could be of better service to you.
You can't stop him alone. You need someone to rebuild your army for you, someone to convince this lord and that lord to fight for you, to bring sellswords and pirates to your side.
This is Stannis Baratheon, the one true king of the seven kingdoms.
What are men of the Night's Watch doing in a Wildling camp?
You're speaking to the one true king boy. You'll address him as your grace.
He came to your aid. Stannis. Now he needs you!
The wildlings will make a difference!
You saved their bloody lives. If they’re going to live in the Seven Kingdoms, safe behind our wall, they ought to fight for the damn place.
Shireen? The princess?
Tell him. Tell him who it belonged to.
Tell him what you did to her. Tell him!
Why?
You burned a little girl alive!
If he commands you to burn children, your Lord is evil!
I loved that girl like she was my own. She was good. She was kind. And you killed her!
The only way for what? They all died anyway! You told everyone Stannis was the one. You had him believing it, all of them fooled. And you lied.
Aye, you were wrong. How many died because you were wrong?
If you ever come back this way, I will execute you myself.
I ask your leave to execute this woman for murder. She admits to the crime.
Aye, but we have the wind. She'll blow us straight to the gates.
King's Landing hasn't been home for 20 years. I spent most of my life dodging the royal fleet. And now I'm sailing right at them.
Of course, there are several royal fleets at the moment.
Gods be good.
But not over them?
Well, first, we have to put it there.

Our army outnumber theirs 5-to-1. Those walls have never been breached, and the men guarding the walls, when they see you, they don't see a liberator, they see a stranger come to set their city on fire.
I've never known bells to mean surrender. They want to play music with us? Let's play. Drums.
Where are their ships?
Lord Varys knows what you had for breakfast three days ago. There are no surprises here.
Maybe.
Draw! Hold.
(confused) There's no one on board.
Wildfire. Steer clear! Steer clear!
Matthos! Get down!
A band of 20 men, maybe less. They were in and out before anyone spotted them, burnt our food stores to the ground, all our siege weapons were destroyed, dozens of tents were--
We’re still getting a count, but hundreds are dead.
The northerners know more about their land than we ever will.
Unless there’s a thaw, we can’t press forward to Winterfell, and we don’t have enough food to get us back to Castle Black.
Forgive me, Your Grace, I never claimed to be an expert in military matters. But if we can’t march forward, and we won’t march back…
You sent for me, Your Grace.
Your Grace, you’ve named me your Hand.
The King’s Hand should never abandon the King, especially in a time of war.
A boy with a scroll could deliver this message.
Your Grace, perhaps Queen and Princess Shireen could accompany me.
At least let me take Shireen. A siege is no place for a little girl.
What’s this one now?
Well that sounds like a proper story.
But the dragon only saw a dumb man, holding a mirrored shield.
Thus ending the dragonslaying career of Ser Byron Swann. I made you something, Princess.
Do you like it?
You’re very welcome.
Of course I will.

Because you deserve it. My son was always on me trying to teach me to read. Gods, I was stubborn about it. Made it this far without reading, seemed to me I could make it to the grave. I wish I had listened to them. This is my own poor way of saying thank you for teaching me to be a grown up. (beat) I’ll be gone for a few days, Princess. I’ll want to hear all about the dance of dragons when I’m back.
That’s not his way. He knows the North is watching. If the other houses sense weakness on his part, they’ll stop fearing him. He can’t have that. Fear is his power.
It’s crucial that we let them charge at us. They’ve got the numbers, we need the patience. If we let him buckle our center, he’ll pursue. Then we’ll have him surrounded on three sides.
We should all get some sleep.
You think there’s hope?
It wasn’t the Boltons defeated Stannis. It was Stannis himself. I loved the man. He lifted me up and made me something. But he had demons in his skull whispering foul things.
What?
No, it’s a manner of speaking. Not actual demons.
Maybe that was our mistake, believing in kings.
No, he’s not.
No, thanks. It does sound delicious, but I better keep a clear head. I can never sleep the night before a battle.
I walk. Think and walk. Think and walk until I’m far enough away from camp that no one can hear me shitting my guts out.
Prepare to charge! Prepare to charge!
Go! Go! Follow your commander!
We’ll just kill our own men. Stand down.
We may as well be taking shits back here.
Follow!
With me, lads! Break their line!
If the wind holds, we'll reach King's Landing in a day.
Can't make promises for the wind, Your Grace.
I thank you, Your Grace.
My son wishes me to change it. Three mermen with tridents, something like that. I understand why the older families look down at me.
My father was a crabber.
Well, sons of lords don't like to break bread with sons of crabbers. Our hands stink.
Many fought bravely for your brother. Many fought for the Mad King.
Some are happy to do it to my face.
No one has forgotten, Your Grace.
He was only a boy.
(exhales)
And potatoes. Some salted beef, I believe.
I was relieved they did not.
Your Grace, I pray I serve you well.
Your Grace.
Two meals a day. Cold for breakfast, hot for supper. I cannot complain.
Well, it's sad to say, but I've seen worse.
I'm sorry about your son. Didn't get the chance to tell you before. Good lad. Loyal lad.
(nods)
I didn't know she'd been gone.
Why?
She's going to kill him.
Forgive me, Your Grace, I'm not a learned man, but is there a difference between kill and sacrifice?  The boy is your nephew.
Because he has your blood in his veins.
Renly wronged you. Renly declared himself king when the throne belonged to you. He raised an army, stole your bannermen. This boy has done you no harm. He's an innocent.
Your Grace, why did you come to see me today?
I swear it. I can't swear never to speak against her.

Quite little, Your Grace. Verging on none. You could have freed me yesterday or tomorrow, but you came to me now before this boy is put to the knife because you knew I'd counsel restraint. You came to hear me say it because you believe it yourself. You're not a man who slaughters innocents for gain or glory. When my son was five he said to me, 'I don't ever want to die. ' I wanted to say to him, 'You won't, child. You won't ever.' I hated the idea of him lying awake in the dark, afraid. I think mothers and fathers made up the gods because they wanted their children to sleep through the night.
40 horses died in the night. We’ll lose more come sunset. We’re running out of food. We can’t open the supply line until the snow clears.
The Stormcrows rode off last night.
We still have a hard march to Wintefell and we won’t be marching anywhere in this weather.
This isn’t our time, we should head back to Castle Black when the snow clears.
Your Grace..
It’s better to wait for the right time than to risk everything.
Your Grace. (leaves)
If it please, my lady, I understand how you feel.
Davos, my lady, of House Seaworth.
You needn’t ask your maester about my house. It’s rather new.

You never thought you’d find yourself in your position. Being responsible for so many lives at such a young age. I never thought I’d be in my position. I was a crabber’s son, then I was a smuggler. And now I found myself addressing the lady of a great house in time of war. But I’m here because this isn’t someone else’s war. It’s our war.

Your uncle, Lord Commander Mormont, made that man his steward. He chose Jon to be his successor because he knew had the courage to do what was right, even if it meant giving his life. Because Jeor Mormont and Jon Snow both understood that the real war isn’t between a few squabbling houses. It’s between the living and the dead. And make no mistake, my lady, the dead are coming.

As long as the Boltons hold Winterfell, the North is divided. And a divided North won’t stand a chance against the Night King. You want to protect your people, my lady. I understand. But there’s no hiding from this. We have to fight and we need to do it together.
If they’re half as ferocious as their lady, the Boltons are doomed.
Stannis camped here on his way to Winterfell.
He was the most experienced commander in Westeros. He chose this place for a reason. Those mountains are a natural fortification. There’s a stream down there for the horses.
Aye, the snows defeated Stannis as much as the Boltons did.
It’s not what we’d hoped for. But we still have a chance if we’re careful and smart.
For fuck’s sake.
Friends, this is not worth fighting over!
This’ll be it, now. Right now, we don’t need to be fighting.
Hold on.
Maybe it still is.
I wish you hadn't done that.
Any ideas as to how we might change that state of affairs?
How old is Tywin Lannister?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Your Grace, if you'd like to sit, I'm sure that--
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Easterners have a difference sense of time, I've often found. Once I was waiting for Salladhor Saan here in Braavos. Together, we were going to run three shiploads of the finest--
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: This is Stannis of the House Baratheon, King of the Andals and the First Men, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men
The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: My lord.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Well, strictly speaking, I didn't do the thieving. That would be the pirates. I just moved what they stole from one place to another.  {DAVOS removes his right glove to show the stubs of his fingers.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: This is the payment that was demanded by King Stannis for my crimes. I consider it an honest accounting. He's an honest man and he's your best chance to get back the money you've sunk into Westeros. Which is a lot, I imagine. Wars are expensive.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: As long as Stannis lives, the war is not over. Who's the real power in King's Landing?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Humor me.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Begging your pardon, I think it's a problem for now. There's only one reliable leader left in Westeros. Stannis. He's got the birthright. He's in his prime. He's a tried and tested battle commander. And he doesn't just talk about paying people back, he does it.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You think they ever met a pirate who didn't tell them that joke?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Only half rotted.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: No time, I'm afraid. We sail at sunrise.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You won't be alone. And you won't be poor.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: There's a chest of the good stuff left back at your house. I gave it to your wife.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: (laughs) I'll see you at sunrise.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: How much more defeated do you want them to be? They're on their knees.
Look around you, friend. We won.
Easy, men. Easy! Easy.
Jon. We should speak with the queen.
Friends, please. We've been cutting each other's throats long enough. Torgo Nudho. Am I saying that properly?

If it weren't for you and your men, we would've lost the war with the dead. This country owes you a debt it can never repay, but let us try. There is land in the Reach. Good land. The people that used to live there are gone. Make it your own. Start your own house with the Unsullied as your bannermen. We've had enough war. Thousands of you, thousands of them. You know how it ends. We need to find a better way.
Who, then?
I'm not sure I get a vote, but aye.
Your Grace.
Long may he reign.
We have. These projects will begin as soon as the Master of Coin and Lord of Lofty Titles provides funding.
"Any more."
(shakes his head no)
Your Grace.
I'm sorry about your brother, Your Grace, I wanted to let you know that people grieve for him.
Your Grace, I --
And I've never known you to hide from the truth.
I've come to tell you that what I saw.
Nothing is worth what this will cost you, not even the Iron
When do we sail for King's Landing?
And will you bring Lady Melisandre with you?
If you take King's Landing with her by your side, the victory will be hers.
Loyal service means telling hard truths.
She's a foreigner preaching her foreign religion. Some believe she whispers orders in your ear and you obey.
You won those bannermen from Renly. Don't lose them to her.
Your Grace, I'm honored, but my time on the sea was spent evading ships, not attacking them. The other lords won't be happy.
Princess? Gods, what are you doing here?
Bert?
Go back to your room. You're not supposed to be here. If your father knew--
I am, child. I disobeyed my king, your father, and now I'm paying the price.
Aye. So they did.
Thank you, my lady. I'm sure it's a fine book, but it's wasted on me.
My lady, I can't read the words.
No. No. No.
Go. We can't.
(laughs) I wouldn't know where to start.
What?
Uh, Your Grace. Wouldn't it be better to wait? When Jon Snow returns with the wildlings, we could have thousands more men.
I'll choose a dozen men to stay and guard the queen and the princess.
It's a tough road ahead, Your Grace. Won't they be safer -
As you wish, Your Grace.
Beg your pardon?
First things first. It's a long march ahead. And then we have to take the castle.
Aye, princess, but you won't be anywhere near -
Yes, my queen.
Well, I am. When the battle comes, promise you'll protect me.
Aside from the Starks and the Boltons, the most powerful houses in the North are the Umbers, the Karstarks, and the Manderlys.
The Umbers and the Karstarks have already declared for the Boltons, so we’re not doing so well there.
I beg your pardon, my lady, but they know that a Stark beheaded their father. I don’t think we can count on them either.
Precious little, my lady.

They may well be loyal, but how many rose up against the Boltons when they betrayed your family? I may not know the North, but I know men. They’re more or less the same in any corner of the world and even the bravest of them don’t want to see their wives and children skinned for a lost cause. If Jon’s going to convince them to fight alongside him, they need to believe it’s a fight they can win.
I don’t doubt it. But Jon doesn’t have the Stark name.
I didn’t know the Tullys still had an army.
That’s good. The Blackfish is a legend. His support would mean a great deal. Stark, Tully, a few more houses, almost starts to look like a winning side.
I can smuggle you in, but if the Goldcloaks were to recognize you, I'm warning you, I'm not a fighter.
They won't follow Ser Jorah.
You can't lead a raid beyond the wall.  You're not in the Night's Watch anymore.  You're King in the North.
Last time I was here you killed my son with wildfire.
There's a path to the left that hugs the cliff.  The guards hardly ever patrol it.  Too many steps.
I've got my own business in Flea Bottom.
Then we're fucked.  Best hurry.
I wasn't sure I'd find you.
I thought you might still be rowing.
I looked in shops, taverns, brothels.  I should have known to come straight to the Street of Steel.
I haven't been here in years.  Why should they recognize me?  Sometimes I hardly do.  Nothing fucks you harder than time.
Anybody give you any trouble?
Don't be so sure.  Safety is never a permanent state of affairs.  Bad things are coming.
I think you need to understand --
You should know what you're heading into.
You might want to bring one of those swords.
Keep your father's name to yourself.  The situation is complicated enough already.
I told you, Clovis, if we don't get out of here soon someone is going to --
No weapons, friends.  I promise.  What can I do for you?
It is.
Still five gold dragons?
I can't say it was a pleasure doing business with you.
Not more gold.
Come and have a look.
Fermented crab.

Fermented crab.  One bucket of this triples the brothel's earnings for the week.  A man thinks he's done, he's ready to go back to his loving family, but before he gets his britches up his lady of the hour pops a tiny spoon of that into his mouth.  Five minutes later, he's back in the race.  Here, have a taste.
I'd hurry to your favorite establishment or you'll put a whole in that chainmail.
I really wouldn't try anymore until you see how the first bite hits you.
Perhaps there's some arrangement?
This is Gendry.
We're leaving today.  The king's got a lot on his mind.
He doesn't need to be thinking about harboring the bastard of a dead king.
You're just Clovis, a smith who's come to pay his respects before he heads off to work at the Winterfell forges.
Begging your pardon, Your Grace.
Yet again.
He was meant to keep that to himself.
Don't be a fool.  You're not a soldier.
He can handle himself.
As my father used to say, it's better to be a coward for a minute than dead for the rest of your life.
Yeah.  Nobody mind me.  All I've ever done is live to a ripe old age.
I've been failing at that job of late.
I'll be staying behind.  I'm a liability out there as you well know.
Our reasons aren't your reasons.
Is that possible?
Your Grace.
Well, life's been good since you hacked them off, Your Grace. And it's four less fingernails to clean.
Pardon?
It reminds me of where I come from and where I am now. It reminds me of your justice. It was an honest punishment and you were good with the cleaver.
A lesson I've tried to teach my son.
To me? Gods, no. But if your red woman told him to leap from the crow's nest
I've lived within the law for 17
Any shore, any night. What am I bringing ashore?
I am true to Your Grace and always will be, but surely there are other ways, cleaner ways.
Someone once told me the night is dark and full of terrors.
I'd say my parts are mixed, my lady, good and bad.
And which are you?
Well, that must be very nice for you.
I do.
Don't talk about my wife.
Strange that this Lord of Light asks you to work in the shadows.
These weren't here before. They've barred the passage.
Gods protect us.
My lady.
Will you stay here at Castle Black?
You serve Jon Snow now?
Forgive me, my lady, I thought that was Stannis.
What happened down there?
And Shireen? What happened to the princess?
My lady, I’m Ser Davos Seaworth.
That’s in the past now.
Who did?
You'll want to discuss this amongst yourselves.  Perhaps --
What do you think of her?
I believe you know of whom I speak.
A good heart?  I've noticed you starring at that good heart.
Fewer.
Speaking of good hearts, Missandei of Naarth.
King Snow, isn't it?  No?  That doesn't sound right.  King Jon?
Is the custom different in Naarth?
The sounds liberating.
If I may, how did a slave girl come to advise Daenerys Targaryen?
That was good of her.  Of course you're serving her now, aren't you?
We heard your uncle attacked your fleet.  We thought you were dead.
Your sister?
To Lord Gendry Baratheon of Storm's End.
The last time I saw her, I told her I'd kill her if I ever saw her again.
I did. I do.

Never got the chance. She did it to herself. Or her god did it to her. Lord of Light. We play his game for him, we fight his war and win, and then he fucks off. No signs, no blessings. Who knows what he wants?
And what if I'm not trying to be happy?
Thank you. I feel much better.
We all did.
That's right, you did.
You'll forgive me if I switch sides.
Your Grace.
Joffrey?
I will find you an army, Your Grace. I've been working day and night...
I've rallied House Peasebury to your 'cause.
House Musgood.
And House Haigh.
Westeros is not the world, Your Grace. We need to look east for ships and men. 10,000 skilled soldiers fight for the Golden Company.
They've never broken a contract.

We're willing to use blood magic to put you on the throne, but we're not willing to pay men to fight? Now the Red Woman's magic is real. Her visions and prophecies may be, too, but I've never heard of visions and prophecies winning a war. Soldiers win wars. Soldiers on the ground. It's dirty on the ground.
Not yet.
I'm sorry, Princess.
The Hand of the King doesn't have much leisure time.
That happened once, weeks ago. You're your father's daughter, no mistake. Bloody relentless, the both of you.
"The Life and Adventures of Elyo Grivas. First Sword of Braavos."
Thank you very much.
No, I was never a pirate. I was a smuggler.
Well, if you're a famous smuggler, you're not doing it right.
Your father lacks an appreciation of the finer points of bad behavior. So do the Braavosi.

Of course. Almost got beheaded by a First Sword of Braavos. I tried to explain to him the difference between pirates and smugglers, but he didn't seem any more interested in the distinction than your father. I suppose if you work for the Iron Bank of Braavos and each one of your gold barges is worth half a kingdom, you tend not to be overly concerned with the kind of distinction...
I need you to write a message.
It's too important for me. I need a smart person to do it. Go on.
To the offices of the Iron Bank of Braavos from Stannis Baratheon the one true king of Westeros.
I need to get their attention. Go on. Write.
He sees something in you. (Jon looks up). Might not be apparent from his tone, but it's the truth. He believes in you.
The king is a complicated man. But he wants what's right for the Seven Kingdoms.
He's the one true king. He has the blood right to that throne.
Have you now? (Turning to Olly). How does the Night's Watch vow go again? I bet you've got it memorized since you got here.
Not that bit, the bit at the end.

Wait, that's enough. “The shield that guards the realm of men.” That's what you swore to be. Now I'm not a learned man, but the best way of helping the most people might not be sitting in a frozen castle at the edge of the world. It just might mean wading in the muck, getting your boots dirty, and doing what needs to be doing.
As long as the Boltons rule the North, (Stands up), the North will suffer. Just one man's opinion.
Easy, easy. Easy.
What do you remember?
The lady brought you back.
You were dead. And now you’re not. That’s completely fucking mad, seems to me. I can only imagine how it seems to you.
I don’t know. Maybe we’ll never know. What does it matter? You go on. You fight for as long as you can. You clean up as much of the shit as you can.
Good. Now go fail again.
Davos Seaworth.
Unluckily for me.
Where are you from?  I can't place the accent.
Ah.  I hear it's beautiful down there.  Palm trees and butterflies.  I haven't been myself.
This place has changed.
This is Jon Snow.
He's King in the North.
Apologies.  I have a Flea Bottom accent, I know.  But Jon Snow is King in the North, Your Grace.  He's not a lord.
I wasn't there, Your Grace.
Not to defeat Cersei.  You could storm King's Landing tomorrow and the city would fall.  Hell, we almost took it and we didn't even have dragons.
You don't believe him.  I understand that.  It sounds like nonsense.

But if destiny has brough Daenerys Targaryen back to our shores, it has also made Jon Snow King in the North.  You were the first to bring Dothraki to Westeros.  He was the first to make allies with Wildlings and northmen.  He was named Lord Commander of the Night's Watch.  He was named King in the North.  Not because of his birthright.  He has no birthright.  He's a damn bastard.  All those hard son's of bitches chose him as their leader because they believe in him.

All those things you don't believe in, he faced those things.  He fought those things for the good of his people.  He risked his life for his people.  He took a knife in the heart for his people.  He gave his own --
If we don't put asside our enmities and band together we will die.  And then it doesn't matter whose skeleton sits on the Iron Throne.
Could you give us a moment?
You'll have your gold when we take the treasury at King's Landing.
Because you're a smart gambler. Stannis has proved himself in war. Twice. His baby brother has never set foot on a battlefield. Neither has the false king, Joffrey.
Stannis has just begun the fight. His bannermen will rally to his cause. There's no man in the Seven Kingdoms more honorable than Stannis Baratheon. Or more worthy of loyalty.
Matthos.
Of course you do. Every time you leave harbor, you're leaving on a promise. A promise that somewhere on the seas, somebody's got some gold and you can take it from them.
As is mine. You're not the young man, Salladhor. And correct me if I'm wrong: Most pirates don't grow old.

You want to spend your last few years on the sea, stealing from Pentoshi cheesemongers and Midanese silk merchants, then go. They're out there waiting for you. That's easy. What I'm offering you is hard. Come with me and plunder the greatest city in Westeros. You'll be the richest man in Lysene. And the most famous. They'll be singing songs about you as long as men have voices to sing.
It is. (Matthos rolls his eyes).
The queen?
He's young yet. I promise you the gold. I promise you the glory. I cannot promise you the queen.
He is the one true king.
Get me to the gates of King's Landing and I will.
As soon as his God wills it.
Oh you and your mother.
I wish I had a God, truly! I'm not mocking you, but I've seen men pray to every God there is. Pray for wind, pray for rain, pray for home. None of it works!
I wasn't praying.

You want me to have a god, fine. King Stannis is my god. He raised me up and blessed me with his trust. He gave you a future I could never have imagined. You know how to read, you'll be a knight someday. You think your fire god commanded all that? It was Stannis. Only Stannis.
Don't tell him that.
Your Grace.
Salladhor Saan will join our fleet. 30 ships. His men know how to fight.
Does seem the wiser choice.
Salladhor Saan is an old friend, I've known him 30 years. I've never trusted him.
At once, Your Grace.
Come.
Lord Florent was your brother by law. STANNIS; He was an infidel.
He worshipped the gods of his fathers and their fathers before him. They were the gods of your fathers, too.
How many ships did he bring to your 'cause? How many men?
I'm sure they're more than grateful, my queen.
Tonight, the Night's Watch elect a new Lord Commander. Ser Alliser Thorne is going to win.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Unpleasant man. He thinks you're a traitor. What's your life going to be like on the Wall with Thorne in command?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I’ve never been much of a fighter.
Apologies for what you’re about to see.
I'm sorry, my lady. I didn’t mean to interrupt.
I assume you know why I’m here.
It’s about the Lord Commander.
Does he have to be?
Do you know of any magic...that could help him? Bring him back?
Can it be done?
How?
Have you seen it done?
But it was. It could be now.
Not for you? I saw you drink poison that should’ve killed you. I saw you give birth to a demon made of shadows.
Fuck him, then. Fuck all of them. I’m not a devout man, obviously. Seven Gods, drowned gods, tree gods, it's all the same.
I’m not asking the Lord of Light for help.I’m asking the woman who showed me that miracles exist.
Have you ever tried?
Sounds like a charmer.  Of course, the casual mention of a Dothraki horde, a legion of Unsullied and three dragons, a bit less charming.
Fire kills Whites, you told me.  What breathes fire?
No, too dangerous.
But if the Army of the Dead makes it passed the Wall, do we have enough men to fight them?

You are now. Look, I made it through most of my years without ever getting near a fight, but then I survived the Battle of the Bastards. Right outside these walls. If I can live through that, you can live through this. They'll outfit you with weapons at the forge. Right that way.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Which way do you want to go?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I'm sure a lot of people would.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: We'll hold off the rest of them for as long as we can.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: The dragons should give us an edge in the field.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Well, what do we have here?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: No, not for me, thanks. Came here for this.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I figured I could wait to die freezing my balls off out there or wait to die nice and warm in here.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Maybe I will have that drink.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: All without a shred of combat ability.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: We're out of wine.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: You'll pray for a quick death.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: (Looks to Stannis) Not now.
Stannis is our king. We follow where he leads, even if we don't like the path.
I don't serve the others. I serve Stannis.
What's the truth?
A harmless courtesy, Your Grace.
Your Grace, the Lannisters are the true enemy. If, for the time being, you could make peace with your brother -
Well, many have already declared for him; Mace Tyrell, Randyll Tarly.
And how many ships has the Lord of Light got in his fleet?
I'm sure he doesn't, but we do if we're going to war. If not Renly, Your Grace, join forces with Robb Stark.
(Whispers to Cressen) Don't.
Here. Here! Help me!
I was in the battle at Blackwater. I was a captain and a knight.
The one true king of Westeros ...  Stannis Baratheon.
(Nods)
No, the wildfire took him. I saw it.
If I was a good father, he'd still be here. Stannis lives?
Will you take me there?
This war is not over.
We're both sworn to King Stannis.
Stannis never gives up. Never. Now he will regroup ...
What?
You're a smuggler.
Take me back there, please.
Maybe not, but I could carve her heart out.
You call me friend. You drank with me on my wedding day.
I have to stop her. Please, do this for me.
Your Grace.
Not yet.
Your Grace, you are the rightful king. Not only by blood. You're an honorable man, a just man. And there is still a war to fight.
By burning prisoners alive?
I do not judge people for the gods they worship. If I did, I'd have thrown you in the sea before you ever set foot on Dragonstone.
You are my enemy.
( gasps )
( growls ) This woman is evil! She's the mother of demons.
Your Grace!
She will destroy us all!
Your Grace!
I’ve been talking to your sworn brothers. Many of them love you.
Many don’t. You were seen taking the body of a wildling girl north of the Wall. Why?
Some of the Night’s Watch feel you have too much affection for the wildlings.
It’s a fair offer. More than fair.
Help me get him inside.
How many of your brothers do you think you can trust?
Does the wolf know you?
We need all the help we can get.
I can’t speak for the flames, but he’s gone.
He’ll have seen we didn’t come. Thorne will have made it official by now. Castle Black is his.
We don’t have the numbers.
It’s not enough. I didn’t know Lord Commander Snow for long, but I have to believe he wouldn’t have wanted his friends to die for nothing.
We need to fight, but we don’t need to die. Not if we have help.
You’re not the only ones who owe your lives to Jon Snow.
Hear that, lads? Nothing to fear.
And some mutton. I’d like some mutton.
I’m not much of a hunter. I’ll need some food if I’m gonna make it south without starving.
Thank you, Ser Alliser. We’ll discuss amongst ourselves and come back to you with an answer.
Boys, I’ve been running from men like that ll my life. In my learned opinion, we open that door --
Aye, but we don’t need to make it easy for them.
There’s always the Red Woman.
You haven’t seen her do what I’ve seen her do.
Can't argue with that.
Not so long ago, the Starks and the Karstarks were slaughtering each other on the battlefield. Jon Snow brought peace to the houses.

Her gratitude is lovely, but that's not my point. The Northmen are loyal to Jon Snow, not to her. They don't know her. The Free Folk don't know her. I've been up here a while, and I'm telling you, they're stubborn as goats. You want their loyalty, you have to earn it.
A proposal is what I'm proposing. On the off chance that we survive the Night King, what if the Seven Kingdoms, for once in their whole shit history, were ruled by a just woman and an honorable man?
The beauty from beyond The Wall.
The question is, is she pretty down south in the real world, or is she pretty because she’s the only girl here?
Come on love, give us a kiss.
If you were my girl I wouldn’t leave you alone.
Come on, give him a kiss.
We just want a little affection.
Look at his hands shaking.
Come on! Come on!
You lying sack of shit! Killed a White Walker, eh? You fatass fuck. Come on, get up. (to Gilly) He’s your hero. He’s a hog!
Stubborn little wilding bitch aren’t ya?
Roll over. Jon Snow’s not here to save you.
Well, no. We haven’t been able to cure that one yet. This is last week’s.
Brought it down from 70 yards. One shot.
So do you do much hunting up at the wall?
Mm-hmmm. Mostly deer or is it elk as well?
There’s no such thing.
You will have to kill me too.
I'm Dickon Tarley, son of Randyll Tarley.
I will not.
We have teams of men collecting it and more farmers in the Reach.
My Lord.
Ser Jaime.
Dickon.
It was glorious.
All my life we've been pledged to House Tyrell.  I knew some of those men.  I hunted with them.
I didn't expect it to smell like that.
Ser, Jaime.
Dickon.
We said we’d fight with you, King Crow, when the time comes and we meant it, but this isn’t what we agreed to. These aren’t White Walkers. This isn’t an army of the dead. This isn’t our find.

Remember Mance’s camp? It stretched all the way to the horizon. And look at us now. Look what’s left of us. And if we lose this, we’re gone. Dozens of tribes, hundreds of generations. Be like we were never there at all. We’ll be the last of the free folk.
I hear the dragon burnt up a thousand Lannister men.
Archie, was it?
Tall handsome William?
They said what's left of him could fit in a wine glass.
The ginger?
How does he sleep with no eyelids?
How are we gonna stop that?
Jon Snow's not Lord Commander.
Mother save me. Father save me.
No, please.
I wish you a safe journey home.
Forgive us, we started without you. Please, sit.
And rather than send a raven or speak to me directly you decided to enter my country in secret and abduct our guest by force.

We can’t behead you. Many in Dorne want war. But I’ve seen war. I’ve seen the bodies piled on the battlefields. I’ve seen the orphans starving in the cities. I don’t want to lead my people into that hell.
And that is precisely what we are doing. (raises glass) Let us drink to Tommen, the first of his name, the King of the Andals and the First Men, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms.
King Tommen insists on his sister’s return to the capital?

I cannot disobey my King’s command. (Ellaria glares at Doran) She will return with you to King’s Landing. And my son, Prince Trystane, will accompany you both. If the alliance between Dorne and the Iron Throne is to continue, their engagement must stand.

One more thing. My brother was named to the small council before his death. Your father understood the importance of keeping Dorne in the loop. With Oberyn gone, Trystane will take his place on the small council.
You are mother to four of my nieces, girls I love very much. For their sake, I hope you live a long life. Talk to me that way again and you won’t.
Tell me, Ser Jaime, in King’s Landing, how do they punish a commoner who strikes a prince?
Prince Trystane must learn judgement if he’s to rule one day. I’ll let him decide.
: I didn’t realize there were knights of the Blackwater.
Perhaps some soup instead?
Your rebellion is over. You can swear your allegiance to me now, or you can die.
I believe in second chances. I don’t believe in third chances.
They make a lovely couple. A Lannister and a Martell. They have no idea how dangerous that is. We must protect them.
You have not had to use that axe of yours in a very long time. I hope you remember how.
Captain. Let her pass.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Oberyn was slain during a trial by combat. By law, that is not murder.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: You don't have to remind me. He was my brother long before he was anything to you.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I will bury him. I will mourn for him.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: You would have me go to war?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Then we are lucky the whole country does not decide.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I loved my brother. And you made him very happy. For that you will always have a place in my heart. But we do not mutilate little girls for vengeance. Not here. Not while I rule.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I always envied Oberyn. He lived. He truly lived. Sailed around the world. Fought men from every country. Lay with the most beautiful women alive.
And men. He experienced everything, while I sat here in Dorne trying my best to keep my people alive and well fed. But that is life. We each have our roles.
Oberyn was born to be an adventurer. And I was born to rule.
The Princess Myrcella…
My son Trystane…
Your mother is a brilliant woman, you know that?
Khaleesi, please. He said you'd never leave Qarth alive.
Please, Khaleesi, I beg you.
I beg you, please!
Please! Please!
You have the honor of addressing Daenerys of the House Targaryen.
Khaleesi! Stop him!
Yes, Khaleesi.
Did you see the dress Xaro had made for you? They say he's the wealthiest man in Qarth.
And if Qarth is the wealthiest city in Essos
(chuckles) You would look like a real princess in Xaro's
Your Grace?
They call you the last dragon...
You have dragon's blood in your veins?
What happened to the dragons? I was told that brave men killed them all.
I have always wanted to see a dragon. There is nothing in the world that I would rather see.

They can fly. And wherever they are, just a few flaps of their wings and they're somewhere else... Far away. And they can kill. Anyone or anything that tries to hurt them gets burned away to nothing... melted... like so many candles. Seeing a dragon would make me very happy.
I was not locked in. I have seen things.

I've seen... A man from Asshai with a dagger of real dragonglass. I've seen a man who could change his face the way that other men change their clothes. And I've seen a pirate who wore his weight in gold and whose ship had sails of colored silk. So... Have you seen one?
A dragon.
What happened to the skulls?
That's very sad.
No, your Grace. To teach your sister.
Forgive me, Khaleesi. I did as you asked.
Khaleesi!
A trader from Garth told me that dragons come from the moon.

He told me the moon was an egg, Khaleesi, that once there were two moons in the sky. But one wandered too close to the sun and it cracked from the heat. Out of it poured a thousand thousand dragons and they drank the sun's fire.
Men like to talk when they're happy. Before your brother bought me for you, it was my job to make men happy.
I was nine when my mother sold me to the pleasure house.
I did not touch a man for three years, Khaleesi. First you must learn.
Yes.
No.
No, Khaleesi. You must look in his eyes always. Love comes in at the eyes. It is said that Irogenia of Lys could finish a man with nothing but her eyes.

Kings traveled across the world for a night with Irogenia. Magisters sold their palaces. Khals burned her enemies just to have her for a few hours. They say a thousand men proposed to her and she refused them all.

You will make him like it, Khaleesi. Men want what they've never had. And the Dothraki take slaves like a hound takes a bitch. Are you a slave, Khaleesi? Then don't make love like a slave. Very good, Khaleesi. Out there he is the mighty Khal, but in this tent, he belongs to you.
If he wanted the Dothraki way, why did he marry you?
What did your brother say about them, Khaleesi?
He didn't tell you what kind of meat?
The prince's health forces him to remain at Sunspear. He sends his brother Prince Oberyn to attend the royal wedding in his stead.
Will he?
Arrived before dawn. Not a man for welcome parties, our prince.
Aye.
Anha afichak mae m'arakhoon!
What do you sell?
You’re not merchants.
Get the others.
Only eighteen goats and eleven sheep.
We know who you are. I remember you eating the stallion’s heart. Why didn’t you come to us after Khal Drogo died?

You were the wife of the Great Khal. You thought he would conquer the world with you at his side. He didn’t. I was the wife of the Great Khal. Khal Savo. I thought he would conquer the world with me at his side. You’re young. We were all young once. But we all understand the way things are. You will learn as well, if you are fortunate enough to stay with us.

Yes. Immediately after the death of their khal. But you went out into the world. That is forbidden. All the khalasars have returned for the Khalar Vezhven. They will decide which cities will be sacked and which tribes will be enslaved. And now they must decide what to do with Khal Drogo’s silver-haired widow. With luck, your place will be here with us, Mother of Dragons. It is the best you can hope for, now.
Go.
Walk up!
Kneel.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Let Theon, your servant, be born again from the sea as you were. Bless him with salt. Bless him with stone. Bless him with steel.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: What is dead may never die. But rises again harder and stronger. Stand.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Traitor. You're a traitor.
Away, degenerate. Away. Away.
Who's got the gold now, Stark?
Challenge me, ruffian.
I'm drowning. I'm drowning.
Where else to sit? Tried to walk back to my hut, hurt too much. Then I remembered they burned my hut down.
I stopped asking a while ago.
Doesn't seem so.
Of what? I know. Time to go. Take matters into my own hands. The thought has occurred to me.
Habit.
Maybe nothing is worse than this.
Who are you?
You her father?
A fair exchange, that is. Always held to the notion of fair exchange in all my dealings. You give me, I give you. Fair. A balance. No balance anymore. Could I have a drink? Dying is thirsty work.
Wish it were wine.
Another visit? lt seems you're my last friend.
On her knees begging for me. Hm! Did you laugh with the others?
l don't know what you want. l've given up trying to guess.
Huh. Can you free me from this pit?
What do you want? Tell me. No riddles, no stories. Tell me, what do you want?
Robb? He's just a boy.
Stannis Baratheon is Robert's true heir. The throne is his by rights.
You want me to serve the woman who murdered my king, who butchered my men, who crippled my son?

You think my life is some precious thing to me? That l would trade my honor for a few more years of... of what? You grew up with actors. You learned their craft and you learnt it well. But l grew up with soldiers. l learned how to die a long time ago.
Baelor!

I am Eddard Stark, Lord of Winterfell and Hand of the King. I come before you to confess my treason in the sight of Gods and men. I betrayed the faith of my King and the trust of my friend Robert. I swore to protect and defend his children, but before his blood was cold I plotted to murder his son and seize the Throne for myself. Let the High Septon and Baelor the Blessed bear witness to what I say: Joffrey Baratheon is the one true heir to the Iron Throne, by the grace of all the Gods, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm.
Your pardon, your Grace.
She did nothing I did not command.
I am the King’s Hand -
He butchered my men.
Jaime has fled the city. Give me leave to bring him back to justice.
If we don’t act… there will be a war.
And what about Jaime Lannister? What about Jaime?
As you command your Grace. With your leave, I will return to Winterfell and set matters straight.
The hunt?
The Targaryen girl -
These men, were they flying a sigil? … A banner.
You’re describing Ser Gregor Clegane.
I cannot give you back your homes or restore your dead to life. But perhaps I can give you justice in the name of our king, Robert. Lord Beric Dondarrion.
You shall have the command. Assemble 100 men and ride to Ser Gregor’s keep.

In the name of Robert of the House Baratheon, the First of his Name, King of the Andals and the First Men, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm, I charge you to bring the king’s justice to the false knight Gregor Clegane and all those who shared in his crimes. I denounce him and attaint him. I strip him of all ranks and titles of all lands and holdings, and sentence him to death.
Grand Maester Pycelle.

Send a raven to Casterly Rock. Inform Tywin Lannister that he has been summoned to court to answer for the crimes of his bannermen. He will arrive within the fortnight, or be branded an enemy of the crown and a traitor to the realm.
Then how come Robert is king and not Tywin Lannister?
I’m sending you both back to Winterfell.
Listen -
What? No.
This isn’t a punishment. I want you back in Winterfell for your own safety.
When you’re old enough, I’ll make you a match with someone who’s worthy of you, someone who’s brave and gentle and strong -
Go on, girls. Get your septa and start packing your things.

“Lord Orys Baratheon, black of hair.” “Axel Baratheon, black of hair.” “Lyonel Baratheon, black of hair.” “Steffon Baratheon, black of hair.” “Robert Baratheon, black of hair.” “Joffrey Baratheon… golden-haired.”
Does Ser Hugh have any family in the capital?
He'd never worn this armor before.
Who determines the draw?
Aye... But who holds the straws? You've done good work, Sisters.
I'm glad we never met on the field, Ser Barristan... as is my wife. I don't think the widow's life would suit her.
My father once told me you were the best he'd ever seen. I never knew the man to be wrong about matters of combat.
And that lad... He was a squire until a few months ago. How could he afford a new suit of armor?
Yes. That will never happen.
If the King got what he wanted all the time, we'd still be fighting a damned rebellion.
You're too fat for your armor.
You're torturing the poor boy.
The breastplate stretcher?
Maybe you should have one invented.
You have no business jousting. Leave that for the young men.
And who's going to hit you back?
There's not a man in the Seven Kingdoms would risk hurting you.
Aye.
I'm not thirsty.
Your squire... A Lannister boy?
Robert?
Where's Arya
Hey.
Ser Loras rides well.
He'll never walk again.
So they say.
I've been in the capital a month. Why have you waited so long to tell me this?
So why do you trust me now?
What sort of doom does the King face?
Who gave it to him?
The squire, Ser Hugh?
If Ser Hugh poisoned him, who paid Ser Hugh?
You know I had half my guard out searching for you? You promised me this would stop.
Who did?
Oh, Arya.
Where did you hear this?
What were you doing in the dungeons?
Your name, friend?
Did Benjen send you?
Ah, we'll find recruits for you.
Know what?
Go on. We'll talk more later.
Jory, take her safely to her room.
Well?
Is it about my wife?
You're speaking of murdering a child.
You will dishonor yourself forever if you do this.
Then we're no better than the Mad King.
You want to assassinate a girl... Because the Spider heard a rumor?
Based on whose information?
Mormont? You bring us the whispers of a traitor half a world away and call it fact?
He broke the law, betrayed his family, fled our land. We commit murder on the word of this man?
The Narrow Sea still lies between us. I'll fear the Dothraki the day they teach their horses to run on water.
I will have no part in it.
And good luck to him. I thought you were a better man.
I'll go ahead with my daughters. Get them ready. Do it yourself. Don't ask anyone for help.
What can I do for you?
Why? What do you care?
I don't have the time.
Round up all the men we have and station them outside the girls' chambers. Who are your best two swords?
Find them and meet me at the stables.
Aye.
I will.
When Jon Arryn came to visit you, what did he want?
She looks healthy enough to me. The girl shall want for nothing.
What do you know of King Robert's bastards?
How many?
And Jon Arryn tracked them all down. Why?
Come.
Jory!
I remember him well.
He was taken at my command to answer for his crimes.
You kill me, your brother's a dead man.
The King's tournament. I assure you the Hand wants no part of it.
You'll get 50. Lord Baelish will see it paid for.
You found money for a champion's purse, you can find money to keep the peace. I'll also give you 20 of my household guards till the crowds have left.
The sooner this is over, the better.
I'm sure the tourney puts coins in many a pocket. Now... If there's nothing else, my Lords?
I've been hoping to talk to you about Jon Arryn.
Why?
What did Jon want the night before he died?
A book? What book?
I'd like to read it.

"Harkon Umber, first of his name, born to lord Hother Umber and lady Amaryllis Umber in the 183rd year after Aegon's landing, at the last hearth. Blue of eye, brown of hair and fair complected, died in his 14th year of a wound sustained
Did Jon Arryn tell you what he wanted with it?
Jon's death...
Did he say anything to you during his final hours?
"The seed is strong"? What does that mean?
And you're quite certain he died of a natural illness?
Poison.
I've heard it said that poison is a woman's weapon.
Everybody knows that.
I've taken enough of your time.
Thank you. I'll find my own way out.
It's a hard fall down these steps.
Cats? Syrio says…
He's right about that.
He needs to get his strength back first.
No. But someday he could be Lord of a holdfast or sit on the King's Council. Or he might raise castles like Brandon the Builder.
You will marry a high Lord and rule his castle. And your sons shall be knights and princes and lords.
Pycelle talks too much.
Knighted for what? Why are you telling me this?
Where is Ser Hugh? I'll speak to him.
Varys or the Queen?
Yes.
Why?
Lord Baelish, perhaps I was wrong to distrust you.
A knight. They strut around like roosters down here. Even the ones who've never seen an arrow coming their way.
Let them look.
What did Lord Arryn want?
I'd like to see him as well.
This is fine work.
There's nothing to forgive. When Lord Arryn came to visit you, what would you talk about?
What kind of questions?
Your mother?
What did you tell him?
Look at me. Get back to work, lad. If the day ever comes when that boy'd rather wield a sword than forge one, you send him to me.
King Robert's bastard son.
Your Grace.
Putting my name on it doesn't make it mine.
She likes it here.
What are you doing here?
The King called on me to serve him and the realm, and that's what I'll do until he tells me otherwise.
If that's my job, then so be it.
I was also trained to kill my enemies, Your Grace.
Get the girls settled in. I'll be back in time for supper. And, Jory, you go with them.
Glad to see you're protecting the throne.
Very handsome armor. Not a scratch on it.
You've chosen your opponents wisely then.
But you just stood there and watched.
Is that what you tell yourself at night? You're a servant of justice? That you were avenging my father when you shoved your sword in Aerys Targaryen's back?
You served him well, when serving was safe.
Lord Varys.
A shame you didn't say a prayer for the butcher's son. Renly! You're looking well.
She has, Lord Baelish. I understand you knew my brother Brandon as well.
Perhaps you chose the wrong man to duel with.
Grand Maester.
And you served another King.
Without the King?
40,000 gold dragons to the champion, 20,000 to the runner-up, 20,000 to the winning archer.
Are you telling me the Crown is three million in debt?
How could you let this happen?
I will not believe Jon Arryn allowed Robert to bankrupt the realm.
I'll speak to him tomorrow. This tournament is an extravagance we cannot afford.
There will be no plans until I speak to Robert. Forgive me, my Lords. I had a long ride.
What's happening here?
Go to your room. We'll speak later. That's for you, love…
The same dollmaker makes all of Princess Myrcella's toys. Don't you like it?
It's all right. Go on.
War was easier than daughters.
Arya, open the door. May I come in? Whose sword is that?
Give it to me. I know this maker's mark. This is Mikken's work. Where did you get this? This is no toy. Little ladies shouldn't play with swords.
Come here. Now what do you want with this?
A blade with a name. And who were you hoping to skewer with Needle? Your sister? Do you know the first thing about sword fighting?
[laughs] That's the essence of it.
No, sweet girl. You didn't kill the butcher's boy.
Sansa was dragged before the King and Queen... And asked to call the Prince a liar.
Darling, listen to me. Sansa will be married to Joffrey someday. She cannot betray him. She must take his side even when he's wrong.
Well… Look at me. You're a Stark of Winterfell. You know our words.
You were born in the long summer. You've never known anything else. But now winter is truly coming. And in the winter, we must protect ourselves, look after one another. Sansa is your sister.
I don't want to frighten you, but I won't lie to you either. We've come to a dangerous place. We cannot fight a war amongst ourselves. All right? Go on. It's yours.
Try not to stab your sister with it. If you're going to own a sword, you'd better know how to use it.
My wife is in Winterfell.
You're a funny man. A very funny man.
It's too dangerous.
Until we know who our enemies are...
Littlefinger's right. I can't do anything without proof.
Then I bring it to Robert... And hope he's still the man I once knew. You watch yourself on the road, huh? That temper of yours is a dangerous thing.
He still loves you.
Off with you.
I have no choice.
You can. You must.
There's great honor serving in the Night's Watch. The Starks have manned the Wall for thousands of years. And you are a Stark. You might not have my name, but you have my blood.
The next time we see each other, we'll talk about your mother. I promise.
I've half a mind to go with you.
You should have asked me 20 years ago.
I recall a few chances.
Bessie. She was one of yours.
Wylla.
Nor will I.
The worst thing about your coronation... I'll never get to hit you again.
Daenerys Targaryen has wed some Dothraki horselord. What of it? Should we send her a wedding gift?
She's little more than a child.
Tell me we're not speaking of this.
But you can't get your hands on this one, can you?
Even a million Dothraki are no threat to the realm, as long as they remain on the other side of the Narrow Sea. They have no ships, Robert.
He will not cross. And if by chance he does, we'll throw him back into the sea.
Where is she?
Who took her?
Right, get back.
Back! Back to the inn. All back!
Are you hurt?
It's all right. What is the meaning of this? Why was my daughter not brought to me at once?
In bed asleep.
Stop it! That's enough of that.
Stop! Arya!
Gladly, your Grace.
You can't mean it.
s this your command... your Grace?
No. Jory... Take the girls to their rooms. If it must be done, then I'll do it myself.
The wolf is of the north. She deserves better than a butcher.
The butcher's boy... You rode him down?
It's moose. People shouldn't live anywhere you need to burn shit to keep warm.
The thing about Gilly that you find so interesting is that she said six words to you.
Come on.
Run!
I think they're in a bigger hurry than you were.
Now!
Oil on the ramp!
Might as well enjoy our last night, right boys? Light the fuckers up. Knock! Draw! Loose!
Drop the scythe, boys!
Hoister up!
They've had enough for one night.
Don't cheer too loud. They still outnumber us a thousand to one.
Our brothers had orders to stay at Castle Black.
I didn't say it was alright! I'm sayin' they shouldn't have been there.
She survived Craster and he was the worst shit I've ever met. She survived the long march to the wall. She survived a white walker for fuck's sake. She might've got out.
A hundred thousand.
If you're counting Black Jack, Kegs, and Mully. A hundred and two.
Whoever dies last, be a good lad and burn the rest of us. Once I'm done with this world, I don't want to come back.
Fuck the glass! We’re gonna die here.
He went off with the Halfhand. He didn't come back. A betting man would go with dead.
So did his father.
If you find something you're not supposed to, best thing to do is pretend you've never seen it.
I always imagined meself doing something much worse.
You lack imagination.
Great rangers never get old is the problem. Shit ones neither. It's them in the middle that last a long time.
I expect it's 'cause he wanted somebody to find it.
Five.
You do move quiet.
What should we do with this lot?
I’ll do my best.
Good luck.
I’m not the Lord Comman --
Yeah, ahem, close the bloody gate.
Where's Rast?
I will follow you anywhere, you know that. But they killed Grenn. And they killed Pyp. (Men mumble in agreement). They killed 50 of our brothers. I can't forget that. I can't forgive it.
(to GRENN) There's nothing more sickening than a man in love.
Before I die, please, stop talking.
Stupid. Smart people don't find themselves in places like this.
So you got to stand there waiting, wondering. One blast for friends, two for foes.
I read it in a book.
and GRENN start to walk off
Aye, we'll live another day. Hurrah.
Where you gonna go?
What are you gonna do?
I was with you at Hardhome. We saw what’s out there. We know it’s coming here. How can you leave us now?
You swore a vow.
For all nights to come.
Sorry about the food. It’s not what we’re known for.
Or the shit.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: And getting attacked or killed or worse. And that.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Oh, look. More shit. I was starting to wonder what to do with the rest of my day.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: He had no way of knowing.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: We're like the sons he never had.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Down White Harbor way.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Never knew Bannen could smell so good.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Thought you'd have blue eyes by now.
We were guests of the mutineers at Craster's Keep.

They're not going anywhere. They've got Craster's food and his wives. Poor girls. Never thought they'd miss their daddy. Karl's running things now. He's the one who put a knife through Craster's mouth.
Your eyes are still brown. Is that still you in there?
That’s funny. You sure that’s still you in there?
It’s time.
We should burn the bodies.
What do you want me to do with this?
The only traitors here are the ones who shoved their knives into their Lord Commander’s heart.
Throw them into the cells where they belong.
All the other wildlings for a hundred leagues have disappeared. Craster’s still here.
He marries his daughters, and they give him more daughters.
Then I said, if the gods wanted us to have dignity, wouldn't make us fart when we died.
My blessed mother was holding her own when she left this world. She farted so hard, the whole bed shook.
We were having a serious discussion.
We need more potatoes. And get another sack for the sludge. (Sam begins to walk away). Get the turnips too.
We met up at the Last Hearth.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: And now our watch begins.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Thenn.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Well, if that's what it's come to, we really are fucked.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Samwell Tarly. Slayer of White Walkers. Lover of Ladies. As if we needed any more signs the world was ending.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Last man left, burn the rest of us.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: 'Course if you don't stop, you'll probably die, too.
Aye, we left you. You're fat and you're slow. We didn't want to die.
Thorne did this.
Trust? The men in this room.
I don’t care who’s sitting at the high table. Jon was my friend, and those fuckers butchered him. Now we return the favor.
We have a direwolf.
If you were planning to see tomorrow, you picked the wrong room. We all die today. I say we do our best to take Thorne with us when we go.
Bolt the door. Don’t let anyone in. I’ll be back as soon as I can.
I was born in a place like this. Later, I fell on hard times.
Craster's daughters.
I'd keep on not seeing them if I were you.
(Smirking) He don't like people messing with his wives.
He marries his daughters, and they give him more daughters, and on, and on it goes.
All the other wildlings for a hundred leagues have disappeared. Craster's still here. He must be doing something right.
You wildlings?
How do I know that's true?
Okay, come on. Let's get them inside.
Stay back, he's got blue eyes!
The Umber boy.
We rode down from Castle Black. We can double up on the horses.
You’re a delight to me, my lady.
Ho-ho!  Careful now, ladies.  Once you set that monster free, there’s no caging him again [Laughs].
Father, Smith, Warrior, Mother, Maiden, Crone, Stranger.  I am hers (I am his) and she is mine (he is mine) from this day until the end of my days.
I have your word?
A son I’ve never met born of a wife that I haven’t seen since our first night together.
Do you imagine yourself a decent person? Is that it? After you’ve massacred my family. Kept me in a cell for years. Stolen our lands.
Says the man who shoved his sword through his king’s back.
You understand. On some level you understand that you’re an evil man.
Well, that is convenient for you. You’re a fine-looking fellow, aren’t you?

Your square jaw, your golden armor. Tell me, I want to know. I truly do. How do you live with yourself? All of us have to believe that we’re decent, don’t we? You have to sleep at night. How do you tell yourself that you’re decent after everything that you’ve done?
Yeah. She should have killed you.
Do you think I care whom you admire and whom you don’t?
Oh. Oh, I see. You’re a madman.
Don’t talk about Cat!
You didn’t come here to talk about our sisters.
Edmure Tully, son of Hoster Tully, and the rightful Lord of Riverrun. I demand entry.
Command all the forces within the castle to lay down their arms. Open the gates.
Your lord has given a command.
Find the Blackfish. Put him in irons and hand him over to the Freys.
I demand entry.
He can take it as he likes. He's getting the wedding he wanted.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I don't think that's--
What?
(chuckles) No.
How old is she?
Could I see her first?
Why should I let that old ferret choose my bride for me? At the very least, I should be offered the same choice you were. I'm his liege lord.
I didn't wound him. My answer is no.
The laws of gods and men are very clear. No man can compel another man to marry.
He's wanted me for one of his daughters since I was 12. He's not gonna stop wanting it now. When I say no, he will come back and offer me a daughter of my choosing.
I had something less permanent in mind.
I'll marry her.
Maybe we should give the dogs a vote as well.
I suppose you want the crown.
Aye.
Word of this can't leave Riverrun. They were Tywin Lannister's nephews. The Lannisters pay their debts. They never stop talking about it.
It wouldn't be lying. We will bury them and remain silent until the war is done.
A hostage. Tell the Karstarks that as long as they remain loyal, he will not be harmed.
If I may, nephew, I encountered a situation with one of my lieutenants at the Stone Mill which may have some bearing--
Robb knows I meant him no disr--

My blunder sent Tywin's mad scurrying back to Casterly Rock with his tail between his legs. I think King Robb understands we're not gonna win this war if he's the only one winning any battles. No, there's glory enough to go around.
I seized an opportunity.
The Mountain was garrisoned across the river from it.
We took the fight to him. He could not withstand us.
We took hostages. Willem Lannister. Martyn Lannister.
No.
No.
But for every man we lost, the Lannisters--
I'm sorry. I didn't know.
Forgive me child. I wish you all the happiness in the world.
You have nothing to fear from us, Lady Olenna.
The Lannisters have declared war on House Tyrell. They have declared war on Dorne. We must be allies now if we wish to survive.
You’re absolutely right. I chose the wrong words. It is not survival I offer. It is your heart’s desire.
Vengeance. Justice.
What are you doing in Dorne?
No, you want to break bread with the Lannisters.
The word of a Kingslayer.
No wonder you can’t stand. You have no spine.
You write like a 7 year old.
He didn’t try to make you pay?
Then maybe he’s changed. The Queen will be thrilled to know you’ll be bringing her daughter home
You love her very much, don’t you?
I wasn’t talking about her.

You think I disapprove. Why? Because people disapprove of that sort of thing where you are from? They disapproved of Oberyn and me where you are from. Here, no one blinked an eye. 100 years ago, no one would have blinked an eye at you if you had been named Targaryen.  It’s always changing, who we’re supposed to love and who we’re not.The only thing that stays the same is that we want who we want.
I know your daughter had no part in the terrible thing that happened to the man I loved. Perhaps even you were innocent of that.
You're going to fight that?
He is the biggest man I've ever seen.
Don't leave me alone in this world.
Unbowed, unbent, unbroken. For Oberyn.
Nym, Obara.
Prince Doran will weep for your father, but nothing else. We must avenge Oberyn ourselves.
We don't need an army to start a war. (Drinks water). Queen Cersei loves her children, and we have one of them.
He's come for Myrcella. If he gets to her before we do, we lose our only chance for revenge. You must choose. Doran's way and peace, or my way and war.
Nym? (Nymeria nods). Obara?
No?
That's quite all right, lover. It's more for you.
Children.
He is a prince of Dorne. Girls and boys will line up to fuck him till the day he dies.
Call my name if you need me, lover.
My lord. My lady.
We are everywhere in Dorne. I have 10,000 brothers and sisters.
Remove myself from my path, or I'll take that long axe and shove it--
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Your brother was murdered, and you sit here at the Water Gardens, staring at the sky and doing nothing.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Your brother--
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: What will you do about his death.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: You would have me go to war?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: The whole country would have you go to war.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: The Sand Snakes are with me. They have the love of their people. They will avenge your father while you sit here in your chair doing nothing.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Oberyn is dead. And this Lannister girl skips about the Water Gardens eating our food, breathing our air. How many of your brothers and sisters do they have to kill?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: And how long will that be?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: It's called war.  You don't have the stomach for it, scurry back into hiding.
She was a Lannister.  There are no innocent Lannisters.  My greatest regret is that Oberyn died fighting for you.
How can you drink this piss?  When we reach Sunspear I'll treat you to a Dornish red, the best in the world.  Have you ever been to Dorne?
A boy in every port?
Could we get some more?
I'm developing a taste for it.
So, you're going to be Queen of the Iron Islands.
And what will you be, handsome?
Oh, I see.  Your protector.
So if someone gets too close to you --
Why are you standing all the way over there then?  A foreign invasion is under way.  It seems like you don't need a protector.
Kill us!  Get it over with!
And I suppose we're providing the Westerosi?
Dorne is with you, Your Grace.
Beautiful. But pale.
Timid. Timid bores me.
(Laughs) Not timid.
This one will do nicely.
Oh, I'm not a lady.
A lie anywhere. Why not use the right words? I'm a bastard. She is a whore. And you're what? A procurer.
Oberyn.
Oberyn, don't.
Come with me, lover.
And men.
The gods are not fools. You would have been a lousy adventurer. And Oberyn, pfft, he would have been a terrible ruler.

When was the last time you left this palace? You don’t know your own people. Their disgust for you. Elia Martell raped and murdered, and you did nothing. Oberyn martell butchered, and you did nothing. You’re not a Dornishman. You’re not our prince.
Your son is weak just like you. And weak men will never rule Dorne again.
Theon! I have your sister
Then why are you talking? You're the smallest concern here.
We don't even let your kind live in the Iron Islands, you know? We kill you at birth. An act of mercy for the parents.
Why?
Can they swim?
Good. I'm taking the Iron Fleet back to the Iron Islands.
I've been around the world. I've seen everything, things you couldn't imagine, and this this is the only thing I've ever seen that terrifies me.
I'm going back to my island. You should go back to yours. When winter's over, we'll be the only ones left alive.
I’m Euron Greyjoy.
I claim the Salt Throne.
Niece.
Nephew. Little Theon.
Heard you managed to fuck things right into the ground. Captured a castle you couldn’t keep. Got yourself taken prisoner. Even heard you have no cock.
Explains why you think a woman can be king.
A few days ago. I had some things to take care of. Long overdue.

I did. I killed him. Threw him right over a rope bridge and watched him fall. He was leading us nowhere and we would still be heading there if it weren’t for me. No one loved him. No one wanted to follow him. He led ius into two wars we couldn’t win. I apologize to you all for not killing him years ago.
Gallivanting? That the sort of thing you start to say once your dick gets chopped off?
Did the great lord of Westeros teach you words like that?
And how will she do that?
