Right idea, except I’m the one who’s going to build the Iron Fleet because I’m the one who knows how to use it. I’ve been all over the world. I’ve seen more of it than all of you combined. And across the sea there is a person who hates the great lords of Westeros just as much as we do. Someone with a large army, three large dragons, and no husband. I’m going to build that fleet and I’m going to gallivant right over and give it to Daenerys Targaryen along with my big cock.
I’m not going to seduce her. The Iron Fleet will seduce her. And together we’re going to take the Seven Kingdoms. I wasn’t born to be king. I paid the iron price and here I stand.
Where are my niece and nephew? Let’s go murder them.
Won’t be enough to save them.

Go back to your homes. Chop down every tree you can find. Quarter-saw the timber and start building. I want every man bending planks. I want every woman spinning flax for sails. Build me a thousand ships and I will give you this world.
I saw it sink beneath the waves.
The glory is yours, my queen.
She's coming for you.
I have to be honest, it's making me hard.  Did you see little Theon's face right before he jumped overboard?
Oh no!
What a twat.
My queen.  Please accept this gift on behalf of all of your loyal subjects in the Iron Islands.
I give you --
A bit more than a true friend.
There's only one reward I want.
There's nothing quite like it, is there?  The love of the people.  I suppose you wouldn't know.
Or yours.  They just like severed heads really.  Listen, if you have any advice at all I'd love to hear it when we have an hour or two to speak as brothers.
Does she like it gentle or rough?  A finger in the bum?
Shhh, shhh, shhh, not now.  We'll talk later.
This is the life.  Look at them.  Cheering for a Greyjoy.
What is dead may never die.
Has the custom changed since I’ve been gone? Aren’t you supposed to repeat the words?
I don’t mock the Drowned God. I am the Drowned God. From Oldtown to Qarth, when men see my sails, they pray.
You’re old, brother. You've had your time. Now let another rule.
They did.
I needed silence.
I am the storm, brother. The first storm and the last. And you’re in my way.
Give your uncle a kiss.
Theon!
Ah, come on your cockless hound.  I have her.  Come and get her.

The moment I was chosen Lord of the Iron Islands, they turned on me. Their own uncle. They stole my best ships and ran. Sailed the right across the world and gave them to the Dragon Queen so she could bring her armies here to attack you.

It's nothing compares to the treason you suffered at the hands of a family members, from what I hear. But still, it bothers me. Murdering them would make me feel a lot better. And since it appears that all our treasonous family members are fighting for the same side, I thought we rightful monarchs could murder them together.

I remember very well. I saw you. I heard so much talk. The best in the world. No one can stop him. I didn't believe it, to be honesty. But I must say, when you rushed through the breach and started cutting people down...it was glorious. Like a dance.
The place was getting crowded. I enjoyed watching it. I truly did.
A good thing for me. If you hadn't crushed us, I wouldn't have gone into exile. If I hadn't done that, I wouldn't be the greatest captain on the 14 seas.

You're not humble. You're the queen of a great nation. You don't care about the Iron Islands. They're nothing but rocks and bird shit and--and a lot of very unattractive people. The Iron Fleet, on the other hand, that's something else entirely. It's the greatest armada Westeros has ever seen. With the Iron Fleet, you own the seas. You can defeat the invaders of the east and the pretenders of the north and south.
Ever since I was a little boy, I wanted to grow up and mary the most beautiful woman in the world. So here I am, with a thousand ships and two good hands.
Why?
You should try it. Feels wonderful.

I don't expect you to trust me outright. You need proof of my honest intentions. In my experience, the surest way to a woman's heart is with a gift. A priceless gift. I won't return to King's Landing until I have that for you.
But we're family. The last Greyjoys left in the world. The last ones with balls anyway.
If I kill you who can I talk to? Hmm?
I've got a crew full of mutes. It gets lonely at sea.
Mm.
Then I'll sail the Iron Fleet somewhere else.
But first I'm gonna fuck the queen.
They cheated at dice. Or maybe I cheated. Someone cheated. They weren't good fighters. You won't miss them.
Am I most welcome here?
Good. As a true friend and an honored guest
I was hoping we could talk in private.
Wars sometimes last years.
How?
I've given her justice, an army and the Iron Fleet, yet she gives me no sign of affection. My heart is nearly broken.
They were lesser men.
So, how do I compare to the fat king?
Are you offended?
And the Kingslayer?
Life is boring.
Do I please the queen?
I'm going to put a prince in your belly.
[Knocking on the tables, chanting] To bed! To bed! To bed!
[Chanting] To bed! To bed! To bed!
[Chanting] To bed! To bed! To bed!
You can't!
What is dead may never die.

Before you freed me, I belonged to Master Mighdal. I was tutor to his children. I taught them languages and history. They know a great deal about your family because of me. Little Calla is only seven, but she admires you very much.
When you took the city, the children begged me not to leave the house. But Master Mighdal and I agreed that I must. So I lost my home. Now I live on the streets.
I do not mean to offend, your grace. I went to one of these places. The young prey on the old. Take what they want and beat us if we resist.
Even if they are safe, who would I be there? What purpose would I serve? With my master, I was a teacher. I had the respect and love of these children.
Your grace, I ask you to let me sell myself back to Master Mighdal.

Please, your grace, the young may rejoice in the new world you've built for them. But for those of us too old to change there is only fear and squalor. I am not alone. There are many outside waiting to beg the same-
Thank you, your grace. Thank you.

Text: (speaks Valyrian).
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: (speaks Valyrian, excitedly thanking her).
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: The lads caught a groom and two maids trying to sneak away with a stolen horse and some gold cups.
Guards! Did you see anything? Go up there. Go and see where he fell from.
Go up the battlement.
Move away. Stand back. He's dead.
Put them on the wagon!
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: There's more here.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Come on.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You're in command here?
I asked you a question.
(Pulls out rolled up paper and hands it to Yoren). I have a royal warrant. For one of these gutter rats you're transporting.

We're looking for a boy named Gendry. (Gendry and Arya look shocked). He carries a bull's head helmet. Anyone turning him over will earn the King's reward. We'll be back. With more men. (To Yoren) And I'll be taking your head home along with that bastard boy.
Spread out! Search every house, every room. Look in every room.

He is a capable young man. Strong military mind. Stern. But sternness in defense of the realm is no vice. lt's far too soon to know what manner of king he will be, but l sense true greatness on the horizon for our new King. Mmm. True greatness.
What thing?
What? Things? What?
Let me see you out, my dear.
Yes, yes. Till the next time. (Pycelle gets off the bed and stretches his limbs with ease, belying his old age and general sickly demeanor) Right.

As we sin, so do we suffer. This man has confessed his crimes in sight of Gods and men. The Gods are just but beloved Baelor taught us they can also be merciful. What is to be done with this traitor, Your Grace?
King Robert's body was still warm when Lord Eddard began plotting to steal Joffrey's rightful throne.
A child born of a traitor's seed is no fit consort for our King.
It is also the wish of His Grace, that his loyal servant Janos Slynt, Commander of the City Watch...
Be at once raised to the rank of Lord.
And granted the ancient seat of Harrenhal, and that his sons and grandsons shall hold this honor after him, until the end of time.
Treason is a noxious weed.
Treason is treason!
Brigands, most likely.
It’s the King’s Hand you’re addressing, not the king. The king is hunting.
Why should Ser Gregor turn brigand? The man is an anointed knight.
If the Lannisters were to order attacks on villages under the king’s protection, it would be -
Wel…
My lord… This… This is a drastic action. It would be better to wait for King Robert’s return.
My Lord.

I bear this girl no ill will, but should the Dothraki invade, how many innocents will die? How many towns will burn? Is it not wiser, kinder even, that she should die now so that tens of thousands might live?
This heat. On days like this, I envy you northerners your summer snows. Until tomorrow my Lord.

Lord Arryn? His death was a great sadness to all of us. I took personal charge of his care, but I could not save him. His sickness struck him very hard and very fast. I saw him in my chambers just the night before he passed. Lord Jon often came to me for counsel.
I have been Grand Maester for many years. Kings and Hands have come to me for advice since...
He came inquiring after a book.
I fear it would be of little interest to you, my Lord. A ponderous tome.
"The lineages and histories of the great houses of the Seven Kingdoms, with descriptions of many high lords and noble ladies and their children."
As I said, my Lord, a ponderous read.
He did not, my Lord. And I did not presume to ask.
Such a tragedy.
Nothing of import, my Lord. There was one phrase he kept repeating: "The seed is strong," I think it was.
The dying mind is a demented mind, Lord Stark. For all the weight they're given, last words are usually as significant as first words.
What else could it be?
A disturbing thought. I don't think it likely. The Hand was loved by all. What sort of man would dare...
Yes. Women, cravens... and eunuchs. Did you know that lord Varys is a eunuch?
Yes, yes, of course. How that sort of person found himself on the King's Council, I will never know.
No trouble at all, my Lord. It's a great honor...
I humbly beg your pardon, my Lord Stark.
How many years has it been? You were a young man.
How forgetful of me. This belongs to you, now. Should we begin?
Can the treasury bear such expense?
Lord Arryn gave wise and prudent advice, but I fear His Grace doesn't always listen.
Lord Stark. I meant to give you this earlier. So forgetful these days. A raven from Winterfell this morning.
Deepest condolences, your grace. This tragedy… I never trusted Varys. I often warned…
(Curses in Valyrian.)
The King in the North!
Expect nothing of Walder Frey and you'll never be surprised. Look.
Not in time. Tywin Lannister marches north as we speak.
Enter the Twins alone and he'll sell you to the Lannisters as he likes.
Unless there was a profit in it.
lf we do that, they'll never get back across.
Come on, pretty man.
For 30 years I've been making corpses out of men, boy.
The bloody Wall will melt before an Umber marches behind a Glover.
Oathbreaker, is it?!
I'll not sit here and swallow insults from a boy so green he pisses grass.
Your meat...is bloody tough.
One army or two, the Kings in the North threw back hosts ten times this large.
Lady Catelyn, you're a welcome sight in these troubled times.
You heard her! Move your asses! Come on, out.
You're not wasting away, are you?
No, we need to get around him and break Jaime Lannister's siege of Riverrun.
So what's it gonna be? Do we move against Jaime or Lord Tywin?
Don't worry, lad.
Are you touched, boy?! Letting him go?
Oi.
(speaking Valerian) If they fail on the battlefield, they will shape Astapor.
Samwell!
No, he's not dead.
Come on, help me get him up.
They'll kill you if they find out you've gone.
But it doesn't matter. You took the oath. You can't leave.
You can't. You said the words.
"l am the sword in the darkness – "
I thought we were coming north to fight wildlings.
Is this goat?
Well, you see a tree, let me know.
Just bloody kill me.
Two blasts is wildlings. You're not fighting them alone.
Three blasts?
The sword! Show us the sword.
Piss off.
Look at its eyes.
Brother Slynt! I've just got word that Ser Alliser needs you below. You're the most experienced man he's got, he said he needs ya.
How do you know that?
Alright.
They won't. Come on Hill! And you Cooper! You three, on me! Come on you lazy bastards!
We have to hold the gate.
Tell Jon! He's in command. *to the men behind him* Let's go!
You heard Jon, we hold the gate.
We hold the gate!
The gods aren't down here. It's the six of us, you hear me?
Night gathers, and now my watch begins. It shall not end until my death. I shall take no wife, hold no lands, father no children. I shall wear no crowns
We're digging latrine pits at the end of the world. I can't imagine anything much worse.
Well, he's better than me and a lot better than you.
What in seven hells is that?
Look.
It's been here a long time.
Dragonglass?
Why would a brother hide it here?
And we just cower in here while they slaughter our brothers?
Oh, so it's alright then?! Black Jack and Kegs and Mully chopped to pieces 'cause they broke the rules?!
We pledged to guard the realms of men.
We can't even guard Mole's Town.
And there's what, 5 of us left.
We can't defend the gate against 100,000 men.
(laughs)
Wildlings?
But if it's been a thousand years, how do you know?
Karl was a top paid cutthroat in Flea Bottom. I've seen what he can do with a knife.
(CHUCKLES) Not yet.
New moon tonight.
What the seven hells could do that to a man?
Locke said there were 11 of them.
Jon.
What in seven hells is that?
No. Yield yield yield! I yield.
Piss off.
Why didn't you get up and fight?
Why not?
A bloody coward. People saw us talking to him. Now they'll think we're cowards too.
You're too stupid to be a...
Come on here!
Where have you been?
Hit me. Go on, hit me! I yield! Yield yield. I yield.
When people talk about the Night's Watch, they never mention the shoveling.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: They tell you about honor, pardoning crimes, and protecting the realm, but shoveling really is most of it.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: But when you're not getting attacked or killed, usually you're shoveling.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: When the Lord Commander says we go, we go.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Craster's been out here surviving.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Didn't think a broke foot could kill a man.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I believe you. We'll go hunting for rabbits one day. Right now, watch and learn.
You broke my nose, bastard!
If we threw you over the Wall, wonder how long it'd take you to hit.
What're you looking at, halfman?
We'll talk later, Lord Snow.
We were held up.
Chains.
We fart when we die?
Would you look at that.
I prefer watching them come towards me.

Well, there was a milkmaid named Violet on the next farm over from where I grew up. And we were wrestling together from the time we were six years old. And then we got older, the wrestling, uh, (laughs), changed.
Well, as many times as I could.
Hey. Get up, Sam.
Get up.
If you stop, you'll die.
Help me get him up.
Help us get him up.
Just get him up!
Aye, I think so.
Your ass killed the sledge.
I just wanted you to shut up about your damn blisters.
What, he don't like people messing with his daughters?
It's beyond foul.
*in Valyrian* Sailors saw him flying over the Black Cliffs three days ago, my queen. Nothing since then.
Jorah the Andal.
You should not be here.
Our queen ordered him exiled from city.
I am sorry. Sorry I am not there to fight for my queen.
I’ll come with you. I’ll find our queen.
I am.
[In Valyrian] I trust him.
...fight and die for the masters who would never fight and die for you, or go home to your families.
Missandei. *in Valyrian* I have come to apologize.
*in Valyrian* I hope I didn't frighten you.
The lessons you give I in Common tongue, these are precious to I.
To me.
Jorah the Andal, he teaches I...he teaches me this word.
I remember nothing. Only unsullied.
Why? Why sorry?

*approaches Missandei* If the masters never cut me, I am never unsullied. I never stand in the Plaza of Pride when Daenerys Stormborn orders us to kill the masters. I never am chosen to lead the unsullied. I never meet Missandei from the Island of Naath. I am sorry I...for today. I am sorry.
*in Valyrian* So am I.
Unsullied never drink.
Rules.
Tastes like it has turned.
The Starks and the lannisters, i thought these were enemies.
That is the worst joke I ever heard.
I am soldier all my life. You think I never hear joke?
I make joke.
No more talking from you. Your talking gave us this.
You’re trying to tell me what the army should do. You do not know what the army should do.
We’ll not go to the beach. If we go to the beach, the Masters will take the pyramid. The pyramid is the only place in the city we can defend. We stay here.
We wait for them to come to us. Then we fight them.
In the name of the one true queen, Daenerys Targaryen, I sentence you to die.
These men are prisoners. It is not over until the queen's enemies are defeated.
They are breathing.
I obey my queen's commands, not yours.
"Kill all who follow Cersei Lannister." These are free men. They chose to fight for her.
He is our prisoner.
We will decide what we do with our prisoners. This is our city now.
And you will find thousands of Unsullied who believe that it is.
We do not need payment. We need justice. Jon Snow cannot go free.
You are not here to speak! Everyone has heard enough words from you.
Make your choice, then.
You cannot.
This man is a criminal. He deserves justice.
It is not enough.
(in Valyrian) Good. We sail for the isle of Naath.
(speaks Valyrian) This one has the honor
(speaks Valyrian) Grey Worm.

(speaks Valeryan) "Grey Worm" gives me pride. It is a very lucky name. The name this one was born with was cursed. That was the name he had when he was taken as a slave. But Grey Worm is the name this one had the day Daenerys Stormborn set him free.
(Valyrian) How long have I been here?
Ser Barristan?
(Valyrian) I failed him. I failed my men, I failed my queen.
This is not why. Wounded in war, there is no shame for this. I am ashamed because when the knife go in and I fall to the ground, I am afraid.
No, not death. I fear I never again see Missandei from the Island of Naath.
None.
Two, but that was the day of the pact. Since then, nothing.
For now.
My name is Grey Worm. I come from the Summer "Ills."
Summer Isles. (speaks Valyrian)
(speaks Valyrian) When they took you?
You remember your home?
(Shakes his head.) Unsullied. Always Unsullied. Before Unsullied, nothing.
I don't want return. (Speaks Valyrian.)
Kill the masters.
Missandei is teacher good, my queen.
(speaking Valyrian.)  All men must die.
(speaking Valyrian.) But I promise you, a single day of freedom is worth more than a lifetime in chains.

(speaking Valyrian.) This one is called Grey Worm. I was taken as a baby by the Masters of Astapor, raised and trained as Unsullied. Now I fight for Daenerys, the Mother of Dragons and Breaker of Chains.
(speaking Valyrian.) There are three slaves in this city for every Master. No one can give you your freedom, brothers. If you want it, you must take it.
You invite the enemy into our city?
I don’t make peace with the queen’s enemies. I kill the queen’s enemies.
Not anymore.
Do not use me for your lies.
They respect me because they know who I am. They know I am loyal.
I am loyal to my queen, not you. If you betray her work, you are my enemy.
You are wrong to trust these men.
You don’t know them. You don’t understand them. We are not human in their eyes. They look at me and see a weapon. They look at her and see a whore.
You will not use them. They will use you. That is what they do.
Half are gone.
Get to the skiff.
Now!
Missandei? Missandei!
Missandei!
We will storm the city, my queen. We will kill your enemies. All of them.
(SPEAKING VALYRIAN) Allow me this honor, mother of dragons. I will not disappoint you.
Patrol. When I am going on patrol with the Unsullied. What we see on patrol. Who we captured on patrol.
Games are for children.
We conquered Asatapor and Yunkai once. We will do it again and execute the Masters.
If we do not fight them, how can we stop them?
So we will fight them?
There are supposed to be more than this.  Many more.
Where are they?  Where are the rest of the Lannisters?
No one here. We go.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Unsullied fear nothing.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: We all guard tonight.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: We are searching for the men who burned the ships, but nobody saw anything.
If a dragon does not want to eat, how do you force him to eat?
Forgive me, my queen. A red priestess from As'shai has come to see you.
Enter.
I will see you when I return.
Missandei --
It is hard for me to say goodbye to you.
You know why.
You are my weakness.

When Unsullied are young their masters learn their fears.  One boy is scared of dogs.  One boy hates high places.  One is frightened of the ocean.  They make the boy sleep with dogs or climb a cliff.  They throw him in the water.  If he learns to swim, good.  If he drowns, good.  Either way, strong Unsullied.  But I had no fears.  I was never the biggest, never the strongest, but I was bravest always.
Until I meet Missandei from the Isle of Naarth.  Now I have fear.
No.

When Daenerys takes her throne there will be no place for us here. I am loyal to my queen. I will fight for her until her enemies are defeated, but when the war is over and she has won, do you want to grow old in this place? Is there nothing else you want to do, nothing else you want to see?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Then I will take you there.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: My people are not peaceful. We will protect you.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: (in valerian) You are not a smart man, Daario Naharis.
White Rat, your grace.
Missandei hin Naath.
Why do you come?
I do not know. I must go.
You men have a choice:
Long may she reign.
No one knows.
Horse approaching! Halt! Halt!
You've got some questions to answer.
Come on, time to kill some wolves.
Who would pass the Bloody Gate?
Then I offer my condolences. Lady Arryn died. Three days ago.
Aye, ser!
We found the Blackfish, my lord.
He died fighting, my lord.
Shh!
My lady, our orders
Go on, move it.
And you. Come on.
Keep him safe!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Lord Petyr Baelish.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Yes, my lord. Come, quickly.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Hold on!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Pull back!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Through the door.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Get back!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Come in! Come in!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Watch out. Hold them back.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: And where are you going?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: What?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Clear the court!
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: This man says he is your friend, Khaleesi.
Your people can't fight.
Welcome to King's Landing, Lady Stark. Would you mind following us?
We've been instructed to escort you into the city.
Follow me, Lady Stark.
Lady Sansa, at the gate.
Ser Loras Tyrell, my lady.
Lady Tyrell, my king.
That's far enough.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: After her!
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Come on.
Guard: Halt. Down.
It's Lady Margaery, Your Grace.
Would you like to speak with her, Your Grace?
It's got to be the Mountain. He's the biggest. He's the strongest.
If the bull had fangs and claws, I would. Right, the Mountain or our man Jaime.
Loras Tyrell?
I don't care about pretty. He's better with a sword than any of them.
They get spooked by their own shadows.
No.
Oh. Oh, you're a right little prick. You should see your face.
There is something out there.
Rennick.
Rennick!
Hold them back!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Come on.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Fall back! Keep him safe!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Come on.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Guard #2: Halt. Down.
Push hard.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: *to Theon* No closer. Who are you?
Off with you. No begging.
D'you want a smack on your ear to help you with your hearing?
You want your father, boy? He's lying on the floor of some tavern, getting pissed on by his friends.
You there.
Is that your boat?
Are you joking?
Apiece.
What's in the boat?
Mmm.
Or hams.
Where did you get that scar?
I want to fuck the Queen, for all the good it does me.
The docks are that way.
You must be even older than you look.  15.
Wine?
I like wine.
What?
Oi, Dwarf!
That dwarf --
We was looking for a dwarf with a scar like that awhile back.
Arrangement?  You gonna arrange to pay us more than Queen Cersei?
Who goes there?
He’s the Lord of Riverrun. I have to obey his commands.
He’s my lord, my lord. I’ve sworn to serve the Lord of Riverrun and obey his commands.
My lord has given an order.
You are not lord of this castle, my lord.
Lower the drawbridge!
Welcome home, my lord.
Find him.
What's she got boiling? Wait. Wait. Let me guess.
Potatoes. Well, no one boils a potato better than your mom. Has she got...
No, my lord.
They’re here, my lord.
But they’re already here, my lord.
Here, my lord.
They weren’t easy to carve. Especially Black Walder.
To tell Her Grace.
Your Grace, I’m sorry to disturb you. A ship from Dorne has sailed into the harbor.
She doesn't belong here. And no matter how many of your traitors call her "Mhysa", she will never be your mother.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Load up. Load up, lads. Go on. Tide's coming in.
Your sister--
She's dead. Even if Euron hasn't cut her throat yet, she's dead.
She's your sister
So, why in fuck's name should we listen to you? They say the dead can't swim. We're going to sail east, find a nice, quiet island, kill all the men, and take their wives for ourselves.
Who says we are?
Run away, little Theon. It's what you do best.
Stay down or I'll kill you!
(panting) I said stay down or I'll kill you!
Yes, Your Grace. A few died in transit.
Two thousand.
Uh, no elephants, Your Grace.
They are excellent beasts, Your Grace, but not well-suited to long sea voyages.
We look forward to fighting on your behalf, Your Grace.
Take care, my lord.

Oh, I have not conducted this experiment. It could well be true. The substance burns so hot, it melts wood, stone, even steel, and, of course, flesh. The substance burns so hot, it melts flesh like tallow.
After the dragons died, wildfire was the key to the Targaryen power.
Our order does not deal in pig shit. The substance is fire given form. And we have been perfecting it since the days of Maegor.
The jars are put in catapults and flung at the enemy.
My lord, this man is insulting.
He would not dare insult my order whilst Aerys Targaryen lived.
We have been working tirelessly, day and night, ever since your royal sister commanded us to do so. Our present count stands at 7,811. Enough to burn Stannis Baratheon's fleet and armies both.
I have just the one. Anara our most expensive girl, but well worth it, I assure you.
Of course. Of course.
Who let this girl in? Shoo shoo!
Of course.
I’ll have you whipped, girl! Go on!
Brea.
Hodor.
You may now cloak the bride and bring her under your protection.
Your Grace, Your Grace, my lords, my ladies, we stand here in the sight of gods and men to witness the union of man and wife. One flesh, one heart, one soul, now and forever.
May the Seven guide the princess on her journey. May the Mother give her health. May the crone give her wisdom. May the warrior give her courage.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: May the Warrior grant him courage and protect him in these perilous times. May the Smith grant him strength that he might bear this heavy burden. And may the Crone, she that knows the fate of all men, show him the path he must walk and guide him through the dark places that lie ahead. In the light of the Seven, I now proclaim Tommen of the House Baratheon First of His Name. King of the Andals and the First Men and Lord of the Seven Kingdoms. Long may he reign!
Special day.
Yes, my lord. All of you.
Thank you, Father.
The Maiden.
And the Stranger.
Yes, yes.
I am the High Septon of the -
(To Cersei) Your Grace, (To Pycelle), Grandmaester, (To Mace) Lord Tyrell, (To Qyburn), uh…

As the High Septon of the Faith of the Seven I give voice to the will of the Gods and am their foremost servant in this world. An insult to me is an insult to the Gods. An assault on my person, is an assault on our very religion.
I was, by those fanatics who call themselves ‘sparrows’. They humiliated me, they they beat me, they left me naked and bleeding on the cobblestones. I am lucky to be alive.
I tend to both the highest born, and the lowliest amongst us. Even prostitutes may earn the mercy of the Mother.

Justice! I ask that you protect our Faith by arresting these criminals and throwing them in the black cells. I ask that you execute their leader, this so-called High Sparrow. He's a threat to everything we hold sacred. If he goes unpunished -
Let it be known that Margaery of House Tyrell and Joffrey of the Houses Lannister and Baratheon are one heart, one flesh, one soul. Cursed be he who would seek to tear them asunder.
You wish to make a confession?
Ser Loras Tyrell. Are you prepared to stand trial and profess your guilt or innocence before the Seven?
To which crimes will you be confessing?
The gods’ judgment is fierce, but also fair. The Warrior punishes those who believe themselves beyond the reach of justice. But the Mother shows her mercy to those who kneel before her.
You understand fully what this means?
Brother Loras…
...I ask you to dedicate your life to the seven gods.
Will you fight to defend your faith against heretics and apostates?
I’ve kept my word. Once the queen mother’s trial is concluded, Brother Loras is free to leave.
It appears the queen mother doesn’t wish to attend her own trial.
You have nothing to fear, Your Grace. The trial will begin shortly.
If the accused is not here, she will be tried regardless. We cannot escape the justice of the gods--
It’s not as good a name as Queen of Thorns, I’ll admit.
Your Grace. What are we reading today?

You learn quickly. There are some who know every verse of the sacred text, but don’t have a drop of the Mother’s mercy in their blood and savages who can’t read at all who understand the Father’s wisdom.
They are hard to love.
The poor disgust us because they are us, shorn of our illusions. They show us what we’d look like without our fine clothes. How’d we smell without perfume.
Can I ask you about a personal matter?
The king mentioned that since your reunion, you haven’t joined him in the marriage bed.
You have a duty, Your Grace. To your husband, your king, your country, to the gods themselves.
Congress does not require desire on the woman’s part, only patience. The king must have an heir if we are to continue our good work.
Hard to find and harder still to walk upon. But you’ve made great progress. I only pray your grandmother follows your lead.
Yes. The Queen of Thorns is a remarkable woman, a strong woman, and an unrepentant sinner. You must teach her the new way as she taught you the old. Or I fear for her safety, body and soul.
You are aware of the rumors concerning you and Renly?
You’re afraid for her?

She’ll be protected the entire way. The Faith Militant are very stern with those who overstep their bounds. And Queen Margaery isn’t your mother. I suspect the gods will predispose the common people to gentleness on her behalf. She’s truly opened her heart to them.
And now she’s devoted to the gods as well. Would you like to see her?

Rich or poor, noble or common, if we win, we must atone. Margaery of House Tyrell came to us a sinner. She stood before the gods in the holy sept and lied. She turned a blind eye to her brother’s sins. She disgraces her house, the king, and herself.
Lord Tyrell.
Ser Jaime.
I don’t have the authority to give them to you. And you don’t have the authority to take them.
The gods don’t recognize his authority in this matter.
To die in the service of the gods would please each and every one of us. We yearn for it. But there is no call for it today. There will be no walk of atonement.
Queen Margaery has already atoned for her sins by bringing another into the true light of the Seven.
Together we announce a new age of harmony. A holy alliance between the Crown and the Faith.
No. (Smiles).
I could say that our minds are temples to the Seven and should be kept pure. But the truth is, (Chuckles), I don't like the taste.
(After a moment). How may I serve?
Wars teach people to obey the sword, not the Gods.
Well, the Faith Militant was disarmed more than two centuries ago.
An army that defends the bodies and souls of the common people.
An honor I never expected. Or indeed, ever wished for.
All sinners are equal before the Gods.
May the Father judge him justly. (Smiles).
If I were to let you leave right now, where would you go? What would you seek out?

Of course, but for you, that means seeking out money, finery, power. Seeking out your family means seeking out sin. I’m not maligning you. I sought those things out, too. To the exclusion of all else. My father was a cobbler. He died with I was young and I took over his shop. He was a simple man and he made simple shoes. But I found that the more work I put into my shoes, the more people wanted them. Fine leather, ornamentation, detailing, and time. Time most of all. Dozens of hours spent on a single pair.

Yes. I imagine you’ve worn a year of someone’s life on your back. The highborn liked to cover their feet with my time and they paid well for the privilege. I used their money to buy a taste of their lives for myself. Each time I indulged, I felt myself ascending to something better.
You know The Seven-Pointed Star.

Yes, yes, she does enjoy reading at people. You’re close. But it wasn’t a graveyard. It was a feast. I bought old fine wine and young pretty girls and invited my friends to come share it all. We passed around the wine, passed around the women, and soon we fell into a stupor. I woke before dawn. I could barely stand. Everyone else was asleep on the couches or on the floor, lying in heaps next to their fine clothes. The truth of their bodies laid bare. I could smell them beneath the incense and the perfume and the fine food that had already started to turn. And I saw it with perfect clarity. I saw what my sins were. The gold I had, the wine I drank, the women I used, my ceaseless struggle to maintain my position. It was all part of a story. A story I was telling myself about who I was. A collection of lies that would disappear in the light. The people I was trying to climb away from, the beggars in the street, the poor, they were closer to the truth than I ever was.
I left to go and find them. I didn’t even put on my shoes. I walked out the door and never went back.
Come, let’s go and see him.
Your brother.

High Sparrow? Sounds ridiculous, doesn't it? Like Lord Duckling, or King Turtle. (Laughs). So, it's meant to. We're often stuck with the names our enemies give to us. (Prepares soup for the next person in line). The notion that we are all equal in the eyes of Seven doesn't sit well with some, so they belittle me.
Seven blessings to you, my dear. It's only a name, quite an easy burden to wear. Far easier than hers. (Prepares soup for the next person in line).
Because I gave them away to someone who needed them more. We all do that. It stops us from forgetting what we really are.
Everyone? I have a hard enough job reminding myself. Oh, I tell them no one's special. And they think I'm special for telling them so.
It would be comforting to believe that, wouldn't it? The Gods sent you here to tempt me? I hope not. I had assumed you'd only come here to arrest me. For that incident with the High Septon.
Hypocrisy is a boil. Lancing a is never pleasant. Although, they could have been more careful with the blade.
I wouldn't presume to know your thoughts on the matter.
Oh, your Grace.
Oh, I'm, sorry, your Grace. That’s not possible. Not yet.
When she's fully atoned for her sins.
No. She must stand trial before seven septons, so we can learn the true extant of her sins.
You are.
It means a great deal to me. The crown and the faith are the twin pillars of the world. Do you know who told me that? Your mother.

How do you think the Mother Above first came to us? How did men and women first come to feel the Mother’s presence? It was through their own mothers. There is a great deal of falsehood in Cersei, you know that. But when she speaks of you, the Mother’s love outshines it all. Her love for you is more real than anything else in this world. Because it doesn’t come from this world. But you know that. You’ve felt it. You’ve seen her when she talks to you.
It’s a great gift. One I never had. Envy. One more thing for me to atone for.
You Grace, may I? Do you mind? It's my knees.
When your mother made her walk of atonement, she did it to get back to you.
It’s not what I want. It’s what the gods want. They make their will known to us and it’s up to us to either accept or reject it.
Please.
If we’re to be just and good, then we accept it, all of us, even kings. A true leader avails himself of the wisest council he can. And no one is wiser than the gods.
The gods worked through him whether he knew it or not. As they work through your mother. There’s so much good in all of us. The best we can do is to help each other bring it out.
Your Grace. Lord Commander.
The gods wait for Queen Margaery to confess her crimes and seek their mercy. The king must wait as well.
On the contrary. I fear a great deal. The Father, the Mother, the Warrior.
Do you know why we use these stones? To remind us not to fear death. We close our eyes on this world and open them on the next.
In truth, I fear that, too.
Your sister sought the gods’ mercy and atoned for her sin.
You would spill blood in this holy place?
Go on, then. I deserve it. We all do. We are weak, vain creatures. We live only by the Mother’s mercy.
I don’t. They’d never reach me before you struck.
No doubt many of us would fall. But who are we, hmm? We have no names, no family. Every one of us is poor and powerless. And yet together, we can overthrow an empire.
Septa Unella.
Our other guests need you.
Septa Unella can be overzealous at times. I’ll speak with her.

It isn’t Ser Loras that brings me here. Your husband misses you dearly. The love between a man and wife is sacred. It reflects the love the gods have for all of us. But sin leads us away from the sacred. And only confession can purge sin.
So you believe you are pure? Perfect? Wholly without sin?
You have started down the path. But you have many miles to go..
Go to the Red Keep and show her the way.
Thank you. (to Cersei) You saw her?
There.
Just making sure everything’s in order.
They’re waiting for you. Clap your hands.
Kings and queens never bet on the games. Perhaps you should go find someone who does.
I have spent much of my life in Meereen, and in my experience, larger men do triumph over smaller man, far more often than not.
You don’t approve?
I’m not talking about myself. I’m talking about the necessary conditions of greatness.
That is a vital part of the great city of Meereen. Which has existed long before you arrived and will remain standing long after we have returned to the dirt.
At your command?
How many people will die to make this happen?
So your reasons are true, and theirs are false. They don’t know their own minds, but you do.
Your Grace--
She cannot.
Your Grace, Your Grace, come with me! I know a way out! I know a way--
For generations, in the days leading up to the great games, it has been customary for our ruler to make the rounds to the lower pits to pay the fighters there the honor of her presence.
Your Grace, it’s a tradition for the queen to stay until the victor has emerged.
Queen Daenerys. Tales of your beauty were not exaggerated.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Mine is one of the oldest and proudest families in Meereen.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: My father, one of Meereen's most respected and beloved citizens, oversaw the restoration and maintenance of its greatest landmarks. This pyramid included.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You have, Your Grace. You crucified him. I pray you'll never live to see a member of your family treated so cruelly.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: My father spoke out against crucifying those children. He decried it as a criminal act, but was overruled. Is it justice to answer one crime with another?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: What's done is done. You are the queen and I am a servant of Meereen. A servant who does not wish to see its traditions eradicated.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: The tradition of funeral rite. Proper burial in the Temple of the Graces. My father and 162 noble Meereenese are still nailed to those posts, carrion for vultures, rotting in the sun.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Your Grace, I ask that you order these men taken down so that they might receive proper burials.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Your Grace, I cannot defend the actions of the masters. I can only speak to you as a son who loved his father. Let me take his body down. Let me have him brought to the temple and buried with dignity so that he might find peace in the next world.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Thank you, my queen.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I am so sorry, my queen. He was a good man.
But... I'm the leader of my family.
No, Your Grace! I had nothing to do with this. Your Grace!
(Valyrian) All men must die.
My, my queen, please do not do this.
I did not want to die a coward. Apparently I do not want to die at all. (Sniffles).
Yes, my queen.
All men must die. But not all can die in glory.

Why else do men fight? Why did your ancestors cross the Narrow Sea and conquer the Seven Kingdoms? So their names would live on. Those who find victory in the fighting pits, will never become kings, but their names will live on. It's the best chance they'll ever have.
Your Grace, today is the traditional start of the fighting season.

Traditions are the only thing that will hold this city, your city, together. (Shots of Sons of the Harpy walking through tunnels with torches). Without them, former slaves and former masters have nothing in common. Nothing but centuries of mistrust and resentment. I can't promise this is the answer to all our problems, but it's a start.
And the information you did get, he is young and poor.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Why should he want to bring back slavery? What did it do for him?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: And how do you know this?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I don't know it, and I'm the head of a great family.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: They did ask for some concessions.
Politics is the art of compromise, your grace.
Forgive me. You’re right, of course. Still, it’s easier to rule happy subjects than angry ones.
They do not ask for the return of slavery. They ask for the re-opening of the fighting pits.
In the new world that you’ve brought to us, free men would fight free men.
The pitfighters that you liberated plead for this opportunity. Bring some here and ask them yourself.
Opening them would show the people of Yunkai and Meereen that you respect their traditions.
If you--
Those men think they’re dying for a good reason.
She should have cut off the traitor's head in the Great Pyramid and been done with it.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: The mission to Yunkai was a resounding success. The Wise Masters of Yunkai have agreed to cede power to a council of elders, made up of both the freedmen and the former slaveholders. All matters of consequence will be brought to you for review.
Hodor. Hodor.
Hodor.
Hodor...
Hodor!
Hodor!
Hodor!
Hodor.
Hodor!
Hodor!
Hodor!
Hodor.
Hodor.
Hodor.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Hodor.
Hodor. Hodor. Hodor.
Hodor. Hodor.
Hodor.

Hodor. Hodor.
Hodor. Hodor.
Hodor! Hodor! Hodor. Hodor.
Hodor. Hodor. Hodor. Hodor. Hodor.

EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Hodor.
Hodor.
Hodor.
Hodor.
Hodor.
Hodor.
Hodor. Hodor. Hodor. Hodor.
Haven’t I always, brother?
Watch yourself, midget.
What's a gutter rat like you doing with a sword?
He ain't no squire. Look at him. He looks like a girl. l bet he stole that sword.
l could use me a sword like that.
Give it here, midget.
l knocked him down and l kicked him in the balls and l kept kicking him until he was dead. l kicked him all to pieces. You better give me that sword!
How did he find us?

You need sour cherries to make it right. And the secret is you dry the stones, and then you break them with a mallet. That's where the real flavor is. You crush 'em up real fine. And then when you're finished, you sprinkle them over the piecrust.
How would I know?
I saw him.
Ow!
They went riding out the gates a few hours ago.
On patrol. Let go of my ears.
What do you want with him anyway?
The sour cherries was all crushed up and ready.
Probably in the piecrust by now. In the oven. The nice, warm oven.
The cheese could be better. I did get some nice sausages. GENDRY What did he want us to do about those guards?
I want to go back to the kitchens.
Can I get you anything else?
Thanks. I do my best.

A good kidney pie is all about the ingredients. Flour, lard, water, eggs, milk. Easy enough. But the meat, peacetime or not, getting your hands on a good bottom round steak and calves' kidneys is not easy. I mean, some people settle for plain old beef kidneys. Got no right to cook anything, them. Oh, and the gravy. Don't get me started on the gravy. Very difficult to get right. See, a lot of people give up on the gravy. You cannot give up on the gravy. No gravy, no pie. Simple as that. That's nice armor. Are you a knight?

Oh, it's just people with armor are usually knights. Generally speaking. From King's Landing, though, aren't you? From there myself originally. Flea Bottom born and bred. What brings you to these parts?
Someone in particular? Maybe they came in here. A lot of people come in here. Just the other day we had 17--
Starks? What, like them lot from "Winterhell"?
sNo, ain't seen anyone like that. I heard they was all traitors. Don't need no traitors in here.
Sure I can't get you a bit more kidney pie?
My lady. My lord. Could I have a word?
You seem like a proper lady. Someone who could be trusted. I never met no Sansa Stark. But I know her sister... Arya.
She weren't when I last spoke to her.
Heading up north with the Night's Watch. She was all dressed up as a boy. Like your ladyship, only without the armor. Going by the name Arry.
Well--

The Lannisters took us prisoner. We escaped. The Brotherhood took us prisoner. They sold me to the innkeep. They were gonna sell Arya to her mother at Riverrun along with another prisoner. Big ugly fellow. Foul mouth and a face like a half-burnt ham. Not friendly.
That's the one. If you find her, could you give her this? She liked the last one I gave her, but, well, this one turned out better.
What kind of fire melts stone?
There's dragons here?
What's that smell?
That's him, the one who picks.
He told me he stares at him every day. That's why he doesn't get picked.
I yield! I yield!
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I yield!
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I baked some brown bread for the innkeep and she said she never had better. Told Thoros she's keeping me as a payment for all the free meals she's given him. Anyway, it's not me the Brotherhood wants. My brother ain't no king. I'm not a Stark of Winterhell.
You sure?
It's a wolf.
Yeah.
Yeah, you, too. Don't get stabbed.
Good-bye, Arry.
I'm not afraid of no battles.
I've seen lots of battles. I saw -
I saw a man kill another man just outside a tavern in Flea Bottom. Stabbed him right in the neck.
They had armor on.
So, if they got armor on, it's a battle.
What's a dyer's apprentice know about battles anyway?
(Hesitates) It's when they've got armor on.
A knight?
Well, cause he got armor on.
How'd you know?
I'm not going with them. The Brotherhood? That's who the Mountain and them lot were looking for. They'll bring us back and put rats in us.
Ary!
You think so?  The secret is browning the butter before making the dough.  Most people don't don't do that because it takes up too much time.
You've been making pies?
I can't believe you're here.  Did you meet the big lady?
The lady knight.  I figured she was a knight because she had armor on.  She was looking for your sister but I told her about you.  Did she ever find you?
What happened to you Ary?
Where are you heading?
Why?
I heard she blew up the Great Sept.  That must have been something to see.  Boom.
I can't believe someone could do that.
I thought you'd be heading for Winterfell.
No.  The Boltons are dead.
Jon Snow came down from Castle Black with a Wildling army and won the Battle of the Bastards.  He's King in the North now.
Why would I lie about that?  He's your brother right?
Friends don't pay.  I can't believe I thought you were a boy.  You're pretty.
No, I won't.  I'm like you, Ary.  I'm a survivor.
Where did you get the knife?
Is that the first man you've killed?
Next time you're going to do something like that, tell me first.
You want something?
I asked if you wanted something.
You’re paid by the Lannisters. You’re here for the bounty on me.
No? Fancy sword you’ve got there. Where’d you get it? I’ve been looking at Lannister gold all my life. Go on, Brienne of fucking Tarth, tell me that’s not Lannister gold.
You heard the girl! She’s not coming with you.

Safety?! Where the fuck’s that? Her aunt in the Eyrie’s dead. Her mother’s dead, her father’s dead, her brother’s dead. Winterfell’s a pile of rubble. There’s no safety, you dumb bitch. If you don’t know that by now, you’re the wrong one to watch over her.
Aye, that’s what I’m doing.
I’m not a knight.
You’re still here? [Coughs] Big bitch saved you.
No, not you. You’re a real killer. With your water dancing, and your needle.

Unless there’s a Maester hiding behind that rock, aye, I’m done. I’d skin you alive for wine. [Arya offers water.] Fuck water! Killed by a woman. (Chuckles) I bet you liked that. Go on, go after her. She’ll help you. Going out alone, you won’t last a day out there.

You remember where the heart is? Fuck it. I’m ready. Go on, girl. Another name off your list. You kept promising me. I cut down your butcher’s boy, the ginger. He was begging for mercy. Please sir, please don’t kill me, please, please. Bled all over my horse. Saddle stunk of butcher’s boy for weeks. And your sister, your pretty sister. I should’ve taken her. That night the Blackwater burned, I should’ve fucked her bloody. At least I’d have one happy memory! Do I have to beg you? Do it. Do it.
Kill me. Kill me! Kill me! Kill me!
You think you're a hard man?
Oh, there's women in the ground. I put some there myself. So have you. You like fucking and drinking and singing. But killing, killing's the thing you love. You're just like me.
Only smaller.
Your Lord Imp's going to miss you.
The king has asked you a question.
Let's go. Stannis is sending us fresh meat. You, too.
Any of these flaming fucking arrows come near me, I'll strangle you with your own guts.

Any man dies with a clean sword, I'll rape his fucking corpse. He and LANCEL engage with the sieging forces. They each cut down a number of Baratheon men before LANCEL is struck in the chest by an arrow. He stumbles and screams. Panting, he retreats.
Someone bring me a drink.
Fuck the water. Bring me wine.
Eat shit, dwarf.
I lost half my men. The Blackwater's on fire.
Fuck the Kingsguard. Fuck the city. Fuck the king.
The lady is starting to panic.
Not here for long. I'm going.
Someplace that isn't burning. North, might be. Could be.
He can die just fine on his own.
I can take you with me. Take you to Winterfell.
I'll keep you safe. Do you want to go home?

Look at me. Stannis is a killer. The Lannisters are killers. Your father was a killer. Your brother is a killer. Your sons will be killers someday. The world is built by killers. So you'd better get used to looking at them.
No, little bird, I won't hurt you.
I'll give you one try, girl.
Kill me and you're free. But if I live, I'll break both your hands. Go on, hit me. Hit me hard.
Sulk all you want. The truth is, you're lucky. You don't want to be alone out here, girl. Someone worse than me would find you.

Ha, you never knew my brother. He once killed a man for snoring. There's plenty worse than me. There's men who like to beat little girls, men who like to rape them. I saved your sister from some of them.
Ask her, if you ever see her again. Ask her who came back for her when the mob had her on her back. They would have taken her every which way and left her there with her throat cut open.
The Blackwater?  Where do you think I'm taking you?
Fuck Joffrey. Fuck the queen. That's the Red Fork. I'm taking you to the Twins.
Because your mother and brother will be there and they'll pay me for you.

Those outlaws you love so much never told you?  The whole countryside is yapping about it. Your uncle is marrying one of the Frey girls. So quit trying to bash my skull in and we might just make it there in time for the wedding.
I thought it'd make me happy, but it doesn't. Not really. Nothing makes you happy.
Like what?
So you're sad because you didn't get to kill Joffrey yourself. Is that it?
Aye, nothing in the world beats that look.
I wasn't the damn wine taster. Little shit deserved to die, but poison...poison's a woman's weapon. Men kill with steel.
*laughs* I'd pay good money to see that. *grunts and rubs his neck*
It's a fleabite.
Well, we won't have to walk too much further.
Aye, she'll pay.
Doesn't matter. You're her blood. Family, honor, all that horseshit. It's all you lords and ladies ever talk about.
The bloody Hound, Sandor Clegane. And his...traveling companion Arya Stark, niece of your lady Lysa Arryn.
Brave? A dog doesn't need courage to chase off rats.
No, it gives me joy to kill people.
Spare me. You can't tell me Lord Eddard Stark of Winterfell never killed a man.
Is that what he told you? He lied. Killing is the sweetest thing there is.
You'll be glad of the hateful things I do someday when you're queen and I'm all that stands between you and your beloved king.
Kick all you like, wolf girl. Won't do you no good.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Could be food.
Who were "they"?
That's not gonna get better.
A bad way to go. Haven't you had enough?
(Shakes his head.) Her captor. Bringing her to her aunt for ransom.
So do I.
That's where the heart is. That's how you kill a man.
The fuck you doing?
Guess that's what the king does when you tell him to fuck off.
And you thought you were going to collect it? Didn't think very hard, did you?
This day's really not working out the way you planned. He on your little list?
What's your name?
You're learning.
Rat cunts. Fucking whore.
No fire.
No fire!
Shut up about it. Shut up about everything. Thanks to you, I'm a walking bag of silver anywhere the Lannisters hold sway. Which is everywhere between where we are now and where we're going.

I'm as stupid as that hog you stuck back in the village, getting myself cut and stabbed and bitten. No reward is worth this much trouble. Wish I'd never laid eyes on you. You say your brother gave you that sword.
My brother gave me this. It was just like you said a while back. Pressed me to the fire like I was a nice juicy mutton chop.

Thought I stole one of his toys. I didn't steal it. I was just playing with it. The pain was bad. The smell was worse. But the worst thing was that it was my brother who did it. My father, who protected him... told everyone my bedding caught fire. You think you're on your own?
And they want the same for you.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You're all right now, little bird. You're all right.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: The little bird's bleeding. Someone take her back to her cage. See to that cut.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I didn't do it for you.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: (shouts)
(shouts)
Looks like their god likes me more than your butcher's boy.
I want my gold.
Piss on that! You're nothing but thieves.
Come try it, archer. I'll shove those arrows up your ass.
Would you shut up?
The names of every fucking person in Westeros?
(CHUCKLES) Hate's as good a thing as any to keep a person going. Better than most. We come across my brother, maybe we can both cross a name off our list.
I'd tell him to shut the fuck up so I can get some sleep. Go on, get it over with, your list of doomed men.
Go on.
The hell you doing?
What, ways to die?
They will if you Nance around like that. That's no way to fight.
Dancing? Maybe you ought to put on a dress. Who taught you that shite?
Braavos. (SCOFFS) Greasy-haired little bastard, I bet. They all are.
I bet his hair is greasier than Joffrey's cunt.
Was? He dead?
How?
Who by?
Meryn Trant? The greatest swordsman who ever lived killed by Meryn fucking Trant?
Any boy whore with a sword could beat three Meryn Trants.

The greatest swordsman who ever lived didn't have a sword? (LAUGHS) All right. You have a sword. Let's see what he taught you. Go on, do it for your Braavosi friend. Dead like all the rest of your friends.
Your friend's dead and Meryn Trant's not 'cause Trant had armor and a big fucking sword. (SCOFFS)
Your uncle left your nameday present and asked me to see that you got it.
Look inside, Your Grace.
You look like a bunch of swineherds.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You're still swineherds and tanners and masons. You think carrying a crooked spear makes you a soldier?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Beric Dondarrion? You've seen better days.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Stark deserters. Baratheon deserters. You lot aren't fighting in a war. You're running from it.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Untie these ropes and we'll find out. What are you doing leading a mob of peasants?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Ned Stark is dead. King Robert is dead. My brother's alive.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You're fighting for ghosts.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You found god? Is that it?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: If you mean to murder me, then bloody well get on with it.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I wasn't at the Mummer's Ford. Dump your dead children at some other door.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Do you take me for my brother? Is being born Clegane a crime?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I never touched the Targaryen babes. I never saw them, never smelled them, never heard them bawling. You want to cut my throat, get on with it! But don't call me murderer and pretend that you're not.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Aye, he was a bleeder.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I was Joffrey's sworn shield. The boy attacked the prince.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Then I should have killed you. Not my place to question princes.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: So, who will it be? Should we find out if your fire god really loves you, priest? Or you, archer? What are you worth with a sword in your hand? Or is the little girl the bravest one here?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You can still smell the burning bodies, and that’s where your head is at.
I'm sure you do.
Of course it's about that, you twat. Why shouldn't it be? The dead are dead. You're not.
Don't touch me.
I'm not done with my drink.
This is my drink.
There's only one thing that'll make me happy.
That's my fucking business.
Used to be you couldn't look at me.
Yes, I've heard. Heard you were broken in. Heard you were broken in rough.
How?
You've changed, Little Bird.
None of it would have happened if you'd left King's Landing with me. No Littlefinger, no Ramsay none of it.
For fuck's sake.
Not anymore. I don't like crowds.
Why not? They all love you now. You're the big hero.
It must have felt good sticking a knife in that horned fucker.
I have some unfinished business.
I don't plan on coming back.
Gonna leave me to die again if I get hurt?
You think you're good with that bow, you little twat?
A coward's weapon. I like to fight up close. I like to see a man's face when I put the steel in him.
Looks like every other shit inn on the road.
Near Fairmarket, I think.
No, I don't have a map.
Just point out the next map shop you see and I'll buy you one.
Far.
Believe me, girl, I want you there as soon as I can. Get my gold, be on my way.
Why do you care? Might book passage across the Narrow Sea. Fight as a sellsword. Second Sons, could be. Seems like a good fit for me.
Why Braavos?
I doubt it.
What do you want?
If I'm standing on it, it's my land.
You got to do all seven of the fuckers?
And we ask the Stranger not to kill us in our beds tonight for no damn reason at all.
Call that a fight? Slaughtering livestock more like.
Guest right don't mean much anymore.
You got any ale?
How can a man not keep ale in his home?
What'll you pay? I don't have much. But I have hidden a bit of silver from the bandits. Fair wages for fair work?
(Nods) Fair wages for fair work.
Get your horse saddled.
I wasn't.
Aye, he took us in. He's a good man and his daughter makes a nice stew. And they'll both be dead come winter.
I do know it. He's weak. He can't protect himself. They'll both be dead come winter. Dead men don't need silver.
There's plenty worse than me. I just understand the way things are. How many Starks they got to behead before you figure it out?
Thoros? The fuck you doing here?
Girl.
What in seven hells are you doing with the Stark bitch?
The little lady wants a pony.
Horses aren't easy to come by. Even if they were, you think I'm gonna put you on your own horse? Watch the only thing of value I've got in the world ride away?
No.
I'm not a thief.
A man's got to have a code.
You've got an aunt in the Vale. Your rich Aunt Lysa. After I sell you to her, maybe she'll have enough left over to buy you that pony you want so much.
Five horses, five men. More than I feel like killing on an empty stomach.
What the fuck's a Lommy?
Got what?
Needle? Of course you named your sword.
Lots of cunts.
What are you... get back here.
Get back here.
I don't care if he ate your friend. We're not going in there.
I could ask the same of you. What are you doing up here?
No need. The war's over.
None taken.
She's all right. I've had better.
I'm not going to King's Landing.
Fuck the king.
Here I am. Bring me one of those chickens.
You paid for it?
Not a penny.
I'll still take that chicken.
You're a talker. Listening to talkers makes me thirsty.
Ah. And hungry. Think I'll take two chickens.
I understand that if any more words come pouring out of your cunt mouth, I'm gonna have to eat every fucking chicken in this room.
Someone is.
As you can see, I'm busy.
I'm sorry. I didn't catch your name, Ser...
I see. Well, it just so happens that I am.
Thank you, Your Grace. Thank you.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: The Dothraki are not known for their punctuality.

(Greets them in Dothraki.) May I present my honored guests? Viserys of House Targaryen, the third of his name. The rightful King of the Andals and the First Men. And his sister, Daenerys, of the House Targaryen.
Come forward, my dear.
The ceremony is over.
Trust me, Your Grace. If he didn’t like her, we’d know.
It won’t be long now. Soon you will cross the Narrow Sea and take back your father’s throne. The people drink secret toasts to your health. They cry out for their true king.
Soon. The Dothraki never stay still for long.
I wouldn’t ask Khal Drogo.
I take you for a king. Kings lack the caution of common men. My apologies if I’ve given offense.
If Khal Drogo has promised you a crown, you shall have it.
When their omens favor war.
A Dothraki wedding without at least three deaths is considered a dull affair.
Dragons’ eggs, Daenerys. From the Shadow Lands beyond Asshai. The ages have turned them to stone, but they will always be beautiful.
And when he knows the truth, what will he do?
What good is war now? We're not ready. If one Hand can die, why not a second?
We need time. Khal Drogo will not make his move until his son is born. You know how these savages are.
It never was.
Please, she's a good girl.

Jaime was taught to fight with sword and lance and mace, and I was taught to smile and sing and please. He was heir to Casterly Rock, and I was sold to some stranger like a horse to be ridden whenever he desired.

EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Aye.
Khaleesi!
Khaleesi?
Oh, Khaleesi!
(Dothraki) I rewove this part of the top. And I fixed the heel on this one.
It is known.
(Dothraki) May he ride forever through the night lands.
She's not a princess. She's a Khaleesi. You should wear it, Khaleesi.
You are their guest. It would be rude not to.
Yes, Khaleesi.
Rakharo ask if you want him dead, Khaleesi.
Rakharo say you should take ear, to teach respect.
Yes, Khaleesi.
When was last time you bleed, Khaleesi? You change, Khaleesi. It’s a blessing from the Great Stallion.
The Khaleesi wants to eat something different tonight. Kill some rabbits.
Find some ducks, she likes ducks.
Dog then. I have seen many dogs.
The Khaleesi have baby inside her. It is true. She does not bleed for two moons. Her belly start to swell.
She does not want to eat horse.
Your hands.
Dragon gone, Khaleesi.
No dragon. Brave men kill them. It is known.
Moon is no egg. Moon is goddess... wife of sun. It is known.
No! (Speaking Dothraki, to Daenerys) They killed his soul!
(Speaking Dothraki) They did! They butchered him like an animal. They did not burn his body. (Daenerys kneels to Irri’s level). He can never join his ancestors in the night lands.
You beast. You beast. You killed my wife and now you’ve taken your father’s life.
Curse the day I named you.
I give you Bobono.
I give you Camello.
No laugh for Ned’s death. These people are worse than animals.
But they loved her. They all love her.
With what you’re given?
Oh, we’re all thinkers now, are we?
Full to the tits with ideas.

You have ideas, I have ideas, he has ideas. Why should my ideas have any more value than yours simply because I’ve been doing this my whole life? Who is anyone to judge my work? This is my profession. I know what I’m doing.
You have no right to an opinion.
Ahh!
Oh, murdered by a boar. The great big hairy whore. He dug in his tusks and dug out my guts and soon I am no more.
Shut up, you swine! Cersei, more wine.
Ned stark will do fine. Now bring me more wine.
I die! I die! And here I now must lie. Oh, woe. Oh, no. Oh, I am about to go.
There are no small parts. You rang very false.
No worse child ever stained this land.
Thank you. Ladies and gentlemen, I give you Bianca, Clarenzo.
And last but not least, sweet Lady Crane.
That crowd was shit.
You can do better, Bianca, dear. You must do.
Lady Stark!
Keep your hands off me, woman.
Forgive me. Lady Stark.
They caught the Kingslayer.
You're a virgin, I take it?

Childhood must have been awful for you. Were you a foot taller than all the boys? They laughed at you, called you names? Some boys like a challenge. One or two must have tried to get inside Big Brienne.
Ah. But you fought them off. But maybe you wished one of them could overpower you, fling you down, tear off your clothes. But none of them were strong enough. I'm strong enough.
Of course you are. You'd love to know what it feels like to be a woman.

Ah. "They lay with lions." Tavern girls, I'd say. Probably served my father's soldiers. Maybe one of them gave up a kiss and a feel. That's how they earned this. The glorious work of the Northern freedom fighters. Must make you proud to serve the Starks.
Tell yourself that tonight when they swing in your dreams.
What are you doing?
We shouldn't stay here. We should get back on the river.
Untie me. Now!
Apparently eating is now a crime. Who knew?
But it's not a crime to starve. That's justice for you.
For stealing a pig?
Have you been to Ashemark?
Then you don't know me.
Is it near Ashemark?
I've never been there.
I wish someone had told me. I wouldn't have had to steal that pig.
Those were Stark men.
Cersei.
You won. One fewer brother. Must be proud of yourself. There's really nothing you wouldn't do, is there?
Tyrion is your family.
You don't get to choose.
Are you really mad enough to blame him for that? He didn't decide to kill her. He was an infant.
The things I did to get back to you, to endure all that, only to find you-
Those are words.
Told him what?
You told him?
You think he'll just accept that?
What did you say?
Someone will walk in.
Is that any way to speak about our mother?
What do you think I’m doing? The galley’s waiting in the bay bound for the Free Cities.
Varys.
You have more friends than you thought.
There’s a locked door at the top of the stairs. Knock on it twice, and then twice again. Varys will open.
Farewell, little brother.
Quickly now.
Thank you.
Try not to lose it this time.

I know you didn’t want to leave Dorne. But I’m glad you’re coming home. Your mother’s desperate to see you. And I’m glad Trystane’s coming with us. He seems like a nice boy. You’re lucky. Arranged marriages are rarely so… so well arranged.
If she sees you’re happy, I’m sure she will.

I’m not so sure about that. Listen. There’s something I’ve wanted to tell you. Something I should have told you long ago. So. Now that you’ve seen more of the world, you’ve learned how complicated things can be. People can be. The Lannisters and the Martells have hated each other for years, but you’ve fallen in love with Trystane. It was an accident, really, I mean what were the chances? You happen to fall in love with the man you were assigned to marry? (Myrcella laughs) My point is… we don’t choose whom we love. We just… It’s beyond our control. I sound like an idiot.
What I’m trying to say. What I’m trying and failing to say…
No. I’m afraid you don’t.
Myrcella?
Myrcella?!
Myrcella?! Myrcella?!
You’re welcome to her.
Those two have their eyes on you.
Not my type.
Ladies, have you met Ser Bronn of the Blackwater, hero of the Battle of Blackwater Bay.
There you go.
I’m sure he would be.
Have you done much fighting yourself, Lord Frey?
No, but back in your day.
Yes, you’re a great conqueror.

They don’t fear the Freys, though. They fear the Lannisters. We gave you the Riverlands to hold the Riverlands. If we have to ride north and take them back for you every time you lose them, why do we need you?
Come on.
Prince Doran. (bows)
Princess Myrcella.
What a lovely dress.
You must be cold.
Prince Trystane. How’s your jaw?
Looking out for the safety of my niece, the princess Myrcella.
We received a threatening message. The princess’s necklace in the jaws of a viper.
Ah. Excellent. The last meal before the beheading.
I’m afraid he does.
I accept.
You have my word.
May I inquire into the fate of my man?
He said it was just a fleabite. The fault is mine. Bronn is merely a soldier following my orders. If anyone should be punished it’s me.
You’re a good man.
Prince Doran, Ser Bronn of the Blackwater.
I believe you’ve already met Prince Trystane.
Prince Trystane here is a man of mercy. He’s agreed to grant you your freedom.
There was one condition.
Your maester kindly agreed to copy it over for me.
She will.
Of course, she’s my niece.
Damn.
You're much uglier in daylight. What's your name? I'm Jaime Lannister of Casterly Rock, son of Tywin.
A captive knight has a right to know his captor's identity.

Tarth, Tarth, Tarth. Crescent moons and starbursts. Lord Selwyn Tarth. Your father. Do you have any brothers and sisters, my lady? It's a long way to King's Landing. Might as well get to know one another.
Have you known many men? I suppose not. Women? Horses? Ah.
I didn't mean to give offense, my lady. Forgive me.
Why do you hate me so much? Have I ever harmed you?
Has anyone ever told you you're as boring as you are ugly?
I already have. Look at you. You're ready to chop my head off. Do you think you could? Do you think you could beat me in a fair fight?
The answer is no. There are three men in the kingdoms who might have a chance against me. You're not one of them.
If you're so confident, unlock my chains. Let's see what happens.
I took you for a fighter, a man Pardon. Woman of honor. Was I wrong? You're afraid.
Soon.
I've never seen him fight.
Oberyn believes in himself.
Beheading.
Cousins.
Of course. Wet-nurse dropped him on his head. Left him simple.
Nothing made you happier. You'd think being tormented from birth would have given you some affinity for the afflicted.
The joke wore thin, though.
I had other interests.
Why?
Volarik. Tried to touch me once.
Every day around the world, men, women, and children are murdered by the score. Who gives a dusty fuck about a bunch of beetles?
He was twice your size.
I don't know.
Good luck today.
I never thought you’d find her. I just assumed Sansa was dead.
In my experience, girls like her don’t live very long.
Well, I’m proud of you. I am. You fulfilled your oath to Catelyn Stark against all odds.
Of course my sister wants Sansa dead. The girl is still a suspect in Joffrey’s murder, so there is that complication. What the hell are you doing here?
You’re welcome to have him.
With what army does she plan on taking Winterfell?
They’re a bit occupied at the moment. I was sent here to reclaim Riverrun currently defended by the Tully rebels, so you can see the conundrum.
Riverrun was granted to the Freys by royal decree.
Exactly.
We shouldn’t argue about politics.
I’m a Lannister. Don’t ask me to betray my own house.
What do you propose?
Why would he abandon his ancestral home?
Have you ever met the Blackfish?
He’s even more stubborn than you are.
All right. Try to talk some sense into the old goat. He won’t listen, but his men might. Not everybody wants to die for someone else’s home.
You have my word. You have until nightfall.
It’s yours. It will always be yours.
Yes, Lady Brienne?
Of course it does.
Let’s hope it doesn’t come to that.
Lord Edmure.
I apologize for the way the Freys treated you. A man of your birth deserved better. You have my word you’ll be properly fed and clothes from now on.
The Blackfish is an old man. A good death is all he can hope for. But you, you have a child now, I’ve heard. A son you sired on your wedding night. You’re a potent man.

You should be with them both. I can arrange that, you realize? Comfortable rooms for you at Casterly Rock. A tutor for your boy, knights to train him to fight and to ride. And when he comes of age, he’ll have a keep of his own.
I’ll remind you that our houses are at war. I’m sorry if this conflict has inconvenienced you, but rebelling against the Crown does have consequences.
Did I give you the impression that this was a negotiation? It’s not.
I’ll leave the judgments to the gods.
I was your sister’s prisoner once. She hit me on the head with a rock if I remember correctly.
Perhaps, but she didn’t. Catelyn Stark hated me just like you hate me. But I didn’t hate her. I admired her. Far more than her husband or her son.
No, I don’t, but I’m telling you anyway because you’re my prisoner. You don’t have a choice. The love she had for her children, I was a little awed by it. Reminded me of my sister.
I’ll talk about whomever I want.
She loved her children. I suppose all mothers do, but Catelyn and Cersei, there’s a fierceness you don’t often see.
They’d do anything to protect their babies.
Start a war. Burn cities to ash. Free their worst enemies. The things we do for love.

That’s exactly why I came here. I love Cersei. You can laugh at that if you want. You can sneer. It doesn’t matter. She needs me. And to get back to her, I have to take Riverrun. I’ll send for your baby boy.

And I’ll launch him into Riverrun with a catapult. Because you don’t matter to me, Lord Edmure. Your son doesn’t matter to me. The people in the castle don’t matter to me. Only Cersei. And if I have to slaughter every Tully who ever lived to get back to her, that’s what I’ll do.
Good. Bring him to me.
Is she the fat one?
No, no. There's only one fat Lannister. If she was your mother, you'd know it.
When?
I went to Willem Frey's wedding?
He threw up on his horse on the way to the tourney grounds. What was his name?
Poor lad. That was my brother's doing, I seem to recall. I remember you. You'd never squired for anyone before.
You didn't, though.
You knew when you were needed and when to go away. It's a rare talent. Most of my squires, they mean well, but young men with big jobs, they tend to overdo them.
You were saying?
More embarrassing than being chained to a post covered in your own shit?
I understand completely.
It's just that I I was 16 once. I also had to replace someone's squire on short notice.
Barristan Selmy. The fight against the Kingswood Outlaws. Before your time.

He was A painter. A painter who only used red. I couldn't imagine being able to fight like that, not back then. And to help him do that, to be a part of something that perfect. I don't need to explain how that felt, not to you.
It's like stepping into a dream you've been dreaming for as long as you can remember, and finding out that the dream is more real than your life.
No. I didn't have your gift. He couldn't take a step without stepping on me. I was awful, a complete liability. Until one of the outlaws decided to take on a 16-year-old squire.

It's a good thing I am who I am. I'd have been useless at anything else. I'm not well-suited for imprisonment. Shocking, I know. Some men are. Ned Stark, I imagine he made an excellent prisoner right up until the end. Not me, though. My life has left me uniquely unfit for constraint.
Of course. Every day.
Good prisoners breed good jailers, apparently. The Starks are very careful.
But there is a way, I think. It wasn't possible until now.
It's actually quite simple.
You'll only have to do one thing.
(whispers)You'll have to die.
Thank you for fighting on my behalf, Lady Stark. I would have come to your defense, but
You've become a real she-wolf in your later years. There's not much fish left in you.
Come to say goodbye, Lady Stark? I believe it's my last night in this world. Is that a woman?
Old Lord Karstark doesn't seem to like me.
Oh. Oh. Was he the one on guard duty? He was in my way. Any knight would have done the same.

So many vows. They make you swear and swear. Defend the king, obey the king, obey your father, protect the innocent, defend the weak. But what if your father despises the king? What if the king massacres the innocent? It's too much. No matter what you do, you're forsaking one vow or another. Where did you find this beast?
