Kingslayer. What a king he was. Here's to Aerys Targaryen, the Second of His Name, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms, Protector of the Realm, and to the sword I shoved in his back.
Do you know, I've never been with any woman but Cersei. So in my own way, I have more honor than poor old dead Ned. What was the name of that bastard he fathered?

No, that wasn't it. Snow, a bastard from the North. Now, when When good old Ned came home with some whore's baby, did you pretend to love it? No. You're not very good at pretending. You're an honest woman. You hated that boy, didn't you? How could you not hate him? The walking, talking reminder that the honorable Lord Eddard Stark fucked another woman.
Tomorrow.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Lord Bolton's traveling tomorrow as well. He's going to the Twins for Edmure Tully's wedding. You're to remain here.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I owe you a debt.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: (nods) I will return the Stark girls to their mother. I swear it.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: (nods)
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: My father will make him Grand Maester if he grows me a new hand.  Roose BOLTON: You will give my regards to Lord Tywin, I trust?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: (nods)  Tell Robb Stark I'm sorry I couldn't make his uncle's wedding. The Lannisters send their regards.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: What's the purpose of an arm with no hand?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Yes, we've stymied the corruption. You're a learned man.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You did well sewing up this mess. You're far better at this sort of work than Grand Maester Pycelle.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: So why did the Citadel take your chain? Did you fondle one boy too many?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: What is?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You performed experiments on living men.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: With their permission?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Dying paupers, I assume. Men with no families to complain. You found them moaning in the poorhouse and had them carted back to your garret and opened up their bellies to see what was inside.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I don't know.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: (shakes his head)
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Countless has a nice ring to it.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: A fair offer.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Why not?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: (sighs)
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: They'd be fools to kill her.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: We have to return to Harrenhal.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I've left something behind.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: And what are those orders?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You think you'll get a reward.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You think you're getting a reward. Let me explain something to you. When my father sees me, the first thing he's going to ask is what happened to my hand. And I'm going to tell him this man chopped it off.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Or I could tell him this man saved my life. We return to Harrenhal now.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Whoa.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Be quiet.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: A wooden sword?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You gave her a wooden sword.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I'll pay her bloody ransom. Gold, sapphires, whatever you want. Just get her out of there.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Get behind me.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Pull her up.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: We're taking her to King's Landing. Unless you kill me.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: What do you think is more important to Lord Bolton?  Getting his pet rat a reward or ensuring Tywin Lannister gets his son back alive?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Well, we must be on our way. Sorry about the sapphires.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I made a deal for you to keep your ungrateful head on your ungrateful neck a little while longer.
You threw your life away. You threw it away. It's not a joke. You understand that, don't you?
You fell in love with a whore.
Careful. I'm the last friend you've got.
Yes, brilliant speech. They'll be talking about it for days to come. I thought you were a realist. Didn't realize you'd die for pride.
I can't save you this time either. My training has proved that I can't beat a stable boy with my left hand.
(laughs)
It is tempting.
If he wins.
No. Not Ser Meryn.
And more hands. How’s Trystane?
I’m sorry for that. It wasn’t supposed to happen that way.
Your mother’s worried about you. Threats have been made. Dorne is too dangerous for you. I’ve come to take you home.
It's for your own good. These are complicated matters.
I don’t understand.
Someone certainly does.
You’re an anointed knight. There’s quite a difference.
You have better instincts than any officer in the Lannister army.
I expect to command all the Lannister forces before long. You can be the right hand I lost.
Lothar, is it?
‘Cause you didn’t set up a proper perimeter. You just allowed 8,000 men to approach unchallenged.
Have Lord Edmure bathed and fed.
Only a fool makes threats he’s not prepared to carry out. Now let’s say I threatened to hit you unless you shut your mouth, but you kept talking. What do you think I’d do?
I’m here by the king’s command to take back this castle. Have him bathed and fed. Unless you’d like to take his place.
The siege is now under my command. The next time the Blackfish looks out from those ramparts, he’ll see an army at his gates, not whatever this is.
As I said, the siege is under my command. If that’s not to your liking, go home.
Get word to the Blackfish. I want a parley.
He’s an old man.
Blackfish.
I don’t have them.
It won’t stop with Edmure. You’ll force me to storm the castle. Hundreds will die.
We’ll breach them and kill every last one of you. But if you surrender, I’ll spare the lives of your men. On my honor.
The war is over, ser. Why sacrifice living men to a lost cause?
You clearly have no intention of saving your men’s lives. Why did you come to treat with me?
Well, now you have.
The thrill will fade.
Gold?
Family.
Maybe it really is all cocks in the end.
Yes, he's always been a champion of the downtrodden.
Perhaps you ought to sit down.
How many?
It's been good to see you. I imagine the next time will be across a battlefield.
Yes, and I'm not looking forward to seeing more of them. But I'm loyal to the queen and you're loyal to Sansa and her dolt brother.
Fuck loyalty?
And tell her what?
At her until she kicked me out. She thinks I was an idiot to trust you. A lot of people seem to think that, actually.
I suppose we should say goodbye, one idiot to another.
Our men in King's Landing will march north in three days' time.

We don't have a fortnight. If the North falls, we fall. Three days. The remaining forces in the Westerlands will take the river road east. We'll meet at Lord Harroway's Town and march together to Winterfell.
Your Grace.
Preparing the expedition north.
You pledged our forces to fight our common enemy.
You saw it with your own eyes. You saw a dead man trying to kill us.
This isn't about noble houses, this is about the living and the dead!
I made a promise.
Our child will never be born if the dead come south.
And then what?

When the fighting in the North is over, someone wins. You understand that, don't you? If the dead win, they march south and kill us all. If the living win, and we've betrayed them, they march south and kill us all!
I faced them in the field. We can't beat them. We can't beat their dragons.
Two.
For all we know, it's guarding her fleet!
We can't beat the Dothraki. We don't have the numbers. We don't have the support of the other houses!
The Golden Company is not here. They're in Essos. How is a mercenary company in Essos going to help us?
You plotted with Euron Greyjoy without telling me, the commander.
I didn't conspire with him.
I pledged to ride north. I intend to honor that pledge.
Treason?
Doesn't matter what I'd call it.
Are you going to order him to kill me? I'm the only one you have left. Our children are gone, our father
Give the order, then.
I  don't believe you.
You should. But instead, you're sitting here watching me fail at dinner. Why might that be?
We both know who would pay the most. Or make you pay the most if he found out you had captured me and sent me back up north for a summary execution.
It would be, if you honestly believed my father would never find out about it.
He'll make time for you.
Shall we drink on it?
You do understand how suspicious that is to ordinary people?
Very well. My lady, may our journey continue without further incident.
I'm afraid I must insist.
Father's orders.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You'd condemn your own son to death?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: This isn't a trial. It's a farce. Cersei has manipulated everything and you know it.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You've always hated Tyrion.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: As did I. Do you know the last order the Mad King gave me? To bring him your head. I saved your life so you could murder my brother?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Justice?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: He'll be executed.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Once you said family is what lives on. All that lives on. You told me about a dynasty that would last 1,000 years. What happens to your dynasty when Tyrion dies? I'm a Kingsguard, forbidden by oath to carry on the family line.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: What happens to your name? Who carries the lion banner into future battles? Your nephews? Lancel Lannister? Others whose names I don't even remember?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: It survives through me. I'll leave the Kingsguard. I'll take my place as your son and heir if you let Tyrion live.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You have my word.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You're going to be found guilty.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: When you are, you need to enter a formal plea for mercy and ask to be sent to the Wall. Father's agreed to it. He'll spare your life and allow you to join the Night's Watch.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Father is not Joffrey. He'll keep his word.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Do you trust me?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Keep your mouth shut. No more outbursts. This will all be over soon.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Alright, thats enough.
We’re trying to blend in. We don’t want everyone in Dorne to hear your accent.
It can wait. Up there.
That’s the Water Gardens.
I like to improvise.
Myrcella.
Let’s speak in private.
Excellent. Good to meet you.
We have to go, Myrcella, now.
You’ll be fine, I promise, but we have to--
My lord.
Sorry to interrupt. We’re here for Queen Margaery and Ser Loras Tyrell. Give them to us and we’ll be on our way.
I speak for King Tommen of House Baratheon, First of His Name.
You already insulted one great house. It won’t happen twice. Every last sparrow will die before Margaery walks down that street.
I’ve been a member of the Kingsguard since before you were born. You don’t have to do this. You don’t have to do anything.
Not when you’re sitting in that chair.
Will I be walking naked in the streets? Or will I spend a few months in the sept dungeons first to teach me about the gods’ mercy?
I’m being sent to deal with the Blackfish. Apparently Walder Frey can’t manage it on his own because he’s 400 years old. Sent me with an army to the Riverlands and a siege that could last months.
I’m not going to the Riverlands.

I’m going to give Bronn the largest bag of gold anyone’s ever seen and have him gather the best killers he knows. I’ll take them to the sept and I’ll remove the High Sparrow’s head and every other sparrow head I can find.
He has our son! He stole our son! He’s torn our family apart. How should we treat people who tear us apart?
You’ll stand trial soon. I need to he here for you.
Lord Bolton.

Is there word from the capital?  Roose BOLTON: You haven't heard? Stannis Baratheon laid siege to King's Landing. Sailed into Blackwater Bay. Stormed the gates with thousands of men. And your sister How can I put this? Your sister is alive and well. Your father's forces prevailed.
Will I die?
Then you'll die. You're no maester. Where's your chain?
I don't need my right hand to kill you.
Mmm.
No milk of the poppy.
I'll scream.
I'll scream loudly.
Not so hard. You'll scrub the skin off.
I need a bath. Help me out of these rags.
Now get out.
This one suits me fine. Don't worry. I'm not interested. If I faint, pull me out. I don't intend to be the first Lannister to die in a bathtub.
You swore a solemn vow, remember? You're supposed to get me to King's Landing in one piece. Not going so well, is it? No wonder Renly died with you guarding him.
That was unworthy. Forgive me. You protected me better than most--
I'm apologizing. I'm sick of fighting. Let's call a truce.
I trust you.
There it is. There's the look. I've seen it for 17 years on face after face. You all despise me. Kingslayer. Oathbreaker. A man without honor. You've heard of wildfire?
Stark? You think the honorable Ned Stark wanted to hear my side? He judged me guilty the moment he set eyes on me. By what right does the wolf judge the lion? By what right?
Jaime. My name is Jaime.
You could have killed me.
Ending the war by killing her.
That was only one of them.  She has two more.  If she decides to use them, to really use them --
Don't you mean we're fucked?
I have to tell Cersei.
We haven't done a full counting.

I just saw the Dothraki fight.  They'll beat any mercenary army.  They'll beat any army I've ever seen.  Killing our men wasn't war for them, it was sport.  Her dragon burned a thousand wagons.  Qyburn's scorpion fired bolts bigger than you.  They couldn't stop it.  And she has three of them.  This isn't a war we can win.
He didn't.
I'm not talking about father.  Tyrion didn't kill Joffrey.  He had nothing to do with it.
It was Olenna.  She confessed before she died.
After.

If you were Olenna would you rather have seen your granddaughter married to Joffrey or Tommen?  Which one would Margerey been better able to control?  Which one would have made Olenna the true ruler of the Seven Kingdoms?  She was telling the truth.
She's dead like her son, her grandchildren, her whole house.  And if we don't find a way out of this war we'll follow them.
We don't have time for this.  We should be preparing the city for a siege.  The Dothraki are coming.
I seem to recall them giving you a bit of trouble as well.
Why down here?
Today might be the day I kill you by accident.
Don't talk about our father.
I once told Bronn that if I ever saw you again I'd cut you in half.
What do you want?
You want Cersei to bend the knee you can ask her yourself.
Why was QYBURN here?
I met with Tyrion.
Daenerys wants to meet.
To discuss an armistice.
Because an army of dead men is marching on the Seven Kingdoms.  Tyrion claims he'll have proof.
Tyrion?
You let it happen.  Why?
Who will you say is the father?
People won't like that.
The lion doesn't concern himself with the opinions of the sheep.
(desperately) Water. Water. Please, water. Wa-water. Water, please. Please. Please.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: If I die, you won't be getting--
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I'm dying.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I don't care about revenge.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: My sword hand. I was that hand.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: What the hell was that?
You're a rare talent. When you're fighting cripples, anyway.
You talk to my brother this way?
Do you think he did it?
We're done for today.
You stood for him in his trial by combat at the Eyrie.
To tell you the truth, this isn't so bad. Four walls. A pot to piss in. I was chained to a wooden post covered in my own shit for months.
Maybe a bit. I'm sorry I didn't come sooner.
How do you think? Her son died in her arms.
Don't. You know what's coming?
Now that you mention it, she did ask.
Depends. Did you do it?
Are you really asking if I'd kill my brother? How can I help you?
You know I can't.
What do you want me to do? Kill the guards? Sneak you out of the city in the back of a cart? I'm the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard.
Inappropriate? You're accused of killing the king.Freeing you is treason.

Which is why we're having a trial. A trial. If the killer threw himself before the Iron Throne, confessed to his crimes, and gave irrefutable evidence of his guilt, it wouldn't matter to Cersei. She won't rest until my head's on a spike.
Not just yours. She's offering a knighthood to whomever finds Sansa Stark.
You sent for me, Your Grace?
Ser Boros is on duty tonight. Tomorrow I believe...
I promise you Tommen's safe. We're protecting him.
What?
You know why. She'd hoped I'd send her daughters back to her.
I swore by all the gods that if her daughters were alive, I'd return them to her.
I wanted to get back to you. Should I have told hert o fuck off?
Catelyn Stark's dead. So if I told you to leave the capital right now and find Sansa, if I told you to find that murderous little bitch and bring me her head, would you do it?
I had to see him. I had to know for myself.
He didn't do it, Cersei.
It's the duty of the Lord Commander to fill those pages. And there's still room left on mine.
Mmm. It's yours.

It was reforged from Ned Stark's sword. You'll use it to defend Ned Stark's daughter. You swore an oath to return the Stark girls to their mother. Lady Stark's dead. Arya's probably dead, too, but there's still a chance to find Sansa and get her somewhere safe. I've got something else for you.
I hope I got your measurements right.
I almost forgot. I have one more gift.
Of course you do.
My brother owes him a debt. He's not safe here. You're keeping him from harm. It's chivalry.
See? He's a good lad.You'll get along.
They say the best swords have names. Any ideas?
Good-bye, Brienne.
Is that Estermont?
This ship's sailing to Oldtown. We'll get off earlier, not far from Sunspear. Row ourselves to the Dornish shores in the night.
No.
You should be happy to go back. (Drinks some water).
We are not kidnapping their princess. We are rescuing my niece, bringing her back to the family,
That's why you're here.
Because I don't want to start a war.
It has to be me.
It has to be me.
(Quietly) Varys set him free.
He murdered my father. If I ever see him, I'll split him in two. And then I'll give him your regards.
As far as I've seen, they're all shit ways to die.
I don't care what's sung about me when I'm dead.
Sounds like all the rest.
What about you? What shit way would you choose?
How disappointing. I thought you'd have something more exciting planned.
In the arms of the woman I love.
Let's go.
Pentoshi.
As I said. Why?
A bag of gold.
It was a heavy bag.
Four.
One, if he's slow.
I thought the sharks would get us. (Bronn looks at him).
I could have sworn those were shark fins.
Luck.
First we need to bury these bodies.
Corpses raise questions, questions raise armies. We're not here to start a war.
(Holding out his hand). I can't dig very well with one hand. Not at all, really.
Cersei is the mother of the king. She has the king’s ear and his trust. And the king has been speaking regularly with the High Sparrow about Queen Margaery and Ser Loras.
You’ll take no action at all. When the Tyrell armies come, you will stand down. Are you expressly forbidden from standing down?

The whole thing will be over before anyone can call on you to do anything. When the High Sparrow is in custody or dead, preferably, and Margaery's back at Tommen’s side, do you think the king will be angry at the outcome?
Halt that wagon!
I'll save my confessions for the High Septon.
No, there isn't, is there?
That's a lot of money I just gave you.
You don't want Highgarden.
We're at war.  Daenerys Targaryen could come take it back the day after you move in.  Besides, think of the upkeep.  The more you own, the more it weighs you down.
They're not mine.  The gold belongs to the Iron Bank.  We pay our debts.

Bronn of the Blackwater, formerly of whatever nameless shit heap you're from with a saddlebag full of gold complaining about not getting paid.  When we win this war all the castles in the Seven Kingdoms will be yours to choose from with no one left to take them away from you.
Stranger things have happened.
The current harvest?
Ser Bronn, will you accompany the Tarleys and help them accelerate this process?
No, but motivating reluctant farmers to hand over their harvest, I've heard you guys have a real talent for that.
Good.
We are stretched a bit thin.
Let's give them fair warning first.  These men fought well at Highgarden.
Rickon.
I hear you fought bravely at Highgarden.  Your first battle?
And?
They didn't deserve to die.  But Lady Olenna chose to betray the queen and support the Targaryen girl.  So here we are.
What?
Spears and shields!  Spears and shields!
Spears and shields!
I'm not abandoning my army.
We can hold them off.
Hold the line!
Archers!  Archers with me!
Knock!
Loose!
Qyburn's scorpion is over there.
I can't shoot with one hand.
Come on!
Take cover!
Come on, boy.  Come on!
We fought dead things and lived to talk about it. If this isn't the time to drink, when is?
(to BRIENNE) Um You are an only child.
You didn't.
I surmised it.
You have danced with Renly Baratheon.
Drink.
Drink.
That's a statement about the present.
You didn't drink.
In the game.
In the game. This is Dornish.
Suit yourself.
You keep it warm enough in here.
That's very diligent. Very responsible.
You know the first thing I learned in the North? I hate the fucking North.
I don't want things growing on me.
How about Tormund Giantsbane? Has he grown on you? He was very sad when you left.
(nods) I do, don't I? It's bloody hot in here.
I'm taking your shirt off.
I've never slept with a knight before.
Then you have to drink. Those are the rules.
She's sworn to protect the Stark girls, so... Say something snide.
To climbing mountains.
What? That's not your concern.
You're a dog.
What are you doing with that?
Power resides where men believe--
And you trust Cersei--
Highgarden? Are you mad?
He's not going to kill us. He wouldn't be talking to us if--
Highgarden will never belong to a cutthroat.
What happened?
Have you ever run away from a fight?

You think I'm a good man? I pushed a boy out a tower window, crippled him for life for Cersei. I strangled my cousin with my own hands just to get back to Cersei. I would have murdered every man, woman and child in Riverrun for Cersei.
She's hateful. And so am I.
I hope you're pleased. If you had armed me, they never would have taken us.
I was in chains if you recall. Our little match would have ended quite quick if my hands weren't bound.
I've been sitting in a muddy pen wrapped in chains for the past year.
You were not beating me.
When we make camp tonight, you'll be raped. More than once. None of these fellows have ever been with a noblewoman. You'd be wise not to resist.
They'll knock your teeth out.
No, I don't think you care about your teeth. If you fight them, they will kill you. Do you understand? I'm the prisoner of value, not you. Let them have what they want. What does it matter?
Close your eyes. Pretend they're Renly.
If I was a woman, I'd make them kill me. But I'm not, thank the gods.
You know who she is, don't you?
She's Brienne of Tarth. Her father is Lord Selwyn Tarth. Heard of Tarth?
They call it the Sapphire Isle. Do you know why? Every sapphire in Westeros was mined on Tarth. Sapphires are gemstones. The blue ones.
Lord Selwyn would pay his daughter's weight in sapphires if she's returned to him. But only if she's alive, her honor unbesmirched.
Not defiled.

I hated to read as a child, but my father forced me to study the books every morning before I could practice with sword or horse. Two hours every day holed up in the maester's chambers. I learned a lot of fancy words.
Fighting bravely for a losing cause is admirable. Fighting for a winning cause is far more rewarding.

Now that we're speaking together man to man, I wonder if you need to keep me chained to this tree. I'm not asking to be freed from my constraints, but if I could sleep lying down, my back would thank you for it. I'm not as young and resilient as I was once.
I'm famished, actually.
Well, I do like partridge.
My father--
Don't.
How are you?
You are. You will be. I'll see to that.
Please give the queen a moment alone with her son.
I don't know what I saw.
Tyrion's my brother. Our brother. There'll be a trial. We'll get to the truth of what happened.
You're a hateful woman. Why have the gods made me love a hateful woman?
No.
(GRUNTS)
I don't care.
I don't care.
I don't care.
What did you do to him exactly? I haven't been able to get a clear answer.
Does he understand what we’re saying? I mean, to the extent that he ever understood complete sentences in the first place.
So tell him to march into the sept and crush the High Sparrow’s head like a melon.
Has the Faith leveled official charges yet?
That is one trial by combat I look forward to watching.
I’m Lord Commander of the Kingsguard. The Lord Commander of the Kingsguard does have a position on the Small Council.
The same women who murdered Myrcella have overthrown House Martell and taken control of Dorne. We've got a lot to discuss. All of us together.
This same mob spat at my sister not long ago, and if you turn on us, they'll cheer to see your head mounted on a spike.
Advice?
No.
Don't.
What are you doing?  No one can see us like this ?
It is.
I suppose as well as can be expected.
The truth is Casterly Rock isn't worth much anymore.  Well, it is to me.  My fond childhood memories won't keep Cersei on the throne.

For now.  They won't be able to hold it.  Euron Greyjoy's navy burned their ships, we emptied the larders before we left.  Eventually they'll be forced to abandon their position and march all the way across Westeros.
As Robb Stark did to me at Whispering Wood.  There are always lessons in failures.
My father always said I was a slow learner.
Widow's Wail.

To you, sure.  To others as well.  But after we've won and there's no one left to oppose us, the people living peaceful in the world she built, do you really think they'll wring their hands over the way she built it?
Possibly.  Not much to be gained from discussing it with you though, is there?
Yes, it has.
I think we're done here.

Cersei had several ideas.  Whipping you through the streets and beheading you in front of the Red Keep.  Flaying you alive and hanging you from the walls of King's Landing.  I talked her out of those.
No.  I made sure of that.
You think my father’s going to negotiate with you? You don’t know him very well.
Do you know how long it's going to take us to get to King's Landing walking through fields and forests?
So how shall we pass the time?
It's going to be a very dull walk.

You know, it doesn't matter how loyal a servant you are, no one enjoys the company of a humorless mute. Trust me on this. People have been serving me since I was born. You think Lady Stark is going to want a giant towheaded plank following her around for the rest of her life? A week's journey with you and she'll order you to fall on your sword.
All the good it's done her.
How did you come into Lady Stark's service? There's something we can talk about.
It had to be recently. You weren't with her at Winterfell.
Because I visited Winterfell. I would have noticed your dour head smacking into the archways.
Were you pledged to Stannis?
Ah, Renly. Really? He wasn't fit to rule over anything more important than a 12-course meal.
Why? I lived with him at court since he was a boy, don't forget. Could hardly escape the little tulip. Skipping down the corridors in his embroidered silks. I knew him far better than you.
Sounds like you quite fancied him.
Oh, gods, you did.
Did you ever tell him? No, of course not. You weren't Renly's type, I'm afraid.
He preferred curly-haired little girls like Loras Tyrell. You're far too much man for him.
Unless they're about me. It's all true about Renly. His proclivities were the worst kept secret at court. It's a shame the throne isn't made out of cocks. They'd have never got him off it.
I don't blame him. And I don't blame you, either. We don't get to choose who we love.
No, you really can't.
He knows who I am.
Maybe you're right.  But what if you're not? What if he tells someone?
More innocent than Lady Stark's daughters?
It's a tough decision-take the bridge and risk being seen or cross the great water.
Anyone can see us on the bridge, but cross by water and the current could take us or I could escape down the river.
It's wonderful to watch you wrestle with these dilemmas. Which will she choose?
Gambler at heart. Wouldn't have guessed.
I need to rest.
I have these, you know, on your feet when you walk too far. What do you call them?
Corns. I never used to get corns. Of course I used to ride everywhere, not march around like a common foot soldier wearing the same shit boots for over a year.
This heel is ruined. There's no way-
I never understood why some knights felt the need to carry two swords.
Ooh. You move well for a great beast of a woman.
You shouldn't grimace before you lunge. It gives away the game.
Bit of a quandary for you. If you kill me, you fail Lady Stark. But if you don't kill me, I'm going to kill you.
You're good. Graceless, but good.
See? If you were willing to hurt me, you might have had me there.
We enjoy a good fight. Gets our juices flowing. The Flayed Man of House Bolton. A bit gruesome for my taste.
Let us go and my father will pay you whatever you want.
Gilded steel.
I'm not hungry.
Oh!
No, I'll do it. Leave us.
I can't fight anymore.
I can hold a sword, but all my instincts are wrong. How can I protect the king when I can hardly wipe my own ass?
I'm not Father. I'm the Kingslayer. When people find out I can't slay a pigeon...
With whom? You? Men talk. Soon as someone discovers I can't fight, he'll tell everyone.
My brother tells me you can keep your mouth shut. Unusual talent for a sellsword.
Is this place safe?
Uh-huh.
I haven't used a sparring sword since I was nine.
Ah! Bold warrior you are, attacking a man when his guard's down.
Come on, then.
Ser Loras, it's quite all right. Your sister looks very beautiful.
So, looking forward to your wedding?
Our fathers are both rather keen on the prospect.

Perhaps they should get married. If you were to marry Cersei, she'd murder you in your sleep. If you somehow managed to put a child in her first, she'd murder him, too, long before he drew his first breath. Luckily for you, none of this will happen because you'll never marry her.
Move away!
Joffrey! Joffrey!
A summons from the queen.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: It’s a threat.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: No note.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Softer.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Not so loud.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: The world can't know knows she’s our daughter.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: If I was the father to any of my children, they’d be stoned in the streets.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I’ll make things better.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I’m going to Dorne. I’ll bring our daughter home.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I’m not going to ask him anything.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: No army. Do you know where they’re keeping her?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I’ll find her. Is there anything else, your grace?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I never said I was going alone. (exit Jaime)
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Ser Bronn of the Blackwater.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Thank you.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: A pleasure. (he kisses her hand)
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Beautiful bride you've chosen. When were you planning on getting married?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: On with what?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: It's very good for you.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Mmm.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I would have advised against that.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: 'Cause you're going to come with me and help me with something important. And when we return, I'm going to give you a much better girl, and a much better castle.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: As far south as south goes.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Almost.
Your mother wouldn’t do that.
Are you angry with her?
Then why didn’t you let her come? She has every right to be here.
They’re not putting your mother in a cell ever again. Not while I’m here. Why haven’t you gone to see her? Everything she endured, she did it for you.
Why not?
We all fail sometimes.
Go and see your mother and ask her to forgive you.
Go and see your mother, Tommen.
You’re a bold man.
You must long for the next life.
You imprisoned and humiliated my sister.
What about my sins?
I broke a sacred oath and stabbed my king in the back. I killed my own cousin. When the gods judged my brother guilty, I helped him escape their justice. What atonement do I deserve?
Oh, the gods won’t mind. They’ve spilled more blood than the rest of us combined.
They should be closer if you mean for them to save you.
I’ve fought against worse odds.
Lord Tarley.
Rickon, isn't it?
That's it.
Thank you for coming.  The other lords of the Reach look to you for guidance now more than ever.  They might not have come if you hadn't.
Do you ride for Hornhill today?
And what side will you be fighting for?  You were the only man to defeat Robert Baratheon in battle.  Not even Rhaegar Targaryen could --
I want you to be my ranking general in the wars to come.  I want you to swear allegiance to Cersei and I want you to help her destroy her enemies.  All of her enemies.
You swore an oath to the Crown as well, Lord Tarley.

She was a great woman, once.  Now she's broken.  She wants revenge so badly she brought the Dothraki to our shores.  The Dothraki in Westeros for the first time in history.  I know you don't like my sister, but you have to make a choice.  Do you fight with us or with the foreign savages and eunuchs?
When the war is won the queen will need a new warden of the south.  I can think of no better man than Randyll Tarley.
She did.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: She lied to me as well. She never had any intention of sending her army north.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: She has Euron Greyjoy's fleet and 20,000 fresh troops. The Golden Company from Essos, bought and paid for. Even if we defeat the dead, she'll have more than enough to destroy the survivors.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I promised to fight for the living. I intend to keep that promise.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Do you want me to apologize? I won't. We were at war. Everything I did, I did for my house and my family. I'd do it all again.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Because this goes beyond loyalty.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: This is about survival.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Thank you, Your Grace.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I'm sorry for what I did to you.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I'm not that person anymore.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: You're not?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: You're not angry at me.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Why didn't you tell them?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: What about afterwards?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Yes, here we are.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: How do they feel about their new queen?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: And she is? Different?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: You're sure about her?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: She didn't seem sure about you.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Hmm.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: No, that part is real.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: She's always been good at using the truth to tell lies. I wouldn't be too hard on yourself. She's fooled me more than anybody.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: What?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Your cock.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Lady Brienne.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: He's come a long way.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I'm sure you'll teach him. I've been told you're commanding the left flank.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: It is.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Yes, I think you're right.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: What?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I truly don't.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: You want me to insult you?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Good.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I came to Winterfell because-I'm not the fighter I used to be. But I'd be honored to serve under your command, if you'll have me.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: So, what can we do?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: If that's true, he'll never expose himself.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: That would be something to see.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Mm.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: It wasn't so simple. I was sleeping with my sister and you had one friend in the world who was sleeping with his sister.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Do you miss it?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Well, my golden-lion days are done, but whore-mongering is still an option for you.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Oh! My lady.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: You really think any of us are going to sleep tonight? Join us.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I'm sure someone does.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Fabled loser of the Battle of Whispering Wood.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: You don't need a king. Any knight can make another knight.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I'll prove it.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Kneel, Lady Brienne.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Do you want to be a knight or not? Kneel.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: In the name of the Warrior, I charge you to be brave.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: In the name of the Father, I charge you to be just.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: In the name of the Mother, I charge you to defend the innocent.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Arise, Brienne of Tarth a knight of the Seven Kingdoms.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: We'd better get some rest.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: As your brother, I feel it’s my duty to warn you: You worry too much. It’s starting to show.
There was nothing to be afraid of until you told father. [In a whisper, mimicking] We’re Lannisters. Lannisters don’t act like fools.
But who would he tell?

If he told the king, both our heads would be skewered on the city gates by now. Whatever Jon Arryn knew or didn’t know, it died with him. And Robert will choose a new Hand of the king, someone to do his job while he’s off fucking boars and hunting whores. Or is it the other way around? And life will go on.
That’s an honor I can do without. Their days are too long, their lives are too short.
Don’t get up.
You’ve much to teach me, no doubt. But our sister craves your attention.
A family trait. Now, the Starks are feasting us at sundown. Don’t leave me alone with these people.
I thought you might say that. But since we’re short on time, (he opens the door; a bevy of whores enter and descend on TYRION) Come on, girls. See you at sundown.
I hear we might be neighbors soon. I hope it’s true.
I’m sure we’ll have a tournament to celebrate the new title, if you accept. It would be good to have you in the field. The competition has become a bit stale.
No? Getting a little old for it?
Well said.
Are you completely mad?
It’s all right. It’s all right.
I heard you the first time. (To BRAN) Quite the little climber, aren’t you? How old are you, boy?
Ten.
The things I do for love.

King in the North. I keep expecting you to leave me in one castle or another for safekeeping, but you drag me along from camp to camp. Have you grown fond of me, Stark? Is that it? I've never seen you with a girl.
You don't trust the loyalty of the men following you into battle?
Smart boy. What's wrong? Don't like being called ‘boy’? Insulted?
If that's true, Stannis is the rightful king. How convenient for him.
I was your prisoner when Ned Stark lost his head.
You have proof? Or do you want to trade gossip like a couple of fishwives?
You think my father's going to negotiate with you? You don't know him very well.
Three victories don't make you a conqueror.
Magnificent.
Looks fresh-forged.
No one's made a Valyrian steel sword since the Doom of Valyria.
Where did you get this much Valyrian steel?
You've wanted one of these in the family for a long time.
Two?
Well, thank you. It's glorious.
Any decent swordsman knows how to use both hands.
No. But as long as I'm better than everyone else, I suppose it doesn't matter.
Where's that written? I can and I will. The Kingsguard oath is for life.
How many people in this city alone would love to see his head on a pike?
Home?
You are the Lord of Casterly Rock.
You know what they call me? Kingslayer. Oathbreaker. Man without honor. Now you want me to break another sacred vow.
No.
No.
There's my answer.
My bloody honor is beyond repair, but my answer is still no. I don't want Casterly Rock. I don't want a wife. I don't want children.
Supper would be nice.
I suppose you want the sword back.
(Groans)
You like it so much, you're welcome to chop off your own hand and take it.
Days?
Uh... a hook would be more practical.
Odd little man.
What symptoms?
You let him touch you?
I'm surprised. You never let Pycelle near you.
I'm not sure I've ever smelled a dead cat.
You drink more than you used to.
Why?
It must have been traumatic for you.
A rather short siege.
Father disowned me today.
You're forgetting Tyrion.
Staying in the Kingsguard means I live right here in the Red Keep with you.
I want... Not now? When? I've been back for weeks.
Something's changed.
What do you want me to apologize for?
You think I wanted to be taken prisoner?
Every day I was a prisoner, I plotted my escape. Every day. I murdered people so I could be here with you.
I... What are you saying?
Go away.
All the Kingsguard will be on duty, of course. Ser Boros will be stationed here. Ser Preston will be stationed here beside the primary entertainment. Your Grace?
Ser Meryn will guard Lady Margaery and Tommen.
And I thank you for it, Ser Meryn. All very good. I don't expect any trouble.
Margaery Tyrell, I've heard.
The war's not won. Not while Stannis lives.
My apologies, Your Grace. I was rather busy.

So this is the famous "Book of Brothers"? All the great deeds of all the great Kingsguard. Ser Arthur Dayne. The Sword of the Morning. (Chuckles) Led the attack on the Kingswood Brotherhood. Defeated the Smiling Knight in single combat. Ser Duncan the Tall. (Laughs) Four pages for Ser Duncan. He must have been quite a man.
So they say.
There's still time.
I use my left hand now, Your Grace. Makes for more of a contest.
Yes, there she is.
To return the Stark girls to their mother who is now dead.

Well, Arya Stark hasn't been seen since her father was killed. Where do you think she is? My money's on dead. There's a certain safety in death, wouldn't you say? And Sansa Stark is now Sansa Lannister. Bit of a complication.
What do you want me to do? Kidnap my sister-in-law? And take her where? Where would she be safer than here?
Are you sure we're not related? Ever since I've returned, every Lannister I've seen has been a miserable pain in my ass. Maybe you're a Lannister, too. You've got the hair for it if not the looks.
What he built. The towers. He built it for us. He meant it for us. They’re going to try to take it away, all of it.
All of them out there are enemies. They’re waiting a night to make sure he’s really dead and as soon as they see the stones on his eyes they’ll set to work on tearing us apart.
This is exactly what they want.
Mother.
Shh, shh. Don’t think about it.
She’s not suffering. She’s gone. No one can hurt her anymore.
I know.
You don’t believe that.
Fuck prophecy. Fuck fate. Fuck everyone who isn’t us. We’re the only ones who matter, the only ones in this world. And everything they’ve taken from us, we’re going to take back and more.
We’re going to take everything there is.
What is this?
He knew it. Made me memorize every damn city, town, lake, forest, and mountain.
No, not angry.
Should I be?
Dragonstone.
They have deep-water ports for the ships. Stannis left the castle unoccupied, and that's where she was born.
Winter is here. We can't win a war if we can't feed our men and our horses. The Tyrells have the grain, the Tyrells have the livestock.
If they think Daenerys will win. No one wants to fight on the losing side. Right now, we look like the losing side.
Three kingdoms, at best. I'm not sure you understand how much danger we're in.
A dynasty for whom? Our children are dead. We're the last of us.
We never talked about Tommen.
Our baby boy killed himself.
Cersei--
Even Lannisters can't survive without allies. Where are our allies now? You saw what happened to Walder Frey and his family.

We couldn't. He was a useless, old coward, but the Freys supported us. Now they're all dead. Whoever killed them is no friend of ours. We need allies. Stronger, better allies. We can't win this war alone.
The Greyjoys? You invited the Greyjoys to King's Landing?
Well, it looks like all of them.
How are they better allies? How are they different from the Freys? They both broke their promises and murdered their former friends as soon as it suited them.
They're not good at anything. I know the Ironborn. They're bitter, angry little people. All they know how to do is steal things they can't build or grow themselves.
Oh, what did he come here for, then?
You're not a rightful monarch, though, are you?

The Greyjoys rebelled agains the throne for the right to be monarchs, but as I recall, you were soundly defeated. Come to mention it, weren't you the one who started that rebellion by sailing to Casterly Rock and burning the Lannister fleet? You certainly caught us there. Very smart move on your part. Of course, we all made it to the Iron Islands anyway. I was there.
The people I was cutting down were your own kin.
And I enjoyed killing Greyjoys.
Have you ever known your mother to like anyone aside from her children?
Whenever you’re ready, My Lord.
You’ve got the second largest army in Westeros. You'll bring them into the city, stop queen Margaery’s humiliation before it starts, and take her back into Crown custody.
It's really heavy, mind you.  MAN 5: We need some more water.  MAN 3: Take it down there!

You look lovely tonight, Lady Stark. Widowhood becomes you. (Catelyn grabs a large rock from the ground)  Your bed must be lonely. ls that why you came? l'm not at my best, but l think l could be of service. You slip out of that gown and we'll see if l'm up to it. (She marches towards him and hits the rock against his forehead)   Oh, l do like a violent woman.
Let me show you how. Hit me again, over the ear. And again and again. You're stronger than you look, it shouldn't take long.
But l don't, my lady. The dark is coming for all of us. Why cry about it?
What gods are those? The trees your husband prayed to? Where were the trees when his head was getting chopped off? lf your gods are real and if they are just why is the world so full of injustice?
There are no men like me. Only me.
l pushed him out the window.
I hoped the fall would kill him.
You should get some sleep. lt's going to be a long war. (Catelyn drops the rock and goes away)
Lady Stark. l'd offer you my sword, but l seem to have lost it.
l've lost them too, l'm afraid.
"Summoned to court to answer for the crimes of your bannerman Gregor Clegane, the Mountain"
Catelyn Stark took my brother.
Tyrion?
One of our men interfered, speared him through the leg before I could finish him.
It wouldn't have been clean.
I could care less what anyone thinks of me.
It's the truth.
Of course it bothers me.
I didn't realize you placed such a high value on my brother's life.
So the lion does concern himself with the opinions of..
Such a small pack of wolves.
Was!... the Hand of the King. Now I'm not sure what he is... Lord of somewhere very far away.
Get back inside where it's safe. I'm looking for my brother. You remember my brother, don't you, Lord Stark? Blond hair, sharp tongue, short man.
It seems he had some trouble on the road. You wouldn't know what happened to him, would you?
Come, Stark. I'd rather you die sword in hand.
Threaten? As in, "I'm going to open your Lord from balls to brains and see what Starks are made of”?
You're right. Take him alive! Kill his men!
My brother, Lord Stark... We want him back.
Listen. Do you hear them? How many do you think are in there with him? Guess.
He likes to do this when I'm on duty... He makes me listen as he insults my sister.
Why do I have to forgive you? Have you wronged me?
Have we? Strange, I've forgotten.
That's where you got your scar?
Vicious sons of whores.
They stopped liking it at the end. That was a proper battle. D'you remember Thoros of Myr charging through the breach?
I saw the youngest of the Greyjoy lads at Winterfell. It was like seeing a shark on a mountaintop.
I doubt it.
I don't serve Lord Stark.
Thank the gods you're here, Stark. About time we had some stern northern leadership.
Sturdy old thing. How many kings' asses have polished it, I wonder? What's the line? The King shits and the Hand wipes.
I know. People have been swinging at me for years, but they always seem to miss.

I have a knack for it. It must be strange for you coming into this room. I was standing right here when it happened. He was very brave, your brother. Your father too. They didn't deserve to die like that. Nobody deserves to die like that.

500 men just stood there and watched. All the great knights of the Seven Kingdoms. You think anyone said a word, lifted a finger? No, Lord Stark. 500 men and this room was silent as a crypt. Except for the screams, of course, and the Mad King laughing. And later... When I watched the Mad King die, I remembered him laughing as your father burned... It felt like justice.
Tell me... If I'd stabbed the Mad King in the belly instead of the back, would you admire me more?
Calm down.
I was thinking of us. You're a bit late to start complaining about it now. What has the boy told them?
Then what are you raving about?
We'll say he was lying. We'll say he was dreaming. We'll say whatever we like. I think we can outfox a 10-year-old.
I'll go to war with him if I have to. They can write a ballad about us : "The War for Cersei's Cunt."
Never.
The boy won't talk. And if he does, I'll kill him. Him, Ned Stark, the King... the whole bloody lot of them, until you and I are the only people left in this world.
One of the outlaws in the Brotherhood.
You killed Simon Toyne with a counter riposte. Best move I ever saw.
I cut his head off, so no.
He said the same thing he'd been saying for hours... "Burn them all." If that's all, your Grace...
Little brother.
Tell me you're not thinking of taking the black.
Even if the boy lives, he'll be a cripple, a grotesque. Give me a good clean death any day.
My dear brother, there are times you make me wonder whose side you're on.
A sword for the wall?
Good man. Have you swung it yet?

At someone, I mean. It's a strange thing, the first time you cut a man. You realize we're nothing but sacks of meat and blood and some bone to keep it all standing. Let me thank you ahead of time for guarding us all from the perils beyond the Wall... Wildlings and White Walkers and whatnot. We're grateful to have good, strong men like you protecting us.
Is it "we" already? Have you taken your vows then?
Give my regards to the Night's Watch. I'm sure it will be thrilling to serve in such an elite force. And if not, It's only for life.
He's been covering himself in glory.
Pick up the pace!
They're attacking the southern gates!
Now!
What?!
You heard the man. Loose!
No discipline. No training. Gang of thieves, that's all this is. I commanded the city watch of King's Landing. Those men obeyed orders.
The bars of those gates are four inches of cold, rolled steel.
No such thing as giants. They're stories for the children.
Needed below? Yes. Yes.
(Scoffs). Greyguard is a ruin.
I was charged with the defense of King's Landing when you were soiling your swaddling clothes. Keep your ruin.
(Stands up, angrily shouts). I will not go meekly off to freeze and die! Give it to one of the fools who cast a stone for you. I will not have it. Do you hear me, boy? I will not have it!
You can stick your order up your bastard arse.
You cannot do this! Get your hands off me! (Everyone moves outside). Scum, all of you! If the boy thinks he can frighten me, he's mistaken, yes, very mistaken.
A disgrace! I have friends, important friends in the capital! You'll see!

(Trembling). I was wrong. You're the Lord Commander! We all serve you. I'm sorry. Not only for this, for all I've done and said. I was wrong! (Jon brings his sword up). My Lord, please, mercy! Mercy! I'll go! I will! Please. I'm afraid. I've always been afraid.
Where is the bastard?
Damn it, boy!
(Podrick leaves). Is that your new squire? Could have found you a proper lad.
Hmm, that's a good red. Dornish?
That I do. A fine dinner, my lord.
Maybe I'll hire this cook of yours.
Hmm. Nasty business, had to be done.
Orders are orders.
I never said they were the queen's orders.
You know your sister better than I do.
I don't listen to filth.
Joffrey is my king, the rest doesn't interest me. (Sips his wine).
I did. And I would again. The man was a traitor. He tried to buy my loyalty.
Are you drunk? I'll not have my honor questioned by an Imp.
You think I'll stand here and take this from you, dwarf.
We shall hear what Joffrey has to say about this.
These men are under my command. (Bronn laughs). I command you to arrest this cutthroat.
I have friends at court! Powerful friends! The king himself made me a lord!
Ser Alliser Thorne is not just a knight, he's a man of true nobility. He was acting commander when the Wall came under attack, and led us to victory against the wildlings.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: He's a veteran of 100 battles and has been a defender of the Watch and the Wall almost all of his life. He's the only true choice.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Another wildling lover just like his friend Jon Snow. How's your lady love Slayer?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Lies!
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: The city's drowning in refugees, Your Grace, fleeing the war. We have nowhere to house them, and with winter coming, it'll only get worse.
I do, Your Grace.
I owe my title and lands to your generosity, Your Grace.
Yes, Your Grace.
Orders.
Someone who doesn't care what Littlefinger thinks.
Enough. Get it done. Kill the bastard.
Give it here.
Where is the bastard?
Where?
How will we know him?
Find him.
Sam the Slayer.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I commanded the City Watch of King's Landing, boy.
We stand behind you, Lord Stark.
Men of the Watch!
I'm surprised you didn't ride over and say hello. The King-beyond-the-Wall is your old friend, isn't he?
It's the Hand's tournament that's causing all this trouble, my Lords.

Call it what you will, Lord Stark Ser, the city is packed with people and more flooding in every day. Last night we had a tavern riot, a brothel fire, three stabbings and a drunken horse race down the Street of Sisters.
I need more men.
Thank you, my Lord Hand Ser. They will be put to good use.
The bastard's well-liked.You're not.

For now. But you can't be acting commander forever. There will be a choosing. The old maester will insist on it. You might reconsider his request to marchon Craster's. Let the mutineer stake care of Snow. Or you might be taking orders from him the rest of your life.
Going to visit your wildling whore?
No? Maybe I'll give her a copper tonight and find out.
A bastard son of a traitor. What would you expect?
The free folk? Listen to him. He even talks like a wildling now.
You admit to breaking your vows, then?
The law is the law. The boy must die.
(Laughs) Giants?
How dare you?!
Waiting for you.
After all the things you have seen, this is your question?
No harder than taking a new name, if you know the way. Show me how. I want to be able to do it, too.
If you would learn, you must come with me.
Far and away across the Narrow Sea to Braavos.

To be a dancing master is a special thing, but to be a faceless man, that is something else entirely. The girl has many names on her lips. Joffrey, Cersei, Tywin Lannister, llyn Payne, the Hound. Names to offer up to the Red God. She could offer them all one by one.
Then we must part. A man has duties as well. Here.
A coin of great value.
It is not meant for the buying of horses.
If the day comes when you must find me again, just give that coin to any man from Braavos and say these words to him, "Valar Morghulis."
Jaqen is dead. Say it again. Valar Morghulis.
Good.
Farewell, Arya Stark.
A girl has taken a life. The wrong life.
You were.
That man’s life wasn’t yours to take. A girl stole from the Many Faced God. Now a debt is owed.
Only death can pay for life.
No one at all. Just as a girl should have been before she took a face from the hall.
The faces are for no one.
You are still someone.
And to someone…
...the faces are as good as poison.
And?
Perhaps that’s why the man is thin.
Tomorrow.
A girl has work to do.
A man has patrol duty.
A girl owes one more name. The Red God demands it. Give the man a name.
A minute, an hour, a month. Death is certain, the time is not.
This a man cannot do.
Help was not promised, lovely girl. Only death. There must be others. Give a name, any name.
By the Seven New Gods and the Old Gods beyond counting, I swear it.
A girl gives a man his own name?
Gods are not mocked. This is no joking thing.
Un-name me.
Please?
Thank you.
This would require more than one life. This is not part of our bargain.
A girl lacks honor. If I do this thing, a girl must obey.
A girl and her friends will walk through the gate at midnight.
Lana is very impressive. Very industrious. She will make a fine servant for the Many Faced God.
She will no longer turn left onto Ragman Lane. She will turn right and go to Ragman Harbor.
She will see.
How can a man tell a girl this. If he knew what she would see, there would be no reason to send her.
The man is a gambler. He wages that his sailor's ship will make it to its destination. It is a strange wager for the captain. He only wins if he loses his life.
A girl tells a man that she has seen.

But perhaps the gambler loses his bet and decides that he doesn’t have to pay after all. A destitute woman and her small child, what can they do to such a man if he keeps their money for himself? To whom can they turn for recourse?

A man had no idea what she would see and what she would not. A girl named Lana will return to the docks. She will watch the gambler. She will come to know as much about him as she knows about herself.
A gift for the Thin Man.
Perhaps she is, perhaps she’s not.
It is all the same to the Many Faced God.
Yes. But here you are. And there she is.
Finally a girl is no one.
A girl has named a second name. A man will do what must be done.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: A girl cannot tell a man when exactly he must do a thing. A man cannot make a thing happen before its time.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: (sighs)
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Who are you?
Who are you?
And where did you come from?
A lie.
A lie.
A lie.
The girl lies. To me. To the Many Faced God. To herself. Does she truly want to be no one?
We never stop playing.
Is the girl ready to give up her ears, her nose, her tongue, her hopes and dreams, her loves and hates, all that makes a girl who she is, forever?
No. The girl is not ready to become no one. But she is ready to become someone else.
And?
A shame. A girl had many gifts.
Don’t let her suffer.
A girl says nothing.
A girl keeps her mouth closed. No one hears, and friends may talk in secret, yes? A boy becomes a girl.
And I was always aware. But the girl keeps secrets. It is not for a man to spoil them.
And you fetch water for one of them now. Why is this right for you and wrong for me?
You did. I did. And here we are.
A man pays his debts. A man owes three.

The Red God takes what is his, lovely girl. And only death may pay for life. You saved me and the two I was with. You stole three deaths from the Red God. We have to give them back. Speak three names and the man will do the rest. Three lives I will give you, no more, no less, and we're done.
A man has said.
A man needs a name.
That is enough. Go now, girl. Your master is thirsty.
She has a point.
None of the first Faceless Men were born to lords and ladies. They began as slaves in the mines of Valyria.

He was no one. The Many-Faced God taught him how to shed his face and how to give the gift. The man taught others in exchange for their service. Many served, many more gifts were given. Soon all the masters and overseers were gone and the Faceless Men fled.

Here. They founded the Free City of Braavos and built this house. These were the faces they wore in life when they were not wearing others. The first Faceless Men. And now a girl is one of them, if a girl desires.

An actress who calls herself Lady Crane. She performs at the theater in Sheelba Square. A girl has been given a second chance. There will not be a third. One way or another, a face will be added to the hall.
A girl is not ready.
So a man has heard.
Does death only come for the wicked and leave the decent behind?
That does not matter. The price was paid.
A girl must decide if she wants to serve the Many-Faced God.
A servant does not ask questions.
Come back here! Help us, boy! A man can fight! Free us!
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Quick, give it to me. Give me the ax. Give me that.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Valar dohaeris.
Good.
No? Why come, then?
A man teaches a girl. (Stands up). Valar dohaeris. All men must serve. Faceless men, most of all. (Walks away).
A girl wants to serve herself. Here we serve the Many-Faced God, to serve well. A girl must become no-one.
There is only one god. A girl knows his name. And all men know his gift.
What are you doing?
A girl is not ready.
For what? For whatever you want. To be a Faceless Man. To be no one.
Whose sword is that? It belongs to Arya Stark. Arya Stark's sword, Arya Stark's clothes, Arya Stark's stolen silver. A man wonders how is it that no one came to be surrounded by Arya Stark's things?
If a girl tells me her name, I will give her eyes back.
Come.
If a girl is truly no one, she has nothing to fear.
Who are you?
(Whispers) Boy.
(Whispers) Lovely boy.
A man has a thirst. The man does not drink for a day and a night. A boy could make a friend.

A man does not choose his companions. These two, they have no courtesy. A man must ask forgiveness. (Pause). You're called Arry. (Arya nods).  This man has the honor to be Jaqen H'ghar. Once of the Free City of Lorath.
(Quietly) The boy has more courage than sense.
There isn't. A man is not Jaqen H'ghar.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: No one. And that is who a girl must become.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Who are you?
If a girl says her name, a man will let her sleep under a roof tonight.
If a girl says her name, a man will feed her tonight.
If a girl says her name, a man will give her eyes back.
Come.
Leave it. A girl is not a beggar anymore.
He was no one.
Rangers with me.
He's not here yet. He'd have seen us, blown the horn.
The Halfhand does things in his own time.
Most of them are true.
The whole winter. He was north of the Skirling Pass when the snows came. Had to wait for the thaw.
Well, possible for the Halfhand.
Where?
How many wildlings have joined him?
You saying we should fall back to the Wall?
We need to get rid of those lookouts.
Aye. You killed a wight. You also let an old man beat you bloody and take your sword.
Well, I hope you make a better ranger than you do a steward. Go on.
He came to us from White Harbor. Never failed in his duty. He kept his vows the best he could. He rode far, fought fiercely. We shall never see his like again.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: And now his watch has ended.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: (shakes his head)
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I'm glad for you.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: As soon as our wounded are strong enough.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Wait outside.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I told you to wait outside.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Enough! Out!
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: The gods will curse us for this. By all the laws--
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Unhand her.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I shall have your head for this.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Wait outside.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Now!
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Leave us.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: What did you do?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: What business is that of yours?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Wildlings serve crueler gods than you or I. Those boys are Craster's offerings.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Aye, many a time that monster has been the difference between life and death for our rangers, your uncle among them. We have other wars to fight out there. Like it or not, we need men like Craster.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Yeah. Whatever it was, I daresay you'll see it again. Now, ready my horse. We leave at dawn.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Don't lose it again.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: We've come a long way.
We can talk inside.
You are a godly man?
What is this? Get up, Tarly. Is he all right?
That's enough. Tarly, I forbid you to die. Do you hear me?
Rast you're responsible for him.
Make sure he gets back alive. If he doesn't, you don't. Move on!
Did you send the ravens?
Tarly, look at me. Did you send the ravens?

That was your job. Your only job. We need to get back to the Wall. It's a long march. We know what's out there, but we have to make it, have to warn them or before winter's done, everyone you've ever known will be dead.
Ham. How many days in a row must a man be expected to start his day with ham? Bring me some beer at least. You look exhausted. Was your moonlight ride that tiring?
l didn't say it was your honor.

And you're gonna bring him back to life, are you? No? Good. We've had enough of that sort of thing. Beyond the Wall, the Rangers are reporting whole villages abandoned. At night they see fires blazing in the mountains from dusk until dawn. A captured wildling swears their tribes are uniting in some secret stronghold, to what ends the gods only know. Outside Eastwatch Cotter Pyke's men discovered four blue-eyed corpses. Unlike us, they were wise enough to burn them. Do you think your brother's war is more important than ours?
When dead men and worse come hunting for us in the night, do you think it matters who sits on the lron Throne?
Good. Because l want you and your wolf with us when we ride out beyond the Wall tomorrow.

l'll not sit meekly by and wait for the snows. l mean to find out what's happening. The Night's Watch will ride in force against the wildlings, the White Walkers and whatever else is out there. And we will find Benjen Stark, alive or dead. l will command them myself. So l'll only ask you once, Lord Snow, are you a brother of the Night's Watch or a bastard boy who wants to play at war?
When does Aemon think you'll be able to use that hand?
Good. You'll be ready for this, then. l thought a wolf was more appropriate for you than a bear so l had a new pommel made. lt's called Longclaw. Works as well for a wolf as a bear, l think.

lt was my father's sword, his father before him. The Mormonts have carried it for five centuries. lt was meant for my son Jorah. He brought dishonor to our House, but he had the grace to leave the sword before he fled from Westeros.
Oh, you can. And you will. l wouldn't be standing here if it wasn't for you and your beast. A bloody dead man tried to kill me, so you'll take it. l'll hear no more about it. ls that understood?
Right. Now, don't think this means l approve of this nonsense with you and Alliser Thorne. That's a man's sword. lt'll take a man to wield it.

No, you won't. l sent him to King's Landing yesterday. The hand that your wolf tore off that thing's wrist – l've ordered Thorne to lay it at the feet of this boy king. That should get young Joffrey's attention. And it, uh, puts a thousand leagues between you and Thorne. Now go and put your sword somewhere safe and bring me my supper.
It's Othor, without a doubt.
Any sign of Benjen or the rest of his party?
Mmm.
I want Maester Aemon to examine them first.
Bring me a horn of ale, Snow, and pour one for yourself.
Sit.
I hope you're not thinking of doing anything stupid.
I'm sure they'll be treated gently.
I told you not to do anything stupid. You're confined to quarters. Go.
Snow!?
You came to us as outlaws, poachers, rapers, killers, thieves.
Here you begin anew.
You'll want to take your vow before a heart tree as your Uncle did.
You'll find a Weirwood a mile North of the Wall. And your old Gods too, maybe.
Does house Tarly keep the old Gods?
As you wish, lad.
I don't need him to be charming. I need him to turn this bunch of thieves and runaways into men of the Night's Watch.
Slowly. A raven came for Ned Stark's son.
Both.
We've been capturing wildlings, more every month. They're fleeing south. The ones who flee... say they've seen the White Walkers.
One of our own rangers swore he saw them kill his companions. He swore it right up to the moment Ned Stark chopped his head off.
Your sister sits by the side of the King. Tell her we need help.
It is known.
It is known.
Your house has come to our aid. The whole realm is in your debt, none more so than I. If your family would ask anything of me, ask it, and it shall be yours.

I, Joffrey of the House Baratheon, first of my name, the rightful King of the Andals and the First Men, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm, do hereby proclaim my grandfather, Tywin Lannister, the savior of the city and the Hand of the King.
Lord Petyr Baelish, step forward.

For your good service and ingenuity in uniting the Houses of Lannister and Tyrell, I declare that you shall be granted the castle of Harrenhal with all its attendant lands and incomes to be held by your sons and grandsons from this day until the end of time.
Ser Loras Tyrell.
Is this what you want, Lady Margaery?
I, too, have heard tales of your beauty and grace, but the tales do not do you justice, my lady. It would be an honor to return your love, but I am promised to another. A king must keep his word.
I would like to heed your wishes and the wishes of my people, but I took a holy vow.
The gods are good. I am free to heed my heart. Ser Loras, I will gladly wed your sweet sister. You will be my queen and I will love you from this day until my last day.
Show him. Come on, show him.

Robb Stark is dead. And his bitch mother. (to Pycelle) Write back to Lord Frey. Thank him for his service and command him to send Robb Stark's head. I'm going to serve it to Sansa at my wedding feast.
Yes, I did. I'm going to have it served to Sansa at my wedding feast.
Everyone is mine to torment. You'd do well to remember that, you little monster.
I could have your tongue out for saying that.
I am the king! I will punish you.
My father won the real war. He killed Prince Rhaegar. He took the crown while you hid under Casterly Rock!
I'm not tired.
I'm not tired!
Sansa.
Sansa, come here.
Your king rides forth to battle. You should see him off with a kiss.
My new blade. Hearteater, I've named it. Kiss it.
You'll kiss it again when I return and taste my uncle's blood.
If Stannis is fool enough to come near me.
A king doesn't discuss battle plans with stupid girls.
Your brother's turn will come. Then you can lick his blood off Hearteater, too.
Why isn't it here now? They're coming.
Hound, tell the Hand that his king has asked him a question.
If I tell the Hound to cut you in half, he'll do it without a second thought.
There they are.
What are you doing? We need to attack them.
There's only one ship. Where are the rest of them? Where are the rest of them?
They're coming. They're coming ashore.
There are too many.
Dog, I command you to go back out there and fight.
What would you have me do?
What did my mother say exactly? Did she have urgent business with me?
Ser Boros, Ser Mandon, stay with my uncle and represent the king on the field of battle.
You're the Master of Whisperers. You're supposed to know everything.
The Stark forces are distracted. Now is the time to strike.
If my Uncle Stannis lands on the shores of King's Landing, I'll ride out to greet him.
They say Stannis never smiles. I'll give him a red smile from ear to ear.
Your father's gone. As the father of the realm, it is my duty to give you away to your husband.
I've got a lifetime for that.
Congratulations, my lady.
Well, you've done it. You've married a Lannister. Soon you'll have a Lannister baby. It's a dream come true for you, isn't it?  What a glorious day.
I suppose it doesn't really matter which Lannister puts the baby into you. Maybe I'll pay you a visit tonight after my uncle passes out. How'd you like that?
You wouldn't? That's all right. Ser Meryn and Ser Boros will hold you down.
Time for the bedding ceremony.

Where's your respect for tradition, Uncle? Come, everyone. Pick her up and carry her to her wedding bed. Get rid of her gown. She won't be needing it any longer. Ladies, attend to my uncle. He's not heavy.
There will be if I command it.
What did you say? What did you say?
Grandfather.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Yes. I'd like a report on the meetings of my small council.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I've been very busy. Many important matters require a king's attention.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You've been holding the council meetings in the Tower of the Hand instead of the small council chamber.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: May I ask why?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: So if I wanted to attend a council meeting, I would now have to climb all the stairs in the Tower of the Hand?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Tell me about the Targaryen girl in the east and her dragons.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Is it true?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Don't you think we ought to do something about it?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: And the biggest was the size of a carriage.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: But how do we know these dragons are just curiosities and not the beasts that brought the whole world to heel?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: But I haven't been counseled.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I should be consulted about such things.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You sound like a little cat mewling for his mother. Princes don't cry.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Did you say something, my lady?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: So?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Is your little brother a prince?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Not really relevant then, is it? Come, dog.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Who threw that? I want the man who threw that. Find who did that and bring him to me!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Just kill them! Kill them all!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: What are you doing? I want these people executed!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You can't insult me.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You can't
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: They attacked me!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You're talking to a king!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Let them have her.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You're here to answer for your brother's latest treasons.
Ser Lancel, tell her of this outrage.
Killing you would send your brother a message.
But my mother insists on keeping you alive. Stand. So we'll have to send your brother a message some other way. Meryn.
Leave her face. I like her pretty.
Meryn, my lady is overdressed. Unburden her.
If you want Robb Stark to hear us, we're going to have to speak louder.
I'm punishing her.
You can't talk to me like that. The king can do as he likes!
What are you doing here, dog?
And? What is it?
My nameday has passed.
No. Her. Touch her.
Could you hit her?
My uncle sent you?
Use this.
Harder.
I said harder. He'd want me to get his money's worth.
Oh, I want him to find out. You will bring her to his chambers when you're finished and show him what you've done, or the same thing will happen to you.
Begin.
Harder!
Rhaenyra Targaryen was murdered by her brother, or rather his dragon. It ate her while her son watched. What's left of her is buried in the crypts right down there.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Over there in that urn, the ashes of Aerion Targaryen. Aerion Brightflame they called him. He thought drinking wildfire would turn him into a dragon.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended
