I can’t begin to contemplate --
He beat you.
Sansa, I --
Did he cut you?
I didn’t know.
I made a mistake, a horrible mistake. I underestimated a stranger.
I’m so sorry.
And I will. You must believe me when I tell you that I will.
Do you want me to beg for my life? If that’s what you want, I will. Whatever you ask that is in my power, I will do.
Then I will die.

I would do anything to undo what’s been done to you. I know that I can’t. Will you allow me to say one more thing before I go? Your great-uncle Brynden the Blackfish has gathered what remains of the Tully forces and retaken Riverrun. You might consider seeking him out. The time may come when you need an army loyal to you.
Your brother’s army.
Half-brother.
Your Grace.
I'm a practical man.
And who would you have me be loyal to? Your brother's corpse?
You still have many friends at court, Your Grace, many who believe Ned Stark erred by not supporting your claim.
I would give priority to my head.

I understand that you don't like me, and while that saddens me greatly, I did not come here today seeking your affection. When you march on King's Landing, you may find yourself facing a protracted siege or open gates.
Your Grace.
All of these tents look the same to me. -Would you be so kind
"Your tent"? Not "Our tent"? The king snores, perhaps? Or simply prefers solitude? Pressures of command, no doubt. Four kings vying for the throne.
If war were arithmetic, the mathematicians would rule the world. I did notice your brother entering His Grace's tent just now.
And on the night of your wedding, who was by the king's side then?
Your marriage is quite interesting. Not only to me, but to the realm. The marriage of a wealthy girl always breeds interest, if nothing else.
I've been unlucky in my affections, sadly.
You may have heard false reports.
Betrayed? I wanted him to serve as Protector of the Realm. I begged him to seize the moment.
No, my lady.
Cat, I've I've loved you since I was a boy. It seems to me that fate has given us this chance
Do you want to see your girls again? Sansa, more beautiful than ever? And Arya, just as wild as ever?
Both girls are healthy and safe. For now. But you know the Queen and you know Joffrey. I fear for their longevity if they remain in the capital.
The Lannisters will trade your daughters for the Kingslayer.
I'm not bringing these terms to him. I'm bringing them to you.
Robb has surprised them all with his skills in battle, but he's not a mother. Consider it, Cat. You may not get another chance. I've brought you a gift.
Your husband was an honorable man. He should rest beside his family in the crypts beneath Winterfell. You may not believe
I'm getting married to your Aunt Lysa. She's waiting for us at the Eyrie. You'll be safe there.
Did I kill Joffrey? I've been in the Vale for weeks.
And who helped me with this conspiracy?
Why not?
Then perhaps it was your husband.
How do you know?
You're right. He wasn't involvedi n Joffrey's death. But you were. Do you remember that lovely necklace Dontos gave you? I don't suppose you noticed that a stone was missing after the feast.
A man with no motive is a man no one suspects. Always keep your foes confused. If they don't know who you are or what you want, they can't know what you plan to do next.
So many men they risk so little. They spend their lives avoiding danger. And then they die. I'd risk everything to get what I want.
Everything. My friendship with the Lannisters was productive. But Joffrey, a vicious boy with a crown on his head, is not a reliable ally. And who could trust a friend like that?

I don't want friends like me. My new friends are predictable. Very reasonable people. As for what happened to Joffrey, well, that was something my new friends wanted very badly. Nothing like a thoughtful gift to make a new friendship grow strong.
My lord. Come and see.
I missed your name day. Go on.
A gyrfalcon. The greatest and rarest of birds.
Indeed I was.
On our way to the Fingers, we were set upon by a large force of Bolton men. Seemed to know exactly when we were traveling and exactly whom we were transporting.
Tell me, Lord Royce, how many people knew of my travel plans with Sansa Stark? I shared my intentions with you and no one else.
Your home is the Vale. The Lord of the Vale stands before us. And only his judgment concerns me.
Lord Royce has served the Vale well. He;s enjoyed a distinguished military career. If we could trust his absolute loyalty, he would make a capable commander in the wars to come.
I think he deserves one more chance. What do you say?
That was my instinct as well.
Our lord has spoken. Gather the Knights of the Vale. The time has come to join the fray.
Tell no one what?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: She imprisoned you. She tried to execute you. And you offer her son a princess?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: And I suppose you want me to broker this agreement?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Yes, I could sing this song to Lysa, if I cared to. What's in it for me?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Harrenhal is cursed.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: With a single stroke, you'd make me one of the greatest lords in the realm.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: So did Janos Slynt, and he was given Harrenhal, too. Until you snatched it away.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: (Nods.)
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: The Queen mustn't know.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I don't appreciate being made a fool of, dwarf. If Myrcella marries the Martell boy, she can't very well marry Robin Arryn, can she?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: And Harrenhal, I suppose that's off the table as well.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Leave me out of your next deception.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Robb Stark will never release the Kingslayer.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: For which I am extremely grateful to you, my lord.  Lady Arryn and I have known each other since we were children. She has always been positively predisposed toward me.
Titles do seem to breed titles.
I'm surprised you're surprised. This is the safest place in the city.
Thank you, my dear.
I hear you owe that boy a significant debt.
You should have him knighted.
If only. I owe you a significant debt. Our redheaded friend. You secured her release when the queen detained her.
Apparently, Her Grace believed that you two had some sort of special relationship.
I know.
I know. But how would the queen get that idea?
Keep a low profile.
Well, you are richer than I am.
They're only numbers. Numbers on paper. Once you understand that, it's easy to make them behave. Trivial even. You want a real challenge? Try whores.
Petyr... Are you hurt, my lady?
Good. Good. I'm sure you've had quite a fright. Rest easy. The worst is past.  Ser Dontos shouts up from his boat.
Softly, my friend... Voices carry over water.
Shh. You don't want the queen to hear, do you? 1,000 gold cloaks are searching for you. And if they found you, how do you think they would punish the girl who murdered the king?
I know. I know. But you must admit it looks suspicious. The king who executed your father, who tormented you for years, and you fled the scene of his murder.
Because he was a drunk and a fool and I don't trust drunk fools.
Saved you? My lady, he followed my orders. Every one of them. And he did it all for gold. Money buys a man's silence for a time. A bolt in the heart buys it forever.
Yes, and he gave you a priceless necklace that once belonged to his grandmother. The last legacy of House Hollard.
I had it made a few weeks ago. What did I once tell you about the capital?
Come, my lady. I know you've had a difficult day. But you're safe now. I promise you that. You're safe with me and sailing home.
Lovely day for it. Watching the ships.
Might I speak with Lady Sansa alone for a moment?
I saw your mother not long ago. She's very eager to see you. And your sister.
You said King's Landing was your home. You are the property of the crown. Stealing you would be treason. If you were to tell just one person ...
How do I know?
I'm waiting for word on an assignment that will take me far away from the capital. When I set sail, I might be able to take you with me. But you'll need to be ready to leave on a moment's notice.
The Defender of the Vale!

I bring good news. My friends in the North tell me Sansa has escaped Winterfell. I expect she’s headed to Castle Black where her brother serves as Lord Commander. But she won’t be safe there, not with the Boltons after her.
Since when do the crows give 2 shits if we live?
You saw this?
There are old stories about ice spiders big as hounds.
Where is Mance?
How?
I say we send the Lord Commander back to Castle Black with no eyes.
My ancestors would spit on me if I broke bread with a crow.

Keep that new life you want to give us. Keep your glass, King Crow. Soon as you get on his ships they’re gonna slit your throats and dump your bodies to the bottom of the Shivering Sea. That’s our enemy. That has always been our enemy.
Shut the gate.
Shut the gate!!
Ready your arrows!
You’re with me, lad! Now!
Get the glass.
Safe journey, Kingslayer. Nothing to say? I liked you better before. I don't remember chopping your balls off, too. And don't you worry about your friend. We'll take good care of her.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Well, this is one shameful fucking performance. Stop running and fight.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I thought you'd gone.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: We've only got one bear.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: All you lords and ladies still think that the only thing that matters is gold.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: This makes me happier than all your gold ever could. And that makes me happier than all her sapphires. So go buy yourself a golden hand and fuck yourself with it.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: What the fuck are you doing to my bear?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: The bitch stays.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: She belongs to me. Lord Bolton's orders.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: On your knees. Lord Bolton, I give you the Kingslayer.
No, my lord, he has it here.
Send it to his father?  Roose BOLTON: You'll hold your tongue unless you want to lose it.  Roose BOLTON: Cut her free. Apologies, my lady. You're under my protection now.
Brothers.
11 men. Most of them already drunk. No guards posted. They don't seem to have a care in the world. We'll carve them up like walnut pie.
Have you seen what I can do with a knife?
There's a hut on the West Side of the keep. We should steer clear of it.
They've got some hounds chained up inside. Closer we can get without the dogs sniffing us, the better.
Rescue party is here, lads. Lady.
Aye. I'll take you to him.
You're Brandon Stark?
The little crippled lord. We're going for a ride, boy.
Keep talking and I'll cut your friends' throats. Starting with the idiot. Do you hear? Do you hear?
Depends if he's had any practice. Is that the sort of thing you and your sister go in for, Kingslayer? She loosened you up for us?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Oh, enough. Here.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Can't say that I've ever seen a man drink horse piss that fast.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Do that again and I'll take your other hand.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: He wouldn't have learned anything that way.
A bastard, eh? Took you for highborn.
Name's Locke.

What brought you up here? A sense of duty. I wanted to do my part for the safety of the realm. I was a game warden in the Stormlands. Fed a prized partridge to my hungry kids. I was stupid enough to get caught. Chose the Wall over losing my hand. Figured I wouldn't have to suck up to any highborn cunts here.
Guess I was wrong.
Snow. I don't mean to interrupt. Thorne wants to see you.
Then let me say my vows. If it's a fight you're heading for, then you need men who know how.
I'll take the big bitch first. When she's good and wet, you lot can finish her off.
Catelyn Stark's a treasonous cunt. Orders were to take the Kingslayer alive. Nobody said shit about you.
Take her over there where it's dark. I'd like a little privacy.
A big dumb bitch from who cares where. Never been with a woman that big.
I know what they are.
Bring her back here.
Unbesmirched.
Fancy word for a fancy man.
I bet you did.
Your father, he'd pay your weight in gold to get you back?
Aye, you have both.
Hard to argue with that.
None of us are. Unchain Ser Jaime from the tree.
Suppose you'll be wanting something to eat.
I think we've got a spare partridge on the fire.
Bring the bird over here and the carving knife. Will this work as a table, my lord?
On the stump.
You think you're the smartest man there is. That everyone alive has to bow and scrape and lick your boots.
And if you get in any trouble, all you've got to do is say 'my father' and that's it, all your troubles are gone.
Have you got something to say?
Careful. You don't want to say the wrong thing. You're nothing without your daddy, and your daddy ain't here. Never forget that.
Here, this should help you remember.
Looks like your woman's getting the better of you, if you can call that a woman.
You sure he's the one?
Give the man his silver.
Enough to buy me a new head? If the King in the North hears I had the Kingslayer and let him go, he'll be taking it right off. I'd rather he takes yours.
Word travels. How he screamed. You would have loved it.
Your pet rat have any thoughts on which way they went after Winterfell?
Who the fuck is Jon Snow?

BRAN is in control of the giant. HODOR holds LOCKE above him with one had around his neck. LOCKE chokes and strains. HODOR twists his hand quickly, spanning LOCKE's neck. HODOR drops the lifeless body.
You've lost a hand.
Pick him up, Locke.
Take this away.
Don't they?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: People eat pigeon pie in the capital.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: You won't.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: We won't get to live at Stokeworth. When Mother dies, my sister gets the castle because she's older.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: She hates me. She calls me mean names, and sometimes even still she pulls my hair when Mother isn't looking.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: She is.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Who's that?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: No sunflowers. I don't like yellow. Roses are very nice, definitely roses. And music. I adore music. But no flutes, I hate flutes! We can have a harp if you like, but no flutes. Then there's the food. Food is the most important thing, don't you agree? We're going to need pigeon pies. That's what they eat in the capital, don't they?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Help! Help me!
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Look at it.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: No. You got to carry me.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: If they come back, I say we yield. Gendry's the one they want. I don't want to get caught in the middle of a battle.
Two men fighting isn't a battle.
No, it isn't.
He's got a sword, this one.
Maybe he's a little squire.
Let's have a look. (wrestles Arya to the ground)
Take it off him.
Look at him! You'd better give Hot Pie the sword. l've seen him kick a boy to death.
Your Grace, my sister Margaery, her husband was taken from us before . . . She remains innocent. I would ask you to find it in your heart to do us the great honor of joining our houses.
There’ll be no need for a trial. I confess before the Seven and freely admit to my crimes.
All of them. I lay with other men including the traitor Renly Baratheon.
I perjured myself before the gods. I am guilty of depravity… dishonesty, profligacy, and arrogance. I see that now. I humble myself before the Seven and accept whatever punishment the gods deem just.

I take full responsibility for my many sins and unburden myself of my desires. My only remaining wish is to devote my life to the Seven. May I be a living example of their grace for others to witness.
I do. I will abandon the Tyrell name and all that goes with it. I will renounce my lordship and my claims on Highgarden. I will never marry and I will never father children.
I will.
Well, my father once told me--
Oh, it's more of a brooch, really. Though I suppose a brooch is a sort of pin, so
Uh, yes. I-I am as well.

Yes, me, too. Definitely. I've dreamed of a large wedding since I was quite young. The guests, the food, the tournaments. And the bride, of course. The most beautiful bride in the world in a beautiful gown of gold and green brocade with fringed sleeves. Have you ever been to Highgarden, my lady?
It's terrible, isn't it? The most terrible place there is.
I don’t pay attention to rumors.
He was my friend. He was my king.
I was wrong to support Renly’s claim. I know that. But I was forgiven by Joffrey. I fought for him at the Battle of the Blackwater.
Why does it matter what I wore?
Of course I deny them.
Never.
Never.
Liar! He’s a liar!
Liar!
Get out.
And you want that privilege for yourself.
Ride back to Highgarden, sister. I'm not running from Stannis.
I don't believe that. You don't believe that. Who gained the most from our king's death?
I will put a sword through his righteous face.
He would have been a true king, a good king.
Revenge.
Squire!
That was no fight. I don't know you.
(chuckles)
How did you know?
That I wanted to.
My lord I'll have you know I'm to be married soon and even my intended hasn't the slightest notion.
You have a good deal of experience, do you?
Get your hands off of me.
Who do you think you are?
I can’t stay strong. I never was strong.
I don’t care about that.
I just want it to stop. Help me.
Let them win. Just make it stop. Please.
Yield! I yield.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Has your son marched against Tywin Lannister yet?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: If Robb Stark wants a pact with us, he should come himself, not hide behind his mother's skirts.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: A member of the Kingsguard? As if I wasn't humiliated enough already.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Jealous? Of Brienne the Beauty? Don't make me laugh.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: No, Your Grace. Not tonight. There's another Tyrell who requires your attention. You didn't win my father's support or his army on charm alone.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Your vassals are starting to snigger behind your back. Brides aren't usually virgins two weeks after their wedding night.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Officially. Shall I bring her to you?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Lady Sansa.
My sister Margaery invites you to take the air with her and our grandmother the Lady Olenna in the gardens. May I escort you to them?
I believe you grow more beautiful every day, Lady Sansa.
Of course I did.
I'll take my leave. Lady Sansa.
Ser Jaime, I'm very sorry.
As does yours.
Yes, very much.
They certainly are.
And neither will you.
Your Grace.
Yes.
Margaery does a great deal of work with the poor back in Highgarden.
It was such a deep, deep shock to us all. Your father was a force to be reckoned with. He truly was. I wouldn’t presume to claim to have known him as such…
But just being in his presence was enough to make it so clear just how formidable a person you were dealing with. What a… what a force to be reckoned with.
No it doesn’t.
Sandstone.
Fine.
It’s Dorne.
Judging by my experience, that would be wonderful. Dorne, Highgarden. Anywhere but here.
You’re very respectful.
Why? They all know about me anyway. Everybody knows everything about everyone. What’s the point in trying to keep a secret in a place like this?
My intended? Please. Tywin’s dead. That means no one can force Cersei to marry me.
Unlucky you.
If she doesn’t marry me, she doesn’t go to Highgarden. Which means she stays in King’s Landing. Which means you’re trapped here with Cersei Lannister as your mother-by-law.
Perhaps?
I owe you my life, Ser.
Lord Stark's lucky he still has a head.
You're jealous.
Only if I slip.
Mhmm.
I want you.
Hold still.
You did vomit when that boy's eye was knocked out in the mêlée.
He shouldn't have entered the mêlée if he didn't know how to fight.
Yes well, I guess we'll never know.
Everywhere. So how did it end up? The Targaryen girl will die?
It's a shame he can't muster the same enthusiasm for his wife.
I have an outrageous amount of money.
But a lot more than you.
Hmm... How did that ever happen?
Do you know who should be King?
I am. My father could be your bank. I've never fought in a war before, but I'd fight for you.
And where was Robert in the line of royal succession? Joffrey is a monster. Tommen is eight.
Stannis has the personality of a lobster.
Look at it.

It's just blood. We've all got it in us. Sometimes a little spills. If you become King, you're going to see a lot of this. You need to get used to it. Go on. Look. People love you. They love to serve you because you're kind to them. They want to be near you.

You're willing to do what needs to be done, but you don't gloat over it. You don't love killing. Where is it written that power is the sole province of the worst? That thrones are only made for the hated and the feared. You would be a wonderful King.
My Lord, may the Old Gods watch over your brother and all our northern sons.

The walls of my Holdfast will not stand the winter. The stones were last mortared in the time of King Aerys, and I'm afraid the masons today are not fit to carry their fathers' hammers. When I was a boy, I remember seeing them put up a new tower at Torrhen's Square in a summer. Men worked back then. Today, my Holdfast looks like it was built by drunk children. At night you can hear the wind howling through the gaps. And the Gods forbid it rains. Why, I might as well sleep beneath a waterfall.
Generally, yes, but I've sent all the young men off to fight Robb Stark's war.
Maybe not, My Lord, but he called in his Banners, and took the men.
Of course I remember!
Walk with me. Has there been any word from Locke?
Famously loyal to the Starks.
You think a horde of wildlings can take Winterfell?
He came upon the bodies on our way here.
Ned Stark’s last surviving son?
Your hold on the North will never be secure as long as a Stark can walk through that door.
The Starks lost my house the day King Rob cut off my father’s head. It’s time for new blood in the North.
My congratulations, Lord Bolton.
You're talking to your lord. Use respect.
He said he planned to stop here on his way to the Frostfangs.
The lad meant no harm.
No one will talk to your daughters, you have my word. (Turns to Jon) Now, sit down and shut your mouth.
We did. We passed through six villages on the way here. All six were abandoned. Where have all the wildlings gone?
There's a barrel of Dornish wine on the sledge. Bring it in here.
He's no friend of mine. He broke his vows, betrayed his brothers!
He's been calling himself that for years. What’s he king of? A frozen lake somewhere?
Give it here.
You'll have another one made at Castle Black. (Hands the axe to Craster). Here.
And where does he plan marching this army?
These are bad times to be living alone in the wild. The cold winds are rising.
We chose different paths.
(Stands up) A roof would be welcome. It's been hard riding. We've brought our own food and good steel for you.
Your roof, your rules.
(Angrily) Who am I? (Jon looks confused) Who am I?
And who are you?
Who are you?
You want to lead one day? (Jon nods). Then learn how to follow.
I already got one crow. Don't need two.
The Halfhand knows more. This one is just a little boy. Gut him.
And now he wishes he did. Gut him.
What does Mance want with a dead man's bastard?
He runs, I'll chop his balls off.
He's not yours to kill, crow.
Last time I saw you the little crow was your prisoner. The other way around now. What happened?
You call that a war? The greatest army the North has ever seen cut to pieces by a southern king.
You don’t give the orders here.
Why aren’t you in chains?
What is he?
You fucking traitor! You fight for the crows now.
Is that right? You and the pretty crow do a lot of talking, Tormund. And when you’re done talking, do you get down on your knees and suck his cock--
We killed his friends. Thought you'd want to question this one.
Our forebearers settled the Vale thousands of years ago. We've fought off invaders ever since.
She told me nothing about you, Baelish. But I didn't need to hear from her. Moneylender. Whoremonger. You've been licking Tywin Lannister's boots so long, it's a wonder your tongue's not black.
And when Jon Arryn named you Master of Coin, no one cared. Always been a grubby job. Why not let a grubby man do it? But when I heard you were lurking here, fawning over the Lady Arryn...
Strange that within days of your arrival, you've married Lady Arryn and she's fallen through the Moon Door.
We were told there was a witness. A girl.
We'd like to speak with her.
Speak up, girl, you're not a damn kitchen mouse. Tell us what you saw.
Sansa Stark? You tell lies right to my face, you little worm?
Your father grew up right here in these halls. We hunted together many times. He was a fine man.
Lady Sansa.
You could see it in the way she raised that boy. Feeding him from her own teats when he was 10 years old.
Apologies, my lady. And to you as well, Baelish. We treated you a bit harshly.
Are you questioning our courage?
Of course we don't support the damn Lannisters.
Aye, it's time.
Lord Stark, you must be thirsty.
I promise you it isn't poisoned.
The younger one seems to have escaped the castle.
Still engaged to Joffrey.
And would again, My Lord.
Ah, the children. It is always the innocents who suffer.
The wrong brother sadly.
Not today, My Lord.
The Realm, My Lord.
Your father has proved to be an awful traitor, dear.
We have nothing but gratitude for your long service, good Ser.
A child's faith Such sweet innocence.
I wonder, Ser Barristan, who gave the king this wine?
Such a dutiful boy to make sure his Grace did not lack refreshment.
I'm afraid those birds have flown.
I fear Lord Renly has left the city.
They ought to be because they cost enough.
Come out and fight us, Blackfish. Yield the castle or we’ll hang him.
This is your last warning. Yield the castle.
Ser Jaime. We didn’t know you were coming.
Walder.
Apologies, Ser Jaime.
Do as he says.

Our father has instructed us to tell you that his alliance with the North can continue if his terms are met. Lord Frey requires a formal apology for your violation of your sacred oath to marry one of his daughters.
Father is old. It will put his heart at peace if he could see her wed to a good husband.  Black WALDER: And his recent experience has made him wary of long engagements.
He surprised us. He knows the castle better than anyone.
The Brotherhood Without Banners is rallying the commoners against us, raiding our supply trains and camps.
He’ll never yield, Father.
They want you to know you're surrounded.
They don't want you to sleep. They want to sap your spirit before
No.
You killed all the ravens.
Lord Stark went out of his way to make it your home.
Theon, listen to me. I serve Winterfell. Now Winterfell is yours. I'm bound by oath to serve you.
Run.
Five hundred Northmen wait outside the walls. You have 20 men. You can't win. Wait for nightfall and run.
Don't go home. Join the Night's Watch.
Once a man has taken the black, he's beyond reach of the law. All his past crimes are forgiven.
There are ways. Hidden passageways built so the Lords of Winterfell could escape. The road will be dangerous. But with a little luck...
The Night's Watch is an ancient, honorable order. You'll have opportunities there.
The opportunity to make amends for what you've done.
I've known you many years, Theon Greyjoy. You're not the man you're pretending to be. Not yet.
What is this? What are you doing?
Not everything. Not you. But they may come back. You have to go. Put on your warmest clothes. Pack as much food as you can carry and go north.
There are too many enemies in the south. Go to the Wall, to Jon. He'll look after you and let your mother know you're safe.

No more than I want to leave you. I pulled you into the world. Both of you. I've seen both your faces almost every day since. And for that, I consider myself very, very lucky. Go now with Hodor. Go on. I'll be right here.
Osha. You must protect them. You're the only one who can. You may have to protect them against your own kind.
Do it quickly.
You're right so far.
Must have been the farmer's boys.
They mustn't know. Bran would blame himself.
So far, hunting seems very similar to riding, my lord.
They're little boys.
Those boys are of far more value to you alive than dead.
Robb will have sent a force to retake Winterfell by now.
We could start the search fresh in the morning, my lord.
Theon, don't do this.
What are you doing? What have you done?
No!
I am.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: My lord.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I urge you not to make a hasty decision.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: He is worth more to you alive than dead. The Starks will pay. Please, Theon, think what you do.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Good.
Torrhen's Square is barely 40 leagues from here. How can the Lannisters strike so far north?
Do you need so many?
Hodor. Rouse Bran, will you? It's time for his lessons.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Well, they may have done. But they're gone from the world, along with much else. These are dreams, Bran, nothing more.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: What about all the dreams you had that didn't come true? Hmm? Right.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: This link is made of Valyrian steel. Only one maester in 100 wears it on his chain. It signifies that I have studied the higher mysteries. And all who study these mysteries try their hand at spells. I was no different. I was young. And what boy doesn't secretly wish for hidden powers to lift him out of his dull life into a special one? But in the end, for all my efforts, I got no more out of it than a thousand boys before me. Come on.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: All right. Maybe magic once was a mighty force in the world. But not anymore. The dragons are gone. The giants are dead. And the Children of the Forest forgotten.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: We’ve brought up eight barrels of ale from the cellar. Perhaps we’ll find out.
Pardon, my lord, my lady. A rider in the night from your sister.
If this news is true, and the Lannisters conspire against the throne, who but you can protect the king?
The king rode for a month to ask Lord Stark’s help. He’s the only one he trusts. You swore the king an oath, my lord.
A different time. Different king.
Maintenance of a Holdfast generally falls to the lord of that Holdfast.
We can spare four masons for a week, My Lord. Will that be sufficient to repair your walls?
We didn't want him here all day, did we?
Nor did I. But listening to people you'd rather not listen to is one of your responsibilities as Lord of Winterfell. (Bran sighs). Lord Portan.
It's all right. We're done here.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Ned?
Is that you? Is that really you?
You’re not a dream.
I’ve missed you, big brother.
I want to be brave.
I’m not.
I don’t want to die.
No, no water.
Please. Listen to me, Ned.
If Robert finds out, he’ll kill him, you know he will. You have to protect him. Promise me, Ned. Promise me.
Promise me, Ned. Promise me.

Your son was butchered at the Red Wedding, Lord Manderly. But you refused the call. You swore allegiance to House Stark, Lord Glover, but in their hour of greatest need, you refused the call. And you, Lord Cerwyn, your father was skinned alive by Ramsay Bolton. Still you refuse the call. But House Mormont remembers. The North remembers. We know no king but the King in the North whose name is Stark. I don’t care if he’s a bastard. Ned Stark’s blood runs through his veins. He’s my king from this day until his last day.
Welcome to Bear Island.
I doubt it. My mother wasn’t a great beauty or any other kind of beauty. She was a great warrior, though. She died fighting for your brother, Robb.
I think we’ve had enough small talk. What are you here?
I remember what it said. “Bear Island knows no king but the King in the North whose name is Stark.”
As far as I understand, you’re a Snow and Lady Sansa is a Bolton. Or is she a Lannister? I’ve heard conflicting reports.
If you say so. In any case, you don’t just want my allegiance. You want my fighting men.
I understand that I’m responsible for Bear Island and all who live here. So why should I sacrifice one more Mormont life for someone else’s war?
I don’t know you, Ser…?
All right, Ser Davos of House Seaworth. How is it you understand how I feel?
Go on, Ser Davos.
Is this true?
House Mormont has kept faith with House Stark for 1,000 years. We will not break faith today.
We are not a large house, but we’re a proud one. And every man from Bear Island fights with the strength of 10 mainlanders.
Father, Smith, Warrior, Mother, Maiden, Crone, Stranger. I am his and he is mine from this day until the end of my days.
His name is Aegon.
I don't need you to remind me of that.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I will not hide underground. I pledged to fight for the North, and I will fight.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I wish you good fortune, cousin.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I don't plan on knitting by the fire while men fight for me. I might be small, Lord Glover, and I might be a girl, but I am every bit as much a Northerner as you.
And I don't need your permission to defend the North.
We'll begin training every man, woman, boy, and girl on Bear Island.
A lord? Nothing at all?
Not important? We named you King in the North.
I have trained my men, women and children. I have fought before. I can fight again.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Winter is here, Your Grace.  We need the King in the North in the north.
"Your Grace."
Come here, Sansa.
Do you know how far the fall is?

Neither do I, precisely. Hundreds of feet. It's fascinating what happens to bodies when they hit the rocks from such a height. The impact breaks them right apart. Like eggs dropped on the floor. Sometimes pieces remain intact. You'll find the head sitting on its own. Every hair in place. Blue eyes staring at nothing. I know what you did.
Don't be coy with me, you little whore. You kissed him. You kissed Petyr.
I saw you! You can't lie to me because I saw it with my own eyes.
Liar! Whore! He is mine!
My father, my husband, my sister, they all stood between us and now they're all dead. That's what happens to people who stand between Petyr and me. Look down! Look down! Look down! Look down!
You want her? This empty-headed child?
She's just like her mother. She'll never love you. I lied for you. I killed for you. Why did you bring her here? Why?
Lord Baelish.
A beautiful gift for a beautiful boy.

Oh, do take down that hood, child. Don't you think know who you are? You think I'd let my intended leave the Eyrie on urgent business without knowing what that business was? I let him go so he could bring you here to me.
My flesh and blood.
You mustn't call me that in front of anyone else.
No one can know you're here. It would put us in a very precarious position.
The Lannisters want to destroy us. They've been trying for years. Now they know what it feels like.
And on top of everything else, they made you marry that filthy troll.
I don't believe that for a moment. Did he force himself on you?
Good. Robin, this is your cousin Sansa. But you're not to call her Sansa in front of anyone but Uncle Petyr and myself. Do you understand? Sansa, this is my son Robin.
Robin, show Sansa to her chamber. Take the back stairs.
What took you so long?
She's here. We've spent more than enough time on her for one evening.
Let's get married tonight.
There's only one Lord of the Vale. The others can all hang. Lurking and simpering on the stairs like buzzards the moment my husband died, trying to get their claws in me.
I'm done waiting, Petyr. We had our wedding night many years ago. Or don't you remember?

What wife would do for you the things I've done for you? What wife would trust you the way I've trusted you? When you gave me those drops and told me to pour them into Jon's wine, my husband's wine... when you told me to write a letter to Cat telling her it was the Lannisters...
(nods)
(LAUGHS)
I'm warning you. I'm going to scream when my husband makes love to me. I'm going to scream so loud, they'll hear me clear across the Narrow Sea.
Your mother always had a sweet tooth, you know?

Oh, yes. At suppertime, she would go straight for honey cakes, candied almonds, custard. Anything sweet. Eventually, your grandfather had to assign a septa to watch her at meals. Cat was the firstborn daughter after all. It was important that she remain desirable so Father could arrange a good match for her and she was starting to get fat.

This is before she married your father and moved to the North. By the time you were born, your father's austerity had become hers. Marriage changes people. I didn't mean for you to stop. Go on, enjoy them. How do you like them?

Oh, gods, no. Petyr had three crates brought all the way from King's Landing. He knew you liked lemon cakes. He's so kind. He really cares for you. Think where you'd be without him. In their clutches and tried for murder.
He feels responsible for you.
Why? Why does he feel responsible for you?
Loved your mother.
That's what you wanted to say.

Your mother never loved him. Never. Cat always went straight for the sweetest thing. The most obvious thing. Your Uncle Brandon. Your handsome, arrogant, cruel Uncle Brandon. He almost killed Petyr in a duel. And your mother loved him anyway. And now Petyr is risking his life to save you, the daughter of a woman who didn't love him no more than those whores in his brothels. Has he told you about them?
He hasn't told you about the vile things they do with their bodies? The vile things they let him do with their bodies?
Are you pregnant?
I wasn't asking about Tyrion. What have you let Petyr do with your body?
Your young, pretty body.
Don't lie to me.
I'll know if you lie.

Shh. Shh. Shh. It's all right. It will all be all right. You'll be a widow soon. They'll execute that dwarf for murdering the king and you'll be free to marry Robin. You'll be the Lady of the Vale. Hmm.
He sent it to me, not you.
A war? Your son against the Lannisters?
Family means everything to me.
Hush now, darling. You just ate.
Soon, love, soon.
Go on, sweet. Time for your bath. I'll feed you after.
Of course I fear for the safety of my son!
The knights of the Vale will stay in the Vale where they belong, to protect their Lord.
You wish to confess your crimes?
Skycells always break them. Speak, Imp. Meet your gods as an honest man.
Silence!
What do you think you’re doing?
You’ve had your little joke. I trust you enjoyed it. Mord, take him back to the dungeon. But this time find a smaller cell, with a steeper floor.
If you’re tried and found guilty, then by the king’s own laws you will pay with your life.
We have no executioner in the Eyrie. Life is more elegant here. Open the Moon Door.
You want a trial, my Lord Lannister. Very well. My son will listen to whatever you have to say, and you will hear his judgement. Then you will leave, by one door or the other.
You have that right.
Ser Vardis… You’re quiet. Did you want to avenge my husband?
You demanded a trial by combat.
The Kingslayer is hundreds of miles from here.
The trial will be today.
I think that we can assume that no one is willing -
You don’t fight with honor!

You bring him here without permission? You pollute my home with his presence? Your aunt has done a bad thing, Robin, a very bad thing. You remember her, don't you? Isn't he beautiful? And strong too. Jon knew it. His last words were, "the seed is strong." He wanted everyone to know what a good, strong boy his son would grow up to be. Look at him, the Lord of all the Vale.
To stay away from them! Not to bring one here!
It is.
He's Tyrion the Imp of House Lannister. He killed your father. He murdered the Hand of the King!
You will watch your tongue! These men are Knights of the Vale. Every one of the loved Jon Arryn. Every one of them would die for me.
Shh... Shh... Shh, my sweet boy. He's just trying to frighten us. Lannisters are all liars. No one will hurt my baby.
Perhaps you will, my little love.
Ser Vardis, my sister's guest is weary. Take him down below so he can rest. Introduce him to Mord.
No, I can’t let them…
Bit of chop the last few days but nothing too awful. Good to see the old chap is still guarding the Harbor.
Vintnors say this might be the best year for red grapes in half a century. If we come to a satisfactory arrangement, I’ll be sure to send the Arbor’s finest your way.
Some consider usury distasteful, dishonorable. Pure nonsense, of course.
Did you know that at one point Maegor the third tried to outlaw it in the Seven Kingdoms? Wanted to arrest anyone caught charging interest and cut off both their hands.
If a man charges low interest on a loan then he has nothing to gain and everything to lose, so why charge him? Whereas a reward makes a man willing to gamble.
You are the world's best gamblers. And all those bets you won built this.
Oh, nonsense. Work's over. Do you sing?

It's not a gift, it’s a skill, anyone can learn it. “So give me a kiss by the Long Canall and give me two kisses in Salty Town, for we are going to die, tomorrow. The bareheaded beggar, the king with his crown…”
Lord Tywin and I already determined that I shall be the Master of Ships long before you--
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Lord Tywin, it's a great honor to have been granted a seat on this council. I--
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Your own brother said this to you?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Do you remember the precise nature of this threat?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: And he said this to you at a meeting of the small council?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I beg your pardon? You said you were his...
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: My lord.
My friends, the hour has come. Madness has overtaken this city and grasped in its claws my children. But now we must drive it back under the rocks whence it came. Madness has had its day!
The Iron Bank has called one tenth of the Crown's debts. Given the expense of rebuilding the royal -
With winter coming, half what they ask. Less.
Well, House Tyrell could front the gold, and the the Crown will pay us back in time, or I'll have words with my daughter.
Absolutely.
(After a moment, looks at her surprised). Me? (Qyburn smiles).
I will be honored, Your Grace.
My very own Kingsguard? Please express -
(Raises his hands). Of course, of course, (Gathers his belongings, and then gets up). I'll give your regards to the Titan of Braavos.
High Septon, this is a rather shocking thing to hear.
From House Tyrell and the people of the Reach, Your Grace, it is my honor to present you with this wedding cup.
May you and my daughter Margaery drink deep and live long.
I shall be honored, Your Grace.
Oh, Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: The honor is all mine, Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: What’s happening?
My mother...
Your Grace, I would be willing to serve provisionally as the King's Hand until he becomes old enough--
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Oh.
According to our records, Jeor Mormont is Lord Commander.
We received no report from the maester at Castle Black.
This is irregular.
The archmaester will discuss these irregularities with you. In the meantime, you are permitted to use the library. Follow me.
No women or children!
Prince Doran.
I put very little stock in it myself.  Prophecies of doom are never in short supply, especially when winter comes.
The north has dulled many good maester's wits.  I'm not sure if it's the cold, the drink, or the company.
A crippled boy claims to have seen dead men on the march beyond the wall thanks to the magical help of a raven with three eyes.
Perhaps you need more scribing work to discipline your mind.
That does seem far more likely than magic birds talking to cripples.
Don't forget the Prophet Lodock that promised the Drowned God would rise up and destroy Aegon the Conquerer.
Is he the one whose father and brother were just burned alive?
It brings to mind the work of Davy Moonstone, the charlatan who claimed to see the children of the forest.
Horrible business.

They came to us from White Harbor and Barrowton, from Fairmarket and King's Landing, from north and south, from east and west. They died protecting men, women, and children who will never know their names. It is for us to remember them. Our brothers, we shall never see their like again.
And now their watch is ended.
I'm not a lord, my dear. Not for many, many years. Every man who joins the Night's Watch renounces all former titles. Among other things. What is your name?
Ah, for the gillyflower. Lovely.
And the child?
Tarly do you remember the oath you swore when you joined this order?
Gilly, you and your son will be our guests for the time being. We certainly cannot send you back beyond the Wall.
Good. Samwell, fetch a quill and inkwell. I hope your penmanship is better than your swordplay.
We had 44 ravens at last count. Make sure they're all fed. Every one of them flies tonight.
And what is it that couldn't wait until morning, Tarly?
That it was you? Who else but Samwell Tarly would be wasting candles to read in the middle of the night?

Oh, no need. I know my way around this library better than any other place in Castle Black. Thousands of books and no eyes to read them. Old age is a wonderful source of ironies if nothing else. But you didn't answer my question, what are you reading?
Ah, the Wildlings. I can assure you that the closest Maester Faull ever got to a Wildling was this very library.

Yes. Imagine the stories Wildlings tell about us! Love is the death of duty. I told that to your friend Jon Snow once. He didn't listen and neither did you. That's why you abandoned your watch atop the wall to come here and read about the things that may have happened to the girl you love.
Yes you do.
Yes you do! I heard it in your voice when you first brought her to me. I remember how it sounds! I was in love once.
You can imagine all manner of horrors befalling that poor girl and her child, is it so difficult to imagine that an old person was once, more or less, like you?

You know who I was before I came here? What I could've been if I'd only said the word? Of course you do. I met many girls when I was Aemon Targaryen. A future king always does. Some of them were quite forward in their attempts to win my affections. One of them succeeded. I was very young.

Ah ha. I could tell you everything about her. Who she was. How we met. The color of her eyes and the shape of her nose. I can see her right in front of me. She's more real than you are. We could spend all night trading tales of lost love. Nothing makes the past a sweeter place to visit than the prospect of imminent death. Go to bed Tarly.
l did indeed. Perhaps you would be kind enough to assist me. Tell me, did you ever wonder why the men of the Night's Watch take no wives and father no children?

So they will not love. Love is the death of duty. lf the day should ever come when your lord father was forced to choose between honor on the one hand and those he loves on the other, what would he do?
Then Lord Stark is one man in 10,000. Most of us are not so strong. What is honor compared to a woman's love? And what is duty against the feel of a newborn son in your arms? Or a brother's smile?
We're all human. Oh, we all do our duty when there's no cost to it. Honor comes easy then. Yet sooner or later in every man's life there comes a day when it is not easy. A day when he must choose.
Oh, it hurts, boy. Oh, yes. l know.

The gods were cruel when they saw fit to test my vows. They waited till l was old. What could l do when the ravens brought the news from the South: the ruin of my House, the death of my family? l was helpless, blind, frail. But when l heard they had killed my brother's son and his poor son and the children! Even the little children.
My father was Maekar, the first of his name. My brother Aegon reigned after him when l had refused the throne. And he was followed by his son Aerys whom they called the Mad King.

l'm a maester of the Citadel, bound in service to Castle Black and the Night's Watch. l will not tell you to stay or go. You must make that choice yourself and live with it for the rest of your days. As l have.
There he is. That laugh. Egg! Egg laughed like that. One of the first things I remember about him.
And before that, he was a jolly fellow. Like this one.
Get him south, Gillyflower. Before it’s too late.
Egg! Egg! Mothers looking for you! Egg!
Egg! Egg.. I dreamed... that I was old
Samwell, you will assist me in the rookery and library.
Certainly. And keep a fire burning in his chambers.
We took you for a man of The Night's Watch.
As you wish.
And she's alone, under siege, no family to guide her or protect her. Her last relation thousands of miles away, useless, dying.
A Targaryen, alone in the world, is a terrible thing.
Lord Commander.
Oh, like a hundred-year-old man slowly freezing to death.
Half the men hate you already, Lord Commander. Do it.

That doesn't matter. You do. You will find little joy in your command. But with luck, you will find the strength to do what needs to be done. Kill the boy, Jon Snow. Winter is almost upon us. Kill the boy, and let the man be born.
How many winters have you seen, Lord Tyrion?
All of them brief?

This summer has lasted nine. But reports from the Citadel tell us the days grow shorter. The Starks are always right eventually : winter is coming. This one will be long and dark things will come with it.

The Night's Watch is the only thing standing between the realm and what lies beyond. And it has become an army of undisciplined boys and tired old men. There are less than a thousand of us now. We can't man the other castles on the Wall. We can't properly patrol the wilderness. We've barely enough resources to keep our lads armed and fed.
When winter does come, gods help us all if we're not ready.
We have just over 100 men. And that's including stewards and builders. And me. We can't afford to lose a single man. We must remember our first responsibility. We are the watchers on the Wall.
Does anyone wish to speak for candidates before we cast our tokens for the 998th Lord Commander of the Night's Watch?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Is there anyone else?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: If there's no one else, we will begin the voting. The triangular tokens count for Ser Alliser Thorne. The square tokens for Ser Denys Mallister. Each brother will--
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Samwell Tarly. Go on.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: It is time.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: If we beheaded every ranger who lay with a girl, the Wall would be manned by headless men.
None of us are free. We are men of the Night's Watch. But we won't be taking your head today, Jon Snow. Go on.
Yes, you are.
He told the truth.
I grew up in King's Landing.
(To Davos) We need to stop her!
(Walks forward, interrupting Melisandre). All you men were named in the Light of the Seven! Is this how you treat the Gods of your fathers? Are you so eager to spit on your ancestors?
This woman will lead him into a war he cannot win.
(Scoffs) King. Since that boar killed his brother, every lord wants a coronation.
As do I, but loyal service means telling hard truths. He's surrounded by fools and fanatics, but he trusts you, Davos. (Davos picks up the sword and inspects it).  If you tell him the truth…
(Stands up). I owe you an apology, my King. My duty is to serve. You have chosen the new God over the Old Gods. May the Lord of Light watch over us all. Shall we drink together?
A cup of wine to honor the one true God.
Bran…
It is your sister's hand, but the Queen's words.
This is a royal command, My Lord.
All of them, My Lord?
They have.
Yeah.
Theon Greyjoy! The lady is our guest.
Are the two mutually exclusive in your experience?
Chances are I won't be nearby the next time that happens.
Why did you come here?
Why? What are you afraid of?
Owls and Shadowcats and...
Bran...
Lords?
And failed rebellions.
Good.
: Mhmm.
Hmm...
No. A common saying, but not their official motto.
We're still on their words.
You do know them. Think.
That's House Martell.
House Hornwood.
Those are Tully words... Your mother's. Are we playing a game?
You know it is.
Your mother had to leave Winterfell to protect the family.
Your mother sat by your bed for three weeks while you slept.
When you were born, I was the one who pulled you from your mother. I placed you in her arms. From that moment until the moment she dies, she will love you. Absolutely. Fiercely.
I still can't tell you, but she will be home soon.
No, I don't.
Sometimes I worry you're too smart for your own good.
And where is that written?
Hmm... If the saddle Lord Tyrion designed actually works, you could learn to shoot a bow from horseback.
Dothraki boys learn when they're four years old. Why shouldn't you?
He has no memory of that day.
The boy has lost the use of his legs.
It's time we reviewed the accounts, my lady. You'll want to know how much this royal visit has cost us.
Poole went south with Lord Stark, my lady. We need a new steward, and there are several other appointments that require our immediate attention...
Very good, my Lord. My Lady.
The boy was always sure-footed before.
What, is there going to be a battle in the Godswood? Too easily words of war become acts of war. We don't know the truth yet. Lord Stark must be told of this.
I’m told he drinks all night.
I am a member of the Small Council. The king’s small --
Obviously not. I’m here to lend my wisdom and my support.
Your Grace.

And now, how to avert disaster with our current predicament. This High Sparrow. I have dealt with fanatics of every description, Your Grace. Not setting them off, that’s the most important thing. You are beset with enemies both within and without.
I am advising the king on our current predicament.

As bad as Lord Varys was Varys was, Qyburn is worse. I told them all. I told them. He’s arrogant, dangerous. You don’t get thrown out of the Citadel without good reason. And no one listened to my advice, so here we are.
And what he's done to Gregor Clegane is an abomination. We never sanctioned this, this experiment. And I for one think it would be in our best interest to have the beast dest --
Well, um...I would say Ser Gerold Hightower had a seat on the Mad King’s council. Of course, that was the Mad King. King Robert saw things differently.
We don't have a king or queen.
Well, we have to choose someone.
Aye.
My lords. Lady Walda has given birth. A boy. Red-cheeked and healthy.
Poisoned by his enemies.
Forgive me, my lord.
She’s resting, my lord.
At once, my lord.
Shall I have a grave dug for her, my lord? Or would you rather the men build a pyre?
At once, Your Grace.
Get out. Get out!
Listen to your friend.
You don’t know what I am.
Everybody wants to know their future. Until they know their future.
Your blood. Give me a taste.
Three questions you get. You won’t like the answers.
You’ll never wed the prince. You’ll wed the king.
Oh yes, you’ll be queen. For a time. In comes another. Younger, more beautiful, to cast you down and take all you hold dear.
No. The king will have 20 children, and you will have 3.
Gold will be their crowns. Gold their shrouds.
Me Fati! Khaleesi vazha anhaan qorasokh anni, ch'anha afichak mae m'arakhoon anni!
You are a foreigner. You do not command me.
A Khal who takes orders from a foreign whore is no Khal.
First you have to kill me!
Lady Sansa, Lord Tyrion to see you. Should I--
Do you like it, Nana?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Thank you.
Your Grace.
It’s too hot, my lady.
My lady. We should have guards, my lady.
Stop, my lady. You'll ruin your dress.

"And who are you," the proud lord said "That I must bow so low?" "Only a cat of a different coat "That's all the truth I know "In a coat of gold or a coat of red "A lion still has claws "And mine are long and sharp, my lord "As long and sharp as yours" And so he spoke, and so he spoke That Lord of Castamere But now the rains weep o'er his hall With no one there to hear Yes, now the rains weep o'er his hall And not a soul to hear
He was our Lord Commander!
He never should have been!

A rare find for the fighting pits, my friends. Not your usual street tough old drunken pirate. This one’s a Westerosi knight from an ancient house, trained in sword and lance. he fought beside the Stag King Robert on the far side of the Narrow Sea. He was first from the breach during the siege of Pyke, slaughtering 50 men with his flaming sword. He killed the great Khal Drogo in single combat. Betrayed by his woman, he sold himself into slavery to repay his debts. Bidding begins at 12 gold honors.
13. Do I have 14?
15? 15 golds honors. Do I have 16?
Sold!
He’s funny. You have to give him that.
#NAME?
Got a lot of fight in you, huh?
That or a galley slave. He looks strong enough.

And then chop off his cock. We can sell it for a fortune. A dwarf’s cock has magic powers.

Come on.

Mhysa!
*in Low Valyrian* I do not understand, my queen. I do not understand, my queen.
*crying, speaking Low Valyrian*

Suck me off! Suck me off, you bitch!
The Boltons are defeated. The war is over. Winter has come. If the maesters are right, it’ll be the coldest one in a thousand years. We should ride home and wait out the coming storms.

They burned most everything in the Riverlands, our fields, our granaries, our homes. They took our women and then they took ‘em again. When they was done, they butchered them as if they was animals. They covered our children in pitch and lit them on fire.
They weren’t thieves, they didn’t steal nothing. They even left something behind, your Grace.
None, your… Hand. The one who was leading them… Taller by a foot than any man I’ve ever met, saw him cut the blacksmith, saw him take the head off a horse with a single swing of his sword.

I have nowhere else to go. I’ve taken her to every healer in Braavos. I’ve spent every penny I had. She suffers every day of her life. I just want it to end.
The Queen in the North!
Over there, go!
Take a company of men, secure the perimeter.
So it's that one there at the top, on the right.
A dancing king, prancing down his bloodstained halls to the tune of a twisted demon monkey.

Please! Please, no. They made me do it! They made me! They made me!

That's it lad, put it inside. Get the fire going again. We're all freezing.
I've got to have a piss.

I knew him and his father before him. The Starks are gone now.
Everyone knows. The Boltons.

Her mother's dead.
For you, my lady.

King in the North!
Bring her about!

To your health.

Valar morghulis.
Open gates!
(Valyrian) It’s good luck to rub a dwarf’s head.
(Across the room). The mother of dragons!
What makes you worth that much?
I bet you bloody are.
Draw!  Loose!
I barely touched her. And you charge twice as much as every other brothel. And this is what I get, crying.
What you carrying?
Don't like wine. Woman's drink. (Returns to his work).
I'll find you a horse.
Where are you headed, then?
Riverrun. Staying off the kingsroad, are you? They get you no matter where you go. You can't win.
Looks like you're safe enough, though. Meaning no offense, my lady, but I wouldn't tangle with you. Seven blessings to you.
Your Grace.
I never liked the skinny ones. Like drinking from a puddle. Not that I'm averse to drinking from a puddle every now and again, you understand.
Yeah!
That's him, all right. I saw him fight at the tourney for Ser Willem Frey's wedding.
Open the gates!
Riders coming in! Let's get these horses seen to!
Stormbringer.
He's gone. Our king is gone.
Don't be afraid. He’s announcing our arrival.

Crow!

Let's have a feel there.

King of the North!

He rode through the streets of the city / Down from his hill on high / O'er the winds and the steps and the cobble / He rode to a woman's sigh / For she was his secret treasure / She was his shame and his bliss / And a chain and a keep are nothing / Compared to a woman's kiss / For hands of gold are always cold / But a woman's hands are warm / For hands of gold are always cold / But a woman's hands are--
You stand in the presence of Kinvara, High Priestess of the Red Temple of Volantis, the Flame of Truth, the Light of Wisdom, the First Servant of the Lord of Light.
We’ve never had a queen. Not once.
We speak the Common Tongue.
Our brothers and sister died fighting these scum. Now you invite them to our city and drink wine with them.
You are a stranger here. So why do you represent Meereen in these talks with our enemies?
We don’t know you. We don’t trust you. We know Torgo Nudho. We fight with him against the Masters. We trust him.
They say her dragons are bigger than Aegons.
If it's true, if she lays siege to King's Landing prices will triple.
One more round.  It's a long ride.
And you, Grey Worm? You want to drink wine with these men? The men who tore us from our mothers’ arms and sold us at auction like cattle?
I had me dagger.
Well, fuck me!
What did he do?
Why Riverrun?
Why not kill him?

I was at Whispering Wood. I saw him. They dragged him out of the woods and threw him down before the king.
Yah! Yah!
Put your backs into it!
Clear!

There's been some trouble with the Freys up at The Twins, so we're part of the army that's been sent to keep the peace.

Girls take care of their papas when their papas grow old. Boys just go off to fight in someone else's wars.

Hang him!

Text: [Laughing] You ready to head home to Winterfell, eh? [Men laughing]
Here we are.

I'm just about to start it.  WOMAN 1: Good luck.  MAN 2: Protect shell right up to the wall.  MAN 1: All right, I'll put another one on it.   MAN 3: He's headed down to the cellar. I'll need a cart over here.  MAN 4: That's it, thanks. Thank you.
Bastard!
Hey, oh.  Where are you going?
Fuck off.
Arya Stark is dead.
Maester's name is Wolkan.
He's a thousand miles away.
Mm-hmm.
You tell us.  You're the one impersonating her sister.
Lady Sansa is too busy to waster her breath on you, just like us.  So for the last time, fuck off.
You tell her.
Because if you don't I'll hit you right in the fucking face as hard as I can.
When we turned around she had gone, My Lady.  She was nothing.  Some winter town girl.
Don't trouble yourself over it, My Lady.  We'll find her.
Lord Gendry!
You owe me one.  Don't forget that.
Hey!
Repent!
Seven blessings.
Sorry ’bout that.
Gendry?
Feast over yet, is it?
I’ll have one.
The Rains of Castamere!
And why do you think the farmers abandoned those villages? Because the wildlings raided them for years. Cut them down! Just like they did this boy's people.
You're an abomination!
There's no Rodrick here.
It's cold.  We're busy.  So, you know, best fuck off.
The Lady of Winterfell, Lady Stark.
You sit there.  Right there.  Don't move.
Go tell Lady Stark.  I'll watch her.
I'm not.
We told her to wait.  We were standing right next to her.
She comes in asking for Ser Rodrick and Maester Llewyn.
Gendry!
You saved the day.
For the rest of my days.
Pour another one.
Shame on you!
Thank you.
He's here.
Aye.
Oh, The Bear and the Maiden Fair!
You're born of sin!
Gendry!
Just a little more...
If we yield, we live? Is that what it says on this paper here?
Abomination!
Gendry!
Lord Gendry!
Gendry!
That's easy, isn't it?
(to GENDRY) Come, my lord.
You're wearing a black cloak again.
It appears my trusting nature got the better of me. It's happened before. I was hoping your loyalty was real when you pledged yourself to us, Jon Snow, truly I was.
All of your vows? She wasn't enough to turn ya, eh? Were you enough to turn her?
Did you see her again at Castle Black?
And?
Your doing?
We'll drink to her.
Of all the ways I'd kill you, poison would be the last.
Ygritte.
No, it's a proper Northern drink, Jon Snow. You did well. Fought hard. Killed some of our strongest men. One of our giants went into your tunnel and never came out again. Mag the Mighty.
He was their king. Last of a bloodline that stretches back before the First Men.
Mag and Grenn.
Kullback, could I trouble you for something to eat? I don't imagine our guest has eaten anything in quite some time. So. You're here to strike a bargain?
You know I know you're low on arrows. You're low on oil. You're low on men. How many are left? 50?

I showed you everything I had. The whole army, a hundred thousand strong, and what did you do? You fired on us with everything you had. It wasn't much. Soon as I saw that I sent four hundred men to climb the wall. An unmanned stretch five miles west of here. A lot of them will die climbing, but most of them will be over by the end of the day. This is me being honest with you, Jon Snow, which is more than you've ever done for me. My people have bled enough. We're not here to conquer, we're here to hide behind your wall just like you. We need your tunnel. Now we both know that winter is coming and if my people aren't south of the wall when it comes in earnest, we'll all end up worse than dead. Wanna strike a bargain with me? Here's a bargain. You go back, you open the gates to us and I swear to you that no one else will die. Refuse and we'll kill every last man in Castle Black.

Ah! Oh, that's why you're here. I reckon you could do it before any of them could stop ya. They'd kill you of course, they'd kill ya slow, but ya knew that when you came in here. Are you capable of that, Jon Snow? Killing a man in his own tent when he's just offered ya peace? Is that what the Night's Watch is? Is that what you are?
You attacking us?
Hold! To me. To me.
Stand down! I said my people have bled enough and I meant it.
Never had the pleasure.
We're not in the seven kingdoms and you're not dressed for this weather.
We do not kneel.
All the same, we do not kneel.
Always the artists.
How many men were here?
And you know what those men are now?

It's not impossible. You don't go far betting against Mormont. But dead or alive, he took a big gamble coming north. And he lost. His best fighting men are dead. And whether he's Lord Commander of the Night's Watch or a blue-eyed corpse, he's a long way from home. Tormund. Climb the Wall. Take Orell and 20 good men. And take this one. He knows Castle Black's defenses better than any of us. And if he's useful, good. If not throw him off the Wall. See if crows can fly.
Hide near Castle Black. When I give the signal, hit them in the night. They've got a big old wall to hide behind, but it only guards one side.
Aye.  If you do your job.  Ah.
Send your eagle above the Wall every night. When it's time, I'm going to light the biggest fire the North has ever seen.
Was it hard for you to kill the Halfhand?
You liked him? I like you, but if you're playing us false, it won't be hard for me to kill you. I've got wildling blood in my veins. These are my people.
Well, how could you understand?

Do you know what it takes to unite 90 clans, half of whom want to massacre the other half for one insult or another? They speak seven different languages in my army. The Thenns hate the Hornfoots. The Hornfoots hate the ice-river clans. Everyone hates the cave people. So you know how I got moon worshippers and cannibals and giants to march together in the same army?
I told them we were all going to die if we don't get south. Because that's the truth.
He's a warg. He can enter the mind of animals, see through their eyes. He's scouting for us.
Orell.
Where were you this time?
What did you see?
Stand, boy. We don't kneel for anyone beyond the Wall.
So, you're Ned Stark's bastard. Thank you for the gift, Lord of Bones. You can leave us.
The girl likes you. You like her back, Snow? That why you want to join us?
This chicken eater you thought was king is Tormund Giantsbane.
He was our enemy and I'm glad he's dead.
He was my brother once. Back when he had a whole hand. What were you doing with him?
Why?
But here you are, a traitor kneeling before the King-beyond-the-Wall.
Why do you want to join us, Jon Snow?
No, I don't think so. I think what you want most of all is to be a hero. I'll ask you one last time ... why do you want to join us?
You saw what?
You're telling me you saw one of them? And why would that make you desert your brothers?
We'll need to find you a new cloak.
So here we are.
When we first met you were my prisoner. And now, for our last meeting…
No, but it will be.
He wants me to bend the knee. And he wants the free folk to fight for him. I’ll give him this much: he’s bold.
Oh, aye. I respect him. If he gets what he wants, I expect he’ll be a better ruler than the fools sitting on the Iron Throne the last hundred years. But I’ll never serve him.
That’s right. But I don’t want them bleeding for Stannis Baratheon either.
Pride? Fuck my pride. This isn’t about that.
They followed me because they respected me. Because they believed in me. The moment I kneel for a Southern king that’s all gone.
Oh, I am afraid. No shame in that. How will they do it? Beheading? Hanging?

Bad way to go. I’ll be honest with you, I don’t want to die. And burnt to death, I don’t want people to remember me like that. Scorched and screaming. But it’s better than betraying everything I believe.
You’re a good lad. Truly, you are. But if you can’t understand why I won’t enlist my people in a foreigner’s war, there’s no point explaining.
The freedom to make my own mistakes was all I ever wanted.
This was my home for many years. I wish you good fortune in the wars to come.

Lady Mormont speaks harshly and truly. My son died for Robb Stark, the Young Wolf. I didn’t think we’d find another king in my lifetime. I didn’t commit my men to your cause ‘cause I didn’t want more Manderlys dying for nothing. But I was wrong. Jon Snow avenged the Red Wedding. He is the White Wolf. The King in the North.
(nods)
(Speaking Valyrian) The noble Hizdahr zo Loraq begs an audience with the Queen.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Open the gate!
He's handsome. You didn't tell me he was handsome.
Forgive me, Your Grace. You told me to come at once if there was anything important.
That's brave.
With all my heart, Your Grace. I have come to love you from afar. Tales of your courage and wisdom have never been far from my ears. And those tales have taken root deep inside of me.
Faith is the way, Father.
You mutilated him. You gave me your word.
And where is the queen mother?
There’s something wrong.
Cersei is not here. Tommen is not here. Why do you think they are not here?

Forget about the bloody gods and listen to what I’m telling you. Cersei understands the consequences of her absence and she is absent anyway, which means she does not intend to suffer those consequences. The trial can wait. We all need to leave.
Loras. Stay with me.
Let me through. Let me through. Get out of my way.
Let us through!
You look radiant, Your Grace.
It's the word that came to mind.
We're going to be sisters soon. We should be friends.
A better dancer than a singer, I'm afraid.
Of course. They play it so often here at court.
Not as well as you, I'm sure.
(nods)
Gone.
Come on. Come walk with me.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I remember the first time I saw you in the throne room. I'd never seen anyone who looked so unhappy. I want very much for you to be happy, Sansa, and so does my grandmother.

Text: Some women like tall men. Some like short men. Some like hairy men. Some like bald men. Gentle men, rough men, ugly men, pretty men, pretty girls. Most women don't know what they like until they've tried it. And, sadly, so many of us get to try so little before we're old and gray. Tyrion may surprise you. From what I've heard, he's quite experienced.
