Text: It can be. We're very complicated, you know. Pleasing us takes practice.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Yes, sweet girl. My mother taught me.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I know you did this.
Lies come easily to you. Everyone knows that. But innocence, decency, concern? You’re not very good at those, I’m afraid. Perhaps that’s why your son was so eager to cast you aside for me.
I don’t want to see you again.
Leave.
Get out you hateful bitch!
The Book of the Mother, Your Holiness. Chapter three, verse 12.
“Smoothing what was jagged, so does a woman’s love calm a man’s brute nature. A wife salves her husband’s wounds, a mother sings her son to sleep.”
For year I pretended to love the poor, the afflicted. I had pity for them, but I never loved them. They disgusted me.
Of course.
No.
It’s just...the desires that once drove me no longer do.
Forgive me. Sometimes the truth path is hard to find.
My grandmother?
Septa Unella has been my true friend and counselor.
Grandmother.
Grandmother, please.
You marched against the High Sparrow, against the Faith.
The gods could have punished you and Father, but they didn’t. They showed mercy.
Loras’s only hope is to confess his crimes and repent. If he does, the Faith will allow him to return to Highgarden. He’ll have to renounce his name and title...
...and live his life as a penitent.
He can begin again.
As a free man.
I am the queen. It is my duty to serve my husband the king.
But you should leave, Grandmother. Your place is at home.
You must.
Go home. Find comfort in prayer and good works.
The Mother watches over us all.
Seven blessings to you, Grandmother.
Shall we pray?
Everyone knew about him and Renly.
But he was the king’s brother.
Cersei is behind this.
You call me forward?
I am the queen.
They are lies.
All of them.
Yes, I swear it.
What?
Tommen! Tommen!
You can’t do this, I am the queen! Tommen, Tommen! I am your queen, how dare you!
No, Your Grace.
We’ll be together again soon and everything will be better than it was before.
Have you spoken to him at all, the High Sparrow?
Yes, I have. He’s not quite what we thought he was, is he?
You think I’m mad. I sound mad.
He is. There’s something about him. His way of looking at the world. It’s not an easy thing admitting to yourself what you really are. It’s taken me a while. He’s helped me.

Ah, yes, I visited their hovels and I fed them soup and I made sure I was seen doing it. I never gave them what they really needed, though. I’ve had lots of time to think about how good I was at seeming good. All those stories I told myself about who I was and why I did the things I did. There were so many lies in those stories.
It’s all right. It really is. It’s such a relief to let go of those lies.

I love my brother. I will always love my brother. His soul is pure and perfect. His sins don’t erase that purity, they only obscure it. He just needs to atone for them. We all do. Sooner or later, one way or another. The gods have a plan for us all.
We need to go home. Loras.
There's no time for this.
Brienne of Tarth murdered Renly.
You can't stay here.
You can't avenge him from the grave. Bring the horses. Please.
He was very handsome.
"Your Grace." Calling yourself king doesn't make you one. And if Renly wasn't a king, I wasn't a queen.
No. I want to be the queen.
I'll plant the seed as soon as Joffrey and I are married. It should grow quickly.
And only one to let you go. Because it will please me.
Your Grace.
Long may he reign.
He sits the throne like he was born to it.
No, he wasn't.
He was my husband. My king.
Your Grace, I feel...
No.
It would be some consolation, wouldn't it? For all the horror that put him there.
He has you.

Oh. After all that's happened... it sounds strange, I know, but I am... I haven't even given any thought to it, what comes next. It would be a great honor, of course. But I will have to speak to my father about it.
We may be faced with an alarming number of weddings soon.
I won't even know what to call you. Sister? Or Mother? Hmm.
Lord Baelish.
It would be my pleasure. It took me weeks to learn my way around the camp. Twice I walked in on officers in stages of undress. And the moment I learn which tent is mine, we're on the move again.
I am not tutored in warfare, but basic arithmetic favors the side with the greater numbers.
The place of a Kingsguard is by the king's side.
You seem quite interested in our marriage.
You've never married, have you?

That is sad. Though perhaps it's for the best. The whole notion of marriage seems to confuse you, so allow me to explain. My husband is my king, and my king is my husband. Here is your tent, Lord Baelish. Good night.
(laughs)
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Oh, no, it's quite all right, Your Grace. I'd love to see their tombs, really. It's like taking a walk through history.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: This is so exciting.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: (laughs)
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I'm glad he did. I'm sorry, Your Grace. I know they did terrible things at the end, but their ancestors built this. Sometimes severity is the price we pay for greatness.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Shall we go and see them? If you give them your love, they will return it a thousandfold. I've spoken with them. I know how they feel about you. You led the defense of King's Landing. They adore you.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Ahem. I didn't mean to disturb you.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: We'd like some privacy, please. If you wouldn't mind waiting back inside the keep. Or if you'd be kind enough to give me your names, I'll ask the king to speak with you himself.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: What did you pray for?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Why not? I'll tell you what I prayed for in the sept this morning. Let's see, for my family's health and happiness, for an end to the war, for a short winter. Boring and traditional, I'm afraid. And you?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: My cousin Alanna was the most beautiful girl I'd ever seen. When I was 12, I was all elbows and knees and Alanna looked like a goddess sent to torture me. Pig-face, she called me.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I think it had something to do with my nose. Whenever she passed me in the halls, she'd oink.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: So I prayed that she'd catch a horrible skin disease. A week after that, she came down with porridge plague.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You don't have it in the North? Your skin starts to look like boiled oats and eventually your face slides off and you die in agony.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: (nods, laughs)
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Don't say that. No, you're not.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Oh, she grew up to be the most beautiful woman and married a handsome lord and they have darling children and live in a castle by the sea. It's all terribly frustrating.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I want us to be friends, good friends.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You must see Highgarden. You'd love it there. I know you would. We have a great masquerade the night of the harvest moon. You should see the costumes. People work on them for months.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: The Queen Regent, you mean. Once I marry Joffrey, I'll be queen. And if you were to marry Loras. . .  Oh, your place would be at Highgarden, wouldn't it? We would be sisters, you and I. Would you like that?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I can't believe you're going. Leaving me alone here with these people.
(laughs)
No. Have they even agreed to the match? No one tells me anything.
(laughs)
Mm-hmm.
(laughs)
Well, he could have done.
You don't know, Grandmother.
What? I don't understand.
Kingsguard.
I'm not a visitor, Your Grace. Word has itI'm to be your bride.
Did you know that people in arranged marriages often never meet until their wedding day? Before we decide to spend our lives together, we ought to get to know one another. Don't you think?
It can be our secret. Hmm? If we're going to be man and wife, we'll have a few secrets from her, I hope. So, Your Grace...
Hello. Aren't you a proper fellow?
Very handsome.
That's very cruel. You don't strike me as cruel.
That's a relief. Because you know what happens when we marry?
When we marry, I become yours. Forever. It's getting late. I should go. May I come and visit you again? All right, then.
Remember, our little secret.
Why is my brother in a cell?
We both know who did. (Walks away). You told me she was returning to Casterly Rock.
She's jealous you're not hers anymore. Arresting my brother is her revenge.
(Scoffs). Oh, my sweet, sweet King. (Sits down). Do you have any affection for me at all?
I can't bear to think of my brother locked away in some grimy cell.
Do you promise?
You're the King of the Andals, the Lord of the Seven Kingdoms, the Protector of the Realm. And you let a band of fanatics imprison your brother by law.
Are you? When?
You don't know?
(Walking out). I have to send word to Grandmother.
I need to be with my family, Your Grace. (Continues walking).
I’d go to my brother, my husband, my family.
Quality takes time.
And one day you walked through a graveyard and realized it was all for nothing and set out on the path to righteousness. Book of the Stranger, verse 25.
Septa Unella reads it to me. At me.
So what did you do?
Who?
Loras. Loras.
Listen to me. You need to stay strong.
You are strong. You are the future of our house, the future of our family.
They want me to help you. They want me told help tear you down. That’s why he’s letting me see you, I know it is. And if either of us give in to what they want, then they win.
All right.
Loras! Highgarden!
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Now, now, my love. My brother fought valiantly for you.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You are very welcome here, Lady Stark. I'm so sorry for your loss.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: As is your right. You are a king.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Thank you, Your Grace.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You think so? I can't decide how I like it better. This way, or this way.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Here, let me.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Do you want my brother to come in and help?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: He could get you started. I know he wouldn't mind. Or I can turn over and you can pretend I'm him.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: There's no need for us to play games. Save your lies for court. You're going to need a lot of them.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Your enemies aren't happy about us. They want to tear us apart. And the best way to stop them is to put your baby in my belly. We can try again later. You decide how you want to do it, with me, with me and Loras, however else you like. Whatever you need to do. You are a king.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: So am I the queen?
Clawing at his own throat, looking to his mother to make stop... it was horrible.
What was it like?
One of my husbands preferred the company of men and was stabbed through the heart. Another was happiest torturing animals and was poisoned at our wedding feast... I must be cursed.
But I would have been the queen.
No. You were lovely.
Yes. (Chuckles).
No, no, no. You're very sweet. The sweetest king who ever lived.
Wouldn't that be glorious? (They laugh. Tommen leans in to kiss her). Shouldn't we rest a little while? Just to catch our breath, there's no rush, is there?
No, I'm fine. I just want you all to myself. I want to know everything there is to know about King Tommen First of His Name.
So strange… Husband.
I understand. But it's not your fault, you know that, don't you? You mustn't feel guilty.
(Sits up). I love to sail.
I think we're going to be very happy, you and I.
Living in a tower so high, it touches the clouds. Of course my grandmother couldn't wait to go home. The capital is not for everyone, I suppose. Does your mother like it here?
It's so wonderful to have her watching over you. A lioness guiding her cub. (Pours them some wine).
You are. And the king. (Hands him win). But you'll always be her baby-boy.

I adore how she has always been so generous with me, so kind. And the horror she's had to injure. (Sits down and begins to comb her hair). Losing her husband, her eldest child and her father. It's no wonder she's so protective of you. She'll never let you out of her sight.
I said, "Darling, surely 4 times is enough, are you trying to set a new record?" (Laugher). And he said, "Well, what is the record? I'm sure we can break it."
Mother! (Gets up and hugs Cersei). Welcome.
Can we bring you anything to eat or drink? I wish we had some wine for you. It's a bit early in the day for us.
You are very sweet.
I absolutely adore him. You raised a gallant young man. I'm forever grateful.
Ecstatic. I really am. Exhausted, to be honest, but what can I expect, he is half lion, half stag. (Margaery’s friends laugh).
Oh, forgive me, I haven't been at Court for long, I get so confused. What's the proper way to address you now? Queen Mother or Dowager Queen?
In any event, judging from the King's enthusiasm, the Queen Mother will be a Queen Grandmother soon.
Can you imagine the celebrations? They'll ring the bells all day and night.
(to a maid)  It's more your color. You take it.
(to LORAS) You're such a dear.
Come. Lady Sansa, it is my honor to present my grandmother the Lady Olenna of House Tyrell.
Renly was brave and gentle, Grandmother. Father liked him and so did Loras.
Grandmother! What will Sansa think of us?
I'm to be his wife. I only want to know what that means.
She's terrified, Grandmother. Just look at her.
You wanted to see me, Your Grace?
That's very thoughtful, Your Grace. I have everything I could want.
A welcome one. A military encampment is no place for a lady.
Your Grace, I tried to do my duty as a wife, that is all.
The duty of any wife to any husband-to provide him with children.
Why? I-I would not speak ill of the dead, Your Grace.
No. I do beg your pardon. The subtleties of politics are often lost on me. Renly I don't believe he was interested in the company of women.

Whenever I wanted to make a child with him, he-he had so many excuses, so many late-night war councils. He never wanted to try. Except one evening, after he'd had far too much wine to drink, he suggested something-something that sounded very painful and couldn't possibly result in children. Maybe the fault was with me.
It's such a relief to hear you say so, Your Grace.
As is your right. You must do whatever you need to do.
You are the king.
It's beautiful. Will you show me how it works?
Would you take me hunting sometime? Forgive me, Your Grace. I know a hunt is no place for a woman.
May I? Please.
I imagine it must be so exciting to squeeze your finger here and watch something die over there.
I don't know, Your Grace Do you think I could?
Would you like to watch me?

We are so fortunate to enjoy this marvelous food and drink. Not all among us are so lucky. To thank the gods for bringing the recent war to a just end, King Joffrey has decreed that the leftovers from our feast be given to the poorest in his city.
Lady Brienne. So good of you to come.
That's not true, my love. Brienne had nothing to do with it.
Thank you. I hope we see more of you.
My love, come back to me. It's time for my father's toast.
(Standing) Look, the pie.
Wonderful. Wonderful. My hero.
He's choking!
Why?
Pardon me.
I have others.
And your mother?
Bad men wanted to come into this city and do terrible things, but your father stopped them.
Whenever you look at this knight, I want you to remember your father.
And what do knights swear to do? Protect the weak and uphold the good. Your father did that. Be proud of him.
Was your father a soldier, too?
You should be proud, too. Under King Joffrey's leadership, your fathers saved the city. They saved us all. From now on, we're going to take care of you. All of you.
Come to me for whatever you need to feed them, clothe them, or house them. Directly to me.
Your Grace.
( laughs )
The climate is a bit more forgiving back in Highgarden, Your Grace.
I am touched by your concern, Your Grace. Luckily for us Tyrells, our blood runs quite warm. Doesn't it, Loras?
Loras, isn't the queen's gown magnificent? The fabric, the embroidery, the metalwork. I've never seen anything like it.
Yes. I paid a visit to an orphanage the High Septon told me about.
The lowest among us are no different from the highest if you give them a chance and approach them with an open heart.

Hunger turns men into beasts. I'm glad House Tyrell has been able to help in this regard. They tell me 100 wagons arrive daily now from the Reach. Wheat, barley, apples. We've had a blessed harvest. And, of course, it's our duty to assist the capital in time of need.
The wedding is in a fortnight, Grandmother. You can't say no to everything.
Perhaps I should just let Joffrey choose it for me. End up with a string of dead sparrow heads around my neck.
A shadow?
Joffrey is our king now.
And you've given none.
We’re late for dinner as it is.
I’m very hungry.
What’s your name?
I’m afraid my brother is keeping the king waiting, Olyvar.
Perhaps you might consider being a bit more discreet?
In any event… (throws Loras his clothes) you shouldn’t keep your intended waiting.
Lucky you.
You think I want that woman married to my brother?
Perhaps.
Perhaps?
I’d like to see my brother.
Please. How is he? At least tell me that.
I am the queen and I demand to see my brother.
My brother, can you tell me how he is?
I have nothing to confess.
None of us are.
We all need to leave now!
I am his/hers, and she/he is mine. From this day, until the end of my days.
I am his/hers, and she/he is mine. From this day, until the end of my days.
(singing)
l'm so sorry, Your Grace. l'll never sing it again, l swear.
Your Grace?
Every man needs hands, Your Grace.
Your Grace, please. l won't sing, Your Grace.
l beg you! Please, no. No!
Your Grace, please. l beg you, l will never sing again! (screaming) No!
(screaming) Your Grace, please!
(screaming) Stop! No!
On that eve, the captive Imp down-wards from his horse did limp, no more would he preen and primp, in garb of red and gold. (singing)
Seven blessings to you, goodfolk!
Good idea, grandfather. I'm starving. A song while we wait or?
Grandfather, may be your last chance if you're heading north. The only music the northerners know is the howling of wolves!
My Lord of Lannister! Might I entertain you while you eat? I can sing of your father's victory at King's Landing!
Willem. Willem!
You're Robb Stark's wife.
Is it true what they say about him?
That he can turn into a wolf at night.
And he eats the flesh of his enemies.
Martyn Lannister.
I remember Wolkan.   A bit timid but a sharp mind.
It is a bit much.  Still we ought to --
Do you know him?
And?
I sense a more detailed proposal is forthcoming.

It could be done.  And this news could be authentic.  It's possible.  It's also possible this message is part of a ploy by the dragon queen to lure southern armies away from the lands they are currently defending to open those lands to easy conquest.
We will write to Maester Wolkan at Winterfell for clarification.  I promise you we will get to the truth of it one way or another.
That will be all, Tarley.
I'm afraid so.
I don't have the heart to tell him yet.  He's a good lad.
Right.  The ravenry was falling apart last time I checked...
Hmm.
Hmm.  Does it hurt?
The infection no longer appears to be active.
Unusual.  Unlikely.  One could almost be forgiven for thinking the entire upper layer of diseased skin was debrided and the underlying region treated with some sort of ointment.
The climate?  You're free to go, Ser.  This chamber is needed for the infectious, which you are no longer.
Tarley, I'd like to speak with you in my study this evening.
You treated him?
Who told you to treat him?
Who forbade you or anyone to attempt to treat him?
But you treated him anyway?

I forbade it because it's dangerous and rarely successful.  Especially on someone of that age.  You could have infected yourself and others.  You could have devastated the entire Citadel.  But you didn't.  It's a meticulous, difficult procedure.  Many maesters whose chains are heavy with healing links have attempted it and failed.  But you succeeded.  How?
That man is alive because of you.  You should be proud.
Come here.
All these manuscripts and scrolls and rotting away.  I need you to make copies of them.
You were expecting a reward?  Your reward is not being immediately expelled from the Citadel.  You'd better get started.  Be mindful of the paper mites.  They like flesh as well.
The infection has spread too far.  You should have cut off your arm the moment you were touched.
It will be years before it kills you.  It could be 10, could be 20.
Your mind?  Six months, maybe fewer.
Does this look like a baby to you?
Have you studied the varying rates of Greyscale progression in infants and fully grown men?
Maester Chrissen discovered Shireen Baratheon's affliction immediately.

This is quite advanced and beyond our skills, Ser.  Were you a commoner I'd have you shipped to Valyria at once to live out your life with the Stone Men.  As an anointed Knight I'll allow you one more day.  How you choose to spend that time is up to you.
Come, Samwell.
Come, Samwell.
It might be useful.  Fouthen was a dreadful writer but an excellent researcher.
And this one.
Maester Faol was quite the opposite.  A brilliant stylist who invented half the stories he tells.

If you need to write histories, Tarley, you have to do the research.  If you want people to read your histories, you need a bit of style.  I'm not writing the Chronicle of the Wars Following the Death of King Robert the First so it can sit on a shelf unread.
What?  You don't like the title?  What would you call it then?
We're not poets, Tarley.
This is your moment.  Use it wisely.
I'm familiar with Archmaester Pylos and the Study of Rare Diseases.
Yes.  Should I tell you how he died?  From Greyscale.
The procedure is far too dangerous which is why it's now forbidden.
Ah, Maester Weyland. A drinker's liver if ever I saw one.
Weigh that.
The triumph of transitory pleasures. Mankind's curse.
What's the weight?
I don't remember your proposition.
That area is reserved for maesters. Are you a maester?
Not a very strong proposition, is it?
Everyone in the Citadel doubts everything. It's their job. But the tales of the Long Night can't be pure fabrication. Too many similarities from unconnected sources.
The very same. And the simplest explanation for your grating obsession with the white walkers is that you're telling the truth and that you saw what you say you saw.
I do. You'll finish weighing that heart?

We're not lie the people south of the Twins. And we're not like the people north of the Twins. In the Citadel, we lead different lives for different reasons. We are this world's memory, Samwell Tarly. Without us, men would be little better than dogs. Don't remember any meal but the last, can't see forward to any but the next. And every time you leave the house and shut the door, they howl like you're gone forever. When Robert's Rebellion was raging, people that the end was near. The end of the Targaryen dynasty. "How will we survive?" When Aegon Targaryen turned his eye westward and flew his dragons to Blackwater Rush--"The end is near! How will we survive?" And thousands of year before that, during the Long Night, we can forgive them for thinking it truly was the end. But it wasn't. None of it was. The Wall has stood through it all. And every winter that ever came has ended.
Be a good lad. Clean this up.
Take her.
I'm sorry, my Lord, we're full up. Every room.
Truly, my lord, we have nothing.
Lady Stark.
You struck me.
You’re a free man, you could have gone anywhere, why did you come back?
Sword up! Attack, my lord! Attack! Don’t cross your feet!
Sword up, my lord!
That's it. The tide's against us.
You're coming home.
This is the royal fleet. And you're not a smuggler anymore, you're the high captain.
Not after tonight. When the sun rises, Stannis will sit on the Iron Throne and you will be his Hand.
God. Father, there is only one and he watches over us.
Over all of us. The people of King's Landing did not choose the false king, Joffrey Baratheon. They will be glad to see his head on a spike.
Our ships outnumber theirs 10-to-1.
I have faith in the Lord of Light. I have faith in our cause. And I have faith in my captain.
They're welcoming the new king.
Drums!
It was wise to attack at night. We took them by surprise.
If it's true there is dissension in their ranks, maybe their sailors have mutinied.
There's only one ship.

(Reading a letter) My brother Robert left no trueborn heirs, the boy Joffrey being born of incest between Cersei Lannister, and her brother Jaime Lannister. By right of birth, and blood, I do this day lay claim to the Iron Throne of Westeros.
Mind your words, pirate. (They all stop walking).
This war isn't about you. We're not attacking King's Landing so that you can rape the queen.
As if she would just let you!
Stannis is the rightful king, and the Lord of Light. The one true god.
You believe your king can win?
When will the King have a sail?
He's my God too, and yours. But you were too blind to see. (As they walk). Let me teach you how to read.
It won't take long. You already know the letters. The holy books are much more persuasive than I can be.
But you always came home.
(They reach their horses). But I was. Every night that you were at sea, I lit a candle and I prayed, for you.
(As Davos is mounting his horse). Stannis is my king, but he's only a man.
Your Grace.
“And I declare upon the honor of my house that my beloved brother Robert le-”

“That my brother Robert left no trueborn heirs; the boy Joffrey, the boy Tommen, and the girl Myrcella, being born of incest between Cersei Lannister and her brother Jaime Lannister. By right of birth -"
"And her brother Jaime Lannister, the Kingslayer. By right of birth and blood, I do this day lay claim -"
(Adding Ser) "Ser Jaime Lannister, the Kingslayer. By right of birth and blood, I do this day lay claim to the Iron Throne of Westeros. (Stannis nods). Let all true men declare their loyalty.”
It's empty.
You'd rather be out there?
No. Who's he?
If the gods turned every killer into a giant white rat--
Who are you?
Who are you?!
Does the well lead to an underground river? Is that how you came here?
How do you know that?
But no one's killed a white walker in thousands of years.
We can stop, we can rest.
We're not going to make it.
JOJEN! Hold on!
Hold on to me!
My brother, he led us to you, and now he…
How do you know?
You’re not coming with us?
Where will you go?
Are you sure you’re ready for this?
It’s good land and there’s no war up here.  Why leave?
It’s as good a place to shelter as any.
Well, we’ll have to open them up again.
There’s a rider out there.
There’s more.
[To BRAN] What did you do?
Is that safe?
Leave him alone.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I know how to skin a rabbit.
I didn't see any game before I got here.
Well, I made this bow myself. Guess you don't learn how to do that north of the Wall.
Oh, you're gonna punch the rabbits to death?
Well, someone else is sitting right here.
Well, some of us would say thank you when someone else hunts down their breakfast for them. Or don't they teach you to say thank you north of the Wall?
Your way of skinning rabbits is quicker than mine.
Thank you. See? Not so hard, is it? Thank you.
Shh. I'm right here with you.
I'm right here with you.
The visions take their toll.
(nods)
I’m so sorry.
Why did you help us?
The Three-Eyed Raven’s dead.
You need water.
Of course it matters.
What does that mean?
How... will we know the end?
(gasps)
(whimpers)
(GASPS)
Come on, Hodor. Help me with the sledge.
We can go home now, Hodor. Well, maybe not home home, but somewhere that isn’t a cave.
Eat something that isn’t moss. I want an egg. How do you like ‘em? Boiled? Fried up with some butter?
With a rasher of bacon and some blood sausage.
Bran. Bran, wake up. Bran.
Bran, wake up.
Hodor.
Help me!
Bran! Bran, wake up!
Bran! Bran! Bran, wake up.
You have to wake up. We need Hodor.
Bran, we’re all going to die!
Bran, you have to wake up! We need Hodor!
Warg into Hodor now!
Summer!
What are you doing?
Hodor, hurry!
Hold the door!
Hold the door!
Is that a baby?
No, we need to stay together.
Bran, what happened?
Who?
If Jon was here, why would they put his wolf in a cage?
They might have been Night'sWatch once, not anymore. We're not safe here.We need to go.
Bran, we need to go now.
Can you remember where the cage was?
If I'm not backsoon, we'll meet...
(screams) No, please. Please.Please, let me help him.
Please.
It's Meera. It's Meera.I'm right here.
I'm right here.
What's that?
It's a very good idea.
I don't want to leave you, but when they come I need to be with my family.  And you're safe, safe as anyone can be now.  You don't need me anymore.
That's all you've got to say?
Thank you?
My brother died for you.  Hodor and Summer died for you.  I almost died for you.  Bran!
You died in that cave.
I'm better with them. Drop the spear.
Drop it.
Why don't you ask?
Where's the shame in that?
Some people will always need help. That doesn't mean they're not worth helping.
You've been gone for hours.
We're all hungry.
You'd forget us, Bran. You'd forget your mother and father. You'd forget your brothers and sisters. You'd forget Winterfell. You'd forget you. And if we lose you, we lose everything.
It’s not safe anywhere.
And we’re going to fight it in there.
For what? I sit in there and I watch him have his visions and nothing ever happens.
I'm Meera Reed, daughter of Howland Reed. This is Brandon Stark, son of Ned Stark.
Bran, wake up! We’re all going to die! Bran! Bran!
Bran, wake up. We need Hodor. We need Hodor. Warg into Hodor now! Now!
Hold the door! Hold the door! Hold the door!
Hold the door!
Hold the door!
(speaking Valyrian.)  You are a fool. The masters are too strong.
(speaking Valyrian.) I've been through two slave revolts, boy. They always end the same way: the Masters in power and the slaves dead.
(speaking Valyrian.) Who are you?
(speaking Valyrian.) You are Unsullied? They taught you how to fight before you could walk. We are not soldiers.
(speaking Valyrian.) I want to live. You saw what they did to those children. What do you think they'll do to us?
(speaking Valyrian.) We have no training, weapons.
We shouldn’t be out here alone.
If your father hears…
But if he finds out…
Are you sure?
We shouldn’t go in.
Let’s go…
Come on, we have to go. We have to go! Cersei!
Sam. Oh, Sam.
I never thought I’d see you again. You look wonderful.
That’s enough, Talla.
Oh, my. You are lovely.
Hello, little one. It’s me, your grandmother.
May I hold him?
They’re on a hunt. They’ll join us for dinner. Come inside. Such a journey. You must be exhausted.
Samwell, would you care for more bread?
Randyll, to be maester of the Night’s Watch is a great honor.
Come, my dear. Talla.
I’ve lost my appetite.
You dishonor yourself.
I imagine it’s common where you’re from. We once met a man, Lord Umber from Last Hearth, wasn’t it, Randyll, who said he taught all his daughters how to hunt.
You have a curious mind, I can tell. You’ll be a scholar like your father.
l I did. I still see it.
The Lord of Light only allows me glimpses.
Will you quit the war just because you've lost a battle?
I've been fighting far longer than you.
Inside you.
We murdered him. Share the weight with me.

This war has just begun. It will last for years. Thousands will die at your command. You will betray the men serving you. You will betray your family. You will betray everything you once held dear. And it will all be worth it because you are the Son of Fire. You are the Warrior of Light. You will sweep aside this pretender and that one. You will be king.
Let me show you.
Look into the fire, my king. Look.
Keep looking. Do you see? Do you see, my king?
I take no credit. I have faith and my faith has been rewarded.
And our king is still no closer to the Iron Throne. A great gift requires a great sacrifice.
Your mercy saved the boy's life. You feel good about that?
You saved one innocent. How many tens of thousands have you doomed?
Take him away.
My king.
This War of Five Kings means nothing. The true war lies to the north, my king. Death marches on the Wall. Only you can stop him.
He's right. You need him. He has a part to play in the war to come.
The Lord of Light has made good on his promise, my king. His fires have melted the snows away. The way ahead is clear.
And you will take it. The Lord has shown me Bolton banners burning. You will receive what is yours by right.
Could have been worse, Jon Snow. You had a family. You had feasts.
The Princess Shireen.
We burned her at the stake.
The army was trapped. The horses were dying. It was the only way.
I only do what my Lord commands!
We are standing here because of him. Jon Snow is alive because the Lord willed it.
So did her father. So did her mother. Her own blood knew it was the only way.
I didn’t lie. I was wrong.

I’ve been ready to die for many years. If the Lord was done with me, so be it, but he’s not. You’ve seen the Night King, Jon Snow. You know the great war is still to come. You know the army of the dead will be upon us soon. And you know I can help you win that war.
It will all be over soon, Princess.
The year is now, my Lord. For you we offer up this girl, that you may cleanse her with your fire and that its light may lead our way.
Accept this token of our faith, my lord and lead us from the darkness. Lord of Light show us the way!
Lord of Light protect us, for the night is dark and full of terrors!
I’m not a soldier.
Don’t lose.
I’ll have to try.
I am not your servant, Jon Snow.
I serve the Lord of Light. I do what he commands.
I interpret his signs as well as I can. If the Lord didn’t want me to bring you back, how did I bring you back? I have no power. Only what he gives me and he gave me you.
I don’t know. Maybe you’re only needed for this small part of his plan and nothing else. Maybe he brought you here to die again.
The one we’ve got.
Show the boy to his chambers. Have the maids draw him a bath and find him some decent clothes. I'll come visit you soon.
You know what I mean to do with him.
Have you ever slaughtered a lamb, my king?
If the lamb sees the knife, she panics. Her panic seeps into her meat, darkens it, fouls the flavor.
And none have seen the blade.
Have you ever seen one like it?
It's shocking, isn't it, the first time you encounter real wealth?  When I was your age, I lived on one bowl of stew a day. And stew is a kind word for it.
Here, taste this.
You think I'm trying to poison you?
Where do you think it's from?

Doesn't matter, does it?  It's the real thing or it's not. You only need a tongue to tell the difference. Go on, have some more. You still don't trust it. You think this is all a mistake and that you'll have to pay somehow for every bite you take.
There are no mistakes. Not for us.

Your father chose your mother because the Lord of Light willed it. He willed it so you could be here right now with a power inside you you can't even begin to understand. And He brought me here to draw it from you and birth it into the world. We're both a part of His plan. He wants this for us. No, He demands this of us.

According to whom?  The Silent Sisters? With their stern looks, muzzled mouths, and dried-up cunts? What have their gods done for you?  Hmm?  When you pray to them, what's their answer?  A god is real or he's not. You only need eyes to see.
Death is coming for everyone and everything. A darkness that will swallow the dawn. And we can stop it, you and I.
Let me show you what you have inside you. Come fight death with me.
You have to trust me.
Don't fight, don't fight. They won't take much.
I told you there's power in the blood of kings. And you have so much blood.
You can blame Ser Davos. He didn't believe in the power of king's blood. He wanted a demonstration.
Wildfire. I should have been here.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Do you miss it?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Your father's house.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Haven't you ever wondered where your strength came from? Your talent for fighting?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Mine was a slave. So was I. Bought and sold, scourged and branded, until the Lord of Light reached down, took me in his hand and raised me up.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Your blood is noble.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: There. Your father's house.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: The bastard of Robert of the House Baratheon, First of His Name, King of the Andals and the First Men. Why do you think the gold cloaks wanted you? There is power in a king's blood.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Come in, my queen. Please.
You could never disturb me.
Could you bring me that vial over there? The blue one.
No, no, no. Not that one. You don't even want to touch that one. Next to it. That's the one.
When I looked into the flames this morning, the Lord spoke to me. He said, "Tonight, you will have your last good bath in a long while. Make it count." A joke. Not a very good one, I'm afraid.
That's because most jokes are lies and you're devoted to the truth.
Not always.
No. Don't be upset. Men never crave what they already have.
It's only flesh. It needs what it needs.
I understand how you feel.
But that is impossible.
You don't need powders and potions, my queen. You don't need lies. You are strong enough to look into the Lord's light and see his truth for yourself.
However harsh it is. However hard for us to understand. You don't need my help, but I will need yours soon. When we set sail, your daughter must be with us. The Lord needs her.
You saw it yourself, my king, when you stared into the flames. A great battle in the snow.
You do know. Trust yourself.
I trust in the Lord.
I have seen myself walk along the battlements of Wintefell. I have seen the flayed men banners lowered to the ground.
But sometimes sacrifices must be made to ensure victory. I have shown you the power of king's blood. The Usurper Robb Stark. The Usurper Joffrey Baratheon.
No, we have someone better, and your blood runs through her veins.
Do you doubt me still? After all that you’ve seen?
There is only one way. You must become king before the Long Night begins. Only you can lead the living against the dead. All your life has led us to this moment. To this decision.
We come as friends.
Valar morghulis.
(speaks Valyrian) You are Thoros of Myr.
(speaks Valyrian) The High Priest gave you a mission. Turn King Robert away from his idols and towards the Lord of Light. What happened?
(speaks Valyrian) You quit, you mean. The heathen continue to slaughter each other and you continue to get drunk.
How many times has the Lord brought him back?
(speaks Valerian) That's not possible.
(speaks Valerian) You should not have this power.
You've been to the other side.
You have someone He needs.
You are more than they can ever be. They're just foot soldiers in the great war. You will make kings rise and fall.
The king has taken for his sigil the fiery heart of the Lord of Light.
You should kneel before your brother. He's the Lord's chosen, born amidst salt and smoke.
Look to your sins, Lord Renly. The night is dark and full of terrors.
Are you afraid, Onion Knight?
You've carried more unpleasant cargo in your time. Are you a good man, Ser Davos Seaworth?
If half an onion is black with rot, it's a rotten onion. A man is good or he is evil.
Oh, good. I'm a knight myself, of sorts, a champion of light and life.
Do you love your wife?
Yet you have known other women.
I'm not. I'm talking about other women. Like me, Ser Davos. You want me. You want to see what's beneath this robe.
And you will.
Shadows cannot live in the dark, Ser Davos. They are servants of light, the children of fire. And the brighter the flame, the darker they are.
They can't bar our passage.
There's only one god, Ser Davos, and he only protects those who serve him.
(Walking towards them). Those scars mean nothing to the Lord of Light.
Her father is the Lord's chosen King. And her father's blood runs through her veins.
You march on Winterfell soon?
Once before you put your faith in Ser Davos and left me behind. I hope you won't make that mistake again.
You only need faith, my King.
To serve my Lord.
Lord Commander.

Come with us when we ride south. (Walks forwards). None of us know the castle as well as you do. Its hidden tunnels, its weaknesses, its people. Winterfell was your home once. Don't you want to chase the rats out of it? (Turns to Jon).
There's only one war. Life against death. Come. Let me show what you're fighting for. (Walks in front of Jon).
No visions. No magic. Just life.
Do you feel my heart beating? This power in you, you resist it, and that's your mistake. Embrace it.
The Lord of Light made us male and female. Two parts of a greater whole. We are joining this power. Power to make life, power to make light, and power to cast shadows.
Then we shouldn't tell him. (Starts unbuttoning Jon’s coat).
Why?
The dead don't need lovers. Only the living.
You know nothing, Jon Snow.
Ser Davos.
I will do as Jon Snow commands.
He’s the prince that was promised.
There was a battle. Stannis was defeated.
I don't know yet. The fires will show me.
I don't know.
I will never abandon you. You are the Son of Fire.  I am sworn to serve you.
When I return, you will understand.
I cannot.
You don't have the strength. It would kill you.
Your fires burn low, my king.
There is another way. A better way.
Yes.
You are. But there are others with your blood in their veins. You will sit on the Iron Throne, but first there must be sacrifices. The Lord of Light demands it.
Afterwards, after they stabbed you, after you died, where did you go? What did you see?
The Lord let you come back for a reason. Stannis was not the prince who was promised, but someone has to be.
I've done my part.  I've brought ice and fire together.
My time whispering in the ears of kings has come to an end.
Neither of us is common folk anymore.  I did not part on good terms with the King in the North, or his advisor.
Because of mistakes I made.  Terrible mistakes.  I would only be a distraction if I stayed.
Volantis.
I will return, dear spider.  One last time.
I have to die in this strange country, just like you.
The Lord of Light shines through you, young warrior.
I told him death by fire is the purest death.
Because it is true. You're troubled my king.
These armies are but toys for the Lord of Light.
I tell him nothing! I pray for his commands and I obey.
You must have faith.
Then we must be the ten.
I have seen the path to victory in the flames. At first, you must give yourself to the Lord of Light.
You must give... all of yourself.
She's sick. Weak. Shut away in the tower. And she disgusts you. And she's given you nothing. No sons. Only stillborns. Only death. (Whispers in his ear). I will give you a son, my King.
Hear us now. Accept these tokens of our faith, my lord, and lead us from the darkness.
Lord of Light, show us the way...
Lord of Light, protect us, for the night is dark and full of terrors.
It's all I knew when I was a child.
Until he found me.
Every mother should fear for her child's soul.
Were you sleeping, Princess?
Did you watch the ceremony on the beach?
And it frightened you?
They're in a better place now, Princess. The fire cleansed them of the sins of the world.
Women scream when they give birth. Afterward they are filled with joy.
You have so many questions, don't you? So did I when I was a child. I was much like you. Only I wasn't a princess.
No. But I suffered in other ways, sweet girl, believe me. What do you know of the gods?
Lies and fables. Septons speak of seven gods. There are but two... a god of light and love and joy and a god of darkness, evil, and fear. Eternally at war.
There's only one hell, Princess. The one we live in now.
Come in.
You interrupt nothing.
I will after you tell me.
The former Lord Commander.
What are you asking?
If you want to help him, leave him be.
There are some with his power.
I don’t know.
Not for me.
Everything I believed, the great victory I saw in the flames, all of it was a lie.
You were right all along. The Lord never spoke to me.
I never had this gift.
Please.
Queen Daeneys, I was a slave once, bought and sold, scourged and branded.  It is an honor to meet the Breaker of Chains.
I am called Melisandre.
No, it didn't.
The Long Night is coming.  Only the prince who was promised can bring the dawn.
Prophecies are dangerous things.  I believe you have a role to play, as does another.  The King in the North, Jon Snow.

As Lord Commander of the Night's Watch he allowed the Wildlings south of the Wall to protect them from great danger.  As King in the North he has united those Wildlings with the northern houses so together they may face their common enemy.
Summon Jon Snow.  Let him stand before you and tell you things that have happened to him, the things that he has seen with his own eyes.
Lord of Light, come to us in our darkness. We offer you these false Gods.
Take them and cast your light upon us. For the night is dark and full of terrors.
After the long summer, darkness will fall heavy on the world. Stars will bleed.
The cold breath of winter will freeze the seas.
And the dead shall rise in the North.
You smell of fear, fear and piss and old bones. Do you want to stop me? Stop me.
In the ancient books, it's written that a warrior will draw a burning sword from the fire. And that sword shall be Lightbringer. Stannis Baratheon, warrior of light, your sword awaits you.
For the night is dark and full of terrors.
Stannis does not need to beg this lord or that lord for support. The Lord of Light stands behind him.
He has no need for ships.
The night is dark and full of terrors, old man, but the fire burns them all away. Your Grace.
How would you punish the infidels, Ser Davos?
I'm not your enemy.

Was it me you fought on Blackwater Bay? Did I set your ships ablaze? I wasn't there when the wildfire killed our men by the thousands. I could have saved those men. You would have taken the city, Stannis would now sit upon his rightful throne, and you would stand beside him. But I wasn't there because you convinced your king to leave me behind. Do you hear them screaming? All those burning men in the water crying for their mothers, for their gods for help? Until the moment the Blackwater swallowed them.
Don't despair, Ser Davos. What I told your son is true.
Death by fire is the purest death.
You've chosen the darkness, Ser Davos.
I will pray for you.
The king wants a word.
Never. The Lord’s fire lives within me, Jon Snow.
Feel.
Are you a virgin?
Good.
Your grace. The bastard of Winterfell.
Man or woman, young or old, lord or peasant, our choices are the same. We choose light, or we choose darkness. We choose good, or we choose evil. We choose the true god or the false.
Free folk, there is only one true king, and his name is Stannis. Here stands the king of lies. Behold the fate of those who choose the darkness.
I saw him in the flames, fighting at Winterfell.
Ser Davos.
Not yet.   But even those who don't worship the Lord can serve his cause.
Help, down below.
Aye!
Aye!
Aye!
Aye!
Aye!
What is dead may never die!
Hear me roar!
We stand together!
(singing) And so he spoke That Lord of Castamere But now the rains weep o'er his halls With no one there to hear Yes, now the rains weep o'er his halls And not a soul to hear -
Pull, pull! Go!
Draw!  Draw!
Kill the Lannisters!
Draw!  Draw!  Loose! Loose!
First and second squads, to the gate! To the Mud Gate!
Heave! Heave!
Heave! Heave!
Heave! Heave! Heave!
Heave!
Roll it over!
Yeah!
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: (singing) The bear, the bear and the maiden fair / From there to here, from here to there All black and brown and covered in hair He smelled that girl in the summer air The bear, the bear, and the maiden fair!
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Valar dohaeris.
“We fight and die for your glory, O glorious queen.”
Yara!
Your Grace.
Lord of Light, defend us.
For the night is dark and full of terrors.
Guilty. Guilty. Guilty. Guilty. Guilty. Guilty. Guilty. Guilty. Guilty. Guilty. Guilty. Guilty.
The King in the North!

(singing) He lifted her high in the air. He sniffed and roared and smelled her there. She kicked and wailed, a maid so fair. But he licked the honey from her hair. From there to here, from here to there. All black and brown and covered with hair. He smelled that girl in the summer air. The bear, the bear and the maiden fair.
Aye! Aye!
Aye!
Ooh!
Yeah!
Hear, hear.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Quickly, go, go!
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Help me! Help me!
You are the Mother of Dragons?  I swear I fucked you once in a pleasure house in Lys.
Why?  I didn't mind hers. She licked my ass like she was born to do it.
(laughs) You, slave girl, bring wine.
You'll all be slaves after the battle unless I save you. Take your clothes off and come and sit on Mero's lap and I may give you my Second Sons.
The Second Sons have faced worse odds and won.
We've taken the slavers' gold. We fight for Yunkai.
Show me your cunt. I want to see if it's worth fighting for.  Grey WORM: (speaks Valyrian) My Queen, shall I slice out his tongue for you?
Only a flagon? And what are my brothers in arms to drink?
Good. The Titan's Bastard does not drink alone. In the Second Sons, we share everything. After the battle, maybe we'll all share you.
I'll come looking for you when this is over.
She won't talk so much when she's choking on my cock.
My cock will find a way. Tell him. Is there any place that my cock can't reach?
Daario Naharis, the whore who doesn't like whores.
She sells her sheath and you sell your blade. What's the difference?
We fight for gold.
You'll die young.
There won't be a battle. And we don't have to deal with her eunuchs. We only have to deal with her.
Tonight's a new moon. One of us slips into her camp past her Unsullied and her knights.
Close your eyes, love.
Three coins. A coin from Meereen, a coin from Volantis, and a coin from Braavos. The Braavosi does the deed. One for each of us, darling. No peeking.
I'm Merry.
I can see I have my work cut out for me.You two. Out.
I thought that old shit would never stop singing.
The Tyrells can all rot in hell, treasonous cunts. They were going to make that boy-fucker Renly king.
This is the place, lads. The sleekest little minks in Braavos.
Oh, I’m buying. But I never was good at sharing.
Too old.
Too old.
Too old.
Do you have what I want or not?
Good.
I’ll tell you when we’re done.
You’ll have a fresh one for me tomorrow.
Protect the king!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I take my orders from the king.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: The kind who serves his king, Imp.
No one threatens His Grace in the presence of the Kingsguard.
I don't think this is a good idea, Your Grace.
Are you trying to deceive your Queen? Shall I throw them in a cell, Your Grace?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I've always guarded the king himself, my lord. Ever since your absence.
The people love their king. They know who keeps them fed.
You heard the man. Over there.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Arya Stark, come with us.
Mind your place, dancing master.
Kill the Braavosi. Bring the girl.
Bloody oafs.

Once we'd got King Joffrey safely away from the mob, the Imp rounded on him. He slapped the king across the face and called him a vicious idiot and a fool. It wasn't the first time the Imp threatened Joffrey. Right here in this throne room, he marched up those steps and called our king a halfwit. Compared His Grace to the Mad King and suggested he'd meet the same fate. And when I spoke in the king's defense, he threatened to have me killed.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Always. Find those boys and I'll give you 1,000 acres and a holdfast.
Ser Meryn Trant.
You're no knight.
You're an up-jumped cutthroat, nothing more.
You put your hand on that door, you lose the hand.
If it please My Lord, Ser Addam bids me report that the Northmen have crossed the neck.
Lord Stark!
Lord Stark, King Joffrey and the Queen Regent request your presence in the Throne Room.
King Robert is gone.
All hail his Grace, Joffrey of Houses Baratheon and Lannister, the first of his name, King of the Andals and the First Men, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm.
Forgive me, Your Grace.  The visitor from Braavos has arrived.
At once, Your Grace.
No, you can't!
No! Please! No! Not my Barra! Please. Please.
She looks like him, don't she, my Lord? She has his nose, his black hair.
Tell him when you see him, my Lord. If it please you... Tell him how beautiful she is.
And tell him I've been with no one else. I swear it, my Lord, by the old gods and new. I don't want no jewels or nothing, just him. The King was always good to me.
He wasn't that sort of man, my Lord. He just wanted to know if the child was happy, healthy.
Cocksucker! (Man screams). Boy fucker! You barbaric filth! There's a special place in the Seventh Hell for your kind.
(Pulls out a knife). Yes, you will! Seize him.
Seize him.
His Holiness is praying. He will not be disturbed.

(entering the tent) Monstrous, twisted. l pulled him out myself. He was scaled like a lizard, blind, with leather wings like the wings of a bat. When l touched him the skin fell from his bones. Inside he was full of graveworms. l warned you that only death can pay for life. You knew the price.
As you command, lady. Come. l will take you to him.
He lives. You asked for life, you paid for life.
When the sun rises in the west, sets in the east. When the seas go dry. When the mountains blow in the wind like leaves.
lt was wrong of them to burn my temple. lt angered the Great Shepherd.
lnnocent? He would have been The Stallion Who Mounts The World. Now he will burn no cities. Now his khalasar will trample no nations into dust.

Saved me? Three of those riders had already raped me before you saved me, girl. l saw my god's house burn, there where l had healed men and women beyond counting. ln the streets l saw piles of heads, the head of the baker who makes my bread, the head of a young boy that l had cured of fever just three moons past. So tell me again exactly what it was that you saved?
Why don't you take a look at your khal. Then you will see exactly what life is worth when all the rest has gone.
You will not hear me scream.
The wound has festered.
You've saved me once more.
He's beyond the healer's skills. All l can do is ease his path.
There is a spell. Some would say death is cleaner.
There is a price.
lt's not a matter of gold. This is blood magic. Only death pays for life.
No, not your death, Khaleesi. Bring me his horse.
Go. Now.
You must go also, lady. Once l begin to sing, no one must enter the tent. The dead will dance here tonight.
I can help the great rider with his cut.
Thank you, silver lady.
I am named Mirri Maz Duur.
My mother was Godswife here before me.
lamb or lion, his wound must be washed and sewn or it will fester.
(speaking Valyrian) This is Daenerys Targaryen, the Stormborn, the Unburnt, the Queen of the Seven Kingdoms of Westeros, Mother of Dragons. It is to her you owe your freedom
It is old Ghiscari, khaleesi. It means 'mother'.

*in Valyrian* You stand before Daenerys Stormborn, the Unburnt, Queen of Mereen, Queen of the Andals and the Rhoynar and the First Men. Khaleesi of the Great Grass Sea, Breaker of Chains and Mother of Dragons.
*in Low Valyrian* The Queen says you may approach.
*translating* I have brought you. He came from the sky. The black one. The winged shadow. He came from the sky and...
*translating* My girl. My little girl.
Zala, your grace.
Three.
(Valyrian) A bit “rusty”.
You are not strong enough to go anywhere.
It’s true. Only the Unsullied can keep the peace in Meereen. If you leave, half the city will consume the other half.
Our queen insists that one of you must die as punishment for your crimes.
Yes, Your Grace.
It only took Your Grace a year to learn Dothraki reasonably well.
Dothraki is difficult for the mouth to master. So guttural and harsh.
Athjahakar.
Ath-ja-hakar.
(nods)
Your High Valyrian is very good, Your Grace. The gods could not devise a more perfect tongue. It is the only proper language for poetry.
(gasps)
No, not spying.
Yes your grace.
No.
He was interested.
I believe he was interested.
All of it?
I don't know, your grace.
Yes, your grace.
*in Valyrian* You don't need to apologize.
*in Valyrian* No.
To me.
I don't remember teaching you the word precious.
Do you remember the name you were given at birth?
When they cut you, do you remember that?
I'm sorry. I'm sorry they did that to you.
It's a terrible thing to do to a boy.
*in Valyrian* Grey Worm.
*in Valyrian* I'm glad you saw me.
You seem happy.
I will be happy when our queen returns.
I have tried wine before. It made me feel funny.
I do not know any jokes.
A joke is like a story, Torgo Nudho. Not a true story, necessarily.
I do.
Two translators are on a sinking ship.
The first says, “Do you know how to swim?” The second says, “No, but I can shout for help in 19 languages.”
You don’t even know what a joke is.
More jokes.
The Masters have come for their property.
That changes nothing.
Now comes the noble Razdal mo Eraz of that ancient and honorable house, master of men and speaker to savages, to offer terms of peace.

(Valyrian) Three days.
(Valyrian) You failed no one. You fought bravely. You’ll fight again. (In Common Tongue) Are you ashamed? You were ambushed, outnumbered. There was no way you could have known.
All men fear death.
Now you have given the Masters what they deserve.
Your Grace, I think that I am not fit to have an opinion on these matters.

I can only tell you what I have seen, Your Grace. (Sitting down). I have seen you listen to your counselors. I have seen you lean on their experience when your own was lacking and weigh the choices they put before you. And I have seen you ignore your counselors, because there was a better choice. One that only you could see.

The master says they are untested. He says you would be wise to blood them early. There are many small cities between here and there, cities ripe for sacking. Should you take captives, the masters will buy the healthy ones and for a good price.

Text: Isles. Summer Isles.
(speaks Valyrian) I am from an island called Naath.
"When did they take me?" I was five years old.
I remember when they rowed us away from shore. How white the beaches were. How tall the trees. And I remember my village burning. Smoke rising into the sky. Do you remember your home?
That's not true. Perhaps one day you will return to the Summer Isles.
Kill the masters.
Very well, Your Grace.
Glory?
Our queen tried to make peace with the Masters and they tried to murder her.
How many days were you a slave?
Not long enough to understand.
She refused them because hundreds of thousands of men, women, and children still lived in chains.
You promised the slavers they could keep slavery.
Seven years is not a short time for a slave.
Your Grace, there's still no word from the Unsullied.
He better.
Many things.
Ser Davos, Lord Snow.
Forgive me, but may I ask a question?
Your name is Jon Snow, but your father's name was Ned Stark?
We don't have marriage in Naarth so the concept of a bastard doesn't exist.
I was stolen away by slavers.
She bought me from my master and set me free.
I serve my queen because I want to serve my queen.  Because I believe in her.
Then she would give me a ship and wish me good fortune.
I know it.  All of us who came with her from Essos, we believe in her.  She's not our queen because she's the daughter of some king we never knew.  She's the queen we chose.
When the people find out what we have done for them, that we saved them--
No!
Dracarys.
All? Did this one's ears mishear, Your Grace?
(speaking Valerian) She wants to buy them all.
There are 8,000 Unsullied in Astapor. Is this what you mean by all?
Master Greizhen says they cannot sell half-trained boys. If they fail on the battlefield, they will bring shame upon all of Astapor.
Master Kraznys says you cannot afford this.
Your ship will buy you Because Master Kraznys is generous.
The gold you have left is worth 10.
But good Master Kraznys will give you 20.
The Dothraki you have with you--
The Dothraki you have are not worth what they cost to feed, but Master Kraznys will give you three Unsullied for all of them.
Master Kraznys asks how you propose to pay for the remaining 7,877 unsullied.
(speaking Valerian)
They want the biggest one.
(speaking Valerian) She asks that you give me to her, as a present. She asks that you do this now.
This one's name is Missandei, Your Grace.
No, Your Grace. No family living.
Yes, Your Grace. Lying is a great offense. Many of those on the Walk of Punishment were taken there for less.
There are no masters in the grave, Your Grace.
All questions have been taken from them. They obey, that is all. Once they are yours, they are yours. They will fall on their swords if you command it.
Valar morghulis.
He says that we're an army of men without man parts. He claims you are no woman at all, but a man who... hides his cock in his own asshole.
He is very brave, Your Grace.
Who said this?
My master Kraznys would sometimes make us play games.
Only the girls.
We do not drink.
You found out?

We cannot. The Masters only speak one language. They spoke it to me for many years. I know it better than my mother tongue. If we want them to hear us, we must speak it back to them. May it be the last thing they ever hear.
Possibly?
Welcome to Dragonstone.  Our queen knows this is a long journey.  She appreciates the effort you have made on her behalf.  If you wouldn't mind handing over your weapons.
Please, this way.
I was born on the Island of Naarth.

You stand in the presence of Daenerys Stormborn of House Targaryen, rightful heir to the Iron Throne, rightful Queen of the Andals and the First Men, Protector of the Seven Kingdoms, the Mother of Dragons, the Khaleesi of the Great Grass Sea, The Unburnt, The Breaker of Chains.
They can still do something to you.  It only takes one arrow.
And what will they face?
They are not eating. They haven’t touched any food since Queen Daenerys left.
How do you know this?
Many times.
Never.

Your Grace, forgive me, but your translation is not quite accurate.  That noun has no gender in High Valyrian, so the proper translation for that prophecy would be the prince or princess who was promised will bring the dawn.
You leave tomorrow.  Were you going to say goodbye?
But you don't know when that will be.
I wish you good fortune.
Why?
I don't.
That's what I am?  Your weakness?
I believe it.
I do too.
I want to see you.  Please.
Hello.

Text: Naath. I'd like to see the beaches again.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: The Unsullied have stood here for a day and a night with no food or water.
They will stand until they drop.
Such is their obedience.
(speaking Valeryan) The Westrosi woman is pleased with them, but speaks no praise to keep the price down. She wished to know how they are trained.

They begin their training at five. Every day they drill from dawn to dusk until they have mastered the shortsword, the shield, and the three spears. Only one boy in four survives this rigorous training.
Their discipline and loyalty are absolute. They fear nothing.
( speaking Valyrian )
( speaking Valyrian ) The knight says even brave men fear death.
( speaking Valyrian ) Truly, master?
Missandei: My master says the Unsullied are not men. Death means nothing to them.
He begs you attend to this carefully, Your Grace.
My master points out that men don't need nipples.

To win his shield, an Unsullied must go to the slave marts with a silver mark, find a newborn and kill it before its mother's eyes. This way, my master says, we make certain there is no weakness left in them.
( speaking Valyrian ) She is offended. She asks if you pay a sliver coin to the mother for her dead baby.
My master would like you to know that the silver is paid to the baby's owner, not the mother.
( Missandei speaking Valyrian )
Master Kraznys asks that you please hurry. Many other buyers are interested.
Since midnight, Your Grace.
Several times, Your Grace, with Master Kraznys.
They say 1,000 slaves died building the Great Pyramid of Meereen.
If they're smart, Your Grace.
Your Grace.
Torgo Nudho.
I wanted to speak with you.

White Rat, the Unsullied who… I have heard they found his body in a brothel. I have heard that more than one Unsullied has been known to visit Meereen’s brothels. May I ask you why? Why would an Unsullied go to a brothel?
I do not trust them. I will never trust them.
But as a wise man once said, “We make peace with our enemies, not our friends.”
Perhaps I should translate for you.
It’s true. And I would be dead if not for the… (look at Tyrion) little man.
You got my money?
Only room and board?
Could find other work for her, too.
There'd be money. Give you a piece of it.
You fucking dick! Same wrong guess every fucking time. Listen, ya cunt.

There's a man with an ear. Too bad you've got a hangnail for a cock. First time I saw you with your britches down, I thought you were a eunuch! You seen it girls. Like a baby snail peekin' out of his shell. *laughs and walks toward Gilly* *to Gilly* Your baby was crying this morning. It waked me up. You hear me?
Well, what are you going to say?
I don't care if you're sorry. Keep that baby quiet, or I will.
Hehehe, little Wildling bitch. You should've stayed with your own kind. *an owl hoots in the distance* Are you listening to me?
What? It's just an owl you dumb bitch.
Precious little thing.
What's his name?
Lovely. That the daddy?
Where's the daddy?
Oh. Where you from?
North of here? You a fucking wildling?
Meaning all respect, Ser Crow, Craster beat us and worse. Your brother crows beat us and worse. We'll find our own way.
Burn it to the ground and all the dead with it.
Dwarf man making noise!
Dwarf man still making noise.
No gold!
No gold! Fuck off.
Noise again!
No gold!
Where?
You want free? Go be free. (points out of the skycell)
What message?
You go sleep, dwarf man. Sleep good, little dwarf. man!
You wear your hair like a real southern lady now.
It’s important to remember where you come from. I’m not sure your mother would like these new styles.
I’m aware of that.
Yes. I have hair.
Would you like to?
I come from a very small village in -
Sansa -
Now you are being rude.
My prince.
And so he spoke and so he spoke That Lord of Castamere.  And now the rains
Weep o'er his hall...
With no one there to hear Yes, not the rains  weep o'er his hall And not a soul to hear.
Gods, look at this one.
Why are you wasting a woman like this on a Dornishman? Bring him a shaved goat and a bottle of olive oil. (Laughs)
(Screams)
(Groans)
You lost, friend?

--and now you are the law!

Text: (speaking Valyrian.) You heard her! She said she came to free us.
(speaking Valyrian.) She will protect us. She defeated the masters' champion.

Text: I do not know the place from where Old Ser comes. Things maybe are different there, I hope. But here, in Meereen, before Daenerys Stormborn, they own us. So we learn much about them or we do not live long. They teach me what they are. Mercy, fair trial: these mean nothing to them. All they understand is blood!
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: He would rather rip your city apart than see slaves lifted from the dirt.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Then who lives in the Pyramids? Who wears gold masks and murders your children? (beat) When Grey Worm came to us, I was the first to take up the knife for you. I remember the look on my father's face as I struck down his Master, who had traded his infant son for a dog. My father died in the fighting. If we allow the Sons of the Harpy to return us to chains, he never lived.

EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Mhysa, please! Forgive me!

Text: Some dead man.
Elia Martell. I killed her children. Then I raped her. Then I smashed her head in like this!
We think it was an infiltrator from the Brotherhood Without Banners.
Killing them isn't the problem. It's finding them. Have you gone soft, Clegane? I always thought you had a talent for violence.
Who am I fighting?
(Shakes his head.)
You.
(to Gendry) You.
We weren't expecting you till tomorrow, Lord Tywin.
Cells are overflowing, my lord.
My lord.
We hanged 20 men last night.
A coat of gold / Or a coat of red / A lion still has claws And mine,  my lord...
I'll get you!
Mycah, my Lord.
She asked me to, my Lord. She asked me to.
It's not a sword, my prince. It's only a stick.

Die? (lowers the bow) Who said anything about dying? You can’t die yet. Your father was Warden of the North, and Ramsay needs you. Though I suppose he doesn’t need all of you. Just the parts he’ll use to make his heir. Until you’ve given him a boy or two and he’s finished using them. (raises bow) He’s got incredible plans for those parts. So. Shall we wait for him to come back, or shall we begin now?
You’re leaving it to me? Good. Let’s begin.
Stop! Stop!
Shh.

Text: Lord Ramsay sent me to draw your bath, my lady. You want to be clean and fresh for your new husband don’t you?
You’re so beautiful. But if you need to keep him happy. Ramsay gets bored easily. You dont want to end up like... Well, like the others.
I shouldn’t gossip. It’s good to see the red again. No point in hiding anymore.

Let’s see. There was Kiera. The blacksmith’s daughter. She was tall. Like you. Lovely figure. But she talked and talked and talked and… Ramsay got tired of that. Then there was Violet. She had gorgeous blond hair. But she got pregnant and, well, that was boring. Then Tamsay. Such a sweet girl. Of course sweet girls get a bit dull after a while, don’t they? Ramsay let me come with him on that hunt.
Mm. Have you ever seen a body after the dogs have been at it? Not so pretty. But it’s your wedding day, why am I talking about such things?
Myranda.
Are you done with your bath, my lady?
I saw you staring at her.
You said you'd marry me.
Do you think she's pretty?
Perhaps I'll marry, too.
(Smiles). Never.
I like your dress. Who made it for you?
Really?
I'm Myranda, the kennel master's daughter. May I? (Takes Sansa’s arm and inspects her sleeve). Oh, wonderful. The stitching. Who taught you?
I'm sorry for what happened to her.
It's good that she taught you. It was a gift. Now every time you wear something you made, you can remember her.
I know. It's not the same. Still, it's good to remember. Remember the way things were. I almost forgot. There's something else to help you remember.
Down there at the end.
That would spoil the surprise. Go ahead, it's perfectly safe. You won't believe it when you see it.
(laughing) I can't see her, Ramsay.
Where is she?
I only wounded her.
She thinks she's pretty. Let me put one through her face.
Me jealous of her?
I’ll never take it off again.
Do you think mother will like him.
She likes you.
No you don’t.
I know what you’re trying to say.
I do.
I know. About you and mother. I think a part of me always knew. And I’m glad. I’m glad that you’re my father.
Uncle.
You don’t like it?
Not at all. The Dornish climate agrees with me.
You looked different when I left. You had more hair.
He’ll be alright.
Why is it happening at all?
This is my home, this has been my home for years. I didn’t want to come here but she told me to. I did what she said, I did my duty and now she’s forcing me to go back?
It’s not complicated at all, it’s simple. I love Trystane, I’m going to marry him, and we’re staying right here.
Of course you don’t! You don’t know me!
What if he says no?
Do you want to marry me because our families arranged it, or do you…
We can’t. Not yet.
You know why not. Someone will see.
How many other girls have you walked through these gardens?
They don’t go squinty, and you didnt answer the question.
Uncle Jaime? I don’t understand, what are you doing here?
Trystane is my intended.
No!
You hurt him!
I don’t want to!
When will Joffrey and Sansa be married?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Mother says I'll have a new gown for the ceremony and another for the feast. But yours will be ivory, since you're the bride.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I'm glad you're not dead.
And you really believe that?
That necklace was stolen from my room.
Is Bran going to die?
Wylis! What’s the matter?
You leave the fighting to the little lords, Wylis. Come along, now. It’s no place for you.
Well, he’s never going to learn to fight because he’s a stable boy. So leave him be.
Lyanna.
No, I’m not a dream. I’m here. Right here.
I’ve missed you, too.
Shh. You are.
You’re not going to die.
Get her some water!
Is there a maester?
I looked for you on the Trident.
The Mad King is dead. Rhaegar lies beneath the ground. Why weren’t you there to protect your prince?
Where’s my sister?
No. Now it ends.
And which one of you was a marksman at 10?
And which one of you was a marksman at ten? Keep practicing, Bran. Go on.
Get the lads to saddle their horses.
He swore an oath, Cat.
Tell Bran he’s coming, too.
He won’t be a boy forever. And winter is coming.
In the name of Robert of the House Baratheon, first of his name …
King of the Andals and the First Men …
Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and protector of the realm, I, Eddard of the House Stark, Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North, sentence you to die.
You understand why I did it?
But do you understand why I had to kill him?
The man who passes the sentence should swing the sword.
The White Walkers have been gone for thousands of years.
A madman sees what he sees.
There are no mountain lions in these woods.
It’s a direwolf.
Tough old beast.
Better a quick death. They won’t last without their mother.
I’m sorry, Bran.
You will train them yourselves. You will feed them yourselves. And if they die, you will bury them yourselves.
You have five northern children. You’re not an outsider.
It’s your gods with all the rules.
Tell me.
Your sister. The boy …
He’s coming this far North, there’s only one thing he’s after.
Hey,, hey, hey, hey. What are you doing with that on? [Pulls off ARYA’s helm]
Your Grace.
Guarding the North for you, Your Grace. Winterfell is yours.
My queen.
Tell me about Jon Arryn.
We both did.
Aye.
I’m not worthy of the honor.
She was my sister. This is where she belongs.
It’s done, Your Grace. The Targaryens are gone.
The boy I beheaded. Did you know him?
He was talking madness. Said the Walkers slaughtered his friends.
A wildling ambush.
Winter is coming.
Your pardon.
Yes, the king has honored me with his offer.
I don’t fight in tournaments.
I don’t fight in tournaments because when I fight a man for real, I don’t want him to know what I can do.
I’m a Northman. I belong here with you, not down south in that rat’s nest they call a capital.
The king takes what he wants. That’s why he’s king.
How did he get so fat?
Send him in.
Stay.
What news?
She’s fresh widowed, Cat. She doesn’t know what she’s saying.
No, but I’m still better than you.
I hope I’ll serve you well.
Now and always!
Varys.
What about my daughters?
And Sansa?
You watched my men being slaughtered and did nothing.
The madness of mercy. That she might save her children.
The Queen can't kill me. Cat holds her brother.
If that's true, then slit my throat and be done with it.
Tell me something, Varys.
I've had worse, my lady.
The South doesn't seem to agree with you.
I know the truth Jon Arryn died for.
Has he done this before?
Your brother Or your lover?
My son saw you with him.
With all my heart.
And they are all Jaime's.
You've always hated him.
When the King returns from his hunt, I'll tell him the truth.
I've made many mistakes in my life, but that wasn't one of them.
You damned fool.
I will.
I'll, I'll do everything I can to honor your memory.
Give him milk of the poppy.
No man could have protected him from himself.
His squire? The Lannister boy?
His Grace has had a change of heart concerning Daenerys Targaryen.
And what should I do with a hundred swords?
What about Stannis?
Stannis is your older brother.
Stannis is a commander. He's led men into war twice.
I will not dishonor Robert's last hours by shedding blood in his halls and dragging frightened children from their beds.
You will sail to dragonstone tonight.
Now leave us.
The King has no true born sons.
The throne passes to his brother Lord Stannis.
There is no "unless".
Do you have a shred of honor?
We?
What you suggest is treason.
Make peace with the Lannisters, you say. With the people who tried to murder my boy.
No. I won't do it.
There is no other choice. He is the heir.
You promised Catelyn you would help me.
No, all right.
King Joffrey?
Good. Is Lord Renly joining us?
Ser Barristan, I believe no man here could ever question your honor.
Ser Barristan is a good man, a loyal man. Do him no harm.
Commander! Take the Queen and her children into custody.
I want no bloodshed.
Whoa, boy, whoa.
No Ser!
No Ser!
No Ser!
No!
Lord Commander!
You earned that, Snow.
Well done.
It’s the Lord Commander!
Who’s gonna help us?
Who killed him?
And they’ll slaughter us all.
It’s a sad fucking statement if Dolorous Edd is our only chance.
What’s one redhead gonna do against 40 armed men?
Traitors!
They want to come in, they’re gonna come in.
Edd is our only chance.
Murderers!
Come on, move 'em out.
Forgive my sister. What she lacks in diplomacy, she makes--
I never miss.
Luck.
You’re too slow. You’ve always been too slow.
Slut.
We may have a problem.
You can't have them all.  It's not fair.
The Mountain killed father.  He'll crush you with one hand.
You want her to do it or me?
Her or me?
Good.
You’re a greedy bitch, you know that?
We invited you to Dorne because we needed your help. You came to Dorne because you needed our help?
Obara.
We’re lucky he’s a singer. If he were a fighter we might have been in trouble.
It’s amazing how many men we beat seem to have this code.
You’re coming with me.
I wasn’t asking, princess.
I am Obara Sand. Daughter of Oberyn Martell. I fight for Dorne. Who do you fight for?
Will it be war?
Without Doran we have no army to march against the Lannisters.
A ship's captain, who found me in Planky Town, claiming he had information to sell. He told me he smuggled Jaime Lannister into Dorne.
When I was a child, (Begins walking), Oberyn came to take me to court. I'd never seen this man, and yet he called himself my father. My mother wept, said I was too young, and a girl.
(Smiles). I made my choice long ago.
I'm oldest.
She's not my momma.  You can have Cersei.  I want The Mountain.
Will you shut up about Momma?  Momma, momma.
We’re not here to feed you.
We’re here to kill you.
Smart boy.
I like to move around.
You learn this during your years in the fighting pits? I always drink before a fight.
Today is not the day I die.
I'm going to kill that.
Size does not matter when you are flat on your back.
Never.
Have they told you who I am?
