There's something else, my lord. A man, I didn't know his face, he came to ask if I'd testify against you. Said I'd be named Ser Podrick Payne if I told the judges you'd bought a poison called the Strangler.
I didn't tell them anything, my lord.
My lord.
You've been good to me, my lord.
I already gave them an answer, my lord.
My lord.
Farewell, my lord.
How do we get through there?
It sets miles out of the way. We'll lose sight of them.
He hates that nickname.
I squired for a knight name Ser Lorimer during the War of the Five Kings. One night, he had a bit too much to drink. And he was famished, so he borrowed a ham.

He wasn't a thief. He was drunk and hungry and he wasn't thinking. I was drunk too. He gave me half the ham. Next morning, one of the guards saw him passed out, under a wagon, with the ham bone still in his hand. They hanged the man afternoon. They tied the noose for me too, but, Lord Tywin heard my family name was Payne. So he pardoned me, and sent me to King's Landing to squire for his son.
It didn't seem that way. Lord Tyrion was always very good to me.
I'm not sorry. You're the best fighter I've ever seen. You beat The Hound. I'm proud to be your squire.
If you wouldn't snap at me I wouldn't learn anything.
Yes.
Thank you.
I suppose that's more important.
You weren't a knight, but you were a Kingsguard to Renly Baratheon, weren't you?
Lord Tyrion said he was a good man.
But how did you end up serving Renly?
But wasn't he... Lord Tyrion said he was...
But you said a shadow murdered him. How do you fight a shadow?
More wine?
Apologies, my lord.
Is it solid gold?
I'll clean it up.
Yes, my lord.
Must be good food if it’s crowded.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: My lady!
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Sansa Stark. (Brienne begins to turn around) Don't look!
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: She's dyed her hair, but it's her. She's sitting with Petyr Baelish.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: A bunch of knights with him.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: 10, I think. Too many. My lady, I don't think this is the right--
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: We only have one horse.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Stop, stop, stop! (Horse stops) That’s a good boy.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Yes, my lady?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Yes, my lady. (stands) Sansa Stark?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: My lady, if those Stark girls refused your service, maybe you’re released from your vow.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: If they don’t want your protection--
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: (pause) No, my lady.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Thank you, milord.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: High in the halls of the kings who are gone, Jenny would dance with her ghosts, the ones she had lost and the ones she had found and the ones who had loved her the most.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Ser Bronn. Ser Bronn.
Apologies, ser.
Lord Tyrion sent me.
He said it was a matter of life and death.
Ser Bronn of the Blackwater was anointed by the king himself.
Wild lemons on a purple field, House Dalt of Lemonwood. A vulture grasping a baby in it's talons, House Blackmont. A crowned skull, the Manwoodys of Kingsgrave.
I don't see it, my lord.
Will we head north at some point? He said Sansa had a brother at Castle Black. We’re a few days ride from the Kingsroad, but that will take us--
But I’m you’re squire.
An attendant to a knight?
Well, where would I go?
You swore to find the Stark girls.
Sansa still might.
Meat and mead at my table.
The ones who'd been gone for so very long.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: She couldn't remember their names. They spun her around on the damp old stones.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Spun away all her sorrow and pain. And she never wanted to leave
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Never wanted to leave. Never wanted to leave. Never wanted to leave.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Never wanted to leave. Never wanted to leave.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: This lot won't be here long. Don't need no permanent place. After we interrogate 'em, we usually just
What are you looking at? Kneel! Kneel or I'll carve your lungs out, boy.
What do we have here?

That was Ser Jaime's doing, not hers. When Queen Cersei wants something, she pays in advance and in gold. Several chests of it, in fact. Waiting for you in a wagon just outside.

Our queen's brothers are unlikely to survive their Northern adventures. But in the event that they do-
She has a keen sense of poetic justice.

When the Citadel expelled me, I thought I would die poor and alone, but in exchange for my service, Queen Cersei made me her Hand. What would she do for the man who rids her of her treasonous brothers?
Ser Bronn of the Blackwater.
Qyburn hopes your father will force the Citadel to give him back his chain.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: He understands well enough.
(nods)
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Ancient and glorious is Yunkai. Our empire was old before dragons stirred in old Valyria. Many an army has broken against our walls. You shall find no easy conquest here, khaleesi.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: If blood is your desire, blood shall flow. But why? 'Tis true you have committed savageries in Astapor. But the Yunkai are a forgiving and generous people.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: The wise masters of Yunkai have sent a gift for the silver queen.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: There is far more than this awaiting you on the deck of your ship.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Yes, khaleesi. As I said, we are a generous people. You shall have as many ships as you require.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: All we ask is that you make use of these ships. Sail them back to Westeros where you belong and leave us to conduct our affairs in peace.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: My life?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You are mad. We are not Astapor or Qarth. We are Yunkai and we have powerful friends. Friends who would take great pleasure in destroying you. Those who survive, we shall enslave once more. Perhaps we'll make a slave of you as well.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You swore me safe conduct.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Take the gold.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: (speaks Valerian)
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: (picking up the dragon eggs from their chest, speaking in Dothraki) ls this your command, Khaleesi? (Daenerys nods, Rakharo places the eggs next to Drogo’s funeral pyre)
Khaleesi, do not do this thing. Let me kill this witch.
This is blood magic. lt is forbidden.
(in Dothraki) Nothing good will come of this.
They will not come. They say she is cursed.
Lamb men make good slaves. Khal Drogo will make a gift of them to the slavers.
She is a lamb girl, Khaleesi. The riders do her honor.
Huh-uh, huh-uh.
Huh.
You walk. [to Viserys]
Dothraki don’t wear steel dresses.
Armor. Armor make a man... Vroz?
Slow.
My father taught me how to fight. He taught me that speed defeats size.
He was bloodrider to Khal Bharbo. And your father, Jorah the Andal? He was a warrior also?
There are no rabbits.
Have you seen any ducks, woman? No rabbits, no ducks. Do you have eyes in your head? Do you?
A blessing from the Great Stallion.
(Speaking Dothraki) What do we seek, Khaleesi?
(Speaking Dothraki) I will not fail you, blood of my blood.
This is… bad time to start.
I'm Kenning, commander of this garrison. And you say you're Theon Greyjoy?
And why should I believe that?
And if you were my prince, why are you marching with a Bolton army?
And what does Lord Bolton want?
He wants us to surrender?
...shame fought with honor.
No shame fought with honor. Only a whipped dog would speak this way. Or a woman. Are you a woman boy?
The Ironborn will not surrender.
You go tell your master that, Theon Greyjoy. Or whoever the fuck you are. What in the hell are you muttering?
Well, the girls weren't lying. You had a good-sized cock.
What? No. Pork sausage. You think I'm some sort of savage?
People talk about phantom limbs. An amputee might have an itch where his foot used to be. So I've always wondered do eunuchs have a phantom cock?
Next time you think about naked girls, would you feel an itch?
Sorry. I shouldn't make jokes. My mother taught me not to throw stones at cripples.
But my father taught me to aim for their head.
Sorry, what?
A little louder.
You're no good to me dead. We need you.
You don't look like a Theon Greyjoy anymore. That's a name for a lord. But you're not a lord, are you? You're just meat.
Stinking meat.
You reek. Reek! That's a good name for you. What's your name?
What's your name?
What is your name?!
Looks like we’re done here.
And I accept your surrender.
My beloved wife. I’ve missed you terribly.

Thank you for returning Lady Bolton safely. Now, dismount and kneel before me, surrender your army and proclaim me the true Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North. I will pardon you for deserting the Night’s Watch. I will pardon these treasonous lords for betraying my house. Come, bastard, you don’t have the men, you don’t have the horses, and you don’t have Winterfell. Why lead those pour souls into slaughter? There’s no need for a battle. Get off your hose and kneel. I’m a man of mercy.

I keep hearing stories about you, bastard. The way people in the North talk about you, you’re the greatest swordsman who ever walked. Maybe you are that good. Maybe not. I don’t know if I’d beat you. But I know that my army will beat yours. I have 6,000 men. You have, what, half that? Not even?
He’s good. Very good. Tell me, will you let your little brother die because you’re too proud to surrender?
Now, if you want to save —
Do you like games, little man? Let’s play a game. Run to your brother. The sooner you make it to him, the sooner you get to see him. That’s it. That’s the game. Easy. Ready? Go.
No, you have to run, remember? Those are the rules.
Now.
Loose!
It’s time, go.
Their army’s gone.
We have Winterfell. They don’t have the men for a siege. All we have to do is wait.
I’ve reconsidered. I think that sounds like a wonderful idea.
Ah. Sansa. Hello, Sansa. Is this where I’ll be staying now? No. Our time together is about to come to an end. That’s all right. You can’t kill me. I’m part of you now.
My hounds will never harm me.
They’re loyal beasts.
Sit. Down! Down! Down! Down! Down!

Bracken. Mmm, strong. As long as they're in the see. But when you take them out of the water, no bones. They collapse under their proud weight and slum into a heap of nothing. You'd think they'd know that. Unfortunately they're not very bright. What do you tell them?
And what are you really?
Are you sure? You do look very much like a lord. Formidable, proud.
Until when?
That's right. Until you're rotting in the ground. Remember what you are and what you're not. Bring me Lord Kenning.
You didn't really think I'd let them go did you? It's falling out of fashion. Fileting. Sad, but true. Traditions are important. Where are we without our history, eh?
I believe we will. To our new home.
Moat Cailin is yours, Father.
No.
Unimportant. A cripple, a young boy. None of the northern lords have seen them. Dead most likely. Most of the Ironborn have fled the North. Now more will follow, thanks to you. Tell me what you see.
Moors, fields, hills.
Tell me what you see.
Nothing.
Ramsay Snow.
You honor me. I swear I will uphold your name and your tradition. I will be worthy of you, Father, I promise.
Come, Reek, I'll be needing a bath.
Stannis isn’t from the North. You are, father. I think you’re missing an opportunity to show the people of the North how House Bolton treats southern invaders.
That we not sit and wait to let Stannis to decide what type of fight this is going to be. That we hit first, and hit hard, and leave a feast for the crows.
I don’t need an army. I need 20 good men.
I'm sorry. I'm sorry. Terrible timing.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: But I was getting jealous.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Well? Should we see this cock everyone's always going on about?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Everyone knows you love girls. I bet you always thought they loved you back.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Your famous cock must be very precious to you. Would you say it's your most precious part?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: This is mercy. I'm not killing you. Just making a few alterations.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Yes, Reek?
My beautiful wife.
When my father told me we were marrying I half expected a fat, bearded beast. Do you know how pleased I was when I saw you? You’ve made me very happy.

Our scouts report that Stannis Baratheon rides for Winterfell. He’s a respected commander. His troops are loyal and battle tested. He’s hired thousands of foreign sell swords to bolster his army. But this storm was a stroke of luck for us northerners. Our people are used to fighting in frost. His army’s out there now, suffering in the snow. One day I’ll be Lord of Winterfell, and Warden of the North. You’ll be my Lady and Wardeness.
What of it?
Then I’ll have a baby brother.
I’m Lord Bolton’s eldest son.
I’ve been naturalized by a royal decree of--
Bastards can rise high in the world. Like your half-brother Jon Snow. Born The Bastard of Winterfell, now the Lord Commander of the Night’s Watch.
You didn’t know? Yes, he’s done very well for himself. Ah. I near forgot why I asked you to join me. Come, my lady.
Your Northern friend. Reek told me you wanted to leave.
Why? Winterfell is your home and I am your husband.

Tough old bird. Everyone talks when I start beating them. But this one. Her heart gave out before I got to her face. We do breed them tough in the North. Bring my wife back to her chamber. It’s far too cold out here for a lady. You should hold on to your candles. The nights are so long now.
Sorry. Were you sleeping?
Wa--? Wa--? Water? You want some water. I wish I had some for you.
So let's play a game. Which body part do you need the least?
Please is not a body part.

But you've already told me everything. Remember? Your daddy was mean to you. The Starks didn't appreciate you. One good bit, though. The Stark boys, they're still alive. Wouldn't that be a hunt to remember? You failed, but I'm a better hunter than you.
Now how about your little finger? You don't use that for much, do you? No? Good. Let's start with that.

You've been wondering why you're here. Haven't you? Where you are. Who I am. Why I'm doing this to you. So guess. If you guess right, I'll tell you. By the old gods and the new, I swear it. You win the game if you can figure out who I am and why I'm torturing you and I win the game if you beg me to cut off your finger.
If you think this has a happy ending, you haven't been paying attention.
You say please again and you'll wish you hadn't. You first. Where are we?
Too vague.
Terrible guess.
Now, where?
Do I look like a fucking Umber to you?
Karhold? How did you know that? Did you see any banners flying when we came in?
Very good, Lord Theon. And who am I?
He's dead. Strangled by the Kingslayer.
You're right.
Yes. You win.
Of course, you forgot to ask one question. You forgot to ask if I'm a liar.
I'm afraid I am.
Everything I told you is a lie.
I win.
This is turning into a lovely evening.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You've got bigger balls than he ever did. But with those big balls of yours...
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: how fast can you run?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I have a treat for you. A reward.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Yes, Reek. Those creatures who came in the night, they wanted to take you away. And you didn't let them. You remained loyal.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Yes, yes, Reek. It's a bath. For you. Remove those rags. Now.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: The britches, too, Reek. Take them off.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Do you love me, Reek?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Good. Because I need you to do something for me. Something very important. There's a castle, you see. Some bad men hold this castle. I need your help to take this castle back.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I need you to play a role. To pretend to be someone you're not.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Theon Greyjoy.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Ramsay of House Bolton. Heir to the Dreadfort and Winterfell. Who gives her?
Are you pleased my lady?
Good. I want you to be happy. My father said you’re still a virgin.
Why? Why are you still a virgin? Afraid of dwarves?
You’re not lying to me?
Lying to your husband on his wedding night, that would be a very bad way to start a marriage. We are man and wife now, we should be honest with each other, don’t you think?
Good. Now take off your clothes.
Oh, no no no. You stay here Reek. You watch. (to Sansa) Do I need to ask a second time? I hate asking a second time.
Reek? I told you to watch.
You’ve known Sansa since she was a girl. Now watch her become a woman.
Come back here. Myranda.
I'm going to marry her. That will involve looking at her from time to time.
And I meant it. When I was a bastard named Snow. But I'm a Bolton now. What I want is no longer the primary consideration. I'm furthering a dynasty.
Of course I do. I'm not blind.
You think she's pretty, too. I'm looking forward to our wedding night. But don't worry, I'll have plenty of time for you.

(Laughs). You're the kennel master's daughter. Who are you going to marry, the stable keeper's son? (She tries to hit him, but he restrains her). You're mine. You're not going anywhere. Unless I have to listen to more of your jealousy. Jealousy bores me. You remember what happens to people who bore me. You're not going to bore me, are you, Myranda?
You smell particularly ripe this evening. Pour me some wine.
Do you have something to tell me?
Reek.
Who?
She saw you?
Come here.
You mustn't keep secrets from me, Reek. Get on your knees.
Give me your hand.
I forgive you.
Allow me.
Mother.
My lady, we are all a family, we northerners. Our blood ties go back thousands of years. So I'd like to drink to our wedding. May our happiness spread from Moat Cailin to the Last Hearth.
You're right. Very strange. More wine, please.
That's his new name, Reek.
Because Reek has something to say to you. Don't you, Reek? (He gestures Theon over). An apology. Apologize to Lady Sansa for what you did. Apologize for murdering her two brothers.
Look at her, Reek. An apology doesn't mean anything if you're not looking the person in the eye.
Sorry about what?

There, over and done with. Doesn't everyone feel better? I do. That was getting very tense. Whew. (Theon turns to leave). You know what, my lady? What with him having murdered your brothers and the rest of your family gone Reek here is the nearest thing to living kin that you have left. Reek. You will give away the bride. Someone has to. What better person? Good? Good?
Wonderful. (Sips wine).
How can you be sure?
That she's pregnant. I mean how can you tell?
So how did you manage it?
Getting her pregnant.
Of course, but how did you find it?
And if it's a boy?
My position is quite clear. I'm your son until a better alternative comes along.
Why would I? She had me, she died. And here we are.
Yes.
Not far, my lord. Your sister is waiting for you there.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Yes. I served them, the men who were torturing you. I did what they told me and waited for the right moment.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I grew up on Saltcliffe, my lord. I was only a boy when they took you away. My father brought me and my brothers up to the bluffs so we could watch the ship that carried you off. I remember the look on my father's face when he told us, 'That's Balon Greyjoy's last living son.' And those are the words I heard over and over again when I saw what they were doing to you.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Not all those are your sister's men. Some of them are loyal to your father.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: What is it?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: What did he tell you to do?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: He lorded it over you?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: The Stark boys?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Maybe it's not too late.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Not everything, my lord. She's up here.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Shh. Wait.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I brought him back. He killed the others.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Put him back where he belongs.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: They've cleaned you up nicely. You know who I am?
Yes. A lord. You’ve seen my banners?
Does that worry you at all?
No.
You served the Starks?
The Starks have been gone for a long time, but you kept protecting Rickon.
Be that as it may, Rickon's not yours to sell anymore. He’s mine. So what use could I possibly have for you?
Are you sure you know what that is?
You’re a good talker.
I like that.
You’re a much better talker than Theon Greyjoy.
I had to work hard to get him talking. But he talked. They all do.
He told me everything. All about the Stark boys. Who helped them escape, and how she did it.
Keep quiet.
You're sure?
Ride east. Follow the rising sun. Your sister's waiting for you.
We're not in the Iron Islands. Go.
Come, my lord.
You're a long way from home and winter is coming.
You can't hold the North if you let these lesser lords insult us.
Lord Cerwyn refused to pay. Said the Warden of the North would always be a Stark, and he'd be damned if he kissed a traitor's boot.
I flayed him living, along with his wife and brother. Made his son watch.
The new Lord Cerwyn paid his taxes.
Our pact with the Lannisters protects -
It's an honor to meet you, my Lady.
She really is lovely. I hope I can make her happy.
I'll never hurt her. You have my word.
I haven't been a lord very long. I was a bastard.
Yes, father. And thank you, Lord Baelish. I'm forever in your debt. (Bows).
The Umbers are a famously loyal house.
When my father became Warden of the North, your house refused to pledge their banners.
My beloved father, the Warden --
My father was poisoned by our enemies.
Why have you come to Winterfell, Lord Umber?
So now you’ve come seeking help?
Pledge your banners to House Bolton.  Swear loyalty to me as Warden of the North and we will fight together to destroy the bastard and all his wildling friends.
Traditionally a bannerman kneels before his lord.
Why would I trust a man who won’t honor tradition?
Then it appears we're at a bit of an impasse.
A girl, I hope. I prefer redheads.
I like them wild.
Who’s this?
How do I know that’s Rickon Stark?
Welcome home, Lord Stark.
Your sister sent me.
I'll come for you tonight when the castle sleeps.
Tansy! Tansy! Tansy!
Tansy! Tansy!
There you are.
Tansy!
If you make it out of the woods, you win! Run, Tansy, run!
Tansy.
Tansy, Tansy, Tansy!
Good girls. Down, girls. Down. Well done. You, too.
You brought her down. That's what matters. A fine shot. Wasn't it, Reek?
Oh, sweet. Don't cry. It will be over soon.
We have to reward the hounds, love. They did all the hard work.
But you made Myranda feel jealous.
You can see that your presence has become a bit of a problem.
Rip her! Rip her! Rip her!
Not so pretty now.
Father. Welcome home.
A pleasure, Mother.
With the hounds. I'll have a look at him.
I hear you took a hand from the Kingslayer.
(laughs)
Father.
I trained him. He was a slow learner, but he learned.
Peeled a few bits. Removed a few others.
We've been flaying our enemies for 1,000 years. The flayed man is on our banners.
I already asked. Lord Greyjoy refused. Savages have no...
You made me acting Lord of the Dreadfort. I acted.
Theon was our enemy. But Reek, Reek will never betray us.
Reek, how could you let me stand before my father unshaven? It's disrespectful.
Give him the razor.
If I'm not a Bolton, Father, what does it matter?
Go on, Reek. A nice close shave.
Reek, tell Father where are Bran and Rickon Stark?
Reek, did you murder the Stark boys?
And crisped them so no one would know.
The Starks have always ruled the North. If Bran and Rickon are alive, the country will rally to their side now that Robb Stark is gone.
Oh, that's right, Reek. Robb Stark is dead. Sorry. I know he was like a brother to you. But my father put a knife through his heart. How do you feel about that?
Even if he doesn't, he's half Stark himself. Could be a threat.
They obviously had help.
We know where she’s going. Her brother’s at Castle Black.
So was I, Father.
Castle Black isn’t defended on the southern side. And the few men left are barely men at all. Farm boys and thieves. With a small force we could storm the castle, kill Jon Snow --
We don’t need every house in the North. The Umbers, the Manderlys, and the Karstarks. command more soldiers than all the other houses combined. With their support, none could challenge us.
Thank you for saying that. It means a great deal to me.
Maester Wolkan, send ravens to all the Northern houses. Roose Bolton is dead. Poisoned by our enemies.
How did he die?
Send for Lady Walda and the baby.
There he is.
Little brother.
Of course. Follow me, Mother.
I am Lord Bolton.
The kennelmaster’s daughter. She smelled of dog. I wasn’t much older, but everybody was already afraid of me. You certainly were.
Myranda wasn’t, though. What could I do to her that those hounds couldn’t? She was fearless. There was nothing she wouldn’t do.
Your pain will be paid for a thousand times over. I wish you could be here to watch.
Buried, burned?
This is good meat. Feed it to the hounds.
No.
No.
I have a team of men after them with some of my best hounds. They won’t get far.

She’s a fine woman, your sister. I look forward to having her back in my bed. And you’re all fine-looking men. My dogs are desperate to meet you. I haven’t fed them for seven days. They’re ravenous. I wonder which parts they’ll try first. Your eyes? Your balls? We’ll find out soon enough. In the morning, then, bastard.
You suggested one on one combat, didn’t you?
Let’s head back. My wife must be lonely.
Down!
She was 11 the first time I saw her.
If you can't ride, we don't stand a chance.
Congratulations, Father. I look forward to meeting my new brother.
May I hold him?
I prefer being an only child.
That’s enough of that.
Not fat enough already?
I read your letter. You’re to be a maester.
I thought the Night’s Watch might make a man of you. Something resembling a man at least. You managed to stay soft and fat.
Your nose buried in books. Spending your life reading about the achievements of better men. I’ll wager you still can’t sit a horse or wield a sword.
Your way down to Castle Black? Where did he kill a white walker? Where are you from? How’d you come to meet my son?
Where?
North of the Wall? You’re a Wildling. The Seven Kingdoms have waged war against these savages for centuries and here I sit hosting one in my hall thanks to my son. See that sword?

It’s called Heartsbane. It’s been in our family for 500 years. It’s Valyrian steel. Only a handful of them left in the world. It’s supposed to go to my firstborn son after I die. To him. He will never wield that sword. If he were to become Lord Tarly of Horn Hill, it would be the end of this house. I took you for a Mole’s Town whore when I saw you and I made my peace with that. Who else would have him? But I overestimated him. No. It was a wildling whore that seduced my son. This you getting back at me, boy? Hmm? Bring that to my table and making me dine with it. And you got what you were after, didn’t you? A bastard. A half-breed bastard. Your invitation into our home.
He dishonors us.
I already have a queen.

There are no easy choices in war.  Say what you will about your sister, she was born in Westeros, has lived here all her life.  You on the other hand, you murdered your own father and chose to support a foreign invader.  One with no ties to this land, an army of savages at her back.
You cannot send me to the Wall.  You are not my queen.
Step back and shut your mouth.
A stupid boy.
The granaries are being emptied and loaded into wagons, My Lord.
My Lord.
All of the gold is safely through the gates of King's Landing.
We need to get the last of these wagons over the Blackwater Rush before nightfall.  If the head of the line is ambushed, the tail will never be able to reinforce in time.
With your permission, flogging stragglers has a marked effect on mobility.
Come on, lads.  Not those wagons.  Fall in line.  Fall in line.
Spears armed!
She has three full-grown dragons, Your Grace.  The same as Aegon when he conquered the Seven Kingdoms.  How do you propose to stop them?
Ser, Jaime.  I believe you know my son.
If my queen summons me, I answer the call.  And I've heard what she does to those that defy her.
I have an army to mobilize.  It won't be long until the fighting starts.
It's a long ride back to the reach, Ser Jaime.  How may I serve?

Including Olenna Tyrell?  I'm a Tarley.  That name means something.  We're not oath breakers.  We're not schemers.  We don't stab our rivals in the back or cut their throats at weddings.   I swore an oath to House Tyrell.
I've known Olenna since I was a child.

Your mother’s a fine woman. You’re not worthy of her. To please her, I’ll take the wildling in. She can work in the kitchens. The bastard will be raised here. But this will be the last night you ever spend at Horn Hill.
Raper. Raper. Horse thief. Ninth born son. Raper. Thief. Thief and raper.
Hey. Hey, get up. Get up.
They're here, the Night's Watch.
Come to take the black pudding.
You are in love, Lord Snow. You girls can do as you please. But if Thorne puts me up against Lady Piggy, I'm gonna slice me off a side of bacon.
We need to get out of here.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: The Lord Commander told us to go to the Fist of the First Men. How'd that turn out for us?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: We do now. We know what's out there.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: So he's your new protector now? Our good friend Craster?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Mormont isn't gonna save you. This lummox here isn't gonna save you. When the walkers come calling, Craster will serve us up like so many pigs. If we want to live we'll have to look out for ourselves.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: It wasn't his foot that killed him. That bastard Craster starved him to death.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You on his side?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: The day we leave, Craster will tap a barrel of our wine and sit down to a feast of ham and potatoes and laugh at us starving in the snow. He's a bloody wildling is all he is.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: He's sitting there drinking our wine, eating his fill while we die.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: So you admit you've got a hidden larder, then? How else'd you make it through winter?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You're a stingy bastard!
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Run fast, Piggy, and sleep well! I'll be cutting your throat one of these nights.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: We should kill that thing.
Pink-eyed fuck. You thirsty?
Fuck!
If I was your size, I'd be king of the fucking world.
What the fuck's wrong with him?
It's Jon Snow's brother.
What do you care about our faces?

What's the matter, Piggy? You crying 'cause you're cold? There's 200 brothers killed by dead men and you're still here whimpering. That seem fair to you? I seen 'em torn to pieces fighting while you was off somewhere hiding in a hole. Why don't you lie down and rest for a while, eh? You know you want to.
Looks like that piggy is done for.
He's slowing us down.
Why? So the rest of us can die? We'll move faster without him.
If he can't, we may as well just go without him.
Why should the rest of us-
What am I supposed to do?
I'm not dying for you, Piggy. You hear me?
Enough of that!
Let 'em fight.
Burn the body.
You don't want this one coming back for you.
Up she goes.
Hey, come on. Put your back into it, huh?
Here you are, son.
Oh, gentlemen, please.
In all my days I’ve never seen a man swing an ax like that. How many men did it take to cut you down?
Ooh. he must have been some kind of monster.
I think some of the men are a bit afraid of you.
When I found you, I thought you’d been dead for days. The way you were stinking already and you had bugs all over you and bone was coming through right there.

I was gonna give you a proper burial and then you coughed. Oh, nearly shit myself. I reckoned you were gonna die by the time I loaded you on the wagon, but you didn’t. Now, I reckoned you’d die a dozen more times over the next few days, but you didn’t. What kept you going?
No, there’s a reason you’re still here.
No, a reason. Gods aren’t done with you yet.
Well, maybe he was right. I don’t know much about the gods.

Oh, there’s plenty of pious sons of bitches who think they know the word of god or gods. I don’t. I don’t even know their real names. Maybe it is the Seven. Or maybe it’s the old gods. Or maybe it’s the Lord of Light. Or maybe they’re all the same fucking thing. I don’t know. What matters, I believe, is that there’s something greater than us. And whatever it is, it’s got plans for Sandor Clegane.
I’ve heard stories.
They have.

I was a soldier once. All my superiors thought I was brave. I wasn’t. I mean, I never ran from a fight. Only because I was afraid my friends would see I was afraid. That’s all I was, a coward. We followed orders no matter the orders. Burn that village. Fine, I’m your arsonist. Steal that farmer’s crops. Good, I’m your thief. Kill those young lads so they won’t take up arms against us. I’m your murderer. I remember once a woman screaming at us, calling us animals as we dragged her son from their hut. But we weren’t animals. Animals are true to their nature and we had betrayed ours. I cut that young boy’s throat myself as his mother screamed and my friends held her back.
That night...I felt such shame. Shame was so heavy on me, I couldn’t eat, I couldn’t sleep.
All I could do was stare into that dark sky and listen to that mother screaming her son’s name. I’ll hear her screaming the rest of my life.

Now, I know I can never bring that lad back. All I can do with time I’ve got left is bring a little goodness into the world. That’s all any of us can do, isn’t it? Never too late to stop robbing people, to stop killing people. Start helping people.
It’s never too late to come back.
And it’s not about waiting for the gods to answer your prayers. It’s not even about the gods. It’s about you. Learning you have to answer your prayers yourself.
Seven save you, friends. How can we help you?
Well, we’re talking about life. You?
Well, we thank you for your protection. Who are you protecting us from?
No horses. No gold. No steel.
I’m sure it is. You’re welcome to stay for supper, but we have hungry mouths here.
I’m a fucking septon. What was I supposed to say?
Aye, well, all are welcome here. Anyway, we got nothing for them.
What do you want to do? Fight them? Kill them?
It’d be you against all of them. I mean, these people don’t know how to fight.
I’m done with fighting.
Violence is a disease. You don’t cure a disease by spreading it to more people.
You’ve done enough work for one day. Come on up for some supper.
I’ll save you a bowl of stew. Might even have some ale hidden away.
I imagine it’s difficult adjust to the new reality. Your reign is over.
Him. He should die.
He’s not one of us. He’s an outsider, a lowborn. He does not speak for us.
We came here to meet the queen and instead we’re greeted by a dwarf and a eunuch.
When we last met, I offered her ships so she could return to Westeros where she belongs. She refused them.
As they have since the dawn of time.

Once before, I offered you peace. If you had not been so arrogant, you could have returned to your homeland with a fleet of ships. Instead, you will flee Slaver’s Bay on foot like the beggar queen you are.
The Lord of Light sent the Mother of Dragons to you, and those who love the darkness chased her away.

How will you respond? Will you wring your hands, while you wait for the Mother of Dragons to return? Or will you  you take up her flames yourselves? Will you fight for your own salvation, now that Queen Daenerys is not here to fight for you?

From the fire she was reborn to remake the world. Daenerys is a gift from the Lord of Light to her children. If we are steadfast in our love for the Queen and her faithful advisors, no man will ever lock us in chains again.
The Starks have overextended their lines. Now that summer's over, they'll have a hard time keeping their men and horses fed.
We've worked through the night, my lord. Perhaps we'd profit from some sleep.
My wife's in Lannisport.
Remember this: Whatever their price, I'll beat it.
You swear it?

Then I see no reason for hostility between us. Your soncan go on calling himself King in the North. The Starks will have dominion over all lands north of Moat Cailin, provided he swears me an oath of fealty.
The same Ned Stark swore to Robert 18 years ago. Cat, their friendship held the kingdoms together.
In the morning, I'll destroy my brother's army.
When that's done, Baratheon and Stark will fight their common enemy together, as they have done many times before.
Negotiate with Stannis? You heard him out there. I'd have better luck debating the wind.
Please bring my terms to your son. I believe we are natural allies. I hope he feels the same. Together we could end this war in a fortnight.
Well, if it isn't my favorite whoremonger. Pray I haven't kept you waiting long.
Now you do the Lannisters' bidding, is that it? Tell me, was my brother's body even cold before you secured your newest patron?
Just not a loyal one.

I don't like you, Lord Baelish. I don't like your face. I don't like the words that come oozing out of your mouth. I don't want you in my tent one minute more than necessary. So tell me, why are you here?
You can trust Brienne. Her loyalty comes without charge.
Now I understand. You know I have the numbers. You know I'm marching on King's Landing. When I take the throne, you hope to retain your position. And your head.
(snorts)
Can that truly be you?
When I saw your standard, I couldn't be sure. Whose banner is that?
I suppose if we used the same one, the battle would be terribly confusing. Why is your stag on fire?
Ah, you must be this fire priestess we hear so much about. Mmm, brother, now I understand why you found religion in your old age.
No, no, I'm relieved. I never really believed you were a fanatic. Charmless, rigid, a bore, yes, but not a godly man.

The whole realm denies it, from Dorne to the Wall. Old men deny it with their death rattle and unborn children deny it in their mother's wombs. No one wants you for their king. You never wanted any friends, brother. But a man without friends is a man without power.
Look across those fields, brother. Can you see all those banners?
No. The men holding those bolts of cloth will make me king.
Would you believe I loved him once?
Well fought. Approach.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Rise. Remove your helm.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You are all your father promised and more, my lady. I've seen Ser Loras bested once or twice, but never quite in that fashion.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: That he did, my queen. But there can only be one champion. Brienne of Tarth, you may ask anything of me you desire. If it is within my power, it is yours.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: What?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Done. Rise, Brienne of the Kingsguard.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Lady Catelyn. I'm pleased to see you. May I present my wife, Margaery of House Tyrell?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: My lady, I swear to you I will see the Lannisters answer for your husband's murder. When I take King's Landing, I'll bring you Joffrey's head.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: There's no need for that. Lady Stark is an honored guest.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Don't worry, my lady. Our war is just beginning.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Gerard. How's your foot?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Good man.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I have 100,000 men at my command. All the might of the Stormlands and the Reach.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Why?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Brienne, escort Lady Catelyn to her tent. She's tired from her journey.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: That won't be necessary. I would pray awhile. Alone.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: That must hurt.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: What? What is it?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Brienne is a very capable warrior. And she's devoted to me. You're jealous.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I'll make it up to you.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: And Margaery's a virgin?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I should warn you, I've had quite a bit of wine.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You look very beautiful.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: It's a lovely gown.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You certainly don't need it.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Although, some say the beauty most desired is the beauty concealed.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Must be the wine.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I'm sorry.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: What?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I don't know what you're talking about.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: (Sighs.)
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Ned! It's Robert.
Lord Stark, a moment.
He named you Protector of the Realm.He did.
She won't care.
Strike! Tonight while the castle sleeps.
Saving the Seven Kingdoms from Cersei and delivering them to Stannis?
This isn't about the bloody line of succession.
Yes, he's a good soldier. Everyone knows that. So was Robert.
A simpler time.
Sounds exhilarating.
Once, I think.
Those were some lucky girls.

What days were those? The ones where half of Westeros fought the other half and millions died? Or before that, when the Mad King slaughtered women and babies because the voices in his head told him they deserved it? Or way before that when dragons burned whole cities to the ground?
I suppose it was all rather heroic. If you were drunk enough and had some poor Riverlands whore to shove your price inside and “make the eight.”
I'll take that bet.
Or you could even buy a friend.
Such a shame, Littlefinger. It would have been so nice for you to have a friend.
What are you two conspiring about? Well, whatever it is, you'd best hurry up. My brother is coming.
We should have had them both killed years ago.
Robert will rant for a few days, but he won't do anything. He adores the man.
Are you sure this won't hurt?
And you prefer me like this?
If you want hairless, maybe you should find a little boy.

My brother thinks that anyone who hasn't been to war isn't a man. He treats me as if I'm a spoiled child. Oh, and you're not? Loras Tyrell, the Knight of the Flowers? How many wars have you fought in? Oh, and how much did your father spend on that armor of yours?
All I ever hear from Robert and Stannis is how I'm not tough enough, how I squirm at the sight of blood.
His eye was dangling out of the damn socket!
Easy for you to say. Not everyone is such a gifted swordsman.
I could work at fighting all day, every day, and still never be as good as you.
Everywhere?
It needs to be done, unpleasant as it is. Robert's rather tasteless about it. Every time he talks about killing her, I swear the table rises six inches.

He does have a deep, abiding lust for her money. You have to give it to the Lannisters, they may be the most pompous, ponderous cunts the gods ever suffered to walk the world, but they do have outrageous amounts of money.
Not as much as the Lannisters.

Robert's threatening to take me hunting with him. Last time we were out there for two weeks, tramping through the trees in the rain, day after day. All so he can stick his spear into something's flesh! Oh. but Robert loves his killing. And he's the King.
Because he loves his killing and he used to be good at it.
Be serious.
I'm fourth in line.
Stannis?
He's still my older brother.
What are you doing?!
You cut me!
If you can't keep the King's peace, perhaps the City Watch should be commanded by someone who can.
And you look tired from the road. I told them this meeting could wait another day, but...
Winter may be coming, but I'm afraid the same cannot be said for my brother.
My brother instructs us to stage a tournament in honor of Lord Stark's appointment as Hand of the King.
"Counting coppers," he calls it.
I'm the rightful king.
Ooh, careful.
I want you to be my prince. Stannis!
RENLY, CATELYN, LORAS and BRIENNE facing STANNIS, DAVOS and MELISANDRE
Bulls are bigger than lions. Doesn't mean I'd pick a bull in a fight.
If he ever gets out.
Loras Tyrell. He's prettier than the Queen.
How good could he be? He's been stabbing Renly Baratheon for years, and Renly ain't dead.
The horses seem a little spooked to you? They're horses.
Shh. Do you hear that?
There's something out there.
I swear you pissed yourself. "Oh, who goes there? Ahh!"
Yeah, don't even try me.
Do you think I'm an idiot?
Father, Smith, Warrior, Mother, Maiden, Crone, Stranger. I am hers and she is mine from this day until the end of my days.
We ride for Vaes Dothrak.
Kash qoy qoyi thira disse. Me qorasokh anni!
This monster killed my son.
But if you have to fight, win.
Remember that you are a Stark. Comport yourself with dignity at the Vale and try to stay out of fights.
l'll have peace on those terms. They can keep their Red Castle and their iron chair too. (bows to Robb and extends him his sword) The King in the North!

I lost one son fighting by your son's side. I lost another to the Kingslayer, strangled with a chain. You commit treason because your children are prisoners? I would carve out my heart and offer it to the Father, if he would let my sons wake from their graves and step into a prison cell.
I don't want your grief. I want my vengeance. And you stole it from me.
Ser Alton's pen is occupied, Your Grace. The prisoners from the Yellow Fork.
Does he need to lie down?
Torrhen.
Get back! I want his head! Any man who stands between a father and his vengeance asks for death.
I will have his head. And if you try and stop me
And where is our king now?
He has gone to the Crag to accept the surrender. Aye, gone to the Crag, but not to negotiate. He brought that foreign bitch with him.
Treason? How can it be treason to kill Lannisters?
When your son returns, I will demand this murderer's head.
Fathers who love their sons do. I will have his head.
Not murder, Your Grace. Vengeance.
Was strangled by the Kingslayer. They were his kin.
Tell your mother to look at them. She killed them as much as I.
It's treason to free your enemies. In war, you kill your enemies. Did your father not teach you that, boy?
Aye. Leave me to the king. He wants to give me a scolding before he sets me free. That's how he deals with treason. Our King in the North. Or should I call him the King Who Lost the North?
I don't want it to save me. I want it to haunt you to the end of your days.
Kill me and be cursed. You are no king of mine.
We're at war. This march is a distraction.
200 Northmen slaughtered like sheep.
Will it? They rot in the ground while their killer runs free.
We'll make you better.
I feel just fine.
(cries)
Old Nan said they turn your skull into a cup and make you drink your own blood from it.
That’s what Old Nan said.
What’s That?
Where?
[Surprised] Me? I’m coming with you.
I’m coming with you.  I’m your brother.  I have to protect you.
We’re not Southerners.
And I don’t want to leave you.
[Crying, looks at OSHA]  I know how to swing a sword.
I'm fine.
Jack and Billy. They'll give us some food.
Do something!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: What's happening?
What's wrong with him?
Here, Shaggydog.
He doesn't like chains.
No, l came to see Father.
He was down here. l saw him.
Last night, when l was sleeping. Here, Shaggydog.
They've all gone away.
No, they won't.
Your Grace, milady. The Kingslayer, he escaped in the night.
Halt!
Come with me, now.
The dead don’t rest.
The Three-Eyed Raven sent for me.
Now he lives again.
Halt!
Who are you?
You're from King's Landing.
Why are you here?
There are no sharks in Dorne.
Throw your swords in the sand.
Swords in the sand! Now!
When I last saw you, you were a boy. A fearless boy. Loved to climb the castle walls, frighten his mother.
Billy climbs a tree better than anyone.
Aye.
I know that.
I love her.
It is important to me.
And that is what would be in store for me with one of Walder Frey's daughters,  what you and father had?
Now you're arguing just to argue because you arranged it.
My father is dead. And the only parent I have left has no right to call anyone reckless.
Father, smith, warrior, mother ,maiden, crone, stranger.
I am hers and she is mine from this day until the end of my days.
No.
If we take Tywin's castle from him, the lords of Westeros will realize he's not invincible.  Take his home, take his gold, take his power.
You begged me not to send Theon to negotiate with his father and I ignored your advice.
Now Winterfell is burned to the ground.  The North is overrun with Ironborn and Bran and Rickon are gone.
So, Casterly Rock?  I'm asking your advice.
If Walder Frey cooperates.
We'll lose the war and die the way father died. Or worse.
We thank you for your hospitality, my lord.
I have come to make my apologies, my lord, and to beg your forgiveness.

My ladies.  All men should keep their word, kings most of all.  I was pledged to marry one of you and I broke that vow.  The fault is not with you.  Any man would be lucky to have any one of you.  I did what I did not to slight you, but because I loved another.  I know these words cannot set right the wrong I have done to you and your house.  I beg your forgiveness and pledge to do all I can to make amends so the Freys of the Crossing and the Starks of Winterfell may once again be friends.
Thank you, my lord.
My mother’s alone with Roose Bolton.  I should rescue her.
Be kind.  She’s finally starting to like you.
Perhaps I’ve made a terrible mistake.
Striking your king is an act of treason.
[Gets up from his seat and addresses WALDER] If you think the time is right, Lord Walder, by all means, let us bed them.
I suppose it does seem strange from a foreigner’s perspective.
It’s tradition. [TALISA stares at ROBB]  Without the bedding ceremony, there’s no real proof the lord and lady consummated their marriage.
Boy or girl?
Oh, do you?   It seems to me the father should have some say in his son’s naming.
I do.
Mother.
You betrayed me.
I've never met her.
Frey, I suppose. I don't know her first name.
An important bridge.
It was before they killed my father. I still thought I could march south and rescue him in time, but only if I crossed that bridge.
He was the best man I ever met. I know children always think that about their fathers, but

He once told me that being a lord is like being a father, except you have thousands of children and you worry about all of them. The farmers plowing the fields are yours to protect. The charwomen scrubbing the floors, yours to protect. The soldiers you order into battle. He told me he woke with fear in the morning and went to bed with fear in the night. I didn't believe him. I asked him, "How can a man be brave if he's afraid?" "That is the only time a man can be brave," he told me.
He would have liked you.
He didn't care much about gold or glory.
Because we'll never be safe until the Lannisters are defeated. And because I believe in justice.
That would be a start.
How? How?
Tell me this isn't true.
Why?
No! You knew I would not allow it and you did it anyway.
Jaime Lannister has played you for a fool. You've weakened our position. You've brought discord into our camp. And you did it all behind my back. Make sure she's guarded day and night.
Send another 40 with our fastest horses.
Still no word?
Theon has my brothers. If we storm the castle
Send word to your son. Any ironborn who surrender will be allowed to return safely to their homes.
Any ironborn with the exception of Theon Greyjoy. He betrayed our cause. He betrayed me. And we will hunt him down no matter where he runs.

How am I? I've had to arrest my mother. The Lannisters have my sisters. The man I considered my closest friend has seized my home and my brothers. I'm fighting a war and I don't know if I should march south or north.
No. Forgive me. You're being kind. I have no right.
That's not the kind of king I want to be.
I don't know. The good kind. Most kings grew up as princes. They spend their whole lives preparing for the crown. I was raised to be Lord of Winterfell.
I'd like to hear you play the harp.
How did you go from reciting Valyrian poetry to sawing off men's feet?
I don't want to marry the Frey girl.
But you needed that bridge. I hope it's a very beautiful bridge.
And what did she say?
And what then?
If every man were held accountable for the actions of every distant relative, Ser Alton, we'd all hang.
You've acted with honor. I thank you for it. Lord Karstark, see that Ser Alton's pen is clean, and give him a hot supper.
Is there room for Ser Alton?
Have the men build him a new pen. Put him in with the Kingslayer for now. Have your boy watch over them.
That will be all.
And my enemies, as some of my bannermen are fond of mentioning.
That's what I tell my bannermen.
If you need help finding these
Continue, my lady.
Yes.
I expect he will.
Come with me to the Crag.
Let the maester show you his stores. I want the wounded men to be treated well, all the wounded men.
You know very well.
Frey will wait. He knows we're coming.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Edmure is the best match a Frey has had in the history of their house. We should all get some sleep.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: If you don't put some clothes on, I can't promise I won't attack you again.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Who are you writing?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Is that Valyrian?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Gaaa? (sighs) Say hello for me.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Ritsas.'
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Does she know her daughter's a queen?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: That will be a surprise, I expect.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: How am I supposed to sit here planning a war when you're over there looking like that?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I will.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: What, now?  Are you certain?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Angry? You're my queen.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Maybe one of each.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I love you. Do you hear me?  I love you.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Quent.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Lady Talisa.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I'm not sure I'm a lady.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: It's hard to keep all the rules straight. But if I remember my lessons, a woman of noble birth is always called a lady. Unless she's a queen or a princess. I could find someone who knows.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Because it's obvious.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I'd call you a liar.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: (nods)
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Better at amputations, I'm afraid.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Quite a pretty spot.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I couldn't really discuss troop movements with you.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Of course, a spy would deny being a spy.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Mother. Mother, this is Lady Talisa. She's been helping with the wounded. She's been very helpful.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I've missed you.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You surprised me, that's all. I didn't think I'd see you today.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I know.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I know.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I haven't forgotten.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: This cannot be true.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Why? Why would Theon
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: My brothers?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I must go north at once.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: How can I call myself king if I can't hold my own castle? How can I ask men to follow me if I can't
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: There will be no talk. He will die for this.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Tell your son Bran and Rickon's safety is paramount. And Theon, I want him brought to me alive. I want to look him in the eye and ask him why. And then I'll take his head myself.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Thank you for riding here so quickly. I know travel isn't easy in these times.
Of course. He deserves as much. I was in the wrong.  Black WALDER: As restitution for this betrayal, he demands Harrenhal and all its attendant lands.
We are fighting for the North. Harrenhal is not in the North. It is his once the war is over and we have no further strategic need for it.
We will do whatever we can to give Lord Frey what he needs.
He has every right to be. Please excuse us while we discuss it.
He's a proud man and we've wounded him.
It's all right. You heard him. If you refuse, our alliance with the Freys is dead.
I have a war to fight. We can't win it without them. I have no time to haggle. You said you wanted to make amends for the Stone Mill.
I've won every battle, but I'm losing this war. If we don't do this and do it now, we're lost.
You're paying for my sins, Uncle. It's not fair or right. I'll remember it.
Bring them in.
Is that all of them? It took five of you to murder two unarmed squires?
Vengeance? Those boys didn't kill your sons. I saw Harrion die on the battlefield and Torrhen--
They were boys! Look at them.
My mother had nothing to do with this. This was your treason.
Leave him.
Escort Lord Karstark to the dungeon. Hang the rest.
This one was only the watcher. Hang him last so he can watch the others die.
Would you make me a liar as well as a murderer?
I'm not fighting for justice if I don't serve justice to murderers in my ranks, no matter how highborn. He has to die.
You tended to their wounds. You brought them supper. Now they're dead.
That didn't stop you from betraying me. And it won't save you now.
Kneel, my lord.
Rickard Karstark, Lord of Karhold, here in sight of gods and men, I sentence you to die. Would you speak a final word?
You were right.
The Karstarks are gone. Almost half our forces. Tywin Lannister knows what he needs to do to make us unravel. Nothing. Only wait.
What can I do? Attack King's Landing? There's nothing he'd like better. He'd crush us in a day.

Winter could last five years. Once my bannermen are home again, sitting by the fire, surrounded by their families, warm and safe, they'll never ride south again. When I gathered my lords together, we had a purpose, a mission. Now we're like a band of bickering children.
What?
Here. And we're here. King's Landing--
I can't force them to meet us in the field and I can't attack them where they're strongest, but I can attack them where they're not.
And Casterly Rock can't run away. I'm going to take their home away from them.
We're not executing prisoners, Lord Bolton.
I doubt it.
My father outlawed flaying in the North.
We're not torturing them.
The Lannisters hold prisoners of their own. I won't give them an excuse to abuse my sisters.
You'll die if she doesn't.
Put this in your mouth and lie down. You don't want to watch.
Bite on it. It's better than biting your own tongue, believe me.
What's your name?
Your last name?
You know my family name. You have me at a disadvantage.
They killed my father.
The family he fights for.
I have no hatred for the lad.
You'd have us surrender, end all this bloodshed. I understand. The country would be at peace and life would be just under the righteous hand of good King Joffrey.
If the gods give me strength.
I don't know. We'll go back to Winterfell. I have no desire to sit on the Iron Throne.
I don't know.
First we have to win the war.
You never told me where you're from.
Volantis? You're far from home. The boy was lucky you were here.
It's not about glory. Your instructions were to wait for him to come to you.
What value was the mill?
Is he there now? Of course not.

I wanted to draw the Mountain into the west, into our country where we could surround him and kill him. I wanted him to chase us, which he would have done because he is a mad without a strategic thought in his head. I could have that head on a spike by now. Instead, I have a mill.
Willem and Martyn Lannister are 14 years old.
Tywin Lannister has my sisters. Have I sued for peace?
Do you think he'll sue for peace because we have his father's brother's great-grandsons?
How many men did you lose?
We need our men more than Tywin needs his!
You would have. Right here today at this gathering if you had been patient.
You know who isn't? Tywin Lannister.
I’m sending one of your cousins down to King’s Landing with my peace terms.
Men who fought my father.
I need his ships.
Did you ever think you'd marry one?
Never?
Let me guess which one is the good news.  ROOSE BOLTON: Word from Riverrun and Winterfell.
We'll travel to the funeral together. Lord Bolton will garrison here until we return.

By the time Bolton's bastard-RAMSAY got to Winterfell, the ironborn were gone. They massacred everyone and put the castle to the torch. And BRAN and RICKON have not been found. They may have escaped. Or Theon may have taken them back to the Iron Islands as hostages.
No.
My grandfather's funeral is not a distraction.  RICKARD KARSTARK: Are we riding to battle at Riverrun?
No.  RICKARD KARSTARK: Then it's a distraction.
My Uncle Edmure has his forces garrisoned there. We need his men.  RICKARD KARSTARK: Unless he's been breeding them, he don't have enough to make a difference.
Have you lost faith in our cause?   RICKARD KARSTARK:  If it's revenge, I still got faith in it.
You don't think we can win?  RICKARD KARSTARK:  May I speak my mind, Your Grace?
Have you not been speaking your mind, Lord Karstark?  RICKARD KARSTARK:  I think you lost this war the day you married her.
Relax your bow arm.
Lad, go run back and get the rest.
There are no direwolves south of the Wall.
What is it?
I hear the prince is a right royal prick.
Go on, Tommy, shave him good. He’s never met a girl he likes better than his own hair.
Uncle Benjen.
I’m good.
Time for bed.

If I left you with one of my bannermen, your father would know within a fortnight, and my bannerman would receive a raven with a message, "Release my son and you'll be rich beyond your dreams. Refuse and your house will be destroyed, root and stem.”
Oh, I trust them with my life. Just not with yours.
You insult yourself, Kingslayer. You've been defeated by a boy.

You're held captive by a boy. Perhaps you'll be killed by a boy. (Grey Wind enters the cage. Jaime looks very nervous). Stannis Baratheon sent ravens to all the high lords of Westeros. King Joffrey Baratheon is neither a true king nor a true Baratheon. (Jaime focuses back on Robb). He's your bastard son.
My father learned the truth. That's why you had him executed.
Your son killed him so the world wouldn't learn who fathered him. And you... you pushed my brother from a window because he saw you with the queen.
I'm sending one of your cousins down to King's Landing with my peace terms.
No, but he's starting to know me.
It's better than three defeats.
You're Ser Alton Lannister?

I offer your cousins peace if they meet my terms. First, your family must release my sisters. Second, my father's bones must be returned to us so he may rest beside his brother and sister in the crypts beneath Winterfell. And the remains of all those who died in his service must also be returned. Their families can honor them with proper funerals.
Third... Joffrey and the Queen Regent must renounce all claim to dominion of the North. From this time, till the end of time, we are a free and independent kingdom.
Neither Joffrey nor any of his men shall set foot in our lands again. If he disregards this command, he shall suffer the same fate as my father, only I don't need a servant to do my beheading for me.
These are my terms. If the Queen Regent and her son meet them, I'll give them peace. If not, I will litter the south with Lannister dead.
Oh, is he? You'll ride at daybreak, Ser Alton. That will be all for tonight.
You don't have to call me ‘Your Grace’ when no one's around.
I'm glad someone's gotten used to it.
Of course they are.
Men who fought my father.
I need his ships. They say he has 200.
I understand you don't trust Lord Greyjoy.
Yes. And now I'm the one rebelling against the throne. Before me, it was father. You married one rebel and mothered another.
If I trade the Kingslayer for two girls, my bannermen will string me up by my feet.
It's more complicated than that! You know it is.
You can't go to Winterfell.
I'll send Rodrik to watch over the boys, ‘cause tomorrow, you'll ride south to the Stormlands.
Because I need you to negotiate with Renly Baratheon. He's rallied an army of 100,000. You know him. You know his family.

Which of these lords do I trust more than you? If Renly sides with us, we'll outnumber them two to one. When they feel the jaws beginning to shut, they'll sue for peace. We'll get the girls back. Then we'll all go home for good.
We will all be together again soon, I promise.
(Pauses) Give Lord Renly my regards.
There won't be a siege. The Mountain can't defend a ruin.
The Lannisters have been running from us since Oxcross. I'd love a fight. The men would love a fight. I don't think we're going to get one.
Robb: A Mallister?
Find her a chamber that will serve as a cell.
She freed Jaime Lannister. The Lannisters robbed them of their sons and she robbed them of their justice.
What's your name, friend?
Put away your blade.
King in the North!
I am the rightful king.
Take that. -
I am the King in the North!
l'll kill them all. Every one of them. l will kill them all.
Renly is not the king.
That doesn't make Renly king. He's Robert's youngest brother. lf Bran can't be Lord of Winterfell before me, Renly can't be king before Stannis.
Now and always.
lt's a birthday message to his grand-niece Walda.
He's Grandfather's bannerman. We can't expect his support?
Father rots in a dungeon. How long before they take his head? We need to cross the Trident and we need to do it now.
Have my horse saddled and ready.
My father would do whatever it took to secure our crossing. Whatever it took. lf l'm going to lead this army, l can't have other men doing my bargaining for me.
Well? What did he say?
Huh.
What does he want in return?
Fine, fine. And?
She won't be happy about that. And?
l see. Did you get a look at his daughters?
And?
Can l refuse?
Then l consent.
By the time they knew what was happening, it had already happened.
He's more use to us alive than dead.
lf we do it your way, Kingslayer, you'd win. We're not doing it your way.
l sent 2,000 men to their graves today.

Aye. But the dead won't hear them. (making a speech to his bannermen) One victory does not make us conquerors. Did we free my father? Did we rescue my sisters from the queen? Did we free the North from those who want us on our knees? This war is far from over.
Treason? Sansa wrote this?
Joffrey puts my father in chains, now he wants his ass kissed?
I won't refuse. His Grace summons me to King's Landing, I'll go to King's Landing.
They've all sworn to defend my father, have they not?
I must be.
Why is that good?
Galbart Glover will lead the Van.
You are welcome to do so, Lord Umber.
My Lord father taught me it was death to bare steel against your Liege Lord.
Shh, it's all right.
South. For father.
The Lannisters have spies everywhere.
Aye, they do.
There must always be a Stark at Winterfell.
I'll send letters whenever I can, but if you don't hear from me, don't be scared.
The River Lords are falling back with Jaime Lannister at their heels.
Mother!
There was no one else.
None of them are Starks.
If you think you can send me back to Winterfell...
There was a letter. From Sansa.
No.
18,000. If I go to King's Landing and bend my knee to Joffrey...
And if I lose?
They were butchered in their sleep.
Well, that makes it simple then.
To do either we need to cross the river and the only crossing is at The Twins.
Where did you find him?
How high did you get?
My father understands mercy, when there is room for it.
Tell Lord Tywin, Winter is coming for him.
You call me boy again.
Go on.
Not too fast.
Not now.
You’re talking about war.
Only the Lord of Winterfell can call in the bannermen and raise an army.
You want me to march on Casterly Rock?!
And it’s not your duty, because it’s not your House.
Where’s Bran?
Drop the knife. Let him go and I’ll let you live.
Are you all right?
Have you lost your mind? What if you’d missed?
You don’t have the right -
What about her?
We’ll keep her alive.
Any man of the Night's Watch is welcome at Winterfell.
I'm not your boy, Lannister. I'm Lord of Winterfell while my father is away.
Why are you here?
Is this some kind of trick? Why do you want to help him?
You've done my brother a kindness. The hospitality of Winterfell is yours.
What are you telling him now?
Get your supper. I want some time with him. One time she told me the sky is blue because we live inside the eye of a blue-eyed giant named Macumber.
How do you feel? You still don't remember anything? I've seen you climb a thousand times. In the wind, in the rain... A thousand times. You never fall.
Don't ever say that.
You've said goodbye to Bran? He's not going to die. I know it.
My mother?
Good. Next time I see you, you'll be all in black.
Farewell, Snow.
I'll make the appointments. We'll talk about it first thing in the morning.
When was the last time you left this room?
He's not going to die, mother. Maester Luwin says the most dangerous time has passed.
Rickon needs you. He's six. He doesn't know what's happening. He follows me around all day, clutching my leg, crying...
Fire. You stay here. I'll come back.
They come into our home and try to murder my brother? If it's war they want...
I'll ride to King's Landing.
Mother, you can't.
What about Bran?
You’ve got fat.
Cat!
Nine years. Why haven’t I seen you? Where the hell have you been?
Who have we here? You must be Robb. (To Sansa) My, you’re a pretty one. (To Arya) Your name is?
Take me to your crypt. I want to pay my respects.
Ned.
One minute he was fine, and then … Burned right through him, whatever it was. I loved that man.
He never had to teach you much, but me … You remember me at 16? All I wanted to do was crack skulls and fuck girls. He showed me what was what.

Don’t look at me like that. Not his fault I didn’t listen. (They laugh. ROBERT sighs.) I need you, Ned. Down at Kings Landing. Not up here, where you’re no damn use to anybody. Lord Eddard Stark, I would name you the Hand of the king.

I’m not trying to honor you. I’m trying to get you to run my kingdom while I eat, drink, and whore my way to an early grave. Damn it, Ned, stand up. You helped me win the Iron Throne, now help me keep the damn thing. We were meant to rule together. If your sister had lived, we would have been bound by blood. Well, it’s not too late. I have a son, you have a daughter. We’ll join our Houses.
Did you have to bury her in a place like this? She should be on a hill somewhere with the sun and the clouds above her.
She belonged with me.
In my dreams, I kill him every night.
Not all of them.
Are you as good with a spear as you used to be?
I know what I’m putting you through. Thank you for saying yes. I only ask you because I need you. You’re a loyal friend. You hear me? A loyal friend. The last one I’ve got.
You will. And I’ll make sure you don’t look so fucking grim all the time. Come on, boys, let’s go kill some boar!
I should have spent more time with you, shown you how to be a man.
My fault. Too much wine, missed my thrust.
Out, all of you!
Paper and ink on the table, write down what I say.
Give it to the council after I'm dead.
And my son Help him, Ned. Make him better than me.
My memory.
Who’d have thought she had it in her?
Oh, will both of you shut your mouths? Catelyn will release Tyrion and you’ll make your peace with Jaime.
Quiet, woman.
Hold your tongue.
Wear it in silence or I’ll honor you again.
See what she does to me? My loving wife. I should not have hit her. That was not… That was not kingly.
So tell your wife to return that little shit of an Imp to King’s Landing. She’s had her fun, now put an end to it. You hear me? Send a raven and put an end to it.

Piss on that! Send a raven. I want you to stay. I’m the king, I get what I want… I never loved my brothers. A sad thing for a man to admit, but it’s true. You were the brother I chose. We’ll talk when I return from the hunt.
Killing things clears my head. You’ll have to sit on the throne while I’m away. You’ll hate it more than I do.

Seven hells! Don’t start with her again! The girl will die and I’ll hear no more of it. Put on the badge. And if you ever take it off again, I swear to the Mother I’ll pin the damn thing on Jaime Lannister.
Oh, what was I saying?
It was! It was. You’re too young to remember. Wasn’t it, Selmy?
The enemy was right there in the open, vicious as you like, all but sending you a bloody invitation. Nothing like today.
Exhilarating, yes. Not as exhilarating as those balls and masquerades you like to throw. (laughs) You ever fucked a Riverlands girl?
You think? I think you’d remember. Back in our day, you weren’t a real man until you fucked a girl from each of the Seven Kingdoms and the Riverlands. We called it “making the eight.”
You ever make the eight, Barristan?
(laughs) Those were the days!
Easy, boy. You might be my brother, but you’re speaking to the King.
Your mother was a dumb whore with a fat arse. Did you know that? Look at this idiot! One ball and no brains. He can't even put a man's armor on him properly.
Fat? Fat, is it? Is that how you speak to your King? That was funny, is it?
Oh, it’s funny, is it?
No? You don't like the Hand's joke?
You heard the Hand. The King's too fat for his armor! Go find the breastplate stretcher, now!
How long before he figures it out?
All right, all right. But you watch me out there. I still know how to point a lance.
Why? Because I'm king? Piss on that. I want to hit somebody!
Anybody who can. And the last man in his saddle... will be you!
Are you telling me those cowards would let me win?
… Drink.
Drink. Your King commands it. Gods! Too fat for my armor.

Hmm... A bloody idiot... But Cersei insisted. I have Jon Arryn to thank for her. "Cersei Lannister will make a good match", he told me. "You'll need her father on your side." I thought being King meant I could do whatever I wanted. Enough of this! Let's go watch 'em ride. At least I can smell someone else's blood.
What? Oh! An inspiring sight for the people, eh? Come! Bow before your King! Bow, you shits!
Stop this madness in the name of your King!
The whore is pregnant.

I warned you this would happen. Back in the North, I warned you, but you didn't care to hear. Well, hear it now. I want 'em dead, mother and child both. And that fool, Viserys as well. Is that plain enough for you? I want them both dead.
Honor?! I've got Seven Kingdoms to rule! One King, Seven Kingdoms. Do you think honor keeps them in line? Do you think it's honor that's keeping the peace? It's fear! Fear and blood!
Careful, Ned. Careful now.
And if he's right? If she has a son? A Targaryen at the head of a Dothraki army… What then?
Do nothing? That's your wise advice? Do nothing til our enemies are on our shores? You're my council? Counsel! Speak sense to this honorable fool.
She dies.
You're the King's Hand, Lord Stark. You'll do as I command or I'll find me a Hand who will.

Out! Out, damn you! I'm done with you. Go! Run back to Winterfell! I'll have your head on a spike! I'll put it there myself, you fool! You think you're too good for this? Too proud and honorable? This is a war!
I'm glad I could do something to make you happy.
I suppose this is where you tell me to give the job to your brother Jaime.
I don't know. But I do know this, if the Targaryen girl convinces her horse-lord husband to invade and the Dothraki horde crosses The Narrow Sea... We won't be able to stop them.
It's a neat little trick you do, you move your lips and your father's voice comes out.

Let's say Viserys Targaryen lands with forty thousand Dothraki screamers at his back. We hole up in our castles, a wise move. Only a fool would meet the Dothraki in an open field. They leave us in our castles. They go from town to town, looting and burning, killing every man who can't hide behind a stone wall, stealing all our crops and livestock, enslaving all our women and children. How long do the people of the Seven Kingdoms stand behind their absentee King, their cowardly King hiding behind high walls? When do the people decide that Viserys Targaryen is the rightful monarch after all.

Five... one. One army, a real army, united behind one leader with one purpose. Our purpose died with the Mad King. Now we've got as many armies as there are men with gold in their purse. And everybody wants something different. Your father wants to own the world. Ned Stark wants to run away and bury his head in the snow.

We haven't had a real fight in nine years. Back-stabbing doesn't prepare you for a fight. And that's all the realm is now, back-stabbing and scheming and arse-licking and money-grubbing. Sometimes I don't know what holds it together.
Ah, so here we sit, seventeen years later, holding it all together. Don't you get tired?
How long can hate hold a thing together?
Yes, it is.
You've never asked about her, not once. Why not?
So why now?

You want to know the horrible truth? I can't even remember what she looked like. I only know she was the one thing I ever wanted... Someone took her away from me, and Seven Kingdoms couldn't fill the hole she left behind.
I know.
No. Does that make you feel better or worse?
