I'll bet you smell of blackberry jam! Let me smell it. Come here.
I've been sitting here for days! Start the damn joust before I piss myself!
Yes, yes. Enough of the bloody pomp. Have at it!
It's been a long time. But I still remember every face. You remember your first?
Who was it?
How'd you do it?

Quick one. Lucky for you. Mine was some Tarly boy at the Battle of Summerhall. My horse took an arrow so I was on foot, slogging through the mud. He came running at me, this dumb high-born lad, thinking he could end the rebellion with the single swing of his sword. I knocked him down with the hammer. Gods, I was strong then. Caved in his breastplate. Probably shattered every rib he had. Stood over him, hammer in the air. Right before I brought it down he shouted, "Wait! Wait!"... They never tell you how they all shit themselves. They don't put that part in the songs. Stupid boy. Now the Tarlys bend the knee like everyone else. He could have lingered on the edge of the battle with the smart boys and today his wife would be making him miserable, his sons would be ingrates, and he'd be waking three times in the night to piss into a bowl. Wine! Lancel. Gods, what a stupid name. Lancel Lannister. Who named you? Some halfwit with a stutter? What are you doing?
What do you mean it's empty?

Is that what empty means?! So get more. Tell your cousin to get in here. Kingslayer! Get in here. Surrounded by Lannisters. Every time I close my eyes I see their blond hair and their smug, satisfied faces. It must wound your pride, huh? Standing out there like a glorified sentry. Jaime Lannister, son of the mighty Tywin... Forced to mind the door while your King eats and drinks and shits and fucks. So come on. We're telling war stories. Who was your first kill, not counting old men?
Your outlaw... Any last words?
What about Aerys Targaryen? What did the Mad King say when you stabbed him in the back? I never asked. Did he call you a traitor? Did he plead for a reprieve?
Gods, this is country! I've half a mind to leave them all behind and keep going.
What do you say, just you and me on the Kingsroad, swords at our sides, a couple of tavern wenches to warm our beds tonight.
There were wars to fight, women to marry... We never had the chance to be young.
There was that one... Oh, what was her name? That common girl of yours? Becca? With the great big tits you could bury your face in.
Bessie! Thank the gods for Bessie and her tits. Yours was... Aleena? No. You told me once. Meryl? Your bastard's mother?
That's it. She must have been a rare wench to make Lord Eddard Stark forget his honor. You never told me what she looked like.
We were at war. None of us knew if we were gonna go back home again. You're too hard on yourself. You always have been. I swear if I weren't your King, you'd have hit me already.
Trust me, that's not the worst thing. There was a rider in the night.
A knife perhaps, a good sharp one, and a bold man to wield it.
Soon enough that child will spread her legs and start breeding.
Oh, it's unspeakable to you? What her father did to your family... That was unspeakable. What Rhaegar Targaryen did to your sister... the woman I loved. I'll kill every Targaryen I get my hands on.
This Khal Drogo, it's said he has 100,000 men in his horde.
There are still those in the Seven Kingdoms who call me Usurper. If the Targaryen boy crosses with a Dothraki horde at his back, the scum will join him.
There's a war coming, Ned. I don't know when, I don't know who we'll be fighting, but it's coming.
Quiet, woman. Sorry, Ned. I never meant to frighten the girl. But we need to get this business done quickly.
Enough! He tells me one thing, she tells me another. Seven hells! What am I to make of this? Where's your other daughter, Ned?
Now, child... Tell me what happened. Tell it all and tell it true. It's a great crime to lie to a King.
What would you have me do, whip her through the streets? Damn it, children fight. It's over.
You let that little girl disarm you? See to it that your daughter is disciplined. I'll do the same with my son.
I'd forgot the damned wolf.
So be it.
As you will.
A direwolf's no pet. Get her a dog. She'll be happier for it.
The answer is no.
I’ve heard enough. We’ve only just taken back this castle from the Ironborn. The Boltons helped us do it. Now you want me to fight against them? I could be skinned for even talking to you.
Have other northern houses pledged to fight for you?
And?
I don’t care about ravens. You’re asking me to join your army. Who is fighting in this army?

Then the rumors are true. I didn’t dare believe them. I received you out of respect for your father. Now I would like you to leave. House Glover will not abandon its ancestral home to fight alongside wildlings.
There’s nothing else to say.
Yes, my family served House Stark for centuries. We wept when we heard of your father’s death. When my brother was lord of this castle, he answered Robb’s call and hailed him King in the North.

And where was King Robb when the Ironborn attacked this castle? When they threw my wife and children in prison and brutalized and killed our subjects? Taking up with a foreign whore. Getting himself and those who followed him killed.
I served House Stark once, but House Stark is dead.
The King in the North should stay in the north.  We did not choose you to rule us, My Lady, but perhaps we should have.
It's about time we taught these boys of summer how to fight.
You expect me to put a spear in my granddaughter's hand?
Indeed you are, my lady. No one has questioned--
We called your brother king, and then he rode south and lost his kingdom.
I've never left home before, Uncle Petyr. I'm afraid to leave.
The Lord of the Vale belongs in the Eyrie, Mother said. She said it's not safe outside.
What are you doing?
Why did you leave?
I stay here in the Eyrie. Mother says it's dangerous on the roads and I have to keep myself safe because I'm the Lord of the Vale and the Lord of the Vale is a very important person.
When will you go back?
Oh. Does Winterfell have a Moon Door?
That sounds dangerous. How do you make people fly?
What do you do with all the bad people and the scary people and the people you don't like?

Well, I'm Lord of the Vale. When I grow up, I'll be able to fly anybody who bothers me. Or you. When we get married, you can tell me if you don't like somebody and then we can bring them back here and, whoosh! Right through the Moon Door.
Let's put a Moon Door in your Winterfell.
It can go in here in this big tower.
I didn't ruin it.
It was already ruined because it didn't have a Moon Door. I was fixing it.
I didn't ruin it!
I didn't ruin it!
Aye.
Uncle Petyr!
Look what Uncle Petyr brought me.
Mummy said they killed your mother and they chopped off your brother's head.
They killed my father, too, with poison. I wanted to make the little Lannister baby man fly, but Mother said I couldn't.
Through the Moon Door.
(GIGGLES)
Uncle Petyr!
A falcon!
Shall we throw him through the Moon Door?
Do you believe him, Uncle Petyr?
Fine.
She’s my cousin. We should help her.
I'm hungry.
But I'm hungry!
What happened next?!
Make the bad man fly!
Is it over?
Can I make the little man fly now?
Mommy? Is that the bad man?
He's little.
You can't hurt us! No one can hurt us here! Tell him, mommy! Tell him!
Mommy... I want to see the bad man fly.
Boy! Bread, meat and beer, quickly.
I'd rather throw myself down a well.
Fewer eyes back here, my Lady. But still too many.
My Lady.
Did you notice the dagger the killer used? It's too fine a weapon for such a man. The blade is Valyrian steel, the handle dragonbone. Someone gave it to him.
I'll send Hal with a squad of guardsmen to escort you.
Let me accompany you at least. The Kingsroad can be a dangerous place for a woman alone.
It grieves me you've less honor than a back alley whore. You were raised here under this roof. These people are your people.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: King Robb thought of you as a brother.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Aye, they died fighting a war your father started. Lord Stark raised you among his own sons.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: If he were alive to see this
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I should have put a sword in your belly instead of in your hand.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: He who passes the sentence should swing the sword.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Coward.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Hush now, child. I'm off to see your father.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Gods help you, Theon Greyjoy. Now you are truly lost.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Bran! Lord Stark.  Torrhen's Square is under siege.
Might be a raiding party led by the mountain. Might be sellswords paid by Tywin Lannister.
Most of the fighting men are away with Robb, but I can gather 200 decent men.
Won't take long, my lord. Southerners don't do well up here.
The scouts report Lord Tywin moves north.
We should go, my lady.
My lady!
Summer snows, My Lady.
Jon.
Should I gag him?
My Lady, this way.
I'll be fine, my Lady. There's no need to bloody yourself.
Lord Stark. My lady. A guardsman just rode in from the hills. They’ve captured a deserter from the Night’s Watch.
The law is law, my lady.
They don’t belong down here.
Never do, my lady.  Dulls the senses.
Aye.  Lord Walder let me choose any of his granddaughters and promised me the girl’s weight in silver as a dowry.  So I have a fat, young bride.
Well, she’s made me very rich.
Every bride suffers the same.  I’m sure you endured yours with grace.
The Lannisters send their regards.
We've sent a dozen ravens. None have returned. My bastard is only a few days from Winterfell. Once he captures the castle
He wouldn't dare hurt the boys. They're his only hope of escaping the North with his head.
A touch of mercy is a virtue, Your Grace. Too much
I expect his countrymen will turn on him the minute they hear the offer.
My lady.

Not nothing. The North. Ride 700 miles that way, you're still in the North. 400 miles that way. 300 miles that way. The North is larger than the other six kingdoms combined. And I am the Warden of the North. The North is mine. Now tell me, what is your name?
No, not Ramsay Snow. *hands Ramsay a scroll* Open it. From this day until your last day, you are Ramsay Bolton, son of Roose Bolton, Warden of the North.

And how high can Stannis’ horses jump? Our walls have been full repaired our walls have been reinforced. We have enough food for six months. We are more prepared for a siege than they could have ever been. All we have to do is wait for them to freeze, starve, and mutiny.
You disagree?
And what do you recommend?

A smart commander does not surrender a defensive advantage. As long as we stay behind these walls they can’t touch us. Not to mention that the snow is so deep that we couldn’t get an army through if we wanted to.
Too many prisoners.
Your Grace. My lady. News from Winterfell.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: We've had ravens from White Harbor, Barrowton and the Dreadfort. I'm afraid it is true.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Because the Greyjoys are treasonous whores.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: We've heard nothing of them. But Rodrik Cassel is dead.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: There's still a war to win, Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You are a king. And that means you don't have to do everything yourself.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Theon holds the castle with a skeleton crew. Let me send word to my bastard at the Dreadfort. He can raise a few hundred men and retake Winterfell before the new moon. We have the Lannisters on the run. If you march all the way back north now, you'll lose what you gained. My boy would be honored to bring you Prince Theon's head.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Who comes before the Old Gods this night?
Lady Sansa, will you take this man?
Ser Jaime isn't well. Take him to Qyburn.
I trust you find your chamber suitable, my lady.
(Raises his cup). To your wedding.
Yes, yes, very good.
Walda and I have some good news as well, since we're all together.
From the way she's carrying, Maester Wolkan says it looks like a boy.
Sure of what?
Maester Wolkan has assured us beyond all doubt.
Manage what?
I imagine you're familiar with the procedure.
You disgraced yourself at dinner, parading that creature before the Stark girl.
You're worried about your position.
You've never asked me about your mother.

She was a peasant girl. Pretty in a common sort of way. She was the miller's wife. Apparently they had married without my knowledge or consent. So I had him hanged and I took her beneath the tree where he was swaying. She fought me the whole time. She was lucky I didn't hang her, too. A year later she came to my gates with a squalling baby in her arms. A baby she claimed was mine. I nearly had her whipped and the child thrown in the river. (Stands up). But then I looked at you and I saw then what I see now. You are my son. (After a moment). Stannis Baratheon has an army at Castle Black. But he won't stay for long. He wants the Iron Throne, and the road to King's Landing comes right through Winterfell. He means to take the North. But the North is ours. It's yours and mine. Will you help me defeat him?
Five Lannisters dead for every one of ours. They're dead. Take everything they've got. We've nowhere to keep all these prisoners. Barely enough food to feed our own.
Of course, Your Grace. The officers will be useful. Some of them may be privy to Tywin Lannister's plans.
Well, we'll learn soon enough. In my family, we say, "A naked man has few secrets. "A flayed man none."
We're not in the North.
The high road's very pretty, but you'll have a hard time marching your army down it.
Surely one of our men needs your attention more than this cub.
We can't hold the North with terror alone.
I sent you there to collect taxes, not bodies.
He left you no choice.
And?
(After a moment). I have something important to tell you. Stop eating and listen.
We don't have enough men to hold the North if the other Houses rise up against us. Do you understand that?

I had a pact with Tywin Lannister, and Tywin Lannister is dead. The remaining Lannisters are a thousand miles away dealing with that fact. They've never once in the history of the Seven Kingdoms sent their army this far north. If you think they will for us, you're a fool. We become a great house by entering in alliances with other houses, and parleying those alliances into greater power. The best way to forge a lasting alliance isn't by peeling a man's skin off. The best way is marriage. (Stands up). Now that you're a Bolton by royal decree, it's time you married a suitable bride. And as it happens, I found the perfect girl to solidify our hold in the North.
Lady Sansa, welcome.
May I introduce my son, (Stands aside), Ramsay Bolton?
(Approaching). And you're not anymore. Allow me a moment alone with Lord Baelish.
Shouldn't he be?
I leave that to the brothel keeper. It's her name I need, not her virtue.
And you prepared for the consequences. When the Lannisters hear I've wed Sansa Stark to Ramsay.
The Queen will be enraged.

And yet she still has friends. Men in important places, whom she can ask for favours. (They stop, and Roose holds up a letter). A message for you, from Cersei Lannister. A rider arrived from Eyrie, shortly before dawn. Apparently, she thinks you're still in the Vale.

I'm sure you understand my position, Lord Baelish. If you received word in the night from the Queen Mother, it does make me question our new alliance. The Lannisters made you one of the great lords of Westeros. Yet, here you are in the North, undermining them. Why gamble with your position?
I had Tywin Lannister backing. Who supports me now? You?
I'd like to read the reply.
Walda, this is Ramsay Snow, my bastard.
See that the horses are fed, watered, and rubbed down. And take Lady Walda to her chambers.
Where is your prize?
What did you do to him?
You flayed him.
This was Balon Greyjoy's son and heir.

My banners, not yours. You're not a Bolton. You're a Snow. Tywin Lannister has given me the North. But he won't lift a finger to help me take it. As long as the ironborn hold Moat Cailin, our armies are trapped south of the Neck. Theon was a valuable hostage, not your plaything. I wanted to trade him for Moat Cailin.
You sent terms to Balon Greyjoy without my consent?
I had to smuggle myself into my own lands thanks to the Greyjoys. I needed Theon. I needed him whole.
I placed far too much trust in you.
You murdered them and displayed their corpses at Winterfell.
You ready for a hunt?
Their bastard brother. He could be sheltering them. He may know where they are.

You want to prove yourself a Bolton? Gather whatever men you can and ride for Moat Cailin. Bring this creature of yours. Maybe he'll be of some use. Take the Moat for the family, for our family, and I'll reconsider your position.
Half a dozen men? Your best hunters?
I didn’t think lady Sansa killed them all by herself. Thank you for this report, Lord Kastark.
Jon Snow is a bastard, not a Stark.
Murder the Lord Commander of the Night’s Watch? You’d unite every house in the North against us.
If you acquire a reputation as a mad dog, you’ll be treated as a mad dog. Taken out back and slaughtered for pig feed.
You’ll always be my firstborn.
Your command of the cavalry was impressive. Thanks to you, the false king Stannis Baratheon is dead. Do you know who struck the killing blow?
A shame.  I’d reward the man.  Still, a great victory. Do you feel like a victor?

I rebelled against the crown to arrange your marriage to Sansa Stark. Do you think that burning wagons in the night and mowing down tired, outnumbered Baratheons is the same as facing a prepared and provisioned Lannister army?

A reckoning will come. We need the North to face it. The entire North. They won’t back us without Sansa Stark. We no longer have Sansa Stark. You played your games with her. You played your games with the heir to the Iron Islands and now they’re both gone.
I’m glad to hear it. Without Sansa, you won’t be able to produce an heir. And without an heir, well…
...let’s hope the maesters are right and Lady Walder’s carrying a boy.
The Blackfish escaped.
As you say.
You'll be needing a new young girl.
(grunts)
He ignored my advice at every turn. If he'd been a trifle less arrogant--
(nods)
Forever young.
At some point perhaps. But Winterfell's in ruins.
I sent my bastard Ramsay to root him out.  Robb Stark offered amnesty for the ironborn if they gave us Theon.
I see my men have finally found you something appropriate to wear.
I should send you back to Robb Stark.
Wars cost money. Many people would pay a great deal for you.
You're right. Perhaps the safest thing to do is to kill you both and burn your bodies.
King Robb is keeping your father quite busy. He doesn't have time for anything else.

As soon as you're well enough to travel, I will allow you to go to King's Landing as restitution for the mistakes my soldiers made. And you will swear to tell your father the truth, that I had nothing to do with your maiming.
I don't partake.
Oh, she won't be going with you.
You are charged with abetting treason.
You're in no place to insist on anything. I would have hoped you'd learned your lesson about overplaying your position.
We should set the siege lines 1,000 yards from Harrenhal.
I imagine the Mountain will defend whatever Tywin Lannister tells him to defend.
The debt will be repaid, my friend. For them and for your sons.
The Kingslayer won't remain free for long. My best hunters are after him.
Look, it's Yoren's little cunt. Used to be a boy, now it's a girl.
Don't care. Where's your stick now, bitch? I promised to fuck you with it.
There's a price on your head.
The king's dead. He drank poisoned wine at his own wedding. The bounty on you is for killing Lannister soldiers. 100 silver stags.
Rorge.
Give us a beer. Before I skin you.
Hey, you little shit. Get us beer!
Come closer. And I'll shove that stick up your bunghole and fuck you bloody.
Come here!
Come here!
So what's the thing?
About kings. You said, "The thing you need to understand about kings".
When you started, you… Never mind.
lt's all right. No need.
Come in. Make yourself comfortable.
That doesn't look very comfortable. Here, watch me.
You sure? Most men like what they see.
That's what most men say.
If you don't want what most men want, then you'd better tell me what you'd like me to do.
Have we met?
I meet a lot of men.
You're very kind, my lord. Allow me to return the favor.
I know who you are.
How do you know about that?
What can I do for you, Lord Varys?
I'm afraid of him.
Come here.
Yes, My Lord.
Why don't you join us, My Lord?
What she doesn't know won't hurt her.
She must be very beautiful.
I do believe my lord's in love.
Is she still married to him?
And what do you want?
Going to King’s Landing.
I’ll find a ship heading south in White Harbor.
Some of my friends are more generous than others.
I’ll have lots of company…
(laughs) And what will happen to me if I stay here? Will I become Lady Greyjoy, Mistress of the Iron Islands?

I hear Jaime Lannister attacked Lord Stark in the streets of King’s Landing. Every man for a hundred miles will be marching off to war soon and most of them will never come back, there’s nothing more for me here. Let’s go, Steffon.
See what?
I know.
I thought you were supposed to be an important person around here.
You're not the only nobleman in my life, you know.
Jealous?
Might surprise you.
He's good with his fingers too. And his tongue.
You are jealous.
And what about the Starks?
A “ward”, that’s a nice word for it. Your father rebelled against King Robert and if he does it again…
You’re a very serious boy.
Oh, yes you are. A serious boy, with a serious cock.
Then get yourself a wife.
Happy nameday, Your Grace.
We won't tell if you won't.
Hmm.
Your Grace, too much pain will spoil the pleasure.
Your Grace, if your uncle finds out
No larger than usual, apparently. And yet they said that he was--
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: The most extraordinary man they've ever had. And they've had a lot of men.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: (nods)
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I don't know, my lord. The girls are usually quite descriptive.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: They said it was hard to describe.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: He was too preoccupied to notice. He's leaving for the Eyrie soon. I'm helping him prepare for his journey.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: He hasn't spoken to her since the last time, as far as I know. But I don't think he's lost interest.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: His shipboard inventory.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Former profession.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Two featherbeds. He's bringing two featherbeds for the cabin.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: He's not interested in them, my lord.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Because I'm one of his girls.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: That's all of them, my lord.
Did you hear the king’s in Winterfell?
And the queen. And her twin brother. They say that he is the most handsome man in the Seven Kingdoms.
The queen has two brothers?
I hear they call him the Imp.
Oh? I hear he’s more than earned it. I hear he’s a drunken little lecher into all manner of perversions.
We’ve been expecting you, Lord Tyrion.
M’lord.
You're her handmaiden?
I help manage his affairs.
So is she. I grew up in the shadow of her father's castle. The day she was born, they rang the bells from sunrise till sunset. We've both done rather well, you and I.
Given where we started.
It's not easy for girls like us to dig our way out. Watch out for her.
Watch out for her with him.
Lord Edmure, I hope I’m not a disappointment to you.
The moment we can get the last infantryman out onto the field, we should shut the gates.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Do the dead frighten you?
You don’t think he’ll ask us how they died? Get back on your horse.
It’s a good thing we’re not children. You want to run away south, run away. Of course, they will behead you as a deserter … If I don’t catch you first. Get back on your horse. I won’t say it again.
Your dead men seem to have moved camp.
What is it?
Great warrior? He sings a sword like a girl with palsy.
He’s 13. Boys go to war at 13.
Does he?
I have done nothing more than my duty, my lady.
He’ll be safe here. As for his skills, I make no promises.
My lady.
I’m sorry, my lord.
No, my lord… It’s Mhaegen.
She works for you. She's the one, the girl whose, they killed her baby.
Can't stop thinking about it, I can't sleep. Her poor little baby.
No, no, no, no. Do you have any idea how ridiculous you sound? Ease into it. Slowly, with passion.
Go wash yourselves. Put some clothes on. Both of you are working tonight.
We do things differently here, Daisy. This isn't some five-copper bawdy house in Hayseed Hall.
Wherever you're from. Our establishment doesn't cater to ploughmen and goat herders. (Nods to a man walking past). Lord Olsen. Taste is everything here.
Don't call him Littlefinger, he doesn't like it. Lord Baelish.
She pretends she doesn't speak the common tongue so that people will think she's exotic. She grew up just down the road in flea bottom.
She's a dumb slut, really, but a lid for every pot. (Janos enters the room). Lord Commander. Good to see you, again.
You do realize this establishment is owned by Lord Petyr Baelish, the King's Master of Coin. Coin which goes in many pockets.
Whose orders?
Father, please –
All these kings fighting for the throne, and this Stannis has the smallest army. Why would I bet on the man with the worst chance?
And yet, they both have larger armies.
What is the world coming to when smugglers must vouch for the honor of kings.
You think I'm insulted? I am a pirate. I'm an excellent pirate. I don't sail for promises.
That's a promise that always comes true.
Only the clever ones.
Salladhor Saan is a good name for songs.
One thing, I want the queen.
Cersei, I want her. I'll sail with your fleet, all 30 of my ships, and if we don't drown at the bottom of Blackwater Bay, I will fuck this blonde queen and I will fuck her well.
I'm not going to rape her, I'm going to fuck her.
You don't know how persuasive I am. I never tried to fuck you.

I've been all over the world, my boy, and everywhere I go, people tell me about the true god. They all think they found the right one. The one true god is what's between a woman's legs, and better yet a queen's legs.
I never thought you'd have a true believer for a son.

You Westerosee are funny people. A man chops off your fingers and you fall in love with him. (They laugh). I'll sail with you, Davos Seaworth. You are the most honest smuggler I ever met. Make me rich.
I thought you were dead. Everyone thought you were dead. And your son?
He may have swam ashore as you did.
I'm so sorry, my friend. I, too, have lost a son. There is nothing worse in this world. But, Davos, you were a good father.
He licks his wounds at Dragonstone.
There is nothing for me at Dragonstone.
Not for you, maybe. But for Salladhor Saan, the war is over.
I am sworn to no man. I promised you 30 ships and you promised me riches and glory. I delivered the ships.

He's a broken man. His fleet lies at the bottom of Blackwater Bay. They say he sees no one ... not his generals, not even his wife. Only the Red Woman whispering in his ear, telling him what she sees in the flames and burning men alive.
They built a great fire when Stannis returned. All those who spoke against her she called servants of darkness. They say she sang to them as they burned.
I'm a pirate.
Servants of darkness. I'm thinking Dragonstone is a good place for us to avoid.
You cannot turn Stannis against her.
You could try. If you fail, they will burn you. If you succeed, they'll burn you. And you've only just come back to life. Stay alive a little longer, my friend.
And you drank with me on four of my wedding days, but I don't ask you for favors.
When you are dead, I will gather your bones in a little sack and let your widow wear them around her neck.

The lookout sees a pirate ship sailing their way. The captain shouts to his first mate, "Bring me my red shirt." The first mate brings the red shirt and the captain puts it on. And when the pirates try to board, the brave captain leads his men to victory. A few days later, the lookout screams, "Two pirate ships!" The crew is shivering like scared mice. But the courageous captain hollers, "Bring me my red shirt!"
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: After the battle, the first mate asks, "Captain, why do you call for your red shirt before battle?" The captain replies, "So that if I am stabbed, you will not see me bleed." The next morning the lookout screams, "10 pirate ships! We are surrounded!" The crew goes silent.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: They all look to their brave captain, waiting for his usual command. Calm as ever, the captain bellows...
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Davos. I heard you were rotting in a dungeon in Dragonstone.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Join us, my friend. Join us. This is Lhara. And this is--
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: She's an artist, this one, truly.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: We?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Once I thought this man loved me. Now I know he despises me. He wants to see me die poor and alone on some cold--
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You're not my friend, my friend.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: If you step back and think about it, the thing about Gilly that's so interesting is
No, truly. The thing about her that I find so interesting is that after all that Craster's done to her, she's still got hope that life might get better.
And the thing about you that I find so interesting is absolutely nothing.
It's Jon and the Halfhand. They're back.
Wait! Wait for me!
Grenn!
Edd!
Don't kill me!
To Castle Black. I'm a brother of the Night's Watch.
Who's your brother?
You're Jon's brother. The one who fell from the window.
I've been around Ghost enough to know a direwolf when I see one. And I've heard all about Hodor.
I'd be dead if it wasn't for Jon. If you're his brother, you're my brother, too. And anything I can do to help you, I will.
What? Why in the world would you want to go--
Come with us. There are steps carved into the south side of the Wall. Hodor can carry you to the top. We'll walk straight to Castle Black.
If Jon is alive, then Castle Black's where he'll be. It's the safest place for you.
What I know is what I saw. And if you saw it, too, you'd run the other way.
How do you know all that?
But you're going to stop them?
We found them at the Fist. Someone buried them a long time ago. Someone wanted us to find them.
Killing white walkers.
Well, I suppose someone had to be the first.
And the archer. I got lucky with one of them. There are more. Many more. And for every one of them, the dead men, more than you could count. I wish you'd come with us.
I know how this must look. What I meant was--
Maester.
Maester.
She hasn't chosen a name yet.

He's not my child, Maester Aemon. She's one of Craster's wives. I remember every word of the oath. Night gathers and my watch begins. I am the shield that guards the realms of men. The realms of men. That means her as well as us. We didn't build 500 miles of ice walls 700 feet high to keep men out. The night is gathering, Maester Aemon. I've seen it. It's coming for all of us.
Miles better.
Jon! Jon! Jon, it's us.
Hush, now. You're home. Carry him inside. Gently.
So what are you gonna do?
But… the Dragonglass?
He’s made of Valyrian steel. How many Valyrian steel swords are left in the Seven Kingdoms?
You were friends with me when I first got here. And I wasn’t winning any elections back then.
I wanted to ask you something. To ask something of you. Send me, Gilly, and the baby to Oldtown so I can become a Maester. That’s what I’m meant to be, not this.
Well, there’s Edd.

I’ll be more use to you as a Maester. More use to everyone now that Maester Aemon’s gone. The Citadel has the world’s greatest library. I’ll learn about history, strategy, healing. And other things. Things that will help when they come.

If Gilly stays here then she’ll die. And the baby that she named after me will die. And I’ll end up dying, too, trying to protect them. Which means that the last thing that I’ll see in this world will be the look in her eyes when I fail them. (pause) I’d rather see a thousand White Walkers than see that.
Thank you.
They’ll bloody try.
What?
I’ll come back.
To my return.
Hello.
From Lord Commander Snow at Castle Black.
He died.
Maester Aemon became quite ill shortly after the election. He’s since passed away. Which is why I’m here.
Yes, well, I suppose that life is irregular.
What was she like?
Oh? How big were her feet?

I want you to tell me what it was like to have someone. To be with someone. To love someone and have them love you back. We're all gonna die a lot sooner than I'd planned. You're the closest I'll ever get to know it.
No...she just had a baby! ...and she never offered.
The interesting thing is, our vows never specifically forbid intimate relations with women.

"I shall take no wife" yes, that's in there, there's no denying that. "I shall father no children" it's very specific. But what our vows have to say about other...activities...is open to interpretation.
Anyway, there's nothing for him to interpret. We didn't. So what's it like?
No, you're really not.
There'll be arrows for all of us soon I imagine.
They've already done the worst thing to me they could ever do.
Maester Aemon. How did you know-
Let me help you.
Maester Faull
But what he says about what they do...it's all true. It's exactly what the survivors of their raids report.
I don't love her.
No.
You were?
I'm sorry Maester, I didn't mean it like that.
Who was she?
Pyp! Open the gate.
Yes!
Oh, Pyp. Open the fucking gate!
Yes, well, best get used to it.
Are you alright? Shh, you're alright. Yeah, of course you are my brave little fella.
I'm so so sorry. I didn't know until I got back.
Never.
Anyone who tries to throw you out will be having words with me. From now on, wherever you go, I go too.
You'll be safe down here. I'll check on you as soon as I can.
I'm not leaving you!
I...I didn't mean in the same room.
Help my brothers.
I can't just hide here with you while they're fighting out there.
Look. I am a man of the Night's Watch, Gilly. I made a promise to defend The Wall and I have to keep it, because that's what men do.
I promise you I won't die.
There's a lot more Wildlings on the other side of the wall than this side. We've got us some weapons and things. This is the perfect place for us to be.
Of course I'm afraid, there's a band of Wildlings coming to kill us.

I didn't know I was going to kill it, but I had to do something I didn't have any choice, he was going to kill Gilly and take the baby. If someone had asked me my name right then, I wouldn't have known. I wasn't Samwell Tarly anymore. I wasn't a Steward in the Night's Watch or son of Randyll Tarly or any of that. I was nothing at all. And when you're nothing at all, there's no more reason to be afraid.
Yes, well, I'm not nothing anymore.
Did you get one?
Yeah?
If you keep missing we will.
Oh, is it over?
Well then. *hands Pyp a loaded crossbow*
You're alright. You're alright. I'll get help. Shh, shh. Maester Aemon is coming, he's gonna make it alright.
*to Grenn* We need more men down here.
But we need men here.
*to Olly* Get me to the top! You hear me?!
Your name's Olly, right? Listen to me Olly, get me to the top and when we give a signal, bring us back down. Do you hear me?
And find a weapon, Olly. Fight them!
The Wildlings are over the walls. Ser Alliser has fallen. The castle walls can't stand much longer.
You can't protect me forever. There won't be anywhere to hide if the castle falls.
We need you boy.
Woah! It's me. It's me. I promised you, didn't I?
We held them off.
This is a great victory.
Where are you going?
To...you can't do that. No one gave you any orders.
Without Mance? You're going to kill him?
They'll never let you within a hundred yards of him. And if you did, even if you managed to kill him.
He won't just kill ya. They'll boil you. They'll filet you. They'll make it last days!
What are you doing?
Jon. Come back.
It wasn’t.
You didn’t fail him, or him, or her. Every one of them is alive because of you, and no one else.
(panting) I'm not cut out for this sort of work.
Where do you think Jon is right now?
He's not dead. No, Jon's a great fighter.
He's got a Valyrian steel sword.
And Qhorin Halfhand is the greatest ranger alive.
The First Men made these marks. Help me with it.
That's a Night's Watch cloak.
Oh. Must be dragonglass.
The maesters call it obsidian.
It's getting dark. We could stay here for the night.
How hard can it be to build a fire?
He winked at me before.
I saw him wink at me.
I suppose it's a rather philosophical difference between a wink and a blink.
Well, I mean, there's no real difference.
You thought of a name yet? It would be easier to refer to him if he had a name.
What? No. This is just the way I talk.
Don't you think he should have a name?
Let's see, there's Duncan, Kevan, Jon Guymon, Feldan, Tristifer.
Uh, yes. Maybe that's not--
That's a lovely idea, but Mormont's a last name.
Well, it's a family name. For instance, I'm Samwell Tarly. Samwell is my birth name and Tarly is my family name. You see? So my father is also a Tarly.
No, Randyll Tarly.
Please don't name him Randyll.
Different manner of cruel.
I'll be back. I just want to look.
Go inside. Go back inside. I'll--
Stay back!
You stay back!
I should never have left her there.
Of course I could've known. They were raiding the villages close by.
She's dead because of me.
Little Same. As if I'd cut their throats myself.
She might have.
No.
How are you?
You know what I mean.
Are you scared?
Me too.
Who’s there?
Come in.
Not to worry. Men brawl from time to time. It’s only natural.
You’re a good lad.
Now then. How can I help?
Yes?
We’ve been fighting them for years.
Wildlings are people, just like us. There are good ones and bad ones.
Olly...
I’ve seen the army of the dead. I’ve seen the white walkers, and they’re coming for us, for all the living. And when it’s time we’ll need every last man we can find.
Jon’s taking a risk, but he has to. We don’t stand a chance otherwise. Sometimes a man has to make hard choices, choices that might look wrong to others but you know are right in the long run.
With all my heart.
Try not to worry Olly. I’ve been worrying about Jon for years. He always comes back.
All right, Jon?
Safe travels, Lord Commander.
Oh, and…
It’s Dragonglass. It’s what I used to kill the White Walker. I hope you don’t need them.
His little brother, Aegon. He became king.
You don’t know that.
I’ll stay too. He’s always been good to me. I can’t leave him now.

His name was Aemon Targaryen. He came to us from King’s Landing, a Maester of the Citadel, chained and sworn. And a sworn brother of the Night’s Watch, ever faithful. No man was wiser or gentler or kinder. At the Wall a dozen Lord Commanders came and went during his years of service, but he was always there to counsel them. He was the blood of the dragon, but now his fire has gone out. And now his watch has ended.
Get your hands off her. Brother Derryk. Brother Brand. I believe you both have guard duty tonight.
Gilly go on, Get back to your room and lock the door.
I’m warning you.
I said get your hands off her.
I killed a White Walker. I killed a Thenn. I’ll take my chances with you.
I’m alright.
I will not. They would’ve… I don’t know what they would have done
No. They were tiring out. I had them in the palm of my hand.
What kind of man would I be if I ran away when I saw someone hurting you?
Of course I will. And I’ll take care of you, too.
Stay. Please.
Oh. Oh my.
I wasn't sure if you'd remember me.
Oh, well, thank you, but, um, I'm not sure that I am. What happened to you beyond the Wall?
Oh! I don't know what that means.
Um, Jon's the one to lead the fight against the dead. I know he is. But he can't do it alone, so I've come here to help him.
Oh.
The truth about what?
It's not.
At the Citadel, I transcribed a High Septon's diary. He annulled Rhaegar's marriage to Elia. He wed Rhaegar and Lyanna in a secret ceremony.
It's what the High Septon wrote in his private diary. I don't know why he'd lie. Is this something you can see?
But the more wood, the bigger the fire.
Huh. You know your fires.
It doesn't get so cold down in the Reach. And there were servants, of course.
Oh, I found something at the Fist of the First Men. Buried treasure. Thousands of years old. I think.
I'm not sure that it does anything. It's beautiful, though, isn't it?
Not so far. A few more days.
Bigger. So big you can't even see the top sometimes. It's hidden in clouds.

I'm not. It's 700 feet high, all made of ice. On a warm day you can see it weeping. Castle Black is nice enough. They keep a fire burning in the long hall day and night. And Hobb makes venison stew with onions. Very tasty. Sometimes, one of the brothers will sing. Dareon sings best.
No. No, no, no. Not very well.
Oh, all right. Let's see.

Oh ahem. (sings) The father's face is stern and strong / He sits and judges right from wrong / He weighs our lives, the short and long / And loves the little children / The mother gives the gift of life / And watches over every wife / Her gentle smile ends all strife  /And she loves her little children.

Oh, yes. Once you get south of the Riverlands, very different sorts of trees start to take over. Maple, elm, beech, poplar. The odd willow. And of course now that summer’s over, you’ll start seeing the autumn colors coming in. Still, you’ll find more green here than anywhere else.
I didn’t think I’d ever come back here after my father made me renounce my title and inheritance and, well, threaten to kill me if I didn’t. A person just doesn’t feel welcome at that point.
And I certainly didn’t expect to be introducing him to my family. So you know what to tell them.
Yes, now that’s very important. If he thinks that Little Sam is his grandchild, he’ll take you in. He’ll get an education. He’ll learn to read. And you can help teach him. Huh?
I told them I met you in the North.
Well, it wasn’t a very large piece of parchment.
The thing is...my father doesn’t like wildlings. He hoped I’d make a man of myself by “killing some bloody wildlings,” so it’s probably a good subject to avoid if at all possible.
Oh, hello, Mother.
Oh.
Talla? Gods, you’re a woman now.
Oh, um, Mother, this is Gilly.
Oh, and this, this is our son Samwell.
Where is Father? And Dickon?
It’s beautiful. You’re beautiful.
This venison, it’s very tasty. Is it from today’s hunt?
Oh, of course. Sorry. Long journey.
Oh, that’s a fair distance.
Oh, yes. Yes. A good amount. North of the Wall, you don’t hunt, you don’t eat.
Oh, yes. Yes, please. Thank you, Mother.
Yes. And once I have my chain, I’ll return to Castle Black.
Gilly.
I’m sorry I let him treat you that way. I was afraid he’d turn you and Little Sam away.
First light.
Say good-bye to him for me.
Good-bye, Gilly.
We’re leaving.
Get your things.
We belong together. All of us.
Oh, they’ll understand.
She won’t mind. Looks better on you anyway.
It’s my family’s sword.
He can bloody well try.
Why just us?
Aye.
A Song of Ice and Fire. Archmaester Ebrose's history of the wars following the death of King Robert. I helped him with the title.
Oh, I wouldn't say that.
He -
I don't believe you're mentioned, ahem.
Your Grace.
He was last spotted flying east, toward--
Long may he reign.
Grandmaester, ahem, it is my theory, based on my years of work on the Casterly Rock sewers, that clean water leads to a healthier population.
The Archmaester has done some research on this subject and it turns out--
Uh the Archmaester is less than enthusiastic about the salutary effects of brothels.
Beautiful, isn't it? Gilly would love it here.
The Fist of the First Men. Think of how old this place is. Before the Targaryens defeated the Andals, before the Andals took Westeros from the First Men.
Thousands and thousands of years ago, the First Men stood here where we're standing all through the long night. What do you think they were like, the First Men?
And three for White Walkers. It's been a thousand years, but that's the only time they blow the horn three times.
Well I read it in a book.
I don't see very well.
I could take up Jon's duties while he's gone, my lord. It would be no trouble.

"And though Daenerys maintains her grip on Slaver's Bay, forces rise against her from within and without. She refuses to leave until the freedom of the former slaves is secure." She sounds like quite a woman.
Don't say that, Maester Aemon.
(Standing up) There is good farmland in the Gift. (Laughter). Land that no one uses now. A dozen abandoned villages.
Every book there is?
Well, no. There are thousands and thousands of books out there. This library is rather small, actually.
My father's not the most literate man. They say the Citadel has the largest library in the world.
The Citadel? In Oldtown.
Gilly, look at me. You know how to do a hundred things I can never do. You can build a fire with wet wood. You can cook. You can stitch a wound.
Well -
It's where they train the maesters.
I wanted to be one when I was young. Instead, I became a man of the Night's Watch. Far more adventure up here anyway. I wouldn't have met you.
Ah, Your Grace.
I am, Your Grace.
I did, Your Grace.
With a dagger made of dragonglass.
What the maesters call obsidian -
I don't know. I've been going through all the old manuscripts hoping to find something, and all I've learned is that the children of the forest used to hunt with dragonglass.
I've seen it, Your Grace.
The army of the dead. And when they come -
No.
I'm sorry. He's beautiful.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: He's beautiful.  Does he have a name?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Are you going to give him one?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Gilly.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I wanted you to have it.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Craster's got his daughters to feed.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: We can't just show up and steal all his food. We're brothers of the Night's Watch, not thieves.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Quickly. Quickly.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: No, we have to go. Now!
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I should neverhave taken her away.
She's not safe out there. I should have known. You told us there were wildlings south of the Wall. If they're raiding the smaller villages, Mole's Town could be next.
I should go back for Gilly.
I remember when you disobeyed orders and rode south to help Robb.
I wish I could have convinced him to come back with me. I tried. Really, I did.
I don't know. They'd pass wildling villages. They could try to find shelter at one of them.
Craster's? You think Bra nmight have found...
Lord Ashford. (Jon signs). Lady Caulfield. (Jon signs). Lord Smallwood.
They haven't heard of you either. (Jon signs). But we need men and they have some.
(Chuckles, looking at the next letter). More than Lord Wibberley.
I know, I'm sorry, but we need men and supplies. And Roose Bolton is the Warden of The North.
We swore to be the Watchers on the Wall. We can't watch the Wall with 50 men. And we can't get more men without help from the Warden of the North.
Apologies, my Lady.
(to GENDRY) Uh, hold on.
Yes, well, the nights have been getting longer and there wasn't that much to do in Oldtown. There's only so many books a person can read, so we--
You're the best friend I ever had.
Lord Commander
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Gilly.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I I wanted to give you something. It belonged to my mother.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Please. I want you to. My mother used it for sewing. She'd let me sit with her in her chamber while she sewed and I'd read to her. My father put a stop to it when he found out. It's the only thing I have of hers. She gave it to me before I left for the Wall.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I'm not giving it away. I'm giving it to you. Keep it safe for me. Till I come back.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Ghost?
She's not a whore.
They all think I'm lying.
Killing the white Walker.
But you're the only one that saw it. And they all think you're just a...
Well, a wildling.
It's not a very nice word, I suppose.
So... are you all right? No one bothering you?
I worry about it sometimes. All the time.
100 men, one woman.
That's the only thing they think about.
I'm not being silly. There's 100 men lying awake at night picturing you.
What about me? Gilly? I worry about you.
For what?
I don't know if you're safe here. They're my brothers, but some of them were thieves before they came here. Some of them were rapers. You saw what they did at your father's keep.
Maybe you'd be safer in Mole's Town.
Bored of you? I... I want to protect you.
He's seen some adventures, hasn't he?
That's right. She'll clean and cook and look after the other girls' babies.
No other work.
No other work.
I promise to come back and visit whenever I can. You'll be safer here. Who's gonna protect you at Castle Black? Me?
This is different. They're brothers of the Night's Watch and I can't just stab them in the back and I can't run away, which is what we did at Craster's.
You have to trust me. It's for the best.
Oh, Gilly. Please don't.
He apologizes for not being here. He's not feeling well.
Alright, alright! Enough of that.
Seems a bit greedy for one man to have so many wives. Wouldn't two, or three, be enough for him?
Nothing like the sight of a woman walking away.
I'm sure that's nice too.
You were with her? How many times?
Wish I'd grew up on a farm.
Oh, oh. Right. I'm sorry. I just wanted to make sure you weren't hurt. (Long pause).
This is Gilly, she's one of Craster's daughters.
She's pregnant. (Gilly encourages Sam to continue). We have to take her with us, when we leave.
I know it sounds a bit mad.
The Lord Commander, (Jon tries and fails to interrupt Sam), we are sworn to protect -
Why did you do that?
You were cruel.
I didn't touch her!
I can't steal her, she's a person not a goat.
I could try. (Jon looks down, annoyed). What? I've read about it. A bit.
No.
I can't.
You don't care.  EDDISON TOLLETT: 'Course we care.
You left me. When the white walkers came, you left me.
(nods)
Hmm! Did you know that the youngest Lord Commander in history, Osric Stark, was elected at the age of 10?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I only said that the more time you spend practicing, the faster you'll be able to read.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Did you ever see them?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: He'll make you a Stark with the stroke of a pen?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: You deserve this. You do. I couldn't be happier for you.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Maester Aemon.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Her name is Gilly. Brother Slynt knows her quite well. They cowered together in the larder during the battle for the Wall.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: A wildling girl, a baby, and Lord Janos. I found him there when the battle was over in a puddle of his own making.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Whilst Lord Janos was hiding with the women and children, Jon Snow was leading.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Ser Alliser fought bravely, it is true. And when he was wounded, it is Jon who saved us. He took charge of the wall's defense. He killed the Magnar of the Thenns. (a man next to Sam nods) He went North to deal with Mance Rayder, knowing it almost certainly meant his own death. Before that, he led the mission to avenge Lord Commander Mormont. Mormont himself chose Jon to be his steward. He saw something in Jon, and now we've all seen it, too. He may be young, but he's the commander we turned to when the night was darkest.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Nothing's killed me yet.
You offered me a ride!
Are those girls?
I haven't seen a girl in six months.
That's foul.
Hmm?
( Panting and breathing heavily. )
Brother?
(Shakes his head.)
Sometimes I want to hate you.
Well, you're better than me at everything. Except reading.
They're... they're ready for you.
No one's going to hang you. You've done nothing wrong.
Well, I’m hardly a new recruit. How many brothers can say they’ve killed a white walker and a Thenn? I might be the first in history!
If Ser Alliser is chosen as the new Lord Commander… he hates the wildlings.
All the wildlings.
It’s not a sure thing. Ser Denys Mallister has commanded the Shadow Tower for 20 years, and people say he’s a good man. He’s running against Ser Alliser--
I told you. Wherever you go, I go, too.
Archmaester, I was wondering...
I was wondering if you had considered my proposition.
I asked you if, in light of what I've seen in the North, if I could have access to the restricted area of the library.
No.

Archmaester, with respect, I've seen them--the Army of the Dead. The white walkers. W was sent here to learn how to defeat them, but everyone in the Citadel, those who will even talk to me, they all doubt the walkers ever existed in the first place.
Sources in the restricted area?
So, you believe me.
That makes you the only person south of the Twins who does.
The dead don't.
The Targaryens used dragonglass to decorate their weapons without even knowing what the First Men used it for.
It's a map of Dragonstone. The Targaryens built their first stronghold there when they invaded Westeros.
A mountain of it. Beneath the ground. Stannis told me, but I didn't think... This is important. Jon needs to know.
Who?
Haven't heard anything.
But… you killed a White Walker.
But you'd be Lord of Winterfell.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Three?! Who taught you, your mother?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Very carefully.
Hyah! Hyah! Hyah! Hyah!

(in unison, reciting the vow) "l am the sword in the darkness, the watcher on the walls, the shield that guards the realms of men. l pledge my life and honor to the Night's Watch, for this night and all nights to come.”
No. Ghost, no. No. Ghost, away. Shoo. Shoo! (Ghost walks away). (To Gilly)  You all right? Did he frighten you?
Just a bit woozy.
You're allowed to look happy.

We're west of Castle Black, but the Nightfort's closest to us.  It was the first castle on the Wall. The Watch abandoned it during the reign of King Jaehaerys I.  Too big, impossible to maintain.  Other than Castle Black itself, the Nightfort's the perfect castle for us.  It's got a secret sally port, the Black Gate, as old as the Wall itself.  No one's used it for centuries, most likely.  It leads through the Wall right down into the Nightfort, if no one knows how to find it, which, it just so happens I do.
I read about it in a very old book.
Yes.
Brandon Stark --
I let him through the Wall years ago.  I saw him go beyond the wall.
Somehow a crippled boy survived for years beyond the wall when no one else could.  Not the Night's Watch, not the Wildlings, no one.  Perhaps we ought to listen to what he has to say.

Everyone in Westeros trusts and respects you.  If you tell people the threat is real they'll believe it.  If you advise all the lords to send their men north to hold the wall they'll do it.  And if you tell every maester in the Citadel to search every word of every faded scroll about the long night, they may find something that lets us defeat the Army of the Dead for good.
Archmaester, please.  It's real.  I've seen it.
Archmaester --
None anymore.
It's when a man sets asside his lawful wife.

These maesters, they set me to the task of preserving that man's window counting and anullments and bowel movements for all eternity while the secret to defeating the Night King's probably sitting on some dusty shelf somewhere completely ignored.  But that's all right, isn't it.  We could all become slavering, murderous imbecils enthralled to evil incarnate as long as we can have access to the full records of High Septon Meynard's 15,782 shits.
I'm tired of reading about the achievements of better men.
Yes. Yes, I’m fine.
I don’t think so.
Yeah, they do.
No. No, no, no. The Citadel doesn’t admit women. There won’t be a place for you there or for Little Sam.
The Citadel isn’t Castle Black. I don’t have a Jon Snow or a Maester Aemon to help me bend the rules.
By yourself? With a baby and no money?
I don’t care about them. Well, no, I do, but I don’t really. I care about you and him.
I’d feel better if you threw something at me and stormed off.
I suppose I'll find out this evening.
Where will you go?
Your father saved me more than once.  It's the least I could do.
I hope they do.
Yes.
No one.
I seem to remember you.
I did.
I read the books and followed the instructions.
Thank you, Archmaester.
Pardon, Archmaester.  I met Stannis Baratheon's daughter at Castle Black.  She had Greyscale as a baby and was cured.  Isn't there some way --
No.
No.
Should we send word to your family, Ser...
Mormont?
Archmaester, I wanted to --
I wanted to ask you --
Possibly something a bit more poetic?
Archmaester, if I could just have a moment.
I may have found a way to treat Ser Jorah.  There are two recorded cases of advanced Greyscale being cured.  I found the procedure in the Study of Rare Diseases by Archmaester Pilos.
Oh, you are?
Oh.
Hello.
You're Jorah Mormont.  You're the only son of Jeor Mormont.

My name is Samwell Tarley.  I'm a sworn brother of the Night's Watch training to serve as maester of Castle Black.  I knew your father.  I was with him when he died.  You're not dying today, Ser Jorah.
Drink this.
Rum.  Drink it all, please.  I'm afraid this is going to hurt.
If you could take off your shirt.
If you wouldn't mind, bite down hard.  I'm sorry but no one knows I'm here.  If they hear you screaming then we're both finished.
No.  No one else will try, so I'm the best you've got.  Have a seat.  I'm going to remove the entire layer of infected tissue, then apply a medicinal ointment to the --
Shh,  shhh.  Again, I'm sorry.  Please try not to scream.
Why? What does he want?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: That's what death is, isn't it? Forgetting. Being forgotten. If we forget where we've been and what we've done, we're not men anymore. Just animals. Your memories don't come from books. Your stories aren't just stories. If I wanted to erase the world of men, I'd start with you.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Have you told her yet?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Mm-hmm. Being careful. Biding your time. Waiting for the perfect-
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: They'll be safe down in the crypt.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Everyone seems to forget that I was the first man to kill a White Walker. I've killed Thenns.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I've saved Gilly more than once. I stole a considerable number of books from the Citadel library, survived the Fist of the First Men. You need me out there.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Well, calling you "fucked" wouldn't be strictly accurate.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Think back to where we started. Us, Grenn, Pyp.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Oh, I'm sorry, I didn't mean to-
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: It's called Heartsbane. It's my family sword.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Yes. And I'd love to defend them with it. But I can't really hold it upright. Your father, he taught me how to be a man. How to do what's right. This is right. It's Valyrian steel. I'd be honored if you'd take it.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I'll see you when it's through.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I hope we win.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Oh!
Um. Which man am I, Your Grace?
Oh, it's my honor to serve you, Your Grace.
If it's not too much trouble, I could use a pardon.
Um, I borrowed a few books from the Citadel.
And also a sword.
From my family. It's been in House Tarly for generations.
It would've been mine anyway, eventually, but my father had other ideas.
You know him?
Well At least I'll be allowed home again, now that my brother's the lord.
What are you doing out here?
No, no. You're his brother. Shouldn't you tell him?
I'm sorry, I know I'm not supposed to be down here.
Of course not.
She's good.
Don't you know?
Daenerys. She executed my father and brother. They were her prisoners.
She didn't tell you.
Would you have done it?
You've also spared men. Thousands of wildlings when they refused to kneel.
But you were. You've always been.
I'm not talking about the King in the North. I'm talking about the King of the bloody Seven Kingdoms.
Bran and I worked it out. I had a High Septon's diary. Bran had whatever Bran has.
Your mother was Lyanna Stark.
She shouldn't be.
It's the truth. You gave up your crown to save your people. Would she do the same?
You can't!
They'll put out the word. They'll send out ravens. People will come after you. Do you know what happens to deserters?
What are you going to do?
You can't leave us now. We need you here.
l won't let you go.
No.
Did we get him?
But we're your brothers now.
"Hear my words and bear witness to my vow"
l'm really not supposed to say.
There was a raven. l read the message to Maester Aemon. lt's your brother Robb.
He's heading south. To war. All his bannermen have rallied to his side, they'll keep him safe.
The smell.
There is none.
Jon, no! Jon, stop, put it down!
They were touched by White Walkers.
I read about it in a book. A very old book in Maester Aemon's library.
The White Walkers sleep beneath the ice for thousands of years.
I hope The Wall is high enough.
Hear my words and bear witness to my vow.
I miss girls.
One blast for a Ranger returning, two for Wildlings, three for...
I wish I could help you, but I'm no Ranger.
There's honor in being a steward.
my lord, might I go as well?
No, my lord.
The Night's Watch is my house now.
Jon, wait! Don't you see what they're doing?
I thought you were caught stealing a wheel of cheese for your starving sister?
Could you sing me a song, Pyp? I'd like to hear a song.
Now listen to me. The old man is the Lord Commander of The Night's Watch.
I always wanted to be a Wizard.
What's he got there?
Gods be good!
Samwell Tarly, of Horn Hill. I mean, I was of Horn Hill. I've come to take the black.
I yield. Please, no more.
Did he hurt you?
You can call me Sam... If you want. My mother calls me Sam.
I wanted to. I just couldn't.
I'm a coward. My father always says so.
You're right. I'm sorry. I just... Wanted to thank you.
Hello. Ser Alliser said I'm to be your new watch partner. I should warn you, I don't see all that well.
No, that's all right. I'm fine.
I don't like high places.

On the morning of my 18th nameday, my father came to me. "You're almost a man now," he said, "but you're not worthy of my land and title. Tomorrow, you're going to take the black, forsake all claim to your inheritance and start north. If you do not," he said, "then we'll have a hunt and somewhere in these woods your horse will stumble and you'll be thrown from your saddle to die. Or so I'll tell your mother. Nothing would please me more." Ser Alliser's going to make me fight again tomorrow, isn't he?
I'm not going to get any better, you know?
I know for a fact that some of the officers go to that brothel in Mole's Town.
Don't you think it's a little bit unfair? Making us take our vows while they sneak off for a little sally on the side?
It's silly, isn't it? What, we can't defend the Wall unless we're celibate? It's absurd.
Why not? Because I'm fat? But I like girls just as much as you do. They might not like me as much. I've never... been with one. You've probably had hundreds.
I find that hard to believe.
You didn't know where to put it?
Was she... Old and ugly?
What color hair?
I like red hair. And her... Her...
That good?
Oh no. So why exactly did you not make love to Ros with the perfect...
Jon Snow?
Because... You're a bastard from the north.
So... You didn't know where to put it?
It is a bit nippy.
For Oberyn.
Where’s the other one? The one with the yellow cloak.
Those are your last words, fuck you? Come on, you can do better.
You’re shit at dying, you know that?
Chasing them. You?
Any particular reason?
Same reason. I was helping build it. They killed a friend of mine.
Not anymore. They’re mine.
Fuck your name. They’re mine. I killed you once before, Dondarrion. Happy to do it again.
Drop that arrow, you bloody girl.
Tougher girls than you have tried to kill me.
Two.
hanging? All over in an instant. Where’s the punishment in that?
We all bloody die.
Except this one here. I’ll only gut one of them.
I’ll chop off one hand.
Bunch of nancies.
There was a time I would have killed all seven of you just to gut these three.
He’s not.
Got anything to eat?
I prefer chicken.
I tried joining. Didn’t work out for me.
Lots of horrible shit in this world gets done for something larger than ourselves.
And you’re going to go stop them?
Last time you saw me, you wanted to execute me.
I beat you because I’m better than you, Beric. I was better than you before you started yammering on about the Lord and I’m better than you now.
Just one.
He was a woman.
I’m used to it.
Hate.
Aye, there’s a reason. I’m a big fucker and I’m tough to kill.
I’ve heard that before. Man was talking about a different god, though.
You’re in the wrong line of work.
You didn’t know me back in my time. You don’t know the things I’ve done.
If the gods are real...why haven’t they punished me?
“Seven save you, friends.”
They don’t believe in your Seven. They’re from the Brotherhood. They follow the Red God.
Sure, you do. You’ve got food, you’ve got steel even if you say you don’t. And you’ve got women.
You do.
Even if it’s to protect yourself?
You don’t cure it by dying, either.
It’s gonna be a cold night. We’ll need firewood.
Fuck off.
Not yet. You came pretty close.
You and me both.
Where?
Who's protecting her if you're here?
It won't be me.
Anyone touches it, I'll kill you first.
I left this shit city because I didn't want to die in it. Am I going to die in this shit city?
And this is all your idea. Seems every bad idea has some Lannister cunt behind it.
Remember me? Yeah, you do. You're even fucking uglier than I am now. What did they do to you? Doesn't matter. That's not how it ends for you, brother.
Sounds all right so far.
Was she naked too?
It could have been worse.
But they didn't, did they?  So what you whinging about?
Your lips are moving and you're complaining about something.  That's whinging.
This one's been killed six times.  You don't hear him bitching about it.
Fuck off.
I don't give two shits about Wildlings.
Gingers I hate.
Don't point your fucking finger at me.
I didn't trip.  I was pushed.
Will you fuck off?
Do you want to suck my dick?  Is that it?
Cock.
I bet you do.
Brienne of Tarth.
You're with Brienne of fucking Tarth?
How does she look at you?  Like she wants to carve you up and eat your liver?
We've met.
How did a mad fucker like you live this long?
That's what I saw in the fire.
A mountain like an arrowhead.
We're getting close.
For fuck's sake, will you shut your hole.  Are we coming with you or not?
You never used to shut up. Now you're just sitting there like a mute.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: What's it look like?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I fought for you, didn't I?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Oh, for fuck's sake. May as well be at a bloody wedding.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Was he on your list?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Thoros isn't here anymore, so I hope you're not about to give a sermon. Because if you are, the Lord of Light's gonna wonder why he brought you back 19 times just to watch you die when I chuck you over this fucking wall.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Where are you going?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: You've got real powerful magic to figure that out. Did the Lord of Light whisper that in your ear? "It's snowing, Thoros. It's windy. It's gonna be a cold night."
Don't like that shit. It's too sweet.
Experience.
These people don't want us here.
I don't like the look of it.
I'll tell you what doesn't scare me: bald cocksuckers like you. You think you're fooling anyone with that top knot? Bald cunt.
They don't.
With death.
Doesn't matter now.
I've known you a long time, Dondarrion.
And I always thought you were dull as dirt.
You're not bad. I don't hate you. Don't like, but you're not bad.
But there's nothing special about you.

So why does the Lord of Light keep bringing you back? I've met better men than you, and they've been hanged from crossbeams, or beheaded, or just shat themselves to death in a field somewhere. None of them came back. So, why you?
And?
Your Lord.
If he's so all-powerful, why doesn't he just tell you what the fuck he wants?
It's my fucking luck I end up with a band of fire worshippers.
There's no divine justice, you dumb cunt. If there was, you'd be dead...and that girl would be alive.
What do you want?
I don't want to look in the damn flames.
I keep asking and no one wants to tell me.
Logs burning.
Ice. A wall of ice. The Wall.
It's where the Wall meets the sea. There's a castle there.
There's a mountain. Looks like an arrowhead. The dead are marching past.
Thousands of them.
Burying the dead.
Not really.
We ask the Father to judge us with mercy. We ask the Mother to... Fuck it, I don't remember the rest. I'm sorry you're dead. You deserved better. Both of you.
You're saying you're good, is that it?
You know who makes weapons for the wildlings? Cripples and cocksuckers. Which one are you?
I heard you were here. You left me to die.
You're a cold little bitch, aren't you? Guess that's why you're still alive.
Do as you're bid, child.
Here, girl. (cleans her bloody lip with a rag)
Save yourself some pain, girl. Give him what he wants.
You'll be needing that again.
Who do you think sent me?
I'm no Ser.
The Prince will remember that, little lord.
Do I frighten you so much, girl? Or is it him there making you shake? He frightens me too. Look at that face.
He hasn't been very talkative these last 20 years. Since the mad King had his tongue ripped out with hot pincers.
He ran... Not very fast.
They're right. I'm not good enough for him.
But, if he's not marrying me
King's Landing is my home now.
What are you doing?
A list of people you mean to kill?
You should learn to ignore them.
You're a Lannister. I am the disgraced daughter of the traitor Ned Stark.
(laughs)
So how should we punish them?
Ser Eldrick Sarsfield and Lord Desmond Crakehall.
I hear that you're a pervert.

(chuckles) We could sheep shift Lord Desmond's bed. You cut a little hole in his mattress and you stuff sheep dung inside. Then you sew up the hole and make his bed again. His room will stink, but he won't know where it's coming from.
My sister used to do that when she was angry with me. And she was always angry with me.
That's the vulgar word for dung.
Well, you asked me.
I know what Ramsay is. I know what he’ll do to me. (looks to Theon) If I’m going to die, let it happen while there’s some of me left.
Mother and Father’s room? You should take it.
You are to me.
He did.
Only a fool would trust Littlefinger. I should have told you about him, about the Knights of the Vale. I’m sorry.
Jon.
A raven came from the Citadel. A white raven. Winter is here.
I’m done with all that. I came here every day when I was a girl. I prayed to be somewhere else. Back then I only ever thought about what I wanted, never about what I had.
I was a stupid girl.
What do you want?
I was wrong.
It’s a pretty picture.
You’ve declared for other houses before, Lord Baelish. It’s never stopped you from serving yourself.
She has, my lord, but King Joffrey sent for me to see him off.
I will pray for your safe return, my lord.
Just as I pray for the king's.
Will you slay him yourself?
So you'll be outside the gates fighting in the vanguard?
I'm sorry, Your Grace. You're right, I'm stupid. Of course you'll be in the vanguard. They say my brother Robb always goes where the fighting is thickest. And he is only a pretender.
Joffrey will. The worst ones always live.
I don't know why she wants me here. She's always saying how stupid I am. She hates me.
I doubt it.
Why would she be jealous?
Yes.
But we have guards to defend us.
You said he was here to protect us.
My queen. What are you doing? Praying.
Praying.
For the gods to have mercy on us all.
Yes, Your Grace.
Of course, Your Grace.
Joffrey is my
Your father doesn't believe in the gods?
They are your guests under your protection. You asked them here.
And if the city should fall?
You were Robert's queen.

Don't be afraid. The queen has raised the drawbridge. This is the safest place we can be. Joffrey's not hurt. He's fighting bravely. His knights have rallied behind him. They will save the city. Shall we sing a hymn? Gentle mother, fount of mercy...
Come with me.
The queen said they'd rape everyone.
What are you doing here?
Where?
What about the king?
I'll be safe here. Stannis won't hurt me.
You won't hurt me.
Yes, I do.
How do we know you have him?
You’re going to die tomorrow, Lord Bolton. Sleep well.
So you’ve met the enemy, drawn up your battle plans.

You’ve known him for the space of a single conversation, you and your trusted advisors, and you sit around and make your plans on how to defeat a man you don’t know. I lived with him. I know the way his mind works. I know how he likes to hurt people. Did it ever once occur to you that I might have some insight?
You think he’s going to fall into your trap. He won’t He’s the one who lays traps.
He plays with people. He’s far better at it than you. He’s been doing it all his life.
You don’t know him.

We’ll never get him back. Rickon is Ned Starks trueborn son, which makes him a greater threat to Ramsay than you, a bastard, or me, a girl. As long as he lives, Ramsay’s claim to Winterfell will be contested, which means he won’t live long.
Listen to me, please. He wants you to make a mistake.
I don’t know! I don’t know anything about battles! Just don’t do what he wants you to do.
You think that’s obvious?
If you had asked for my advice earlier, I would have told you not to attack Winterfell until we have a larger force, or is that obvious, too?
It’s not enough!
If Ramsay wins, I’m not going back there alive. Do you understand me?
No one can protect me. No one can protect anyone.
Jon.
Where is he?
Your words will disappear. Your house will disappear. Your name will disappear. All memory of you will disappear.
You haven’t fed them in seven days. You said it yourself.
They were. Now they’re starving.
You look very handsome, my lord.
I hope I will not disappoint you, my lord.
I doubt that very much, my lord.
When I have to.
What are you doing?
Will you pardon me, my lord?
Thank you, Your Grace.
Yes, Your Grace.
Is that wise, my lord?
Is that wise, Tyrion?
What?
But your father--
What if I never want you to?
He can stay.
*to Petyr* I'm sorry, Lord Baelish. I have to tell the truth. *to the council* I'll tell you everything.
My name is not Alayne. It's Sansa Stark, eldest daughter of Lord Eddard Stark. Lord Royce, we met when you came to Winterfell. You were escorting your son Ser Waymar to the Wall.

Lord Baelish has told many lies. All to protect me. Since my father was executed, I have been a hostage in King's Landing. A plaything for Joffrey to torture or Queen Cersei to torment. They beat me, they humiliated me, they married me to the Imp. I had no friends in King's Landing...except one. He saved me. Smuggled me away when he had the chance. He knew I'd be safe here in the Eyrie with my own blood, my Aunt Lysa. The Lannisters have friends everywhere. Even the Vale. He made me swear not to tell anyone my true name.

You knew her well, my lords, my lady. You knew she was a troubled woman. She always loved Lord Baelish. She told me herself. From the moment he arrived at the gates of Riverrun, a boy of eight carrying everything he owned in a little sack. She confessed to me she never loved Lord Arryn. She did as her father commanded, as so many of us have. When the gods finally allowed her to be with Lord Baelish, she was so happy. For a time. My aunt was a jealous lady. She was terrified that Lord Baelish didn't love her anymore. That he would abandon her for a younger woman. And then...one day she saw him kiss me.

It was a peck on the cheek, Lord Royce, nothing more. Lord Baelish is my uncle now, in truth, by marriage. He's always been so kind to me. I was so happy to be here, to be free. All because of him. But my aunt turned on me. She cursed me. Called me a whore. Promised to throw me through the Moon Door. When Lord Baelish tried to calm her, she struck him. She said she didn't want to live anymore. She stood on the edge of that Moon Door. He tried to reason with her. Promised her she was the only one he had ever loved, but she stepped through those doors and she was...*begins sobbing*
Yes?
They would have thrown you through the Moon Door if they found you guilty.
If they'd have executed you, what would they have done with me?
Neither do I.
I know what you want.
Shall we go?
Why. Why Theon.
Reek. Why did you tell him, Reek?
Good. If it weren’t for you I’d still have a family. If I could do to you what Ramsay did to you right here, right now, I would.
Only what?
Tell me!
Tell me, they weren’t what?!
You didn’t…?
Do you know where they went, Bran and Rickon?
Theon, you have to tell me, do you have any idea where Bran and Rickon--
I beg pardon, ser. I should have come to you after to thank you for saving me. You were so brave.
Does it give you joy to scare people?
(gasps)
It was his duty. He never liked it.
Why are you always so hateful?
No, no!
No. Oh, no.
If the Queen sees, I can have Joffrey's children now.
My mother told me, but I thought it would be different.
I thought it would be less Less messy.
I'm fit to bear children for the king?
(nods)
His Grace was not with you?
I love His Grace with all my heart.
But shouldn't I love Joffrey, Your Grace?
How do I make the best of my circumstances? I have to marry him.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: No.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: He's tried.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: He's a Lannister.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I'm sorry. Here I am complaining to you.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I'm not afraid of the pain. Not after what Joffrey's done to me.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: And that's a good thing?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: How do you know all this? Did your mother teach you?
