Text: Wait! Open the gate! Let us in! Help us! Please open it!
Begging us? It wasn't very long ago you were threatening us.
Craster's last child. A boy.
He didn't kill them. He offered them.
To the gods. The white walkers.

Many worthy fighters have died trying to make it to the Great Games. When most of you worthless scum join them in death this afternoon it will represent a significant rise in your station. But if you happen to triumph here you will fight at the great pit of Daznak itself in front of the queen. So whatever happens, the gods have smiled upon you today. Today is the day your lives actually start to mean something.
Salt mines.
What about the dwarf?
It will be a dwarf-sized cock.
Mhysa! Mhysa! Mhysa!
Mhysa! Mhysa! Mhysa! Mhysa!
Mhysa! Mhysa!
Who owns the North?
Who owns the North?
Show me!
And you, Lord Karstark? Your people share blood with the Starks, don’t they?  But here we are. Times change.
Your father was a cunt.
Your father was a cunt and that’s why you killed him.  I might’ve done the same to my father, if he hadn’t have done me the favor of dying on his own.
Mmm.

The bastard Jon Snow led an army of wildlings past the Wall.  We’re farther north than any of you fuckers. Wildlings come down, we always have to fight them first.  I like fighting wildlings, been doing it all my life.  But there are too many of them for us to beat back alone.
We need to help each other.  The colder it gets, the farther South those goat fuckers will roam. Won't take them long to get here.
If they get Jon Snow leading them, maybe.  He knows this place better than we ever will.
I’m not kissing your fucking hand.
I’m not doing that either.
Your father honored tradition. Knelt for Robb Stark. Called him King of the North. Was Robb Stark right to trust your father?
Fuck kneeling and fuck oaths. I've got a gift for you.
A girl, aye.  A wild one.
And the boy, nice and young. The way Karstark likes them.
Rickon Stark.
I saw you cry.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Your Grace.
Your Grace.
The men. Many deserted before dawn.
Nearly half. All the sell-swords, with all the horses.
Your Grace. The Lady Melisandre was just seen riding out of camp.
There’s not going to be a siege, Your Grace.
Lannisters! Where'd you learn the Lannister song?
Archers, to your marks!
Archers! Nock your arrows!
Hold fast!
Boulders ready!
Your Grace.
The wildfire.
We're too far from the gates. The fire, their archers Hundreds will die.
Nock! Draw!
Forty, Your Grace.
Mind where you're going.
Pitch, my lord.
500, my lord.
Yes, my lord.
What's in there?
Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Move.
Move, boy! Move!
Ha! Ha!
We found no trace of the direwolf, your Grace.
Chained up outside, your Grace.
Follow me!
You know how we deal with runaways here, do you?
Nock arrows!
Hold fast!
Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: (to BRIENNE) You'll die for this.
Take off his pants.
You little bastard.
My honor, Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I'm gonna fuck you into the dirt.
Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Spears!
The wolf's not here. Look down by the stream.
I'll check down here.
Your Grace?
They're coming round again!
It's a direwolf.
I think I heard a noise.
Go!
Clear!
Go! Go!
Man your stations!
Clear!

ARYA  to BERIC, SAM to EDDISON TOLLETT, JON to LYANNA MORMONT. JON looks at DAENERYS. As one, they ignite the pyres. DAENYRYS watches the flames spread under JORAH, then turns away. The living return to the crowd and the flames rise higher. The air fills with a thick black smoke.
Soldiers and freefolk hand torches to JON, SANSA, ARYA, SAM, TORMUND, DAENERYS and GREY WORM. They fan out to various pyres in the field, joining their fallen comrades for one final visit.
Your Grace will be dealt with according to her sins.
Name him.
Your cousin. And the King’s squire.
You had a husband.
His sins do not pardon your own.
Other men?
No others?
Speaking falsehoods before the Gods is a great crime. You understand this?
There are those who say your children were not fathered by King Robert. That they are bastards born of incest and adultery.
Good. But these are terrible charges. And the realm must know the truth of them. If Your Grace has given honest testimony, your trial will prove your innocence.
To a single sin. Others you have denied. Your trial will separate the truths from the falsehoods.
You have taken the first step on a path back to righteousness. In light of this, I will permit you to return to the Red Keep.
The Mother is merciful. It is her you should thank.
Good.
After your atonement.

A sinner comes before you. Cersei of House Lannister, mother to his Grace King Tommen, widow to his Grace King Robert. She has committed the acts of falsehood and fornication. She has confessed her sins and begged for forgiveness. To demonstrate her repentance, she will cast aside all pride, all artifice, and present herself as the gods made her, to you. The good people of the city. She comes before you with a solemn heart. Shorn of secrets. Naked before the eyes of gods and men. To make her walk of atonement.
You should have the decency to kneel before the gods
For me, it’s the knees. You?
Huh.
The people always do the dirty work.
A useful talent.
Your grandson and granddaughter swore sacred vows and lied. The Father judges us all. Sons of high lords, sons of fishermen. If you break his laws you will be punished.
You don’t give commands here, Lady Olenna.
I imagine this is strange for you. Everyone you meet has a hidden motive, and you pride yourself on slitting in and out. But I’m telling you a simple truth: I serve the gods. The gods demand justice.
By the holy text. The seven pointed star. If you don’t have one in your library I’ll give you my own.
Then you’ll remember the passages concerning buggery and perjury. Your grandchildren will be punished in the same manner as anyone who breaks the sacred laws.
Yes. The Gods’ laws must be applied to all equally.

Have you ever served the field, Lady Olenna? Have you ever reaped the grain? Has anyone in House Tyrell? A lifetime of wealth and power has left you blind in one eye. You are the few. We are the many. And when the many stop fearing the few…
And?

Well she and her brother are both being held under fair suspicion. A trial will be held for each of them to determine if they are guilty of any acts that might constitute a violation of the tenants of the faith.
Seven septons. As it was in all trials before the Targaryens.
I will.
Then they will be at The Mother’s mercy.
It depends on the situation. The acts themselves, the degree of contrition.
Did you know this chapel is one of the oldest structures in King’s Landing?
Baelor built his sept around it. But men worshipped here long before him.
Who carved this altar do you know?

No one does. There’s no name on it. Or anywhere else in the chapel. The people who built this place didn’t inflict their vanity on the people who came after them. The way Baelor did with that great gilded monstrosity out there. Their faith was clean.

A young man came to us not long ago, broken in body and spirit. He had so much to strip away, so much weighing him down. But piece by piece he unburdened himself. Let go of vanity, pride, sin, now his soul is so light he will float through the Seven Heaves like a bird.
And he has much to say about you.
You were said to be despondent when he died. Witnesses said that you refused to leave his bedside, even as Stannis’ army closed in
Wasn’t Joffrey your king? He was anointed by the 7, not Renly.
Wearing Renly’s armor.
Do you deny all of the charges against you? Fornication, buggery, blasphemy.
You never lay with Renly Baratheon.
Nor any other man.
That will be all Ser Loras.
Faith calls Queen Margaery forward.
Yes. We have some questions for you.
You are. And according to Law of the Seven, neither kings nor queens are exempt from testimony at a holy inquest.
How do you respond to these charges against your brother?
All of them?
Queen Margaery, in the presence of the gods, do you swear that your brother is innocent of these charges against him, to the best of your knowledge?
Thank you, your grace.
Do you know this man?
How did you come to meet?
And what occurred in his chamber?
You laid with him?
Is there anyone else who can support your claim?
The Faith is satisfied that there is enough evidence to bring a formal trial for Ser Loras... and Queen Margaery.
Bearing false witness before the gods is as grave a sin as any. Take her.
I did not leave the comfort of my home to come to yours and be called a thief.
It will be for the best. Your dragons will bring the world nothing but death and misery, my dear. If I knew where they were, I would not tell you.
Your ambition is an inspiration. But like all upstarts, you overreach. Three dragons the size of cats and an alliance with a charlatan do not make you a king.
The Mother of Dragons!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Forgive me. I had terrible dreams last night. Terrible dreams. I could not sleep until the sun was shining and the birds were singing. Look what a beauty you are now the Red Waste has been washed off you. I am sorry about all that unpleasantness. The silver hair of a true Targaryen. Xaro Xhoan Daxos, she is far too lovely for a glorified dockworker like yourself.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Every lady alive was lovelier and higher born than my grandfather. Did my servants not offer you something to eat, to drink? I'll have them flogged in the square.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Oh, she has a talent for drama, this one. So, my little princess, what is it you want?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I fear I'm no better than a servant in this regard. I cannot give you what I do not have.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I need to cross the Narrow Sea. I need my ships as well. I use them, you see, to bring spices from one port to another.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Retake? Did you once sit on the Iron Throne?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: But if you did not sit on it yourself, would it not be correct to say "take the Iron Throne"?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Of course not. You came to take my ships. So let me explain my position, little princess. Unlike you, I do not have exalted ancestors. I make my living by trade. And I judge every trade on its merits. You ask for ships. You say I shall be repaid triple. I do not doubt your honesty or your intentions. But before you repay your debts, you must seize the Seven Kingdoms. Do you have an army?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You do not have an army. Do you have powerful allies in Westeros?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: When were you there last?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: So, in truth, you have no allies.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Ah. Forgive me, little princess, but I cannot make an investment based on wishes and dreams. Now if you'll pardon me.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: A shrewd man.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I admire your passion. But in business, I trust in logic, not passion. I'm sorry, little princess.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Yes, my lady, but not with my ships.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Daenerys Stormborn of the house Targaryen.
Only by reputation, Khaleesi. And I'm no lord, merely a humble merchant. They call you the Mother of Dragons.

Oh, my name is quite long and quite impossible for foreigners to pronounce. I am simply a trader of spices. But we are the Thirteen, charged with the governance and protection of Qarth, the greatest city that ever was or will be.
Might we see the dragons?
Forgive me, Mother of Dragons, but no man alive has seen a living dragon. Some of my more skeptical friends refuse to believe your children even exist. All we ask is the chance to see for ourselves.
Oh, I don't think you are. But as I've never met you before, my opinion on the matter is of limited value.
Then perhaps you should return to where you come from. We wish you well.
We have received you. Here we are, and here you are.
Which we shall deeply regret. But Qarth did not become the greatest city that ever was or will be by letting Dothraki savages through its gates.
Ah. You are a true Targaryen. Only, as you said a moment ago, if we don't let you into the city, you will all die. And so
The discussion is over, Xaro Xhoan Daxos. The Thirteen have spoken.
The girl threatens to burn our city to the ground and you would invite her in for a cup of wine?  She is the Mother of Dragons.
Our decision is final.
Be it on your head.
Lady Olenna.
My lord. (hands Baelish a note)
There she is.
I want you, wolf girl. Come here.
She'll reward me, the Queen!
You said you saw my victory in the flames.

The flames lied. And I'm no better than a savage trusting in a fire god. I fought for your god in Blackwater Bay. I led my men to the gates of the seventh hell as their brothers burned alive, and for what? Attacked from behind by Tywin Lannister and the Tyrells. If you see so much in your flames, why didn't you warn me?
You claimed to speak for a god.
You talk about war as if you understand it.
Have you? Show me how you fight.
Show me.
Where's your god now? Will he save you? Where is your god?
I murdered my brother.
He wasn't your brother.
You promise these things, but you don't know. None of us know.
I see fire.
The usurper Robb Stark is dead. Betrayed by his bannerman.
Do you know who had this table carved and painted, Ser Davos?
And do you know how Aegon Targaryen conquered Westeros?

He had a smaller fleet than the kings he faced and a smaller army, but he had three dragons. Dragons are magic, Ser Davos. My enemies have made my kingdom bleed. I will not forget that. I will not forgive that. I will punish them with any arms at my disposal.
What is the life of one bastard boy against a kingdom?
The boy must die.
You don't deny it?
What other way?! Tell us about this other way.
Very well, Ser Davos Seaworth. I, Stannis of the House Baratheon, First of My Name, Rightful King of the Andals and the First Men, sentence you to die.
Why am I gonna need you?
When did you learn to read?
I've made my decision.
(chuckles) You see, Ser Davos? You've been saved by that fire god you like to mock. You're in his army now.
You're the king beyond the wall?
You know who I am?
It is customary to kneel when surrendering to a king.
I'll have thousands of your men in chains by nightfall, I'll have nowhere to put them and nothing to feed them. I'm not here to slaughter beat dogs. Their fate depends on their king.
Take these men away.
Your father was an honorable man.
What do you think he'd have done with him?
Very well. *to Davos* Take him.
We ride for Winterfell.
Prepare to form up.
Tell me.
How many?
Speak up. It can’t be worse than mutiny.
Cut her down.
Get the men into marching formation. On to Winterfell.
Trench here. Another one 300 yards from the castle wall. Hurry them along. And send out a foraging party immediately. The siege begins at sunrise.
Bolton has women fighting for him.
I did.
Go on, do your duty.
Prepare to land.
The dwarf has played his little trick.
He can only play it once.
Thousands.
Come with me and take this city!
To the Mud Gate, go now!
Get the ladders up!
Stand and fight! Stand and fight, damn you!
No!
Horses?
20 men rode into our camp without a single guard sounding the alarm?
Put last night’s guards in chains, either they fell asleep or they were conspiring with the enemy. Find out the truth and then hang them.
We’re not returning to Castle Black.
Have the dead horses butchered for meat.

Find you some healthy horses and a few good men to guard you, I’m sending you back to Castle Black. Tell the Lord Commander to send food, supplies, fresh horses, in return when I take the throne I’ll make sure the Night’s Watch has all the men it requires. He can guard all 19 castles if he so wishes.
Yes.
You’re not abandoning me, you’re obeying a command.
And if Jon Snow refuses a boy with a scroll, what does the boy say? I didn’t name you hand for your expertise in military matters, ride for Castle Black, don’t come back empty handed.
My family stays with me.
My family stays with me.
No. What are you reading?
What’s it about?
“The dance of dragons”? Why is that a dance?
Doesn’t make much sense.
If you had to choose between Rhaenyra and Aegon, who would you have chosen?

Sometimes a person has to choose. Sometimes the world forces his hand. If a man knows what he is and remains true to himself, the choice is not choice at all. He must fulfill his destiny and become who he is meant to be. However much he may hate it.
You don’t even know what I’m talking about.
Yes there is.
Forgive me.
There’s no other way. She has King’s blood.
Will it hold?
I admire you, Ser Davos.
Pleased to hear it. Some highborn fools call you Onion Knight and think they insult you. So you take the onion for your sigil, sew it on your coat, fly the onion flag.
Do you? Why?
And?
And where were those lords when Storm's End starved?
You defend these men who insult you behind your back.

We were forgotten. Robert and Ned Stark, they were the heroes. The glorious rebels marching from battle to battle, liberating towns from the yoke of the Mad King, while I held Storm's End with 500 men.
No? Robert did. He gave Storm's End to Renly after the war. Renly never fought a day in his life.
Then why'd he give him Storm's End?

First, we ate the horses. We weren't riding anywhere, not with the castle surrounded. We couldn't feed them, so fine, the horses. Then the cats. Never liked cats. So fine. I do like dogs. Good animals. Loyal. But we ate them. Then the rats. The night before you slipped through, I thought my wife was dying. She couldn't speak anymore, she was so frail. And then you made it through the lines. Slipped right through in your little black sailboat with your onions.
Every man in Storm's End wanted to kiss you that night.

Robert told me to hold Storm's End, so I held it. Then he told me he was giving it to Renly, so I gave it up. Insult or no, I gave it up. Because Robert was my older brother, and he was the king and I've always done my duty. But now, I'm the rightful king by every law of Westeros. And when I sit the Iron Throne, you'll be my Hand.
I expect you'll be the first crabber's son to wear the badge.
Half Robert, half lowborn.
What do you mean to do with him?
Then why bathe him and dress him in fine clothes?  If it needs to be done, do it. Don't torture the boy.
No.
You've slaughtered many lambs?
They feeding you enough?
You don't belong in a place like this.
Yeah.
Melisandre's returned.
Came back with a bastard boy. Robert's bastard boy.
She says there's power in king's blood.
Sacrifice him.
What of it? We're at war. Why should I spare the son of some tavern slut Robert bedded one drunken night?
So did Renly.

How many boys live in Westeros? How many girls? How many men? How many women?The darkness will devour them all, she says. The night that never ends. Unless I triumph. I never asked for this. No more than I asked to be king. We do not choose our destiny, but we must do our duty, no?  Great or small, we must do our duty. What's one bastard boy against a kingdom?
I came to free you. If you swear never to raise your hand to the Lady Melisandre again.
You have little regard for your own life.

I saw a vision in the flames. A great battle in the snow. I saw it. And you saw whatever she gave birth to. I never believed, but when you see the truth, when it's right there in front of you as real as these iron bars, how can you deny her god is real?
The usurper Robb Stark. The usurper Balon Greyjoy. The usurper Joffrey Baratheon.
What else?
500 men.
Sell swords, loyal to nothing but gold.
And?
I retreated from King’s Landing, Ser Davos. If I retreat again, I become “The King Who Ran.”
Winter is coming. Those arent just the Stark words, it’s a fact. If we march back to Castle Black, we winter at Castle Black. And who can say how many years this winter will last.
This is the right time, and I will risk everything. Because if I don't, we’ve lost. We march to victory or we march to defeat, but we go forward only forward,
I don’t know what I saw.
And you, do you trust yourself?
Are you sure?
We don’t have Robert’s bastard here.
Have you lost your mind?
There must be another way. Leeches or something.
She’s my daughter.
Get out.
We've been here since midday.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: He shares no blood with me. He is a bastard born of incest. As was his brother before him.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: It's not a story. It's the truth.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Gold you loaned him.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: More than any man living.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men
The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: None.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: 67. And when he dies, who's in command? A half-grown boy, the product of incest? Cersei Lannister, a queen whose people despise her? Jaime Lannister, a man best known for killing the king he was sworn to protect? When Tywin's gone, who do you back?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: What is it?
Fools love a fool. I grieve for him as well, for the boy he was, not the man he grew to be.
I thought I made it clear to you there'd be no need to speak to me on this matter.
I've never known you to need to hear a thing twice.
You've come to lecture me on truth?
All my brother's bannermen have come to my side. Except the Tyrells, who fled like cowards. They won't be able to resist us now. Soon I shall be sitting on the Iron Throne.
I'll hear no more about it.
As soon as I've consolidated my troops. We'll make short work of the Lannisters' fleet. Once Blackwater Bay is cleared, we'll deliver our troops to their doorstep and take the city.
That's not your concern.
I never thought I'd have reason to doubt your loyalty. Was I wrong?
Oh, truth again. All right, what's the truth? The hard truth?
What do you believe?
We set out for King's Landing without Lady Melisandre. And you lead the fleet into Blackwater Bay.
Most of those lords should consider themselves lucky I don't hang them for treason. Hard truths cut both ways, Ser Davos.
My lady.
I meant to come sooner. The battle--
Yes, of course.
I believed that once.
Selyse. I've broken a sacred vow. I've sinned.
I've wronged you. I have shamed you.
That's not true.
She's my daughter. I want to see her.
Shireen.
You've grown since I last saw you.
No.
He did. He fought bravely.
He won't be visiting, child.
Ser Davos is a traitor. He's rotting in a dungeon cell for his crime. Best forget him.
Fewer.
Nothing.
You're Samwell Tarly?

Your father is Randyll Tarly. He defeated my brother at the Battle of Ashford. Only battle Robert ever lost. I told him he shouldn't go so far west so soon, but he never listened. Fine soldier, your father. You don't look like a soldier. But I'm told you killed a white walker.
How?
Dragonglass?
I know what it is. We have it in Dragonstone. Why would obsidian kill a walker?
The Lady Melisandre told me that death marches on the Wall.
Seen what?
We have to know how to fight them. (After a moment). Keep reading, Samwell Tarly.
It's time.

If Jon Snow returns with the wildlings. We can't wait that long. We have the advantage, more men, more horses, all fed and rested. But every day we wait, the odds shift in Bolton's favor. This could turn to winter at any moment. We have to act now. Give the order. We march at sunrise.
No need, they're coming with us.
Here? Half these watchmen are killers and rapists. No, they march with us.
I hope you know what you're doing with these wildlings. I need those ships.
Lady Stark, I had not thought to find you in the Stormlands.
Who else might it be?
My own.
Watch yourself, Renly.
That's twice I've warned you.
It is strange to find you beside my brother, Lady Stark. Your husband was a supporter of my claim. Lord Eddard's integrity cost him his head. And you sit beside this pretender and chastise me.
The Iron Throne is mine by right. All those that deny that are my foes.

For the sake of the mother who bore us, I will give you this one night to reconsider. Strike your banners, come to me before dawn, and I will grant you your old seat in the Council. I'll even name you my heir until a son is born to me. Otherwise I shall destroy you.
You think a few bolts of cloth will make you king?
We shall see, Renly. Come the dawn, we shall see.
Do your knucklebones bring you luck?
Fewer.
Four fewer fingernails to clean. Never understood why you had to wear them.
You were a hero and a smuggler. A good act does not wash out the bad, nor a bad the good.
Does he listen?
She has a name. I trust you've not forgotten your smuggler's tricks.
I want you to be a smuggler this time.
The red woman. No one must know what you do and we'll not speak of this again.
Cleaner ways don't win wars.
Lord Commander of the Night's Watch.
Perhaps, but that wasn't Ned Stark's way.
Not your fault.
We must. To beat the snows.
I won't. I need you.
(Walking away). And you, my Lady, what do you need?
Are you lonely?
My father used to tell me that boredom indicates a lack of inner resources.
I know Castle Black is no place for a child, but I -
Why do you say that?
She shouldn't have said that.

When you were an infant, (Stands up and walks around his desk), the Dornish trailer landed on Dragonstone. His goods were junk except for one wooden doll. He'd even sewn a dress on it in the colors of our House. No doubt he'd heard of your birth, and assumed new fathers were easy targets. I still remember how you smiled when I put that doll in your cradle, (Shireen smiles), and you pressed it to your cheek. (Looks down. Shireen’s smile drops). By the time we burnt the doll, it was too late. (Walks to the table and adjusts the objects on it). I was told you would die. Or worse, the grayscale would go slow. (Turns to her). Let you grow just enough to know the world before taking it away from you. Everyone advised me to send you to the ruins of Valyria to live out your short life with the Stone men, before the sickness spread to the castle. I told them all to go to hell. (Shireen smiles). I called in every maester on this side of the world. Every healer, every apothecary. They stopped the disease and saved your life. Because you did not belong across the world with the bloody Stone men. (Walks towards her). You are the Princess Shireen of House Baratheon. And you are my daughter.
You refuse to tell me where you're going?
How long will you be gone?
You're abandoning me.
Then serve me.
My enemies think they've destroyed me. They're laughing at me the way Renly laughed at me. I want Joffrey dead. I want Robb Stark dead. Make me another son.
Why?
I'm not so easily killed. Men have been trying for years.
(whispering) I want you.
You told me your magic requires a king's blood.
I'm the one true king.
You're a literary man now. What do you make of that?

The usurper Joffrey Baratheon. I said those words when I tossed a leech into the fire. A leech filled with bastard blood. A bastard you set free. I am now faced with a great opportunity and I am powerless to take advantage of it.
What progress have you made?
House Peasebury.
House Musgood.
House Haigh. They don't have enough men between them to raid a pantry.
The Golden Company?
They're sellswords.
We don't have any gold.
If I do not press my claim, my claim will be forgotten. I will not become a page in someone else's history book. I'm running out of time, Ser Davos. Which means you're running out of time. -
Lord Commander.
(To Olly as he closes the door). I'd like to speak alone.
Very well. (They all sit down). Have you considered my offer?
Say the word and you will be.
I'm giving you a chance to avenge your family. To take back the castle where you grew up. To rule the North.
You're as stubborn as your father. And as honorable.
I didn't mean it as praise. Honor got your father killed. (Stands up). But if your mind’s made up, I won't try to persuade you.
Are you bored of us already?
I know it. We march on Winterfell within the fortnight. Before the snows trap us here.

They'd rather burn than fight for me, so be it. I leave their fate to you. You could execute them, that's the safest course. (After a moment). Or you could see if this Tormund fellow is more willing to compromise than Mance ever was. I assume the brothers of the Night's Watch would rather see the wildlings dead.
You're the Lord Commander. Your decision.
You have many enemies in Castle Black. Have you considered sending Alliser Thorne elsewhere? Give him command of  Eastwatch-by-the-Sea.
Whoever said that didn't have many enemies.
Send copies of that letter to every corner of the realm.
How did it fare with your pirate?
In my experience, pirates prefer fighting unarmed men.
It won't be a choice this time. Do you trust him?
Well if he does his job, he'll have his share. Leave us.
What did you say to him?
Why?
Yeah.
Tell your lord to burn them then.
My little brother has a hundred thousand men according to the scouts. Men whose allegiance rightly belongs to me.
Faith? In a real war, the side with the greater number wins nine times out of ten.
I cannot defeat my brother in the field, and I can't take King's Landing without the men he stole.
I've said the words, damn you! I burnt the idols.
I have a wife. I took a vow.
(Gasps). A son?
I ordered him to tear down his idols. He disobeyed.
A good deal more than you.
Meat's off.
Serve fish, then. We're on an island.
I hate a good many things, but I suffer them all the same.
Of course I remember.
She's a child.
She's my daughter. You will not strike her.

I ordered Mance Rayder burnt at the stake. You prevented that order from being carried out. You showed mercy to Mance Rayder. A king's word is law. Perhaps you should ask Ser Davos how much mercy I show to lawbreakers. Show too much kindness, people won't fear you. If people don't fear you, they don't follow you.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Who then? You?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Do you know this wretched girl Lyanna Mormont?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Lady of Bear Island, and a child of 10. I asked her to commit her house to my cause, that's her response.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: That amuses you?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Your bravery made him look weak. He'll punish you for it. I don't punish men for bravery. I reward them.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: You can give me the North.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Kneel before me. Lay your sword at my feet. Pledge me your service and you'll rise again as Jon Stark, Lord of Winterfell.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: For the night is dark and full of terror.
He wasn't my beloved brother. (Matthos looks up). I didn't love him, he didn't love me.
A lie. Take it out.
Jaime Lannister, the Kingslayer. Call him what he is.
Make it Ser Jaime Lannister, the Kingslayer. Whatever else he is, the man's still a knight.

When Eddard Stark learned the truth, he told only me. I'll not make the same mistake. Send copies of that letter to every corner of the Realm, from the Arbor to the Wall. The time has come to choose. Let no man claim ignorance as an excuse.
I'll not make peace with Renly while he calls himself King.

Who would steal the northern half of my kingdom. I've always served thieves according to their desserts, as you well know, Ser Davos. Joffrey, Renly, Robb Stark, they're all thieves. They'll bend the knee or I'll destroy them.
I heard you were dead.
I had hoped to speak to you alone, Your Grace.
We are alone.
I am fighting.
Take him to the dungeon and lock him in a cell.
You know who rules at Winterfell now?
The traitor who plunged a dagger into Robb Stark’s heart. Don’t you want to avenge him?

No matter. I shall take back the North from the thieves who stole it. Tywin Lannister is dead. He can’t protect them now. I shall mount Roose Bolton’s head on a spike. But if I’m to take Winterfell I’ll need more men.
I’m not talking about the damn Night’s Watch. I’m talking about the wildlings.
If they swear to follow me, I’ll pardon them. We’ll take Winterfell. Once the North is won, I’ll declare them citizens of the realm. I’ll give them land to live on.
I’ll offer them their lives and their freedom if Mance kneels before me and swears his loyalty.
You admire him, don’t you?
He likes you. Convince him to bend the knee...
...or he burns.
Nightfall. The sun drops fast this time of year. Hurry, Jon Snow.
Mance Rayder, you’ve been called the King Beyond the Wall. Westeros only has one king. Bend the knee, I promise you mercy.
Kneel and live.
I’ve trusted in your visions, in your prophecies for years.
Renly, you're no king.
Go on.
Not wildfire!
Yes.
Hyah! Hyah!
Lady Stark.
Your pardon, my lords.
In the brush above the encampment.
Why?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Absolutely not. I've got orders from Lord Bolton.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: To deliver you to your father at King's Landing.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I serve Lord Bolton. Any appreciation your father--
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I had nothing--
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Eyes open, lads.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Lord Bolton charged me with bringing him back to King's Landing alive and that's what I aim to do.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Father, you forget yourself. Lady Stark is –
Lord Stark, your presence has been requested in the small council chamber. A meeting has been called.
The King is at the small council meeting, my Lord. He has summoned you.
No, my Lord. I believe it concerns Daenerys Targaryen.
If you break anything, the Septa will have my head. What is...
Right, so there she is, high and mighty Queen Cersei, tits and ass jiggling in the breeze.

All of a sudden she looks my way. Gives me a little smile like, “Well?” So, all right, you know, I’ve never been shy and I’m blessed down south, so I put it right out. She’s never seen anything like it. I mean, she’s used to her brother, and from what I hear, Jaime Lannister’s half an inch shy of an inch. She sees it and I swear to the gods she licks her lips. Well, you all know me. You know I wouldn’t lie about this.
They're taking him to the Sept of Baelor.
The Hand of the King.
I fight and die for your glory, oh glorious queen.
You've got a lot to say about killing. Even more words than arrows.
Yes. But none of those were your Crow lover.
So you've said.
And more words. Know what I think you do when you see him? Serve him up a nice juicy slice of ginger minge.
Maybe I have.
How high are the walls?
You know how to get to Castle Black?
Those your parents? Open your eyes. I'm going to eat them. Do you hear me? I'm going to eat your dead mama and I'm going to eat your dead papa. Go tell the crows at Castle Black.
(Grunts)
Why does the meat down here taste so much better than it does on our side of the Wall?
(Chuckles) Maybe everything's just better fed down here.
Fat and lazy. Easier for us, hmm?
You didn't see us coming? Lost your warg? And that baby crow you had with you, lost him too?
She yours?
Too scrawny. Not like those crows at Castle Black.
Think of them stuck in their larders stuffing their faces with ham and blood sausage and stew, getting nice and fat and marbled.
I know we've had our differences, Tormund, but just one time before you die, you really ought to try crow.
I surrender. I surrender!
Yah! Left high, left low.
Right low, lunge right.
This way! Left, right.
And now you are a dead girl.
My tongue lied.
Watching is not seeing, dead girl.
And why is it that Lord Eddard is sending Lannister men in place of his own? I am wondering.
Are you men or snakes, that you would threaten a child?
I am Syrio Forel.
And you will be speaking to me with more respect.
Arya child, we are done with dancing for the day.
Be gone now, Arya.
The first sword of Braavos does not run.
What do we say to the God of death?
Go.
No?
You are troubled.
Good! Trouble is the perfect time for training. When you are dancing in the meadow with your dolls and kittens, this is not when fighting happens.
You’re not here. You’re with your trouble. If you’re with your trouble when fighting happens…
… More trouble for you. Just so… How can you be quick as a snake, or as quiet as a shadow, when you are somewhere else? You are fearing for your father, hmm? That is right. Do you pray to the gods?
The old and the new.
You are late, boy. Tomorrow you will be here at midday.
Your dancing master, Syrio Forel.
Tomorrow you will catch it. Now pick it up. That is not the way, boy. This is not a great sword that is needing two hands to swing it.

It is heavy as it needs to be to make you strong. Just so. One hand is all that is needed. Now you are standing all wrong. Turn your body side-face. So. You are skinny. That is good. The target is smaller. Now the grip... Let me see. The grip must be delicate.
The steel must be part of your arm. Can you drop part of your arm? No. Nine years Syrio Forel was first sword to the Sealord of Braavos. He knows these things. You must listen to me, boy.
Boy, girl... You are a sword, that is all. That is the grip. You are not holding a battle-axe. You are holding...

Ahhh… Just so. Now we will begin the dance. Remember, child, this is not the dance of the Westeros we are learning... The knight's dance, hacking and hammering. This is the Bravo's dance... The water dance. It is swift and sudden. All men are made of water, do you know this? If you pierce them, the water leaks out and they die. Now you will try to strike me.
Up! Dead. Dead. Very dead. Come. Again, faster.
Yes, Your Grace.
Father, smith, warrior, mother ,maiden, crone, stranger.
I am his and he is mine from this day until the end of my days.
[Looking at the band and talking to LOTHAR] They’re good, aren’t they?
Your mother is less in need of rescue than any woman I’ve ever met.
And I like her.  But if she had her way, I would be back in Volantis, playing my harp and you would be sitting over there [Looks at EDMURE and ROSLIN], eating blackberries out of Roslin Frey’s hand.
No, don’t.  Don’t insult them.
That is a very strange custom.
It seems normal to you?
No, but there are other ways of providing proof.
I don’t know.  But if it’s a boy, I know what we should name him.
Eddard. [Catches ROBB by surprise] Don’t you want to teach little Ned Stark how to ride horses?
Is she beautiful?
What's her name?
I'm sure you'll be very happy.
And you're marrying her for a bridge?
Ah.
When I speak to people from the North, they all loved your father.
Children do not always think that about their fathers, believe me.
I wish I could have met him.
(chuckles) Most lords worry more about their gold and their glory than the charwomen scrubbing their floors.
And you? You think I'm fighting this war so they'll sing songs about me? I want to go home. I want the men following me to go home.
Then why don't you?
Chopping off Joffrey's head, you mean.
Your pardon, my lord. Your Grace.
How are you?
I'm sorry. It was a stupid question. I'm sorry.
You have every right. You're a king.
What kind do you want to be?
I was raised to be a proper little lady, to play the harp and dance the latest steps and recite Valyrian poetry.
No. No, you would not.

When I was 12, my mother and father went to a wedding. Weddings in Volantis last for days, you know. And they left me with my little brother. The second afternoon they were gone was the hottest day in the three-year summer. We couldn't bear to be inside, so we ran down to the Rhoyne. Every child in Volantis was in the Rhoyne that day. The rich, the poor, we were all there. Naked, screaming, racing to the little islands. Drummers were playing for coppers on the east bank. I was treading water, talking to a friend, when I realized I hadn't seen my brother. I called his name. Then I started screaming his name. And then I saw him floating face down. My heart just stopped. I was I dragged him from the water. My friend helped me, I think. I don't even remember. He was so little. Then we pulled him onto the riverbank. And I screamed at him and I shook him. And he was dead. Just dead. A man ran over. He had a fish tattoo on his face. In Volantis, the slaves have tattoos. So you know what they are without having to talk to them. And this man worked on a fishing boat. And he pushed me out of the way. You have to understand, for a slave to push a highborn girl, that's death for the man, a terrible death. But he pushed me out of the way, and he started pressing on my brother's chest again and again and again, until my brother spat out half of the Rhoyne and cried out. And the man cradled his head and told him to be calm. I decided two things that day. I would not waste my years planning dances and masquerades with the other noble ladies. And when I came of age, I would never live in a slave city again. I'm sorry, Your Grace. You told me of your problems and I've blathered on.
I don't want you to marry her.
Your Grace. A minute of your time?
I've been treating your wounded men.
They're not my enemies.

I've already run through the supplies I brought with me. Some are easily replaced. Egg yolks, turpentine, oil of roses. But some are not. I need silk for stitching. I need fennel root for those with fever, willow bark. Mostly I need milk of the poppy. You saw what it was like to amputate a foot without any. I assume there will be more loss of limb before this war is over.
I know where to find them. I'm sorry, I shouldn't interrupt you.
You're riding to the Crag to negotiate a surrender?
The Crag will have a maester and he will have what I need.
If I could write a list.
I don't think that will be
I've spent the last two years nursing wounded men, watching them bleed and die. I'm not afraid of wet shits.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Attack. Attack.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: My mother.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Gaaa.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Rytsas.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: That was close enough.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Not yet.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Many surprises for her.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Will you come with me one day to Volantis?  When all this is over?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I promise. I know she'd love to meet you. And her grandchild.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Are you angry with me?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: And I have your little prince or princess inside me.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Don't get greedy.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Can you leave the war for one night?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Westerosi customs are still a bit foreign to me.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Why are you so sure I'm of noble birth?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: What if I told you my father sold lace on the Long Bridge, and my mother, my brother and I lived with him above our shop?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Not very noble to accuse a lady of dishonesty.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I always thought I was a brilliant liar.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Will we be here long?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I'm not a spy.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You're right. You've found me out. I'm writing a letter to the Lannisters. "The young wolf is on the move. " Perhaps you'd join me. If you've got time, of course, for, well
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Lady Stark.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Maegyr.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: An uncommon name here. An old name in Volantis. Excuse me, my lady. Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Your mother's right. If you do this, the Karstarks will abandon you.
And more boys will keep dying until this war is over. You need Karstark men to end it.
Come to bed.
Don't let him.
We could ride north. Take your land back from the Greyjoys. Wait out the winter.
Give them a new purpose.
Oh, I don't know. I don't even know where Winterfell is.
What is it?
Can you do it?
Shhh
The rot's set in.
Shh.
The rot will spread. If we don't take the foot now
Your men are not my men, my lord.
Talisa.
You want to know what side my family fights on?
That boy lost his foot on your orders.
That boy did?
Do you think he's friends with King Joffrey? He's a fisherman's son that grew up near Lannisport. He probably never held a spear before they shoved one in his hands a few months ago.
That should help his foot grow back.
You're going to kill Joffrey?
And then what?
So who will?
You're fighting to overthrow a king, and yet you have no plan for what comes after?
Volantis.
He was unlucky that you were.
Hold still.
I don't know. What do they say about him?
True.
True. You're a Lannister, aren't you?
Martyn Lannister. You've nothing to fear. My husband doesn't eat children. Unless it's a full moon. It's not a full moon tonight, is it? See? Nothing to fear.
My mother always told me you Westerosi were a grim lot. Grim, bearded, stinking barbarians that would row across the Narrow Sea and steal us from our beds.
I never thought I'd marry anyone at all.
Not until I met the king of the grim, bearded, stinking barbarians.
Lord Bolton.
Whoa, whoa.
I'm not afraid of her. May I help you, Lady Stark?
I'm sorry, I shouldn't have-
You've made them before?
Did they work?
(Nods.)
Which boy?
And he lived.
Talisa: She's your mother.
This needs to be cleaned and closed.
You're lucky to be alive.

Come, you’ll be wanting a bath after all that travel. Oh, and a dress for dinner. You can wear one of mine. What’s your color? Midnight blue or silver? Silver, maybe. Come. You can choose one of my bedrooms if you want.
Your father taught you to hunt? Our father would never teach us. I think our father could learn a thing or two from your father.
Get her up.
Karl Tanner from Gin Alley drinking wine from the skull of Jeor fucking Mormont.
Any command for us, Lord Commander? What's that? Fuck 'em till they're dead?
Did you hear that, boys? Fuck 'em till they're dead.
Rast.
Go outside and feed the beast.

You should shuty our fucking hole, ugly little cunt. You look likea fucking ballsack. Ugly. Look at your stupid cunt face. I could piss in any gutte rand soak five of you. Know how much they paid me to kill a man in King's Landing? Seven silvers. They told me a man's name and that man never saw daylight again. None of them cocksuckers got away from me.
Haven't lost a fight since I was nine. Maybe it's time. What do you think, eh?
Maybe you're the man. Eh, cunt?
I was a fucking legend in Gin Alley. A fucking legend! I would take any knight... any knight, any time. Fucking cunts in steel plate. Fucking cowards.
What the fuck is that?
What am I supposed to do with him? What did Craster do with 'em? Kill 'em before they could grow up and do the same to him? All right.
Don't need another mouth to feed. Hand him over.
To who?
Shut up!
So... if it worked for him... Let's give the walkers what they want.
Rast is headed that way.
Help him up.
This is nice. Fine leather. You're no wildling. Important. Highborn. Who are you?

You see, where I come from, a commoner like me slaps a little lord like you, I'd lose my right hand. But we're a long way from home, aren't we? And the two of you, fancy-looking folks north of the Wall creeping through the woods. Isn't that a bit odd?
I like your curly hair. My mom had curls like that. Beautiful brown curls. Why'd you drag a crippled boy all the way up here?
See you haven't played this game before. A highborn hostage, that's valuable. But three of them, that's a lot of mouths to feed.
Come here.
Who are you?
Who are you?
And I thought this was gonnabe another boring day.
(screams)
Please, my lord. It hurts.
Why? I did whatever you asked.
My lord, please.
Which way should I go?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: All the children will be going below when the time comes.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: But both me brothers were soldiers. I want to fight too.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: All right. I'll defend the crypt, then.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Tell me something: Do you still believe good soldiers make good kings?
(In unison) For the night is dark and full of terrors.
Remember what happens to children who run.  I'm your father and I'll do the talking.
Need a hand?
Dead rats don't squeak.
More than anyone you know.
That so?
That him?
Good.
You're very kind.  Someday it'll get you killed.
Best part of the animal.
Don’t worry, they’re still there.
You check every five minutes like you’re afraid they’re gonna move.

Of course you are.  You’re almost there and you’re afraid you won’t make it.  The closer you get, the worse the fear gets.  No point in trying to hide behind that face.  I know fear when I see it.  Seen it a lot.
That give you some ideas?
Go ahead then.  You might get away.  Might even make it there on your own.  They’re just over the river.  The closest you’ve been to family since Ilyn Payne snipped your daddy’s neck.
Whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa.
Got salt pork for the feast.
It doesn’t sound like it’s over.
Got pig’s feet, too [ARYA looks up].
It’s too late.
Rough night, Imp?
I didn’t pick you for a hunter.
It’s not hunting if you pay for it.
The girl is right. What a man sows on his name day, he reaps all year.
King in the North.
It's time.
I will kill that man. I don't care how many arrows they feather me with, how many spears they run through me, I will kill that horn-blowing cunt before I fall.
I know I'm surrounded. I know that because I stood on the battlements and saw I was surrounded.
Thank you, wise bald man. Thank you for explaining siege tactics to me.
No word from my father?
Send more ravens.
The first time I saw Winterfell

First time I saw Winterfell, it looked like something that had been here for thousands of years and would be here for thousands of years after I was dead. I saw it and I thought, "Of course, Ned Stark crushed our rebellion and killed my brothers. "We never stood a chance against the man who lives here."

Yes, my captors were so very kind to me. You love reminding me of that. Everyone in this frozen pile of shit has always loved reminding me of that. You know what it's like to be told how lucky you are to be someone's prisoner? To be told how much you owe them? And then to go back home to your real father
I will kill that man. I swear to the Drowned God, the Old Gods, the New Gods, to every fucking god in every fucking heaven, I will kill that man.
And what's your counsel, trusted friend?

There's nowhere to run. I'd never make it back to the Iron Islands. And even if I did, even if by some miracle I slipped through the lines and made it home, I'd be a coward. The Greyjoy who ran. The shame of the family.
I won't make it to the Wall. I won't make it 10 feet past the Winterfell gates.
The opportunity for Jon Snow to cut my throat in my sleep.
I've done a lot, haven't I? Things I never imagined myself doing.
You may be right. But I've gone too far to pretend to be anything else.
You hear that? That's the mating call of the Northmen. They want to fuck us. Well, I haven't had a good fuck in weeks. I'm ready for one.
They say every ironborn man is worth a dozen from the mainland.
You think they're right?

We die today, brothers. We die bleeding from 100 wounds with arrows in our necks and spears in our guts, but our war cries will echo through eternity. They will sing about the battle of Winterfell until the Iron Islands have slipped beneath the waves. Every man, woman and child will know who we were and how long we stood. Aggar and Gelmarr, Wex and Urzen, Stygg and Black Lorren. Ironborn warriors will cry out our names as they leap onto the shores of Seagard and Faircastle.
Mothers will name their sons for us.
Girls will think of us with their lovers inside them.
And whoever kills that fucking horn-blower will stand in bronze above the shores of Pyke!
What is dead may never die!
(cries)
Kill me.
Kill me.
Kill me!
(groans)
Theon Greyjoy.
Please.
Reek. My name is Reek.
Go with her. Please.
He’s coming back.
It was a long time ago.
I didn’t murder the Stark boys. But I did things that were just as bad or worse.
Not my claim. Hers.
I’m not fit to rule.
Our Uncle Euron returned home after a long absence. He murdered our father and took the Salt Throne from yara. He would have murdered us if we had stayed.
There are and Euron is building more. He’s going to offer them to you.
The Iron Fleet isn’t all he’s bringing. He also wants to give you --
He murdered our father and would have murdered us. He’ll murder you as soon as you have what he wants.
All of them.
Your ancestors defeated ours and took the Iron Islands. We ask you to give them back.
Envy isn't attractive.
You should be proud of your brother's achievement. I took the great castle of Winterfell with 20 men.
I treated the Stark boys with honor, and they repaid me with treachery.
Exactly.
They made me a promise
Don't call me
If I hadn't killed them, the Northerners would think me weak.
I'm warning you.
You haven't brought enough men. How am I supposed to defend Winterfell with just this lot?
Is this a joke? Winterfell is the heart of the North.
He won't find out. We've killed all the ravens. We have all the horses. I've taken Winterfell. And I will keep Winterfell.
I don't intend to die.
We should bury them.
If they haven't got the damn message yet, a few more days won't help. As you say. But it don't hurt to let 'em rot a while longer.
Gold for the farmer. For his trouble.
I'm Theon Greyjoy, son of Balon, heir to the Iron Islands.
I'm Reek.
I'm Reek.
Always. Forever.
Yes.
I was born on Pike to Balon Greyjoy. His third and last-living son. I am your prince. I swear it by the drowned god. What is dead may never die.
Lord Bolton took me captive after Winterfell. He sent me to treat with you in good faith as your fellow Ironborn.

You are fading from sickness. You are badly outnumbered and you're hundreds of miles from the sea. Lord Bolton implores you to protect your men and abandon this fortress you can no longer hold. Do this and he will be just and fair with you as he is being with me. The Boltons will give you safe passage to the stony shore.
My own father, your king, surrendered years ago to Robert Baratheon. I watched him bend the knee. There was no shame there. He fought with honor, as did you.
I'm sorry, I didn't hear...
*scared* You don't know.
*whispering* Reek. Reek.
Reek.
Yes. Have your men raise the white flag and open the gates. You're going home.
Yes m'lord. Will we go home now?
Not Theon. There is no Theon. Reek.

I was helping you. You were trying to escape, there is no escape. Not ever. Theon Greyjoy tried to escape. The master knew. He knows everything. He hunted him, he caught him, he strapped him to a cross, and cut away piece after piece until there was no Theon left.
I deserved everything. I deserve to be Reek. I did terrible things. Turned on Robb. Captured Winterfell. Killed those boys.
They weren’t “those boys”, they were Bran and Rickon. They were your brothers, you’d know them since they were born.
They weren’t. They were only--
I can’t..
I can’t, not unless the master says...
They weren’t...
They weren’t Bran and Rickon! I couldn’t find them. It was two farm boys. I killed them and burned them so no one would know.
I can’t talk to you anymore.
Not Theon, Reek!
A cripple? You let a cripple escape? The boy can't walk, but somehow he slipped past you?
The giant? Hodor? Oh, that's all right, then. You let a halfwit escape with a cripple. And Rickon, too? The little one?
Right. Get the horses and the hounds.
Enjoying your first hunt?
With hunting, there's blood at the end.
I was a little boy when I was torn away from my home and brought here. So I kept my word. I never ran away. If I find them soon enough, I won't hurt them. Well, I'll hurt them, but I won't kill them.
They have no value to me missing.
Robb's in the Riverlands. My sister's in Deepwood Motte. She'll get here long before they do. And Ned Stark always said 500 men could hold Winterfell again 10,000.
The hounds have the scent. Come, Maester, don't look so grim. It's all just a game.
Try again.

I'm looking at spending the rest of my life being treated like a fool and a eunuch by my own people. Ask yourself, is there anything I wouldn't do to stop that from happening? The hounds will find the scent again. I'll beat them until they do. I'll whip every man here until I have both those boys in hand. And once I've got them, the pampered little shits It's better to be cruel than weak.

Think harder.
(nods)

I told you what would happen. All of you. I told you what would happen if you served me loyally. And what would happen if you did not.

Jon.

You've always known what was right. Even when we were all young and stupid, you always knew. Every step you take it's always the right step.

All of us chose to follow Yara. We left the Iron Islands for Yara. She would never leave one of us behind. We're not leaving her behind.
She's not dead.
She's our queen

We're done with all that.

Text: If you serve me as loyally as you served Ned Stark, I will be as good to you as he ever was. Betray me, and you will wish you hadn't. Inform her that she's to bring You are a maester of the Citadel, sworn to serve the Lord of Winterfell, are you not?

Sansa of the House Stark comes here to be wed. A woman grown, trueborn and noble. She comes to beg the blessings of the gods. Who comes to claim her?

The Stony Shore, to raid their villages. There'll be spoils in it for you, and women, if you do your jobs well.
I do. Your captain.
You could do that, take the ship, head out on your own. And I will hunt you down, drag you back here in chains and hang you for a traitor.

Come on! Come on! Come on! Come on.

I’m his only living son. He’ll listen to me.
(Removes his jacket). Looks smaller.
(Removes more of his clothes). They'll be waiting for me on the docks.

Anyone who matters, (Takes off his shirt), this is a big day for them. They haven't had much to get excited about since I left. It's a hard place the Iron Islands always has been. It's cold and it's wet.
They say hard places breed hard men, (Unbuttons his pants), and hard men rule the world.
(Kneels down). Try smiling with your lips closed. (She does). Hmm, better.
(Taking off his pants). I don't blame him.
Quit talking about your father. He's right about us. Our wives from the Iron Islands, they're for breeding. That's not enough for us. That's why we take saltwives, from the women we capture.

I need to get to Pyke.
I bet you can.
None like you.
You don't know what you're like. You need someone to teach you. Know who I am?

I have a proposal for my father. One that will make him king again. And me after him. You may stay at the castle tonight, if you're lucky.
An order, from your future king. You can tell your grandchildren about this night.
Father.
A man. Your blood and your heir.
Lord Stark is gone.
What's done is done. I've brought you a proposal from Robb Stark.
If my clothes offend you, I will change them.
Gold.
(Steps forward). My blood is salt and iron.
The offer he makes, is one I proposed.
I've lived with him, hunted with him, fought at his side, he thinks of me as a brother.
I forget nothing. I remember my brothers. And I remember when my father was a king.
I will lead the attack myself.
I'm your son. Your only living heir. Who else?
I told you to wait outside. (The person who entered is revealed to be Yara). How did you get past the guards?

Stop! Stop!
(screaming) About what?

What truth?
I took it to-I took it-

Hold it. Rule it.
Why did you do that?
I took it to bring glory to my house and my father.
(panicking) Wait. Wait, wait, wait. I took it because I hated the Starks. I hated them for holding me prisoner. I wanted to hurt them.
What are you doing?
Please.
I'll tell you anything. Just take it off. Please. Please, just take it off.
Yara?
No, don't go. Please, please. No, please don't leave me. Please, please. Please, please don't go! Please. Please. Don't leave me. Please don't go.
A fine shot, master. My lady.
I'm sorry, my lord.
I don't know, my lord.
No, my lord. Just two farm boys.
Yes, my lord.
Jon Snow is at Castle Black.
We shouldn’t be lighting fires. It’s not safe. He won’t stop hunting us.
Jon will have me killed the moment I step through the gate.
And the truth about the farm boys I killed in their place? The truth about Ser Rodrik, who I beheaded?
And the truth about Rob, who I betrayed?
I don’t want to be forgiven. I can never make amends to your family for all the things that I’ve done. They’ll keep you safer than I ever could.
I would have taken you all the way to the Wall. I would have died to get you there.
May I take one of the horses?
Home.
It's fine.
Whatever my queen demands.
My queen.

It’s a freak.

I take orders from your father, not you.

It’s for the queen, I bet. I hear she’s [sleek as a mink?]
Think of all those southern girls he gets to stab with his right royal prick.
A word, Your Grace?
It's not so bad once you get used to it.
The Lannisters are going to reject your terms, you know?

We can fight them in the fields as long as you like, but we won't beat them until you take King's Landing. And we can't take King's Landing without ships. My father has ships and men who know how to sail them.

Men who fought King Robert to free themselves from the yoke of the South, just like you're doing now. I'm his only living son. He'll listen to me. I know he will. I'm not a Stark. I know that. But your father raised me to be an honorable man. We can avenge him together.
We can’t stop.
We have to cross here.
It’s the only way to throw off the hounds.
I’ve seen what his hounds do to a person. This way is better.
Over here. Come on.
Sansa. Sansa.
Stay here. I’ll lure them away.
You will. Go north, only north. Jon is Lord Commander at Castle Black. He’ll help you.
Dead.
She broke her leg jumping from the ramparts. I left her to die in the snow.
Daenerys went north.
You're my queen. I go where you command.
What is dead may never die.
Am l your brother, now and always?
(takes out his sword, bows to Robb) My sword is yours in victory and defeat, from this day until my last day.
Or so Walder Frey would have you think.
Just march up to his gates and tell him you're crossing. We've got five times his numbers. You can take the Twins if you have to.
Or throw you in a dungeon. Or slit your throat.
Kill him, Robb. Send his head to his father. He cut down ten of our men. You saw him.
The bards will sing songs of their sacrifice.
Are you afraid?
Good.
It means you're not stupid.
We had not thought to meet you here, My Lady.
Lord Frey controls that bridge.
Robb's right.
You're a very lucky girl. Do you know that?
The Iron Islands.
You've never heard of the Iron Islands?
Trust me, My Lord. You're not living in the wilderness anymore.
Lord.
Why? What do you mean why? My father is Balon Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands.
I will be lord after my father.
No, you...You having a go at me? Is that it?
I'm not a Southerner.
You're an impudent little wench, aren't you?
Impudent. It means rude. Disrespectful.
I thought she was our prisoner.
When are you gonna tell him?
Blood for blood. You need to make the Lannisters to pay for Jory and the others.
I’m talking about justice.
A Lannister put his spear through your father’s leg. The Kingslayer rides to Casterly Rock where no one can touch -
You’re not a boy anymore. They attacked your father. They’ve already started the war. It’s your duty to represent your House when your father can’t.
I don’t know. It’s not my House.
Tough little lad. In the Iron Islands, you’re not a man until you’ve killed your first enemy. Well done.
He would have killed you and cut Bran’s throat.
To what, to save your brother’s life? It was the only thing to do, so I did it.
Stop! Stop! What are you doing?!
In a turnip cart?
And you can afford that?
There’s a thousand girls like you in King’s Landing.
Yes, you’ll be very popular until some fat lord comes to visit with a big belly and a little prick and he can’t get it up so he knocks all your teeth out.
Don’t be a fool.
Let me see it one more time?
I’m going to miss you!
Famed for their skills at archery, navigation and lovemaking.
Shh... Keep it down. You're not supposed to be inside the castle walls.
Important enough for the likes of you.
Who? the Imp? I'd call him half a nobleman.
Why should I be jealous?

Hmm?

I’m not a boy.
I don’t want to pay for it.

My lady's whereabouts...

Careful, Imp.
(Theon joins a little late). What's dead may never die.
Saw what, my lady?
If it comes to that, you know I'll stand behind you.
Pardon me, Your Grace. My queen.
Some oysters, girl! I’m talking to you! Girl?! Are you deaf? Girl?!
Your oysters fresh?
You wouldn’t lie to an old man would you?
Very nice. Give me 4. With vinegar.
Can’t do it.
Come on.
What the fuck you doing here?
Hanging them.
You’ve got friends?
No, no, no.
We’re not butchers. We hang them.
They die.
You’re getting old, Clegane.

Clegane, we’re here for a reason. The Lord of Light is keeping Beric alive for a reason. He gave a failed, drunk priest the power to bring him back for a reason. We are part of something larger than ourselves.
Not talking, eh? That's a first.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: What do you say, boys? Time for a lion hunt?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: It will still be west of here two days from now.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Valar dohaeris. (speaks Valyrian) I don't see many priestesses of R'hollor in the Riverlands.
(nods)
(speaks Valyrian) I failed.
(speaks Valyrian) You worship Him in your way, and I'll worship Him in mine. Do you speak the common tongue?
My friends here don't speak High Valyrian. Why are you here, my lady?
Lucky for the ladies.
Six.
(speaks Valerian) The Lord has smiled upon me.
(speaks Valerian) I have no power. I ask the Lord for His favor, and He responds as He will.

I've always been a terrible priest. Drank too much rum. Fucked all the whores in King's Landing. It's a terrible thing to say, but by the time I came to Westeros, I didn't believe in our Lord. I decided that He, that all the gods, were stories we told the children to make them behave. So I wore the robes and every now and then I'd recite the prayers, but it was just for show. A spectacle for the locals. Until the Mountain drove a lance through this one's heart. I knelt beside his cold body and said the old words. Not because I believed in them, but he was my friend and he was dead. And they were the only words I knew. And for the first time in my life, the Lord replied. Beric's eyes opened and I knew the truth. Our god is the one true god. And all men must serve Him.
We're doing it for both, girl. We can't defend the people without weapons and horses and food. And we can't get weapons and horses and food without gold.
Are you still mad at us, boy?
Priestess.  I'll admit, it is a subtle distinction.
She needed your blood.
Good lad.
Are you sure?
Lord, cast your light upon us.
Show us the truth. Strike this man down if he is guilty. Give strength to his sword if he is true. Lord of Light, give us wisdom. For the night is dark and full of terrors.
Get back.
Lord, cast your light upon this man, your servant. Bring him back from death and darkness. His flame has been extinguished. Restore it.
For the night is dark and full of terrors. Lord, cast--
Bring him back from death and darkness
It says it clearly right there on that note you'll be repaid in full when the war is over.
At first light we'll ride for Riverrun. Your brother's there now. He'll make a contribution to our cause and you can go home.
Don't think of it that way.
It is. And it isn't.
Beric admired your father a great deal, you know. He wanted to refuse your ransom altogether.
We need the gold.
Five, I think. No, this makes six.
Then it was an arrow in the back. And that axe in the side.
Both. Fuckers couldn't decide.
And the Hound makes six.
You'd think you'd learn. It's not getting any easier, you know.
I don't think it works that way, child.
Ser Jorah Mormont --
They won't give me anything to drink down here.  I haven't been feeling like myself.
Is it worse than sitting in a freezing cell waiting to die?
I do apologize, little lady, but it's better for you if you don't see where we're going.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Blackstrap rum.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Not easy finding molasses in wartime.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Let's go home.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Somewhere neither wolves nor lions come prowling.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You'll die soon enough, dog. But it won't be murder, only justice.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: House Clegane was built upon dead children. I saw them lay Prince Aegon and Princess Rhaenys before the Iron Throne.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Doesn't have to look pretty. It just has to keep the arrows out of my heart.
You're not our prisoner, little lady.
Our guest. No one's put any chains on you.
These woods aren't safe for Ned Stark's daughter. ou're lucky we found you.
A lion still has claws  HOT PIE:  A minstrel's got gold sometimes. We could jump him, tie him up, steal his gold, and buy some food.
And so he spoke, and so he spoke That Lord of Castamere But now the rains Weep o'er his hall With no one there to hear.
What's lurking behind that wall? A lion? A wolf?
Loose a few more shafts.
Put the sword down, girl.
You're a dangerous person. I like dangerous people. Why are your friends so shy?
Three young ones on the run carrying castle-forged swords. You escape from Harrenhal?
Thoros of Myr. And the fellow here with the bow is Anguy.
The Brotherhood without Banners. Now come along. I want to hear how two boys and a very dangerous girl escaped Harrenhal.

You've got nothing to fear from us, son. The lords of Westeros want to burn the countryside. We're trying to save it. Now come on. We'll talk some more over brown bread and stew. And then you can go on your way.
Maybe he's the reason half the country's starving.
There's no story so good a drink won't make it better.
You see? They've all suffered through my bouts of sobriety. It's very tedious for all concerned. Now, how did three children-
How did three young persons such as yourselves, untrained in the art of war, escape from Harrenhal?
A smith, eh? Where'd you train?
That criminal. He charges twice as much as every other armorer in the city.
Aha! A smith and a salesman.
Ah, fought your way out of Harrenhal, I see.
To your brothers!
You can finish your meals before you go. It may be awhile before you see another.
I gave you my word.
Now that is an uncommonly large person. How does one manage to subdue such an uncommonly large person?
Poor man. You have my sympathy.
Aha, not a man at all. A Hound!
So good to see you again, Clegane.
Drinking and talking too much. Same as ever.
A pretty prize, lads.
Bad night to be outdoors.
You're a grouchy old bear, aren't you, Clegane? You want some rum?
Why are you always in such a foul mood?
For a big, hard man, you scare easy.
Come on. Maybe they've got some ale hidden away.
See if there's a larder. They always leave something behind.
Clegane.
Come over here. Don't worry. The fire won't bite. I want to show you something.
Look into the flames.
You saw me bring him back from the dead after you cut him down. Don't you want to know what gave me the power?
We can't tell you. Only the fire can tell you.
What do you see?
Keep looking. What do you see?
What else?
What the hell are you doing, Clegane?
You knew these people.
Enjoying yourself?
He touched you.
He touched you.
He knows you are here. He’ll come for you.
He can now. His mark is on you. You must leave, all of you.
The time has come.
For you to become me.
No.
Listen to your friend, Brandon.
The time has come. Leave me.
The man beside him is Howland Reed, Meera’s father.
The Sword of the Morning.
Far better.
Did he?
That’s enough for one day. We’ll visit again another time.
Time to go.
No.
Maybe. Maybe he heard the wind.
The past is already written. The ink is dry.
I’ve told you many times, stay too long where you don’t belong and you will never return.
You think I wanted to sit here for 1,000 years watching the world from a distance as the roots grew through me?
I was waiting for you.
I don’t blame you. You won’t be here forever. You won’t be an old man in a tree. But before you leave, you must learn.
Everything.
Look for me... beneath the tree.
North.
So were you once.
It is beautiful beneath the sea, but if you stay too long, you’ll drown.
It is time to go.
Is there gold and silver in the village?
Gems?
Where is the Brotherhood?
Where is the Brotherhood?
Where is the Brotherhood?
Which of the villagers aided them?
Who?
Who?
Who?
Who helped him?
That's better.  You've been very helpful.
Is there gold or silver in the village?
Where is the Brotherhood?
There are no goats, halfman.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: BRAAVOS
The former Hand did call on me, my Lord, several times. I regret to say he did not honor me with his patronage.
He always came to see the boy.
As you wish, my Lord. Gendry! Here he is. Strong for his age. He works hard. Show the Hand the helmet you made, lad.
Boy, this is the King's Hand! If his lordship wants the helmet...
Forgive him, my Lord.
Khuu! Khuu! Khuu!
Yes, My Lord.
All right. I’m ready.
They're still fighting.
The Kingswood?
Like what?
Stags aren't evil. They only eat grass.

Lords and ladies, the Faith and the Crown are the two pillars that hold up this world. One collapses, so does the other. The Father judges us all. If you break his laws...you will be punished. After conferring with His Holiness the High Septon, we have determined that Loras Tyrell and Cersei lannister’s trial will be held in the Great Sept of Baelor on the first day of the Festival of the Mother.

Furthermore, after much prayer and reflection, the Crown has decided that from this day forward, trial by combat will be forbidden throughout the Seven Kingdoms. The tradition is a brutish one, a scheme devised by corrupt rulers in order to avoid true judgment from the gods. Cersei Lannister and Loras Tyrell will stand trial before seven septons as it was in the earliest days of the Faith. Seven blessings to all.
She’s in a prison cell and there’s nothing I can do. I am the king! The queen is in prison and there’s nothing I can do!
I’ll call in the army. I’ll take back the sept and kill every last one of them. I’ll start a war if I have to.
I love her. I love her. I can’t help her.
I will speak to the high sparrow. There must be something--
I know.

I, Tommen of the House Baratheon, First of my Name, King of the Andals and the First Men, and Lord of the Seven Kingdoms, do hereby recuse myself from this trial. Tywin of the House Lannister, Hand of the King, Protector of the Realm, will sit as judge in my stead. And with him Prince Oberyn of the House Martell and Lord Mace of the House Tyrell. And if found guilty... may the gods punish the accused.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Is there no other way?
Of course I’m afraid for her! It just seems very dangerous.
She’s always been very devoted to the poor and the unfortunate.
Have they hurt you? Have they mistreated you?
I’ve missed you. More than you could know.
Yes, a few times. Have you?
What are you talking about? You’re a good person. You’ve always been a good person. You’re the best person I know.
I was just telling him about your devotion to the poor.
I don’t understand.
What about Loras?
The crown and the faith are the twin pillars upon which the world rests. Together we will restore the Seven Kingdoms to glory.
When you attack the Faith, you attack the Crown. Anyone who attacks the Crown is unfit to serve as Lord Commander of the Kingsguard.
I have to answer to the gods.
The Crown’s decision on this matter is final.
You have served your house and your king faithfully for many years. And you will continue to do so. But not in this city.
Ser Pounce?
How did you get past the Kingsguard?
I don't think you're supposed to be here. Mother doesn't allow me to have visitors at night.
Yes. But if my mother found out...
Yes? Tell me a secret.
That's Ser Pounce.
Joffrey didn't like him. He threatened to skin him alive and mix his innards up in my food so I wouldn't know I was eating him.
No. I don't think I am.
We say our vows in front of the High Septon. And after the ceremony, there's a feast.
I, I don't know. I, I didn't order it.
(Standing up). Are you saying my mother is behind this?
Aren't you and mother getting along?
(Takes her hand). Of course I do. You're my Queen.
I'll set him free for you.
I demand that Ser Loras be freed now!
Well, no... But you armed the Faith Militant. You gave the High Sparrow an army.
Then can I tell Margaery you'll have Ser Loras released?
You mean, kill them? Here, in the sept?
We'll find another way.
My Queen?
There was no way to free Ser Loras without violence.
I'm going to speak to the High Sparrow.
I--I don't know.
He was praying just now.
Will you come back later? (Margaery stops).
Of course.
Thank you for your counsel, Grand Maester. That will be all for now.
I’ve been thinking about the High Sparrow.

We need to be careful in dealing with such a man. To prevent things from escalating any further. We have to be careful not to antagonize him. He has Margaery. We can’t put her at risk. He’s dangerous.
You don’t like Margaery, do you?
Mother...there’s something I want to tell you. Something the High Sparrow told me.
I promised him I wouldn’t tell anyone. If he found out I told --
Is Joffrey going to kill Sansa's brother?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: No. I don't think so.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: It means I'll become king.
A good king?
Holiness?
Justice.
No.
What about strength?
Wisdom.
Wisdom is what makes a good king.
No.
A king needs a queen.
Yes.
I don't think so. It's all relatively straightforward.
I'm all right.
Did I hurt you?
It all happened so fast.
I was scared maybe I hurt you, it sounded
This is what I want to do all day, every day for the rest of my life.
Of course not. Are you hungry? Shall I have them bring you some cake or pomegranate juice?
King Tommen. It still sounds strange to me. Does Queen Margaery sound strange to you?
Wife. (They laugh). Sometimes it feels odd. I'm the king, I've married the most beautiful woman in the world, (She chuckles), and it's all because my brother died.
I don't feel guilty. That's what's odd.
Do you like to sail?
I do, too.
I do, too.
I don't think so. She told me never to trust anyone in King's Landing.
Well, but I'm a man now.
I suppose.
Do you ever miss Casterly Rock?
That's where you grew up. (They stop). You always told me that you liked the people there better. You said that King's Landing smelled of horse dung and sour milk.
The way that you talked about it, I always thought that you missed it. That you... That it was your real home.
