I want you to be happy, mother.
But wouldn't you be happier in Casterly Rock?
My mother would like to see her daughter’s final resting place.
When will it be possible?
You’ve cut off her hair and marched her naked through the streets in front of the whole city. That wasn’t a full atonement?
I want you to let her see Myrcella’s resting place.
I am the king.
And what does that mean to you?
My mother who is unclean? My mother who still needs to atone after all you’ve put her through?
Yes.
Of course.
I still don’t understand why you want to put her through any more than she's already endured.
My grandfather once said something similar. Except for the part about the gods.
She grew up.
Have we caught Prince Trystane’s killers? I expect it was Mother.
Yes, she would.
Of course not.
They told me she wouldn’t be allowed in the sept. If she had tried --
Don’t you think I know that? I’m sorry. But I can’t.
When the Faith Militant seized her and Margaery, what did I do? When they paraded her through the streets like a whore, what did I do?
The king is supposed to be the Protector of the Realm. If I can’t even protect my own wife or my own mother, what good am I?
I want to see my wife.
Mother.
Gold.
I know I should have come sooner. I wanted to. I was wrong.
No, it isn’t.

I should have executed all of them. I should have pulled down the sept onto the High Sparrow’s head before I let them do that to you, as you would have for me. You raised me to be strong...and I wasn’t. But I want to be. Help me.
Your old blind man patched me up. Why?
You want me alive so you can torture me?
So how do we die? Hanging? Beheading? Drop us from the top of the wall?
Who decides?
He your king now?
You spent too much time with us, Jon Snow. You can never be a kneeler again.
Words? What kind of words?
Do what?
The dead can't hear us, boy.
Snow. Did you love her? She loved you.
No. All she ever talked about was killing you. That's how I know. She belongs in the North. The real North. You understand me?
We didn’t invade. We were invited.
What’s one old man doing with eight horses?
And proper steel.
I hope so.  Killing crows in their castle is tough.  Killing them out here in the open, that’s what we do.
Spread out.  Surround the hut and move in.
Clear the barn!
Thunder.
Maybe it's ghosts.
That won’t help you, grandpa.
Where you riding?
No, it doesn’t matter now.
You understand.
Go on, boy.
Go on!
Kill him!
He’s one of them!
Do you hear me?
You’re not gonna die for one of them.
Did I ever tell you about her? My Sheila?

Oh that was a night to remember. Of course, I'd had a good bit to drink *laughs* the fangs were sharp, but she knew how to use them. And she was nice and soft down below. No, she was no ordinary beast. Many's the man who--
We could be waiting here a while.
Let's kill some Crows!
*yells* Come on! *puts out the fire*
You, over there.
This is how a man ends.
I should've thrown you from the top of your wall boy!
It’s not his men that worry me. It’s his horses. I know what mounted knights can do to us.
You and Stannis cut through us like piss through snow.
Good.
Rest, Jon Snow. We need you sharp tomorrow.
I’ve never seen these Bolton fuckers fight. And they’ve never seen the free folk fight, so, yes, there’s hope. You want to avenge your king, don’t you?
You saw these demons?

Oh. Well, you loved that cunt Stannis and I loved the man he burned. Mance didn’t have demons in his skull. He didn’t torch people or listen to some red witch. I believed in him. I thought he was the man to lead us through the Long Night. But I was wrong just like you.
Jon Snow’s not a king.
I need a good drink to help me sleep the night before a fight. You want some? I have a jug of sour goat’s milk stronger than any of that grape water you southern twats like sucking on.
So what do you do all night?
Happy shitting.
Run and fight!
Hey!
Fuck this, come on!
You trust me Jon Snow?
We are fools together now.
Lord of Bones. Been a long time.
War.
We should gather the elders find somewhere quiet to talk.
I’m not giving an order.
I don’t fight for the crows.
Gather the elders and let’s talk.

None of you saw Mance die! I did. The Southern King who broke our army, Stannis, wanted to burn him alive to send him a message. Jon Snow defied that cunt’s orders. His arrow was mercy. What it did took courage, and that’s what we need today: the courage to make peace with men we’ve been fighting for generations.
He’s prettier than both my daughters, but he knows how to fight. He’s young, but he knows how to lead. He didn’t have to come to Hardhome. He came because he needs us. And we need him.
This is the way.
I’m not good at counting.
The free folk are stubborn. You know how long it took Mance to band them together. 20 fucking years.
They’re running out of food and there’s nothing to hunt. They’ll come around.
If they get through, everyone dies !
Wun Wun, to the sea.
Most men fuck like dogs.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: No grace, no skill. A few dozen thrusts and done. You need to be patient. Give her time. Your cock shouldn't go near her till she's slick as a baby seal. And then you go inside, but slowly. Don't jam it in like you're spearing a pig. (laughts) Come on! Hey, come on.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: If it weren’t for him, none of us would be here. All of you would be meat in the Night King’s army. And I’d be a pile of charred bones just like Mance.

The crows killed him because he spoke for the free folk when no other southerners would. He died for us. If we are not willing to do the same for him, we’re cowards. And if that’s what we are, we deserve to be the last of the free folk.
We’re not clever like you southerners. When we say we’ll do something, we do it.
Yeah. The crows keep telling me I'll get used to it.
Come on!  Run! Run!
Used to be you couldn't find a tree within a mile of the Wall. Crows would come out every morning with axes.
Sink your metal deep and make sure it holds before taking your next step. And if you fall, don't scream. You don't want that to be the last thing she remembers.
Look out!
Just seeing if you can take a hit, lad.
(grunts)
(grunts)
No!
Beautiful, eh?  I can breath again.  Down south the air smells like pig shit.
I've been to Winterfell.
You have to keep moving.  That's the secret.  Walking is good, fighting is better, fucking is best.
We have to make due with what we've got.
This one is maybe not so smart.
Good.  That's more important than being smart.
Smart people don't come up here looking for the dead.  So, you met this Dragon Queen, huh?  And?

You spent too much time with the free folk and now you don't like kneeling.  Mance Rayder was a great man, a proud man.  The king beyond the wall never bent the knee.  How many of his people died for his pride?
You’re the one they call the dog.
They told me you were mean.  Were you born mean or you just hate Wildlings?
Gingers are beautiful.  We are kissed by fire.  Just like you.
Did you trip into the fire when you were a baby?
Ever since you've been mean.
I don't think you're truly mean.  You have sad eyes.
Dick?
Ah.  Dick.  I like it.
Nope.  It's pussy for me.  I have a beauty waiting for me back at Winterfell.  If I ever get back there.  Yellow hair.  Blue eyes.  Tallest woman you've ever seen.  Almost as tall as you.
You know her?
Well, not with her yet, but I see the way she looks at me.
You do know her.
I want to make babies with her.  Think of the great big monsters.  They'd conquer the world.
I'm good at killing people.
Orell says there are crows patrolling on the Wall. Tell me what you know.
(sighs)  There are 19 castles guarding the Wall. How many are manned?
How many men remain in Castle Black?
I like you, boy. But if you lie to me, I'll pull your guts out through your throat.
We'll find out soon enough.
They followed Mance. They won't follow anyone else.
Hard to lead when you're in chains.
Why would you do that?
You sure seemed like my enemy when you were killing my friends.
And now everything is going to change?
Why now?
What would you have me do, (Leans forward), Lord Commander?
They won't kneel for you and neither will I.
The day I ask my people to fight with the crows is the day my people cut my guts from my belly and make me eat them.
(Stands up). Easy thing to say to a man in chains.
Most of them are at Hardhome. You know where that is?
We'll need ships.

All right, then. You're coming with me. You're the Lord Commander of the Night's Watch. They need to hear it from you. They need to know the ships they are boarding won’t be torched in the middle of the sea. You come with me, or I don't go.
Isn't it your job to talk him out of stupid fucking ideas like this?
How many queens are there now?
And you need to convince the one with the dragons or the one who fucks her brother?
How many men did you bring?
The big woman?
You are.
You really want to go out there again?
You're not the only ones.
My scouts found them a mile south of the Wall.  Said they were on their way here.
They want to go beyond the Wall too.
You're a fucking Mormont?  Like the last Lord Commander?
He hunted us like animals.
How many men does he have in his army?
That can march and fight? 2,000. The rest are children and old people.
All of it.
We have to celebrate our victory.
Yes, it is.
Ah! To the Dragon Queen!
I'm fine!
I saw him riding that thing.
No. No. I saw him riding that thing.
I did. That's why we all agreed to follow him. That's the kind of man he is.
(laughing) He's little but he's strong. Strong enough to befriend an enemy and get murdered for it!
Most people get bloody murdered, they stay that way.
Not this one.
Ah! He comes back and keeps fighting. Here, north of the Wall, and then back here again. He keeps fighting. He keeps fighting.
He climbed on a fucking dragon and fought. What kind of person climbs on a fucking dragon? A madman or a king!
We did it! We faced those icy fucks. Looked right into their blue eyes, and here we are. Now, which one of you cowards shit in my pants?
Maybe you should be.
Well, Clegane it's time to drown our sorrows.
You're not gonna ride the dragon south?
(scoffs). You weigh as much as two fleas fucking.
I'm taking the Free Folk home. We've had enough of the south. The women down here don't like me.
It isn't home. We need room to wander. I'll take them back through Castle Black as soon as the winter storms pass. Back where we belong.
So would you.
You never know.
You've got the North in you. The real North.
We're finally going to war, old friend?
We'll meet again.
They think you’re some kind of god. The man who returned from the dead.
I know that.
I saw your pecker. What kind of god would have a pecker that small?
Shouldn't be long now.
Until you.
Took a lot of knives. I’ll have my men get the wood for a fire. Bodies to burn.
My little crow.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Almost.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: The dead got there first.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: We had to travel around them to get here. Whoever's not here now is with them.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Before the sun comes up tomorrow.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: The big woman still here?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: We're all going to die.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: But at least we die together.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: It could be our last night in this world, you know.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Brought my own.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: They call you "King Killer."
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: They call me "Giantsbane." Want to know why?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I killed a giant when I was 10. Then I climbed right into bed with his wife.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: When she woke up, you know what she did? Suckled me at her teat for three months. Thought I was her baby. That's how I got so strong. Giant's milk.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: She's not a ser? You're not a knight?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Why not?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Fuck tradition.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I'm no king. But if I were, I'd knight you 10 times over.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Mm-mm.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I smell a crow.
What do we want with a baby crow?
That half-handed cunt killed friends of mine. Friends twice your size.
Plenty of little men tried to put their swords through my heart. And there's plenty of little skeletons buried in the woods. What's your name, boy?
Your Grace?
Don't panic, boy. This isn't the damned Night's Watch where we make you swear off girls.
Can't believe this pup killed the Halfhand.
You plan on killing all the crows yourself?
We wait for Mance's orders.
And what is it you want? March on Castle Black with just this lot? Your pretty crow said 1,000 men are nesting there.
He is? Shouldn't it be "he was"? You said you put three arrows in him.
I've seen you slip a shaft through a rabbit's eye at 200 yards. If that boy's still walking, it's 'cause you let him go.
Thenns. I fucking hate Thenns.
Mance sent you?
How did you find us?
You came from the south, not the north.
Help yourself.
I'll answer to Mance. I won't answer to you.
You want us to man the castles for you?
Then that's where I'll go. Looks like we're the Night's Watch now.
You're a lucky man.
I've always had blue eyes!
His army's between us and Winterfell. We're on foot.
If the horses last, we'll get there before the dead.
We just have to hope the Night King doesn't come first-
Don’t.
Did you really think that cunt would fight you man to man?
I want to do this.
Tell us the truth.
I want the truth.
Why'd you take Winterfell?
Who gave the orders?
Why?
What were you going to do once you took it?
Good. That's very good.
I told you. Why did you take Winterfell?
Half man! Half man! Half man! Half man! Half man! Half man!
You're beautiful.
A fleabite.
I have learned the value of mercy from my father. I will set your man free.
On one condition.
I’m going to ask him tomorrow.
He won’t. You’ve waited long enough. I want you to be my wife now. What is it?
Why not?
You are going to be my wife. I am going to be your husband. We are allowed to walk through the gardens together.
I like the way your eyes go squinty when you’re jealous.
I am Trystane Martell.
We weren’t expecting you, Ser Jaime.
I told you I’m not hungry.
We are family. I don’t want to hurt you.
My future queen.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Too close to Casterly Rock.
Burn the villages, burn the farms. Let them know what it means to choose the wrong side.
A stonemason who could read? Hmm.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: The crown may call its first witness.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You're excused, Lord Varys.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Welcome to the Iron Bank. Please, sit.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: What can we do for you, Lord Stannis?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: The Iron Throne is currently occupied by Tommen of the House Baratheon, King of the Andals and the First Men, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Yes, we have heard this story.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: The king's grandfather tells a different story. A story about a jealous uncle whose attempts to usurp the throne from the rightful king cost the Seven Kingdoms dearly in blood and gold.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: And you feel your blood gives you a claim on our gold?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Across the Narrow Sea, your books are filled with words like "usurper" and "madman" and "blood right." Here, our books are filled with numbers. We prefer the stories they tell. More plain. Less open to interpretation. How many fighting men remain loyal to you?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: And how many ships do you have? The ones still afloat, Ser Davos, not at the bottom of Blackwater Bay.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: And how much wheat and barley and beef and pork do you produce on Dragonstone to feed your 4,000 men on your 32 ships?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You can see why these numbers seem unlikely to add up to a happy ending from our perspective. I'm afraid we must respectfully decline your request. But we thank you for paying us the honor of your visit.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I'm not a lord, Ser Davos Seaworth. You would not be either here. In Braavos, thieves are not rewarded with titles.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: The war is over.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Ser Davos...
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Tywin Lannister.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men
The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: That is a problem for another time.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I must say, I don't think the Iron Bank has ever had a debt of this magnitude repaid in a single installment.  I always considered your father a very effective and efficient man, but you appear to be redefining those terms entirely.

I am neither kind, nor a lord, Your Grace.  I am merely an instrument of the institution I represent, its wellbeing is a matter of arithmetic, not sentiment.  And the current arithmetic is outstanding.  The gold --
Some at the Iron Bank will be disappointed.  They've grown rather fond of your interest payments.
Yes.  Perhaps we could be of assistance in some current venture.
I see a great deal of potential in that venture.  I imagine that it would require outside investment?
I know them well.  They've helped us recover significant sums from parties who had fallen into deep arrears.
Rest assured, Your Grace, you can count on the Iron Bank's support, as soon as the gold arrives.
No, thank you.
My condolences, Your Grace, on the death of your son.  From all reports he was a fine young man.

Condolences and congratulations.  You've become the first ruling queen in the Seven Kingdoms.  That's quite an accomplishment.  The Iron Bank appreciates how you cast off the yoke of superstition freeing the crown from elements that sought to subvert the rule of law.
Indeed.  But sometimes tragedies are necessary to restore order and rational leadership.
Your father never minced words either.  But, yes, your debts are considerable.  And you are now engaged in a conflict on several fronts.  We both know how expensive war can be.
Your wealthiest ally is the Tyrells who are now your enemies.  You're surrounded on all sides by rivals for the throne.
We don't make bets.  We invest in endeavors we deem likely to be successful.
Euron Greyjoy's armada owns the Narrow Sea.
For now.  Daenerys Targaryen has three full grown dragons.  How well do wooden ships fare against fire breathing dragons?
The slave trade has entered a down turn, it's true.
Your father's daughter indeed.
Maybe I’ll come visit you.
You’re going to miss.
You will. You’re thinking too much. Now you’re nervous. “What if she’s right?”
Now it’s my turn.
Say it one more time, handsome. Who am I?
And that’s the truth!
He’s got a good voice.
How’s your arm?
You think you’re leaving Dorne?
Thank you.
I’m not the most beautiful woman you’ve ever seen?
Name one more beautiful than I am.
There was a what? In King’s Landing you were saying.
There was a woman more beautiful than I am.
How’s your arm now?
And how about your head?
Your nose is bleeding.
My dagger was coated with a special ointment from Asshei. They call it the Long Farewell. It takes time to work, but if a single drop makes contact with the skin, death.
The only antidote. Who’s the most beautiful woman in the world?
Sorry? Who?
Don’t drop it.
I think you’re very handsome as well.
Nama!
(Walks towards her). I'm with you. Always. (Elaria puts a hand to her face).
Momma!
Momma will want Cersei for herself.
Father got careless.  That's what Momma says.
Maybe I'll kill you both before we take King's Landing and then I won't have to share.
You want a good girl, but you need the bad pussy. (bites his ear)
Ned Stark was promised the same thing and we both know how that turned out.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Pod.
Pod! Find Bronn or Varys. Tell them I am here with Maester Pycelle. Tell them I am very much alive.
What happened?
Where am I?
Why should I believe you?
To create strife between my sister and me.
Pod.
Would it be excessive of me to ask you to save my life twice in a week?
You're a good lad. Get Bronn, tell him I want four of his most loyal gold cloaks outside my door at all times.
Then my hill tribesmen.
Don't want to swim too close to a drowning man? And I thought we were friends.
It was good of you to come.
Believe me, you don't.
(shakes his head)
Well?
I'm a monster as well as a dwarf. You should charge me double.
That was the arrangement we made. I pay you and you lie to me.
Leave?
What would we do in Pentos?
I want to go with you.

I can't. I do belong here. These bad people are what I'm good at. Outtalking them, outthinking them. It's what I am. And I like it. I like it more than anything I've ever done. Are you going to leave?
Hmm. Ser Eldrick Sarsfield and Lord Desmond Crakehall. Ser Eldrick Sarsfield and Lord Desmond Crakehall.
I have a list.
For laughing at me? Do I look like Joffrey to you? No, death seems a bit extreme. Fear of death, on the other hand--
My lady, people have been laughing at me far longer than they've been laughing at you. I'm the Halfman, the Demon Monkey, the Imp.
The disgraced daughter and the Demon Monkey. We're perfect for each other.
Who? Whom?
Ah. I could speak to Lord Varys and learn their perversions. Anyone named Desmond Crakehall must be a pervert.
I am the Imp. I have certain standards to maintain.
Lady Sansa!
Why sheep shift?
My lady--
Killed a few puppies today?
Roslin caught a fine fat trout. Her brothers gave her a pair of wolf pelts for her wedding. Signed Walder Frey.' Is that bad poetry or is it supposed to mean something?
No. She is no longer yours to torment.
Oh, I'm a monster. Perhaps you should speak to me more softly, then. Monsters are dangerous and just now kings are dying like flies.
You just sent the most powerful man in Westeros to bed without his supper.
A treasonous statement. Joffrey is king.
No. I think armies give you power.
Robb Stark had one, never lost a battle, and you defeated him all the same.

Oh, I know. Walder Frey gets all the credit. Or the blame, I suppose, depending on your allegiance. Walder Frey is many things, but a brave man? No. He never would have risked such an action if he didn't have certain assurances.
I'm all for cheating. This is war.
So that's why you did it? To save lives?
The northerners will never forget.
Do you think she'll open her legs for me after I tell her how we murdered her mother and brother?
I will not rape her.
Use small words. I'm not as bright as you.
(smirks)
No, it's a very good lesson. Only it's easy for you to preach utter devotion to family when you're making all the decisions.
When have you ever done something that wasn't in your interest but solely for the benefit of the family?
Sansa.
Keep up.
It's not easy being drunk all the time. Everyone would do it if it were easy.
She doesn't deserve this.
There's nothing worse than a late-blooming philosopher.
Will you be facing your marriage to Ser Loras with the same philosophical spirit?
I seem to remember saying something similar about my own marriage.
So you can tell Father it was you who finally talked me into it?
You have children. How happy would you say you are?
Even Joffrey?
How long does it go on?
Every time we deal with an enemy, we create two more.
Oh, get on with it you son of a whore.
What’re you doing?
Who’s helping you?
Varys?
I suppose this is goodbye, then.
Jaime, thank you. For my life.
I’m sorry. I’m sorry.
All my life, you’ve wanted me dead.
I loved her.
Shae.
I murdered her. With my own hands.
It doesn’t matter?
Say that word again.
I am your son, and you sentenced me to die. You knew I didn’t poison Joffrey, but you sentenced me all the same. Why?
I can’t go back there. She’s in there.
I am your son. I have always been your son.
(Valyrian) “Rusty” (Common Tongue) Thank you.
He’s right. The dragon headed north. If we’re going to find her, that’s where we’re to go.
And I intend to help her do it.
You betrayed her!
And she exiled you, twice, I believe?
Don’t blame me for your crimes, Mormont.
Fine, fine, I suppose he can join us. Just as long as he promises not to kill me in my sleep.
Pardon me?
Not precisely, but I have other skills that’d be very useful--
I have fought. I don’t claim to be a great warrior.
Middling.
And drink. I’ve survived so far.
I suppose there’s no point in asking how you found me.
Well, she didn’t execute me, so that’s a promising start. Now the heroes are off to find her, and I’m stuck here, trying to placate a city on the brink of civil war. Any advice for an old comrade?
If only I knew someone with a vast network of spies.
I did miss you.
How did he take it?

I know it was hard for you. You turned away a man who truly loves you because he would have been a liability in the Seven Kingdoms. That’s the kind of self-sacrifice that makes for a good ruler, if it’s any consolation.
No, I suppose not. I’m terrible at consoling.

All right, how about the fact that this is actually happening? You have your armies, you have your ships, you have your dragons. Everything you’ve ever wanted since you were old enough to want anything. It’s all yours for the taking. Are you afraid?
Good. You’re in the great game now. And the great game’s terrifying. The only people who aren’t afraid of failure are madmen like your father.
He wasn’t the first to love you and he won’t be the last.
I believe in you. It’s embarrassing, really. I’d swear you my sword, but I don’t actually own a sword.
It’s yours. Now and always.
If the city falls, Stannis will burn every Lannister he can find. Of course I'm afraid.
I'm a Lannister. I don't have a choice in all this. But it's not your war.
You can't fuck your way out of everything.
A wedding.
"Is that it?" Nice touch. As if you don't know the name of every boy in town.
I'm entirely sure you're entirely sure what I'm suggesting.
Oddly enough, I do.
There must be 20 miles of tunnels beneath the city.
I'm not escaping. Strange as it sounds, I'm the captain of the ship, and if the ship goes down, I go with her.
Well, I'm not.
What of it?
Blood spells, curses, shape-shifting. What do you think?
Mmm, don't take it personally. I don't entirely trust myself.
No, I don't believe you have.
Remember, wait till the ships
They must be far enough in
I chopped wood once. No, I watched my brother chopping wood.
Nor you, my friend.
Of course we are. Just because I pay you for your services doesn't diminish our friendship.
Oh! "Enhances." Fancy word for a sellsword.
Lady Sansa and Sheila.
Shae, yes. Surely my sister has asked you to join the other highborn ladies in Maegor's holdfast.
Always been a great romantic, my nephew.
Will you?
Stay safe, my lady.
On the way.
Ser Lancel, tell the Hound to tell the king that the Hand is extremely busy. LANCEL: The Hand of the King would like me to tell you to tell the king-

That would make me the quarterman. It just doesn't have the same ring to it. Cut me in half and I won't be able to give the signal. No signal, no plan. No plan and Stannis Baratheon sacks this city, takes the Iron Throne, puts your pinched little head atop a gate somewhere. It might be quite amusing, except that my head would be up there, too. I've never much liked my head, but I don't want to see it removed just yet.
Archers to their marks.
Hold fast.
Hold fast.
He's a serious man, Stannis Baratheon.
Rain fire on them.
Hound, form a welcome party for any Baratheon troop that manages to touch solid ground. Pod, run to the King's Gate. Bring any men guarding it here, now.
Can I get you some iced milk and a nice bowl of raspberries, too?
You're on the wrong side of the wall.
You're Kingsguard, Clegane. You must beat them back or they're going to take this city. Your king's city.
If you won't defend your own city, why should they?
Lead. Get down there and lead your people against the invaders who want to kill them.
I'll lead the attack. I'll lead the attack!
Pod, my helmet. Ser Mandon, you will bear the king's banner. Men, form up. Men Men! They say I'm half a man. But what does that make the lot of you?
There's another way out. I'm going to show you. We'll come out behind them and fuck them in their arses.

Don't fight for your king and don't fight for his kingdoms. Don't fight for honor. Don't fight for glory. Don't fight for riches, because you won't get any. This is your city Stannis means to sack. That's your gate he's ramming. If he gets in, it will be your houses he burns, your gold he steals, your women he will rape.
Those are brave men knocking at our door. Let's go kill them.
Attack!
Oh, fuck me.
The smaller man it is.
There’s always been more than enough death in the world for my taste. I can do without it in my leisure time.
It’s easy to confuse what is with what ought to be. Especially when what is has worked in your favor.
My father would have liked you.
Well said, you’re an eloquent man. Doesn’t mean you’re wrong. In my experience, eloquent men are right every bit as often as imbeciles.
You can end this.
You can!
Stay close!
Despite appearances, I think you’ll find the city’s on the rise.
Perhaps we should take shelter.
Meereen is strong. Commerce has returned to the markets. The people are behind you.

Well, not all the people, of course. No ruler that ever lived had the support of all the people. But the rebirth of Meereen is the cause of this violence. The Masters cannot let Meereen succeed. Because if Meereen succeeds, a city without slavery, a city without Masters…
...it proves that no one needs a Master.
Do we have a plan?
You once told me you knew what your father was. Did you know his plans for King’s Landing when the Lannister armies were at his gates?

Probably not. Well, he told my brother and Jaime told me. He had caches of wildfire hidden under the Red Keep, the Guildhalls, the Sept of Baelor, all the major thoroughfares. He would have burned every one of his citizens. The loyal ones and the trators. Every man, woman, and child. That’s why Jaime killed him.
You’re talking about destroying cities. It’s not entirely different. I’d like to suggest an alternate approach.
We are here to discuss terms of surrender, not to trade insults.

Thank you for the armada. Our queen does love ships. Now, last time we spoke, we made a pact. You violated that pact. You declared war upon us. Though our queen does have a forgiving nature, this cannot be forgiven.
It always seems a bit abstract, doesn’t it, other people dying?

Tell your people what happened here. Tell them you live by the grace of Her Majesty. When they come forward with notions of retribution or ideas about returning the slave cities to their former glory, remind the what happened when Daenerys Stormborn and her dragons came to Meereen.
It was. And how have things been going for you since then? Not so well, I gather. Can’t imagine you would have murdered the Stark boys if things had been going well.
Doesn’t seem like it. He’s still alive. It was complicated for you, I’m sure, growing up at Winterfell. Never quite knowing who you were. But then, we all live complicated lives, don’t we?
We can agree upon that at least.
With the former Masters’ fleet, possibly. Barely. There are more than 100 ships in the Iron Fleet.
The Seven Kingdoms.
What if everyone starts demanding their independence?
Yes, but do you have to do it here?
You should start wearing the gold cloak.
You're commander of the City Watch. You shouldn't be dressed like a common
Well, you're not sneaking through alleyways any longer. You're supposed to stand out.
Fine, fine. No gold cloak.
What?
What? What? Why are you staring at me?
To help me plan the defense of King's Landing.
Stannis will be here any day.
"An History of the Great Sieges of Westeros. " By Archmaester Shevelathin. Shevelatesh.
I did not know. And how did you accomplish this marked drop in thievery?
For questioning?
We talked about this.

Stannis knows King's Landing. He knows where the walls are strongest, and he knows which gates are weakest. The Mud Gate. A good ram will batter it down in minutes, and it's only 50 yards from the water. That's where he'll land.
Pig shit.
Thank you, Podrick.
I have a certain sympathy for odd little boys.

Excellent lamprey pie. Were you slaving away in the kitchen all day? I hear Joffrey plans on fighting. An inspiration for the troops. The young king in his shining armor fighting to defend the capital against the wicked old rebel.
Younger boys are off fighting his war.
What is he, 17? Remember Jaime at 17?
It's not on the throne, either, sadly for all of us.
No, I'd say you possess above-average intelligence.

He'll have his Kingsguard protecting him. He has the finest armor gold can buy. He needs to be out there. The men will fight more fiercely seeing their king fighting beside them instead of hiding behind his mother's skirts. Any dessert?
Because he has thousands of spies in his employ. Because he knows everything we do before we do it.
Neither do you.
It's not that little.
Why are you smiling?
And why are you happy?
I thought you preferred blondes.
Why do you care who I fuck?
This is madness. Stannis will be here in days. You need me.
Where did you find her?
You've forgotten the most important thing about whores.
You don't buy them, you only rent them.
And how do I know you haven't killed her already?
I'm sorry they hurt you. You must be brave.
I promise I will free you.
Never.
I will hurt you for this. A day will come when you think you're safe and happy, and your joy will turn to ashes in your mouth. And you will know the debt is paid.
Shae!
We have to be more careful.
There are people who want to hurt me.
I would kill for you. Do you know that? I expect I'll have to before this is over. You're mine.
Promise me.
To strike? My dear nephew, you do see these men preparing the walls for siege? You do understand Stannis Baratheon sails this way?
A brave choice, Your Grace. I'm sure your men will line up behind you.
Imagine Stannis' terror.
You're an intelligent man. I'd like to think I'm an intelligent man.
I wish we could converse as two honest, intelligent men.
What do you want? Tell me.
My brother was the youngest Kingsguard in history. My sister became queen at the age of 19. When I reached manhood, my father put me in charge of all the drains and cisterns in Casterly Rock.
The water never flowed better. And all the shit found its way to the sea. I never expected to have any real power. So when my father named me acting Hand
I do. Last thing I expected.
I'd like to keep playing it. If Stannis breaches the gates, the game is over.
The Lord of Light wants his enemies burned, the Drowned God wants them drowned. Why are all the gods such vicious cunts? Where is the god of tits and wine?
We should sail there immediately.
A girl at the edge of the world is the least of our problems.
One game at a time, my friend.
Lady Sansa.
Oh, yes. The husband of your dreams. But you do look glorious. Perhaps we could have a moment alone. Do you mind? Podrick, could you escort Lady Stark's handmaiden?
My lady, I want you to know I didn't ask for this.
You're right. I have no idea how you feel. And you have no idea how I feel.
But I promise you one thing, my lady. I won't ever hurt you. Do you drink wine?
Well, today you have to.
Could you-
Thank you.
(snorts)
Of course. Of course. En--enjoy.
Rather less than I plan to be. Isn't it a man's duty to be drunk at his own wedding?
And?
What did you once call me?  A drunken little lust-filled beast.
There you have it. Nothing to worry about. Drinking and lust, no man can match me in these things. I am the god of tits and wine. I shall build a shrine to myself at the next brothel I visit.
There will be no bedding ceremony.
There will be no bedding ceremony.
Then you'll be fucking your own bride with a wooden cock.
A bad joke, Your Grace. Made out of envy of your own royal manhood. Mine is so small. My poor wife won't even know I'm there.
My tiny drunk cock and I have a job to do. Come, wife.
I vomited on a girl once in the middle of the act. Not proud of it. But I think honesty is important between a man and wife, don't you agree?  Come, I'll tell you all about it. Put you in the mood.
Tyrion, Sansa. My name is Tyrion.
Nothing was ever wiser.
Astoundingly long.
Neck. You have one. How old are you exactly?
Well, talk won't make you any older. My lord father has commanded me to consummate this marriage.
Stop. I can't. I could, I won't.
If my father wants someone to get fucked, I know where he can start. I won't share your bed. Not until you want me to.
And so my watch begins.
You really ought to knock.

Wine always helps. I thank you for it. Trial by combat. Deciding a man's guilt or innocence in the eyes of the gods by having two other men hack each other to pieces. Tells you something about the gods. How much longer?
Do you think Oberyn has a chance? The Red Viper of Dorne. You don't get a name like that unless you're deadly, right?
Oh, he's going to die. I'm going to die.
That's putting it mildly. What's the punishment for regicide? Drawing and quartering? Hanging? Breaking at the wheel?

Seems rather ordinary. And he was my nephew as well, so what is that? Fratricide is brothers. Filicide is sons. Nepoticide. That's the one. Matricide, patricide, infanticide, suicide. There's no kind of killing that doesn't have its own word.
Cousins, you're right. There is no word for cousin killing. Well done. Do you remember cousin Orson? Orson Lannister?
Simple? Used to sit all day in the garden. Crushing beetles with a rock.
Nothing made him happier.
On the contrary. Laughing at another person's misery was the only thing that made me feel like everyone else.
For you. You drifted away.
Yes, other interests. But I stayed with Orson.

I was curious. Why was he smashing all those beetles? What did he get out of it? First thing I did was ask him. "Orson, why are you smashing all those beetles?" He gave me an answer. "Smash the beetles. Smash 'em. Khuu! Khuu! Khuu!" I wasn't deterred. I was the smartest person I knew. Certainly I had the wherewithal to unravel the mysteries that lay at the heart of a moron. So I went to Maester Volarik's library.

Turns out, far too much has been written about great men and not nearly enough about morons. Doesn't seem right. In any case, I found nothing that illuminated the nature of Orson's affliction or the reason behind his relentless beetle slaughter. So I went back to the source. I may not have been able to speak with Orson, but I could observe him, watch him the way men watch animals to come to a deeper understanding of their behavior. *picks up a beetle* And as I watched, I became more and more sure of it. There was something happening there. His face was like the page of a book written in a language I didn't understand. But he wasn't mindless. He had his reasons. And I became possessed with knowing what they were. I began to spend inordinate amounts of time watching him. I would eat my lunch in the garden, chewing my mutton to the music of "khuu, khuu, khuu". And when I wasn't watching him, I was thinking about him. Father droned on about the family legacy and I thought about Orson's beetles. I read the histories of Targaryen conquests. Did I hear dragon wings? No. I heard "khuu, khuu, khuu" and I still couldn't figure out why he was doing it. And I had to know, because it was horrible that all these beetles should be dying for no reason.

I know, I know. But still, it filled me with dread. Piles and piles of them, years and years of them. How many countless living crawling things smashed and dried out and returned to the dirt? In my dreams I found myself standing on a beach made of beetle husks stretching as far as the eye could see. I woke up crying, weeping for their shattered little bodies. I tried to stop Orson once.

He just pushed me aside with a "khuu" and kept on smashing. Every day until that mule kicked him in the chest and killed him. *puts the beetle back on the ground* So what do you think? Why did he do it? What was it all about?
Looks like very light armor.
You could at least wear a helmet. You shouldn't drink before a fight.
It could get you killed. It could get me killed.
Thank the gods.
If only I were otherwise.

You want revenge against the Lannisters? I killed my mother Joanna Lannister the day I was born. I killed my father Tywin Lannister with a bow to the heart. I am the greatest Lannister killer of our time.
Into your service? Your Grace we have only just met. It’s too soon to know if you deserve my service.

When I was a young man I heard a story about a baby born during the worst storm in living memory. She had no wealth, no lands, no army, only a name and a handful of supporters, most of whom probably thought they could use that name to benefit themselves. They kept her alive, moving her from place to place, often hours ahead of the men who had been sent to kill her. She was eventually sold off to some warlord on the edge of the world and that appeared to be that. And then a few years later the most well informed person I knew told me that this girl without wealth, lands, or armies had somehow acquired all 3 in a very short span of time, along with three dragons. He thought she was our best, last chance to build a better world. I thought you were worth meeting at the very least.

Because you can not build a better world on your own. You have no one at your side who understands that land you want to rule. The strengths and the weaknesses of the houses that will either support or oppose you.

Killing and politics aren’t always the same thing. When I served as Hand of the King I did quite well with the latter considering the king in question preferred torturing animals to leading his people. I could do an even better job advising a ruler worth the name.
I know.

Whomever Ser Jorah was when he started informing on you, he is no longer that man. I can’t remember seeing the same man as devoted to anything as he is to serving you. He claims he would kill for you and die for you, and nothing I have witnessed gives me reason to doubt him. And yet he did betray you.
Did he have an opportunity to confess his betrayals?
And did he?

A ruler who kills those that are devoted to her is not a ruler that inspires devotion. And you’re going to need to inspire devotion, and lots of it, if you’re ever going to rule across the Narrow Sea. But you cannot have him by your side when you do.
Have you decided yet whether you’re going to have me killed?
I can see why you would think so. It’s what your father would have done.
My father? Who publicly sentenced me to death? I’d say his thoughts on having me killed were abundantly clear.
Some day if you decide not to execute me, I’ll tell you all about why I killed my father. And on that day, should it ever come, we’ll need more wine than this.
So here we sit, two terrible children of two terrible fathers.
I’ve heard stories.
To see if you were the right kind of terrible.
The kind that prevents your people from being even more so.

Yes, that was wise. And you agreed to marry someone you loathed for the greater good. Very impressive. My own sister married someone she loathed as well, though not by choice and certainly not for the greater good, Gods forbid. She ended up having him killed.
It’s not impossible that Varys was right about you after all.
Yes. He was the one who convinced me to come find you. He was my travelling companion before Ser Jorah seized that role for himself.
He did what he had to do to survive. He did a lot of other things that he didn’t have to do. I suspect he’s the main reason you weren’t slaughtered in your crib.
Yes, oddly. He might be the only person in the world that I trust. Besides my brother.
That’s the one.
Your queenly prerogative. I had given up on life until Varys convinced me you might be worth living for. You chop off my head? Well, my final days were interesting.
No? Banish me?
So if I’m not going to be murdered, and I’m not going to be banished...
Advise you on what?
The Iron Throne. Perhaps you should try wanting something else.

I’m not entirely joking. There’s more to the world than Westeros after all. How many hundreds of thousands of lives have you changed for the better here? Perhaps this is where you belong. Where you can do the most good.
And when you get back to your home, who supports you?

Let’s be generous and assume that’s going to happen. Here in Slaver’s Bay you had the support of the common people and only the common people. What was that like, ruling without the rich? House Targaryen is gone, not a single person who shares your blood is alive to support you. The Starks are gone as well, our two terrible fathers saw to that. The remaining Lannisters won’t back you, not ever. Stannis Baratheon won’t back you either, his entire claim to the Throne depends on the illegitimacy of yours. That leaves the Tyrells. Not impossible, but not enough.
It’s a beautiful dream. Stopping the wheel. You’re not the first person to have dreamt it.
I’d call that a successful gambit.
Look around. The city has come back to life.
I did and it worked.
Spoken like a man who has never had to shave.
I’m going to miss you.
I hope you’re right about this expedition of yours.
Most of all, we need our queen.
Varys.
The most famous dwarf in the world.
I am happy. You should both be as well. Not so long ago, this city was ready to devour itself.
Now it’s like a man reborn.
Why don’t you drink? Why don’t either of you ever drink?
Why not?
And who made these rules? Your former masters?
Those miserable old shits didn’t want you to be human. Have a drink with me.
That’s how you know it’s working.
Here’s to our queen. Anyone not drinking is disrespecting our queen.
To Daenerys Stormborn, Mother of Dragons, Breaker of Chains, long may she reign.
Do you like it?
Yes, yes. Fermentation. One day, after our queen has taken the Seven Kingdoms...I’d like to have my own vineyard. Make my own wine. The Imp’s Delight. Only my close friends could drink it.
Tell me a joke, Missandei of Naath.
Grey worm?

...but when the barkeep brings them over, each of them finds a fly in his cup. The Lannister, outraged, shoves the cup aside and demands another. The Martell plucks the fly out and swallows it whole. The Stark reaches into his cup, pulls out the fly and shouts, “Spit it out, you see shit. Spit it out.”
It’s funnier in Westeros.
Yes.
A story that’s supposed to make you laugh, ideally. Not at the moment, perhaps.
Missandei, do you like the wine?
Tell a joke.
You lied to us.
I was wrong. I admit it.
All right, what should the army do?
And then?
Since when do you light your own candles?
Stannis Baratheon's fleet has been spotted sailing north past Tarth, 200 ships.
Yes, that's more than we have. He'll be at our gates within five days, four if he has the wind.
"Rain fire on them from above." You're quoting Father, aren't you?

Call it tactics, not strategy. But, yes, he does have a good mind for it. The best mind, some would say. Sadly, he's not here. It's just you, me and Joffrey, the Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm.
He needs to start acting like a king. This war you started is coming to our doorstep. And if the entire city wants Joffrey dead
I thought the girls might help him.
I was wrong. If we can't control him
It's hard to put a leash on a dog once you've put a crown on its head.
The boy is more Robert than Jaime.
Sins?
You've beaten the odds. Tommen and Myrcella are good, decent children, both of them.
She's a child.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: A tall child.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Not that young.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Older.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Shae isn't going to like it.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I should never have told you about that.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Two women to despise me and a whole kingdom to join them.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I don't pay you to put evil notions in my head. The ones already there don't need company.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Solid gold from the mines outside Lannisport. Smithed in Casterly Rock.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You could buy a ship with these.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: What?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Of course you're not going anywhere.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I didn't ask for this marriage. I didn't want it.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: That doesn't mean that--this is duty, not desire.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I don't have a choice. My father--
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: What would I do there? Juggle? I am a Lannister of Casterly Rock.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: My feelings for you have not changed. I will marry Sansa Stark and do my duty by her.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I swear to you it will not be like that.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I will buy you a good home somewhere in the city. You will have fine clothes, guards to keep you safe, servants. Any children we might have will be well provided for.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Listen. Listen to me, my lady.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You are. You'll always be my lady.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I'm sorry, what am I grateful for? The opportunity to live and die at the Wall as punishment for a crime I did not commit?

Yes, I fell in love with a whore. And I was stupid enough to think that she had fallen in love with me. That deal you made, it was everything Father wanted. You do see that? He gets you back as his heir. The future Lord of the Rock. And he ships me off to Castle Black, out of sight at last. All so perfect. It felt good to take that from him. He knows I'm innocent and he's willing to sacrifice me anyway. He's willing to sacrifice any of us. Not you. You're the golden son. You could kill a king, lose a hand, fuck your own sister, you'll always be the golden son.
At least I got to tell them what they really are.
Don't give up on me just yet. I survived one trial by combat, even though you weren't there to save me.
Where's your sense of adventure? Even if you lose, imagine the look on Father's face when you fall.
Our family name snuffed out with a single swing of the sword.
Well... Bronn fought for me once, he'll do it again. If he wins, I expect I'll be in his debt for the rest of my life.
Will you find him for me? Who does Cersei plan on naming as a champion? I hope it's Ser Meryn Trant. I'd enjoy watching Bronn disembowel that pompous child beater.
You have new clothes.
I sent for you days ago.
Doing what?
Lollys Stokeworth? She doesn't strike me as your sort of girl.
She's dim-witted.
When my sister arranged this love match, did she mention that Lollys has an older sister?
And you understand the rules of inheritance?
She still gets Castle Stokeworth when her father dies.
(scoffs.)
You and my sister deserve each other. Why did you bother to come here?
Is it two wives you want or two castles?
I'm a bit short on castles at the moment. But I can offer you gold and gratitude.
You might be surprised. A Lannister always pays his debts.
My wife is heir to Winterfell. If I emerge from this with my head still on my shoulders, I may one day rule the North in her name. I could carve you out a big piece of it.
Does he frighten you so much?
Because you're my friend.
I understand.
Why are you sorry? Because you're an evil bastard with no conscience and no heart? That's what I liked about you in the first place.
Yes, we did.
I suppose I'll have to kill the Mountain myself. Won't that make for a great song?
I imagined you'd be back at the brothel at this hour.
Mm, do tell. I've got every kind of filth down here except the kind I like.
Oh.
Making honest feelings do dishonest work is one of her many gifts.
She didn't need to bother you. It looks as though I've taken care of that myself. The joy she will feel when my head leaves my neck. She's wanted this for a long time.
I think I would have remembered that.
You and my family have more in common than you might admit.
That would have made things so much easier.
Well... sooner or later, Cersei always gets what she wants.
If you want justice, you've come to the wrong place.
Wait! You have to buy me as well.
We’re a team. I’m a great fighter, too.
Thank you my Lord. Thank you.
My Lord, if I may. I’ve heard Mereen’s become a free city. You might run into trouble if you try to fight slaves in the fighting pits. Now if you were to fight free men who were paid a wage…
What are you doing?
I am the gift. It’s a pleasure to meet you, Your Grace. My name is Tyrion Lannister.
A million, give or take.
There's more work in the city. And the brothels are far superior.

I imagine it was a sad joke at the end. An entire arena for a few sickly creatures smaller than dogs. But in the beginning, when it was home to Balerion the Dread, it must have been the most dangerous place in the world.
A pleasant surprise in an unpleasant situation.
Supporting the enemy, no less.
Cersei will anyway.
(Shakes his head.)
Here we are the heroes of Blackwater Bay. Strange place for a reunion.
I don't think I'm anyone's lord anymore, Podrick. Save the titles for Ser Bronn of the Blackwater.
You've been thinking about our new queen.
Are you? Helping me to arrange this meeting wasn't exactly looking after yourself, was it? You put yourself at risk.
It's good to see you again.
You might.
And some Clegane cunt to help them see it through.
She'll be here soon.
No.
We are all facing a unique
I think we ought to begin with larger concerns.
Do you remember when we discussed dwarf jokes? His wasn't even good. He explained it at the end. Never explain. It always ruins it.

We are a group of people who do not like one another, as this recent demonstration has shown. We have suffered at each other's hands. We have lost people we love at each other's hands. If all we wanted was more of the same, there would be no need for this gathering. We are entirely capable of waging war against each other without meeting face-to-face.
We all know that will never happen.
There is no conversation that will erase the last 50 years. We have something to show you.
I'm pleased you bent the knee to our queen
That is indeed a problem. The more immediate problem is that we're fucked.
Only one. Everyone stays here, and I go and talk to my sister.
I don't want Cersei to murder me either. I could have stayed in my cell and saved a great deal of trouble.
She'll definitely murder you. I go see my sister.
You spoke with her?
I'm about to step into a room with the most murderous woman in the world who's already tried to kill me twice, that I know of. Who's an idiot?
A foreign whore you can't abduct, beat, or intimidate. That must be difficult for you.
I didn't know about that.
Cersei, I didn't know.

I am the one preventing that from happening. Daenerys didn't want to debate and negotiate. She didn't want to bring you words. She wanted to bring you fire and blood until I advised her otherwise. I don't want to destroy our family. I never have.
After he sentenced me to death for a crime I didn't commit, yes, I killed him. Hate me for it if you want. I hate myself for it in spite of what he was, in spite of what he did to me.
I have never been more sorry about anything.
(starts)
All right. You love your family, and I have destroyed it. I will always be a threat. So put an end to me.
If it weren't for me, you'd have a motherIf it weren't for me, you'd have a father.
I am more sorry about the children than you could ever know.
I don't care. I loved them. You know I did. You know it in your heart if there's anything left of it.
If there's no future, then why are we here? Why did you allow me to come?
Yes, no, not what you hoped for, but you must have hoped for something.
Mm, not like this.
Yes.
Because I think she will make the world a better place.
She knows herself. She chose an advisor who would check her worst impulses instead of feeding them.
You're pregnant.
Get the Prince back to the Keep now.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Move. Move!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Where's Sansa?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You blind, bloody fool.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: We've had vicious kings and we've had idiot kings, but I don't know if we've ever been cursed with a vicious idiot for a king.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I can, I am.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: They threw a cow pie at you, so you decided to kill them all? They're starving, you fool. All because of a war you started.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: And now I've struck a king. Did my hand fall from my wrist? Where is the Stark girl?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: If she dies, you'll never get your Uncle Jaime back. You owe him quite a bit, you know.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Take some men and go and find the Stark girl.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Are you hurt, my lady?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Well done, Clegane.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I don't suppose there's anything we can do about this.
It's hard to say which of the four of us is getting the worst of this arrangement. Probably Sansa. Though Loras will certainly come to know a deep and singular misery.
On a boat you built.
I'm your family. A member of your family who has actively contributed to that family's survival. Whether or not you or Father or anyone else wants to admit it.

Trying to have me killed is an odd way of saying thank you. There are two people in King's Landing who can give an order to a Kingsguard. Did you or did you not order Ser Mandon to kill me during the Battle of the Blackwater?

The impulse I understand. He hates me because I'm the only one who tells him what he really is. So, fair enough. He wants me dead. But his stupidity? He could have had me poisoned and no one would have known. But the king orders a Kingsguard to murder the Hand of the King in full view of his own army. The boy's an idiot.
I want you to tell me if my life is still in danger.
Seven kingdoms united in fear of Tywin Lannister.
You may escape, at least. Once Jaime gets back, Ser Loras may come down with a terrible case of sword through bowels.
Jaime or not, I'm truly fucked.
I beg your pardon, my lady.
Yes, it should be quite a wedding. I need to speak with you, Lady Sansa.
Alone, if I may.
Sometimes we think we want to hear something and it's only afterwards when it's too late that we realize we wished we'd heard it under entirely different circumstances.
Let me guess, I've been pardoned.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Really?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Well, we mustn't disappoint Father.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: No.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Not that I know of.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Choked on his pigeon pie.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Or the pigeons. Just leave me out of it.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Oh, why don't you tell them what Joffrey was doing?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Pointing a loaded crossbow at Sansa Stark while you tore at her clothes and beat her.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: (shakes his head)
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Father, may I ask the witness one question?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You once said that without me, this city would have faced certain defeat. You said the histories would never mention me, but you would not forget. Have you forgotten, Lord Varys?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Not going well, is it?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Oh, you think so?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: (nods)
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Shae. Please don't.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Father, I wish to confess. I wish... to confess.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I saved you. I saved this city and all your worthless lives. I should have let Stannis kill you all.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Yes, Father. I'm guilty. Guilty. Is that what you want to hear?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: No, of that I'm innocent. I'm guilty of a far more monstrous crime. I am guilty of being a dwarf.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Oh, yes, I am. I've been on trial for that my entire life.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: (To Cersei) Watching your vicious bastard die gave me more relief than 1,000 lying whores.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: (To the crowd) I wish I was the monster you think I am. I wish I had enough poison for the whole pack of you. I would gladly give my life to watch you all swallow it.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I will not give my life for Joffrey's murder. And I know I'll get no justice here. So I will let the gods decide my fate. I demand a trial by combat.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: So, those villages we were supposed to find. We can’t live on berries and roots.
You’re not hungry?
You’re an awful travelling companion, do you know that? Possibly the least charming man I have ever met.
We are travelling together. In each other’s company. Therefore…
I sailed from King’s Landing to Pentos in a crate. Without ever saying a word.
Never occurred to you to ask why until now? You never wondered why Tyrion Lannister decided to visit a brothel in Volantis.
I killed my father. He wanted to execute me for a crime I didn’t commit. And he was fucking the woman I loved.
As miserable as you are, Mormont, at least your father was a good man.

I met him. I visited the Wall. When I asked him about his men he knew all the stories, every one of them. He actually cared about the people in his command. How did they put it in the Watch? “We shall never see his like again.”
You didn’t know. I’m sorry. I am.
I only know what I heard
He was leading an expedition beyond the wall. There was a mutiny. He was murdered by his own men.
Why Daenerys? Why is she worth all this? As I recall, the Mormonts fought against the Targaryens during Robert’s Rebellion.
I believe in many things.
No.
No.
Doesn’t mean she’s going to be a great queen.
The Targaryens are famously insane. What if she conquers the world? Then what? 1,000 years of peace and prosperity?

We? Alright, let’s assume your dreams come true. She’s ecstatic when you bring her this enemy dwarf. She hacks off my head and restores your position. You command her army, sail to Westeros, defeat all your enemies, then watch as she climbs those steps and sits on the Iron Throne. Hurrah, long live the Queen. Then what?
So a woman who has not spent a single day of her adult life in Westeros becomes the ruler of Westeros? That’s justice?
Why, because her father who burned living men for amusement was the king?
Why is it anchored?
Wait, wait! Wait, wait, wait, let’s discuss this!
Wait, wait, wait, wait! You can’t just hand a dried cock to a merchant and expect him to pay for it. He has to know it came from a dwarf. And how could he know unless he sees the dwarf?
Guess again.
The fighting puts in Mereen! You’re in luck then, you’re about to be rich. You are looking at one of the great warriors in the seven kingdoms.
(looks at Jorah) Him.
Sure, he’s a bit long in the tooth, a bit withered, been in the sun too long. We can all see that. But he is a veteran of 100 battles. They wrote songs about him!
He won the tournament at Lannisport, unseating Ser Jaime Lannister himself.
The Kingslayer!
I honestly don't.
Oh.  I've been heroic on occasion.
I once charged through the mud gate of King's Landing.
It's interesting these heroes you name.  Drogo, Jorah, Dario, even this Jon Snow, they all fell in love with you.
Oh, my mistake.  I suppose he stares at you longingly because he's hopeful for a successful military alliance.
I'll find you later.
I'm going alone.
I freed my brother. And you slaughtered a city.
Did you bring any wine?

Ah... Well... thank you for coming to see me. Our queen doesn't keep prisoners for long. I suppose there's a crude kind of justice. I betrayed my closest friend and watched him burn. Now Varys's ashes can tell my ASHES: "See, I told you." It just occurred to me. I'm talking to the only man alive who knows where I'm going.
So is there life after death?
I should be thankful. Oblivion is the best I could hope for. I strangled my lover. I shot my own father with a crossbow. I betrayed my queen.
I did. And I'd do it again, now that I've seen what I've seen. I chose my fate. The people of King's Landing did not.
Is it? When you heard her talking to her soldiers, did she sound like someone who's done fighting?
She liberated the people of Slaver's Bay. She liberated the people of King's Landing. And she'll go on liberating until the people of the world are free and she rules them all.
Until today. Varys was right. I was wrong. It was vanity to think I could guide her. Our queen's nature is fire and blood.

My father was an evil man. My sister was an evil woman. Pile up all the bodies of all the people they ever killed, there still won't be half as many as our beautiful queen slaughtered in a single day.
The moment the gates fell, the battle was over.
And she burned down a city for it.
Would you have done it?
You've been up there, on a dragon's back. You've had that power. Would you have burned the city down?
(scoffs) Yes, you do. You won't say because you don't want to betray her but you know.
It matters more than anything. When she murdered the slavers of Astapor, I'm sure no one but the slavers complained. After all, they were evil men.

When she crucified hundreds of Meereenese nobles, who could argue? They were evil men. The Dothraki khals she burned alive? They would have done worse to her. Everywhere she goes, evil men die and we cheer her for it. And she grows more powerful and more sure that she is good and right. She believes her destiny is to build a better world for everyone. If you believed that if you truly believed it, wouldn't you kill whoever stood between you and paradise?
I know you love her. I love her too. Not as successfully as you. But I believed in her with all my heart. Love is more powerful than reason. We all know that. Look at my brother.
You just came up with that?

Sometimes duty is the death of love. You are the shield that guards the realms of men. And you've always tried to do the right thing. No matter the cost, you've tried to protect people. Who is the greatest threat to the people now?
It's a terrible thing I'm asking. It's also the right thing. Do you think I'm the last man she'll execute? Who is more dangerous than the rightful heir to the Iron Throne?
And your sisters? Do you see them bending the knee?
Why do you think Sansa told me the truth about you? Because she doesn't want Dany to be queen.
No. But you do. And you have to choose now.
It's not for you to decide.
(nods) You're right. And no one's any better for it. But it's not for you to decide. Jon committed his crime here. His fate is for our king to decide. Or our queen.
You're the most powerful people in Westeros. Choose one.
Me? The Imp? Half the people hate me for serving Daenerys, the other half hate me for betraying her.  Can't think of a worse choice.
I've had nothing to do but think these past few weeks. About our bloody history. About the mistakes we've made.

What unites people? Armies? Gold? Flags? Stories. There's nothing in the world more powerful than a good story. Nothing can stop it. No enemy can defeat it. And who has a better story than Bran the Broken? The boy who fell from a high tower and lived. He knew he'd never walk again, so he learned to fly. He crossed beyond the Wall, a crippled boy, and became the Three-Eyed Raven. He is our memory, the keeper of all our stories. The wars, weddings, births, massacres, famines. Our triumphs, mm, our defeats, our past. Who better to lead us into the future?
Good. Sons of kings can be cruel and stupid, as you well know. His will never torment us. That is the wheel our queen wanted to break.
From now on, rulers will not be born. They will be chosen on this spot by the lords and ladies of Westeros to serve the realm.

I know you don't want it. I know you don't care about power. But I ask you now, if we choose you will you wear the crown? Will you lead the Seven Kingdoms to the best of your abilities from this day until your last day?
To Brandon of House Stark I say aye.
All hail Bran the Broken, First of His Name, King of the Andals and the First Men, Lord of the Six Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm.
I don't deserve it. I thought I was wise, but I wasn't. I thought I knew what was right, but I didn't. Choose Ser Davos. Choose anyone else.
Giving you to the Unsullied would start a war. Letting you walk free would start a war. So our new king has chosen to send you to the Night's Watch.
The world will always need a home for bastards and broken men.

You shall take no wife, hold no lands, father no children. The Unsullied wanted your head of course, but Grey Worm has accepted the justice of a life sentence. Sansa and Arya wanted you freed, but they understand our new king needs to make peace. No one is very happy. Which means it's a good compromise, I suppose.
What we did.
Ask me again in 10 years.
I wouldn't be so sure. A few years as Hand of the King would make anyone want to piss off the edge of the world.
What's this?
I suppose I come in for some heavy criticism.
Oh, he's kind to me. Never would've guessed.
He's not kind?
He what? What does he say about me?
Your Grace.
Yes, Your Grace. Suitable prospects will be brought to you for an audience in the coming weeks.
As you wish, Your Grace.
(clearing his throat) We serve at your pleasure, King Bran the Broken, ruler of the Six Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm. Long may he reign.
That will improve.
Ser Bronn of the Blackwater, Lord of Highgarden, Lord Paramount of the Reach and Master of Coin, would you say the crown's debt to you has been paid?
Good. Time to start incurring a new one. We have hungry people to feed. Can we expect some assistance in this regard?
Lord Davos, we have an armada to rebuild and ports to repair.
Find the best builders and set them to the task.
I once brought a jackass and a honeycomb into a brothel.
Accounts differ. Most seem to implicate Catelyn Stark in some way.
Some say it was one of his own Kingsguard, while still others say it was Stannis himself who did it after negotiations went sour.
It's not what Varys says. He says Renly's army is flocking to support Stannis, which would give Stannis superiority over us on both land and sea.
And I say Father raised you to have too much respect for money. Stannis Baratheon is coming for us, sooner rather than later.
She'll be safer in Dorne.
It so happens that I am. Myrcella is a sweet, innocent girl and I don't blame her at all for you.
Schemes and plots are the same thing. They are going to attack us. We need to be ready.
May I ask specifically what the king has in mind?
It's important that we talk about this.
Wildfire? You wouldn't lie to me, would you, cousin?
That's a lie right there.
Tell me, if the vile allegations against my brother and sister are true, do you think it will make Jaime more likely to kill you or less likely? When I tell him you're fucking her, I mean.
The smart money would be on more likely.
But then perhaps his own unnatural urges will give him sympathy for yours.
I suppose there's only one way to find out.
When did she tell you this?
Swear to me on what?
But I don't care about your life.
All right, all right. Enough. Even torturing you is boring. Just get out.
Oh, Lancel, tell my friend Bronn to please kill you if anything should happen to me.
Stannis has more infantry, more ships, more horses. What do we have?
Well, I've never actually been able to kill people with it.

He hasn't sent a raven in weeks. He's very busy. Being repeatedly humiliated by Robb Stark is time-consuming. We won't be able to hold the city against Stannis, not the way Joffrey's planning on holding it.
It's hard to argue with his assessment.
The king is a lost cause. It's the rest of us I'm worried about now.
You have to admire his imagination.
What? Demon monkey?
Blame me? I'm trying to save them.
Demon monkey.
I remember reading an old sailors' proverb, "Piss on wildfire and your cock burns off. "
My companion takes issue.
How much do you have?
My friend remains unconvinced.
I'm afraid I have to concur with my advisor, Wisdom Haylene. The contents of this room could lay King's Landing low. You won't be making wildfire for my sister any longer. You'll be making it for me.
Thank you for seeing me, my lady. I had hoped we might discuss a few financial matters.
It's the royal wedding. I'm told you had a hand in planning it.
It's shaping up to be a very involved affair. The word extravagant has been used.
I understand that.
But, as Master of Coin, it falls upon me to calculate the cost for the crown. As of now, it's a huge expense.
And we're at war, Lady Olenna.
Yes, and maintaining supply lines--
And we are so grateful for your contributions which are necessary for the preservation of the realm.
I would.
(nods)
My lady--
Quite sufficient. Thank you.
Give me that.
What's she doing here?
You'll be pleased to learn that after one conversation with Olenna Tyrell, I've saved the crown hundreds of thousands on this wedding.
I'm Master of Coin. Saving money is important.
(to Cersei) Stop that. You're making me uncomfortable.
Very well. She's a lovely girl. Missing some of Loras' favorite bits, but I'm sure they'll make do.
It wasn't my best, but--
Sansa is the key to the North? I seem to remember she has an older brother.
The Tyrell army is helping us to win this war. Do you really think it's wise to refuse them?
And how do we do that?
Wonderful.
You can't mean it.
Joffrey has made this poor girl's life miserable since the day he took her father's head. Now she's finally free of him and you give her to me? That's cruel even for you.
She's a child!
And if I refuse?
I was wed. Or don't you remember?

Don't worry. I'll be fine. Nothing broken, I don't think. Where are we now? Not the Rhoyne. (No response). Long, sullen silences and an occasional punch in the face. The Mormont way. Let's start over. I apologize for before. My mouth sometimes runs away from me. This doesn't have to be an unpleasant trip. We're going to be spending a lot of time together on the way to Meereen.
What would make our time together truly enjoyable would be some wine.
I am a person who drinks. People who drink need to keep drinking. Otherwise, they're not…
I know where we are. You're taking us through Valyria.
Have you sailed this route before?
You're going to bring Daenerys a souvenir from her ancestral homeland in case I'm not enough?
You know what they say. The Doom still rules Valyria. What about the demons and the flames? Aren't you afraid of the Doom?
Oh.
The Smoking Sea. How many centuries before we learn how to build cities like this again? Thousands of years the Valyrians were the best in the world at almost everything. And then -

And then they weren't. "They held each other close and turned their backs upon the end. The hills that split asunder and the black that ate the skies; The flames that shot so high and hot that even dragons burned; Would never be the final sights that fell upon their eyes. A fly upon a wall, the waves the sea wind whipped and churned -"
I would clap.
I suppose this is it, then. This is what remains.
What was that?
Mormont, cut me free! (Jorah falls into the water). Mormont!
Mormont! Mormont!
(Shakes his head). You? (Jorah shakes his head). I've seen greyscale before, but nothing like that.

It'd be kinder to put daggers in their hearts and be done with it. (Jorah spits). Thank you for saving me. Of course, I wouldn't have needed saving if you hadn't kidnapped me in the first place. (Jorah gets up). So what now?
Without luck?
That's the best idea you've had all day.
It’s not enough.

It’s not enough for Meereen to have peace. They need to know Daenerys is responsible for it. The Sons of the Harpy have a good story. Resist the foreign invaders. Our queen has an even better story. Mother of Dragons, Breaker of Chains and...all that.
Yes, but do they know who brought them security? Who brought about an end to the violence? We need someone the people trust, someone they know who cannot be bought or influenced.
Who said anything about “him”?
That’s about the extent of my Valyrian.
Thank you for travelling all this way. I know from personal experience how uncomfortable the journey can be.
The truth is we need your help. We had hoped that we could somehow persuade you to --
Yes.
She did indeed.
Ideally we’d avoid purifying too many nonbelievers. The Mother of Dragons has followers of many different faiths.
I’d settle for obeyed.
That sounds most excellent --
We’d be most grateful for any support you could provide the queen.
My friend has a healthy skepticism of religion, but we are all loyal supporters of the queen.
My sister, she wasn't your queen until recently though, was she, until she murdered your rightful queen and destroyed House Tyrell for all time.  So it appears your allegiances are somewhat flexible.
Perhaps he could take the black, Your Grace.  Whatever else he is, he is a true soldier.  He would be invaluable at the Wall.
You are the future of your house.  This war has already wiped one great house from the world.  Don't let it happen again.  Bend the knee.
Your Grace, nothing strips bold notions from a man's head like a few weeks in a dark cell.
Your Grace, if you begin beheading entire families --
Your Grace --
All rulers demand that people bend the knee.  It's why they're rulers.  She gave Tarley a choice.  A man who had taken up arms against her.  What else could she do?
I am her hand, not her head.  I can't make her decisions for her.
Daenerys is not her father.
Who is that for?
Did you read it?
What's it say?
Perhaps not.  Cersei thinks the Army of the Dead is nothing but a story made up by wet nurses to frighten children.  What if we prove her wrong?
So bring the dead to her.
We don't have to bring the whole army.  Only one soldier.
Bring one of these things down to King's Landing and show her the truth.
The only person she listens to is Jaime.  He may listen to me.
Last time I was here I killed my father with a crossbow.
You're not staying here?
What if someone takes the boat?
I needed to see you and I knew you'd never agree to meet.

You made me look like a complete fool.  I thought I'd surprise you by hitting Casterly Rock, but you were three steps ahead of me.  You abandoned the family home.  Completely unsentimental.  Father would have been proud.
Listen to me --
It would take you awhile with a sparring sword.
