I'm looking at you. You've got an interesting face. Very distinctive faces. All of you.
It's just I think they would look marvelous decorating spikes in King's Landing. Perhaps I'll write my sister, the Queen, about it.

Grenn's father left him too... Outside a farmhouse when he was three. Pyp was caught stealing a wheel of cheese. His little sister hadn't eaten in three days. He was given a choice: his right hand or the Wall. I've been asking the Lord Commander about them. Fascinating stories.
It's a lucky thing none of them were trained by a master-at-arms like your Ser Rodrik. I don't imagine any of them have ever held a real sword before they came here. Your brother Bran. He's woken up.
You're joking?
And how do a bear's balls taste?
Do Dornish girls count? So you roam the Seven Kingdoms, collaring pickpockets and horse thieves and bringing them here as eager recruits?
They have a better chance finding feed than glory.
You don't have enough men to be an army and aside from Yoren here, none of you are particularly funny.
Do you think I'm plump? Listen, Benjen... May I call you Benjen?
I'm not sure what I've done to offend you. I have great admiration for the Night's Watch. I've great admiration for you as First Ranger.

But... I don't believe that giants and ghouls and White Walkers are lurking beyond the Wall. I believe that the only difference between us and the wildlings is that when the Wall went up, our ancestors happened to live on the right side of it.
I think he's starting to like me. "Going below"?
So you're heading down to King's Landing too.
Let's share the road. I could use some decent company.
Not this time. We'll be staying at the finest castles and inns. No one turns away a Lannister.
Eight... no, nine.
They say the winter of my birth was three years long, Maester Aemon.
Yes, and the fishermen of Lannisport say they see mermaids.
It's either me or this cold. And it doesn't appear to be going anywhere.
I expect I will. Gods know there aren't many feather beds between here and King's Landing.
Of course.
If you're going to be a cripple, it's better to be a rich cripple. Take care, Snow.
Before you go, you will call on Lord and Lady Stark and offer your sympathies.
None. But it is expected of you. Your absence has already been noted.
One word and I'll hit you again.

Go! Tell her. But first you will get to Lord and Lady Stark and you will fall on your knees in front of them and tell them how very sorry you are, that you are at their service, and that all your prayers are with them. Do you understand?
Do you understand?
I hope so. If he forgets, be a good dog and remind him. Time for breakfast...
Bread. And two of those little fish. And a mug of dark beer to wash it down. And bacon, burnt black.
Beloved siblings.
Apparently not.
The Maester says the boy may live.
Only the gods know for certain. All the rest of us can do is pray. The charms of the north seem entirely lost on you.
Where's your sense of wonder? The greatest structure ever built, the intrepid men of the Night's Watch, the wintry abode of the White Walkers.
And go celibate? The whores would go begging from Dorne to Casterly Rock. I just want to stand on top of the Wall and piss off the edge of the world.
Speaking for the grotesques, I'd have to disagree. Death is so final, whereas life... Life is full of possibilities. I hope the boy does wake. I'd be very interested to hear what he has to say.
My dear brother, you wound me. You know how much I love my family.

Rapers. They were given a choice no doubt... Castration or the Wall. Most choose the knife. Not impressed by your new brothers? Lovely thing about the Watch... You discard your old family and get a whole new one.
Look at me and tell me what you see.

What you see is a dwarf. If I'd been born a peasant they might've left me out in the woods to die. Alas, I was born a Lannister of Casterly Rock. Things are expected of me. My father was the Hand of the King for 20 years.

Yes, until my brother killed him. Life is full of these little ironies. My sister married the new King and my repulsive nephew will be King after him. I must do my part for the honor of my house, wouldn't you agree? But how? Well, my brother has his sword and I have my mind. And a mind needs books like a sword needs a whetstone. That's why I read so much. And you? What's your story, bastard?
A bastard boy with nothing to inherit, off to join the ancient order of the Night's Watch, alongside his valiant brothers-in-arms.

Yes yes, against grumpkins and snarks and all the other monsters your wet nurse warned you about. You're a smart boy. You don't believe that nonsense... Everything's better with some wine in the belly.
Pay each of them 20 gold when this is done.
*standing up* The gods have made their will known. Tyrion Lannister, in the name of King Tommen of the House Baratheon, First of His Name, you are hereby sentenced to death.
Thank you, Your Grace.
Any man who must say, 'I am the king' is no true king. I'll make sure you understand that when I've won your war for you.
The king is tired. See him to his chambers.
Grand Maester, perhaps some essence of nightshade to help him sleep.
Not you.
You're a fool if you believe he's the most powerful man in Westeros.
You really think a crown gives you power?
Mm-hmm.
Mm-hmm.
Which he got from me. Do you disapprove?
But to slaughter them at a wedding. Explain to me why it is more noble to kill 10,000 men in battle than a dozen at dinner.
To end the war. To protect the family. Do you want to write a song for the dead Starks? Go ahead, write one. I'm in this world a little while longer to defend the Lannisters, to defend my blood.

Good. Let them remember what happens when they march on the south. All the Stark men are dead. Winterfell is a ruin. Roose Bolton will be named Warden of the North until your son by Sansa comes of age.
I believe you still have some work to do on that score.
One way or another, you will get that girl pregnant.
Shall I explain to you in one easy lesson how the world works?

The house that puts family first will always defeat the house that puts the whims and wishes of its sons and daughters first. A good man does everything in his power to better his family's position regardless of his own selfish desires.
Does that amuse you?
Easy for me, is it?
The day that you were born. I wanted to carry you into the sea and let the waves wash you away. Instead, I let you live. And I brought you as my son. Because you're a Lannister.
Not another word. We've been over this, the matter's closed.
You are betrothed to Loras Tyrell. Still betrothed to Loras Tyrell and you will marry Loras Tyrell as soon as Tommen marries Margaery.

Jaime cannot marry or inherit lands. Tyrion's sentence will be carried out tomorrow. You have, on several occasions, made great claims about your commitment to this family's future. Your role in that future is more vital now than it ever was.

When you were nine years old, I was called to the capital. I decided to take your brother with me and not you. You insisted that you would not be left at Casterly Rock under any circumstances and if you recall-
Do you think you'll be the first person dragged into a sept to be married against her will?
No.
And how will you do that?
And what truth would that be?
Known what?
No, no, no, no, no.
I don't believe you.

Tyrion! Put down the crossbow. Who released you? Ah, your brother ,I expect. He always had a soft spot for you. Come, we’ll go and talk in my chambers. Is this how you want to speak to me? Shaming your father has always given you pleasure…

Yes. But you refused to die. I respect that, even admire it. You fight for what’s yours. I’d never let them execute you; is that what you fear? I’d never let Illyn Payne take your head. You’re a Lannister. You’re my son.
Who?
Bah, Tyrion. Put down that crossbow.
It doesn’t matter.
She was a whore!
And what? You’ll kill your own father in the privy? No. You’re my son. Enough of this nonsense…
Enough. We’ll go back to my chambers and speak with some dignity.
What, are you afraid of a dead whore?
You shot me. You’re no son of mine.
The battle is over. We have won.
Surrender the Iron Throne?
No, a king who runs will not be king for long. He's a Lannister. He'll stand and fight. Stannis, two days from the capital and the wolf at my doorstep.
Ha! The last time the scouts assured us of Stark's movement, he lured us into a trap. Which is why my son is his prisoner.
He's a boy and he's never lost a battle. He'll risk anything at any time because he doesn't know enough to be afraid.

We'll ride at nightfall. I want a full night's march before he knows we're on the move. Clegane, you'll maintain a garrison here at Harrenhal. Track down this brotherhood and destroy them. The girl has proven herself a good servant. She'll stay on with you. See that he doesn't get drunk in the evenings. He's poor company when he's sober, but he's better at his work.
You seem rather drunk.
This isn't about your wedding. Renly Baratheon had a wedding. Your wife needs a child, a Lannister child, as soon as possible.
If you're going to give her one, you need to perform.
More than once.
You can drink, you can joke, you can engage in juvenile attempts to make your father uncomfortable, but you will do your duty.
I believe we can dispense with the bedding, Your Grace. I'm sure Tyrion did not mean to threaten the king.
Your uncle is clearly quite drunk, Your Grace.
Wolfsbane. A rare substance. This is no common assassin.
I don't care if you hanged 100. A man tried to kill me. I want his name and I want his head.

Pretentious name for a band of outlaws. We can't allow rebels behind our lines to harass us with impunity. We look like fools and they look like heroes. That's how kings fall. I want them dead, every one.
Mmm. Is that mutton?
Don't like mutton.
Leave it. Are you hungry?
Of course you are. Eat.
It's bad manners to refuse a lord's offer. Sit. Eat.
You're small for your age. I suppose you've been underfed your whole life.
Mmm. This will be my last war. Win or lose.
Do you think I'd be in my position if I had lost a war?
But this is the one I'll be remembered for. The War of Five Kings, they're calling it. My legacy will be determined in the coming months.

Do you know what legacy means? It's what you pass down to your children and your children's children. It's what remains of you when you're gone. Harren the Black thought this castle would be his legacy. The greatest fortress ever built.
Yes. Dragons happened.

Harrenhal was built to withstand an attack from the land. A million men could have marched on these walls, and a million men would have been repelled. But an attack from the air with dragon fire Mmm-mmm. Harren and all his sons roasted alive within these walls. Aegon Targaryen changed the rules. That's why every child alive still knows his name
Mmm?
Correct. A student of history, are you?
I'm sure I knew that when I was a boy.
Hmm. She's a heroine of yours, I take it?
Aren't most girls more interested in the pretty maidens from the songs? Jonquil with the flowers in her hair?
You remind me of my daughter. Where did you learn all this stuff about Visenya and her Valyrian steel sword?
He was a well-read stonemason. Can't say I've ever met a literate stonemason.
Careful now, girl. I enjoy you, but be careful. Take that back to the kitchen. Eat what you want.
And, girl.
"M'lord.
" Lowborn girls say "m'lord," not "my lord. " If you're going to pose as a commoner, you should do it properly.
You're too smart for your own good. Has anyone told you that?
Go on.
Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You wanted to speak to me?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You're welcome to attend the meetings of your small council, Your Grace. Any or all of them.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Of course.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I have, yes.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: The Tower of the Hand is where I work. To walk from there to here would take time, time I could otherwise spend productively.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: We could arrange to have you carried.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Where did you hear about this?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Apparently so.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: When I was Hand of the King under your father's predecessor, the skulls of all the Targaryen dragons were kept in this room. The skull of the last of them was right here. It was the size of an apple.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Yes, and the creature to whom it belonged died 300 years ago. Curiosities on the far side of the world are no threat to us.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Because we have been told as much by the many experts who serve the realm by counseling the king on matters about which he knows nothing.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You are being counseled at this very moment.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: From now on, I will see to it that you are appropriately consulted on important matters whenever necessary. Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Can you read?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Can you read?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: This letter detailing our infantry movements was meant for Lord Damon of House Marbrand. It was sent to Lord Marlyn of House Dormand.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Girl, fetch me the History of the Greater and the Lesser Houses. It's the one on this
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: My cupbearer can read better than you.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: To whom does House Dormand owe allegiance?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: To the Starks of Winterfell! Who have 20,000 men and my son! I judged you might be good for something more than brutalizing peasants. I see I overestimated you. If you ever put my son's life at risk again, I'll Leave us.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Put the book away, girl. Maybe you should devise our next battle plan while you're about it.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Mmm. Give us the room.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: (to Arya) Clear all this.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: (not amused) Baelish.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Wine?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: What news from the capital?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Ah, the late King Renly. Rather a short reign. Murdered by a woman, I hear.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Here. Men love to blame demons when their grand plans unravel.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You say that as if you were the first man alive to think it. Yes, a crisis is an opportunity. What other brilliant insights have you brought me today?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Yes, yes, yes.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Wants to be queen.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: House Tyrell rebelled against the Iron Throne. Against my grandson.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: (to Arya) More wine for Lord Baelish.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You would ride there yourself?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I'll have an answer by nightfall. That'll be all, girl.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: And what else?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Why?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Hmm.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Who taught you to read?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Hmm. I taught my son Jaime to read. The maester came to me one day, told me he wasn't learning. He couldn't make sense of the letters. He reversed them in his head. The maester said he'd heard tell of this affliction and that we simply must accept it. Ha! After that, I sat Jaime down for four hours every day until he learned. He hated me for it, for a time. For a long time. But he learned. Hmm. Where is your father? Is he alive? Who was he?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Quite a man. What killed him?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You're a sharp little thing, aren't you?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: No. But you've already begun.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I did. I grew up with him. I watched him grow old.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: He loved us. He was a good man, but a weak man. A weak man who nearly destroyed our house and name. I'm cold.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Mmm.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Guard!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Why?
Is rich, the most beautiful woman in all seven kingdoms and the mother of the king.
Old?
The years punish us as well, I promise you that. My stomach remains quite strong, however. The only thing that might turn it are details of your grandson's nocturnal activities. Do you deny them?
And a boy with his affliction should be grateful for the opportunity to marry the most beautiful woman in the kingdoms and remove the stain from his name.
Of course.
No.
Never.
Perhaps Highgarden has a high tolerance for unnatural behavior.
I will not breathe further life into a malicious lie by discussing it.
I don't care what people believe. And neither do you.
Now, if the rumors about my children were true, then Joffrey is no king at all and House Tyrell is throwing its prized flower into the dirt.
The uncertainty makes you uncomfortable. All right. I'll remove it for you.
If you refuse to marry Loras to Cersei, I will name him to the Kingsguard. I'm sure you're familiar with the Kingsguard vows. He will never marry. He will never have children.
The Tyrell name will fade. And Highgarden will go to the children of Joffrey and Margaery.
I would have my grandson protected by a skilled warrior who takes his vows seriously. So, shall I draw up the order? Or do you consent to this marriage?
The trial begins this afternoon. We only have the morning for affairs of state. Shall we begin?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: What would it take to make the common soldier stupid enough to try his luck with the Hound?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Make it 100. What else?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: The Targaryen girl?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Joffrey didn't die on his watch. Dismissing him was as insulting as it was stupid.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Dragons haven't won a war in 300 years. Armies win them all the time. She must be dealt with.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Eventually, if it comes to that. Can your little birds find their way into Meereen?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Mmm. Lord Tyrell, be a good man. Fetch my quill and paper.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Tyrion of the House Lannister, you stand accused by the Queen Regent of regicide. Did you kill King Joffrey?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Did your wife, the Lady Sansa?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: How would you say he died, then?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: So you would blame the bakers?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Silence.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Silence! You will not speak unless called upon. You're dismissed, Ser Meryn.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: By whom?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Grand Maester, you examined King Joffrey's corpse. Was it without question poison that killed him?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Was this one of the poisons stolen from your store?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Thank you, Your Grace, for the courage of your testimony.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: One.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: We will adjourn for now.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Toll the bells in an hour's time.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I've condemned no one. The trial is not over.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I know nothing of the sort.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: He killed his king.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: It won't be murder. It'll be justice.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I'm performing my sworn duty as Hand of the King. If Tyrion is found guilty, he will be punished accordingly.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: No, he'll be punished accordingly.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I'm well aware of your oath.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: What happens to my dynasty if I spare the life of my grandson's killer?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Done.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: When the testimony's concluded and a guilty verdict rendered, Tyrion will be given the chance to speak. He'll plead for mercy. I'll allow him to join the Night's Watch. In three days' time, he'll depart for Castle Black and live out his days at the Wall. You'll remove your white cloak immediately. You will leave King's Landing to assume your rightful place at Casterly Rock. You will marry a suitable woman and father children named Lannister. And you'll never turn your back on your family again.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: And you have mine.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: The crown may call its next witness.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: State your name.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Do you swear by all the gods that your testimony will be true and honest?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Do you know this man?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: How do you know him?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: This man stands accused of murdering King Joffrey. What do you know of this?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Silence! Continue.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: How did you come to be in his service?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Silence. Silence!
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You wish to confess?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Tyrion. Do you wish to confess?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You admit you poisoned the king?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You are not on trial for being a dwarf.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Have you nothing to say in your defense?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Ser Meryn. Ser Meryn. Escort the prisoner back to his cell.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: The Starks understand winter better than we ever will. The cold won't beat them.

And I'm sure if those same spies snuck into our own encampments, they would report growing discontent amongst the Southern lords. This is war, no one's content. We've underestimated the Stark boy for too long. He has a good mind for warfare, his men worship him. And as long as he keeps winning battles, they'll keep believing he is King in the North. You've been waiting for him to fail. He is not going to fail, not without our help. So how do we stop him?
Yes, I think you would, Reginald. And because you're my cousin, I might even let you wake from that sleep. Go, I'm sure your wife must miss you.
Well, then you'd better start riding. Go, before I change my mind and send her your head.
If your name wasn't Lannister, you'd be scrubbing out pots in the cook's tent. Go!
Not wine, water. We'll be here for some time. Girl, where are you from?
And who are the Lords of Maidenpool? Remind me.
And what is their sigil?
A red salmon. I think a Maidenpool girl would remember that. You're a Northerner, aren't you?
Good. One more time, where are you from?
And what do they say of Robb Stark in the North?
And?
And do you believe them?
Fetch that water.
You're late.
Our business concerns her, too. Sit.
Never mind that now. We have something important to discuss.
Your sister has learned that your new friends the Tyrells are plotting to marry Sansa Stark to Ser Loras.
Your jokes are not appreciated.
I bring them into the royal fold and this is how they repay me, by trying to steal the key to the North out from under me.

The Karstarks have marched home. The young wolf has lost half his army. His days are numbered. Theon Greyjoy murdered both his brothers. That makes Sansa Stark the heir to Winterfell. And I am not about to hand her over to the Tyrells.

There's nothing to refuse. This is a plot. Plots are not public knowledge. And the Tyrells won't carry this one out until after Joffrey's wedding. We need to act first and kill this union in its crib.
We find Sansa Stark a different husband.
I can and I do.
There, you see? You will wed her, bed her, and put a child in her. Surely you're capable of that.
You wanted to be rewarded for your valor in battle. Sansa Stark is a finer reward than you could ever dare hope for. And it is past time you were wed.
Only too well.
Tyrion will do as he's bid. As will you.
You'll marry Ser Loras.
The boy is heir to Highgarden. Tyrion will secure the North, you will secure the Reach.
Yes, you will. You're still fertile. You need to marry again and breed.
You're my daughter! You will do as I command and you will marry Loras Tyrell and put an end to the disgusting rumors about you once and for all.
Not another word. My children. You've disgraced the Lannister name for far too long.
When will the wedding take place in your mind?
A fortnight?
No jugglers, no jousting dwarves, no 77-course meals.
And your wedding to Loras?
Shortly?
I know you don't like them. I didn't like your husband. Used to pat me on the back a lot. I didn't trust him.
You don't need to make formal alliances with people you trust.
Ourselves alone.
The Tyrells are our only true rivals in terms of resources and we need them on our side.
Robert had me funding him. Wars swallow gold like a pit in the earth.
Do you know how much gold was mined in the Westerlands this past year?
Go on, your best guess.
Doesn't matter. The answer is the same.
Our last working mine ran dry three years ago.
The crown owes the Iron Bank of Braavos a tremendous amount of money.
A tremendous amount.
The Iron Bank is the Iron Bank. There is no someone.

And a temple is comprised of stones. One stone crumbles and another takes its place. And the temple holds its form for 1,000 years or more. That's what the Iron Bank is... a temple. We all live in its shadow and almost none of us know it. You can't run from them. You can't cheat them. You can't sway them with excuses. If you owe them money and you don't want to crumble yourself, you pay it back. Vesting the Tyrells in the crown will help a great deal in this respect.
I know you're building a strong case against Tyrion. And as a mother, that is your right. But as a judge, I cannot discuss the trial with you.
What's this?
Evidently not. Why are these prisoners not in their cells?
Are we so well-manned that we can afford to discard able young bodies and skilled laborers?
You, do you have a trade?
He'll do no such thing. This one's a girl, you idiot, dressed as a boy. Why?
Smart. More than I can say for this lot. Get these prisoners to work. Bring the girl. I need a new cupbearer.
You wanted to speak to me?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: What about him?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: When Catelyn Stark took Tyrion prisoner what did I do in response?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: And if I would start a war for that lecherous little stump, what do you think I am doing for my oldest son and heir?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Whatever I can.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You're still here.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Why?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: All right. Contribute.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: The Tyrells helped us defeat Stannis Baratheon. The Tyrells saved your life, your children's lives.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Good. I wish you knew how to manipulate him. I don't distrust you because you're a woman. I distrust you because you're not as smart as you think you are. You've allowed that boy to ride roughshod over you and everyone else in this city.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I will.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: What news of Jaime?

20,000 unwashed northerns have known about his escape for weeks. Collectively, you control more spies and informants than the rest of the world combined. Do you mean to tell me that none of you has any notion of where he is?
Try harder. What do we have, then?
Well, let him have it. The name suits our purposes far more than that useless pile of rubble. The Lord of Harrenhal will make a worthy suitor for the widow Arryn.
You'll leave for the Eyrie as soon as possible and bring Lysa Arryn into the fold. Then the young wolf can add his own aunt to the list of people who have taken up arms against him.
Fully agreed. Which is why I'm naming you new Master of Coin.
It would appear to be a position that best suits your talents.
Find her. Bar the gates of the city. Seize every ship in the harbor.
No one leaves the capital! No one!
Your brother is dead. Do you know what that means?
I'm not trying to trick you.
Yes, you will become king. What kind of king do you think you'll be?
Mm, I think so as well. You've got the right temperament for it. But what makes a good king? Hmm? What is a good king's single most important quality?

Hmm. Baelor the Blessed was holy. And pious. He built this sept. He also named a six-year-old boy high septon because he thought the boy could work miracles. He ended up fasting himself into an early grave because food was of this world and this world was sinful.

Yeah. A good king must be just. Orys I was just. Everyone applauded his reforms. Nobles and commoners alike. But he wasn't just for long. He was murdered in his sleep after less than a year by his own brother. Was that truly just of him? To abandon his subjects to an evil that he was too gullible to recognize?
No.

Yes. Strength. King Robert was strong. He won the rebellion and crushed the Targaryen dynasty. And he attended three small council meetings in 17 years. He spent his time whoring and hunting and drinking until the last two killed him. So, we have a man who starves himself to death, a man who lets his own brother murder him, and a man who thinks that winning and ruling are the same thing. What do they all lack?
Yes!

Yes. But what is wisdom? Hmm? A house with great wealth and fertile lands asks you for your protection against another house with a strong Navy that could one day oppose you. How do you know which choice is wise and which isn't? You've any experience of treasuries and granaries or shipyards and soldiers?

No. Of course not. A wise king knows what he knows and what he doesn't. You're young. A wise young king listens to his counselors and heeds their advice until he comes of age. And the wisest kings continue to listen to them long afterwards... Your brother was not a wise king. Your brother was not a good king. If he had been, perhaps he'd still be alive. Now, as the king, you will have to marry. Do you understand why?
Yes, but why? To further the family line. Do you know how that happens?
Yes, but has anyone explained the details to you?
Prince Oberyn.
May we have the room?
No, thank you.
No, thank you.
Are you?
Which way is that?
Some believe the king choked.
I hear you studied poisons at the Citadel.
Your hatred for my family is rather well known. You arrive at the capital, an expert in poisoning, some days later my grandson dies of poisoning.
You spoke with Tyrion in this very brothel on the day that you arrived. What did you discuss?
What did you discuss?
For which you blame me.
Here I stand unarmed, unguarded. Should I be concerned?
Men at war commit all kinds of crimes without their superiors' knowledge.
Categorically.
I'm sure he would enjoy speaking with you.
I could arrange for this meeting.
There will be a trial for my son. And as custom dictates, three judges will render a verdict. I will preside. Mace Tyrell will serve as the second judge. I would like you to be the third.
Not long ago, the Tyrells sided with Renly Baratheon. Declared themselves enemies of the throne. Now they are our strongest allies.
I will also invite you to sit on the small council to serve as one of the new king's principal advisors.

We are not the Seven Kingdoms until Dorne returns to the fold. The king is dead. The Greyjoys are in open rebellion. A wildling army marches on the Wall. And in the East, a Targaryen girl has three dragons. Before long, she will turn her eyes to Westeros. Only the Dornish managed to resist Aegon Targaryen and his dragons.
We need each other. You help me serve justice to the king's assassins and I will help you serve justice to Elia's.
Have her brought to the Tower of the Hand before the wedding.
One of only two Valyrian steel swords in the capital, Your Grace, freshly forged in your honor.
Bit much, wouldn't you say?
Proportionate to what?
People who spend their money on this sort of nonsense tend not to have it for long.
Shall we stick golden roses in half the meat pies to commemorate your generosity?
I'm not worried about the Iron Bank.
You're in rather a good mood.
I won't ask why.
Prince Oberyn.
Charmed.
Please give him our regards. With any luck, the gout will abate with time and he will be able to walk again.
Noblemen in my part of the country don't enjoy the same lifestyle as our counterparts in Dorne.
Am I enjoying it?
( chuckles ) Yes. I heard how happy you were. ( blows ) You brought a whore into my bed.
I sent you here to advise the king. I gave you real power and authority. You chose to spend your days as you always have, bedding harlots and drinking with thieves.
What do you want, Tyrion?
Maester Pycelle assured me your wounds were not fatal.

Jugglers and singers require applause. You are a Lannister. Do you think I demanded a garland of roses every time I suffered a wound on a battlefield? Hmm? Now, I have seven kingdoms to look after and three of them are in open rebellion. So tell me what you want.
You want Casterly Rock?

( sighs ) We'll find you accommodations more suited to your name and as a reward for your accomplishments during the battle of Blackwater Bay. And when the time is right, you will be given a position fit for your talents so that you can serve your family and protect our legacy. And if you serve faithfully, you will be rewarded with a suitable wife. And I would let myself be consumed by maggots before mocking the family name and making you heir to Casterly Rock.

Why? You ask that? You, who killed your mother to come into the world? You are an ill-made, spiteful little creature full of envy, lust, and low cunning. Men's laws give you the right to bear my name and display my colors since I cannot prove that you are not mine. And to teach me humility, the gods have condemned me to watch you waddle about wearing that proud lion that was my father's sigil and his father's before him. But neither gods nor men will ever compel me to let you turn Casterly Rock into your whorehouse. Go, now. Speak no more of your rights to Casterly Rock. Go.
Oh, one more thing. The next whore I catch in your bed I'll hang.
Mm-hmm.
It is.
There are three living smiths who know how to rework Valyrian steel. The finest of them was in Volantis. Came here to King's Landing at my invitation.
From someone who no longer had need of it.
And now we have two.
The original weapon was absurdly large. Plenty of steel for two swords.
You'll have to train your left hand.
You'll never be as good.
You can't serve in the Kingsguard with one hand.
The war is over. The king is safe. The king is never safe.
Other knights protected the king while you were a prisoner. They will continue to do so when you go home.
You'll return to Casterly Rock and rule in my stead.
I am the King's Hand. My place is here. I don't expect to see the Rock again before I die.
You won't be breaking anything. There is a precedent to relieve a Kingsguard of his duties. The king will exercise that prerogative.
No?
I don't believe I asked you a question.
If you think your bloody honor comes before...
What do you want?
For 40 years I've tried to teach you. If you haven't learned by now, you never will. Go. If serving as a glorified bodyguard is the sum of your ambition, go serve.
Keep it.
A one-handed man with no family needs all the help he can get.
They have my son.

(shouting)  They have my son! Get out, all of you. (to Tyrion) Not you. You were right about Eddard Stark. lf he were alive, we could have used him to broker a peace with Winterfell and Riverrun, which would have given us more time to deal with Robert's brothers. But now – madness. Madness and stupidity. l always thought you were a stunted fool. Perhaps l was wrong.

No one will stay here. Ser Gregor will head out with 500 riders and set the Riverland on fire from God's Eye to the Red Fork. The rest of us will regroup at Harrenhal. And you will go to King's Landing.

Rule. You will serve as Hand of the King in my stead. You will bring that boy king to heel, and his mother too, if needs be. And if you get so much as a whiff of treason from any of the rest – Baelish, Varys, Pycelle…
You're my son. Oh, one more thing. You will not take that whore to court. Do you understand?
The boy may lack experience and sense, but he does have a certain mindless provincial courage.
l do hope your savages are going to be of some use, otherwise we've wasted good steel on them.
When the battle commences, you and your wildlings will be in the vanguard.
When soldiers lack discipline, the fault lies with their commander.
There'll be no more discussion on the matter.
You're wounded.
Huh! The scouts were wrong. There were 2,000 Stark bannermen, not 20 [thousand].
He wasn't here.
With his other 18,000 men.
The rumors of your demise were unfounded.
And who are these Companions of yours?
This is Shagga Son of Dolf, Chieftain of the Stone Crows.
You left us no choice. The honor of the House was at stake.
Is our hostage.
Robert Baratheon is dead.
A green boy.
The wolf rushes into the lion's jaws.
It is said that the men of the Mountain clans are great warriors.
Attacking him was stupid.
Lannisters don't act like fools.
Why is he still alive?
Ned Stark.
Why is he still alive?
Clean...
You spend too much time worrying about what other people think of you.
Now that's what you want people to think of you.
When you hear them whispering "Kingslayer" behind your back, doesn't it bother you?
The lion doesn't concern himself with the opinions of the sheep.
He's a Lannister. He might be the lowest of the Lannisters, but he's one of us.
No, it's not an opinion, it's a fact!
Hmm.
We need more horses and wagons, if it please my lady.
And my lord.
And my queen. Sorry.
Well done. Well done.
( speaking Valyrian ) This one is pleased to have served you.
(shouts in Valyrian.)
There are none left.
(in Valyrian) All the men have boarded.
The Unsullied and the Second Songs are foreign soldiers brought here by a foreign queen to destroy our city and our history.
Yes, you’re a true liberator, aren’t you? You won’t torture me, you’ll just threaten my son.
If I tell you anything, they’ll kill me. So either you kill me or they do.
You can’t expect Knights of the Vale to side with wildling invaders.
Not by me.
Aye.
You're far from home, Lady Stark.
Ser Vardis Egen, Knight of The Vale. Is Lady Arryn expecting your visit?
May I ask, my Lady, why he is with you?
He doesn't look like a prisoner.
Yes, my Lady. She will at that.
Stop!
When you imagine yourself up there, how do you look? Does the crown fit? Do all the lords and ladies simper and bow, the ones who sneered at you for years?
A man with great ambition and no morals… l wouldn't bet against you.
l must be one of the few men in this city who doesn't want to be King.
Oh, you can do better than that.
Have you? Do you spend a lot of time wondering what's between my legs?
l am flattered, of course, to be pictured at all.
Am l? That is good to know. Do you lie awake at night fearing my gash?
And l admire you, Lord Baelish. A grasper from a minor House with a major talent for befriending powerful men – and women.
So here we stand in mutual admiration and respect.
(Joffrey arrives, Varys bows)  Serving a new King.
My king.
No need for that, my dear.
I'm not like most men.
(laughs)
I would like you to tell me if working for Lord Baelish has been all you'd hoped it would be.
You wouldn't remember me if we had?
And I think you remember all of them. I think your true talents are wasted on them.
You're afraid. Why? Nothing dangerous down there.
And, unlike your current employer, I protect those who work for me. I don't abuse them to satisfy royal whims or force them to abuse each other.
I thought you said you knew who I was.
Littlefinger looks at you and sees a collection of profitable holes. I see a potential partner.
Oh, you should be. He's a dangerous man. But everyone has their weaknesses. Your current employer hides his very well, but not as well as he thinks.
Why would I lie about it?
Where before there was nothing but love. Ser Mandon Moore tried to kill you on your sister's orders. If it weren't for your squire's bravery, you'd be a dead man.
I'm afraid your friend has been relieved of his command of the City Watch. The gold cloaks are now firmly in the hands of your father or your sister. It varies from cloak to cloak.
Have gone home. Your father paid them quite handsomely. I'm afraid we won't be seeing each other for some time, my lord.
We are. Podrick, would you mind?
There are many who know that without you this city faced certain defeat. The king won't give you any honors, the histories won't mention you, but we will not forget.
Come along now, Podrick.
Your Grace, Lady Sansa is your aunt by marriage.
When did you come to this strange country?
You were only a child.
You've been a good influence on our mutual friend, you know. He used to drink from sundown to sunup, visit three brothels a night, gamble away his father's money. Now it's just the drinking.
She is a sweet young thing. None of this is her fault.
No, I expect not.

We break bread with them, but that doesn't make us family. We've learned their language, but we'll never be their countrymen. If you let yourself believe that a foreign girl with no name could spend her life with the son of Tywin Lannister--
You have one name. As do I. Here only the family name matters.

Diamonds. I'd tell you to beware carrying so much wealth, but you know how to protect yourself. Get on one of those ships. Sail to Pentos or Lys or Myr. You can buy a house with these diamonds, a very large house. Hire servants. Start a new life, a good life, far from here. The mysterious foreign beauty. You'll have suitors lining up.
What have you done? Quickly. Trust me, my friend, I’ve brought you this far.
I thought we were so happy together until you abandoned me.
The birds sing in the west, the birds sing in the east. If one knows how to listen. They tell me you’ve already found favor with the Mother of Dragons.
Information is the key. You need to learn your enemies’ strengths and strategies. You need to learn which of your friends are not your friends.
If only. (beat) A grand old city, choking on violence, corruption, and deceit. Who could possibly have any experience managing such a massive, ungainly beast?
Oh, I know.
Fire and blood.
You've seen better days, my lord.
No, no, many still love you. Sansa came to court this morning to plead for your life.
You do me wrong, my lord. Your blood is the last thing l want.
l could. But will l? No. As l said, I'm no hero.
Peace. Did you know that your son is marching south with an army of Northmen? Loyal lad. Fighting for his father's freedom.

Boys have been conquerors before. But the man giving Cersei sleepless nights is the king's the late king's brother. Lord Stannis has the best claim to the throne. He is a proven battle commander and he is utterly without mercy.
Sansa pleaded so sweetly for your life. lt would be a shame to throw it away. Cersei is no fool. She knows a tame wolf is more use to her than a dead one.

l want you to serve the realm! Tell the queen you will confess your vile treason, tell your son to lay down his sword and proclaim Joffrey as the true heir. Cersei knows you as a man of honor. lf you give her the peace she needs, and promise to carry her secret to your grave, l believe she will allow you to take the black and live out your days on the Wall with your brother and your bastard son.
Pity. Such a pity. What of your daughter's life, my lord? ls that a precious thing to you?
I've always hated the bells. They ring for horror. A dead king, a city under siege.
Exactly. Podrick, is that it?
I'm not entirely sure what you're suggesting.
Do you trust him?
Good.
The map you asked for.
Closer to 50. The Targaryens built this city to withstand a siege and to provide escape if necessary.
That is good to hear. Though I'm sure many captains say the same while their ship is afloat. You look well suited for battle, my lord.
For all our sakes, I hope you are wrong. My little birds tell me that Stannis Baratheon has taken up with a red priestess from Asshai.
You don't believe in the old powers, my lord?
I think you believe in what you see and in what those you trust have seen. You probably don't entirely trust me.
And yet I have seen things and heard things, things you have not, things I wish I had not. I don't believe I've ever told you how I was cut.

One day, I will. The dark arts have provided Lord Stannis with his armies and paved his path to our door. For a man in service to such powers to sit on the Iron Throne, I can think of nothing worse. And tonight, I believe you are the only man who can stop him.
My Lord Hand. Commander. I must compliment you on the gold cloaks' performance these last few weeks. Did you know there has been a marked drop in thievery?
Given the circumstances, my lord, I believe extreme measures are warranted.
Ah. "The Great Sieges of Westeros." Thrilling subject. Shame Archmaester Ch'Vyalthan wasn't a better writer.
And if Stannis does attack the Mud Gate, what is our plan?
We don't have that many books.
What do we have?
No man can be in all rooms at all times. I have many little birds in the North, my lord, but I haven't heard their songs since Theon Greyjoy captured Winterfell.
I am trying.
Oh, no one disputes that, my lord, not even the multitudes who despise you.
I wish we could, too.
If we're going to play, you'll have to start.
A most highborn plumber.
And you play it well.
They say he burns his enemies alive to honor the Lord of Light.
In the Summer Isles, they worship a fertility goddess with 16 teats.
This morning, I heard a song all the way from Qarth beyond the Red Waste. Daenerys Targaryen lives.
She has three dragons. But even if what they say is true, it'll be years before they are fully grown. And then there will be nowhere to hide.
Would you?
You made a pact with fanatics.
If you shaved your beard with a straight razor, you’d say the razor worked. That doesn’t mean it won’t cut your throat.
I know.
If I don’t return, you’ll know I was wrong.
We need friends in Westeros. And we need ships.
She’ll come back. She has to. My heart’s been broken too many times already.
I’ll walk the rest of the way myself. I can’t go off on a secret mission in the company of the most famous dwarf in the city.
And that would be double what now, exactly?
1000 blades taken from the hands of Aegon's fallen enemies, forged in the fiery breath of Balerion the Dread.
Ha, I'm sure you have. Ugly old thing. Yet it has a certain appeal. The Lysa Arryn of chairs. Shame you had to settle for your second choice.
Thwarting you has never been my primary ambition, I promise you. Although who doesn't like to see their friends fail now and then?
I did what I did for the good of the realm.
But what do we have left once we abandon the lie? Chaos. A gaping pit waiting to swallow us all.
Sandor Clegane has been spotted in the Riverlands, my lord.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: My birds tell me the Hound slaughtered five of our soldiers. I believe the phrase "fuck the king" was uttered.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: 10 silver stags seems a generous bounty.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: More whispers from the east, my lord.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Daenerys has taken up residence in Meereen. She has conquered the city and rules as its queen.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: No longer. He appears to be fully devoted to her. As for Ser Barristan, it would seem he took his dismissal from the Kingsguard a bit harder than anticipated.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: A child with two seasoned warriors counseling her and a powerful army at her back, Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Most certainly, my Lord Hand.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Prince Oberyn.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Only Varys. I'm not actually a nobleman. No one is under obligation to call me lord.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You seem quite knowledgeable about the Unsullied. Did you spend much time in Essos?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: May I ask why?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Most of us aren't princes.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I have an ear for accents. I've lost my accent entirely.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: It's a long story.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: People I trust.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: (shakes his head)
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Not at all. But I was never interested in girls, either.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Nothing.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Not me. When I see what desire does to people, what it's done to this country, I am very glad to have no part in it. Besides, the absence of desire leaves one free to pursue other things.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I'm afraid I do, my lord. He said, "Perhaps you should speak more softly to me, then. Monsters are dangerous and just now kings are dying like flies."
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Yes. After we received word of Robb Stark's death. He didn't seem gladdened by the news. Perhaps his marriage to Sansa Stark had made him more sympathetic to the northern cause.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Sadly, my lord, I never forget a thing.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: How is your son, my Lord?
But his mind is sound?
A blessing then. I suffered an early mutilation myself. Some doors close forever... others open in the most unexpected places. May I?

If the wrong ears heard what I'm about to tell you, off comes my head. And who would mourn poor Varys then? North or South, they sing no songs for spiders. But there are things you must know. You are the King's Hand and the King is a fool... your friend, I know, but a fool... and doomed unless you save him.
I didn't trust you.
The Queen is not the only one who has been watching you closely. There are few men of honor in the capital. You are one of them. I would like to believe I am another, strange as that may seem.
The same sort as Jon Arryn. The tears of Lys, they call it. A rare and costly thing, as clear and tasteless as water. It leaves no trace.
Some dear friend, no doubt. But which one? There were many. Lord Arryn was a kind and trusting man. There was one boy... all he was he owed to Jon Arryn.
Pity what happened to him, just when his life seemed to be going so nicely.
Someone who could afford it.
He started asking questions.
He's found one bastard already. He has the book. The rest will come.
The gods alone know. The fools tried to kill his son. What's worse, they botched it. The wolf and the lion will be at each other's throats. We will be at war soon, my friend.
This Hand is not the other.
"Delay," you say. "Move fast, " I reply. This is no longer a game for two players.
The first to arrive and the last to leave. I admire your industry.
We all have our qualities.
I think you're mistaking business with pleasure.
Oh, I'm sure. Lord Redwyne likes his boys very young, I hear.
Though I suppose beauty is a subjective quality, no? Is it true that Ser Marlon of Tumblestone prefers amputees?

And I heard the most awful rumor about a certain lord with a taste for fresh cadavers. Must be enormously difficult to accommodate that inclination. The logistics alone... to find beautiful corpses before they rot.
Strictly speaking.
Do you know, I have no idea where they are? And we had been so close. But enough about me. How have you been since we last saw each other?
Now the last time I saw you, you were talking to the Hand of the King.
Eyes I own.

Everyone's well aware of your enduring fondness for Lord Stark's wife. If the Lannisters were behind the attempt on the Stark boy's life and it was discovered that you helped the Starks come to that conclusion... To think... A simple word to the Queen...
Oh.
Have you?
Was that you under the bed?
Oh, please.
Disturbing news from far away. Haven't you heard?
No rumor, my Lord. The princess is with child.
Ser Jorah Mormont. He is serving as adviser to the Targaryens.

I understand your misgivings, my Lord. Truly, I do. It is a terrible thing we must consider, a vile thing. Yet, we who presume to rule must sometimes do vile things for the good of the realm. Should the gods grant Daenerys a son, the realm will bleed.
Your Grace.
In the last fortnight since our pact with the Masters, how many killings have been carried out by the Sons of the Harpy?
And how many Masters have been butchered by the free men.
So it’s safe to say that a fragile peace has taken hold.
For now is the best we get in our profession.
Considering the city was on the brink of civil war, I’d say it’s a good start.
Sounds like quite the hero. Where would we find him?

A man named Stannis Baratheon was anointed as the chosen one by one of your priestesses. He, too, had a glorious destiny. He attacked King’s Landing and was soundly defeated by the man standing beside me. Last I had heard, he’d been defeated again, this time at Winterfell and this time for good.
I suppose it’s hard for a fanatic to admit a mistake. Isn’t that the whole point of being a fanatic? You’re always right. Everything is the Lord’s will.
And you, an honest servant of the lord, why should I trust you to know any more than the priestess who counseled Stannis?
Not burn him alive alongside his son.
That's what I used to tell myself about her father.  I found the traitors but I wasn't the one burning them alive.  I was only a purveyor of information.

It's what I told myself when I watched them beg for mercy, "I'm not the one doing it."  As the pitch of their screams rose higher, "I'm not the one doing it."  When their hair caught fire and the smell of the burning flesh filled the Thone Room, "I'm not the one doing it."
And she never will be... with the right counsel.  You need to find a way to make her listen.
Jon Snow.
It's a sealed scroll for the King in the North.
Nothing good.
The Wall has kept them out for thousands of years.  Presumably --
Anything you bring back will be useless unless Cersei grants us an audience and is somehow convinced not to murder us the moment we set foot in the capital.
Fair point.  How do you propose to find one?
Dreadful.
The realm prospers from such events, my Lord. They give the great a chance at glory, and the lowly a respite from their woes.
Oh, Lord Tyrion. Come in, come in.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Oh, always in confidence.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Oh, no. It's a wonderful time.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: End your life?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Proof? Will there be a trial?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I have no proof, only whispers. Before all this nastiness, I was going to tell you the story of how I was cut. Do you want to hear it still?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: As a boy, I travelled with a troupe of actors through the Free Cities. One day in Myr, a certain man made my master an offer too tempting to refuse. I feared the man meant to use me as I'd heard some men used small boys. But what he wanted was far worse. He gave me a potion that made me powerless to move or speak, yet did nothing to dull my senses. With a hooked blade he sliced me, root and stem, chanting all the while. He burned my parts in a brazier. The flames turned blue and I heard a voice answer his call. I still dream of that night. Not of the sorcerer, not of his blade. I dream of the voice from the flames. Was it a god? A demon? A conjuror's trick? I don't know. But the sorcerer called and a voice answered. And ever since that day, I have hated magic and all those who practice it. But you can see why I was eager to aid in your fight against Stannis and his Red Priestess. A symbolic revenge of sorts.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You do not possess at the moment.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: But influence is largely a matter of patience, I have found. Once I had served the sorcerer's purpose, he threw me out of his house to die. I resolved to live to spite him. I begged. I sold what parts of my body remained to me. I became an excellent thief and soon learned that the contents of a man's letters are more valuable than the contents of his purse. Step by step, one distasteful task after another, I made my way from the slums of Myr to the small council chamber. Influence grows like a weed. I tended mine patiently until its tendrils reached from the Red Keep all the way across to the far side of the world where I managed to wrap them around something very special.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Hello, my old friend. It's been a long time. I have no doubt the revenge you want will be yours in time if you have the stomach for it.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Is he very large?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Extraordinary.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: We're talking about the same Podrick? The quiet boy in Lord Tyrion's service? Seems a bit simple?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: What did he do to them?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: So what did they say?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Huh. Prodigies appear in the oddest of places. And what did Littlefinger say about this loss of income?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Ah, yes, to pursue his enduring love of the Lady Arryn and the title that comes with her. Sad, really. Is that all it takes to make our friend lose interest in poor Sansa Stark?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: What makes you say so?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Ah. You can read. Rare for a woman in your profession.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Of course. I'm missing something obvious you're about to point out?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Who is important enough to Littlefinger to merit a featherbed besides Littlefinger? Could he be bringing one of his girls?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: How do you know?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Prodigies in odd places indeed.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: My lady. I wanted to personally welcome you to King's Landing. The city has been made brighter by your presence.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: A little obvious, perhaps.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Might I sit?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You've taken an interest in Sansa Stark.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Is she?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: She has, sadly. Well, forgive me for wasting your time, then. I thought we shared certain hopes for her well-being.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I choose my allies carefully and my enemies more carefully still.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Neither. A babe in the woods. I admired her father.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I could not help Lord Stark. Perhaps I can help his daughter.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You're not the only one who has taken an interest in her.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Indeed. She'd make a lovely match for the right suitor.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Littlefinger is not long for the capital. A confidant of mine has told me that when he goes, Sansa Stark goes with him.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Littlefinger was born with no lands, no wealth, no armies. He has acquired the first two. How long before he has the army?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Perhaps you'll laugh, but I know him better than most and this is the truth. Littlefinger is one of the most dangerous men in Westeros. If Robb Stark falls, Sansa Stark is the key to the North.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Which seems such a shame. Why should a man with such a low reputation steal away such a lovely bride?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Actually, I rather enjoy him. But he would see this country burn if he could be king of the ashes.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You are too kind. I believe I have a possible solution.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: And the Golden Company has arrived in King's Landing, courtesy of the Greyjoy fleet. The balance has grown distressingly even.
Thankfully, she's losing allies by the day. Yara Greyjoy has retaken the Iron Islands in her queen's name. The new Prince of Dorne pledges his support.
How many others know?
Well, then it's not a secret anymore. It's information. If a handful of people know now, hundreds will know soon. Then what happens?
No, it's worse than that. He has the better claim to the throne.
I'm not sure it matters what he wants. The fact is, people are drawn to him. Wildlings, Northmen. He's a war hero.
She's his aunt.

No, but Jon grew up in Winterfell. Is marrying your aunt common in the North? You know our queen better than I do. Do you think she wants to share the throne? She does not like to have her authority questioned.
I worry about her state of mind.
Your Grace. I promised you I would look you in the eye and speak directly if I ever thought you were making a mistake. This is a mistake.
(nods)

Cersei needs to be destroyed, but if we attack King's Landing with Drogon and the Unsullied and the Dothraki, tens of thousands of innocents will die. That is why Cersei is bringing them into the Red Keep. These are the people you came here to protect. I beg you, Your Grace. Do not destroy the city you came to save. Do not become what you have always struggled to defeat.
I've served tyrants most of my life. They all talk about destiny.
Perhaps that's the problem. Her life has convinced her that she was sent here to save us all.
Then there's the problem of Jon Snow. Perhaps it's actually a solution. You know them both. Tell me, who do you think would make a better ruler?
Have you considered the best ruler might be someone who doesn't want to rule?
Don't pretend you haven't thought about it.
He's temperate and measured. He's a man, which makes him more appealing to the lords of Westeros, whose support we are going to need.
And he's the heir to the throne. Yes, because he's a man. Cocks are important, I'm afraid.
She's too strong for him. She'd bend him to her will, as she already has.
As you have? You're drinking quite a lot. A Targaryen father and a Stark mother. Jon's the one man alive who might actually be able to keep the North in the Seven Kingdoms.
You've always known my reasons.
Even if you know it's a mistake?
You know where my loyalty stands. You know I will never betray the realm.

Millions of people, many of whom will die if the wrong person sits on that throne. We don't know their names, but they're just as real as you and I. They deserve to live. They deserve food for their children. I will act in their interest, no matter the personal cost.
I've spoken as honestly as I can. Each of us has a choice to make. I pray we choose wisely.
Lord Stark.
I was grievously sorry to hear of your troubles on the Kingsroad. We are all praying for Prince Joffrey's full recovery.
His Grace has many cares. He entrusts some small matters to us that we might lighten the load.
You are the King's Hand, Lord Stark, we serve at your pleasure.
Lady Stark.
To see you again after so many years is a blessing. Your poor hands.
Knowledge is my trade, my Lady. Did you bring the dagger with you, by any chance? My little birds are everywhere. Even in the north. They whisper to me the strangest stories. Valyrian steel.
I must admit I do not.
Ooh. "The Queen mustn't know." I love conversations that begin this way.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Theon Greyjoy? Forgive me, my lord, but how? He grew up a ward of Winterfell. He fights for Robb Stark.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You'll be pleased to know our mutual friend is doing quite well in Lady Sansa's service.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: And it seems the Grand Maester has found his way into a black cell? Well played, My Lord Hand. But should I be worried? Janos Slynt, Pycelle. The Small Council grows smaller every day.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Power is a curious thing, my lord. Are you fond of riddles?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Three great men sit in a room. A king, a priest and a rich man. Between them stands a common sellsword. Each great man bids the sellsword kill the other two. Who lives, who dies?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Does it? He has neither crown nor gold nor favor with the gods.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: But if it's swordsmen who rule, why do we pretend kings hold all the power? When Ned Stark lost his head, who was truly responsible? Joffrey? The executioner? Or something else?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Power resides where men believe it resides. It's a trick, a shadow on the wall. And a very small man can cast a very large shadow.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: We are trying, my lord.

Robb Stark and most of his bannermen are in Riverrun for the funeral of his grandfather Lord Hoster Tully. In Stark's absence, Roose Bolton holds Harrenhal, which would seem to make him Lord of Harrenhal, in practice if not in name.
Volantis is a large city.
The likelihood of you being spotted here increases a hundredfold.
I'm not sure how many new ways I can find of saying this.
It's a perfectly good face.
If anyone recognizes you, you'll lose more than that.

Yes. The Volantene masters are very organized. (Shot of a man with a fly tattooed onto his cheek). Flies for dung shovelers. (Shot of a man with a hammer tattooed onto his cheek). Hammers for builders. (Shot of a man inspecting women with tears tattooed onto their cheeks). Tears for whores. Lest they forget.
(Aside) We should keep moving.
Thank you.
Someone who inspires priests and whores is worth taking seriously.
Where're you going?
If we could have the room.
You look lovely today, my dear. You really do. How you climbed all those steps without breaking a sweat.
I am not a torturer.
Though it so often is what people deserve.

And it does provide answers. But they’re usually the wrong answers. My job is to find the right answers. Do you know how I do that? I do it by making people happy. I’d like to make you happy, Vala. That’s your name, isn’t it, Vala?

That’s all right. I know who you are and what you’ve done. You’ve done a lot. You’ve sacrificed your body for a cause, which is more than most people do. And you’ve helped the Sons of the Harpy murder the Unsullied and the Second Sons.

Children are blameless. I have never hurt them. Your boy is in no immediate danger, this I swear to you. But between us, dear, you did conspire to kill the queen’s soldiers. We both know the penalty for that crime. How will poor Dom get on without his mother? And with his breathing problem.

Yes, from your perspective, this is a problem. There is a third option, though. A ship leaving tomorrow for Pentos. I’ve already booked passage for a woman and her young son. I’ll even throw in a bag of silver to help you start again.
Though I’m afraid we’ll have to ask one of our leather-clad friends back in to carry it. Far too heavy for me. The boat sails at dawn. You need to decide now. A new life for you and Dom or…
Sorry. I was busy learning who funds the Sons of the Harpy. Some things you can’t rush.
The good masters of Astapor. And the wise masters of Yunkai. With help from their friends in Volantis.
Of course. Man can be fickle, but birds always trust.
I wondered why you weren't there to meet our guests.  You begged us to summon the King in the North.  Don't you want to see him again?
Strange.  You spoke so highly of Jon Snow but when he arrives you hide on a cliff.  I didn't take you for a bashful girl.
Oh, I doubt that.  Give us common folk one taste of power we're like the lion who tasted man.  Nothing is ever so sweet again.
Why?
So where will you go?
Good.  If you don't mind my saying, I don't think you should return to Westeros.  I'm not sure you'd be safe here.
My Lady --
Our Iron Born and Dornish allies were attacked on route to Dorne.
Two or three ships escaped.  The rest sunk our captured.  Ellaria and the sand snakes dead or captured.  The Greyjoys are dead or captured.
Your Grace, he's already destroyed a good portion of our fleet.  To send our remaining ships after him --
The Unsullied will be there soon.
A difficult situation.  They know we're coming.
Your father has named Lord Tyrion to (Cersei scoffs) serve as hand in his stead.
My lord.
We were just speaking of your bravery in the victory against the Stark auxiliary forces.
I heard you suffered a terrible head wound. The northerners are such fearsome warriors.
To find so lovely creature working in your father's kitchen. It almost beggars belief.
How can you tell?

I am glad your new friend was able to accompany you to the capital. Friends are such an important part of life. Unfortunate that your father didn't want her to come. But rest easy my lord, I am very good at keeping secrets for my good friends.

How unspeakable of me to go on and on, when all you want to do is rest. I will leave you. (Kisses Shae’s hand). Welcome to Kings Landing, my dear. The city is made brighter by your presence. We have a council meeting, my lord.
Who threatened you?
Ned Stark was a man of honor.
You might be disappointed in the results. The storms come and go. The big fish eat the little fish. And I keep on paddling. Come, my lord. We shouldn't keep the queen waiting.
Trouble with the wildlings?
Somewhat less wild these days. It seems they've stopped killing each other and started following this King-Beyond-the-Wall.
How do you kill a dead man?
I'm afraid foreigners aren't welcome at such exclusive affairs.
Ahem.
Shae has been noticed. Sansa's maid saw you with her. She already told your sister. It's only a matter of time before your father hears.

No, I will not. How long do you imagine your father and sister would let me live if they suspected me of lying? I have no pet sellsword to protect me. No legendary brother to avenge me. Only little birds who whisper in my ear.
No one weeps for spiders or whores. I have friends across the sea who could help her.
Your father has promised to hang the next whore he finds you with. Have you ever known your father to make an idle threat?
Yes, best be careful. You might accidentally consume some solid food.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: You don't like it?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: You can't. Cersei has offered a lordship to the man who brings her your head.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Are we really going to spend the entire road to Volantis talking about the futility of everything?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: The road to Meereen.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I told you. A ruler.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: You were quite good, you know. At ruling. During your brief tenure as Hand.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Still, a man of talent.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Yes, but showed great promise in other areas as well.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Why?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: People follow leaders. And they will never follow us. They find us repulsive.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: And we find them repulsive, which is why we surround ourselves with large, comfortable boxes to keep them away. And yet, no matter what we do, people like you and me are never really satisfied inside the box. Not for long.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: No.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I do not make dwarf jokes.
So, the fleet. It’s been burned.
Astapor and Yunkai have stopped asking us for aid.
The Masters have retaken both cities. Outside of Meereen, the whole of Slaver’s Bay has returned to the slavers.
Do they know that?
I remember that storm. All the dogs in King's Landing howled through the night.
Cersei controls fewer than half the Seven Kingdoms. The lord of Westeros despise her. Even before your arrival, they plotted against her. Now...
They joined our side, my queen, because they believe in you.
I did.
I had a choice, Your Grace--serve Robert Baratheon or face the headman's axe.
Robert was an improvement on your father, to be sure. There have been few rulers in history as cruel as the Mad King. Robert was neither mad nor cruel. He simply had no interest in being king.
Until your marriage to Khal Drogo, Your Grace, I knew nothing about you, save your existence and that you were said to be beautiful.
Which you turned to your advantage.
King Robert.
Your Grace, I did what had to be done to--

The kind the realm needs. Incompetence should not be rewarded with blind loyalty. As long as I have my eyes, I'll use them. I wasn't born into a great house. I came from nothing. I was sold as a slave and carved up as an offering. When I was a child, I lived in alleys, gutters, abandoned houses. You wish to know where my true loyalties lie? Not with any king or queen, but with the people. The people who suffer under despots and prosper under just rule. The people whose hearts you aim to win. If you demand blind allegiance, I respect your wishes. Grey Worm can behead me or your dragons can devour me. But if you let me live, I will serve you well. I will dedicate myself to seeing you on the Iron Throne because I choose you. Because I know the people have no better chance than you.
I swear it, my queen.
I would expect nothing less from the Mother of Dragons.
She once served another who wanted the Iron Throne.  It didn't end well for Stannis Baratheon, did it?
And why do you think the Lord of Light singled out this Jon Snow aside from the visions you've seen in the flames, that is?
The peasants say, a long summer means an even longer winter.
Your father has named Lord Tyrion to serve as Hand in his stead while he fights -
Apologies.
I saved your life. If they catch you, they catch me. I cannot say I feel overly guilty about leaving you in that “fucking crate”.
No. I only know what it’s like to pick up your shit and throw it overboard.

The home of my colleague. Ilyrio Mopatis. A merchant. He and I met many years ago, through mutual friends. A group of people who saw Robert Baratheon for the disaster he was. We tried to do what was best for the realm by supporting the Targaryen restoration. And thus began the chain of mistakes that has led us both here. Things have gotten worse, not better. Westeros needs to be saved from itself.
My lord?
You already drank yourself across the Narrow Sea.
Because we are talking about the future of our country.
Imp, half-man.
There are faster ways to kill yourself.
You are many things, my friend, but not a coward.
Your brother asked me to.
Refuse the Kingslayer? A dangerous proposition.
I didn’t do it for you. I did it for the Seven Kingdoms.
I don’t believe in saviors. I believe men of talent have a role to play in the war to come.
You have many admirable qualities, self-pity is not one of them. Any fool with a bit of luck can find himself born into power. But earning it for yourself, that takes work.
I think you are. You have your father’s instinct for politics, and you have compassion.
I never said you were perfect.
Peace. Prosperity. A land where the powerful do not prey on the powerless.
Perhaps. And perhaps we’ve grown so used to horror we assume there’s no other way. If you sat on the Iron Throne, would you spread misery throughout the land?

No, you won’t. But you could help another climb those steps and take that seat. The Seven Kingdoms need someone stronger than Tommen, but gentler than Stannis. A monarch who can intimidate the high lord and inspire the people. A ruler loved by millions with a powerful army and the right family name.

Who said anything about him? You have a choice my friend. You can stay here at Ilyrio’s palace and drink yourself to death, or you can ride with me to Mereen, meet Daenerys Targaryen, and decide if the world is worth fighting for.
You walk like a rich person.
You walk as though the paving stones were your personal property. I used to steal from people like you when I was a boy.
Mhysa means mother in Valyrian.
Who wrote it?
The Sons of the Harpy?
She did fly away on a dragon and not come back.
The Sons of the Harpy planned their attack in the fighting pits very carefully, which means they take orders from someone.
My little birds have already taken wing. Soon they’ll return, singing songs of men in gold masks.
You take great offense at dwarf jokes, but love telling eunuch jokes. Why is that?
The Karstarks.
You overestimate our influence. Jon and Daenerys don't want to listen to lonely old men.
Of course she does. Respect is how the young keep us at a distance, so we don't remind them of an unpleasant truth.
Nothing lasts.
His Valyrian is terrible. He only wants to give you money, so your baby can eat.
I don’t know how you stand it in all that leather.
She thinks you want to eat her baby.
Gentle, my lord. Gentle.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Shh shh shh. Myranda knows what she's doing. Let her. She trained as a septa, only (giggles) she had other urges.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Who, my lord?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Don't you want us to see it?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: We sent ourselves, Lord Greyjoy.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Oh! There it is.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Oh. Do you think we're ugly?  He thinks we're ugly.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: We need to make him feel better.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I have an idea.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Oh, she's shy. You know how they are, these religious girls.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair
