Text: As good as they say?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: And why should you get all the fun?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Plenty there for both of us.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Daenerys! There’s our bride to be! Look – a gift from Illyrio. Touch it. Come on. Feel the fabric. Mmmm. Isn’t he a gracious host?

Illyrio is no fool. He knows I won’t forget my friends when I come into my throne. You still slouch. Let me see. (He pulls off her gown.) You have a woman’s body now. (She endures it as he strokes her breast.) I need you to be perfect today. Can you do that for me? You don’t want to wake the dragon, do you?
When they write the history of my reign, sweet sister, they will say it began today.
Where is he?
Where’s he going?
But he didn’t say anything. Did he like her?
When will they be married?
Is it true they lie with their horses?
Do you take me for a fool?
I know how to play a man like Drogo. I give him a queen and he gives me an army.
So do I. I want us both to go home. But they took it from us. So tell me, sweet sister, how do we go home?
We go home with an army. With Khal Drogo’s army. I would let his whole tribe fuck you, all 40,000 men and their horses too, if that’s what it took.
When do I meet with the Khal? We need to begin planning the invasion.
When?
I piss on Dothraki omens. I waited 17 years to get my throne back.
Make him happy.
Daenerys!
She has to eat a whole heart? I hope that wasn’t my horse.
She’ll never keep it down.
Tell me what she is saying.
He won’t be a real Targaryen. He won’t be a true dragon.
They love her.
It’s not my law.
Whatever is hers is also mine.

I need a large army… I’m the last hope of a dynasty, Mormont. The greatest dynasty this world has ever seen, on my shoulders since I was five years old and no one has ever given me what they gave to her in that tent. Never. Not a piece of it… How can I carry what I need to carry without it? Hmm? Who can rule without wealth, or fear, or love? Oh, you stand there, all nobility and honor. You don’t think I see you looking at my little sister, hmm? Don’t think I know what you want? I don’t care. You can have her. She can be queen of the savages and dine on the finest bloody horseparts, and you can dine on whichever parts of her you like. But let me go.
You swore an oath to me. Does loyalty mean nothing to you?
And yet here you stand?
Daenerys! Where’s my sister?
Where is she? I’m here for the feast. The whore’s feast.
Get your hands off me! No one touches the dragon!
Khal Drogo! I’m here for the feast.
That is no place for a king.
Keep away from me!
There she is.

They can’t kill us. They can’t shed blood in their sacred city. (moving his sword over everyone in the room, stopping on Daenerys’ stomach) But I can. I want what I came for. I want the crown he promised me. He bought you. But he never paid for you.
Tell him I want what was bargained for or I’m taking you back. He can keep the baby. I’ll cut it out and leave it for him.
What’s he saying?
That was all I wanted. W-what was promised.
No! You cannot touch me! I am the dragon! I am the dragon! I want my crown! Ahh!
No. Dany! Dany, tell them, make them! You can’t!
Dany please!
I'll call them what I like, because they're my people. This is my army. Khal Drogo is marching the wrong way with my army.
Yes, my dear?
They do.
It's entirely possible.

The brave men didn't kill dragons. The brave men rode them. Rode them from Valyria to build the greatest civilization this world has ever seen. The breath of the greatest dragon forged the Iron Throne, which the Usurper is keeping warm for me. The swords of the vanquished, a thousand of them... melted together like so many candles.
Really? Why dragons?
Well, after 15 years in a pleasure house, I imagine just seeing the sky makes you happy.
What have you seen?
A pirate ship?

No, the last one died many years before I was born. I'll tell you what I have seen : their skulls. They used to decorate the throne room in the Red Keep. When I was very young, just 3 or 4, my father used to walk me down the rows and I'd recite their names for him. When I got them all right, he'd give me a sweet. The ones closest to the door were the last ones they were able to hatch and they were all stunted and wrong. Skulls no bigger than dog skulls. But as you got closer to the Iron Throne... They got bigger and bigger and bigger. There was Ghiscar and Valryon, Vermithrax, Essovius, Archonel, Meraxes, Vhagar... And Balerion the Dread... whose fire forged the Seven Kingdoms into one.
I don't know. The Usurper had them smashed to powder, I expect. Scattered to the wind.
Yes, it is. What did I buy you for? To make me sad?
To teach my sister how to be a better lover? You think I bought you to make Khal Drogo happy? You pretty little idiot. Go on then. Get on with it.
You send this whore to give me commands? I should have sent you back her head!
How many times do I have to tell you? You do not command me.
What's this?
Dothraki rags? Are you going to dress me now?
This stinks of manure. All of it.
You would turn me into one of them, wouldn't you? Next you'll want to braid my hair.
You do not talk back to me!
You are a horselord's slut. And now you've woken the dragon...
You dare! You give commands to me? To me? You do not command the dragon. I am Lord of the Seven Kingdoms. I don't take orders from savages or their sluts. Do you hear me?
Kill these Dothraki dogs! I am your King!
I have no interest in hospitality or comfort. I'll stay with Drogo until he fulfils his end of the bargain and I have my crown.
Well, Mormont, as brutish as this life is, I suppose it is preferable to beheading. What did Ned Stark want you for? Buying from a slaver?
Under my reign, you won't be punished for such nonsense. You can rest assured of that.
Hello, old friend.
And engage!
I was right about her.
You’re not ready. Not at all.
Why are you crying?
No he wasn’t. Didn’t you listen to him?
She is not ready.
And if she’s not?
If you’d have done your job, she would have died painlessly.
Instead…
The Many-Faced God was promised a name. He must always receive what is his. You can’t change that. I can’t change that. No one can. And now he’s been promised another name.
It will all be over soon. On your knees or on your feet?
Haven’t we been through this already? That won’t help you.
Get back to work.
You will know why.
When it’s time. Not before. Get back to work.
Game?
You tried already. You failed.
Who are you?
Get back to work.

I’m from Westeros. Just like you. Daughter of a Lord. Just like you. Except I was an only child. Heir to his fortune. My mother died. My father remarried and his new wife gave birth to a girl. My stepmother didn’t want any rivals for her precious daughter so she tried to kill me with poison. I found out, sought help from the Faceless Men and my father was widowed again. I’ve been serving them ever since.
Was that true or a lie?
Did you believe every word I said?
Get back to work.
As I expected.
You promised me.
You’re not ready. You should go home before it’s too late.
You’ll never be one of us, Lady Stark.
Who are you?
You, who walk in here with a coin you never earned, whose value you do not respect. (Stops and walks to Arya). Who are you?
A lie, a sad little lie. Who are you?
Who are you?
We were only playing. The game of faces.
Clearly not.
Who are you?
Who were you before you came here?
Tell me about Arya Stark’s family.
And where are they now?
Tell me about The Hound.
Why? Didn’t she want him dead any longer?
She sounds confused.
Who else was on Arya Stark’s funny little list?
That’s a short list. That can’t be everyone you want to kill.
Are you sure you’re not forgetting someone?
What’s your name?
What’s your name?
I don’t believe that.
You don’t believe that.
Are you listening, blind girl? Do you hear them talking? Do any of them talk to you? No. So sad.
Stand and fight, blind girl.
That’s your problem, not mine.
See you tomorrow.
Ale? (pours ale and leaves table; goes to another table) Ale?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Ale?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Thank you, Ramsay.
(Raises her cup). To your wedding.
It must be difficult for you being in a strange place.
We're going to have a baby.
Hello.

'The late Walder Frey,' old Hoster called me because I didn't get my men to the Trident in time for battle. He thought he was witty. Look at us now, Tully. You're dead, your daughter-Catelyn's dead, your grandson's dead, your son spent his wedding night in a dungeon and I'm Lord of Riverrun. (laughs)
An old man on the run with no allies. I have Tywin Lannister backing me. Who does he have?

They all laughed at me, all those high lords. They all thought they were better than me. Ned Stark, Hoster Tully. People snigger when I marry a young girl, but who said a word when Jon Arryn married the little Tully bitch?
Yeah. Got that to look forward to.
And you, the Warden of the North. No more Starks to bow and scrape to. Must have been torture following that stupid boy all over the country.
Calling himself the Young Wolf. How's that for pomposity?
Well, here's to the Young Wolf! (howls)
Will you move to Winterfell now that the war's over?
Yeah. What happened up there? I heard the Greyjoy boy seized the place. I heard he killed all the ravens. And after that, nothing.
Oh. And?
For House Lannister!
For House Frey!
May we stand together through the centuries, good friends to the last.
And when we drive our swords through our enemies’ hearts, may we speak the words of our alliance, “The Freys and the Lannisters send their regards.”
This came together rather well. Your father would be pleased.
Edmure is back in a cell. Can’t go killing my son by law. It wouldn’t be right. Give the family a bad name.
So the famous Blackfish killed by footsoldiers, eh? Legendary warrior, everyone said.
I’m a bit old for all that.
The purpose of fighting is defeating your enemies, isn’t it?
I’ve defeated mine. Riverrun belonged to House Tully for a thousand years. Now it’s mine. What do you call that? Victory.

Go on, mock me, boy. You think I mind? The Tullys mocked me for years. The Starks mocked me. Where are they now? You talk about war as if you’re an expert but the one battle I remember you fighting, you were captured by Robb Stark, the Young Wolf. But it doesn’t matter. Here we are now, two kingslayers. We know what it’s like to have them grovel to our faces and snigger behind our backs. We don’t mind, do we? Fear is a marvelous thing.
You’re not one of mine, are you?
Didn’t think so. Too pretty.
Where are my damn moron sons? Black Walder and Lothar promised to be here by midday.
Well, what are they doing, trimming their cunt hairs? Tell them to come here now.
What…
My honored guests, be welcome within my walls and at my table.  I extend to you my hospitality and protection in the light of the Seven.
Don't beg my forgiveness, your Grace.  It wasn't me you spurned, it was my girls.
One of them was supposed to be queen, now none of them are.
This is Arwyn, my daughter.  My daughter Walda, my daughter Derwa, my daughter Waldra.
My eldest granddaughters Ginia and Neila.
Serra and Sarra, granddaughters, twins.  You could have had either.  Could have had both for all I care.
My granddaughter Marianne.  My granddaughter Freya.  My granddaughter...W-W-Wertha? Waldra? Waldina?
Fine.  And here's my youngest daughter Shirei, though she hasn't bled yet.  Clearly you don't have the patience for all that.
Very good.
There she is.  Come closer, let me have a look at you.
Still can't see you.  Old eyes.

Love.  That's what the Starks of Winterfell call it, eh?  Heh. Very honorable, I call it a pretty face.  Mmmm, very pretty.  Prettier than this lot, that's for sure.  Very shapely as well.  Oh, you try to hide her under that dress.  If you wanted to hide her, you shouldn't have brought her here in the first place.
I can always see what's going on beneath a dress.  Been at this a long time.  I bet when you take that dress off, everything stays right where it is.  Doesn't drop an inch.
Your king says he betrayed me for love.  I say he betrayed me for firm tits and a tight fit.

And I can respect that.  When I was your age, I'd have broken fifty oaths to get into that without a second thought.  Well, I've enough room in the hall for you lot.  We'll set up tents outside with food and ale for the rest of your men.
Well, let's get ready.  The wine will flow red and the music will play loud and we'll put this mess behind us.
[Addressing ROBB loudly so everybody can hear] Your Grace.

The Septon has prayed his prayers, some words were said and Lord Edmure has wrapped my daughter in a cloak.  But they are not yet man and wife.  A sword needs a sheath [Everybody laughs]. And a wedding needs a bedding [Everybody cheers].  What does my sire say?
Your Grace.
I feel I’ve been remiss in my duties.
I’ve given you meat and wine and music but I haven’t shown you the hospitality you deserve.  My king has married and I owe my new queen a wedding gift.
The King in the North arises, heh heh.
[Angrily] You already swore me one oath right here in my castle.  You swore by all the Gods, your son would marry my daughter!
And why I would let him do that?
I’ll find another.
You’ve lost it?
It’s a castle, not a bloody sheep. Presumably you still know where it is. You didn’t lose Riverrun. You let the Blackfish take it from you.

You did lose the Blackfish after the Red Wedding. You had him right here in this hall and you let him leave. Then when I told you to hunt him down and kill him, you couldn’t find him. That’s what it means to lose something. Now he’s come back and taken Riverrun.
For 300 years we kissed Tully boots, swore oaths to them and their stinking fish banners. Not again. Riverrun is ours. Take it back.
We’ve got 10 times as many men as the damn Blackfish.
If I want excuses, I’ll put her in charge. They’re laughing at us. All across the Riverlands right down to King’s Landing, they’re laughing at us.
I hear it in my sleep! I’m not dead yet, unfortunately for you. And I’ll not leave this world until they all choke on that laughter. Take that castle back.
Oh, he’ll yield.

You’ll show him the knife you used to kill Robb Stark’s child in his whore mother’s belly, and you’ll show him the knife you used to open his niece’s throat. And you’ll remind him who it was that got married at the Red Wedding in the first place. His nephew.
Cheer up, Lord Edmure. You’re going home.
Not you. I'm not wasting good wine on a damn woman.
Maybe I'm not the most pleasant man. I'll admit it. But I'm proud of you lot. You're my family, the men who helped me slaughter the Starks at the Red Wedding.

Yes, yes. Cheer. Brave men, all of you. Butchered a woman pregnant with her babe. Cut the throat of a mother of five. Slaughtered your guests after inviting them into your home. But you didn't slaughter every one of the Starks.
No, no, that was your mistake. You should have ripped them all out, root and stem.
Leave one wolf alive and the sheep are never safe.
What do you want?
Oh, spare me. Your boy's too proud to come before me himself. What am l supposed to do with you?
Who asked you? You're not Lord Frey yet, not until l die. Do l look dead to you?
l need lessons in courtesy from you, bastard? Your mother would still be a milkmaid if l hadn't squirted you into her belly.
We're talking right now. Fine. Out! All of you!
Huh. Your father didn't come to the wedding.
Huh. Didn't come to the last one, either. Or the one before that. Your family has always pissed on me.
Don't deny it. You know it's true. The fine Lord Tully would never marry any of his children to mine.
l didn't need reasons. l needed to get rid of sons and daughters. You see how they pile up? Why are you here?
Why should l let him?
There will be 20,000 corpses when Tywin Lannister gets here. Don't try and frighten me, Lady Stark. Your husband's in a cell beneath the Red Keep and your son's got no fur to keep his balls warm.

Oh, yes, l said some words. And l swore oaths to the crown too, if l remember right. Joffrey's king now, which makes your boy and his corpses-to-be nothing but rebels, it seems to me. lf l had the sense the gods gave a fish, l'd hand you both over to the Lannisters.
Stark, Tully, Lannister, Baratheon. Give me one good reason why l should waste a single thought on any of you?
If you’re wrong, we’re just surrendered our most valuable prisoner.
You think I won’t do it, old man?
I sliced your niece’s throat from ear to ear. And where were you? Running and hiding like a fucking coward. Yield the castle or I cut his throat.
Whoa, whoa.
Edmure is a prisoner of House Frey.
The roads are crawling with cutthroats and bandits. But when the King of the North summons us, we come.
And there's something else.
Not what, whom.
You want to count her teeth? We depart for the Twins in the morning. We need an answer before we leave and a wedding not more than a fortnight thereafter or this alliance is at an end.
Yes, father.
We don’t have the men.
The Mallisters have risen against us. And the Blackwoods.
Riverrun can withstand a siege for a year.
You're wonder why I brought you all here. After all, we just had a feast. Since when does old Walder give us two feasts in a single fortnight?
Well, it's no good being lord of the Riverlands if you can't celebrate with your family. That's what I say!

I've gathered every Frey who means a damn thing so I can tell you my plans for this great house now that winter has come. But first, a toast! No more of that Dornish horse piss! This is the finest Arbor gold! Proper wine for proper heroes!
Stand together!
Took a detour. Got some supper from a village down that way.
Yes, commander.
What d’you expect? They’re savages. One lot steals a goat from another lot and before you know it, they’re ripping each other to pieces.
How close did you get?
You… you don’t have to.
Don't forget me.
Don't you want to leave something to the imagination?
I want you to take it off.
Mouth.
(Shakes her head no, looking down. )
When they look like her, my lord.
Blue-eyed women are witches.
I told you she was a witch. Cut off her head.
Vaes Dothrak. The Temple of the Dosh Khaleen.
It is known.
To live out her days with the widows of dead Khals. It is known.
Cut off her head, before she casts a spell on you.
Aye.
All right, up, lad.
I don’t see him. Got him hidden under your cloak?
We’ll take the pin. And the horse. Get down… Be quick about it.
What’s wrong with you? You some kind of cripple?
Piss on Mance rayder and piss on the North. We’re going as far south as south goes. There ain’t no white walkers down in Dorne.
Shut up! Drop the blade!
Do it.
Come on, on your feet, now. Move it.
Bring them over here.
That was the last of them.  The rest are dead.
Cut his little cock off and stuff it in his mouth.
I’m with Tormund. We stay here we’re dead men. At least with King Crow there’s a chance.
I’ve never seen wildlings do a thing like this. I’ve never seen a thing like this, not ever in my life.
Close as any man would.
Whatever did it to them could do it to us. They even killed the children.
They were here.

I know I broke my oath. And I know I’m a deserter. I should have gone back to the Wall and warned them. But I saw what I saw. I saw the White Walkers. People need to know. If you can get word to my family, tell them I’m no coward. Tell them I’m sorry.
White Walkers. I saw the White Walkers. White Walkers. The White Walkers, I saw them.
Forgive me, lord.
You can touch me.
I'm not afraid of wildlings.
Is this a rescue?
Lannister filth! Take him!
Virzetha gizikhven! Mra qora! Mra qora!
A taste for the Khaleesi? I have a sweet red from Dorne, my lady.
My Lady, you are from Westeros.
Princess.
That? Dornish swill.
The honor...the honor is all mine.
You know there are many in your homeland that pray for your return, princess.
The wine is for the Khaleesi. It's not for the likes of you.
It would be a crime to drink a wine this rich without at least giving it time to breathe.
As the princess commands.
Sweet, isn't it? Can you smell the fruit, Ser? Taste it, My Lord.
Me? I'm afraid I am not worthy of the vintage.
Who, m'lord?
I don't know. I seen no one.
I swear, m'lord. I don't know.

And it's not just thieves, my lord. There's wolves in them hills now, more than I ever seen. They come down in the night and they kill my sheep. My three sons is away fighting for your brother, my lord. They'll fight, keep fighting till they're told to go home. I have no one to man my flock now. Only me. I can't keep watch all day and all night.
My wife always prayed for more children. We'll look after them. Thank you, my lord. And may the gods bless you and yours.
I found it, my Lord.
It took some searching.  Maester Llewyn's archives are thorough.
Yes, my Lord.
4,000 bushels, My Lady.
I'm not entirely certain.  I'll check Maester Llewyn's records.  He kept a copy of every raven scroll.
No, My Lady, most likely not.
A raven, My King, from the Citadel.
Apologies, my lady. Your Grace.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: A raven from King's Landing, Your Grace.
Welcome back, my lady. If you'd follow me
Tywin… my lion...
I’ve had half as many cocks as the queen!
Children, come inside, quick. Come on.
Sweet girl.
Please, Your Grace, give us some food!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Long live King Tommen and bless you, Queen Margaery!
And you must visit the night market. The Qartheen night market is like no night market you've ever seen.
The Meereenis think they have a night market. I will take you there myself.
That is strange.
Beg pardon, my lady. I've come to refill the wash basin.
(Closes the door). You still have friends in the North. If you're ever in trouble, light a candle in the highest window of the broken tower.
You're not alone.
He's dead. He was my son. My sister was three days ago. My husband, the day before that.
(nods)
Please, Polliver. Some food. Just a crust of bread.
Lady Margaery!
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: No, don't. Don't, please.
Filthy bastard!
(Speaking another language) Sons of the Harpy.
I have to go down and get another barrel.
Drink!
They need some water.
Here we are.
That's it. You're nearly there. One more push should do it.
I'll bring you a bowl of hot water. You must want to wash.
Welcome home, Lady Stark. (Quieter) The North remembers.
Thank you ever so for the soup!
If you’re going to torture me, just call them back and get on with it.
Is it as you remember my lord?
Everything looks bigger when you're a child. I remember my father's cabin, felt like a palace when I was little. Now look at it.
Who will?
I love the cold and wet.
Then you'll be king before long.
My father doesn't trust you Iron islanders.
He says that you're all reavers and rapers. And that no matter how many women you have, you'll never be satis-
Capture me! Take me with you when you go ashore. Make me your saltwife.
Not after you leave. Father will punish me. He'll call me a whore.
This way, my lady.
Thank you.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Ah, look, crow coming!
Lady Margaery!
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Please don't. Ow! You're hurting me.
Tether it and I'll be inside.
Sharpen these for me, would ya?
Bless you, Lady Margaery!
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I see the head. Push.
Lady Margaery!
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Lady Margaery!
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Margaery!
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Long live Lady Margaery!
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: King Joffrey!
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: King Joffrey!
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Seven blessings to you.
"Bring me my brown pants!"
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: A gift for the gods.A gift for the gods. A gift for the gods.A gift for the gods. A gift for the gods.A gift for the gods.
No, don't! Don't! Please!
No, don't! No, don't!
Please, don't! It'll get better. It doesn't even hurt.
No, you can't!
Ser! Please, ser.  I can't lose
I don't want to be a cripple, please.
No! You can't!
Lokh kif rukh?
Snow.
What the
I am king of Qarth. I can help you now, truly help you. We can take the Iron Throne. I'll bring you a thousand ships.
All that you have dreamed is within your reach!
Khaleesi.
Khaleesi!
I've demanded a meeting of the Thirteen. One of them did this or knows who did.
If I wanted your dragons, I would have taken them. They mean nothing to me on their own.
Shall we look inside my vault and see what selling your dragons would buy me I cannot buy already? We will get them back.

I took you under my protection in front of the rulers of my city. A man is what others say he is and no more. If they say that Xaro Xhoan Daxos is a liar, my word is worth nothing. I cannot let this thing happen to you under my roof.
Khaleesi, many times in my life, I have been
No one is calling you a thief. Who is she to accuse us?
There is now. That was the other half of the arrangement.

You would keep your gates and your minds closed to everything outside your walls, but Qarth cannot remain the greatest city that ever was if it refuses to change. I will open Qarth to the world as I have forced it to open itself to me.
An upstart and a charlatan? Empires have been built by less. Those on the margins often come to control the center, and those in the center make room for them, willingly or otherwise.
The Spice King is the second wealthiest man in Qarth. He makes everyone wait. Of course, you could have avoided this embarrassment if you married the wealthiest man in Qarth.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Khal Drogo is gone, Khaleesi. You are far too young to be a widow forever and far too beautiful.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I have traveled very far in my life and met many women, but none that are immune to flattery.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Ah, here he comes.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Very true, and yet they say that your grandfather, who sold pepper off the back of a wagon, married a lady far lovelier and higher born than himself.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: When I came to this city, I had nothing. Truly nothing. I slept by the docks. And when I could find work loading the ships, I would eat. If not, I dreamed of food. Today, I am the richest man in Qarth. Do you think the path from poverty to wealth is always pure and honorable? I have done many things, Khaleesi, that a righteous man would condemn. And here I am, with no regrets.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Bar the gates. Ring the bells. Slowly, Khaleesi.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: My apologies. Pyatt Pree is one of the Thirteen. It was customary for me to extend him an invitation. Customs die slow deaths in Qarth.
It is where the warlocks go to squint at dusty books and drink shade of the evening. It turns their lips blue and their minds soft. So soft, they actually believe their parlor tricks are magic.
So, tell me, how long has your manservant been in love with you?
Unlikely. I can almost always tell what a man wants.
Much more complicated. You, for example, what do you want?
Why?
You want to conquer the Seven Kingdoms for the Dothraki?
Ah, a conqueror.
No. I come from nothing. I hit the docks like a piece of cargo, except someone normally cares what happens to cargo.
(chuckles)
I will show you why.

The door and the vault is made of Valyrian stone. The hardest steel does not make a mark. I offered the greatest locksmiths in Qarth their weight in gold if they could break into it. I made the same offer to the greatest thieves. They all went home empty-handed. The only thing that can open this door is this key.
(chuckles)
All? Let us say half. More than enough to buy horses, ships, armies.  Enough to go home.
Is marry me.

I've already married once for love, but the gods stole her from me. I come from nothing. My mother and father never owned a pair of shoes. But marry me, and I will give you the Seven Kingdoms and our children will be princes and princesses.
See? I have more ambition than you thought. The time is right, Daenerys Targaryen, First of Your Name. Robert Baratheon is dead.
Retreating in fear from a little girl is unbecoming of the greatest city that ever was or will be.
I am one of the Thirteen, and I am still speaking.

Do you expect her to watch her people starve without breathing fire? I believe we can allow a few Dothraki through our gates without dooming our city. After all, here I am, a savage from the Summer Isles, and Qarth still stands.
Very well. I invoke soumai.
I will vouch for her, her people, and her dragons, in accordance with the law.
Welcome to Qarth, my lady.
He's your son.
He's your son. He's my brother. He's a Greyjoy.
And I've made mine. I'm going to pick the fastest ship in our fleet.
(voiceover)  I'm going to choose the 50 best killers on the Iron Islands.
(voiceover) I'm going to sail up the Narrow Sea all the way to the Weeping Water. I'm going to march on the Dreadfort. I'm going to find my little brother and I'm going to bring him home.
And he paid for them.
No more than Westeros.
You and I have that in common.
His big cock, I think he said. Euron’s offer is also an offer of marriage, you see. You won’t get one without the other.
I never demand, but I’m up for anything, really.
We’d like you to help us murder an uncle or two who don’t think a woman’s fit to rule.
That’s our way of life.
No more.
Why, it's the Prince of Winterfell.
What envy?
You're a great warrior. I saw the bodies above your gates. Which one gave you the tougher fight, the cripple or the six-year-old?
You treated them with honor? By butchering them?
You seized their home, as is your right. We're ironborn, we take what we need.
Then you made them prisoners in their home and they ran away. Is that treachery? I'd call it bravery.
Your little boy prisoners made you a promise and you got mad when they broke it? Are you the dumbest cunt alive?
A cunt. A dumb cunt who killed the only two Starks in Winterfell. You know how valuable those boys were?
You are weak. And you're stupid.
Go on, then. Warn me.
You're not. I've come to bring you home. Father wants a word.

Aye, it is, hundreds of miles from the sea. We're islanders, baby brother. Had you forgotten that? Our power comes from our ships. And now that you've decorated your walls with the bodies of the Stark boys, every man in the North wants to see you hanged. When Robb Stark finds out
(to her men) Leave us.
Theon, you're my blood. We both loved our mother. We both endured our father. Come home with me. Don't die here alone.

You were a terrible baby, do you know that? Bawling all the time, never sleeping. And one night, you just wouldn't shut up, screaming like a dying pig. I walked over to your crib, I looked down at you. I wanted to strangle you. And you looked up at me and you stopped screaming. You smiled at me. Don't die so far from the sea.
Some of us still like it. Have a drink at least.
Don’t go far, love.
I’ll find you in a bit.
Nothing on the Iron Islands has an ass like that. Doesn’t interest you anymore?
I’m sorry. I won’t joke about it. I’ll never hurt you, little brother. Don’t you know that?
Of course he is. As long as we’re alive, we’re a threat.
It’s a great big world and we have fast ships. Drink.
I don’t care what you want. Drink.
Drink it all.
You’re Ironborn, Theon. I know you’ve had some bad years.
But I’m tired of watching you cower like a beat dog. Drink the goddamn ale.
Now listen to me. I need you. The real Theon Greyjoy, not this rat shit pretender. Can you find him for me? Drink.
You escaped, do you hear me? You got away and you’re never going back. We’ll get justice for you.
Fuck justice, then. We’ll get revenge. Drink.

Listen to me. If you’re so broken that there’s no coming back, take a knife and cut your wrists. End it. But if you’re staying, Theon, I need you. We’re gonna sail to Meereen. We’re gonna make a pact with this Dragon Queen. And we’re gonna take back the Iron Islands.
Are you with me? Are you really with me?
Now, since it’s my last night ashore for a long while, I’m gonna go fuck the tits off this one.
They skinned our countrymen and they mutilated my brother, your prince. Your prince.  Everything they've done to him, they've also done to you.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: As long as they can hurt our prince with impunity, the word ironborn means nothing.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Theon Greyjoy.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I'm here for Theon Greyjoy. Take me to the dungeons.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Thank you.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: This way!
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: We're going home.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: It's all right. It's me, Yara.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I'm not tricking you, Theon. I'm saving you.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: If they catch us in here, we're trapped. Help me with him.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: You're Theon Greyjoy.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Aah! Theon!
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Give me my brother and no more of your men will die.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Make for the ship, now.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: My brother is dead.
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Some of you may be quick to forgive. The Ironborn are not. I swore to follow Daenerys Targaryen.
She freed us from a tyrant. Cersei is gone because of her, and Jon Snow put a knife in her heart. Let the Unsullied give him what he deserves.
Aye.
Stop. We yield.
Congratulations on your first command.
Oh, I'm not here for you. I was just on my way to Red Harbor. I've got 30 ships. There's nowhere to put them here. Too narrow.
That would never happen. My crew would wait on deck for a year if I asked them to. This lot, though Enjoy the Stony Shore.
I am Yara Greyjoy, daughter of Balon Greyjoy, King of the Iron Islands. I claim the Salt Throne.

There are many things we’ve never done. We’ve never made our mark upon the world. The great lords of Westeros pay us no mind until our little raids buzz through their kingdoms long enough to become a nuisance. Then they swat us down. They conquer us, humiliate us, and go right back to forgetting we exist.
We are a sea people.
Our god is a sea god.
When I am queen, we will build a fleet that —
I am not finished!
When did you return, Uncle?
I’m glad you’re here. Now I know what my first act as queen will be. To execute the man who killed my father.
I will build the largest fleet the world has ever seen.
You’re going to seduce the Dragon Queen?
They told me you were home and I didn’t believe it. “Theon Greyjoy?” I said. “He’s dead. He’s been dead a long time." He let you go?
I can’t hear you.
Look at me. Look at me! Men died trying to rescue you. Good men. My men.
You were my brother. You’re a spoiled little cunt, but you were my brother and I risked everything for you and you betrayed me.
Stop saying that.
I know.
He send us one of those pieces. That’s why I came for you.
Why did you come here?
You heard father was dead and thought you’d claim the crown?
You happen to show up on Pyke right before the Kingsmoot?
You think any Ironborn wants you to be king? After what you’ve done?
What do you want?
That doesn’t matter anymore. Stop crying.  Look at me. Tell me what you want.
I live here. Are you angry with me, brother?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: It's not my fault you didn't recognize me.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You were a fat little boy, too, but I recognized you.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I wanted to see who you were first. And I did.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Father.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I've always wanted a castle.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: The Sea Bitch. We thought she'd be perfect for you.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Be careful of their nets.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You'd have our father bow down to your other family?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Don't you? Make your choice, Theon, and do it quickly. Our ships sail with or without you.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I'm heading that way. I can take you there.
You been at sea long? Or were there just no women where you came from?
You don't know what I'm like.
You think I offer free rides to every man in jewelry. Lord Greyjoy.
Nine years. Do you still know your way around a ship? Have these hands ever touched a rope?
Your blood will be in the sea if I don't watch where I'm going.
Is that an offer from my future king?
I don't imagine it will be a story fit for children.
Anything with a cock is easy to fool.
So good to see you, brother. This is a homecoming I'll tell my grandchildren about.
You're the one in skirts.
The Glovers have retaken Deepwood Motte.
They died fighting to a man.
What is dead may never die.
But they did and our invasion died with them. Deepwood Motte was our last stronghold on the mainland.
Why? For more pinecones and rocks?
We can defeat anyone at sea, but we’ll never hold lands and castles against mainland armies.
I won’t apologize for trying to rescue Theon.

Where is your kingdom? We took those castles because the northerners marched off to war. That war is over. The last time we provoked them too far, I watched from that window as they breached our walls and knocked down our towers. I lost two brothers that day.
I’m going to find out who did this. I’m going to feed them to the sharks while they live. I swear it by the Salt Throne.
My father would’ve wanted me to rule.
If you want the Iron Throne, take it.  We have an army, a fleet, and three dragons.  We should hit King's Landing now.  Hard.  With everything we have.  The city will fall within a day.
You have mine.
A few times.  Never stayed long.
A boy, a girl.  Depends on the port.
I thought it wasn't for you.
He's not your servant.
Once I kill my uncle.
He'll be my advisor, my protector.
He might have to intervene.
Until they've made their intentions known.
Stay here.
Euron.
Your mother is below deck.  Keep her safe.
Why don't you just get it over with and kill me?
Are we in King's Landing?
You picked the losing side.
We can take our home back.
Daenerys will need somewhere to retreat if they can't hold the North. Somewhere the dead can't go.
You want to go to Winterfell. To fight for the Starks. Go. What is dead may never die.
But kill the bastards anyway.
Euron can't defend the Iron Islands, not if he's in King's Landing with all his men and his ships.
(Pauses.) No.
Aye.
We need to teach them a lesson about the way we deal with their kind.
Three dozen bodies with slit throats tossed off the top of the Wall. Seems like that would be a good lesson.

The terms are simple. You and your foreign friends will abandon the Great Pyramid and the city of Meereen. The Unsullied you stole from Kraznys mo Nakloz will remain to be sold again to the highest bidder. The translator you stole from Kraznys mo Nakloz will remain to be sold again to the highest bidder. The dragons beneath the Great Pyramid will be slaughtered.
Please.
I bought this dwarf for a single gold honor. And somehow you’ve risen to the top of the Great Pyramid of Meereen. It’s most impressive.
You think you’re a free man now? You still follow orders. Just because your master has silver hair and tits doesn’t mean she’s not a master.
Slavery is the way of our world.
What does that mean?
We do not support the Sons of the Harpy.
We should be there by sundown. Won't be a fun night for you. Mance knows how to make crows sing. If you know what to say, you might just make it through the night. Not talking's not the way to go.
You can tell Mance that's the man who killed Qhorin Halfhand.
Come along, Jon Snow.
Time to meet the King-beyond-the-Wall.
(grunts)
You know nothing, Jon Snow.
How’s he keep folks from stealing them?
He’s an old man.  A spear through the heart’s a better way to die than coughing up your last with no one but your horses to hear.
[Looks at Jon]  Do it.  Do it.
No!
Yes.

I know you never fucked a bear. You know you never fucked a bear. Right now I don't wanna think about the bear you never fucked. Right now all I want to think about is each one of these arrows finding its way into a Crow's heart.

Good. The longer we wait, the more arrows I'll have. When they came up here, to our land, and put up a big wall and said it was their's. Then they started hunting us down. But this time, we're the one's doing the hunting.
Back in those villages I killed just as many of them as you did. More, I reckon.
I probably killed him already.
And if not. The only thing left of him is gonna be his fun-bits hanging around me neck.
What, you been thinkin' about that ginger minge? Wondering what it tastes like?
Jon Snow is mine. *shouting* Anyone else tries to kill him, I'll have an arrow for them. *to Styr* And not one of your bald friends is fast enough to stop me.
Most of their men are up top. Didn't count more than twenty down there. Leftovers. They've got a little fat one and a little bony one manning the front.
Well, we'll be up and over before they know what's happened.
Jon Snow.
Do you remember that cave?
We should've stayed in that cave.
You know nothing, Jon Snow.
I've brought you a present, Lord of Bones.
Mance will want to question this one. Knows all about where the crows are and what they're planning.
He could have killed me half a dozen times.
He's a bastard of Winterfell, Ned Stark's son.
I don't know. I think he'll want to decide for himself.
He runs, I'll do it meself.
We're even now, Jon Snow.
Did you pull a knife on me in the night?
What's the matter? Can't be the first time you pressed your bone against a woman's arse.
It is the first time. How old are you, boy?
You're a boy who's never been with a girl. Don't your stones start to hurt if your bone never gets
What, stones? Or bone?

I heard they get all swollen and bruised if you don't use them. Of course, maybe that's just what the lads say when they want me feeling sorry for them. As if I'd feel sorry for them. Are there no girl crows?
So the lads just do it with each other?
Never?
You have sheep at the Wall? With your hands, then? No wonder you're all so miserable.
And you think you're free? You swore some stupid oath and now you can never touch a girl.
So you don't like girls?
So instead of getting naked with a girl, you'd prefer to invade our lands
Yhey're not your lands! We've been here the whole time. You lot came along and just put up a big wall and said it was yours.
So why are you fighting us?

You think we're savages because we don't live in stone castles. We can't make steel as good as yours, it's true, but We're free. If someone tried to tell us we couldn't lie down as man and woman, we'd shove a spear up his arse. We don't go serving some shit king who's only king because his father was.

We chose Mance Rayder to lead us. He was a crow, same as you, but he wanted to be free. You could be free, too. You don't need to live your whole life taking commands from old men. Wake up when you want to wake up. I could show you the streams to fish, the woods to hunt. Build yourself a cabin and find a woman to lie with in the night.
You're a pretty lad. The girls would claw each other's eyes out to get naked with you.
I could teach you how to do it.
You know nothing, Jon Snow.
How long till we get back to your crows?
What, a day? A half-day? You don't know, do you? Do you even know where they are?
What do you think they'll say when they hear about you and me?

"I swear it, Old Master King Crow, ser, "we were only close together for warmth. "And then I felt it, right up against me backside like a club. "I can show you the bruise on me tailbone. "And before I knew what was where, his his "Well, it was all out in the open, all angry as you like. "And I didn't want to want it, but oh, I did! "And he spread me legs and ruined. "The shame of it. "Now I can never marry a perfumed lord. "What will me poor savage father say?"

"And I thought that we were done, but he said, "'Turn back around." I'll tell you what, Jon Snow. Since it's gonna be your word against mine, and since you can't talk about it without blushing, we may as well just JON: What, right here in the muck?
I'll keep you warm enough.
Are you that afraid of it?
It's nice and wet and warm.
It don't have teeth.
All right! All right. Gods, you're dull.
Should have took me while you had the chance.
How far are we?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You think? You don't know?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You and your roads. Is that how you lot do your fighting?  You march down the road banging drums and waving banners?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: How do the men holding the banners fight?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: So what good are they?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: How?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Oh, you mean right foot, left foot, right foot, left foot, right foot? You need help remembering that? When Mance gives the signal and we hit Castle Black, we won't be banging on drums to let them know we're coming.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You know nothing, Jon Snow.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: No, he didn't tell me anything. He barely talks.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: What, you're jealous?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: And you're one of my own? I've never heard a kind word from your mouth.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: (nods)
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Don't touch me.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I'm not gonna scare it. I'm gonna kill it.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: (smiles)
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Is that a palace?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Windmill. Who built it?  Some king?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: They must have been great builders, stacking stones so high.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I'm Jon Snow and I'm from Winterfell. My daddy was a fancy lord and I lived in a tower that touched the clouds.'
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: What's swooning?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: What's fainting?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Why would a girl see blood and collapse?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Well, girls see more blood than boys. Or do you like girls who swoon, Jon Snow? (gasps) Oh, a spider! Save me, Jon Snow.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: My dress is made of the purest silk from Tralalalaleeday.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Would you?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Well, you rip my pretty silk dress, I'll blacken your eye.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Or maybe one day I'll take you there. After we've taken our land back.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You know nothing--
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: And how do you know that?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Mance is different.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You don't know that.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: All of us.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You're mine as I'm yours. And if we die, we die. But first we'll live.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Ygritte.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I gave you my name.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You ought to burn them you killed.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Burn them or maybe you'll need those swords again.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: The free folk.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Hundreds and thousands. More than you've ever seen, crow.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Qhorin Halfhand.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: They'd take your head off your shoulders if they was feeling kind. If not, they'd kill you slow.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You never killed a woman before, did you? You don't need to do it. Mance would take you. I know he would. There's secret ways. The crows would never catch us.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Will you burn me? After?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Strike hard and true, Jon Snow, or I'll come back and haunt you. That's cold.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Do it. Bastard, do it!
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You can't do it. We both know it.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: The sun's going down, Jon Snow. And your friends are nowhere close.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Call for them. Go on. Call loudly.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Your brothers desert you? I can tell you which way to go.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Here? There's no shelter here.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: There is if you know where to look. The cold could kill us both. If you light a fire
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: But a fire is
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Have it your way.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: We'll stay warmer if we stay close.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Bet you freeze to death before I do. Bet your life.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Think they're out looking for you?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Think they'll find you?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You're brave. Stupid, but brave.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I'm just trying to get comfortable.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Was I? I didn't notice that time.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: No. But Tormund's done it half a hundred times. You're afraid.
Aye. It's a long way up and a long way down. But I've waited my whole life to see the world from up there. Here, sit down.
I brought a pair for you. They're too big for you, but they're good.
Nah. I didn't kill him, but I bet his balls are still bruised.
He wasn't good to me the way you're good to me. He didn't do that thing you do with your tongue.
'Can we not talk about that here? I'm Jon Snow. I've killed dead men and Qhorin Halfhand, but I'm scared of naked girls.'
You were trembling like a leaf.
Only in the beginning. You're a proper lover, Jon Snow. And don't worry. Your secret's safe with me.
Do you think I'm as dumb as all those girls in silk dresses you knew growing up? You're loyal and you're brave. You didn't stop being a crow the day you walked into Mance Rayder's tent.

But I'm your woman now, Jon Snow. You're going to be loyal to your woman. The Night's Watch don't care if you live or die. Mance Rayder don't care if I live or die. We're just soldiers in their armies and there's plenty more to carry on if we go down.
It's you and me that matters to me and you. Don't ever betray me.
Cause I'll cut your pretty cock right off and wear it 'round my neck.
You staring at my ass, Jon Snow?
You all right?
(screams)
(screams)
(screams)
(screams)
He's no crow.
I'm not afraid of you.
Of course you do. Who stopped Rattleshirt when he was about to cut your throat? Who vouched for you with Mance? It seems you owe me a debt.
Well, I stole it. It's mine. If you want it, come steal it back.
Is Orell right? Are you still a crow? It's time you proved yourself.
You swore some vows. I want you to break 'em. I want you to see me.
All of me.
We should.
Why are you still dressed? You know nothing, Jon Sno--
Hmm.
Aye, I liked it some. Who taught you that?
A maid. You were a maid.
What do you think?

Just a boy. He came trading with his brothers. He had red hair like me. Kissed by fire. But he was weak. Not like you. That was the first one. Then there was this Thenn boy. Spoke no Common, but gods, he was built like a mammoth.
I'm not done with you yet.
How long's it been since you had a bath?
Let's not go back. Let's stay here a while longer. I don't ever want to leave this cave, Jon Snow. Not ever.
You said there was dead crows.
What, you've never met a warg?
First time you've seen a giant, Jon Snow?
Well, don't stare too long. They're shy. When they stop being shy, they get angry. And when they're angry, I've seen them pound a man straight into the ground like a hammer on a nail.
You're wearing the wrong color.
In your hearts, all you crows want to fly free.
Sure, you will.
And I'll be free to kill you. Got no respect, this lot. Got no fathers to slap 'em when they're foul.
Some of them were killed by crows like you. Don't look so grim, Jon Snow. If Mance Rayder likes you, you'll live another day. And if he don't...
This baby killed Qhorin Halfhand. He wants to be one of us.
Do you plan on sitting here scratching your balls till winter?
You sent a man over the Wall at the full moon. If he's not back yet, he's not coming back.
Yeah, well, he's a liar.
I did.
I'm not anybody's.
The Knights of the Vale came here for you, Lady Stark.
For the current occupants of the castle it's enough food for a year, perhaps more.
Very wise, My Lady.
No, My Lady.
They should indeed.  Pardon me, My Lady.
You there, why isn't there leather on these?
The Umbers and the Karstarks betrayed the North. Their castles should be torn down with not a stone left standing.
I'll ask my horse.
Aye.
Last time I saw you, Baelish, you told me you were taking Sansa Stark home with you to the Fingers.
And yet not long ago, we received reports that she has been married to Ramsay Bolton in Winterfell.
Do you take me for a fool?
Slander a man in his own home, you might find yourself crossing swords with him.
My lord. My lord, I have always been faithful to House Arryn. To your father, to your mother, and now to you.
You can trust my absolute loyalty, my lord.
Your Grace, with respect I must agree with Lady Sansa.  I remember the Mad King all too well.  A Targaryen cannot be trusted, nor can a Lannister.
My sons have had swords in their hands from the time they could walk. This one…
Look at me. Look at me! Do you remember me now, boy, eh? Remember me? There's a bright boy. You'll be coming with me, boy, and you'll be keeping your mouth shut.
Keep your mouth shut, boy. (takes out a knife)

You're not a smart boy, is that what you're trying to say? Do you want to live, boy?! (starts cutting Arya’s hair as the screams and shouts from the crowd are heard above) North, boy, we're going north.
You're Arry now, hear me? Arry the orphan boy. No one asks an orphan too many questions, ‘cause nobody gives three shits. What's your name?

You've a long way to travel, and in bad company. l've 20 this time –  men and boys all bound for the Wall. Your lord father gave me the pick of the dungeons, and l didn't find no little lordlings down there. This lot – half of them would turn you over to the King quick as spit for a pardon. And the other half would do the same, except they'd rape you first. So keep to yourself – and when you piss, do it in the woods alone. You stay with this lot, boy. And stay or l'll lock you in the back of the wagon with these three.
(shouting) Come on, you sorry sons of whores! lt's a thousand leagues from here to the Wall, and winter is coming!
Don't look!
Shut your mouth. Look at me.
Yoren, if it please. This must be your son. He has the look.
No one sent me, my Lord. I'm here to find men for The Wall, see if there's any scum in the dungeons that might be fit for service.

Thank you, my Lord. But that's not why I disturb you now. Your brother, Benjen... his blood runs black, makes him as much my brother as yours. It's for his sake I rode here so hard I damn near killed my horse! There are others riding too. The whole city will know by tomorrow.
Best said in private, my Lord.
It's about your wife, my Lord. She's taken the Imp.
Aye, my Lord.
A bear's balls.
And his brains and his guts, his lungs and his heart all fried in his own fat. When you're a hundred miles north of the Wall and you ate your last meal a week ago, you leave nothing for the wolves.
A bit chewy. And what about you, my Lord? What's the strangest thing you've eaten?
But it's not all of 'em's done bad things. Some of 'em's just poor lads looking for steady feed. Some of 'em's high-born lads looking for glory.
Are you going below? Keep well, keep warm.
I always do.
Into the tunnel and out the other side. He'll be north of the Wall for a month or two.
Day after tomorrow. I get about half of my recruits from their dungeons.
I travel a bit on the grubby side, my Lord.
You should be sleeping. Tomorrow's a long march. Thirty miles, if it don't piss on us.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You don't drink it for the flavor, to be honest. What?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Same as most men, I think.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Aye. I've seen some pretty things, too, but not nearly so many.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You didn't see that. I made damn sure.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You know, we've got something in common, me and you. You know that? I must have been a couple of years older than you. I saw my brother stabbed through the heart right on our doorstep. He weren't much of a villain what skewered him. Willem, the lad's name was. He ran off before anyone could spit. And I just stood there, watching my brother die. Here's the funny part. I can't picture my brother's face anymore. But Willem, oh, he was a nice-looking boy. He had good white teeth, blue eyes, one of those dimpled chins all the girls like. I would think about him when I was working, when I was drinking, when I was having a shit. It got to the point where I would say his name every night before I went to bed. Willem. Willem. Willem. A prayer almost. Well, one day, Willem came riding back into town. I buried an ax so deep into Willem's skull they had to bury him with it. Willem's horse got me to the Wall and I've been wearing black ever since. Well... That'll help you sleep, eh?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Ho! Get up, you lazy sons of whores! Arm yourselves.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Keep out of sight, both of you.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Keep out of sight. If things go wrong, you run. Do you hear me? You run along north and don't look back. Hey, there's men out there who want to fuck your corpses. Outside, now!
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Got more than a few bastards here. Who's asking?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Now, which king would that be?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I don't think I will.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I always hated crossbows. Take too long to load.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You're a long way from home.
Aye, you did. You asked without manners. And I chose not to answer.
Well the thing is, (Hands back the paper), these gutter rats belong to the Night's Watch now. That puts them beyond the reach of kings and queens.

It's a funny thing. People worry so much about their throats, they forget about what's down low. Now I sharpened this blade before breakfast. I could shave a spider's arse if I wanted to. Or, I could nick this artery in your leg and once it's nicked, there's no one around here who knows how to un-nick it.

We'll just keep that. Good steel is always needed on the wall. Seems you have a choice. You can die here at this crossroads a long way from home. Or you can go back to your city and tell your masters you didn't find what you were looking for.
Lyanna!
Would you get out of here? We’re sparring.
I don’t know.
But he’s got giant’s blood.
Hold the door!
Hold the door! Hold the door! Hold the door! Hold the door! Hold the door! Hold the door!

Hold the door! Hold the door! Hold the door! Hold the door! Hold the door! Hold the door! Hold the door! Hold the door! Hold door! Hold door! Hold door! Hold door! Hodor! Hodor! Hodor! Hodor! Hodor! Hodor! Hodor!
And lowers it when he’s going to dodge, my lady.
Who are you gonna spar with when Ned goes off to the Eyrie?
What about him?
Benjen always lifts his chin when he’s about to charge.
Course we do.
I’m ready.
Fuck you! Disgusting old twats.
Got off on that, did you, you old fuck?
Yes, Father.
Stop showing off.
Wylis, come here.
And you’ve got training. Sounds like a fair match to me.
Oh, Nan, look at the size of him. If he ever learned to fight, he’d be unstoppable.
Keep your shield up or I’ll ring your head like a bell.
Give him another go, Ned.
How do you know?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: We begin where “Baelor” left off; after the execution of Ned Stark, we see Ilyn Payne’s bloodied sword and the Hound picking up Ned’s head to show it to the rapturous townsfolk. We then pan to Arya, her eyes closed and her face pressed against Yoren’s chest.
Yoren takes Arya with him away from the mob and into a flight of stairs; on the courtyard, Sansa faints.
At Winterfell, Bran has a dream in which he can walk and sees a three-eyed raven. He then talks about this dream with Osha.
Osha takes Bran to the crypts. She carries him with her arms while he lights the way with a torch.
A loud roar is heard and a shadow moves in front of them. A direwolf runs towards them; Osha falls to the ground with him. We see Shaggydog barking at them viciously.
Rickon leaves with Shaggydog, while Osha looks puzzled at Bran. We cut to a shot of her carrying him outside.
Catelyn Stark walks alone through the Stark camp. Several bannermen bow to her respectfully as she passes.

She reaches a small forest outside camp and collapses in grief by one of the trees, crying and sobbing. She hears some screams and the sound of a sword slashing wood nearby. As she gets closer, she realizes it’s Robb, fully armoured, slashing his sword against a tree while crying.
Robb drops his sword to the ground and goes to Catelyn. She hugs him and caresses his neck, trying to console him.
At the Throne Room on the Red Keep, Marillion the bard is singing a song for Joffrey and his retinue. Cercei stands by his side, while Sansa overlooks the scene from one of the colonnades.
The boar's great tusks, they boded ill
For good King Robert's health
And the beast was every bit as fat
As Robert was himself
But our brave king cried,
"Do your worst!
l'll have your ugly head!
You're nowhere near as murd'rous as
the lion in my bed"
King Robert lost his battle and
He failed his final test,
The lion ripped his balls off,
And the boar did all the rest.
Sansa looks at Joffrey, despondent.
Ilyn Payne marches towards the fire to heat up his dagger.
As Joffrey leaves with Meryn Trant and the Hound, he passes by Sansa. Marillion is still screaming and begging in the background.
Joffrey and Sansa are outside, walking past across a row of spears in the Red Keep. Trant and Clegane follow behind them.
The pikes have impaled heads on them. Sansa looks away.
Sansa looks up at the head of her father.
Sansa tries to give him is handkerchief back.
Sandor leaves, leaving Sansa alone at the bridge. She looks at the heads atop the pikes and cries.
War meeting at the Stark camp. They sit at the table at night by torchlight.
They all take out their swords and swear fealty to Robb as Catelyn looks wearily at the whole scene.
Catelyn goes to the outskirts of their camp, to the dungeons where prisoners are being kept.
They walk for a while until they reach a single prisoner (Jaime), kept away far from the rest.
Cersei and Lancel are having one of their rendezvous on her bedchamber. Cersei is reading a message while Lancel speaks, naked.
Back in the Riverlands, we sit with the Lannister war meeting. Tywin, Tyrion and Kevan are in assistance.
Across the Narrow Sea, Daenerys is sleeping inside a yurt. Jorah is sitting beside her when she wakes up.
They leave the tent/yurt, following Mirri Maz Duur to the place where Drogo is lying.
Daenerys and Mirri Maz Duur have a conversation alone.
At Castle Black, Jon is preparing to leave in the night and desert the Night’s Watch; Sam is there to try to talk him out of it.
Jon rides his horse away from Castle Black, his horse kicking Sam to the ground as he was standing in the way. Ghost follows him out.
In the Lannister camp in the Riverlands, Tyrion and Shae are talking inside his tent about his recent appointment.
Tyrion laughs.
Jon and Ghost are being chased by his fellow Night’s Watch brothers through a forest not long after he left.
During the chase, Sam hits his head on a tree branch and falls off his horse. The others and later Jon stop to assist him.
Sam brings Longclaw to Jon, who accepts it.
Across the Narrow Sea, Daenerys is tending to Drogo and his wounds inside a yurt.
We cut to Daenerys standing with her head pressing against Drogo’s chest, now visibly more distressed.
Daenerys kisses Drogo, then uses a pillow to smother him.
In King’s Landing, Pycelle is having a conversation with someone (unseen at first) in his bedchambers.
Ros gets dressed while Pycelle keeps talking.
Ros is listening, but seems really bored by the whole thing.

He gets dressed in his robes and looks at himself in the mirror with pride. Before he leaves his chambers, he moves from an upright position to the hunchback he’s always seen with in public. He’s faking it.
In the Throne Room, Littlefinger and Varys have a chat overlooking the Iron Throne.
Elsewhere in King’s Landing, Yoren is taking Arya to safety, away from the city.
Arya bumps into a fat recruit, Hot Pie.
Hot Pie bumps into Gendry, Robert’s bastard.
The caravan of the Night’s Watch leaves the walls of King’s Landing.
Back at Castle Black, Jon is serving Commander Mormont his breakfast.

(Jon looks at the Lord Commander with surprise) Don't look so terrified. lf we beheaded everyone that ran away for the night, only ghosts would guard the Wall. At least you weren't whoring in Mole's Town. Honor made you leave, honor brought you back.
The Men of the Night’s Watch gather their forces at leave to range Beyond the Wall. Lord Commander Mormont leads them, with Jon amongst his brothers.
Across the Narrow Sea, Daenerys is leading a ceremony for Drogo after his death.
Mirri Maz Duur is shown tied and bound by rope.
(to the crowd)  l am Daenerys Stormborn of House Targaryen of the blood of old Valyria. I am the Dragon’s daughter. And I swear to you that those who would harm you will die screaming.

Daenerys walks towards the pyre and lights it on fire with a torch. Mirri Maz Duur starts uttering some incantations which quickly turn into screaming. Daenerys walks into the burning pyre as her dress catches fire. The flames engulf everything, and the screen fades to black.

We then cut to the next day in the morning. Jorah goes to the pyre to see if there’s anything left of it. Amidst the smoke, a naked Daenerys appears, covered in soot and carrying three baby dragons in her arms and back.
Everyone else bows to her as her dragons start screeching. We cut to black over a final screech from one her dragons.
TYRION wakes to echos of the sounds of battle. PYCELLE's face comes into focus. TYRION's head is crudely bandaged, with only one eye revealed. He is disoriented.
PODRICK bursts into the room
PYCELLE turns and begins to leave. A thought occurs to him. He takes out a coin and flicks it to Tyrion
Pycelle exits.
A white horse expels manure at the doorway leading to the throne room. TYWIN rides the horse in, through the gathered crowd.  From his throne, JOFFREY addresses the room.
Bowing, a page presents a pillow to JOFFREY, who places the Hand's brooch up to TYWIN. TYWIN bows.
He turns his mount and exits.
LITTLEFINGER steps forward and kneels before JOFFREY.
LORAS steps forward from the crowd and kneels.
MARGAERY takes a few steps forward.
The crowd murmurs.
JOFFREY stands and gestures to quiet the crowd.
CERSEI looks across to SANSA. CERSEI is pleased and SANSA is quite worried.
The crowd applauds.
SANSA walks away, quite solemn. Once she is out of view, she smiles and laughs in relief. LITTLEFINGER catches up to her.
ROS applies make-up to her eye. There is a knock on the door. ROS stands up to welcome her client.
A hooded figure walks in, silently. He sits on the end of the bed.
The man removes his hood. It is VARYS.
ROS reaches to fondle his cock, but finds that VARYS is a eunuch. She is taken aback, and steps backward.
BRIENNE pulls the boat up to the shore. JAIME waits, his hands chained.
Annoyed, BRIENNE pushes him forward. He walks and she follows.
They encounter three hanged women in the trees.
BRIENNE pushes him roughly below the bodies, finally pinning him against a tree, and tying him fast against it.
Some men approach
JAIME, nervously, gesters to BRIENNE.
Three men approach, in leather armor and bearing swords.
The three men laugh hysterically
The men laugh even harder.
She turns to take care of JAIME.
BRIENNE works to unbind JAIME from the tree.
BRIENNE does not answer.
MAN #2 moves closer.
BRIENNE frees him from the tree, and she and JAIME start to walk away.
Again, they try to move along.

BRIENNE pushes JAIME aside, draws her sword, and sets on the men, visciously. JAIME is surprised at her prowess in battle. She bests them all, and turns to MAN, who lies on the forest floor. She drops her long sword and draws her short sword.
She stabs the MEN in the gut, painfully. She plunges deeper, and the MAN dies. She withdraws her sword and steps away.
BRIENNE turns and chops the rope that holds the three women's corpses with a large grunt. JAIME winces as the bodies thud to the ground.
ROBB sits in his tent. Catelyn sits as well.
Robb exits.
STANNIS sits by a hearth.
STANNIS leaps to his feet and starts choking MELISANDRE. She gasps painfully, grabbing his hands.
MELISANDRE removes her hands from his and raises them in surrender.
STANNIS releases her suddenly and MELISANDRE falls to the follow, panting.
MELISANDRE walks over to STANNIS.
MELISANDRE caresses his head, soothingly.
She leads him over to the fire.
THEON throws another log onto his fire, and sits before it, frustrated. A lone horn blows outside the gates of Winterfell.
The horn continues.
LUWIN crosses the room, and rests.
The horn blows again. THEON rolls his eyes in anger.
The horn sounds again. THEON gets up in a fury. He shakes his fist at the window.
THEON sits again.
LUWIN leans over THEON's shoulder.
LUWIN faces THEON
THOEN rises.
LUWIN comforts him with a gentle pat on the shoulder.
THEON stands in the Winterfell courtyard, addressing his men.
The ironborn men laugh.
DAGMER hits THEON on the back of the head, knocking him unconscious. The ironborn watch him fall.
A man placed a hood over THEON's head.
LUWIN enters.
DAGMER stabs LUWIN in the gut with his spear. LUWIN crumbles to the ground, groaning in sharp pain.
DAGMER and the ironborn exit, dragging THEON with them. LUWIN collapses.
TYRION sits in bed, his face still bandaged
PODRICK opens the door and SHAE enters briskly. She comes to TYRION's side and sits.
VARYS bows.
VARYS and PODRICK exit.
SHAE reaches to adjust TYRION's bandages, but he prevents her.
She removes the bandages. They stick to the deep wound across TYRION's cheek. He grunts in pain. SHAE takes in the wreck of his cheek.
TYRION bursts into tears. SHAE envelops him in her arms. TYRION holds her as well.
A SEPTON marries ROBB and TALISA to one another.
They kiss.
DAENERYS, JORAH and KOVARRO walk through a garden. It is known.
They come to the base of the House of the Undying.
They ascend the first flight of stairs, and gaze up to the top of the tower. They cannot find an entrance.
They circle the base. DAENERYS disappears from JORAH's view. He encounters KOVARRO instead.
DAENERYS stands in darkness. She picks up a torch from the wall.
A dragon screeches. DAENERYS follows the sound up a stairwell.
ARYA, GENDRY and HOT PIE trek through the Shadowlands. High above them, JAQEN stands atop a steep cliff.
As they pass to the other side of the cliff, JAQEN disappears. Suddenly, he is at ground level, next to ARYA.
They begin to walk.
He offers a coin.
JAQEN begins to walk away
JAQEN turns away and touches his hand to his face. When he turns back, his face has changed completely.
JAQEN exits.
OSHA, BRAN, HODOR and RICKON leave their hiding place and enter the courtyard.
They walk through the smouldering courtyard, among bodies and wreckage. They spy Summer and Shaggydog, whimpering in an alcove. BRAN commands HODOR to bring him that way.

LUWIN lays by the godswood, dying. HODOR, BRAN, OSHA and RICKON walk toward him. Rickon starts to run, and OSHA cannot catch him. LUWIN reaches out, happy to see him and BRAN. RICKON notices the great bloodstain on LUWIN's shirt.
The four of them get up to leave, but OSHA steps back.
OSHA shows LUWIN her knife, and he nods his asent.
OSHA draws her blade while looking LUWIN in the eyes.
Summer and Shaggydog walk across the fields. OSHA, RICKON, HODOR and BRAN follow. BRAN is in a cart. Behind them, Winterfell burns.

DAENERYS continues to search for her dragons. She follows the sound of their screeching through a door. She looks down into a pit before trying a door on the opposite side of the chamber. She goes through and the wind whistles. The large chamber has a broken roof, and snow falls. She sets down her torch, and ascends to a stage with a throne. Dragons screech again, and she does not touch the snowy throne. She walks toward the noises, and a large portal door opens before her. She exits, finding herself in a land of snow and ice. She sees a lone tent in the distance. She walks to it.
DROGO sits, holding their son Rhaego
DAENERYS cannot believe her eyes. She walks closer.
DAENERYS sits next to DROGO, and she caresses RHAEGO's head.
DROGO And DAENERYS stare deeply into one another's eyes.
They touch foreheads and DROGO caresses her face, gently.
DAENERYS looks down at RHAEGO. Dragons screech.
She caresses DROGO's face and touches RHAEGO one more time. Then DAENERYS gets up a quickly walks away. DROGO is heartbroken.
When DAENERYS leaves the tent, she finds herself back in the House of the Undying. Her three dragons sit on a pedestal. They call out to her, excited. She walks closer, and holds out a hand for them
DAENERYS turns around, startled. Behind her, another PYATT PREE clone watches while she looks at the one who spoke. The two PYATTs share the dialog, speaking with one mind.
A third PYATT PREE appears in an alcove.
The sound of chains turning starts to her right.

DAENERYS finds that her wrist is chained. PYATT PREE tightens the chain that binds her. DAENERYS is confused. Another PYATT PREE pulls on a chain now attached to her other wrist. Her arms are now outstreched and straining.
DAENERYS turns to look at her dragons. PYATT PREE peers down at them. DAENERYS turns back to the warlock.

The dragons breath smoke. Then one spurts out a burp of fire. It catches PYATT PREE's sleeve, and he starts to panic. All three dragons spit fire, past DAENERYS who experiences the ecstasy of the fire. PYATT PREE is consumed by flame. He screams horrible, and falls to the ground. DAENERYS looks back to her dragons. The chains break.
YGRITTE swings the sword again, JON feints and she misses.
YGRITTE challenges JON. QHORIN uses the distraction to punch his captor in the face, and steal his sword. He pushes by RATTLESHIRT and attacks JON. JON falls.
Two wildlings hold QHORIN back.

YGRITTE drops JON's sword for him. The wildlings release QHORIN, who charges at JON. He tries to grab his sword while scrambling away from the attack. He gets to his feet, and they battle convincingly. QUORIN forces JON to stumble into two wildlings, who push him back into the fight.
QHORIN attacks again. JON counters.
JON goes on the attack, but QHORIN parries.
QHORIN headbutts JON, who falls. He gathers himself and charges, disarming QHORIN. He runs Quorin through.
QHORIN falls, dead.
Rattleshirt takes the sword from JON, and then slices through his bounds.
RATTLESHIRT exits and YGRITTE comes to JON's side.
They walk together to the edge of the cliff.
She walks down the slope. JON looks back to see the wildlings burning QHORIN's body. He follows YGRITTE.
XARO sleeps with DOREAH next to him. A curved sword hooks his amulet and steals it away. XARO awakes startled and sits up.
JORAH, DAENERYS, KOVARRO and the Dothraki have entered his bedroom. DOREAH sits up next to XARO
DAENERYS turns and walks. XARO makes a sudden move, but JORAH draws his sword and keeps him motionless.
KOVARRO uses the amulet to open the vault's door. JORAH helps him to open the door. DAENERYS enters first. The vault is empty.
She turns around
The Dothraki push XARO and DOREAH forward into the vault.
The Dothraki close the vault door behind the pair.
JORAH shuts the door and locks it. He hands the amulet key to DAENERYS.
They are looking for things to loot.
DAENERYS hands him a golden vase.
DAENERYS walks away.
The Dothraki cheer, and being to loot the palace. DAENERYS walks lovingly with her dragons.
GRENN, EDDISON TOLLETT and SAM continue to labor in the cold and snow.
GRENN finds a piece of dung.
EDDISON TOLLETT takes it, placing it in his basket.
They continue to dig.
A horn blows in the distance.
The horn blows again
The horn blows a third time.
GRENN and EDDISON TOLLETT flee. SAM is slower to follow.
SAM falls, spilling his basket.
GRENN and EDDISON TOLLETT are running further away, not waiting or helping.

SAM stands. There is a high-pitches squeal. SAM sees figures coming towards him. He hides behind a large boulder. The figures comple closer, walking slowly. They are undead and decaying with blue eyes. An undead horse stops next to sam. A white walker rides it, and he notices SAM. SAM begins to sob. The white walker shrieks and orders his undead followers forward. There are scores of them, a seemingly endless onslaught. Another white walker is mounted behind. He also gives the order forward.
ROOSE BOLTON climbs the stairs to the top of the Twins. The sounds of the battle below draw him to the edge.  The Stark camp is engulfed in flames as the battle rages on.
The battle outside the castle walls is fierce. Stark bannermen and  Frey forces battle for their lives. Swords clash, and some men are set aflame. One man is strung up by his neck into the trees.
The HOUND rides out from the castle, ARYA slumped in front of him. He surveys the battle and picks up a Stark banner as he rides by. He urges his mount forward and they trot into the flames.
The HOUND pauses amidst the blinding smoke and flames. His horse whinnies nerviously. The chants of the Frey men grow louder.

Frey men march out of the castle. Mounted behind them, ROBB STARK's headless body has been turned into a grotesque effigy. It is tied roughly onto a post. Grey Wind's head has been mounted to the corpse's shoulders.
ARYA rouses in front of the HOUND. She watches in horror as the Frey men lead the awkward effigy into the yard.
ARYA is dejected and the HOUND has fear in his eyes. He spurs the horse forward, and they ride away through the fire and battle. A Stark banner succumbs to the flames.
SANSA and TYRION stroll side-by-side through the gardens on a beautiful day. SHAE follows, a few paces back. ELDRICK and DESMOND pass, snickering at them
TYRION realizes that SHAE overheard that comment. He looks back, embarrassed, and she returns his look, cooly.
